IDX.first_line: a asesa gunera nidhi gaurangasundara
IDX.search: a asesa gunera nidhi gaurangasundara a anande vibhora sada premera sagara i indu jini vadanera sobha manohara i isvara brahmadi jare bhave nirantara
IDX.author: premadāsa
IDX.verses:
a, aśeṣa guṇera nidhi gaurāṅgasundara
ā, ānande vibhora sadā premera sāgara

i, indu jini’ vadanera śobhā manohara
ī, īśvara, brahmādi jā̐’re bhāve nirantara

u, uddhārilā jaga⋅jane diyā premadhana
ū, ūna pāpī⋅tāpī nāhi kailā vicāraṇa

ṛ, ṛṇa śudhibāre prabhu śrīmatī rādhāra
ṝ, rītimata nadīyāya hailā avatāra

ḷ, lipta śrī⋅gaurāṅga⋅tanu śrī⋅haricandane
ḹ, līlāvalī sabe heri’ haya acetane

e, emana dayālu prabhu nāhi ha’be āra
ai, aikāntika kṛṣṇa-bhakti karila pracāra

o, oḍhradeśe jāiyā prabhu bahu līlā kaila
au, audārya⋅guṇete sārva-bhaume nistārila

caturdaśa svarāvalī je kare kīrtana
acire labhaye sei gaurāṅga⋅caraṇa

śrī⋅jāhnavā⋅rāmacandra⋅pada kari’ āśa
caturdaśa svarāvalī gāya premadāsa
IDX.en_translation:
A - Gaurāṅgasundara is a treasure trove of endless virtues. Ā - He is always immersed in joy, an ocean of divine love.

I - The charm of His face surpasses the moon. Ī - The Supreme Lord, whom Brahmā and others meditate upon constantly.

U - He delivered the people of the world by giving the wealth of love. Ū - He did not consider anyone less, whether sinner or distressed.

Ṛ - To repay the debt to Śrīmatī Rādhā⋅rāṇī, Ṝ - He properly incarnated in Nadīyā.

Ḷ - Śrī Gaurāṅga's body is anointed with sandalwood paste. Ḹ - Seeing His divine pastimes, everyone becomes enraptured.

E - There will never be another Lord so merciful. Ai - He preached exclusive devotion to Lord Kṛṣṇa.

O - Going to the land of Odisha, the Lord performed many pastimes. Au - By His quality of magnanimity, He delivered Sārvabhauma Bhaṭṭācārya.

Whoever chants these fourteen vowels soon attains the lotus feet of Lord Gaurāṅga.

With hope in the feet of Śrī Jāhnavā and Rāmacandra, Premadāsa sings these fourteen vowels.

IDX.first_line: acyuta kesava rama narayana
IDX.search: acyuta kesava rama narayana krsna damodara vasudeva bhaje sridhara madhava gopika vallabha janaki nayaka ramacandra bhaje radhika nayaka krsna candra bhaje
IDX.verses:
acyuta̐, keśava̐, rāma, nārāyaṇa̐,
kṛṣṇa̐ dāmodara̐, vāsudeva̐ bhaje
śrīdhara̐, mādhava̐, gopikā⋅vallabha̐,
jānakī nāyaka̐ rāmacandra̐ bhaje
rādhikā nāyaka̐ kṛṣṇa⋅candra̐ bhaje
IDX.en_translation: I worship Acyuta, Keśava, Rāma, Nārāyaṇa, Kṛṣṇa, Dāmodara, and Vāsudeva. I worship Śrīdhara, Mādhava, Gopikā-vallabha, and Rāmacandra, the hero of Jānakī. I worship Kṛṣṇa⋅candra, the hero of Rādhikā.

IDX.first_line: adharam madhuram vadanam madhuram
IDX.search: adharam madhuram vadanam madhuram nayanam madhuram hasitam madhuram hrdayam madhuram gamanam madhuram madhuradhi pateh akhilam madhuram vacanam madhuram caritam madhuram vasanam madhuram valitam madhuram calitam madhuram bhramitam madhuram madhuradhi pateh akhilam madhuram
IDX.author: śrīmad vallabhācārya
IDX.verses:
adharaṁ madhuraṁ vadanaṁ madhuraṁ
nayanaṁ madhuraṁ hasitaṁ madhuram
hṛdayaṁ madhuraṁ gamanaṁ madhuraṁ
madhurādhi⋅pateḥ⋅akhilaṁ madhuram

vacanaṁ madhuraṁ caritaṁ madhuraṁ
vasanaṁ madhuraṁ valitaṁ madhuram
calitaṁ madhuraṁ bhramitaṁ madhuraṁ
madhurādhi⋅pateḥ⋅akhilaṁ madhuram

veṇurmadhuro reṇurmadhuraḥ
pāṇirmadhuraḥ pādau madhurau
nṛtyaṁ madhuraṁ sakhyaṁ madhuraṁ
madhurādhi⋅pateḥ⋅akhilaṁ madhuram

gītaṁ madhuraṁ pītaṁ madhuraṁ
bhuktaṁ madhuraṁ suptaṁ madhuram
rūpaṁ madhuraṁ tilakaṁ madhuraṁ
madhurādhi⋅pateḥ⋅akhilaṁ madhuram

karaṇaṁ madhuraṁ taraṇaṁ madhuraṁ
haraṇaṁ madhuraṁ ramaṇaṁ madhuram
vamitaṁ madhuraṁ śamitaṁ madhuraṁ
madhurādhi⋅pateḥ⋅akhilaṁ madhuram

guñjā madhurā mālā madhurā
jamunā madhurā vīcī madhurā
salilaṁ madhuraṁ kamalaṁ madhuraṁ
madhurādhi⋅pateḥ⋅akhilaṁ madhuram

gopī madhurā līlā madhurā
juktaṁ madhuraṁ bhuktaṁ madhuram
hṛṣṭaṁ madhuraṁ śliṣṭaṁ madhuraṁ
madhurādhi⋅pateḥ⋅akhilaṁ madhuram

gopā madhurā gāvo madhurā
jaṣṭirmadhurā sṛṣṭirmadhurā
dalitaṁ madhuraṁ phalitaṁ madhuraṁ
madhurādhi⋅pateḥ⋅akhilaṁ madhuram
IDX.en_translation:
His lips are sweet, His face is sweet, His eyes are sweet, His smile is sweet, His heart is sweet, His walking is sweet—everything about the Lord of sweetness is sweet.

His words are sweet, His character is sweet, His garments are sweet, His graceful posture is sweet, His movements are sweet, and His wandering is sweet—everything about the Lord of sweetness is sweet.

His flute is sweet, the dust from His feet is sweet, His hands are sweet, His feet are sweet, His dancing is sweet, and His friendship is sweet—everything about the Lord of sweetness is sweet.

His singing is sweet, His drinking is sweet, His eating is sweet, His sleeping is sweet, His form is sweet, and His sacred mark is sweet—everything about the Lord of sweetness is sweet.

His actions are sweet, His victory is sweet, His charm is sweet, His play is sweet, His emanations are sweet, and His peacefulness is sweet—everything about the Lord of sweetness is sweet.

His berries are sweet, His garland is sweet, the Yamunā is sweet, its waves are sweet, its water is sweet, and its lotus flowers are sweet—everything about the Lord of sweetness is sweet.

His gopīs are sweet, His divine pastimes are sweet, His association is sweet, His enjoyment is sweet, His happiness is sweet, and His embrace is sweet—everything about the Lord of sweetness is sweet.

His cowherd friends are sweet, His cows are sweet, His staff is sweet, His creation is sweet, His conquering is sweet, and His blessings are sweet—everything about the Lord of sweetness is sweet.

IDX.first_line: advaita acarya gosani saksat isvara
IDX.search: advaita acarya gosani saksat isvara jahara mahima nahe jivera gocara mahavisnu srsti karena jagadadi karya tara avatara saksat advaita acarya
IDX.author: kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa
IDX.verses:
advaita⋅ācārya gosāñi sākṣāt īśvara
jā̐hāra mahimā nahe jīvera gocara

mahāviṣṇu sṛṣṭi karena jagadādi⋅kārya
tā̐ra avatāra sākṣāt advaita ācārya

jagat⋅maṅgala advaita, maṅgala⋅guṇadhāma
maṅgala⋅caritra sadā, ‘maṅgala’ jā̐ra nāma

kamala⋅nayanera te̐ho, jāte ‘aṅga’, ‘aṁśa’
‘kamalākṣa’ bali’ dhare nāma avataṁsa

mahāviṣṇura aṁśa⋅advaita guṇadhāma
īśvare abheda, teñi ‘advaita’ pūrṇanāma

bhakti⋅upadeśa vinu tā̐ra nāhi kārya
atoeva nāma haila ‘advaita ācārya’

advaita ācārya⋅īśvarera aṁśavarya
tā̐ra tattva⋅nāma⋅guṇa, sakali āścarya

jā̐hāra tulasīdale, jā̐hāra huṅkāre
svagaṇa sahite caitanyera avatāre

jā̐ra dvārā kaila prabhu kīrtana pracāra
jā̐ra dvārā kaila prabhu jagat nistāra

ācārya gosāñira guṇa⋅mahimā apāra
tā̐hāra caraṇe mora koṭi namaskāra

jaya jayādvaitācārya caitanyera ārya
svacaraṇe bhakti deha’ jayādvaitācārya
IDX.en_translation:
Advaita Ācārya is directly the Supreme Lord. His glory is not comprehensible to the ordinary soul.

Lord Mahā-Viṣṇu performs the activities of creation of the universe. His incarnation is directly Advaita Ācārya.

Advaita is the auspiciousness of the world and the abode of auspicious qualities. His character is always auspicious, and His Name is also "Auspicious."

He is the one with lotus eyes, in whom there is both the "limb" and the "portion" of the Lord. He bears the Name "Kamalākṣa" as an ornament.

Advaita is a portion of Lord Mahā-Viṣṇu and is the abode of all qualities. He is non-different from the Supreme Lord; therefore His full Name is "Advaita."

Without teaching devotion, He has no activity. Therefore, His Name became "Advaita Ācārya."

Advaita Ācārya is the best portion of the Supreme Lord. His spiritual identity, Name, and qualities are all wonderful.

By His offering of Tulasī leaves and by His roar, Lord Caitanya incarnated with His own associates.

Through Him the Lord propagated congregational chanting and delivered the world.

The qualities and glory of Advaita Ācārya are boundless. At his feet, I offer millions of obeisances.

All glories, all glories to Advaita Ācārya, the venerable associate of Lord Caitanya! Please grant me devotion at your feet.

IDX.first_line: ahankara abhimana asatsanga asajjnana
IDX.search: ahankara abhimana asatsanga asajjnana chadi bhaja gurupada padma kara atmanivedana deha geha parijana guruvakya parama mahattva sri krsnacaitanyadeva niravadhi tare seva prema kalpataru varadata sri vraja rajanandana radhika jivanadhana aparupa ei saba katha
IDX.verses:
ahaṅkāra, abhimāna, asatsaṅga, asajjñāna,
chāḍi’ bhaja gurupāda⋅padma
kara ātmanivedana, deha⋅geha⋅parijana,
guruvākya parama mahattva

śrī⋅kṛṣṇacaitanyadeva, niravadhi tā̐re seva,
prema⋅kalpataru⋅varadātā
śrī⋅vraja⋅rājanandana, rādhikā jīvanadhana,
aparūpa ei saba kathā

navadvīpe avatari’, rādhābhāva aṅgīkari,
tā̐ra kānti aṅgera bhūṣaṇa
tina vāñchā abhilāṣi’, śacīgarbhe parakāśi’,
saṅge lañā pāriṣadagaṇa

gaura⋅hari avatari’, premera bādara kari’,
sādhilā manera tina kāja
rādhikāra prāṇapati, kibā bhāve kā̐de niti,
ihā bujhe bhakata⋅samāja

gopane sādhile siddhi, sādhana⋅navadhā bhakti,
prārthanā kariba dainya sadā
kari’ hari saŋkīrtana, sadāi vibhola mana,
iṣṭalābha vinu saba bādhā

saṁsāra⋅vāṭoyāre, kāma phā̐se bā̐dhi’ māre,
phukāri kahaye haridāsa
karaha bhakata saṅga, premakathā⋅rasaraṅga,
tabe habe vipada vināśa

strī⋅putra⋅bāndhava jata, mari’ jābe śata śata,
āpanāke hoo sāvadhāna
muñi se viṣaye hata, nā bhajinu haripada,
mora āra nāhi paritrāṇa

rāmacandra kavirāja, sei saṅge mora kāja,
tā̐ra saṅga vinu saba śūnya
jadi haya janma punaḥ, tā̐ra saṅga laya jena,
tabe haya narottama dhanya

āpana bhajana kathā, nā kahiba jathā tathā,
ihāte hoio sāvadhāna
nā kariha keha roṣa, nā loiha mora doṣa,
praṇamaha bhaktera caraṇa
IDX.en_translation:
Abandoning ego, false pride, bad association, and ignorance, worship the lotus feet of your spiritual master. Perform self-surrender with your body, home, and relatives, for the words of the guru are of supreme importance.

Constantly serve Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya, the bestower of the wish-fulfilling tree of divine love. The son of the King of Vraja is Rādhā's treasure of life—all these topics are wonderful.

Appearing in Navadvīpa and accepting the mood of Rādhā, Her luster became the adornment of His body. Desiring to fulfill three wishes, He manifested in the womb of Mother Śacī, taking along His associates.

Gaura⋅hari descended, creating a flood of divine love, and accomplished the three works of His heart. In what mood the beloved of Rādhā constantly cries—only the society of devotees understands this.

If perfection is attained secretly through the nine processes of devotional service, I will always pray with humility. By performing the congregational chanting of the Holy Name with a mind always absorbed, all obstacles are removed for the desired attainment.

In the marketplace of the material world, lust binds and kills with its noose. A servant of the Lord loudly proclaims: "Seek the association of devotees and engage in the joyful pastimes of loving discussions; then all dangers will be destroyed."

Wife, sons, and all relatives—hundreds and hundreds will die. Be careful of yourself! I am ruined by these material affairs; I did not worship the lotus feet of the Lord. For me, there is no more deliverance.

My purpose lies in the association of Rāmacandra Kavirāja. Without his association, everything is void. If I am reborn, may I attain his association; then Narottama will be blessed.

I will not speak of the topics of my devotional service indiscriminately here and there; be careful in this regard. Let no one be angry, and do not take my fault. Offer obeisances to the feet of the devotees.

IDX.first_line: ahe nila saila prabada mata barana
IDX.search: ahe nila saila prabada mata barana mo arata nalini banaku kara dalana gajaraja cintakala thai ghora jalena cakrapesi graha nasi uddharila apana
IDX.author: kālavega
IDX.verses:
āhe nīla śaila, prabaḍa mata bāraṇa
mo ārata nalinī banaku kara dalana

gajarāja cintākalā, thāi ghora jalena
cakrapeśī grāha nāśi uddhārila āpana

ghora vane mṛguṇiki paḍithilā vasana
kete baḍa vipattiru kari acha tāraṇa

kurusabhātaḍe śuni draupadīra janāna
koṭī vastra dei heḍe, lajjā kala bāraṇa

rāvaṇara bhāi vibhīṣaṇa, galā śaraṇa
caraṇa sambhāḍi tāku laṅke kala rājana

prahlāda pitā se je baḍa duṣṭa dāruṇa
stambharu bāhāri tāku vidārila takṣaṇa

kahe kālavega hīna jātire mu javana
śrī⋅raṅgā̐ caraṇa taḍe karu āchi janāna
IDX.en_translation:
O Lord of Nīlācala! Like a mighty elephant, please trample upon the lotus grove of my distress.

The king of elephants, Gajendra, meditated while in the midst of terrible waters. You, the wielder of the disc, destroyed the crocodile and saved him.

In a dense forest, a fawn was trapped. From what great calamity did You save her?

Hearing Draupadī's appeal in the assembly of the Kauravas, O Lord, You averted her shame by bestowing millions of garments.

Rāvaṇa's brother, Vibhīṣaṇa, took refuge in You. Protecting his feet, You made him the king of Laṅkā.

Prahlāda's father was very wicked and terrible. You emerged from the pillar and instantly tore him apart.

The poet Kālavega says: "I am a person of humble origin. I am appealing at Your lotus feet."

IDX.first_line: aju saci nandana nava abhiseka
IDX.search: aju saci nandana nava abhiseka ananda kanda nayana bhari dekha nityananda advaita mili range ga ota unamata bhakatahim sange
IDX.author: govinda dāsa
IDX.verses: āju śacī⋅nandana nava abhiṣeka ānanda⋅kanda nayana bhari dekha nityānanda advaita mili raṅge gā⋅ota unamata bhakatahiṁ saṅge hera⋅ite nirupama kāñcana⋅deha barikhaye sabahu nayana ghana⋅mehā puna puna nirakhite gora⋅mukha⋅indu uchalala prema⋅sudha⋅rasa⋅sindhu jaga bhari pūrala prema⋅taraṅge vañcita govinda⋅dāsa parasaṅge
IDX.en_translation:
Today is the new bathing ceremony of the Son of Śacī. Behold with your own eyes the very root of all bliss!

Nityānanda and Advaita together in joy sing with the ecstatic devotees.

Behold His unequaled golden body! All eyes shower tears like dense clouds.

Gazing again and again upon the moon-like face of Gaura, the ocean of the nectar of divine love overflows.

The entire world is filled with waves of divine love, but Govinda Dāsa remains deprived in this association.

IDX.first_line: akrodha paramananda nityananda raya
IDX.search: akrodha paramananda nityananda raya abhimana sunya nitai nagare bedaya adhama patita jivera dvare dvare giya harinama mahamantra dicchena vilaiya
IDX.author: śrīla locana⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
akrodha paramānanda nityānanda⋅rāya
abhimāna⋅śūnya nitāi nagare beḍāya

adhama patita jīvera dvāre dvāre giyā
harināma⋅mahāmantra dicchena vilāiyā

jā’re dekhe tā’re kahe dante tṛṇa dhari’
‘āmāre kiniyā laha, bala gaura⋅hari’

eta bali’ nityānanda bhūme gaḍi’ jāya
sonāra parvata jena dhūlāte loṭāya

hena avatāre jā’ra rati nā janmila
locana bale sei pāpī ela āra gela
IDX.en_translation:
Free from anger and full of supreme bliss is Lord Nityānanda; devoid of all ego, He wanders through the city.

Going from door to door to reach the most fallen souls, He is distributing the great mantra of the Holy Name.

To whomever He sees He says, holding a straw in His teeth: "Please purchase Me; just say Gaura⋅hari!"

Saying this, Nityānanda rolls on the ground, like a golden mountain rolling in the dust.

For one in whom attraction was not born for such an incarnation, Locana says: that sinner has simply wasted his life.

IDX.first_line: ali mhanne lage vrndavana nikoghara ghara tulasi thakura pujadarsana govindaji ko
IDX.search: ali mhanne lage vrndavana nikoghara ghara tulasi thakura pujadarsana govindaji ko nirmala nira bahata yamuna kobhojana dudha dahi ko
IDX.author: mīrābāi
IDX.verses:
ālī! mhānne lāge vṛndāvana nīko,ghara ghara tulasī, ṭhākura pūjā,darśana govindajī ko

nirmala nīra bahata yamunā ko,bhojana dūdha dahī ko

ratna siŋhāsana āpa virāje,mukuṭa dharyo tulasī ko

kuñjana kuñjana rahata rādhikā,śabda sunata muralī ko

mīrā ke prabhu giridharanāgara,bhajana vinā nara phīko
IDX.en_translation:
O friend! To me, Vṛndāvana is very pleasing. In every home there is Tulasī, and there is worship of the Deity. There is the vision of Lord Govinda.

The pure water of the Yamunā flows. The food is milk and yogurt.

Upon a jeweled throne, He is seated beautifully. He wears a crown of Tulasī.

In every grove resides Rādhikā. She hears the sound of the flute.

Mīrā’s Lord is the clever lifter of Govardhana Hill. Without worship, a person’s life is tasteless.

IDX.first_line: amanda karunya gunakara sri
IDX.search: amanda karunya gunakara sri caitanyadevasya dayavatarah sa gaura saktirbhavita punah kim padam drsorbhaktivinoda devah srimajjagannatha prabhupriyo ja ekatmako gaura kisorakena sri gaura karunyamayo bhavet kim nityam smrtau bhaktivinoda devah
IDX.author: śrīmadbhaktideśika ācārya mahārāja
IDX.verses:
amanda⋅kāruṇya⋅guṇākara⋅śrī⋅
caitanyadevasya dayāvatāraḥ
sa gaura⋅śaktirbhavitā punaḥ kiṁ
padaṁ dṛśorbhaktivinoda⋅devaḥ

śrīmajjagannātha⋅prabhupriyo ja
ekātmako gaura⋅kiśorakena
śrī⋅gaura⋅kāruṇyamayo bhavet kiṁ
nityaṁ smṛtau bhaktivinoda⋅devaḥ

śrī⋅nāma⋅cintā⋅maṇi⋅sampracārai⋅
rādarśamācāra⋅vidhau dadhau jaḥ
sa jāgarūkaḥ smṛti⋅mandire kiṁ
nityaṁ bhaved bhaktivinoda⋅devaḥ

nāmāparādhai rahitasya nāmno
māhātmyajātaṁ prakaṭaṁ vidhāya
jīve dayālurbhavitā smṛtau kiṁ
kṛtāsano bhaktivinoda⋅devaḥ

gaurasya gūḍhaprakaṭālayasya
sato‛sato harṣa⋅kunāṭyayośca
prakāśako gaura⋅jano bhavet kiṁ
smṛtyāspadaṁ bhaktivinoda⋅devaḥ

nirasya vighnāniha bhakti⋅gaṅgā⋅
pravāhanenoddhṛta⋅sarvalokaḥ
bhagīratho nityadhiyāṁ padaṁ kiṁ
bhavedasau bhaktivinoda⋅devaḥ

viśveṣu caitanya⋅kathā⋅pracārī
māhātmyaśaṁsī guru⋅vaiṣṇavānām
nāmagrahādarśa iha smṛtaḥ kiṁ
citte bhaved bhaktivinoda⋅devaḥ

prayojanaṁ sannabhidheya⋅bhakti⋅
siddhānta⋅vāṇyā samamatra gaura⋅
kiśora⋅sambandha⋅yuto bhavet kiṁ
cittaṁ gato bhaktivinoda⋅devaḥ

śikṣāmṛtaṁ sajjana⋅toṣaṇīñca
cintāmaṇiñcātra sa⋅jaiva⋅dharmam
prakāśya caitanyaprado bhavet kiṁ
citte dhṛto bhaktivinoda⋅devaḥ

āṣāḍhadarśe‛hani gaura⋅śakti⋅
gadādharābhinna⋅tanurjahau jaḥ
prapañcalīlāmiha no bhavet kiṁ
dṛśyaḥ punarbhaktivinoda⋅devaḥ
IDX.en_translation:
Will that Śrīla Bhaktivinoda Ṭhākura, the incarnation of the mercy of Śrī Caitanyadeva and the reservoir of unlimited compassion and virtues, who is the very energy of Lord Gaura, again become visible to my eyes?

Will Śrīla Bhaktivinoda Ṭhākura, who is dear to Śrīla Jagannātha dāsa Bābājī Mahārāja and one in heart with Śrīla Gaurakiśora dāsa Bābājī Mahārāja, always remain in my memory, full of the mercy of Śrī Gaura?

Will Śrīla Bhaktivinoda Ṭhākura, who established the ideal standard of conduct by widely preaching the wish-fulfilling jewel of the Holy Name, always remain awake in the temple of my memory?

Will Śrīla Bhaktivinoda Ṭhākura, who mercifully revealed to the living entities the glories of the Holy Name free from offenses, remain seated in my memory?

Will Śrīla Bhaktivinoda Ṭhākura, the associate of Gaura who revealed the Lord's hidden and manifest abodes, as well as the difference between truth and untruth, and true joy and false theater, always be the object of my remembrance?

Will Śrīla Bhaktivinoda Ṭhākura, who like King Bhagīratha removed all obstacles and delivered the whole world by bringing down the current of the Ganges of devotion, be the object of my eternal meditation?

Will Śrīla Bhaktivinoda Ṭhākura, who preached the message of Lord Caitanya throughout the world, glorified the greatness of the Guru and Vaiṣṇavas, and is the ideal of chanting the Holy Name, always remain in my heart?

Will Śrīla Bhaktivinoda Ṭhākura, whose teachings established the ultimate goal (Prayojana), the process (Abhidheya), and the conclusion of devotion, and who is intimately connected with Śrīla Gaurakiśora dāsa Bābājī, remain within my heart?

Will Śrīla Bhaktivinoda Ṭhākura, who bestowed the mercy of Lord Caitanya by publishing books such as 'Śrī Śikṣāmṛta', 'Sajjana-toṣaṇī', 'Harināma-cintāmaṇi', and 'Jaiva-dharma', be held in my heart?

On the new moon day of Āṣāḍha, he who was non-different from Śrī Gadādhara Paṇḍita, the energy of Lord Gaura, left his earthly pastimes. Will Śrīla Bhaktivinoda Ṭhākura not again become visible to us?

IDX.first_line: amara balite prabhu ara kichu naitumii amara matra pita bandhu bhai
IDX.search: amara balite prabhu ara kichu naitumii amara matra pita bandhu bhai bandhu dara suta suta tava dasi dasasei ta sambandhe sabe amara prayasa
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
‘āmāra’ balite prabhu! āra kichu nāitumii āmāra mātra pitā⋅bandhu⋅bhāi

bandhu, dārā, suta⋅sutā⋅tava dāsī⋅dāsasei ta’ sambandhe sabe āmāra prayāsa

dhana, jana, gṛha, dāra, ‘tomāra’ baliyārakṣā kari āmi mātra sevaka hoiyā

tomāra kāryera tare upārjiba dhanatomāra saṁsāra⋅vyaya kariba bahana

bhālamanda nāhi jāni sevā mātra karitomāra saṁsāre āmi viṣaya⋅praharī

tomāra icchāya mora indriya⋅cālanāśravaṇa, darśana, ghrāṇa, bhojana⋅vāsanā

nijasukha lāgi’ kichu nāhi kari ārabhakativinoda bale, tava sukha sāra
IDX.translation_intro: From The Harmonist, January 1928. Edited by Bhaktisiddhanta Sarasvati.
IDX.en_translation:
Lord, there is now nothing left that may be called mine, Thou alone art my father, friends, brother.

Friends, wife, sons, daughters are now Thy servants: My efforts for them are on Thy account.

Wealth, followers, house, way, — in as much as they belong to Thee, — I maintain, myself being a mere servant.

For Thy service I earn riches, And bear the expenses of Thy household.

I know neither evil nor good, I only serve, A watchman set to guard the properties of Thy household.

My senses I exercise according to Thy direction And desire to hear, see, smell and taste.

Nothing I do for my own pleasure, “Thy pleasure is the essence,” says Bhakti-vinode.

IDX.first_line: amara emana bhagya kata dine habe
IDX.search: amara emana bhagya kata dine habe amare apana bali janibe vaisnave sri gurucaranamrta madhvika sevane matta haye krsnaguna gaba vrndavane
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
āmāra emana bhāgya kata dine ha’be
āmāre āpana bali’ jānibe vaiṣṇave

śrī⋅gurucaraṇāmṛta⋅mādhvika⋅sevane
matta ha’ye kṛṣṇa-guṇa gā’ba vṛndāvane

karmī, jñānī, kṛṣṇadveṣī bahirmukha⋅jana
ghṛṇā kari’ akiñcane karibe varjana

karmajaḍa⋅smārtagaṇa karibe siddhānta
ācāra⋅rahita ei nitānta aśānta

vātula baliyā more paṇḍitābhimānī
tyajibe āmāra saṅga māyāvādī jñānī

kusaṅga⋅rahita dekhi’ vaiṣṇava⋅sujana
kṛpā kari’ āmāre dibena āliṅgana

sparśiyā vaiṣṇava⋅deha e durjana chāra
ānande labhibe kabe sāttvika vikāra
IDX.en_translation:
When will such good fortune be mine? When will the Vaiṣṇavas consider me their own?

By serving the honey-sweet nectar of śrī-guru's feet, Becoming absorbed, I will sing the glories of Kṛṣṇa in Vṛndāvana.

The those engaged in fruitive activities, those engaged in speculative knowledge, those envious of Kṛṣṇa, and those averse to Kṛṣṇa consciousness Will scornfully reject this one who considers himself to possess nothing.

The smārta-brāhmaṇas rigid in fruitive rituals will declare, "This person is devoid of proper conduct and utterly restless."

Considering me a madman, those proud of their scholarship, The impersonalists philosophers will abandon my company.

Seeing me devoid of bad company, the good devotees Will mercifully embrace me.

When will this wicked and insignificant wretch, by touching a body of a Vaiṣṇava, Joyfully attain spiritual transformations?

IDX.first_line: amara jivana sada pape ratanahika punyera lesaparere udvega diyachi je katadiyachi jivere klesa
IDX.search: amara jivana sada pape ratanahika punyera lesaparere udvega diyachi je katadiyachi jivere klesa nija sukha lagi pape nahi daridayahina svarthaparapara sukhe duhkhi sada mithyabhasiparaduhkha sukhakara
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
āmāra jīvana, sadā pāpe rata,nāhika puṇyera leśaparere udvega, diyāchi je kata,diyāchi jīvere kleśa

nija sukha lāgi’, pāpe nāhi ḍari,dayāhīna svārthaparapara⋅sukhe duḥkhī, sadā mithyābhāṣī,paraduḥkha sukhakara

aśeṣa kāmanā, hṛdi mājhe mora,krodhī dambhaparāyaṇamadamatta sadā, viṣaye mohita,hiṁsā⋅garva⋅vibhūṣaṇa

nidrālasya⋅hata, sukārye virata,akārye udyogī āmipratiṣṭhā lāgiyā, śāṭhya ācaraṇa,lobhahata sadā kāmī

e hena durjana, sajjana⋅varjita,aparādhī nirantaraśubhakāryaśūnya, sadānarthamanā,nānā duḥkhe jara jara

vārdhakye ekhana, upāyavihīna,tā’te dīna akiñcanabhakativinoda, prabhura caraṇe,kare duḥkha nivedana
IDX.translation_intro: From The Harmonist, October 1927. Edited by Bhaktisiddhanta Sarasvati.
IDX.en_translation:
My life Is ever given to sin, In it there is not a particle of good; Torment to others Much have I caused; I have pained all jivas.

For my own pleasure I was never afraid of sinning, Selfish and devoid of pity as I am. Dejected by happiness of others, Always untruthful, The misery of others has been my delight.

Endless are the selfish desires That fill my breast; To anger and insolence I am so prone, Ever drunk with vanity, Bewitched by worldliness, I wear malice and pride as cherished ornaments.

Ruined by sleep and idleness, Abstaining from all good works, I am ever most zealous in evil deeds. For the sake of worldly fame An adept in the practice of duplicity, A covetous victim of greed am I.

Even such a sinner, Shunned by all the good, And a constant offender, I am. Void of all good work, Always evilly disposed And poisoned with various misery.

Now in old age, Bereft of all help, Humbled and hoping naught, Bhaktivinode At the feet of the Lord Tells his grief.

IDX.first_line: amara samana hina nahi e samsareasthira hayechi padi bhava parabare
IDX.search: amara samana hina nahi e samsareasthira hayechi padi bhava parabare kuladevi jogamaya more krpa kariavarana sambaribe kabe visvodari
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
āmāra samāna hīna nāhi e saṁsāreasthira ha’yechi paḍi’ bhava⋅pārābāre

kuladevī joga-māyā more kṛpā kari’āvaraṇa sambaribe kabe viśvodarī

śunechi āgame⋅vede mahimā tomāraśrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅vimukhe bā̐dhi’ karāo saṁsāra

śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅sāmmukhya jā’ra bhāgyakrame hayatā’re mukti diyā kara’ aśoka abhaya

e dāse janani! kari’ akaitava dayāvṛndāvane deha sthāna tumi joga-māyā

tomāre laṅghiyā kothā jīve kṛṣṇa pāyakṛṣṇa rāsa prakaṭila tomāra kṛpāya

tumi kṛṣṇa⋅anucarī jagata⋅jananītumi dekhāile more kṛṣṇa⋅cintāmaṇi

niṣkapaṭa ha’ye mātā cāo mora pānevaiṣṇave viśvāsa vṛddhi ha’ka pratikṣaṇe

vaiṣṇava⋅caraṇa vinā bhava⋅pārābārabhakativinoda nāre hoibāre pāra
IDX.en_translation:
There is no one as lowly as I in this world. I have become restless, fallen into the ocean of material existence.

O Yogamāyā! When will you be merciful to me and remove this covering, O goddess who holds the universe in her womb?

I have heard Your glories in the scriptures: You bind those who are averse to Śrī Kṛṣṇa and make them wander in material existence.

But whoever by good fortune becomes favorable to Śrī Kṛṣṇa, You grant them liberation and make them fearless and free from sorrow.

O mother! Be causelessly merciful to this servant; O Yogamāyā, grant me a place in Vṛndāvana.

How can a soul attain Kṛṣṇa by bypassing You? Kṛṣṇa’s rāsa dance manifested by Your mercy.

You are Kṛṣṇa’s companion and the mother of the universe. You showed me the touchstone of Kṛṣṇa.

O mother, look upon me with a guileless heart; may my faith in the devotees increase moment by moment.

Without the feet of the devotees, Bhaktivinoda can never cross this ocean of material existence.

IDX.first_line: ami ati pamara durjana
IDX.search: ami ati pamara durjana ki karinu haya haya prakrtira dasa taya katainu amulya jivana katadina garvbhavase katainu anayase balya gela baladharmavase gramyadharme e jauvana miche dinu visarjana vrddhakala ela avasese
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
āmi ati pāmara durjana
ki karinu hāya hāya, prakṛtira dāsa tāya,
kāṭāinu amūlya jīvana

katadina garvbhāvāse, kāṭāinu anāyāse,
bālya gela bāladharmavaśe
grāmya-dharme e jauvana, miche dinu visarjana,
vṛddhakāla ela avaśeṣe

viṣaye nāhika sukha, bhoga-śakti suvai-mukha,
anta danta, śarīra aśakta
jīvana jantraṇāmaya, maraṇete sadā bhaya,
bala’ kise hoi anurakta

bhogyavastu⋅bhoga-śakti, tā’te chila anurakti,
je⋅paryanta chila dehe bala
samasta vigata ha’la, ki loiyā thāki bala,
ebe citta sadāi cañcala

sāmarthya thākite kāya, hari nā bhajinu hāya,
āsanna kālete kibā kari
dhik mora e⋅jīvane, nā sādhinu nitya-dhane,
mitra chāḍi’ bhajilāma ari
IDX.en_translation:
I am extremely fallen and wicked. What have I done? As a servant of material nature, I have wasted my invaluable life.

I spent many days with ease in the womb, and my childhood passed in childish play. This youth I uselessly wasted in mundane activities, and now old age has finally arrived.

There is no happiness in sense objects; the power to enjoy has turned away, my teeth are gone, and my body is weak. Life is full of torment, and I am always afraid of death; tell me, what should I be attached to now?

I was attached to objects of enjoyment and the power to enjoy them as long as there was strength in my body. Now all that is gone. Tell me, what do I have left? My mind is always restless.

While my body still had strength, alas, I did not worship Hari. What can I do now as death approaches? Shame on this life of mine! I did not attain the eternal wealth; leaving my true friend, I worshiped the enemy.

IDX.first_line: ami ta durjana ati sada duracara
IDX.search: ami ta durjana ati sada duracara koti koti janme mora nahika uddhara e hena dayalu keba e jagate ache emata pamare uddhariya labe kache
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
āmi ta’ durjana ati sadā durācāra
koṭi koṭi janme mora nāhika uddhāra

e hena dayālu kebā e jagate āche
emata pāmare uddhāriyā la’be kāche

śuniyāchi, śrī⋅caitanya patita⋅pāvana
ananta⋅pātakī jane karilā mocana

emata dayāra sindhu kṛpā vitariyā
kabe uddhāribe more śrī-caraṇa diyā

eibāra bujhā jā’be karuṇā tomāra
jadi e pāmara⋅jane karibe uddhāra

karma nāi, jñāna nāi, kṛṣṇa⋅bhakti nāi
tabe bala’ ki-rūpe o śrī-caraṇa pāi

bharasā āmāra mātra karuṇā tomāra
ahaitukī se karuṇā vedera vicāra

tumi ta’ pavitra⋅pada, āmi durāśaya
kemane tomāra pade pāiba āśraya

kā̐diyā kā̐diyā bale e patita chāra
‘patita-pāvana’ nāma prasiddha tomāra
IDX.en_translation:
I am indeed a very wicked person, always acting badly. In millions of births, I have no deliverance.

Who in this world is so merciful? Who will deliver such a sinner and take him near?

I have heard that Śrī Caitanya is the deliverer of the fallen. He has liberated infinite sinners.

Spreading such an ocean of compassion, when will You deliver me by bestowing Your holy feet?

This time Your mercy will be understood, if You deliver this sinful person.

I have no karma, no knowledge, no devotion to Kṛṣṇa. Then tell me, how can I obtain those holy feet?

My only hope is Your mercy. That causeless mercy is the conclusion of the Vedas.

You are the abode of purity, and I am evil-minded. How will I find shelter at Your feet?

Weeping and weeping, this fallen wretch says: Your name 'Patita-pāvana' (Deliverer of the Fallen) is famous.

IDX.first_line: ami ta svananda sukhadavasi
IDX.search: ami ta svananda sukhadavasi radhikamadhava caranadasi duhara milane ananda kari duhara viyoge duhkhete mari
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
āmi ta svānanda⋅sukhadavāsī
rādhikāmādhava⋅caraṇadāsī

du̐hāra milane ānanda kari
du̐hāra viyoge duḥkhete mari

sakhīsthalī nāhi heri nayane
dekhile śaibyāke paḍaye mane

je⋅ye pratikūla candrāra sakhī
prāṇe duḥkha pāi tāhāre dekhi’

rādhikā⋅kuñja ā̐dhāra kari’
loite cāhe se rādhāra hari

śrī⋅rādhā⋅govinda⋅milana⋅sukha
pratikūlajana nā heri mukha

rādhā⋅pratikūla jateka jana
sambhāṣaṇe kabhu nā haya mana

bhakativinoda śrī⋅rādhācaraṇe
sa̐peche parāṇa atīva jatane
IDX.translation_intro: From The Harmonist, January 1929. Edited by Bhaktisiddhanta Sarasvati.
IDX.en_translation:
I, indeed, am a denizen of the grove called the pleasaunce of the joy of my own self, A hand-maid tending the feet of Radhika and Madhaba.

I rejoice at the union of the Divine Pair, And die by grief at separation that parts them.

I never cast my glance on the place of pastime of the maidens who are their confidential associates; I am reminded of Shaibya whenever I behold the site.

My heart is smitten with grief as I see those Who are the confidantes of Chandra opposed to Radhika.

She wants to take away Hari of Radha, Causing the grove of Radhika to be overcast with the darkness of gloom!

I never see the faces of those Who are opposed to the joy of the union of Sri Radha Govinda.

Never do I find any pleasure In greeting those who are opposed to Radha.

(Bhaktivinoda, at the feet of Sri Radha, Hath surrendered his very life-breath with the utmost care.)

IDX.first_line: ami tomara duhkhera duhkhi sukhera sukhi
IDX.search: ami tomara duhkhera duhkhi sukhera sukhi tai tomare bali bhai re nitai era hate giye ore o bhai nama enechi tomara tare gaura candra marka kara e harinama rase bhara name nami padache dhara loo jadi vadanabhare
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
āmi tomāra duḥkhera duḥkhī, sukhera sukhī,
tāi tomāre bali, bhāi re
nitāi⋅era hāṭe giye (ore o bhāi)
nāma enechi tomāra tare

gaura⋅candra⋅mārkā karā,
e hari-nāma rase bharā,
nāme nāmī paḍache dharā,
loo jadi vadana-bhare

pāpa⋅tāpa saba dūre jā’be,
sāramaya saṁsāra ha’be,
āra kona bhaya nāhi ra’be,
ḍubbe sukhera pāthāre

āmi kāṅgāla arthahīna,
nāma enechi kare’ ṛṇa,
dekhe’ āmāya ati dīna,
śraddhā-mūlye deo dhare’

mūlya la’ye tomāra ṭhā̐i,
mahā-janake diba, bhāi,
je kichu tāya lābha pāi,
rākhbo nijera bhāṇḍāre

nadīyā⋅godrume thāki’,
cā̐da⋅bāula* baliche ḍāki’,
‘nāma vinā āra sakala phā̐ki,
chāyā-bājī e saṁsāre’
IDX.en_translation:
I share in your sorrow and in your happiness, that is why I tell you, O brother: having gone to Nitāi’s market, I have brought the Holy Name for you.

This Harināma is marked with Gaura⋅candra and is full of rasa. By this Name, even the renowned are captured. If you take it wholeheartedly,

All your sins and miseries will go far away, your life will become full of essence, no more fear will remain, and you will drown in an ocean of happiness.

I am poor and without wealth; I have brought this Name by incurring a debt. Seeing me very lowly, please give the price of faith.

Taking the price from you, I will give it to the Mahājana, O brother. Whatever profit I get from it, I will keep in my own treasury.

Staying in Nadīyā-Godrūma, Cānda Bāula is calling out: “Without the Holy Name, everything else is a deception; this material world is just a play of shadows.”

IDX.first_line: ana katha ana vyatha nahi jena jai tathatomara carana smrti majheavirata avikala tuya guna kala kalagai jena satera samaje
IDX.search: ana katha ana vyatha nahi jena jai tathatomara carana smrti majheavirata avikala tuya guna kala kalagai jena satera samaje anya vrata anya dana nahi karo vastu jnanaanya seva anya devapujaha ha krsna bali bali bedaba ananda karimane ara nahe jena duja
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
āna kathā āna vyathā, nāhi jena jāi tathā,tomāra caraṇa⋅smṛti⋅mājheavirata avikala, tuyā guṇa kala kala,gāi jena satera samāje

anya vrata anya dāna, nāhi karo vastu⋅jñāna,anya sevā anya devapūjāhā hā kṛṣṇa bali’ bali’, beḍāba ānanda kari’,mane āra nahe jena dujā

jīvane maraṇe gati, rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa prāṇapati,dohāra pirīti rasasukhejugala bhajaye jā̐’rā, premānande bhāse tā̐’rā,ei kathā rahu mora buke

jugala⋅caraṇasevā, ei dhana more dibā,jugalete manera pirītijugala⋅kiśora⋅rūpa, kāma⋅rati⋅guṇa⋅bhūpa,mane rahu o⋅līlā⋅pirīti

daśanete tṛṇa dhari’, hā hā kiśora⋅kiśorī,caraṇābje nivedana karivraja⋅rāja⋅suta śyāma, vṛṣabhānu⋅sutā nāma,‘śrī-rādhikā’ nāma manohārī

kanaka⋅ketakī rāi, śyāma marakata tāya,kandarpa darapa karu cūranaṭavara⋅śiromaṇi, naṭinīra śikhariṇī,du̐hu guṇe du̐hu mana jhura

śrī-mukha sundaravara, hema⋅nīlakāntidhara,bhāva⋅bhūṣaṇa karu śobhānīla pīta vāsadhara, gaurī śyāma manohara,antarera bhāve du̐he lobhā

ābharaṇa maṇimaya, prati aṅge abhinaya,tachu pāye narottama kahedivāniśi guṇa gāṅa, parama ānanda pāṅa,mane ei abhilāṣa haye
IDX.en_translation:
May I not engage in other talk or other pain, but remain in the remembrance of Your lotus feet. Constantly and without break, may I sing Your qualities in the assembly of devotees.

I will not perform other vows or other charities, nor will I seek knowledge of other things. I will not engage in other services or worship other deities. Calling out “O Kṛṣṇa!”, I will wander joyfully, and may no second thought arise in my mind.

In life and death, my only goal is Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa, the Lords of my life. I find joy in the sweet mellows of Their love. Those who worship the Divine Couple float in the bliss of divine love. May this truth always remain in my heart.

Please grant me this wealth: service to the lotus feet of the Divine Couple and love for Them in my mind. May the love for the pastimes of the youthful Divine Couple—whose qualities make Them the kings who captivate even the god of love and his consort—remain in my mind.

Holding a straw between my teeth, I cry out “O Youthful Couple!” and offer my plea at Their lotus feet. Śyāma, the son of the King of Vraja, and the daughter of Vṛṣabhānu, whose enchanting name is Śrī Rādhikā.

Rādhā is like a golden flower, and Śyāma is like an emerald beside her. They crush the pride of the god of love. Kṛṣṇa is the crown jewel of dancers, and Rādhā is the peak of female dancers. Both Their minds are captivated by each other’s qualities.

Their beautiful faces, with golden and dark-blue complexions, are adorned with emotional expressions, adding to Their splendor. Wearing blue and yellow garments, the fair and dark pair are enchanting, both alluring with inner loving sentiments.

IDX.first_line: ana katha na suniba ana katha na baliba
IDX.search: ana katha na suniba ana katha na baliba sakali kahiba paramartha prarthana kariba sada lalasa abhista katha iha vinu sakali anartha isvarera tattva jata taha na kahiba kata ananta apara keba jane vrajapura prema nitya ei se parama satya bhaja sada anuraga mane
IDX.verses:
āna kathā nā śuniba, āna kathā nā baliba,
sakali kahiba paramārtha
prārthanā kariba sadā, lālasā abhīṣṭa kathā,
ihā vinu sakali anartha

īśvarera tattva jata, tāhā nā kahiba kata,
ananta apāra kebā jāne
vrajapura prema nitya, ei se parama satya,
bhaja sadā anurāga mane

govinda gokulacandra, parama ānanda kanda,
parivāra gopa⋅gopī saṅge
nandīśvara jā̐ra dhāma, giridhārī jā̐ra nāma,
sakhī⋅saṅge bhaja tā̐re raṅge

premabhakti⋅tattva ei, tomāre kahila bhāi,
āra durvāsanā parihari’
śrī⋅guruprasāde bhāi, e saba bhajana pāi,
premabhakti sakhī anucari’

sārthaka bhajanapatha, sādhusaṅga avirata,
smaraṇe bhajana kṛṣṇakathā
premabhakti haya jadi, tabe haya manaḥ śuddhi,
tabe jāya hṛdayera vyathā

viṣaya vipatti jāna, saṁsāra svapana māna,
naratanu bhajanera mūla
anurāge bhaja sadā, premabhāve līlākathā,
āra jata hṛdayera śūla
IDX.en_translation:
I will not listen to or speak of anything else; everything I say will be about the ultimate truth. I will always pray and long for my desired spiritual goals; without this, everything else is meaningless.

How much can I speak of the Supreme Lord's truth? Who can know the endless and boundless? The love of Vraja is eternal; this is the ultimate truth. Worship with deep affection in your heart.

Govinda is the moon of Gokula and the root of supreme bliss, accompanied by His family of gopas and gopīs. Nandīśvara is His dwelling place, and Giridhārī is His name. Worship Him joyfully with the companions.

This is the truth of loving devotion, O brother! Abandon all other evil desires. By the grace of the spiritual master, we attain this worship, with loving devotion as our constant companion.

The successful path of worship is constant association with the saintly and the remembrance of topics about Kṛṣṇa. If there is loving devotion, then there is purity of mind, and the heart's distress vanishes.

Know that material enjoyment is a calamity; consider the material world a dream. The human body is the root of worship. Always worship with deep affection, chanting the pastimes with loving sentiment, and all the thorns of the heart will be removed.

IDX.first_line: anadi karama phale padi bhavarnava jale
IDX.search: anadi karama phale padi bhavarnava jale taribare na dekhi upaya e visaya halahale diba nisi hiya jvale mana kabhu sukha nahi paya asa pasa satasata klesa deya avirata pravrtti urmira tahe khela kama krodha adi chaya batapade deya bhaya avasana haila asi bela
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
anādi karama⋅phale, paḍi’ bhavārṇava⋅jale,
taribāre nā dekhi’ upāya
e viṣaya⋅halāhale, dibā⋅niśi hiyā jvale,
mana kabhu sukha nāhi pāya

āśā⋅pāśa śataśata, kleśa deya avirata,
pravṛtti⋅ūrmira tāhe khelā
kāma⋅krodha ādi chaya, bāṭapāḍe deya bhaya,
avasāna haila āsi’ belā

jñāna⋅karma⋅ṭhaga dui, more pratāriyā loi,
avaśeṣe phele sindhu⋅jale
e hena samaye bandhu, tumi kṛṣṇa kṛpāsindhu,
kṛpā kari’ tola more bale

patita kiṅkare dhari’, pāda⋅padmadhūli kari’,
deha bhaktivinode āśraya
ami tava nityadāsa, bhuliyā māyāra pāśa,
baddha ha’ye āchi dayāmaya
IDX.en_translation:
Fallen by the results of beginningless karma into the waters of the ocean of material existence, I see no way to cross. By this poison of sense gratification, my heart burns day and night, and my mind never finds happiness.

Hundreds of ropes of desire give constant distress; the waves of material tendencies play there. The six enemies beginning with lust and anger, like highway robbers, instill fear. My time has come to an end.

The two cheats, dry knowledge and fruitive action, having deceived me, finally throw me into the ocean. At such a time, O friend, You, Kṛṣṇa, are the ocean of mercy; kindly lift me up by Your strength.

Holding this fallen servant, make him the dust of Your lotus feet. Give Bhaktivinoda shelter. I am Your eternal servant, but having forgotten the noose of illusion, I remain bound, O merciful Lord.

IDX.first_line: ananda kanda nitai canda
IDX.search: ananda kanda nitai canda aruna nayana karuna chanda karuna pura saghane jhura hari hari dhvani bola re nataka ranga bhakata sanga vividha bhasa rasa taranga isata hasa madhura bhasa saghane gimadola re
IDX.author: rādhāvallabha dāsa
IDX.verses:
ānanda kanda, nitāi⋅canda,
aruṇa nayana karuṇa chanda
karuṇa⋅pūra, saghane jhura,
hari hari dhvani bola re

naṭaka⋅raṅga, bhakata⋅saṅga,
vividha⋅bhāsa rasa⋅taraṅga
īṣata⋅hāsa, madhura⋅bhāṣa,
saghane gīmadola re

patita kora, japata gaura,
dina⋅rajanī ānanda bhora
prema⋅ratana, kariyā jatana,
jaga-jane karu dāna re

kīrtana⋅mājha, rasika⋅rāja,
jaichana kanayā⋅giri virāja
vraja-vihāra, rasa⋅vithāra,
madhura madhura gāna re

dhūli⋅dhūsara, dharaṇī⋅upara,
kabahu̐ luṭhata preme garagara
kabahu̐ calata, kabahu̐ khelata,
kabahu̐ aṭṭahāsa re

kabahu̐ sveda, kabahu̐ kheda,
kabahu̐ pulaka, svara⋅vibheda
kabahu̐ lampha, kabahu̐ jhampa,
kabahu̐ dīrgha-śvāsa re

karuṇā⋅sindhu, akhila bandhu,
kaliyuga⋅tama⋅pūraṇa⋅indu
jagata⋅locana, paṭala⋅mocana,
nitāi pūrala āśa re

andha⋅adhama, dīna⋅durajana,
premadāne karala mocana
pāola jagata, kevala vañcita,
e rādhāvallabhadāsa re
IDX.en_translation:
Lord Nitāi is the root of all joy. With reddish eyes and a merciful demeanor, He constantly sheds tears while chanting the name of Hari.

In a playful mood and in the company of devotees, He manifests various waves of mellows; with a gentle smile and sweet words, He constantly sways His neck.

Embracing the fallen and chanting the name of Gaura, He is full of joy day and night. With great care He bestows the jewel of divine love upon the people of the world.

In the midst of congregational chanting He is the king of relishes, shining like a golden mountain. Expanding the mellows of His pastimes in Vraja, He sings sweet songs.

Covered in dust upon the earth, He sometimes rolls about, overwhelmed with love. Sometimes He walks, sometimes He plays, and sometimes He laughs loudly.

Sometimes He perspires, sometimes He laments, sometimes He thrills with ecstasy, and sometimes His voice falters. Sometimes He jumps and leaps, and sometimes He sighs deeply.

He is an ocean of mercy and a friend to all—the full moon dispelling the darkness of this age. Opening the coverings of the eyes of the world, Nitāi fulfills all desires.

The blind, the fallen, the poor, and the wicked—all are liberated by His gift of divine love. The whole world received it, but only this Rādhāvallabha dāsa has been deprived.

IDX.first_line: anga syamalima chatabhir abhito mandi krtendivaram
IDX.search: anga syamalima chatabhir abhito mandi krtendivaram jadyam jaguda rocisam vidadhatam pattambarasya sriya vrndaranya vilasinam hrdi lasad damabhir amodaram radha skandha nivesitojjvala bhujam dhyayema damodaram phullendivara kantim indu vadanam varhavatamsa priyam srivatsankam udara kaustubha dharam pitambaram sundaram gopinam nayanotpalarcita tanum go gopa sanghavrtam govindam kala venu vadana param divyanga bhusam bhaje
IDX.verses:
aṅga⋅śyāmalima⋅chaṭābhir abhito mandī⋅kṛtendīvaraṁ
jāḍyam⋅jāguḍa⋅rociṣām⋅vidadhatam⋅paṭṭāmbarasya śriyā
vṛndāraṇya⋅vilāsinam⋅hṛdi lasad⋅dāmābhir āmodaraṁ
rādhā⋅skandha⋅niveśitojjvala⋅bhujam⋅dhyāyema dāmodaram

phullendīvara⋅kāntim indu⋅vadanaṁ varhāvataṁsa⋅priyaṁ
śrīvatsāṅkam udāra⋅kaustubha⋅dharaṁ pītāmbaraṁ sundaram
gopīnāṁ nayanotpalārcita⋅tanuṁ go⋅gopa⋅saṅghāvṛtaṁ
govindaṁ kala⋅veṇu⋅vādana⋅paraṁ divyāṅga⋅bhūṣaṁ bhaje

kasturī⋅tilakaṁ lalāṭa⋅paṭale vakṣaḥ⋅sthale kaustubhaṁ
nāsāgre vara⋅mauktikaṁ kara⋅tale veṇuḥ kare kaṅkaṇam
sarvāṅge hari⋅candanaṁ sulalitaṁ kaṇṭhe ca muktāvalī
gopa⋅strī⋅pariveṣṭito vijayate gopāla⋅cūḍāmaṇiḥ

varhāpīḍaṁ naṭa⋅vara⋅vapuḥ karṇayoḥ karṇikāraṁ
vibhrad vāsaḥ kanaka⋅kapiśaṁ vaijayantīṁ ca mālām
randhrān veṇor adhara⋅sudhayāpūrayan gopa⋅vṛndair
vṛndāraṇyaṁ sva⋅pada⋅ramaṇaṁ prāviśad gīta⋅kīrtiḥ

śyāmaṁ hiraṇya⋅paridhiṁ vanamālya⋅varha⋅
dhātu⋅pravāla⋅naṭa⋅veṣam anavratāṁse
vinyasta⋅hastam itareṇa dhunānam avjaṁ
karṇotpalālaka⋅kapola⋅mukhābja⋅hāsam

naumīḍya te’bhra⋅vapuṣe taḍid⋅ambarāya
guñjāvataṁsa⋅paripiccha⋅lasan⋅mukhāya
vanya⋅sraje kavala⋅vetra⋅viṣāṇa⋅veṇu⋅
lakṣma⋅śriye mṛdu⋅pade paśupāṅgajāya

athāpi te deva padāmbuja⋅dvaya⋅
prasāda⋅leśānugṛhīta eva hi
jānāti tattvaṁ bhagavan⋅mahimno
na cānya eko’pi ciraṁ vicinvan

tad astu me nātha sa bhūri⋅bhāgo
bhave’tra vānyatra tu vā tiraścām
jenāham eko’pi bhavaj⋅janānāṁ
bhūtvā niṣeve tava pāda⋅pallavam

jūyaṁ nṛ⋅loke bata bhūri⋅bhāgā
lokaṁ punānā munayo’bhiyanti
jeṣāṁ gṛhān āvasatīti sākṣād
gūḍhaṁ paraṁ brahma manuṣya⋅liṅgam

śyāma⋅sundara śikhaṇḍa⋅śekhara
smera⋅hāsa muralī⋅manohara
rādhikā⋅rasika māṁ kṛpā⋅nidhe
sva⋅priyā⋅caraṇa⋅kiṅkarīṁ kuru

aho bhāgyam aho bhāgyaṁ
nanda⋅gopa⋅vrajaukasām
jan⋅mitraṁ paramānandaṁ
pūrṇaṁ brahma sanātanam

nandaḥ kim akarod brahman
śreya evaṁ mahodayam
jaśodā ca mahā⋅bhāgā
papau jasyāḥ stanaṁ hariḥ
IDX.en_translation:
We meditate on Dāmodara, whose body's dark luster completely dims the blue lotus; who by the splendor of His silken garment puts to shame the brilliance of saffron; who sports in Vṛndāvana; who is delightful with shining ropes around His belly; and whose radiant arm is placed on Rādhā's shoulder.

I worship Govinda, whose splendor is like a blooming blue lotus, whose face is like the moon, who loves a peacock feather ornament; who bears the Śrīvatsa mark, wears the large Kaustubha jewel, is dressed in yellow garments, and is beautiful. His body is worshiped by the lotus-like eyes of the gopīs, and He is surrounded by cows and cowherds. He is dedicated to playing the sweet flute and has divine bodily ornaments.

A musk tilaka on His forehead, a Kaustubha on His chest; a fine pearl on the tip of His nose, a flute in His palm, and a bracelet on His wrist. Hari-candana gracefully applied all over His body, and a pearl necklace around His neck. Surrounded by the gopīs, the crest-jewel of cowherds is victorious.

He entered Vṛndāvana, which is charming with His own footprints, His glories being sung by the cowherd boys. He wore a peacock feather on His head, His body like that of a fine dancer, with karṇikāra flowers in His ears. He wore garments of golden-tawny color and a Vaijayantī garland, filling the holes of His flute with the nectar of His lips.

I remember Kṛṣṇa, who is dark-complexioned, wearing golden garments, a forest garland, and peacock feathers. Dressed like a dancer with mineral colors and new leaves, with one hand placed on the shoulder of His friend. With the other, He twirls a lotus. His lotus-like face is smiling, with lotus-like ears, locks of hair, and cheeks.

I praise You, O adorable One, whose body is like a cloud, dressed in garments like lightning. Whose face shines with an ornament of guñjā and a peacock feather. Adorned with a forest garland, carrying a morsel of food, a stick, a horn, and a flute. Whose beauty is characterized by these emblems, with soft feet, the son of the cowherd.

Yet, O Lord, only one who is graced by a trace of the mercy of Your two lotus feet truly knows the truth of Your greatness, O Lord, and no one else, even by long deliberation alone.

Therefore, O Lord, may that great fortune be mine, in this birth, or in any other birth, even as an animal; by which I, becoming even one of Your devotees, may serve Your lotus feet.

Oh, you are indeed most fortunate among human beings in this world! The sages approach you, purifying the world; in whose homes resides directly the hidden Supreme Brahman in human form.

O Śyāmasundara! O Peacock-feathered One! O One with a smiling face! O Enchanter with the flute! O Connoisseur of Rādhikā! O Ocean of Mercy! Please make me a maidservant of the feet of Your beloved.

Oh, the great fortune, the great fortune of the residents of Nanda's Vraja! Whose friend is the Supreme Bliss, the complete Brahman, the Eternal.

What pious deeds did Nanda perform, O brāhmaṇa, to attain such exceedingly great prosperity? And the greatly fortunate Yaśodā, from whose breast Hari drank milk.

IDX.first_line: anga upanga astra parsada sangenacoi bhava murati gora range
IDX.search: anga upanga astra parsada sangenacoi bhava murati gora range gaota kaliyuga pavana namabhramoi saci suta nadiya dhama
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
aṅga⋅upāṅga⋅astra⋅pārṣada⋅saṅgenācoi bhāva⋅mūrati gorā raṅge

gāota kaliyuga⋅pāvana nāmabhramoi śacī⋅suta nadīyā dhāma

(hari) haraye namaḥ kṛṣṇa jādavāya namaḥgopāla govinda rāma śrī-madhusūdana
IDX.en_translation:
Accompanied by His associates and His weapons, Gaura⋅candra, the embodiment of divine ecstasy, dances joyfully.

Singing the Holy Name that purifies the age of Kali, the son of Śacī wanders through the sacred land of Nadiyā.

Obeisances to Hari! Obeisances to Kṛṣṇa, the descendant of Yadu! All glories to Gopāla, Govinda, Rāma, and Śrī Madhusūdana.

IDX.first_line: anupama madhuri jodi
IDX.search: anupama madhuri jodi hamare syama syama ki rasili rasabhari ankhiya hamare syama syama ki chavili hai ada baki sughara surata madhura batiya lataka gardana ki mana basiya hamare syama syama ki mukuta aura candrika mathe adhara para pana ki lali aho kaisi bhali chavi hai hamare syama syama ki
IDX.verses:
anūpama mādhurī joḍī
hamāre śyāma⋅śyāmā kī
rasīlī rasabharī aṅkhiyā̐
hamāre śyāma⋅śyāmā kī

chavīlī hai adā bā̐kī sughara
sūrata madhura batiyā̐
laṭaka gardana kī mana basiyā
hamāre śyāma⋅śyāmā kī
mukuṭa aura candrikā māthe
adhara para pāna kī lālī
aho kaisī bhalī chavi hai
hamāre śyāma⋅śyāmā kī

paraspara mila ke jaba vihare̐
śrī vṛndāvana kī kuñjana me̐
nahī̐ baranata vane śobhā
hamāre śyāma⋅śyāmā kī

nahī̐ kucha lālasā dhana kī
nahī̐ nirvāṇa kī icchā
sakhī śyāmā ko de darśana
dayā ho śyāma⋅śyāmā kī
IDX.en_translation:
The unparalleled sweetness of the pair, of our Śyāma and Śyāmā! Their eyes are full of divine nectar and delight.

Their charming and graceful demeanor is beautiful, and Their words are sweet. The sway of Their necks captivates the mind. With crowns and moon-crests upon Their foreheads, and the redness of betel nuts upon Their lips—oh, how beautiful is this sight of our Śyāma and Śyāmā!

When They roam together in the groves of Śrī Vṛndāvana, Their beauty cannot be fully described.

I have no desire for wealth, nor do I wish for liberation. O friend! Just grant us the sight of Śyāmā; may we receive the mercy of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa.

IDX.first_line: anya abhilasa chadi jnana karma parihari
IDX.search: anya abhilasa chadi jnana karma parihari kaya mane kariba bhajana sadhusange krsnaseva na pujiba devideva ei bhakti parama karana mahajanera jei patha tate habe anurata purvapara kariya vicara sadhana smarana lila ihate na kara hela kaya mane kariya susara
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
