kāya⋅mane kariba bhajana
sādhusaṅge kṛṣṇasevā, nā pūjiba devīdevā,
ei bhakti parama kāraṇa

mahājanera jei patha, tāte habe anurata,
pūrvāpara kariyā vicāra
sādhana⋅smaraṇa⋅līlā, ihāte nā kara helā,
kāya mane kariyā susāra

asatsaṅga sadā tyāga, chāḍa anya gītarāga,
karmī, jñānī parihari’ dūre
kevala bhakata⋅saṅga, prema⋅kathā⋅rasaraṅga,
līlākathā vrajarasapure

jogī, nyāsī, karmī, jñānī, anyadeva pūjaka, dhyānī,
ihaloka dūre parihari’
karma, dharma, duḥkha, śoka, jebā thāke anya joga,
chāḍi’ bhaja girivaradhārī

tīrthayātrā⋅pariśrama, kevala manera bhrama,
sarvasiddhi govindacaraṇa
dṛḍhaviśvāsa hṛde dhari’, mada⋅mātsarya parihari’,
sadā kara ananyabhajana

kṛṣṇabhaktasaṅga kari’ kṛṣṇabhakta⋅aṅga heri
śraddhānvita śravaṇa⋅kīrtana
arcana, vandana, dhyāna, navabhakti⋅mahājana,
ei bhakti parama kāraṇa

hṛṣīke govinda⋅sevā, nā pūjiba devīdevā,
ei ta’ ananyabhakti kathā
āra jata upālambha, viśeṣa sakali dambha,
dekhite lāgaye mane vyathā

dehe baise ripugaṇa, jateka indriyagaṇa,
keha kāra vādhya nāhi haya
śunile nā śune kāṇa, jānile nā jāne prāṇa,
daḍhāite nā pāre niścaya

kāma, krodha, lobha, moha, mada, mātsarya, dambhasaha,
sthāne sthāne niyukta kariba
ānanda kari’ hṛdaya ripu kari’ parājaya
anāyāse govinda bhajiba

‘kāma’ kṛṣṇa⋅karmārpaṇe, ‘krodha’ bhaktadveṣi jane,
‘lobha’ sādhu⋅saṅge hari⋅kathā
‘moha’ iṣṭalābha⋅vine, ‘mada’ kṛṣṇaguṇagāne,
niyukta kariba jathā tathā

anyathā svatantra kāma, anarthādi jāra dhāma,
bhaktipathe sadā deya bhaṅga
kibā vā karite pāre, kāma⋅krodha sādhakere,
jadi haya sādhujanāra saṅga

krodhe vā nā kare kibā, krodha⋅tyāga sadā dibā,
lobha moha ei ta’ kathana
haya ripu sadā hīna, kariba manera adhīna,
kṛṣṇa⋅candra kariyā smaraṇa

āpani palābe saba, śuniyā govinda raba,
siŋha⋅rabe jena karigaṇa
sakala vipatti jābe, mahānanda sukha pābe,
jāra haya ekānta bhajana

nā kariha asat ceṣṭā, lābha, pūjā, pratiṣṭhā,
sadā cinta’ govinda⋅caraṇa
sakala santāpa jābe, parānanda sukha pābe,
premabhakti parama kāraṇa

asatsaṅga kuṭināṭi, chāḍa anya paripāṭī,
anya deve nā kariha rati
āpana āpana sthāne, pirīta sabāi ṭāne,
bhakti⋅pathe paḍaye vigati

āpana bhajana⋅patha, tāhe haba anurata,
iṣṭadeva⋅sthāne līlā gāna
naiṣṭhika bhajana ei, tomāre kahila bhāi,
hanumāna tāhāte pramāṇa

tathāhi⋅
śrīnāthe jānakīnāthe
abhedaḥ paramātmani
tathāpi mama sarvasvaṁ
rāmaḥ kamalalocanaḥ

devaloka, pitṛloka, pāya tārā mahāsukha,
‘sādhu sādhu’ bale anukṣaṇa
jugala bhajaye jārā, premānande bhāse tārā,
tā̐dera nichani tribhuvana

pṛthak āyāsa⋅yoge, duḥkhamaya viṣayabhoge,
vraje vāsa govinda⋅sevana
kṛṣṇakathā, kṛṣṇa⋅nāma, satya satya rasadhāma,
vraja⋅jana⋅saṅge anukṣaṇa

sadā sevā⋅abhilāṣa, manete kari’ viśvāsa,
sadākāla hoiyā nirbhaya
narottama⋅dāsa bole, paḍinu asat bhole,
paritrāṇa kara mahāśaya
IDX.en_translation:
Giving up other desires, abandoning jñāna and karma, I will perform worship with body and mind. Service to Kṛṣṇa in the association of sādhus—I will not worship demigods and goddesses; this bhakti is the supreme cause.

Whatever path the Mahājanas have followed, I will be devoted to that, judging what is prior and posterior. Sādhana, smaraṇa, līlā—do not neglect this; make it the essence with body and mind.

Always reject bad association, give up other songs and attachment; keep karmīs and jñānīs far away. Only the association of devotees, the loving mellows of topics of love, the topics of pastimes in the abode of Vraja-rasa.

Yogīs, renunciants, karmīs, jñānīs, worshipers of other gods, meditators—abandoning them far away in this world. Karma, dharma, sorrow, lamentation—whatever other connection exists, leave it and worship the Holder of Govardhana.

The labor of pilgrimage is only the confusion of the mind; all perfection is the feet of Govinda. Holding firm faith in the heart, giving up pride and envy, always perform exclusive worship.

Associating with Kṛṣṇa's devotees, seeing the bodies of Kṛṣṇa's devotees, hearing and chanting with faith. Worship, prayer, meditation—the nine-fold bhakti of the Mahājanas; this bhakti is the supreme cause.

Service to Govinda with the senses—I will not worship demigods and goddesses; this indeed is the topic of exclusive devotion. And all other reproaches, specifically all are arrogance; seeing them, pain arises in the mind.

Enemies reside in the body; all the senses—no one is obedient to anyone. If it hears, the ear does not hear; if it knows, the life does not know; it cannot be made firm, certainly.

Lust, anger, greed, illusion, pride, envy, along with arrogance—I will engage them in their respective places. Making the heart joyful, defeating the enemies, I will worship Govinda easily.

'Lust' in offering work to Kṛṣṇa; 'anger' towards those who hate devotees; 'greed' for topics of Hari in the association of sādhus. 'Illusion' without the attainment of the Ishta; 'pride' in singing Kṛṣṇa's qualities—I will engage them here and there.

Otherwise, independent lust, which is the abode of anarthas, always causes a break in the path of devotion. What can lust or anger do to the practitioner if there is the association of saintly persons?

Or what does anger not do? Giving up anger day and night—this indeed is the statement regarding greed and illusion. The enemies are always weak; I will make them subordinate to the mind, remembering Kṛṣṇa⋅candra.

Everything will flee of its own accord upon hearing the sound of Govinda, like elephants at the roar of a lion. All calamities will go; one will obtain the happiness of great bliss, whose worship is exclusive.

Do not make bad endeavors for profit, adoration, or prestige; always think of Govinda's feet. All lamentation will go; one will obtain the happiness of supreme bliss; loving devotion is the supreme cause.

Bad association, fault-finding—give up other arrangements; do not have attachment to other gods. In their own respective places, love pulls everyone; a deviation occurs on the path of devotion.

My own path of worship—I will be devoted to that, singing the pastimes in the place of the Worshipable Deity. This is steadfast worship; I told you, brother. Hanumān is the proof of that.

As it is said: "Although there is no difference between the Lord of Śrī (Viṣṇu) and the Lord of Jānakī (Rāma) in the Supreme Soul, still, my all-in-all is the lotus-eyed Rāma."

The Devaloka, Pitṛloka—they attain great happiness; they say "Sādhu! Sādhu!" at every moment. Those who worship the Couple float in the bliss of love; the three worlds are an offering to them.

Through the connection of separate endeavor, in the miserable enjoyment of objects—residence in Vraja, service to Govinda. Topics of Kṛṣṇa, Name of Kṛṣṇa—truly, truly the abode of rasa—with the people of Vraja at every moment.

Always the desire for service, having faith in the mind, always being fearless. Narottama Dāsa says: I have fallen into the error of the wicked; deliver me, O Great Soul!

IDX.first_line: apara karuna sindhu vaisnava thakura
IDX.search: apara karuna sindhu vaisnava thakura mo hena pamare daya karaha pracura jati vidya dhana jana made matta jane uddhara kara he natha krpavitarane
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
apāra karuṇā⋅sindhu vaiṣṇava ṭhākura
mo hena pāmare dayā karaha pracura

jāti⋅vidyā⋅dhana⋅jana⋅made matta jane
uddhāra kara he nātha, kṛpāvitaraṇe

kanaka⋅kāminī⋅lobha, pratiṣṭhā⋅vāsanā
chāḍāiyā śodha more, e mora prārthanā

nāme ruci, jīve dayā, vaiṣṇave ullāsa
dayā kari’ deha more, ohe kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa

tomāra caraṇachāyā ekamātra āśā
jīvane maraṇe mātra āmāra bharasā
IDX.en_translation:
O Vaiṣṇava Master! You are a boundless ocean of mercy. Please bestow abundant mercy upon a wretch like me.

Please deliver those who are intoxicated by pride in their caste, education, wealth, and followers by distributing your mercy.

Greed for wealth, attraction to the opposite sex, and the desire for fame—please free me from these and purify me; this is my sincere prayer.

O servant of Lord Kṛṣṇa! Please mercifully bestow upon me a taste for the Holy Name, compassion for all living entities, and joy in the company of the devotees.

The shade of your lotus feet is my only hope. In life and in death, it is my only reliance.

IDX.first_line: aparadha phale mama citta bhela vajrasama
IDX.search: aparadha phale mama citta bhela vajrasama tuya name na labhe vikara hatasa hoiye hari tava nama ucca kari bada duhkhe daki barabara dina dayamaya karuna nidana bhavabindu dei rakhaha parana
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
aparādha⋅phale mama, citta bhela vajrasama,
tuyā nāme nā labhe vikāra
hatāśa hoiye hari, tava nāma ucca kari’,
baḍa duḥkhe ḍāki bārabāra

dīna dayāmaya karuṇā⋅nidāna
bhāvabindu dei rākhaha parāṇa

kabe tava nāma uccāraṇe mora
nayane jharaba daradara lora

gadagada⋅svara kaṇṭhe upajaba
mukhe bola ādha ādha bāhirāba

pulake bharaba śarīra hāmāra
sveda⋅kampa⋅stambha habe bārabāra

vibarṇa⋅śarīre hārāoba jñāna
nāma⋅samāśraye dharabu̐ parāṇa

milaba hāmāra kie aiche dina
roye bhaktivinoda matihīna
IDX.en_translation:
Due to the fruits of my offenses, my heart has become hard as a thunderbolt; it feels no transformation from Your Holy Name. Becoming hopeless, O Hari, I loudly utter Your Name and call out again and again in great sorrow.

O merciful abode of compassion for the fallen, please give me a drop of spiritual emotion and sustain my life.

When, by uttering Your Name, will incessant tears flow from my eyes?

When will a choked voice arise in my throat, and half-articulated words come from my mouth?

When will my body be filled with thrills of ecstasy, and when will perspiration, trembling, and paralysis occur again and again?

With a discolored body, I will lose consciousness, and by taking complete shelter of the Holy Name, I will sustain my life.

Will such a day ever come for me? Bewildered, Bhaktivinoda cries.

IDX.first_line: ara kena mayajale paditecha jiva mina
IDX.search: ara kena mayajale paditecha jiva mina nahi jana baddha haye rabe tumi ciradina ati tuccha bhoga ase bandi haye maya pase rahile vikrtabhave dandya jatha paradhina
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
āra kena māyājāle paḍitecha jīva mīna
nāhi jāna baddha ha’ye ra’be tumi cira-dina

ati tuccha bhoga⋅āśe, bandī ha’ye māyā⋅pāśe
rahile vikṛta-bhāve daṇḍya jathā parādhīna

ekhana bhakatibale, kṛṣṇapremasindhu⋅jale
krīḍā kari anāyāse thāka tumi kṛṣṇādhīna
IDX.en_translation:
O soul, like a fish, why are you still falling into the net of illusion? Do you not know that you will remain bound forever?

For the hope of very trivial enjoyment, you have become a prisoner in the ropes of illusion; you remain in a perverted state, punishable and subordinate.

Now, by the power of devotion, play easily in the waters of the ocean of love for Kṛṣṇa and remain under His shelter.

IDX.first_line: ara ki emana dasa haba
IDX.search: ara ki emana dasa haba saba chadi vrndavane jaba ara kabe sri rasamandale gadagadi diba kutuhale
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
āra ki emana daśā haba
saba chāḍi’ vṛndāvane jāba

āra kabe śrī⋅rāsamaṇḍale
gaḍāgaḍi diba kutūhale

āra kabe govardhana giri
dekhiba nayanayuga bhari

śyāma⋅kuṇḍe rādhā⋅kuṇḍe snāna
kari’ kabe juḍāba parāṇa

āra kabe jamunāra jale
majjane hoiba niramale

sādhusaṅge vṛndāvane vāsa
narottama⋅dāsa kare āśa
IDX.en_translation:
Will I ever attain such a state that I will go to Vṛndāvana, leaving everything behind?

When will I again roll on the ground in the rāsa-maṇḍala with great delight?

When will I again see Govardhana Hill, filling my eyes?

When will I soothe my life by bathing in Śyāma-kuṇḍa and Rādhā-kuṇḍa?

When will I become purified by immersing myself in the waters of the Yamunā?

Residing in Vṛndāvana in the association of saintly persons—Narottama Dāsa hopes for this.

IDX.first_line: are bhai bhaja mora gauranga carana
IDX.search: are bhai bhaja mora gauranga carana na bhajiya mainu duhkhe dubi grha visakupe dagdha kaila e paca parana tapatraya visanale aharnisi hiya jvale deha sada haya acetana ripuvasa indriya haila gorapada pasarila vimukha hoila hena dhana
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
āre bhāi! bhaja mora gaurāṅga⋅caraṇa
nā bhajiyā mainu duḥkhe, ḍubi’ gṛha⋅viṣakūpe,
dagdha kaila e pā̐ca parāṇa

tāpatraya⋅viṣānale, aharniśi hiyā jvale,
deha sadā haya acetana
ripuvaśa indriya haila, gorā-pada pāśarila,
vimukha hoila hena dhana

hena gaura dayā-maya, chāḍi’ saba lāja⋅bhaya,
kāyamane laha re śaraṇa
parama durmati chila, tāre gorā uddhārila,
tā̐’rā hoila patita-pāvana

gorā dvija⋅naṭarāje, bāndhaha hṛdaya⋅mājhe,
ki karibe saṁsāra⋅śamana
narottama⋅dāse kahe, gorā⋅sama keha nahe,
nā bhajite, deya prema-dhana
IDX.en_translation:
O brother! Worship the lotus feet of my Gaurāṅga. By not worshiping Him, I died in sorrow, sinking into the poisonous well of household life; my five life-airs were scorched.

In the poisonous fire of the threefold miseries, my heart burns day and night, and my body always becomes unconscious. My senses became controlled by enemies, I forgot the lotus feet of Gaura, and I became averse to such spiritual wealth.

Such a merciful Lord—giving up all shame and fear, take refuge in Him with body and mind. Even those who were extremely sinful were delivered by Him; He became the savior of the fallen.

Bind Lord Caitanya, the king of dancers among the twice-born, within your heart; what can the lord of death do to you then? Narottama dāsa says: there is no one like Gaura—even without proper worship, He bestows the treasure of divine love.

IDX.first_line: are bhai nitai amara dayara avadhi
IDX.search: are bhai nitai amara dayara avadhi jivere karuna kari dese dese phiri phiri prema dhana jace niravadhi advaitera sange ranga dharane na jaya anga gora preme gada tanukhani dhuliya dhuliya cale bahu tuli hari bale dunayane vahe nitai era pani
IDX.author: śrīla locana⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
āre bhāi! nitāi āmāra dayāra avadhi
jīvere karuṇā kari’, deśe deśe phiri’ phiri’,
prema⋅dhana jāce niravadhi

advaitera saṅge raṅga, dharaṇe nā jāya aṅga,
gorā⋅preme gaḍā tanukhāni
ḍhuliyā ḍhuliyā cale, bāhu tuli’ hari bale,
du’nayane vahe nitāi⋅era pāni

kapāle tilaka śobhe, kuṭila⋅kuntala⋅lole,
guñjāhāra ā̐ṭuni cūḍā tāya
keśarī jiniyā kaṭi, kaṭitaṭe nīladhaṭī,
bājana nūpura rāṅgā pāya

bhuvana-mohana veśa, majāila saba deśa,
rasāveśe aṭṭa aṭṭa hāsa
prabhu mora nityānanda, kevala ānanda⋅kanda,
guṇa gāya vṛndāvana⋅dāsa
IDX.en_translation:
O brothers! My Nitāi is the ultimate limit of mercy. Bestowing mercy upon all souls and wandering from place to place, He ceaselessly offers the wealth of divine love.

He enjoys playful pastimes with Advaita Ācārya. His body, which cannot be contained, is fashioned from Lord Gaura's divine love. He walks swaying while raising His arms and chanting the Name of Lord Hari, and tears flow from His eyes.

The mark of tilaka shines upon His forehead. Amidst His curly, playful hair, a garland of forest berries binds His topknot. His waist surpasses that of a lion, and He wears a blue dhoti. Jingling ankle bells are upon His beautiful reddish feet.

His enchanting appearance captivates all lands. In an ecstasy of spiritual emotion, He laughs loudly. My Lord Nityānanda is the very root of all joy. Thus Vṛndāvana Dāsa sings His glories.

IDX.first_line: are mora acarya thakura
IDX.search: are mora acarya thakura dayara sagara vara jagabhari vitharala radha krsna lila rasa pura gauranga cadera hena nirupama guna gana dvijaraja gauda bhuvane malla bhupati adi hari rase unamadi bhela jara karuna kirane
IDX.author: rādhāvallabha dāsa
IDX.verses: āre mora ācārya ṭhākura dayāra sāgara⋅vara, jagabhari vithārala, rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa līlā⋅rasa pūra gaurāṅga⋅cādera hena, nirupama⋅guṇa⋅gaṇa, dvijarāja gauḍa⋅bhuvane malla⋅bhūpati ādi, hari⋅rase unamādi, bhela jā’ra karuṇā⋅kiraṇe jatna kariyā ati, rasa⋅līlā⋅ngraha tathi, vṛndāvana bhūmi saṇe (sane) āni rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa rasa⋅līlā, deśe deśe pracārilā, āsvādana kariyā āpani emana dayāla pahu, cakṣu bhari nā dekhilu, hṛdaye rahala śela phuṭi e rādhāvallabha dāsa, kare mane abhilāṣa, kabe se dekhiba pada du’ṭī
IDX.en_translation:
Oh, my Advaita Ācārya! He is the best ocean of mercy, who has spread the nectar of the pastimes of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa throughout the world.

Like Lord Gaurāṅga, he has incomparable qualities and is the King of the twice-born in the land of Gauḍa. Even great kings became intoxicated with the nectar of devotion to Lord Hari by the rays of his mercy.

With great effort, he compiled the pastimes of divine love and brought the mood of Vṛndāvana to this world. He preached the pastimes of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa in every land, tasting them himself.

Such a merciful Master I have not seen to my heart's content; a spear remains pierced in my heart. This Rādhāvallabha Dāsa cherishes a desire in his mind: "When will I see his two lotus feet?"

IDX.first_line: are mora are mora gauranga gosani
IDX.search: are mora are mora gauranga gosani dine daya toma vina kare keha nai ei ta brahmanda majhe jata renu praya ke ganibe papa mora ganana na jaya
IDX.author: rādhāvallabha dāsa
IDX.verses:
āre mora āre mora gaurāṅga gosāñī
dīne dayā tomā vinā kare keha nāi

ei ta’ brahmāṇḍa⋅mājhe jata reṇu⋅prāya
ke gaṇibe pāpa mora gaṇana nā jāya

manuṣya durlabha janma nā hoibe āra
tomā nā bhajiyā kainu bhā̐ḍera ācāra

hena prabhu nā bhajinu ki gati āmāra
āpanāra mukhe dilāma jvalanta aṅgāra

kena vā āchaye prāṇa ki sukha lāgiyā
vallabhadāsiyā kena nā jāya mariyā
IDX.en_translation:
Oh my, oh my, my Lord Gaurāṅga! No one else shows mercy to the fallen like You.

In this universe, there are as many dust particles as there are sins of mine—they are countless.

This rare human birth will not come again. Without worshipping You, I have acted only like a fool.

Having not worshipped such a Lord, what will be my fate? I have effectively put burning coals into my own mouth.

Why do I still live? For what happiness? Why does Vallabha Dāsa not simply die?

IDX.first_line: aruna kamala dale sesa vichaiba
IDX.search: aruna kamala dale sesa vichaiba basaiba kisora kisori alaka avrta mukha pankaja manohara marakata syama hema gauri pranesvari kabe more habe krpadithi ajnaya aniya kabe vividha phulabara sunaba vacana duhu mithi
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
aruṇa⋅kamala⋅dale, śeṣa vichāiba,
basāiba kiśora⋅kiśorī
alakā⋅āvṛta mukha⋅ paṅkaja manohara,
marakata⋅śyāma hema⋅gaurī

prāṇeśvari! kabe more habe kṛpādiṭhi
ājñāya āniyā kabe, vividha phulabara,
śunaba vacana du̐hu miṭhi

mṛgamada⋅tilaka, sasindūra banāyaba,
lepaba candana⋅gandhe
gā̐thi’ mālatīphula, hāra pahirāoba,
dhāoyāba madhukaravṛnde

lalitā kabe more, jīvana deoyaba,
vījana māruta manda
śramajala sakala, miṭaba du̐hu⋅kalevara,
heraba parama ānande

narottama⋅dāsa⋅, āśa padapaṅkaja,
sevana mādhurīpāne
hooyaba hena dina, nā dekhiye kona cihna,
du̐hujana heraba nayāne
IDX.en_translation:
Upon red lotus petals I will spread a bed and seat the youthful Divine Couple. Their captivating lotus-faces are adorned with wavy hair—Kṛṣṇa dark as emerald, and Rādhā golden as pure gold.

O Mistress of my life! When will Your merciful glance fall upon me? When, by Your command, will I bring various excellent flowers and hear the sweet words of both of You?

I will apply musk tilaka made with vermilion and anoint You both with fragrant sandalwood paste. Stringing jasmine flowers, I will offer garlands, making swarms of bees chase after You.

When will Lalitā grant me the service of fanning You both with a gentle breeze, so that all perspiration will vanish from Your bodies and I will behold You with supreme joy?

Narottama dāsa hopes to drink the sweetness of service at Your lotus feet. Though I see no sign of such a day arriving, I long to behold both of You with my own eyes.

IDX.first_line: aruna udaya bela jata sisu haiya mela
IDX.search: aruna udaya bela jata sisu haiya mela sabe gela nandara duyara singa venu basirava karaye rakhala saba gothe aisa nandera kumara gopala tumi jabe ki na jabe aji mathe ek bola vilila amara jaibe chale dhavali sali gela gothe
IDX.author: govinda dāsa
IDX.verses: aruṇa udaya belā, jata ṣiśu haiyā melā sabe gela nandara duyāra śingā venu bā̐śīrava, karaye rākhāla saba goṭhe āisa nandera kumāra gopāla tumi jābe ki nā jābe āji māṭhe ek bola vilila, āmarā jāibe cha’le dhavalī śālī gela goṭhe jadi vā eḍiyā jāi, antarete vyathā pai cita nivārite morā nāri kibā suṇa⋅jñāna jāna, sadāi antare ṭāna ekatila nā dekhile mari suniyā śisura vāṇī, hāse deva cuḍāmaṇi mudita nayāna parakāśe govinda⋅dāsa pahu, hāsiyā hāsiyā rahu calilena vihārera rase
IDX.en_translation:
At sunrise, as many children gathered, all went to the door of Nanda Mahārāja. All the cowherd boys make the sound of horns and flutes. "O Son of Nanda! Come to the pasture!"

"O Gopāla! Will You go or not go to the field today? We are leaving now," they said in one voice. "The white and fair cows have already gone to the pasture."

"If we go away without You, we feel pain in our hearts. We are unable to control our minds. What magic do You know? There is always a pull in our hearts toward You. If we don't see You for even a moment, we die."

Hearing the children's words, the crest-jewel of the demigods smiles, revealing His bliss with half-closed eyes. Govinda Dāsa's Master remains smiling and went forth in the mood of joyful sport.

IDX.first_line: asadhu sange bhai krsna nama nahi haya
IDX.search: asadhu sange bhai krsna nama nahi haya namaksara vahiraya vate tabu nama kabhu naya kabhu namabhasa haya sada nama aparadha esaba janibe bhai krsna bhaktira badha
IDX.verses:
asādhu⋅saṅge bhāi, kṛṣṇa⋅nāma nāhi haya
nāmākṣara vāhirāya vaṭe, tabu nāma kabhu naya

kabhu nāmābhāsa haya, sadā nāma⋅aparādha
esaba jānibe bhāi kṛṣṇa⋅bhaktira bādha

jadi karibe kṛṣṇa⋅nāma, sādhu⋅saṅga kara
bhukti⋅mukti⋅siddhi⋅vāñchā dūre parihara

‘daśa aparādha’ tyaja, māna⋅apamāna
anāsaktye viṣaya bhuñja, āra laha kṛṣṇa⋅nāma

kṛṣṇa⋅bhaktira anukūla⋅saba karaha svīkāra
kṛṣṇa⋅bhaktira pratikūla saba kara parihāra

jñāna⋅yoga⋅ceṣṭā chāḍa, āra karma⋅saṅga
markaṭa⋅vairāgya tyaja, jāte deharaṅga

‘kṛṣṇa āmāya pāle, rākhe, jāna sarvakāla
ātma⋅nivedana⋅dainye ghucāo jañjāla

sādhu pāoyā kaṣṭa vaḍa jīvera jāniyā
sādhu⋅bhakta⋅rūpe kṛṣṇa āila nadīyā

gorā⋅pada āśraya karaha buddhimān
gorā vai sādhu⋅guru āche kevā āna
IDX.en_translation:
O brother! In the company of an unholy person, the Holy Name of Kṛṣṇa does not appear. The syllables of the Name may certainly be uttered, but it is never truly the Holy Name.

Sometimes there is a shadow of the Name, but there is constantly offense to the Name. Brother, know all of this to be an obstacle to devotion to Lord Kṛṣṇa.

If you wish to chant the Holy Name of Kṛṣṇa, then keep the company of the saintly. Cast far away all desires for material enjoyment, liberation, and mystic perfections.

Abandon the ten offenses and all concern for honor or dishonor. Enjoy sense objects without attachment and chant the Holy Name of Kṛṣṇa.

Accept everything that is favorable for devotion to Lord Kṛṣṇa, and reject everything that is unfavorable.

Give up the endeavor for speculative knowledge and mystic yoga, as well as attachment to fruitive work. Abandon "monkey-like" renunciation, which is merely a bodily show.

Always know that Lord Kṛṣṇa maintains and protects you. Remove all your troubles through self-surrender and humility.

Knowing that it is very difficult for the living entity to find a saintly person, Lord Kṛṣṇa came to Nadīyā in the form of a saintly devotee.

O intelligent one! Take shelter of the lotus feet of Lord Gaura. Who else is a saintly Master besides Him?

IDX.first_line: asala katha balte ki
IDX.search: asala katha balte ki tomara kenthadhara kapni ata saba phaki dharmapatni tyaji ghare paranari sanga kare arthalobhe dvare dvare phire rakhle ki baki
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
āsala kathā balte ki
tomāra kenthādharā,
kapni’ ā̐ṭā⋅saba phā̐ki

dharmapatnī tyaji’ ghare,
paranārī⋅saṅga kare,
arthalobhe dvāre dvāre
phire’, rākhle ki bākī

tumi guru balcho baṭe,
sādhuguru niṣkapaṭe,
kṛṣṇa⋅nāma dena karṇapuṭe,
se ki emana haya meki

jebā anya śikṣā deya,
tā’ke ki ‘guru’ balte haya ?
dudhera phala ta’ ghole naya,
bheve’ cinte dekha dekhi

śama⋅dama⋅titikṣā⋅bale,
uparati, śraddhā ha’le,
tabe bheka, cā̐da⋅bāula bale,
e̐caḍe peke’ ha’be ki
IDX.en_translation:
To tell the real truth, your wearing of a patched quilt and a loincloth is all a pretense.

Abandoning your lawful wife at home, associating with other women, and wandering from door to door out of greed—what else of your hypocrisy is left?

You call yourself a spiritual teacher, but a true saintly teacher without deceit gives the Holy Name in the ear; how can such a one be false?

Should one who gives other teachings even be called a guru? The result of milk is not buttermilk; just think and see.

When detachment and faith arise through the power of self-control and tolerance, only then should one adopt the external dress of a renunciant. Bāul Cānd says: will a jackfruit be any good if it is forced to ripen prematurely?

IDX.first_line: asraya kariya vando sri guru carana
IDX.search: asraya kariya vando sri guru carana jaha haite mile bhai krsna premadhana jivera nistara lagi nanda suta hari bhuvane prakasa pana guru rupa dhari
IDX.author: sanātana dāsa
IDX.verses:
āśraya kariyā vando śrī⋅guru⋅caraṇa
jāhā haite mile bhāi kṛṣṇa⋅premadhana

jīvera nistāra lāgi’ nanda⋅suta hari
bhuvane prakāśa pāna guru⋅rūpa dhari’

mahimāya ‘guru’ ‘kṛṣṇa’ eka kari’ jāna
guru⋅ājñā hṛde saba satya kari’ māna

satyajñāne guruvākye jāhāra viśvāsa
avaśya tāhāra haya vraja-bhūme vāsa

jāra prati gurudeva hana parasanna
kona vighne sei nāhi haya avasanna

kṛṣṇa ruṣṭa ha’le guru rākhi-bāre pāre
guru ruṣṭa ha’le kṛṣṇa rākhi-bāre nāre

guru⋅mātā, guru⋅pitā, guru ha’na pati
guru vinā e saṁsāre nāhi āra gati

guruke ‘manuṣya’⋅jñāna nā kara kakhana
guru⋅nindā kabhu karṇe nā kara śravaṇa

guru⋅nindukera mukha kabhu nā heribe
jathā haya guru-nindā, tathā nā jāibe

gurura vikriyā jadi dekhaha kakhana
tathāpi avajñā nāhi kara kadācana

gurupāda⋅padme rahe jāra niṣṭhā⋅bhakti
jamat tārite sei dhare mahā-śakti

hena gurupāda⋅padma karaha vandanā
jāhā haite ghuce bhāi sakala jantraṇā

gurupāda⋅padma nitya je kare vandana
śire dhari’ vandi āmi tā̐hāra caraṇa

śrī⋅guru⋅caraṇapadma hṛde kari’ āśa
śrī⋅guru⋅vandanā kare sanātana dāsa
IDX.en_translation:
Taking shelter, I worship the feet of the spiritual master, from whom, O brother, one obtains the treasure of divine love for Kṛṣṇa.

For the deliverance of the souls, Kṛṣṇa—the son of Nanda—appears in the world assuming the form of the spiritual master.

Know that in greatness, the spiritual master and Kṛṣṇa are one. Accept all of the master's commands in your heart as truth.

One who has faith in the spiritual master's instructions with true understanding will surely reside in the sacred land of Vraja.

One with whom the spiritual master is pleased is never disheartened by any obstacles.

If Kṛṣṇa becomes angry, the spiritual master can protect you. If the spiritual master becomes angry, Kṛṣṇa cannot protect you.

The spiritual master is mother, father, and protector. Without him, there is no other refuge in this material world.

Never consider the spiritual master as an ordinary man. Never listen to blasphemy against him.

Never look at the face of one who blasphemes the spiritual master. Do not go where such blasphemy takes place.

If you ever see any peculiar behavior in the spiritual master, never disrespect him.

One whose steadfast devotion remains at the lotus feet of the spiritual master possesses great power to deliver the world.

Therefore, worship those lotus feet from which all sufferings cease.

I reverently hold on my head the feet of him who constantly worships the lotus feet of his spiritual master.

Cherishing those lotus feet in his heart, Sanātana dāsa offers his prayers.

IDX.first_line: atmanivedana tuya pade karihoinu parama sukhiduhkha dure gela cinta na rahilacaudike ananda dekhi
IDX.search: atmanivedana tuya pade karihoinu parama sukhiduhkha dure gela cinta na rahilacaudike ananda dekhi asoka abhaya amrta adharatomara caranadvayatahate ekhana visrama labhiyachadinu bhavera bhaya
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
ātmanivedana, tuyā pade kari’,hoinu parama sukhīduḥkha dūre gela, cintā nā rahila,caudike ānanda dekhi

aśoka⋅abhaya, amṛta⋅ādhāra,tomāra caraṇadvayatāhāte ekhana, viśrāma labhiyā,chāḍinu bhavera bhaya

tomāra saṁsāre, kariba sevana,nahiba phalera bhāgītava sukha jāhe, kariba jatana,ha’ye pade anurāgī

tomāra sevāya, duḥkha haya jata,seo ta’ parama sukhasevā⋅sukha⋅duḥkha, parama⋅sampad,nāśaye avidyā⋅duḥkha

pūrva⋅itihāsa, bhulinu sakala,sevā⋅sukha pe’ye maneāmi ta’ tomāra, tumi ta’ āmāra,ki kāja apara dhane

bhakativinoda, ānande ḍubiyā,tomāra sevāra taresaba ceṣṭā kare, tava icchā⋅mata,thākiyā tomāra ghare
IDX.translation_intro: From The Harmonist, April 1928. Edited by Bhaktisiddhanta Sarasvati.
IDX.en_translation:
Having surrendered Myself at Thy feet I feel supremely happy. Sorrow has departed, There are no cares, I find joy on all sides.

Free from all sorrows and fears, Ever the seat of un-alloyed bliss, Are those twin feet of Thine. In them even now Resting in repose I give up all worldly fear.

Thy household I shall serve I shall not enjoy the fruit For what-so-ever pleases Thee I shall endeavour Being enamoured of Thy feet.

In Thy service The sorrows that befall Verily are the highest bliss. The sorrows and joys of Thy service Are the best of estates, They destroy the misery of ignorance.

My past history I have completely forgot In my mind experiencing the joy of Thy service. I am, indeed, Thine Thou art truly my own, What other treasure I need?

Bhaktivinode, Unto Thy service Immersed in joy, Devotes all his efforts, In pursuance of Thy will, Living in Thy house.

IDX.first_line: avatara sara gora avatarakena na bhajili tarekari nire vasa gela na piyasaapana karama phere
IDX.search: avatara sara gora avatarakena na bhajili tarekari nire vasa gela na piyasaapana karama phere kantakera taru sadai sevili manaamrta paibara aseprema kalpataru srigauranga amaratahare bhavili vise
IDX.author: śrīla locana⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
avatāra sāra, gorā⋅avatāra,kena nā bhajili tā̐rekari’ nīre vāsa, gela nā piyāsa,āpana karama phere

kaṇṭakera taru, sadāi sevili (mana),amṛta pāibāra āśeprema⋅kalpataru, śrī-gaurāṅga āmāra,tā̐hāre bhāvili viṣe

saurabhera āśe, palāśa śu̐kili (mana),nāśāte paśila kīṭaikṣudaṇḍa bhāvi’, kāṭha cuṣili (mana),kemane pāibi miṭha

hāra baliyā, galāya parili (mana),śamana⋅kiṅkara sāpaśītala baliyā, āguna pohāli (mana),pāili vajara tāpa

saṁsāra bhajili, śrī-gaurāṅga bhulili,nā śunili sādhura kathāiha⋅parakāla, du’kāla khoyāli (mana),khāili āpana māthā
IDX.en_translation:
The best of all incarnations is the incarnation of Gaura. Why did you not worship Him? Like one who lives in water but still remains thirsty, you are caught in the cycle of your own actions.

O mind, you always served a thorny tree in the hope of finding nectar. My Śrī Gaurāṅga is the wish-fulfilling tree of divine love, but you considered Him to be poison.

O mind, in the hope of fragrance you sniffed a palāśa flower, and an insect entered your nose. Thinking it to be a sugarcane stalk, you sucked on wood; how will you find sweetness in that?

O mind, thinking it to be a necklace, you put the snake of the lord of death around your neck. Thinking it would be cool, you warmed yourself by the fire and received the searing heat of a thunderbolt.

You worshiped the material world, forgot Śrī Gaurāṅga, and did not listen to the words of the saintly. O mind, you lost both this world and the next and thus ruined yourself.

IDX.first_line: avirata rati bandhu smerata bandhura srih
IDX.search: avirata rati bandhu smerata bandhura srih kavalita iva radhapanga bhangi tarangaih mudita vadana candras candrakapida dhari mudiba madhura kantir bhati kunje vihari tata susira ghananam nadam anaddha bhajam janayati taruninam mandale manditanam tata bhuvi nataraja kridaya bhanu putrya vidadhad atula carir bhati kunje vihari
IDX.author: śrīla rūpa gosvāmī
IDX.verses:
avirata⋅rati⋅bandhu⋅smeratā⋅bandhura⋅śrīḥ
kavalita iva rādhāpāṅga⋅bhaṅgī⋅taraṅgaiḥ
mudita⋅vadana⋅candraś candrakāpīḍa⋅dhārī
mudiba⋅madhura⋅kāntir bhāti kuñje vihārī

tata⋅suṣira⋅ghanānāṁ nādam ānaddha⋅bhājāṁ
janayati taruṇīnāṁ maṇḍale maṇḍitānām
taṭa⋅bhuvi naṭarāja⋅krīḍayā bhānu⋅putryā
vidadhad⋅atula⋅cārīr bhāti kuñje vihārī

śikhini kalita⋅ṣaḍje kokile pañcamāḍhye
svayam api nava⋅vaṁśyoddāmayan grāma⋅mukhyam
dhṛta⋅mṛga⋅mada⋅gandhaḥ suṣṭhu gāndhāra⋅saṁjñaṁ
tribhuvana⋅dhṛti⋅hārīr bhāti kuñje vihārī

anupama⋅kara⋅śākhopātta⋅rādhāṅgulīko
laghu laghu kusumānāṁ paryaṭan vāṭikāyām
sarabhasam anugītaś citra⋅kaṇṭhībhir uccair
vraja⋅nava⋅yuvatībhir bhāti kuñje vihārī

ahiripu⋅kṛta⋅lāsye kīcakārabdha⋅vādye
vraja⋅giri⋅taṭa⋅raṅge bhṛṅga⋅saṅgīta⋅bhāji
viracita⋅paricaryāś citra⋅tauryatrikeṇa
stimita⋅karaṇa⋅vṛttir bhāti kuñje vihārī

diśi diśi śuka⋅śārī⋅maṇḍalair gūḍha⋅līlāḥ
prakaṭam anupaṭhadbhir nirmitāś carya⋅pūraḥ
tad atirahasi vṛttaṁ preyasī⋅karṇa⋅mūle
smita⋅mukham abhijalpan bhāti kuñje vihārī

tava cikura⋅kadambaṁ stambhate prekṣya kekī
nayana⋅kamala⋅lakṣmīr vandate kṛṣṇa⋅sāraḥ
alir alam alakāntaṁ nauti paśyeti rādhāṁ
sumadhuram anuśaṁsan bhāti kuñje vihārī

madana⋅tarala⋅bālā⋅cakravālena viṣvag⋅
vividha⋅vara⋅kalānāṁ śikṣayā sevyamānaḥ
skhalita⋅cikura⋅veśe skandha⋅deśe priyāyāḥ
prathita⋅pṛthula⋅bāhur bhāti kuñje vihārī

idam anupama⋅līlā⋅hāri kuñje⋅vihārī⋅
smaraṇa⋅padam adhīte tuṣṭa⋅dhīr aṣṭakaṁ jaḥ
nija⋅guṇa⋅vṛtayā śrī⋅rādhayā rādhitas taṁ
nayati nija⋅padābjaṁ kuñja⋅sadmādhirājaḥ
IDX.en_translation:
He whose beauty is enhanced by a continuous loving smile, as if enveloped by the waves of Rādhā’s sidelong glances; whose moon-like face is joyful while wearing a peacock feather; and whose sweet complexion is like a cloud—that enjoyer shines in the forest grove.

Performing incomparable dance movements like the king of dancers on the bank of the Yamunā, He creates the sound of stringed, wind, and percussion instruments among the adorned young women in a circle, and thus He shines in the grove.

With the peacock singing the tonic note and the cuckoo the fifth note, He Himself plays the main rāga on His new flute; bearing the fragrance of musk and stealing the composure of the three worlds, He shines in the grove.

Whose incomparable hands hold Rādhā’s fingers as He lightly wanders through the flower garden, eagerly followed by the high-pitched, wonderfully voiced young women of Vraja—He shines in the grove.

Where peacocks perform a delicate dance and flutes begin to play on the stage-like banks of the hills of Vraja, filled with the music of humming bees and receiving service from wonderful musical instruments, His senses are stilled and His activities absorbed as He shines in the grove.

In every direction, flocks of parrots and mynas openly recount His hidden pastimes, creating a flood of wonder; that very secret event He speaks with a smiling face near His beloved’s ear, and thus He shines in the grove.

"Seeing your dense hair, the peacock is stunned; the blackbuck bows to the beauty of your lotus-like eyes; and the bee praises your locks, saying 'Look, Rādhā!'" Sweetly proclaiming this, He shines in the grove.

Surrounded by a circle of restless young women intoxicated by love, being served by their skill in various excellent arts—with His broad arms spread on His beloved’s shoulder, He shines in the grove.

Whoever with a contented mind studies this eight-verse prayer for remembering the incomparable, enchanting pastimes of the Lord who sports in the groves—him, who is worshiped by Śrī Rādhā, the King of the groves leads to His own lotus feet.

IDX.first_line: bada krpa kaile krsna adhamera prati
IDX.search: bada krpa kaile krsna adhamera prati ki lagi anile hetha karo ebe gati ache kichu karya tava ei anumane nahe kena anibena ei ugrasthane
IDX.author: śrīmadbhaktibedānta svāmī prabhupāda
IDX.verses:
baḍa⋅kṛpā kaile kṛṣṇa adhamera prati
ki lāgi ānile hethā karo ebe gati

āche kichu kārya tava ei anumāne
nahe kena ānibena ei ugrasthāne

rajastamo guṇe erā sabāi ācchanna
vāsudeva⋅kathā ruci nahe se prasanna

tabe jadi tava kṛpā haya ahaitukī
sakaloi sambhava haya tumi se kautukī

ki bhāve bujhāle tārā bujhe sei rasa
eta kṛpā karo prabhu kari’ nija⋅vaśa

tomāra icchāya saba haya māyā⋅vaśa
tomāra icchāya nāśa māyāra paraśa

tava icchā haya jadi tādera uddhāra
bujhibe niścayoi tabe kathā se tomāra

bhāgavatera kathā se tava avatāra
dhīra hoiyā śune jadi kāne bāra bāra

śṛṇvatāṁ svakathāḥ kṛṣṇaḥ
punyaśravaṇakīrtanaḥ
hṛdyantaḥstho hyabhadrāṇi
vidhunoti suhṛtsatām

naṣṭaprāyeṣvabhadreṣu
nityaṁ bhāgavatasevayā
bhagavatyuttamaḥśloke
bhaktirbhavati naiṣṭhikī

tadā rajastamobhāvāḥ
kāma⋅lobhādayaśca je
ceta etairanāviddhaṁ
sthitaṁ sattve prasīdati

evaṁ prasannamanaso
bhagavadbhaktiyogataḥ
bhagavattattvavijñānaṁ
muktasaṅgasya jāyate

bhidyate hṛdayagranthiś⋅
chidyante sarva⋅saṁśayāḥ
kṣīyante cāsya karmāṇi
dṛṣṭa evātmanīśvare

rajastamo ha’te tabe pāibe nistāra
hṛdayera abhadra saba ghucibe tāhāra

ki ka’re bujhāba kathā vara sei cāhi
kṣudra āmi dīna hīna kono śakti nāhi

athaca enecha prabhu kathā balibāre
je tomāra icchā prabhu karo ei bāre

akhila jagatguru! vacana se āmāra
alaṅkṛta karibāra kṣamatā tomāra

tava kṛpā ha’le mora kathā śuddha habe
śuniyā sabāra śoka⋅duḥkha je ghucibe

āniyācha jadi prabhu āmāre nācāte
nācāo nācāo prabhu nācāo se⋅mate
kāṣṭhera putula jathā nācāo se⋅mate

bhakti nāi veda nāi nāme khuba baḍa
‘bhaktivedānta’ nāma ebe sārthaka kara
IDX.en_translation:
O Lord Kṛṣṇa! You have bestowed great mercy upon this fallen soul. For what purpose have You brought me here? Now, please determine my destination.

I assume You have some business here; otherwise, why would You bring me to such a terrible place?

Everyone here is covered by the modes of passion and ignorance. They have no taste for topics concerning Lord Kṛṣṇa, nor are they happy.

But if Your causeless mercy falls upon them, everything becomes possible, for You are the supreme magician.

How will I make them understand this mellow of devotion? O Lord, show such mercy and bring them under Your control.

By Your will, everyone falls under the control of illusion, and by Your will alone, the touch of illusion is destroyed.

If it is Your will to deliver them, then surely they will understand Your message.

The message of the Śrīmad Bhāgavatam is Your literal incarnation.

If they listen to it repeatedly with patience, then Kṛṣṇa, who is the benefactor of the truthful devotee, will dwell within their hearts and cleanse them of all inauspicious things.

By regular service to the Bhāgavatam and the pure devotee, all that is troublesome to the heart is destroyed, and firm devotion to the Supreme Lord is established.

Then the effects of passion and ignorance, such as lust and greed, disappear from the heart. The mind is no longer pierced by them and becomes situated in pure goodness.

Thus established in the satisfaction of the mind through devotional service, one realizes the scientific knowledge of God, being freed from all material association.

The knot in the heart is pierced, and all doubts are cut to pieces. All fruitive actions are exhausted when one sees the Supreme Lord as the Master.

Then they will find relief from passion and ignorance, and all inauspicious things in their hearts will be removed.

How will I make them understand this message? I only desire that benediction. I am small, destitute, and fallen; I have no power of my own.

Yet You have brought me here to speak Your message. Whatever is Your will, O Lord, please do that now.

O Master of the universe! These words of mine—You have the power to decorate them. If Your mercy is there, my words will be purified. Hearing them, everyone's grief and misery will disappear.

Since You have brought me here to dance, O Lord, then make me dance!

Make me dance as You like, just like a wooden doll in Your hands.

I have no devotion and no knowledge, but my name is very grand. Now, please make the name 'Bhaktivedānta' successful.

IDX.first_line: bada sukhera khavara gaisurabhi kunjete namera hata khuleche khoda nitai
IDX.search: bada sukhera khavara gaisurabhi kunjete namera hata khuleche khoda nitai bada majara katha tayasraddhamulye suddhanama sei hatete vikaya
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
baḍa sukhera khavara gāisurabhi⋅kuñjete nāmera hāṭa khuleche khoda⋅nitāi

baḍa majāra kathā tāyaśraddhāmūlye śuddhanāma sei hāṭete vikāya

jata bhaktavṛnda vasi’adhikārī dekhe’ nāma becche dara kaṣi

jadi nāma kinbe bhāiāmāra saṅge cala mahā-janera kāche jāi

tumi kinbe kṛṣṇa⋅nāmadasturi loiba āmi, pūrṇa ha’be kāma

baḍa dayāla nityānandaśraddhā-mātra la’ye dena parama ānanda

ekabāra dekhle cakṣe jalagaura ba’le nitāi dena sakala sambala

dena śuddha kṛṣṇa⋅śikṣājāti, dhana, vidyā⋅bala nā kare apekṣā

amani chāḍe māyājālagṛhe thāka, vane thāka, nā thāke jañjāla

āra nāiko kalira bhayaācaṇḍāle dena nāma nitāi dayā-maya

bhakativinoda ḍāki’ kayanitāicā̐dera caraṇa vinā āra nāhi āśraya
IDX.en_translation:
I sing very happy news: in the fragrant Surabhi grove, Lord Nitāi Himself has opened a marketplace of the Holy Name.

There is a very interesting thing there: for the price of faith, the pure Name is sold in that market.

All the devotees sit there and, seeing who is qualified, they sell the Name after settling the price.

If you want to buy the Name, dear brother, come with me—let’s go to those great souls.

You will buy the name of Kṛṣṇa; I will take a small commission, and your desire will be fulfilled.

The very merciful Nityānanda, taking only faith in return, gives supreme bliss.

If He once sees tears in your eyes as you call out “Gaura!”, Nitāi gives you all his resources.

He gives the pure teachings of Kṛṣṇa and does not wait for caste, wealth, or the strength of knowledge.

Immediately the net of illusion is released. Whether you stay at home or in the forest, you will be free from trouble.

There is no more fear of the Kali age. To everyone, down to the most fallen outcast, the merciful Nitāi gives the Holy Name.

Bhaktivinoda calls out and says: “Without the feet of Lord Nitāi, there is no other refuge.”

IDX.first_line: badhila maya jedina hate
IDX.search: badhila maya jedina hate avidya moha dore aneka janma labhinu ami phirinu maya ghore deva danava manava pasu patanga kita haye svarge narake bhutale phiri anitya asa laye
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
“bā̐dhila māyā, jedina ha’te,
avidyā⋅moha⋅ḍore
aneka janma, labhinu āmi,
phirinu māyā⋅ghore

deva⋅dānava, mānava⋅paśu,
pataṅga kīṭa ha’ye
svarge⋅narake, bhūtale phiri,
anitya āśā la’ye

nā jāni kibā, sukṛti⋅bale,
śrīvāsa⋅suta hainu
nadīyā⋅dhāme, caraṇa tava,
daraśa paraśa kainu

sakala bāre, maraṇa⋅kāle,
aneka duḥkha pāi
tuyā prasaṅge, parama sukhe,
ebāra cale’ jāi

icchāya tora, janama jadi,
ābāra haya, hari
caraṇe tava, prema⋅bhakati,
thāke, minati kari”

jakhana śiśu, nīraba bhela,
dekhiyā prabhura līlā
śrīvāsa⋅goṣṭhī, tyajiyā śoka,
ānanda⋅magana bhelā

gaura⋅carita, amṛta⋅dhārā,
karite karite pāna
bhaktivinoda, śrīvāse māge,
jāya jena mora prāṇa
IDX.en_translation:
Since the day illusion bound me with the ropes of ignorance and delusion, I have taken many births, wandering in the whirlpool of material existence.

As a demigod, demon, human, animal, insect, or worm, I have wandered in heaven, hell, and on earth, clinging to impermanent hopes.

I do not know by what pious credits I became the son of Śrīvāsa. In Nadiyā, I saw and touched Your lotus feet.

Every time, at the moment of death, I experienced much suffering. Now, in Your company, with supreme happiness, I depart.

By Your desire, if I take birth again, O Hari, I pray that loving devotion may remain at Your feet.

When the child became silent, seeing the pastimes of the Lord, Śrīvāsa's family, abandoning grief, became absorbed in bliss.

By continuously drinking the nectarean current of Lord Caitanya's life and pastimes, Bhaktivinoda begs Śrīvāsa that his life may pass in this way.

IDX.first_line: badhu he nayane lukaye thoba
IDX.search: badhu he nayane lukaye thoba prema cintamani rasete gathiya hrdaye tuliya laba sisukala haite ana nahi cite o pada karechi sara dhana jana mana jivana jauvana tumi se galara hara
IDX.author: śrī⋅caṇḍīdāsa
IDX.verses:
ba̐dhu he nayane lukāye thoba
prema cintāmaṇi, rasete gā̐thiyā,
hṛdaye tuliyā la’ba

śiśukāla haite, āna nāhi cite,
o pada karechi sāra
dhana jana mana, jīvana jauvana,
tumi se galāra hāra

śayane svapane, nidrā jāgaraṇe,
kabhu nā pāśari tomā
abalāra truṭi, haya śatakoṭi,
sakali karibā kṣamā

nā ṭhelio bale, abalā akhale,
je haya ucita tora
bhāviyā dekhilāma, tomā ba̐dhū vine,
āra keha nāhi mora

tile ā̐khi āḍa, karite nā pāri,
tā’hale je mari āmi
caṇḍīdāse kahe, anugata jane,
dayā nā chāḍio tumi
IDX.en_translation:
O Friend! I will keep the touchstone of love hidden in my eyes. Stringing it with mellows, I will lift it into my heart.

From childhood, I knew no other. I have made Your feet the essence. Wealth, people, mind, life, and youth—You are the necklace around my neck.

In sleeping or dreaming, in sleep or wakefulness, I never forget You. A helpless woman has millions of faults; You should forgive them all.

Do not push me away by force; I am a helpless woman, and You are everything to me. I have considered and seen that without You, O Friend, I have no one else.

I cannot turn my eyes from You for even a moment; if I do, I shall die. Chandidasa says: Do not give up Your mercy towards Your surrendered servant.

IDX.first_line: bahujanma bhagyavase cinmaya madhura rase
IDX.search: bahujanma bhagyavase cinmaya madhura rase sprha janme jivera hiyaya sei sprha lobha hana vrajadhame jive lana rupanuga bhajane mataya bhajana prakara jata sakalera sara mata sikhaila sri rupa gosani se bhajana na janiya krsna bhajibare giya tuccha kaje jivana katai
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
bahujanma⋅bhāgyavaśe, cinmaya madhura rase,
spṛhā janme jīvera hiyāya
sei spṛhā lobha hañā, vraja-dhāme jīve lañā,
rūpānuga⋅bhajane mātāya

bhajana⋅prakāra jata, sakalera sāra mata,
śikhāila śrī⋅rūpa gosāñi
se bhajana nā jāniyā, kṛṣṇa bhajibāre giyā,
tuccha kāje jīvana kāṭāi

bujhibāre se bhajana, bahu jatne akiñcana,
viracila bhajana⋅darpaṇa
vraje rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa⋅sevā, karite utsuka jebā,
sukhe te̐ha karuna śravaṇa

lobhete janama pāi, atiśīghra bāḍi jāi,
śraddhā, rati, tabe haya prīti
sahaja bhajana rati, nāhi cāya śikṣā mati,
tabu śikṣā prāthamika rīti

putra-sneha jananīra, sahaja hṛdaye sthira,
dūṣita hṛdaye śikṣā cāi
kṛṣṇaprema seirūpa, nityasiddha aparūpa,
baddha-jīve aprakaṭa bhāi

sei ta’ sahaja rati, pāiyāche apagati,
śikṣānuśīlana jadi pāya
se rati jāgiyā uṭhe, jīvera bandhana chuṭe,
vrajānanda tāhāre nācāya
IDX.en_translation:
Due to the good fortune accumulated over many births, a desire awakens in the heart of the soul for the spiritual, sweet taste of devotion. That desire, turning into intense greed, takes the soul to Vraja-dhāma and intoxicates one in devotional service following Śrī Rūpa Gosāñi.

All types of devotional service, their essence, were taught by Śrī Rūpa Gosāñi. Not knowing that service, when I go to worship Kṛṣṇa, I spend my life in insignificant activities.

To understand that worship, with great effort, the humble one composed Bhajana-darpaṇa. Whoever is eager to perform the service of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa in Vraja, may they happily listen.

Born from intense greed, I quickly grow. First comes faith, then attachment, and finally pure love develops. Natural attachment to devotional service does not desire education or intellect; still, education is the primary method.

A mother's affection for her son is naturally fixed in her heart. In a polluted heart, education is needed. Love for Kṛṣṇa is like that - eternally perfect, unparalleled. In the conditioned soul, it is unmanifest, brother.

That natural attachment has attained a degraded state. If it receives education and practice, that attachment awakens, the soul's bondage breaks, and the bliss of Vraja makes one dance.

IDX.first_line: balabhid upala kanti drohini srimad ange
IDX.search: balabhid upala kanti drohini srimad ange ghusrna rasa vilasaih susthu gandharvikayah sva madana nrpa sobham vardhayan deha rajye pranayatu mama netrabhista purtim mukundah udita vidhu parardha jyotir ullanghi vaktro nava tarunima rajyad balya sesatiramyah parisadi lalitalim dolayan kundalabhyam pranayatu mama netrabhista purtim mukundah
IDX.author: śrīla rūpa gosvāmī
IDX.verses:
balabhid⋅upala⋅kānti⋅drohiṇi śrīmad⋅aṅge
ghusṛṇa⋅rasa⋅vilāsaiḥ suṣṭhu gāndharvikāyāḥ
sva⋅madana⋅nṛpa⋅śobhāṁ vardhayan deha⋅rājye
praṇayatu mama netrābhīṣṭa⋅pūrtiṁ mukundaḥ

udita⋅vidhu⋅parārdha⋅jyotir ullaṅghi⋅vaktro
nava⋅taruṇima⋅rajyad⋅bālya⋅śeṣātiramyaḥ
pariṣadi lalitālīṁ dolayan kuṇḍalābhyāṁ
praṇayatu mama netrābhīṣṭa⋅pūrtiṁ mukundaḥ

kanaka⋅nivaha⋅śobhānandi pītaṁ nitambe
tad⋅upari navaraktaṁ vastram itthaṁ dadhānaḥ
priyam iva kila varṇaṁ rāga⋅yuktaṁ priyāyāḥ
praṇayatu mama netrābhīṣṭa⋅pūrtiṁ mukundaḥ

surabhi⋅kusuma⋅vṛndair vāsitāmbhaḥ⋅samṛddhe
priya⋅sarasi nidāghe sāyam ālī⋅parītām
madana⋅janaka⋅sekaiḥ khelayann eva rādhāṁ
praṇayatu mama netrābhīṣṭa⋅pūrtiṁ mukundaḥ

parimalam iha labdhvā hanta gāndharvikāyāḥ
pulakita⋅tanur uccair unmadas tat⋅kṣaṇena
nikhila⋅vipina⋅deśān vāsitān eva jighran
praṇayatu mama netrābhīṣṭa⋅pūrtiṁ mukundaḥ

praṇihita⋅bhuja⋅daṇḍaḥ skandha⋅deśe varāṅgyāḥ
smita⋅vikasita⋅gaṇḍe kīrtidā⋅kanyakāyāḥ
manasija⋅jani⋅saukhyaṁ cumbanenaiva tanvan
praṇayatu mama netrābhīṣṭa⋅pūrtiṁ mukundaḥ

pramada⋅danuja⋅goṣṭhyāḥ ko’pi saṁvarta⋅vahnir
vraja⋅bhuvi kila pitror mūrtimān sneha⋅puñjaḥ
prathama⋅rasa⋅mahendraḥ śyāmalo rādhikāyāḥ
praṇayatu mama netrābhīṣṭa⋅pūrtiṁ mukundaḥ

svakadana⋅kathayāṅgīkṛtya mṛdvīṁ viśākhāṁ
kṛta⋅caṭu lalitāṁ tu prārthayan prauḍha⋅śīlām
praṇaya⋅vidhura⋅rādhā⋅māna⋅vidhvaṁsanāya
praṇayatu mama netrābhīṣṭa⋅pūrtiṁ mukundaḥ

paripaṭhati mukundasyāṣṭakaṁ kākubhir jaḥ
sakala⋅viṣaya⋅saṅgāt saṁniyamyendriyāṇi
vraja⋅nava⋅yuva⋅rājo darśayan svaṁ sa⋅rādhe
svajana⋅gaṇana⋅madhye taṁ priyāyās tanoti
IDX.en_translation:
May Kṛṣṇa fulfill the desires of my eyes! His glorious body defeats the luster of sapphire and is beautifully adorned with the saffron decorations of Rādhā, thus increasing the splendor of the god of love within the kingdom of His body.

May Kṛṣṇa fulfill the desires of my eyes! His face surpasses the brilliance of countless rising moons, and He is extremely charming with the last traces of boyhood beautified by fresh youth. In the assembly, He sways Lalitā and her companions with His earrings.

May Kṛṣṇa fulfill the desires of my eyes! He wears a yellow garment on His hips, delighting in the splendor of gold, and above that He wears a fresh red cloth as if adopting the color of His beloved's affection.

May Kṛṣṇa fulfill the desires of my eyes! In His beloved lake—rich with fragrant flower clusters and scented water—He causes Rādhā to play by sprinkling water during the summer evenings while surrounded by His companions.

May Kṛṣṇa fulfill the desires of my eyes! Having obtained the fragrance of Rādhā, His body bristles with joy; at that moment, He becomes highly ecstatic and smells all the scented regions of the forest.

May Kṛṣṇa fulfill the desires of my eyes! With His arm placed on the shoulder of the daughter of Kīrtidā—whose cheeks blossom with a smile—He generates the joy of love with a kiss.

May Kṛṣṇa fulfill the desires of my eyes! He is a fire of universal destruction for the proud assembly of demons, yet in the land of Vraja He is a personified mass of affection for His parents—the great king of transcendental mellows, dark-complexioned, and Rādhā's own.

May Kṛṣṇa fulfill the desires of my eyes! Accepting gentle Viśākhā through His own stories and charmingly entreating dignified Lalitā, He acts to soothe Rādhā's love-sick pride.

Whoever recites these eight verses of Mukunda with humble prayers, while restraining all senses from material attachments, will be established among His own dearest associates by the young prince of Vraja, who shows Himself along with Rādhā.

IDX.first_line: bandhugana sunaha vacana mora
IDX.search: bandhugana sunaha vacana mora bhavete vibhora thakiye jakhana dekha deya cita cora vicaksana kari dekhite cahile haya akhi agocara punah nahi dekhi kadaye parana duhkhera nahi thake ora
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
bandhugaṇa! śunaha vacana mora
bhāvete vibhora, thākiye jakhana,
dekhā deya cita⋅cora

vicakṣaṇa kari’, dekhite cāhile,
haya ā̐khi agocara
punaḥ nāhi dekhi’, kā̐daye parāṇa,
duḥkhera nāhi thāke ora

jagatera bandhu sei
kabhu more laya sātha
jathā tathā rākhu more,
āmāra se prāṇa-nātha

darśana⋅ānanda⋅dāne, sukha deya mora prāṇe,
bale more praṇaya⋅vacana
punaḥ adarśana diyā, dagdha kare mora hiyā,
prāṇe more māre prāṇadhana

jāhe tāra sukha haya,
sei sukha mama
nija sukhe duḥkhe mora
sarvadāi sama

bhakativinoda, saṁyoge⋅viyoge,
tāhe jāne prāṇeśvara
tā̐’ra sukhe sukhī, sei prāṇa-nātha,
se kabhu nā haya para
IDX.en_translation:
Friends, listen to my words! When I am immersed in spiritual emotion, the thief of my heart appears.

When I try to see Him carefully, He becomes invisible to my eyes. Not seeing Him again, my heart cries; there is no end to my sorrow.

He is the friend of the world. Sometimes He takes me along with Him. Wherever He may keep me, He is the Lord of my life.

By granting the joy of His sight, He gives happiness to my soul and speaks words of love to me. Again, by disappearing, He burns my heart; the treasure of my life thus kills me.

Whatever gives Him happiness, that happiness is mine. In my own happiness and sorrow, I remain always the same.

Bhaktivinoda knows Him as the Lord of his life in both union and separation. Happy in His happiness, that Lord is never a stranger.

IDX.first_line: bandhusange jadi tava ranga parihasa thake abhilasathake abhilasatabe mora katha rakha jeyo nako jeyo nakomathuraya kesitirtha ghatera sakasa
IDX.search: bandhusange jadi tava ranga parihasa thake abhilasathake abhilasatabe mora katha rakha jeyo nako jeyo nakomathuraya kesitirtha ghatera sakasa govindavigraha dhari tathaya achena harinayane bankima drsti mukhe mandahasakiba tribhangama thama varna samujjvala syamanavakisalaya sobha sriange prakasa
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
bandhusaṅge jadi tava raṅga parihāsa, thāke abhilāṣa(thāke abhilāṣa)tabe mora kathā rākha, jeyo nāko jeyo nāko,mathurāya keśītīrtha⋅ghāṭera sakāśa

govindavigraha dhari’, tathāya āchena hari,nayane baṅkima⋅dṛṣṭi, mukhe mandahāsakibā tribhaṅgama ṭhāma, varṇa samujjvala śyāma,navakiśalaya śobhā śrīaṅge prakāśa

adhare vaṁśīṭī tā’ra, anarthera mūlādhāra,śikhicūḍākeo bhāi karo nā viśvāsase mūrti nayane here, keha nāhi ghare phire,saṁsārī gṛhīra je go haya sarvanāśa

(tāi mora mane baḍa trāsa)ghaṭibe vipada bhārī, jeyo nāko he saṁsāri,mathurāya keśītīrtha ghāṭera sakāśa
IDX.en_translation:
If you desire playful jokes and fun with your friends, Then heed my words, do not go, do not go, Near Keśītīrtha-ghāṭa in Mathurā.

Assuming the form of Govinda, Hari is there, With a captivating glance in His eyes and a gentle smile on His face. What a beautiful three-fold bending posture, His complexion brilliantly dark, The beauty of new sprouts manifests on His divine limbs.

The flute on His lips is the root cause of all unwanted things, Brother, do not even trust His peacock feather crown. Seeing that form with one’s eyes, no one returns home, Indeed, it becomes complete ruin for the worldly householder.

(Therefore, there is great fear in my heart) A great danger will occur, do not go, O worldly person, Near Keśītīrtha-ghāṭa in Mathurā.
