IDX.first_line: baso mere nayanana me nandalalamohani murati syamari suratinayana vane visala
IDX.search: baso mere nayanana me nandalalamohani murati syamari suratinayana vane visala adhara sudharasa murali bajataura vaijantimalaksudra ghantika katitata sobhitanupura sabda rasala
IDX.author: mīrābāi
IDX.verses:
baso mere nayanana me̐ nandalālamohanī mūrati, śyāmarī sūrati,nayanā vane viśāla

adhara sudhārasa, muralī bājata,ura vaijantīmālakṣudra ghanṭikā, kaṭitaṭa śobhita,nūpura śabda rasāla

mīrā prabhu santana sukha-dāyī,bhakata⋅vatsala gopāla
IDX.en_translation:
O Son of Nanda! Please reside in my eyes. With Your enchanting form, dark complexion, and wide, beautiful eyes.

With nectar upon Your lips, You play the flute, and a beautiful garland of forest flowers hangs upon Your chest. Small bells adorn Your waist, and the sweet sound of Your anklets is delightful.

Lord of Mīrābāī, the Giver of happiness to the saintly! O Gopāla, You are affectionate toward Your devotees.

IDX.first_line: baula baula balcha sabe
IDX.search: baula baula balcha sabe hacche baula kon jana dadi cuda dekhiye o bhai karche jivake vancana dehatattva jadera tattva tate ki chadaya mayara garta cidananda paramartha jante ta taya parbe na
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
‘bāula bāula’ balcha sabe,
hacche bāula kon janā
dāḍi⋅cūḍā dekhiye (o bhāi)
karche jīvake vañcanā

dehatattva⋅jaḍera tattva,
tā’te ki chāḍāya māyāra garta ?
cidānanda paramārtha,
jānte ta’ tāya pārbe nā

jadi bāula cāore ha’te,
tabe cala dharmapathe,
joṣitsaṅga sarvamate
chāḍa re manera vāsanā

veśabhūṣā⋅raṅga jata,
chāḍi’ nāme hoo re rata,
nitāicā̐dera anugata,
hoo chāḍi’ saba durvāsanā

mukhe ‘hare kṛṣṇa’ bala,
chāḍa re bhāi kathāra chala,
nāma vinā ta’ susambala,
cā̐da⋅bāula āra dekhe nā
IDX.en_translation:
Everyone says 'Bāul, Bāul', but who truly is a Bāul? Showing their beards and topknots, O brother, they are deceiving the jīvas.

Philosophy of the body is the philosophy of inert matter; can it free one from the pit of illusion? Consciousness and bliss are the supreme goal, but they will not be able to know it.

If you wish to be a Bāul, then walk on the path of righteousness, renounce the association of women completely, and abandon the desires of the mind.

Give up all external dress and show, be devoted to the Holy Name, become a follower of Nitāicāṅda, abandoning all evil desires.

Chant 'Hare Kṛṣṇa' verbally, give up the deception of words, for without the Holy Name, there is no good support; Cāṅda Bāul sees no other.

IDX.first_line: beda helani ama prabhu tyaji ratnasana
IDX.search: beda helani ama prabhu tyaji ratnasana sri nidacada sikhari hoi patha cahi basilani kanja badani sri radha parana jugaye badha nayana lotaka sada gadouchanti jasoda
IDX.verses:
beḍa helāni āma, prabhu tyaji ratnāsana
śrī⋅nīḍācaḍa śikhari hoi, patha cāhi basilāni

kañja badanī śrī⋅rādhā, parāṇa jugāye bādhā
nayana⋅lotaka sadā gaḍouchanti jaśodā

dvāparara avasāna, helānī jagamohana
baḍa deuḍe taḍe, ghaṇṭā ārati helāni

āsa āsa nārāyaṇa, diyabāre daraśana
kalira kaḍāra rūpa netra paṭe niśi lāni
IDX.en_translation:
The time has come, O Lord, for You to leave Your jeweled throne. At the summit of Nīlācala, You are seated, waiting on the path.

The lotus-faced Śrīmati Rādhikā experiences the pain of separation in Her heart. Mother Yaśodā's eyes are always flowing with tears.

The end of the Dvāpara-yuga has come, and the Jagamohana hall is established. In the great temple, the ārati is being performed with the sound of bells.

Come, come O Lord Nārāyaṇa, to give us Your divine sight. The dark shadow of the Age of Kali is falling upon our eyes.

IDX.first_line: bhaire bhaja goracadera carana
IDX.search: bhaire bhaja goracadera carana e tina bhuvane ara dayara thakura nai gora bada patitapavana hena avatare jara nahila bhakati lesa bala tara ki habe upaya ravira kirane jara akhi parasanna naila vidhata vancita kaila taya
IDX.author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
bhāire! bhaja gorācā̐dera caraṇa
e tina bhuvane āra, dayāra ṭhākura nāi,
gorā baḍa patita-pāvana

hena avatāre jā’ra, nahila bhakati leśa,
bala tā’ra ki habe upāya
ravira kiraṇe jā’ra, ā̐khi parasanna naila,
vidhātā vañcita kaila tāya

hena⋅jalada kāya, prema-dhārā variṣaya,
karuṇāmaya avatāra
gorā hena prabhu peye, je⋅jana śītala naila,
ki jāni kemana mana tā’ra

kali⋅bhava⋅sāgare, nija⋅nāma bhelā kari’,
āpane gaurāṅga kare pāra
tabe je ḍubiyā mare, ke tā’re uddhāra kare,
e premānandera parihāra
IDX.en_translation:
O brothers! Worship the lotus feet of Lord Gorācānd. In these three worlds, there is no other Master as merciful as Him. Lord Gaura is truly the deliverer of the most fallen.

If one has not even a trace of devotion during such an incarnation, tell me, what will be his destination? One whose eyes do not open to the pleasing rays of the sun has surely been deprived by destiny.

With a body like a monsoon cloud, He showers divine love—He is the most merciful incarnation. Having received such a Master as Lord Gaura, if a person's heart does not become pacified, I wonder what kind of mind he possesses.

In the ocean of material existence in this Age of Kali, Lord Gaurāṅga Himself makes His own Holy Name a raft to help souls cross over. Then, if someone still drowns and dies, who can deliver them? This is the conclusion of Premānanda.

IDX.first_line: bhaireekadina santipure prabhu advaitera gharedui prabhu bhojane basilasaka kari asvadana prabhu bale bhaktaganaei saka krsna asvadila
IDX.search: bhaireekadina santipure prabhu advaitera gharedui prabhu bhojane basilasaka kari asvadana prabhu bale bhaktaganaei saka krsna asvadila hena saka asvadane krsnaprema aise manesei preme kara asvadanajadabuddhi parihari prasada bhojana karihari hari bala sarvajana
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
bhāire!ekadina śāntipure, prabhu advaitera ghare,dui prabhu bhojane basilaśāka kari’ āsvādana, prabhu bale,⋅“bhaktagaṇa,ei śāka kṛṣṇa āsvādila

hena śāka āsvādane, kṛṣṇaprema āise mane,sei preme kara āsvādanajaḍabuddhi parihari, prasāda bhojana kari’,hari hari bala sarvajana”
IDX.en_translation:
O brother! One day in Śāntipur, in the house of Prabhu Advaita, The two Lords sat down to eat. Tasting the greens, Prabhu said, “O devotees, Kṛṣṇa has tasted these greens.

Tasting such greens, love for Kṛṣṇa comes to mind. Eat with that love. Giving up material intelligence, eating sanctified food, Everyone chant Hari Hari!

IDX.first_line: bhaireramakrsna gocarane jaivena dura vaneeta cinti jasoda rohiniksira sara chana nani dujane khaoyana anivatsalye ananda mane gani
IDX.search: bhaireramakrsna gocarane jaivena dura vaneeta cinti jasoda rohiniksira sara chana nani dujane khaoyana anivatsalye ananda mane gani vayasya rakhalagane khaya rama krsna sanenace gaya ananda antarekrsnera prasada khaya udara bhariya jayaara deo ara deo kare
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
bhāire!rāmakṛṣṇa gocāraṇe, jāivena dūra vane,eta cinti’ jaśodā⋅rohiṇīkṣīra, sara, chānā, nanī, du’jane khāoyāna āni’,vātsalye ānanda mane gaṇi’

vayasya rākhālagaṇe, khāya rāma⋅kṛṣṇa sane,nāce gāya ānanda antarekṛṣṇera prasāda khāya, udara bhariyā jāya,‘āra deo’ ‘āra deo’ kare
IDX.en_translation:
O brother! Thinking that Rāma and Kṛṣṇa will go to the distant forest to graze the cows, Yaśodā and Rohiṇī bring condensed milk, cream, cheese, and butter and feed them both, their hearts full of joy and parental affection.

The cowherd friends eat with Rāma and Kṛṣṇa, dancing and singing with joy in their hearts. They eat Kṛṣṇa’s remnants, filling their stomachs, and keep saying, “Give more! Give more!”

IDX.first_line: bhairesacira angane kabhu madhavendrapuri prabhuprasadanna karena bhojanakhaite khaite tara aila prema sudurbarabale suna sannyasira gana
IDX.search: bhairesacira angane kabhu madhavendrapuri prabhuprasadanna karena bhojanakhaite khaite tara aila prema sudurbarabale suna sannyasira gana moca ghanta phulavadi dali dalna caccadisacimata karila randhanatara suddha bhakti heri bhojana karila harisudha sama e anna vyanjana
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
bhāire!śacīra aṅgane kabhu, mādhavendra-purī prabhu,prasādānna karena bhojanakhāite khāite tā̐’ra, āila prema sudurbāra,bale⋅“śuna, sannyāsīra gaṇa

mocā⋅ghaṇṭa phulavaḍi, ḍāli⋅ḍālnā⋅caccaḍi,śacīmātā karila randhanatā̐’ra śuddhā bhakti heri’, bhojana karila hari,sudhā⋅sama e anna⋅vyañjana

joge jogī pāya jāhā, bhoge āja ha’be tāhā,hari bali’ khāo sabe bhāikṛṣṇera prasāda anna, trijagat kare dhanya,tripurāri nāce jāhā pāi”
IDX.en_translation:
O brothers! In Śacī’s courtyard, sometimes, Madhavendra Purī Prabhu partook of prasādānna. While eating, irresistible prema arose in him, and he said, “Listen, O assembly of sannyāsīs!

Mochā-ghaṇṭa (banana flower curry), phulvaṛī (fried lentil balls), ḍāli (lentil soup), ḍālnā (vegetable curry), caccari (mixed vegetable dish)-Mother Śacī cooked them. Seeing her pure bhakti, Hari partook of this food as if it were nectar.

That which a yogī obtains through yoga, today will be obtained through eating. Saying “Hari”, eat all of you, O brothers! Kṛṣṇa’s prasād blesses the three worlds, at the attainment of which Tripurāri (Lord Śiva) dances.”

IDX.first_line: bhairesri caitanya nityananda srivasadi bhaktavrndagauridasa panditera ghareluci cini ksira sara mithai payasa arapithapana asvadana kare
IDX.search: bhairesri caitanya nityananda srivasadi bhaktavrndagauridasa panditera ghareluci cini ksira sara mithai payasa arapithapana asvadana kare mahaprabhu bhaktagane parama ananda maneajna dila karite bhojanakrsnera prasada anna bhojane hoiya dhanyakrsna bali dake sarvajana
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
bhāire!śrī⋅caitanya⋅nityānanda, śrīvāsādi bhakta-vṛnda,gaurīdāsa⋅paṇḍitera ghareluci, cini, kṣīra, sara, miṭhāi, pāyasa āra,piṭhāpānā āsvādana kare

mahāprabhu bhakta-gaṇe, parama ānanda⋅mane,ājñā dila karite bhojanakṛṣṇera prasāda⋅anna, bhojane hoiyā dhanya,kṛṣṇa bali’ ḍāke sarva-jana
IDX.en_translation:
O brother! Caitanya and Nityānanda, along with Śrīvāsa and other devotees, are at the house of Gaurīdāsa Paṇḍita tasting luci (fried bread), chini (sugar), kṣīra (condensed milk), sara (cream), miṭhāi (sweets), pāyasa (rice pudding), and piṭhā-pānā (cakes and drinks).

With a greatly joyful heart, Mahāprabhu ordered them to take the meal. Blessed by eating the food offered to Kṛṣṇa, everyone calls out His name!

IDX.first_line: bhaja bhai caitanya nityananda
IDX.search: bhaja bhai caitanya nityananda ghucibe sakala jvala paibe ananda vadana bhariya bhai bala haribola apane vaisnavagana dhari dibe kola
IDX.author: śyāmānanda dāsa
IDX.verses:
bhaja bhāi! caitanya nityānanda
ghucibe sakala jvālā pāibe ānanda

vadana bhariyā bhāi! bala haribola
āpane vaiṣṇavagaṇa dhari’ dibe kola

minati kariyā kahi śuna sarvajanā
vraje rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa⋅līlā karaha bhāvanā

emana janama bhāi! nā hoibe āra
śyāmānanda kahe, keha nahe āpanāra
IDX.en_translation:
O brother! Worship Lord Caitanya and Lord Nityānanda. All miseries will end, and you will attain supreme bliss.

O brother! With a full mouth, chant "Haribol!" The Vaiṣṇavas themselves will embrace you.

I humbly request, listen all people: Meditate upon the pastimes of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa in Vraja.

O brother! Such a human birth will not come again. Śyāmānanda says: "No one in this world is truly your own."

IDX.first_line: bhaja bhaja hari mana drdha kari
IDX.search: bhaja bhaja hari mana drdha kari mukhe bola tara nama vrajendranandana gopi pranadhana bhuvanamohana syama kakhana maribe kemane taribe visama samana dake jahara pratape bhuvana kapaye na jani mara vipake
IDX.author: śrīla locana⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
bhaja bhaja hari, mana dṛḍha kari’,
mukhe bola tā̐’ra nāma
vrajendra-nandana, gopī⋅prāṇadhana,
bhuvana-mohana śyāma

kakhana maribe, kemane taribe,
viṣama śamana ḍāke
jā̐hāra pratāpe, bhuvana kā̐paye,
nā jāni mara vipāke

kula⋅dhana pāiyā, unamata haiyā,
āpanāke jāna baḍa
śamanera dūte, dhari’ pāye hāte,
bā̐dhiyā karibe jaḍa

kibā jati, satī, kibā nīca jāti,
jei hari nāhi bhaje
tabe janamiyā, bhramiyā bhramiyā,
raurava⋅narake maje

e dāsa locana, bhāve anukṣaṇa,
vṛthāi janama gela
hari nā bhajinu, viṣaye majinu,
hṛdaye rahala śela
IDX.en_translation:
Worship Hari, making your mind firm. Speak His name with your mouth—the son of the King of Vraja, the treasure of the gopīs' lives, the dark-complexioned Lord who enchants the universe.

When will you die? How will you be saved? The fierce lord of death calls. By whose prowess the universe trembles, you do not know when you will die in great distress.

Having obtained family and wealth and becoming intoxicated by them, you consider yourself great. The messengers of the lord of death, catching you by your hands and feet, will bind you and make you helpless.

Whether an ascetic, a chaste woman, or one of a low caste, whoever does not worship the Lord will wander through countless lives and drown in a hellish planet.

This servant Locana constantly thinks: "My life has passed in vain! I did not worship the Lord and instead drowned in worldly affairs; a spear of regret remains in my heart."

IDX.first_line: bhaja bhakata vatsala srigaura harisrigaura hari sohi gosthaviharinanda yasomati cittahari
IDX.search: bhaja bhakata vatsala srigaura harisrigaura hari sohi gosthaviharinanda yasomati cittahari bela hala damodara aisa ekhanabhoga mandire vasi karaha bhojana
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
bhaja bhakata⋅vatsala śrī-gaura⋅hariśrī-gaura⋅hari sohi goṣṭhavihārī,nanda⋅yaśomatī⋅cittahārī

belā ha’la dāmodara, āisa ekhanabhoga⋅mandire vasi’ karaha bhojana

nandera nirdeśe baise girivaradhārībaladeva-saha sakhā baise sāri sāri

śuktā śākādi bhāji nālitā kuṣmāṇḍaḍāli ḍālnā dugdha-tumbī dadhi mocā-khaṇḍa

mudgavaḍā māṣavaḍā roṭikā ghṛtānnaśaṣkulī piṣṭaka kṣīra puli pāyasānna

karpūra amṛtakeli rambhā kṣīrasāraamṛta rasālā, amla dvādaśa-prakāra

luci cini sarapurī lāḍḍu rasāvalībhojana karena kṛṣṇa ha’ye kutūhalī

rādhikāra pakva anna vividha vyañjanaparama ānande kṛṣṇa karena bhojana

chale⋅bale lāḍḍu khāya śrīmadhumaṅgalabagala bājāya āra deya haribola

rādhikādi⋅gaṇe heri’ nayanera koṇetṛpta ha’ye khāya kṛṣṇa jaśodā⋅bhavane

bhojanānte piye kṛṣṇa suvāsita vārisabe mukha prakṣālaya ha’ye sāri sāri

hasta⋅mukha prakṣāliyā jata sakhāgaṇeānande viśrāma kare baladeva⋅sane

‘jāmbula’ ‘rasāla’ āne tāmbūla⋅masālātāhā kheye kṛṣṇa-candra sukhe nidrā gelā

‘viśālākṣa’ śikhipuccha cāmara ḍhulāyaapūrva śayyāya kṛṣṇa sukhe nidrā jāya

jaśomatī⋅ājñā peye dhaniṣṭhā⋅ānītaśrī-kṛṣṇa-prasāda rādhā bhuñje ha’ye prīta

lalitādi sakhīgaṇa avaśeṣa pāyamane mane sukhe rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa⋅guṇa gāya
IDX.en_translation:
Worship bhakta-vatsala (one who is affectionate to His devotees) Śrī Gaura⋅hari. Śrī Gaura⋅hari is the same goṣṭha-vihārī (one who enjoys pastimes in the cowherd village), Nanda and Yaśomatī’s citta-hārī (one who steals the heart).

It’s time, Dāmodara, please come now. Sit in the bhoga-mandira (offering house) and take your meal.

Nanda’s order, Girivaradhārī (the one who lifted Govardhana Hill) sits, Baladeva and the sakhās (friends) sit in rows.

Śukta, various vegetable fries, nālitā (jute leaves), kuṣmāṇḍa (winter melon), Dāli (lentils), ḍālanā (vegetable curry), milk-tumbī (gourd), dadhi (yogurt), mocākhaṇḍa (banana flower curry).

Mudgavaṛā (lentil fritters), māṣavaṛā (black gram fritters), roṭikā (bread), ghṛtānna (rice with ghee), Śaṣkulī (sweet cakes), piṣṭaka (cakes), kṣīra (sweet rice), puli (dumplings), pāyasānna (sweet rice).

Karpūra (camphor), amṛtakeli (a type of sweet), rambhā (banana), kṣīrasāra (cream of milk), Amṛta rasālā (sweet nectar), amla (sour dishes) of twelve types.

Lucī (fried bread), cini (sugar), sarapurī (a type of sweet), lāḍḍu (sweet balls), rasāvalī (sweet dish), Kṛṣṇa eats with great curiosity.

Rādhikā’s pakva anna (cooked rice) and various vyañjana (curries), Kṛṣṇa eats with supreme joy.

Trickery, Śrī Madhumaṅgala eats lāḍḍu, He claps his armpits and shouts Haribol.

Beholding Rādhikā and others from the corner of His eye, Kṛṣṇa eats contentedly in Yaśodā’s bhavana (house).

After eating, Kṛṣṇa drinks suvasita vāri (fragrant water), Everyone cleans their mouths, sitting in rows.

Having washed their hands and mouths, all the sakhā-gaṇe (groups of friends), Happily rest with Baladeva.

“Jāmbula” (jambu fruit) and “rasāla” (mango) are brought, and tāmbūla-masālā (betel nut preparation), Eating that, Kṛṣṇa⋅candra goes to sleep happily.

Viśālāksa waves the peacock feather cāmara (fan), Kṛṣṇa sleeps happily on an extraordinary bed.

Receiving Yaśomatī’s order, brought by Dhaniṣṭhā, Rādhā partakes of Śrī Kṛṣṇa-prasāda (mercy) with pleasure.

Lalitādi sakhī-gaṇa (Lalitā and other female friends) receive the remnants, In their minds, they happily sing the glories of Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa.

Harilīlā (Kṛṣṇa’s pastimes) is the only source of joy for whom, Bhaktivinoda sings this bhogārati (song glorifying the offering of food).

IDX.first_line: bhaja gauranga kaha gauranga laha gaurangera nama re
IDX.search: bhaja gauranga kaha gauranga laha gaurangera nama re je jana gauranga bhaje sei haya amara prana re
IDX.author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
bhaja gaurāṅga kaha gaurāṅga laha gaurāṅgera nāma re

je jana gaurāṅga bhaje sei haya āmara prāṇa re
IDX.en_translation: Worship Lord Gaurāṅga, speak of Lord Gaurāṅga, and chant the Name of Lord Gaurāṅga. Whoever worships Lord Gaurāṅga is my very life.

IDX.first_line: bhaja mana nandakumara
IDX.search: bhaja mana nandakumara bhaviya dekhaha bhai gati nahi ara dhana jana putra adi keba apanara atoeva karaha mana haripada sara
IDX.author: rādhāmohana dāsa
IDX.verses:
bhaja mana! nandakumāra
bhāviyā dekhaha bhāi gati nāhi āra

dhana jana putra ādi kebā āpanāra
atoeva karaha mana hari-pada sāra

kusaṅga chāḍiyā sadā satsaṅge thāka
parama nipuṇa hoi, nāma bali’ ḍāka

tā̐’ra nāma⋅līlā⋅gāne sadā hoo matta
se caraṇa⋅dhana pābe, hoibe kṛtārtha

rādhāmohana bale, mana! ki baliba tore
saṁsāra⋅yātanā āra nāhi deha more
IDX.en_translation:
O my mind! Worship the Son of Nanda. Reflect and see, O brother—there is no other way.

Wealth, family, children, and so on—who is truly your own? Therefore, O mind, make the lotus feet of Lord Hari your only essence.

Giving up bad association, always stay in good company. Becoming very expert, call out the Holy Name.

Always be intoxicated in singing His Name and pastimes. You will receive the treasure of His lotus feet and your life will be fulfilled.

Rādhāmohana says: "O my mind! What more shall I say to you? Do not cause me to suffer in this material existence anymore."

IDX.first_line: bhaja radha krsna gopala krsna
IDX.search: bhaja radha krsna gopala krsna krsna krsna bala mukhe name buka bhare jaya abhava mitaya svabhava jagaya mahasukhe hari dinabandhu ciradina bandhu jivera cira sukhe duhkhe tai bhajare andha caranaravinda dustara maya vipake
IDX.verses:
bhaja rādhā kṛṣṇa, gopāla kṛṣṇa
kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa bala mukhe
nāme buka bhare jāya, abhāva miṭāya,
svabhāva jāgāya mahāsukhe

hari dīnabandhu, ciradina bandhu,
jīvera cira sukhe duḥkhe
(tāi) bhajare andha, caraṇāravinda,
dustara māyā⋅vipāke

bhaja mūḍhamati, tava cirasāthī,
jā̐hāra karuṇā loke loke
tabe kena pāntha, eta tumi bhrānta,
kothāya chuṭicha dike dike?

(sei) līlāmaya hari, eseche nadīyāpurī
rādhāra pirīti la’ye buke
bhaja bhagavāne, sadā maneprāṇe,
esechena nite tore buke
IDX.en_translation:
Worship Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa! Worship Gopāla Kṛṣṇa! Chant "Kṛṣṇa! Kṛṣṇa!" with your mouth. The heart fills with the Holy Name, all wants are fulfilled, and one's true nature awakens in great joy.

Lord Hari, the Friend of the distressed, is the eternal friend of the soul in both happiness and sorrow. Therefore, O blind one, worship His lotus feet in this insurmountable entanglement of illusion.

Worship your eternal companion, O foolish mind! His mercy is in every world. Why then, O traveler, are you so bewildered? Where are you running in every direction?

That playful Lord Hari has come to the city of Nadīyā with the love of Rādhā in His heart. Worship the Supreme Lord always with your mind and soul, for He has come to take you to His heart.

IDX.first_line: bhaja re bhaja re amara mana ati mandabhajana vina gati nai rebhaja vrajavane radha krsna caranaravindajnana karma parihari re
IDX.search: bhaja re bhaja re amara mana ati mandabhajana vina gati nai rebhaja vrajavane radha krsna caranaravindajnana karma parihari re bhaja gaura gadadharadvaita guru nityanandagaura krsne abheda jene reguru krsnapriya jene resmara srinivasa haridasa murari mukundagaurapreme smara smara re
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
bhaja re bhaja re āmāra mana ati manda(bhajana vinā gati nāi re)(bhaja) vraja-vane rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa⋅caraṇāravinda(jñāna⋅karma pari-hari re)

(bhaja) gaura⋅gadādharādvaita guru⋅nityānanda(gaura⋅kṛṣṇe abheda jene re)(guru kṛṣṇa-priya jene re)(smara) śrīnivāsa haridāsa⋅murāri⋅mukunda(gaura-preme smara, smara re)

(smara) rūpa⋅sanātana⋅jīva⋅raghunāthadvandva(yadi bhajana karbe re)(smara) rāghava⋅gopālabhaṭṭa⋅svarūpa⋅rāmānanda(kṛṣṇa-prema jadi cāo re)

(smara) goṣṭhīsaha karṇapūra, sena śivānanda(ajasra smara, smara re)(smara) rūpānuga sādhujana bhajana⋅ānanda(vraje vāsa jadi cāo re)
IDX.en_translation:
Worship, worship, my mind, which is very wicked. Without worship, there is no destination, oh! Worship in the forest of Vraja the lotus feet of Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa. Abandoning knowledge and fruitive actions, oh!

Worship Caitanya Mahāprabhu, Gadādhara, Advaita, spiritual master, and Nityānanda. Knowing Gaura and Kṛṣṇa to be non-different, oh! Knowing the spiritual master to be dear to Kṛṣṇa, oh! Remember Śrīnivāsa, Haridāsa, Murāri, Mukunda. Remember with love for Gaura, remember, remember, oh!

Remember Rūpa, Sanātana, Jīva, and the two Raghunāthas. If you will perform worship, oh! Remember Rāghava, Gopāla Bhaṭṭa, Svarūpa, Rāmānanda. If you desire love for Kṛṣṇa, oh!

Remember Karṇapūra along with his family, Sena Śivānanda. Remember profusely, remember, oh! Remember the followers of Rūpa Gosvāmī, saintly persons who rejoice in worship. If you desire residence in Vraja, oh!

IDX.first_line: bhajahu re mana srinandanandana
IDX.search: bhajahu re mana srinandanandana abhaya caranaravinda re durlabha manava janama satsange taraha e bhavasindhu re sita atapa vata varisana e dina jamini jagi re viphale sevinu krpana durajana capala sukha lava lagi re
IDX.author: govinda dāsa
IDX.verses:
bhajahu̐ re mana, śrī-nanda-nandana,
abhaya caraṇāravinda re
durlabha mānava⋅, janama satsaṅge,
taraha e bhava-sindhu re

śīta⋅ātapa, vāta⋅variṣaṇa,
e dina jāminī jāgi’ re
viphale sevinu, kṛpaṇa durajana,
capala sukha⋅lava lāgi’ re

e dhana, jauvana, putra⋅parijana,
ithe ki āche paratīti re
kamaladala⋅jala, jīvana ṭalamala,
bhajahu̐ hari-pada niti re

śravaṇa, kīrtana, smaraṇa, vandana,
pādasevana, dāsya re
pūjana, sakhī-jana, ātmanivedana,
govindadāsa abhilāṣa re
IDX.en_translation:
O mind, worship the fearless lotus feet of the son of Nanda. Having attained this rare human birth, cross over this ocean of material existence in the association of saintly persons.

Remaining awake day and night in cold, heat, wind, and rain, I have fruitlessly served miserly and wicked people just for a fleeting drop of happiness.

What reliability is there in wealth, youth, children, or family? Life is as unsteady as a drop of water on a lotus petal; therefore, always worship the feet of the Lord.

Hearing, chanting, remembering, offering prayers, serving the Lord's feet, servitude, worship, friendship, and self-surrender—this is the heartfelt desire of Govinda dāsa.

IDX.first_line: bhavarnave pade mora akula paranakise kula paba tara na pai sandhana
IDX.search: bhavarnave pade mora akula paranakise kula paba tara na pai sandhana na ache karama bala nahi jnana balajaga yoga tapodharma na ache sambala
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
bhavārṇave pa’ḍe mora ākula parāṇakise kūla pā’ba, tā’ra nā pāi sandhāna

nā āche karama⋅bala, nāhi jñāna⋅balajāga⋅yoga⋅tapodharma⋅nā āche sambala

nitānta durbala āmi, nā jāni sā̐tārae vipade ke āmāre karibe uddhāra

viṣaya⋅kumbhīra tāhe bhīṣaṇa⋅darśanakāmera taraṅga sadā kare uttejana

prāktana vāyura vega sahite nā pārikā̐diyā asthira mana, nā dekhi kāṇḍārī

ogo śrī⋅jāhnavā devi! e dāse karuṇākara āji nija-guṇe, ghucāo jantraṇā

tomāra caraṇa⋅tarī kariyā āśrayabhavārṇava pāra ha’ba karechi niścaya

tumi nityānanda⋅śakti kṛṣṇa⋅bhakti⋅gurue dāse karaha dāna pada-kalpataru

kata kata pāmarere ka’recha uddhāratomāra caraṇe āja e kāṅgāla chāra
IDX.en_translation:
My anxious heart has fallen into the ocean of material existence, and I find no way to reach the shore.

I have no strength of action, nor the strength of knowledge; sacrifice, yoga, and austerities are resources I do not possess.

I am extremely weak and do not know how to swim; in this danger, who will deliver me?

The crocodiles of sense objects are fearsome to behold, and the waves of lust constantly incite agitation.

Unable to bear the force of my past karma, my mind is restless from crying, and I see no helmsman.

O Śrī Jāhnavā Devī! Today, by your own divine qualities, bestow mercy upon this servant and remove this suffering.

Taking shelter of the boat of your lotus feet, I have certainly decided to cross the ocean of material existence.

You are the potency of Nityānanda and the spiritual master of devotion to Kṛṣṇa. Please bestow upon this servant the wish-fulfilling tree of your feet.

You have delivered so many fallen souls; today, this wretched person is at your feet.

IDX.first_line: bhola mana ekabara bhava parinama
IDX.search: bhola mana ekabara bhava parinama bhaja krsna kaha krsna laha krsna nama krsna bhajibara setha pratijna karile samsare asibamatra sakala bhulile
IDX.verses:
bholā mana, ekabāra bhāva pariṇāma
bhaja kṛṣṇa, kaha kṛṣṇa, laha kṛṣṇa⋅nāma

kṛṣṇa bhajibāra sethā pratijñā karile
saṁsāre āsibāmātra sakala bhulile

kata kaṣṭe pāla’ bhāi⋅bhāryā, beṭī, beṭā
kṛṣṇapada bhajitei bādhe saba leṭhā

śata jihvā⋅paranindā, para⋅toṣāmode
kṛṣṇa⋅nāma kahitei rasanāya bādhe

parapada dhari’ sadā karicha lehane
niyukta nā kara’ kara se padasevane

āre mana, bhavaroge ghirila tomāre
hāsaphāsa karitecha viṣama vikāre

kṛṣṇapada nā bhajiyā mara upasarge
kṛṣṇapada bhaja, lābha habe caturvarge

loite madhura nāma kenare kātara
kena bhāi michāmichi hoicha phā̐para

kahe e adhama dāsa ghucibe vikāra
nāma bhaja, nāma cinta, nāma kara sāra
IDX.en_translation:
O forgetful mind! Once consider the ultimate consequence. Worship Lord Kṛṣṇa, speak of Him, and chant His Holy Name.

Before your birth, you vowed to worship Lord Kṛṣṇa, but as soon as you entered this world, you forgot everything.

With great difficulty you maintain your family, but all these worldly troubles hinder you from worshipping the lotus feet of Lord Kṛṣṇa.

A hundred tongues are engaged in criticizing and flattering others, but as soon as you try to speak the Holy Name, your tongue becomes choked.

You are always busy flattering others, but you do not engage yourself in the service of Lord Kṛṣṇa's lotus feet.

O mind! The disease of material existence has surrounded you, and you are gasping from its terrible effects.

Without worshipping the feet of Lord Kṛṣṇa, you are dying from worldly complications. Worship His lotus feet and you will gain the four fruits of human life.

Why are you so distressed to chant the sweet Holy Name? O brother! Why are you unnecessarily troubled?

This fallen servant says that your worldly ailments will cease. Worship the Holy Name, meditate upon it, and make it your only essence.

IDX.first_line: bhraturantakasya pattanebhipatti harini
IDX.search: bhraturantakasya pattanebhipatti harini preksayati papinopi papasindhu tarini nira madhuribhirapyasesa citta bandhini mam punatu sarvadaravinda bandhu nandini hari vari dharayabhimanditoru khandava pundarika mandalodyadandajali tandava snanakama pamarogra papasampadandhini mam punatu sarvadaravinda bandhu nandini
IDX.author: śrīla rūpa gosvāmī
IDX.verses:
bhrāturantakasya pattane‛bhipatti⋅hāriṇī
prekṣayāti⋅pāpino‛pi pāpasindhu⋅tāriṇī
nīra⋅mādhurībhirapyaśeṣa⋅citta⋅bandhinī
māṁ punātu sarvadāravinda⋅bandhu⋅nandinī

hāri⋅vāri⋅dhārayābhimaṇḍitoru⋅khāṇḍavā
puṇḍarīka⋅maṇḍalodyadaṇḍajāli⋅tāṇḍavā
snānakāma⋅pāmarogra⋅pāpasampadandhinī
māṁ punātu sarvadāravinda⋅bandhu⋅nandinī

śīkarābhimṛṣṭa⋅jantu⋅durvipāka⋅mardinī
nanda⋅nandanāntaraṅga⋅bhaktipūra⋅vardhinī
tīra⋅saṅgamābhilāṣi⋅maṅgalānubandhinī
māṁ punātu sarvadāravinda⋅bandhu⋅nandinī

dvīpacakravāla⋅juṣṭa⋅saptasindhu⋅bhedinī
śrīmukunda⋅nirmitoru⋅divyakeli⋅vedinī
kānti⋅kandalībhirindranīlavṛnda⋅nindinī
māṁ punātu sarvadāravinda⋅bandhu⋅nandinī

māthureṇa maṇḍalena cāruṇābhimaṇḍitā
premanaddha⋅vaiṣṇavādhva⋅vardhanāya paṇḍitā
ūrmi⋅dorvilāsa⋅padmanābha⋅pāda⋅vandinī
māṁ punātu sarvadāravinda⋅bandhu⋅nandinī

ramya⋅tīra⋅rambhamāṇa⋅gokadamba⋅bhūṣitā
divyagandhabhāk⋅kadamba⋅puṣparāji⋅rūṣitā
nandasūnu⋅bhaktasaŋgha⋅saṅgamābhinandinī
māṁ punātu sarvadāravinda⋅bandhu⋅nandinī

phullapakṣa⋅mallikākṣa⋅haṁsalakṣa⋅kūjitā
bhaktividdha⋅deva⋅siddha⋅kinnarāli⋅pūjitā
tīra⋅gandhavāha⋅gandha⋅janmabandha⋅randhinī
māṁ punātu sarvadāravinda⋅bandhu⋅nandinī

cidvilāsa⋅vāripūra⋅bhūrbhuvaḥ⋅svarapinī
kīrtitāpi durmadoru⋅pāpamarma⋅tāpinī
vallavendra⋅nandanāṅgarāga⋅bhaṅga⋅gandhinī
māṁ punātu sarvadāravinda⋅bandhu⋅nandinī

tuṣṭa⋅buddhiraṣṭakena nirmalormi⋅ceṣṭitāṁ
tvāmanena bhānuputri! sarvadeva⋅veṣṭitām
jaḥ stavīti vardhayasva sarvapāpa⋅mocane
bhaktipūramasya devi! puṇḍarīka⋅locane
IDX.en_translation:
May the Daughter of the Sun, the friend of the lotus, always purify me. She removes the calamity of the city of her brother Antaka (Yama). By her sight, she delivers even the great sinner from the ocean of sin. She binds the endless heart with the sweetness of her water.

May the Daughter of the Sun, the friend of the lotus, always purify me. She adorned the great Khāṇḍava forest with her captivating stream of water. She has the dancing of rising flocks of birds in the circle of lotuses. She blinds the wealth of terrible sin of the wretches desiring to bathe.

May the Daughter of the Sun, the friend of the lotus, always purify me. She crushes the bad destiny of the living beings touched by her spray. She increases the flood of intimate devotion to the Son of Nanda. She causes auspiciousness for those desiring the contact of her bank.

May the Daughter of the Sun, the friend of the lotus, always purify me. She pierces the seven oceans visited by the circle of islands. She knows the great divine pastimes created by Śrī Mukunda. She mocks the multitude of sapphires with shoots of luster.

May the Daughter of the Sun, the friend of the lotus, always purify me. She is adorned by the beautiful circle of Mathurā. She is expert in increasing the path of the Vaiṣṇavas bound by love. She worships the feet of Padmanābha with the play of her arms of waves.

May the Daughter of the Sun, the friend of the lotus, always purify me. She is adorned with herds of cows mooing on her beautiful banks. She is smeared with the pollen of Kadamba flowers possessing divine fragrance. She rejoices in the contact of the group of devotees of the Son of Nanda.

May the Daughter of the Sun, the friend of the lotus, always purify me. She is resounded by hundreds of thousands of swans with blooming wings and jasmine eyes. She is worshipped by the gods, Siddhas, and Kinnaras pierced by devotion. She destroys the bondage of birth by the scent of the wind on her bank.

May the Daughter of the Sun, the friend of the lotus, always purify me. She is the form of Bhūr, Bhuvaḥ, and Svaḥ (the three worlds) in the flood of water of spiritual pastimes. Even when glorified, she burns the core of the terrible sin of the proud. She has the scent of the broken body-ointments of the Son of the King of Cowherds.

O Daughter of the Sun! O Devi! O Lotus-eyed one! Whoever praises you with this Octet with a satisfied mind, increase the flood of devotion of him who is active with pure waves and surrounded by all the gods, while liberating him from all sins.

IDX.first_line: bhuliya tomare samsare asiya
IDX.search: bhuliya tomare samsare asiya peye nanavidha vyatha tomara carane asiyachi ami baliba duhkhera katha janani jathare chilama jakhana visama bandhanapase ekabara prabhu dekha diya more vancile e dina dase
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
bhuliyā tomāre, saṁsāre āsiyā,
peye nānāvidha vyathā
tomāra caraṇe, āsiyāchi āmi,
baliba duḥkhera kathā

jananī⋅jaṭhare, chilāma jakhana,
viṣama bandhanapāśe
ekabāra prabhu, dekhā diyā more,
vañcile e dīna dāse

takhana bhāvinu, janama pāiyā,
kariba bhajana tava
janama hoila, paḍi’ māyā⋅jāle,
nā hoila jñāna⋅laba

ādarera chele, svajanera kole,
hāsiyā kāṭānu kāla
janaka⋅jananī, snehete bhuliyā,
saṁsāra lāgila bhāla

krame dina dina, bālaka hoiyā,
khelinu bālaka⋅saha
āra kichu dine, jñāna upajila,
pāṭha paḍi’ aharahaḥ

vidyāra gaurave, bhrami’ deśe deśe,
dhana upārjana kari
svajana⋅pālana, kari ekamane,
bhulinu tomāre hari

vārdhakye ekhana, bhakativinoda,
kā̐diyā kātara ati
nā bhajiyā tore, dina vṛthā gela,
ekhana ki ha’be gati
IDX.translation_intro: From The Harmonist, July 1927. Edited by Bhaktisiddhanta Sarasvati.
IDX.en_translation:
Forgetting thyself, coming into this world, Having suffered manifold pain, At Thy feet have I come, Thee I shall tell my sorrows:

In mother's womb while I lay In the deadly fetters of bondage, For once, O Lord, showing Thyself to me Didst Thou abandon Thy poor servant.

Then thought I: after I have birth, I shall worship Thee. I was born, but falling into the meshes of Maya There was not a particle of knowledge.

A fondled child in the lap of kindred, Laughingly I passed the time. The affection of the father and mother madest me forget, This world did taste sweet.

By slow degrees, day by day, growing up as a boy With other boys did I play. In a few more days the power of understanding was aroused I conned my lessons night and day.

In the pride of learning I journeyed to many a place, Earning wealth, Supporting kindred with all my heart, Hari, Thee I forgot.

Now in old age Bhaktivinode Crying feels so sad; Not worshipping Thee the days passed in vain, What help is there now?

IDX.first_line: bhuvana ananda kanda balarama nityananda
IDX.search: bhuvana ananda kanda balarama nityananda avatirna hoila kalikale ghucila sakala duhkha dekhiya se cada mukha bhase loka ananda hillole jaya jaya nityananda rama kanaka campaka kanti anule cadera pati rupe jitala koti kama
IDX.author: kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa
IDX.verses:
bhuvana ānanda kanda, balarāma nityānanda
avatīrṇa hoila kalikāle
ghucila sakala duḥkha, dekhiyā se cā̐da mukha
bhāse loka ānanda hillole

jaya jaya nityānanda rāma
kanaka campaka kānti, āṅule cā̐dera pā̐ti
rūpe jitala koṭi kāma

o mukhamaṇḍala dekhi, pūrṇacandra kise lekhi,
dīghala nayana bhānu dhanu
ājānulambita bhuja, tala thala paṅkaja kaṭi
kṣīṇa kari ari janu

caraṇa⋅kamala⋅tale, bhakata⋅bhramara bule
ādha vāṇī amiyā prakāśa
iha kaliyuga jīve, uddhāra hoibe sabe
kahe dīna duḥkhī kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa
IDX.en_translation:
The source of joy for the universe, Balarāma and Nityānanda, have descended in this age of Kali. All sorrows are dispelled upon seeing Their moon-like faces, and people float in waves of joy.

All glories to Nityānanda Rāma! His effulgence is like golden jasmine flowers, with rows of moons on His limbs. By His beauty, He has conquered millions of Cupids.

Seeing that moon-like face, how can I compare it to a full moon? His long eyes are like the sun and a bow. His arms reach down to His knees, and His waist is like a firm lotus. He makes even a lion seem thin with His slender form.

At His lotus feet, devotee-bees wander. From His half-spoken words, nectar manifests. All living entities of this age will be delivered. So says the humble and distressed Kṛṣṇa dāsa.

IDX.first_line: bola hari bola x3
IDX.search: bola hari bola x3 manera anande bhai bola hari bola bola hari bola x3 janame janame sukhe bola hari bola bola hari bola x3 manava janma peye bhai bola hari bola bola hari bola x3 sukhe thaka duhkhe thaka bola hari bola
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
bola hari bola (x3)
manera ānande, bhāi, bola hari bola
bola hari bola (x3)
janame janame sukhe bola hari bola

bola hari bola (x3)
mānava⋅janma pe’ye, bhāi, bola hari bola
bola hari bola (x3)
sukhe thāka, duḥkhe thāka, bola hari bola

bola hari bola (x3)
sampade vipade, bhāi, bola hari bola
bola hari bola (x3)
kṛṣṇera saṁsāre thāki’, bola hari bola

bola hari bola (x3)
asatsaṅga chāḍi’, bhāi, bola hari bola
bola hari bola (x3)
vaiṣṇava⋅caraṇe paḍi’, bola hari bola

bola hari bola (x3)
gaura⋅nityānanda bola (x3)
gaura⋅gadādhara bola (x3)
gaura⋅advaita bola (x3)
(śrīla bhaktivinoda ṭhākura)
IDX.en_translation:
Chant "Hari Bol!" O brother, in the joy of the mind, chant "Hari Bol!" Happily, birth after birth, chant "Hari Bol!"

O brother, having obtained a human birth, chant "Hari Bol!" In happiness or in sorrow, chant "Hari Bol!"

O brother, in wealth or in danger, chant "Hari Bol!" Remaining in Kṛṣṇa’s family, chant "Hari Bol!"

O brother, forsaking bad association, chant "Hari Bol!" Falling at the feet of a devotee, chant "Hari Bol!"

Chant "Hari Bol! Hari Bol!" Chant the names of Gaura and Nityānanda, Gaura and Gadādhara, Gaura and Advaita!

IDX.first_line: campaka sona kusuma kanakacala
IDX.search: campaka sona kusuma kanakacala jitala gaura tanu labani re unnata gima sima nahi anubhava jaga mana mohana bhanani re
IDX.author: govinda dāsa
IDX.verses: campaka śoṇa kusuma kanakācala jitala gaura⋅tanu⋅lābaṇi re unnata gīma sīma nāhi anubhava jaga⋅mana⋅mohana bhāṅanī re jaya śacī⋅nandana tribhuvana⋅vandana kali⋅yuga⋅kāla⋅bhujaga⋅bhaya⋅khaṇḍana re vipula pulaka kula ākula kalevara gara gara antare prema⋅bhare lahu lahu hāsanī gada gada bhāṣaṇī kata mandākinī nayane jhare nija⋅rase nācatanayana ḍhulāyata gāyata kata kata bhakatahiṁ meli jo rase bhāsi avaśa mahī⋅maṇḍala govinda⋅dāsa tahiṁ paraśa nā bheli
IDX.en_translation:
The beauty of Gaura's golden body conquers the beauty of the reddish flowers and the golden mountain.

His raised neck is charming beyond limit, and His demeanor enchants the minds of all in the world.

All glories to the Son of Śacī! He is worshipped by the three worlds and is the destroyer of the fear of the serpent of the Age of Kali.

His entire body is covered with goosebumps from ecstasy, and His heart is overwhelmed with divine love.

His gentle smile, His faltering speech, and the countless streams of tears that flow from His eyes!

He dances in His own transcendental mellows, His eyes rolling, and He sings together with many devotees.

In that mellow, the entire earth floats, completely overwhelmed. Only Govinda Dāsa could not attain that experience.

IDX.first_line: chadi anya abhilasa jnana karma sahavasa
IDX.search: chadi anya abhilasa jnana karma sahavasa anukulye krsnanusilana suddhabhakti bali tare bhaktisastra suvicare srirupera siddhanta vacana sravana kirtana smrti sevarcana dasya nati sakhya atmanivedana haya sadhana bhaktira anga sadhakera jahe ranga sada sadhujana sangamaya
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
chāḍi’ anya⋅ abhilāṣa, jñāna⋅ karma⋅ sahavāsa,
ānukūlye kṛṣṇānuśīlana
‘śuddhabhakti’ bali tā’re, bhaktiśāstra⋅suvicāre,
śrī-rūpera siddhānta ⋅vacana

śravaṇa, kīrtana, smṛti, sevārcana, dāsya ,nati,
sakhya, ātmanivedana haya
sādhana⋅bhaktira aṅga, sādhakera jāhe raṅga,
sadā sādhujana⋅saṅgamaya

sādhana⋅bhaktira bale, bhāvarūpā bhakti⋅phale,
tāhā punaḥ premarūpa pāya
preme jīva kṛṣṇa bhaje, kṛṣṇa-bhakti-rase maje,
sei rasa śrī-rūpa śikhāya
IDX.en_translation:
Giving up other desires, and association with knowledge and fruitive action, the favorable cultivation of Kṛṣṇa is called pure devotion. This is the conclusive statement of Śrī Rūpa Gosvāmī, after careful consideration of the devotional scriptures.

Hearing, chanting, remembering, serving and worshiping, servitorship, offering obeisances, friendship, and self-surrender are the limbs of devotional practice, in which the practitioner finds joy, always in the association of saintly persons.

By the strength of devotional practice, the fruit is devotion in the stage of spiritual emotion, which again attains the form of divine love. In divine love, the soul worships Kṛṣṇa, becoming immersed in the nectar of devotion to Kṛṣṇa. That nectar Śrī Rūpa teaches.

IDX.first_line: chadi mana hari vimukhana ko sanga
IDX.search: chadi mana hari vimukhana ko sanga jinake sanga kubudhi upajati hai parata bhajaname bhanga kaha hota paya pana karaye visa nahi tajata bhujanga kagahi kaha kapura cugaye vana nhavaye ganga
IDX.verses:
chā̐ḍi mana, hari⋅vimukhana ko saṅga
jinake saṅga kubudhi upajati hai̐,
parata bhajaname̐ bhaṅga

kahā hota paya pāna karāye,
viṣa nahi̐ tajata bhujaṅga
kāgahi kahā kapūra cugāye,
vāna nhavāye gaṅga

kharako kahā aragajā⋅lepana,
marakaṭa bhūṣana aṅga
gajako kahānhavāye saritā,
bahuri gharai khahi chaṅga

pāhana patita bā̐sa nahī̐ bedhata,
rīto karata niṣaṅga
sūradāsa khala kārī kāmari,
caḍhata na dūjo raṅga
IDX.en_translation:
O my mind! Abandon the company of those who are averse to Lord Hari. By their association, evil thoughts arise and one's devotional service is interrupted.

What is the use of giving milk to a snake? It will never abandon its poison. What is the point of feeding camphor to a crow or bathing it in the Ganges?

What is the use of applying fragrant paste to a donkey or adorning a monkey with ornaments? What is the point of bathing an elephant in the river if it returns home and immediately smears mud upon its body?

A stone cannot be pierced by an arrow, and a hollow bamboo only empties the quiver. Sūrdāsa says: "For a wicked person who is like a black blanket, no other color will ever stick."

IDX.first_line: chilama kothaya elama hethaya
IDX.search: chilama kothaya elama hethaya kothaya jaba bhavi na sadhu sastra vani katoi je suni tabu bojha ta gela na ana gona sara sakaloi asara bujhaile ta bujhina ki jena abhava bhebe kata bhava svabhava chada gela na
IDX.author: śrīmadbhaktikumuda santa gosvāmī mahārāja
IDX.verses:
chilāma kothāya, elāma hethāya,
kothāya jāba bhāvi nā
sādhu śāstra vāṇī, katoi je śuni’
tabu bojhā ta’ gela nā

ānā gonā sāra, sakaloi asāra,
bujhāile ta’ bujhinā
ki jena abhāva, bhebe kata bhāva,
svabhāva chāḍā gela nā

jete habe ṭhika, saba je beṭhika,
saṭhika jānā gela nā
ki kariba hāya, bhāvi je sadāya,
śeṣa ha’la nā vāsanā

bhāvi sadā mane, janame janame,
bhugechi kata jātanā
jātanāra kathā, jene lāge vyāthā,
se vyāthā dūra ha’la nā

“śuni” śrī⋅hari sādhanā, karile jātanā,
saba haya avasāna
bhāviyā ākūla mana

kata upadeśa, svadeśa videśa,
diyechi śunechi āmi
ebe bhāvi hāya, la’ba je vidāya,
saṅge ki jābe nā jāni

peyechinu hari! bhavera kāṇḍārī,
sadguru ei bhave
kintu bhava pāra, hoimu apāra,
baliyāchi ucca rabe

antima āgata, tava pade nata,
haye dāsa sante kaya
rāṅā pade ṭhā̐i, dio go gosā̐i,
hari pade sthāna cāya
IDX.en_translation:
Where was I? I came here. I do not think about where I will go. Despite how much I hear from the saints and scriptures, still I did not understand.

Coming and going is the essence, all else is insubstantial. Even if explained, I do not understand. Whatever the lack may be, despite thinking in many ways, my innate nature could not be abandoned.

I will surely have to go, for all is uncertain. The right way was not known. What shall I do, alas? I always think, yet my desires did not end.

I always think in my mind how much suffering I have endured in birth after birth. Knowing the talk of suffering, pain arises, yet that pain did not go away.

I hear that by performing spiritual practice for Lord Hari, all suffering comes to an end. My mind is distressed by this thought.

How much advice, in my own country and foreign lands, I have given and heard. Now I think, alas, that I will take my leave, and I do not know what will go with me.

I had found You, O Lord Hari! The boatman of material existence, the true spiritual master in this world. But I will be unable to cross over this boundless ocean, despite having proclaimed it with a loud voice.

The end has come, and I bow at Your feet. The servant Santa says: "Please give me a place at Your lotus feet, O Master! I desire a place at the feet of Lord Hari."

IDX.translation_intro: Translation by Bhaktisiddhanta Sarasvati.
IDX.en_translation:
I worship Govinda, the primeval Lord, the first progenitor who is tending the cows, yielding all desire, in abodes built with spiritual gems, surrounded by millions of purpose trees, always served with great reverence and affection by hundreds of thousands of lakṣmīs or gopīs.

I worship Govinda, the primeval Lord, who is adept in playing on His flute, with blooming eyes like lotus petals with head decked with peacock's feather, with the figure of beauty tinged with the hue of blue clouds, and His unique loveliness charming millions of Cupids.

I worship Govinda, the primeval Lord, round whose neck is swinging a garland of flowers beautified with the moon-locket, whose two hands are adorned with the flute and jeweled ornaments, who always revels in pastimes of love, whose graceful threefold-bending form of Śyāmasundara is eternally manifest.

I worship Govinda, the primeval Lord, whose transcendental form is full of bliss, truth, substantiality and is thus full of the most dazzling splendor. Each of the limbs of that transcendental figure possesses in Himself, the full-fledged functions of all the organs, and eternally sees, maintains and manifests the infinite universes, both spiritual and mundane.

I worship Govinda, the primeval Lord, who is inaccessible to the Vedas, but obtainable by pure unalloyed devotion of the soul, who is without a second, who is not subject to decay, is without a beginning, whose form is endless, who is the beginning, and the eternal puruṣa; yet He is a person possessing the beauty of blooming youth.

I worship Govinda, the primeval Lord, only the tip of the toe of whose lotus feet is approached by the yogīs who aspire after the transcendental and betake themselves to prāṇāyāma by drilling the respiration; or by the jñānīs who try to find out the nondifferentiated Brahman by the process of elimination of the mundane, extending over thousands of millions of years.

He is an undifferentiated entity as there is no distinction between potency and the possessor thereof. In His work of creation of millions of worlds, His potency remains inseparable. All the universes exist in Him and He is present in His fullness in every one of the atoms that are scattered throughout the universe, at one and the same time. Such is the primeval Lord whom I adore.

I adore the same Govinda, the primeval Lord, in whose praise men, who are imbued with devotion, sing the mantra-sūktas told by the Vedas, by gaining their appropriate beauty, greatness, thrones, conveyances and ornaments.

I worship Govinda, the primeval Lord, residing in His own realm, Goloka, with Rādhā, resembling His own spiritual figure, the embodiment of the ecstatic potency possessed of the sixty-four artistic activities, in the company of Her confidantes [sakhīs], embodiments of the extensions of Her bodily form, permeated and vitalized by His ever-blissful spiritual rasa.

I worship Govinda, the primeval Lord, who is Śyāmasundara, Kṛṣṇa Himself with inconceivable innumerable attributes, whom the pure devotees see in their heart of hearts with the eye of devotion tinged with the salve of love.

I worship Govinda, the primeval Lord, who manifested Himself personally as Kṛṣṇa and the different avatāras in the world in the forms of Rāma, Nṛsiṁha, Vāmana, etc., as His subjective portions.

I worship Govinda, the primeval Lord, whose effulgence is the source of the nondifferentiated Brahman mentioned in the Upaniṣads, being differentiated from the infinity of glories of the mundane universe appears as the indivisible, infinite, limitless, truth.

I worship Govinda, the primeval Lord, who is the absolute substantive principle being the ultimate entity in the form of the support of all existence whose external potency embodies the threefold mundane qualities, viz., sattva, rajas, and tamas and diffuses the Vedic knowledge regarding the mundane world.

I worship Govinda, the primeval Lord, whose glory ever triumphantly dominates the mundane world by the activity of His own pastimes, being reflected in the mind of recollecting souls as the transcendental entity of ever-blissful cognitive rasa.

Lowest of all is located Devī-dhāma [mundane world], next above it is Maheśa-dhāma [abode of Maheśa]; above Maheśa-dhāma is placed Hari-dhāma [abode of Hari] and above them all is located Kṛṣṇa's own realm named Goloka. I adore the primeval Lord Govinda, who has allotted their respective authorities to the rulers of those graded realms.

The external potency Māyā who is of the nature of the shadow of the cit potency, is worshiped by all people as Durgā, the creating, preserving and destroying agency of this mundane world. I adore the primeval Lord Govinda in accordance with whose will Durgā conducts herself.

Just as milk is transformed into curd by the action of acids, but yet the effect curd is neither same as, nor different from, its cause, viz., milk, so I adore the primeval Lord Govinda of whom the state of Śambhu is a transformation for the performance of the work of destruction.

The light of one candle being communicated to other candles, although it burns separately in them, is the same in its quality. I adore the primeval Lord Govinda who exhibits Himself equally in the same mobile manner in His various manifestations.

I adore the primeval Lord Govinda who assuming His own great subjective form, who bears the name of Śeṣa, replete with the all-accommodating potency, and reposing in the Causal Ocean with the infinity of the world in the pores of His hair, enjoys creative sleep [yoga-nidrā].

Brahmā and other lords of the mundane worlds, appearing from the pores of hair of Mahā-Viṣṇu, remain alive as long as the duration of one exhalation of the latter [Mahā-Viṣṇu]. I adore the primeval Lord Govinda of whose subjective personality Mahā-Viṣṇu is the portion of portion.

I adore the primeval Lord Govinda from whom the separated subjective portion Brahmā receives his power for the regulation of the mundane world, just as the sun manifests some portion of his own light in all the effulgent gems that bear the names of sūryakānta, etc.

I adore the primeval Lord Govinda, whose lotus feet are always held by Gaṇeśa upon the pair of tumuli protruding from his elephant head in order to obtain power for his function of destroying all the obstacles on the path of progress of the three worlds.

The three worlds are composed of the nine elements, viz., fire, earth, ether, water, air, direction, time, soul and mind. I adore the primeval Lord Govinda from whom they originate, in whom they exist and into whom they enter at the time of the universal cataclysm.

The sun who is the king of all the planets, full of infinite effulgence, the image of the good soul, is as the eye of this world. I adore the primeval Lord Govinda in pursuance of whose order the sun performs his journey mounting the wheel of time.

I adore the primeval Lord Govinda, by whose conferred power are maintained the manifested potencies, that are found to exist, of all virtues, all vices, the Vedas, the penances and all jīvas, from Brahmā to the meanest insect.

I adore the primeval Lord Govinda, who burns up to their roots all fruitive activities of those who are imbued with devotion and impartially ordains for each the due enjoyment of the fruits of one's activities, of all those who walk in the path of work, in accordance with the chain of their previously performed works, no less in the case of the tiny insect that bears the name of indragopa than in that of Indra, king of the devas.

I adore the primeval Lord Govinda, the meditators of whom, by meditating upon Him under the sway of wrath, amorous passion, natural friendly love, fear, parental affection, delusion, reverence and willing service, attain to bodily forms befitting the nature of their contemplation.

I worship that transcendental seat, known as Śvetadvīpa where as loving consorts the Lakṣmīs in their unalloyed spiritual essence practice the amorous service of the Supreme Lord Kṛṣṇa as their only lover; where every tree is a transcendental purpose tree; where the soil is the purpose gem, all water is nectar, every word is a song, every gait is a dance, the flute is the favorite attendant, effulgence is full of transcendental bliss and the supreme spiritual entities are all enjoyable and tasty, where numberless milk cows always emit transcendental oceans of milk; where there is eternal existence of transcendental time, who is ever present and without past or future and hence is not subject to the quality of passing away even for the space of half a moment. That realm is known as Goloka only to a very few self-realized souls in this world.

IDX.first_line: cintamanimaya radha kunda tata
IDX.search: cintamanimaya radha kunda tata tahe kunja sata sata pravala vidruma maya tarulata muktaphale avanata svananda sukhada kunja manohara tahate kutira sobhe basiya tathaya gaba krsnanama kabe krsnadasya lobhe
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
cintāmaṇimaya, rādhā⋅kuṇḍa taṭa,
tāhe kuñja śata śata
pravāla⋅vidruma⋅ maya taru-latā,
muktā-phale avanata

svānanda⋅sukhada⋅ kuñja manohara,
tāhāte kuṭira śobhe
basiyā tathāya, gā’ba kṛṣṇa-nāma,
kabe kṛṣṇa-dāsya lobhe

emana samaya, muralīra gāna,
paśibe e dāsī⋅kāne
ānande mātiba, sakala bhuliba,
śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅vaṁśīra gāne

rādhe rādhe bali’, muralī ḍākibe,
madīya īśvarī⋅nāma
śuniyā camaki’, uṭhibe e dāsī,
kemane karibe prāṇa
IDX.en_translation:
The bank of Rādhā-kuṇḍa is made of wish-fulfilling jewels, with many hundreds of groves there. The trees and creepers are made of coral and red coral, bent low with pearls.

A small cottage is beautifully situated within the enchanting grove known as Svānanda-sukhada-kuñja. Sitting there, when will I sing Kṛṣṇa's name, longing for His service?

At such a time, the song of the flute will enter the ears of this maidservant. I will become ecstatic with joy and forget everything by the song of Śrī Kṛṣṇa's flute.

Saying "Rādhe Rādhe," the flute will call the name of my Queen. Hearing this, this maidservant will be startled and stand up; how then will I sustain my life?

IDX.first_line: ciradine goracadera ananda apara
IDX.search: ciradine goracadera ananda apara kahaye bhakatagane purava vihara palake purala tanu apada mastaka sonara kesara janu kadambakoraka
IDX.author: kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa
IDX.verses:
ciradine goracā̐dera ānanda apāra
kahaye bhakatagaṇe pūrava vihāra

palake pūrala tanu āpāda⋅mastaka
sonāra keśara janu kadambakoraka

bhāve bharala mana gadagada bhāṣa
aneka jatane vihi pūrāyala āśa

śacīra nandana gorā jāti prāṇadhana
śuni cā̐damukhera kathā juḍāila mana

gorācā̐dera līlāya jāra hoila viśvāsa
dukhī kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa tāra dāsa anudāsa
IDX.en_translation:
Forever, Lord Gorācāndra's joy is boundless. He speaks to His devotees about His previous pastimes.

Instantly His body was filled with emotion from head to toe, as if golden filaments were blooming like a Kadamba flower bud.

His heart was filled with divine emotion and His speech was choked. With great care, destiny fulfilled His hopes.

The golden Lord, the Son of Mother Śacī, is the life's treasure of His community. Hearing the words from His moon-like face, the heart is soothed.

Whoever has faith in Lord Gorācāndra's pastimes—humble Kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa is a servant of their servants.

IDX.first_line: ciramuktaganadrta kamyadhanam
IDX.search: ciramuktaganadrta kamyadhanam dhanadepsita vandita kalpatarum tarurajita cinmaya dhama caram pranamami ha kesava puta padam kuliyaiva varaha sudhamavaram varadayaka deva vikasa krtam krtadosa samuha tamoharanam pranamami ha kesava puta padam
IDX.author: śrīmad⋅bhaktivedānta ūrdhvanmahī mahārāja
IDX.verses:
ciramuktagaṇādṛta⋅kāmyadhanaṁ
dhanadepsita⋅vandita⋅kalpatarum
tarurājita⋅cinmaya⋅dhāma⋅caraṁ
praṇamāmi ha keśava⋅pūta⋅padam

kuliyaiva⋅varāha⋅sudhāmavaraṁ
varadāyaka⋅deva⋅vikāśa⋅kṛtam
kṛtadoṣa⋅samūha⋅tamoharaṇaṁ
praṇamāmi ha keśava⋅pūta⋅padam

naṭana⋅priya⋅bhāva⋅kalādruciraṁ
ciradhāma⋅virājita⋅nitya⋅prabhum
prabhupāda⋅rasāvdhi⋅kṛtīratanaṁ
praṇamāmi ha keśava⋅pūta⋅padam

raghunātha⋅nibhaiva⋅virāgaparaṁ
paramojjvala⋅rāga⋅sumūrtisuram
suranandita⋅tarpita⋅deva⋅varam
praṇamāmi ha keśava⋅pūta⋅padam

prabhupāda⋅manogata⋅bhāvadharaṁ
dharaṇī⋅jaḍaraṅga⋅vihīna⋅naram
nararūpa⋅vilāsa⋅vibhāva⋅mayaṁ
praṇamāmi ha keśava⋅pūta⋅padam

praṇatābhaya⋅dāyaka⋅tīrthapadaṁ
padasaṁśrita⋅dīna⋅samuttaraṇam
taraṇonmukha⋅jīva⋅bhavāpagamaṁ
praṇamāmi ha keśava⋅pūta⋅padam

pitṛbhāva⋅parāyaṇa⋅śiṣyagatiṁ
gatimukti⋅vidhāyaka⋅śānta⋅varam
varaṇāgata⋅durmati⋅śāntapadaṁ
praṇamāmi ha keśava⋅pūta⋅padam

nigamānta⋅sabhā⋅navakīrtidharaṁ
dharaṇījana⋅tāraka⋅gauraparam
parasevya⋅padābja⋅rajastamahaṁ
praṇamāmi ha keśava⋅pūta⋅padam
IDX.en_translation:
I offer my obeisances to the sacred feet of Kesava, which are the desired treasure of the eternally liberated souls, the wish-fulfilling tree worshiped by the giver of wealth (Kubera), and which wander in the spiritual abode adorned with trees.

I offer my obeisances to the sacred feet of Kesava, which revealed the excellent abode of Kuliya (Navadvipa) like the boar incarnation, and which destroyed the darkness of the mass of accumulated sins.

I offer my obeisances to the sacred feet of Kesava, which are beautiful with the art of loving emotions fond of dancing, which are the eternal Lord shining in the eternal abode, and which are the jewel of accomplishment in the ocean of Prabhupāda's mellows.

I offer my obeisances to the sacred feet of Kesava, which are devoted to renunciation like Raghunatha Dasa, which are the divine form of the supreme blazing attachment (ujjvala-raga), and which are the best of gods, pleasing and satisfying the surfactant gods.

I offer my obeisances to the sacred feet of Kesava, which hold the internal mood of Prabhupāda, which are a human form devoid of the dull enjoyments of the earth, and which are full of the ecstatic emotions of the pastimes of the human-like form.

I offer my obeisances to the sacred feet of Kesava, whose feet are a holy place giving fearlessness to the surrendered, which deliver the fallen souls who have taken shelter of them, and which remove the material existence of the living entities eager to cross over.

I offer my obeisances to the sacred feet of Kesava, who is the shelter of the disciples devoted to the mood of their father (guru), who is the best of the peaceful, arranging for their destination and liberation, and whose feet are the auspicious destination for the wicked who have surrendered.

I offer my obeisances to the sacred feet of Kesava, who holds new fame in the assembly of vedanta, who is devoted to Lord Gaura, the deliverer of the people of the earth. I bow to the dust of his lotus feet, which are worshipable by others.

IDX.first_line: citkana jiva krsna cinmaya bhaskara
IDX.search: citkana jiva krsna cinmaya bhaskara nitya krsna dekhi krsne karena adara krsna bahir mukha hoo bhoga vancha kare nikatastha maya tare japatiya dhare
IDX.verses:
citkaṇa jīva, kṛṣṇa cinmaya bhāskara
nitya kṛṣṇa dekhi’ kṛṣṇe karena ādara

kṛṣṇa bahir⋅mukha hoo bhoga⋅vāñchā kare
nikaṭastha māyā tāre jāpaṭiyā dhare

piśācī pāile jena mati⋅cchanna haya
māyā⋅grasta jīvera haya se⋅bhāva udaya

‘āmi siddha kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa’⋅ei kathā bhule’
māyāra naphara hoo cira⋅dina bule

kabhu rājā, kabhu prajā, kabhu vipra, śūdra
kabhu duḥkhī, kabhu sukhī, kabhu kīṭa kṣudra

kabhu svarge, kabhu martye, narake vā kabhu
kabhu deva, kabhu daitya, kabhu dāsa prabhu

ei⋅rūpe saṁsāra bhramite kona⋅jana
sādhu⋅saṅge nija⋅tattva avagata hana

nija⋅tattva jāni’ āra saṁsāra nā cāya
‘kena vā bhajinu māyā’⋅kare hāya hāya

ke̐de bale,⋅“ohe kṛṣṇa, āmi tava dāsa
tomāra caraṇa chāḍi’ haila sarvanāśa”

kākuti kariyā kṛṣṇe jadi ḍāke eka⋅bāra
kṛpā kari’ kṛṣṇa tā’re chāḍāna saṁsāra

māyāke pichane rākhi’ kṛṣṇa⋅pāne cāya
bhajite bhajite kṛṣṇa⋅pāda⋅padma pāya

kṛṣṇa tā’re dena nija cicchaktira bala
māyā ākarṣaṇa chāḍe hoiyā durbala

‘sādhu⋅saṅge kṛṣṇa⋅nāma’⋅ei⋅mātra cāi
saṁsāra jinite āra kona vastu nāi
IDX.en_translation:
The jīva is a particle of consciousness; Kṛṣṇa is the sun of consciousness. Seeing Kṛṣṇa eternally, he shows affection to Kṛṣṇa.

Turning away from Kṛṣṇa, desiring enjoyment, the Māyā nearby catches him and holds him tight.

Just as one's intelligence becomes covered when possessed by a ghost, that state arises in the jīva gripped by Māyā.

Forgetting the fact "I am an eternal servant of Kṛṣṇa," he becomes a slave of Māyā and wanders forever.

Sometimes a king, sometimes a subject, sometimes a brāhmaṇa, a śūdra. Sometimes unhappy, sometimes happy, sometimes a small insect.

Sometimes in heaven, sometimes in the mortal world, or sometimes in hell. Sometimes a god, sometimes a demon, sometimes a servant, a master.

Wandering in the material world in this way, some person becomes aware of his own truth in the association of a saint.

Knowing his own truth, he no longer wants the material world. He laments, "Alas, alas! Why did I worship Māyā?"

Weeping, he says: "O Kṛṣṇa, I am Your servant. Leaving Your feet, I have been ruined."

If he calls Kṛṣṇa once with piteous words, Kṛṣṇa mercifully releases him from the material world.

Keeping Māyā behind, he looks towards Kṛṣṇa. Worshipping and worshipping, he attains Kṛṣṇa's lotus feet.

Kṛṣṇa gives him the strength of His own spiritual energy. Māyā, becoming weak, lets go of her attraction.

'Kṛṣṇa-nāma in the association of saints'—this is all I want. There is no other object to conquer the material world.

IDX.first_line: darsana aslesanvita anukulye sevasrita
IDX.search: darsana aslesanvita anukulye sevasrita ullase arudha jei bhava juvadvandva hrdimajhe rasakare suviraje sambhogakhya tara haya labha mukhya gauna dviprakara sambhogera suvistara tadubhaya cariti prakara saŋksipta saŋkirna jana sampanna samrddhimana purvabhavavastha anusara
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
darśana⋅āśleṣānvita, ānukūlye sevāśrita,
ullāse ārūḍha jei bhāva
juvadvandva⋅hṛdimājhe, rasākāre suvirāje,
sambhogākhyā tā’ra haya lābha

mukhya, gauṇa⋅dviprakāra, sambhogera suvistāra,
tadubhaya cāriṭī prakāra
‘saŋkṣipta’ ‘saŋkīrṇa’ jāna, ‘sampanna’ ‘samṛddhimāna’,
pūrvabhāvāvasthā anusāra

pūrva-rāgāntare jā̐hā, ‘saŋkṣipta’ sambhoga tā̐hā,
mānāntare ‘saṅkīrṇa’ pramāṇe
kṣudra pravāsāvasāne, ‘sampanna’, ‘samṛddhimāne’⋅,
sudūra pravāsa⋅avasāne

sampanna dvi-vidha bhāva, āgati o prādurbhāva,
manohara sambhoga tāhāya
svapne ai saba bhāva, jabe haya āvirbhāva,
tabe gauṇa⋅sambhoga jānāya
IDX.en_translation:
That emotion which climbs to great joy, accompanied by vision and embrace, sheltered by favorable service, and which reigns beautifully in the form of rasa in the hearts of the Youthful Couple, attains the name 'Sambhoga' (Union).

Union is of two kinds: primary and secondary. Its expansion is vast. Know both of them to be of four kinds: 'brief' (saṁkṣipta), 'narrow' (saṅkīrṇa), 'accomplished' (sampanna), and 'enriched' (samṛddhimān), according to the previous emotional state.

Where it follows pūrva-rāga, it is 'brief' union. After māna, it is known as 'narrow'. After a short separation, it is 'accomplished'; and after a very long separation, it is 'enriched'.

The 'accomplished' state is of two kinds: arrival and manifestation. There is enchanting union in that. When all these emotions appear in a dream, it indicates secondary union.

IDX.first_line: davanala sama samsara dahane
IDX.search: davanala sama samsara dahane dagdha jivakula uddhara karane karuna varida krpavari dane vandi gunasindhu gurura carana kamala nrtya gita vadya sriharikirtane rahena magana mahamatta mane romanca kampasru haya gaurapreme vandi sei gurura carana kamala
IDX.author: śrīmadbhaktibibeka bhāratī gosvāmī mahārāja
IDX.verses:
dāvānala⋅sama saṁsāra⋅dahane
dagdha⋅jīvakula⋅uddhāra kāraṇe,
karuṇā⋅vārida kṛpāvāri⋅dāne
(vandi) guṇasindhu gurura caraṇa⋅kamala

nṛtya⋅gīta⋅vādya⋅śrī-harikīrtane
rahena magana mahāmatta mane,
romāñca kampāśru haya gaura-preme
vandi sei gurura caraṇa⋅kamala

sadā rata jini vigraha⋅sevane
śṛṅgārādi āra mandira⋅mārjane,
karena niyukta anugatajane
vandi sei gurura caraṇa⋅kamala

carvya⋅cuṣya⋅lehya⋅peya⋅rasamaya
prasādānna kṛṣṇera ati svādu haya,
bhakta⋅āsvādane nija tṛpta raya
vandi sei gurura caraṇa⋅kamala

śrī⋅rādhā⋅mādhava⋅nāma⋅rūpa⋅guṇe
ananta mādhurya⋅līlā⋅āsvādane,
lubdha citta jini hana pratikṣaṇe
vandi sei gurura caraṇa⋅kamala

vrajayuvadvandva⋅rati⋅samvardhane
jukti kare sakhī-gaṇe vṛndāvane,
ati dakṣa tāhe, priyatama-gaṇe
vandi sei gurura caraṇa⋅kamala

sarva-śāstre gāya śrī-harira svarūpa
bhakta-gaṇa bhāve sei anurūpa,
kintu jini prabhu⋅priyatama⋅rūpa
vandi sei gurura caraṇa⋅kamala

jā̐hāra prasāde kṛṣṇa-kṛpā pāi
jā̐’ra aprasāde anya gati nāi,
tri⋅sandhyā kīrtira stava dhyāne bhāi
vandi sei gurura caraṇa⋅kamala

gurudevāṣṭaka ati jatnakari’
brāhma⋅muhūrte paḍe ucca kari’
vṛndāvana⋅nātha sākṣāt śrī-hari
sevā pāya sei vastusiddhi⋅kāle
IDX.en_translation:
In this material world which is burning like a forest fire, I worship the lotus feet of the spiritual master—an ocean of good qualities who bestows the water of mercy like a cloud of compassion for the deliverance of the suffering souls.

He remains deeply immersed in the congregational chanting of the Lord's names with dance, song, and musical instruments. Thrills of ecstasy, trembling, and tears manifest on his body due to love for Lord Caitanya. I worship those lotus feet of the spiritual master.

Always engaged in the worship of the Deity, in decorating Him and cleaning the temple, he engages his obedient followers in these services. I worship those lotus feet of the spiritual master.

The delicious foods offered to Kṛṣṇa—to be chewed, sucked, licked, and drunk—are extremely palatable. By tasting them, the spiritual master remains satisfied. I worship those lotus feet of the spiritual master.

The spiritual master's mind is always greedy to relish the names, forms, and qualities of Śrī Rādhā and Mādhava and Their endless sweet pastimes. I worship those lotus feet of the spiritual master.

To increase the loving affairs of the youthful Divine Couple, he devises means with the companions of Rādhā in Vṛndāvana. He is very expert in this among the dearmost ones. I worship those lotus feet of the spiritual master.

All scriptures sing of the true nature of the Lord, and the devotees meditate on Him accordingly. But the spiritual master is the most beloved form of the Lord. I worship those lotus feet of the spiritual master.

By his grace we obtain Kṛṣṇa's mercy; without his grace, there is no other path. O brother, meditate on his glories three times daily. I worship those lotus feet of the spiritual master.

This prayer glorifying the spiritual master should be recited with great care and chanted loudly during the auspicious hour before dawn. Thus one obtains the service of Śrī Hari, the Lord of Vṛndāvana, at the time of attaining the ultimate goal.

IDX.first_line: dayala nitai caitanya bale nac re amara mana
IDX.search: dayala nitai caitanya bale nac re amara mana nac re amara mana nac re amara mana emana dayala to nai he mara kheye prema deya ore aparadha dure jabe pabe premadhana o name aparadha vicara to nai he takhana krsna name ruci habe ghucibe bandhana
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
‘dayāla nitāi caitanya’ ba’le nāc re āmāra mana
nāc re āmāra mana, nāc re āmāra mana

(emana dayāla to nāi he, māra khe’ye prema deya)
(ore) aparādha dūre jā’be, pā’be premadhana
(o nāme aparādha⋅vicāra to nāi he)
(takhana) kṛṣṇa⋅nāme ruci ha’be, ghucibe bandhana

(kṛṣṇa⋅nāme anurāga to ha’be he)
(takhana) anāyāse saphala ha’be jīvera jīvana
(kṛṣṇarati vinā jīvana to miche he)
(śeṣe) vṛndāvane rādhāśyāme pā’be daraśana
(gaura⋅kṛpā ha’le he)
IDX.en_translation:
My mind, dance by chanting ‘Dayāl Nitāi Caitanya’. Dance, oh my mind, dance, oh my mind.

(Oh, there are none so merciful; even after being struck, they bestow divine love). (Oh,) all offenses will depart, you will attain the treasure of divine love. (Oh, in these names, there is no consideration of offenses). (Then) a taste for Kṛṣṇa's name will awaken, and bondage will be broken.

(Oh, then attachment to Kṛṣṇa’s name will surely blossom). (Then) the life of the jīva (living entity) will effortlessly become successful. (Oh, without Kṛṣṇa-rati (love for God), life is certainly meaningless). (Finally) in Vṛndāvana, you will attain darśan (vision) of Rādhāśyāma (Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa, the divine couple). (Oh, if the kṛpā (mercy) of Gaura is attained).

IDX.first_line: dekha nitaicadera madhuri
IDX.search: dekha nitaicadera madhuri pulake purala tanu kadamba kesara janu bahu tuli bale hari hari srimukhamandala dhama jini kata kotikama se na vidhi kise niramila mathiya lavanya sindhu tahe ningadiya indu sudha diya mukhani gadila
IDX.author: śrīla locana⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
dekha nitāicā̐dera mādhurī
pulake pūrala tanu, kadamba keśara janu,
bāhu tuli’ bale hari hari

śrīmukhamaṇḍala⋅dhāma, jini kata koṭīkāma,
se nā vidhi kise niramila
mathiyā lāvaṇya⋅sindhu, tāhe niṅgāḍiyā indu,
sudhā diyā mu’khāni gaḍila

nava kañjadala ā̐khi, tāraka bhramara pākhī,
ḍubi’ rahu prema⋅makarande
se⋅rūpa dekhila jeha, se jānila rasa-meha,
avanī bhāsala premānande

purave je vrajapure, vihare nandera ghare,
rohiṇī⋅nandana balarāma
ebe padmāvatī⋅suta, nityānanda avadhūta,
bhuvana⋅pāvana haila nāma

se pa̐hu patita heri’, karuṇāya avatari’,
jīvere bolāya gaura⋅hari
paḍiyā se bhavabandhe, kā̐daye locana andhe,
nā dekhiyā se rūpa⋅mādhurī
IDX.en_translation:
Behold the sweetness of Nitāicāñda! His body is covered with horripilation like the filaments of a kadamba flower. Raising His arms, He chants "Hari! Hari!"

The abode of His beautiful face conquers millions of Cupids. How did the Creator fashion it? Churning the ocean of beauty, extracting the moon from it, and adding nectar, He molded that face.

His eyes are like fresh lotus petals, and His pupils are like bumblebees, remaining immersed in the honey of divine love. Whoever sees that beauty knows the cloud of rasa. The earth floats in the bliss of divine love.

He who previously in the town of Vraja sported in the house of Nanda as Balarāma, the Son of Rohiṇī—now He is the Son of Padmāvatī, the Avadhūta Nityānanda, and His Name has become "the Savior of the World."

Seeing the fallen souls, that Lord descended out of mercy and induces the living entities to chant "Gaura⋅hari!" Fallen in the bondage of material existence, the blind Locana weeps, not seeing that sweet beauty.

IDX.first_line: dekhata jhulata gaura candra
IDX.search: dekhata jhulata gaura candra aparupa dvijamaniya vidhira avadhi rupa nirupama kasita kancana jiniya jhulaota kata bhaktavrnda gaura candra vediya ananda saghana jaya jaya rava uthale nagara nadiya
IDX.author: vasudeva ghoṣa
IDX.verses: dekhata jhulata, gaura⋅candra, aparūpa dvijamaṇiyā vidhira avadhi, rūpa nirupama, kaṣita kāñcana jiniyā jhulāota kata, bhaktavṛnda, gaura⋅candra veḍiyā ānanda saghana, jaya jaya rava, uthale nagara nadīyā nayana kamala, mukha niramala, śarada cāñda jiniyā nagarera loka, dhāya eka⋅mukha, hari hari dhvani śuniyā dhanya kaliyuga, gorā avatāra, suradhanī dhani dhaniyā gorācāñda vina, āna nāhi māne, vāsughoṣa kahe jāniyā
IDX.en_translation:
Behold Lord Gaura⋅candra swinging on the swing! He is the incomparable jewel of the twice-born. His beauty is the ultimate limit of creation and is beyond comparison, surpassing even the purest gold.

Many devotees surround and swing the golden Lord. Intense joy and sounds of "Victory! Victory!" overflow in the town of Nadīyā.

His lotus-like eyes and pure face surpass the beauty of the autumn moon. All the residents of the town run in one direction, hearing the sound of "Hari! Hari!"

Blessed is the Age of Kali because of the incarnation of the golden Lord. Those who possess Him are the wealthiest of all. Without the golden Lord, they accept no one else—Vāsudeva Ghoṣa says this, knowing it well.

IDX.first_line: dekhite dekhite bhuliba va kabe
IDX.search: dekhite dekhite bhuliba va kabe nija sthula paricaya nayane heriba vrajapura sobha nitya cidanandamaya vrsabhanupure janama loiba javate vivaha habe vrajagopi bhava hoibe svabhava ana bhava na rahibe
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
dekhite dekhite, bhuliba vā kabe,
nija⋅sthūla⋅paricaya
nayane heriba, vrajapura⋅śobhā,
nitya cidānanda-maya

vṛṣabhānu-pure, janama loiba,
jāvaṭe vivāha ha’be
vrajagopī⋅bhāva, hoibe svabhāva,
āna⋅bhāva nā rahibe

nija siddhadeha, nija siddhanāma,
nija⋅rūpa⋅svavasana
rādhākṛpā⋅bale, labhiba vā kabe,
kṛṣṇaprema⋅prakaraṇa

jāmuna⋅salila, āharaṇe giyā,
bujhiba jugala⋅rasa
prema-mugdha ha’ye, pāgalinī⋅prāya,
gāiba rādhāra jaśa
IDX.translation_intro: From The Harmonist, April 1932. Edited by Bhaktisiddhanta Sarasvati.
IDX.en_translation:
And by me unawares When shall I forget The memory of my gross self? Shall behold with my eyes The beauty of the realm of Braja Full of the eternal spiritual bliss?

In Brishabhanu's town When shall I have my birth, Shall be married in Yabata? The mood of the milkmaid of Braja Will be my nature, And there be no other disposition?

My own realised body, My own realised name, My own form, my own apparel, By the power of Radha's mercy, O when shall I gain? And initiation into the love of Krishna?

Going out to fetch The water of the Yamuna Shall understand the love of the Divine pair? And smitten by love, Like a distracted maiden, Shall sing the praise of Radha?

IDX.first_line: deva deva deva krsna dinabandhu pahi mam
IDX.search: deva deva deva krsna dinabandhu pahi mam nila megha syama krsna nitya mukta raksa mam nanda nandana mukundarama ramakrsna pahi mam
IDX.verses:
deva deva deva kṛṣṇa, dīnabandhu pāhi mām
nīla megha śyāma kṛṣṇa, nitya mukta rakṣa mām

nanda⋅nandana mukundarāma, rāmakṛṣṇa pāhi mām

veṇu gāna lola kṛṣṇa, vimala lola pāhi mām
viśvarūpa vāsudeva, vīra rāma rakṣa mām

putanānāśa hare kṛṣṇa, bhuvananātha pāhi mām
puṇḍarīkākṣa nayana kṛṣṇa, pūrṇarūpa rakṣa mām
IDX.en_translation:
O Lord Kṛṣṇa! O Friend of the distressed! Please protect me. O Kṛṣṇa, dark like a blue cloud! O eternally liberated Lord! Please save me.

O Son of Nanda! O Giver of liberation! O Rāma and Kṛṣṇa! Please protect me.

O Kṛṣṇa, enchanted by the song of the flute! O pure and playful Lord! Please protect me. O Lord of the universal form! O Son of Vasudeva! O heroic Lord Rāma! Please save me.

O Destroyer of Pūtanā! O Hare Kṛṣṇa! O Lord of the universe! Please protect me. O lotus-eyed Lord Kṛṣṇa! O perfect form! Please save me.

IDX.first_line: devadideva gaura candra gauridasa mandirenityananda sange gaura ambikate vihare
IDX.search: devadideva gaura candra gauridasa mandirenityananda sange gaura ambikate vihare caru aruna gunjahara hrtkamale je dharevirinci sevya pada padma laksmi sevya sadare
IDX.author: gaurīdāsa paṇḍita
IDX.verses:
devādideva gaura⋅candra gaurīdāsa⋅mandirenityānanda⋅saṅge gaura ambikāte vihare

cāru⋅aruṇa⋅guñjāhāra hṛtkamale je dhareviriñci⋅sevya⋅pāda⋅padma lakṣmī⋅sevya sādare

taptahema⋅aṅgakānti prātaḥ⋅aruṇa⋅ambarerādhikānurāga prema⋅bhakti vāñchā je kare

śacī⋅suta gaura⋅candra ānandita antarepāṣaṇḍa⋅khaṇḍa nityānanda saṅge raṅge vihare

nityānanda gaura⋅candra gaurīdāsa⋅mandiregaurīdāsa karata āśa sarva-jīva uddhāre
IDX.en_translation:
The Supreme Lord Gaura⋅candra is in the temple of Gaurīdāsa Paṇḍita. Gaura enjoys pastimes in Ambikā with Nityānanda.

He who wears a beautiful red seed garland on His lotus heart has lotus feet that are lovingly served by Brahmā and the goddess of fortune.

His bodily effulgence is like molten gold, like the reddish glow of the morning; He desires the loving attachment of Rādhikā and loving devotional service.

The son of Śacī, Gaura⋅candra, is joyful in His heart. He sports merrily with Nityānanda, the vanquisher of all heresies.

Nityānanda and Gaura⋅candra are in the temple of Gaurīdāsa Paṇḍita, who hopes for the deliverance of all living entities.

IDX.first_line: devaloka pitrloka paya tara maha sukha
IDX.search: devaloka pitrloka paya tara maha sukha sadhu sadhu bale anuksana jugala bhajaye jara premanande bhase tara tandera nichani tri bhuvana prthak ayasa yoge duhkha maya visaya bhoge vraje vasa govinda sevana krsna katha krsna nama satya satya rasa dhama vraja jane sange anuksana
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses: devaloka pitṛloka⋅pāya tāra mahā⋅sukha sādhu sādhu bale anukṣaṇa jugala bhajaye jara⋅premānande bhāse tāra tāṇdera nichani tri⋅bhuvana pṛthak āyāsa⋅yoge⋅duḥkha⋅maya⋅viṣaya⋅bhoge vraje vāsa govinda⋅sevana kṛṣṇa⋅kathā kṛṣṇa⋅nāma⋅satya⋅satya rasa⋅dhāma vraja⋅jane⋅saṅge anukṣaṇa sadā sevā⋅abhilāṣa⋅manete kari’ viśvāsa sadā⋅kāla haiya nirbhaya narottama⋅dāsa bole paḍinu asat⋅bhole paritrāṇa kara mahāśaya
IDX.en_translation:
Those who attain the heavenly planets and the world of the forefathers find great happiness there. They always exclaim, "Excellent, excellent!" But for those who worship the Divine Couple and float in the ecstasy of Their love, the three worlds are insignificant in comparison.

Abandoning separate endeavors for sense enjoyment, which is full of misery, one should reside in Vraja and serve Govinda. Topics of Kṛṣṇa and the Holy Name of Kṛṣṇa are truly, truly the abode of nectar, always to be enjoyed in the company of the residents of Vraja.

Always maintaining the desire for service and keeping faith in the mind, one becomes fearless at all times. Narottama Dāsa says, "I have fallen, bewildered by illusion. Please deliver me, O great soul."

IDX.first_line: devi suresvari bhagavati gange
IDX.search: devi suresvari bhagavati gange tri bhuvana tarini tarala tarange sankara mauli nivasini vimale mama matirastam tava pada kamale bhagirathi sukha dayini matas tava jala mahima nigame khyatah naham jane tava mahimanam trahi krpamayi mamajnanam
IDX.author: śrī⋅śaṅkarācārya
IDX.verses:
devi! sureśvari! bhagavati! gaṅge!
tri⋅bhuvana⋅tāriṇi! tarala⋅taraṅge
śaṅkara⋅mauli⋅nivāsini! vimale!
mama matirāstāṁ tava pada⋅kamale 

bhāgīrathi! sukha⋅dāyini! mātas⋅
tava jala⋅mahimā nigame⋅khyātaḥ
nāhaṁ jāne tava mahimānaṁ
trāhi kṛpāmayi! māmajñānam 

hari⋅pāda⋅padma⋅vihāriṇi! gaṅge!
hima⋅vidhu⋅muktā⋅dhavala⋅taraṅge !
dūrīkuru mama duṣkṛti⋅bhāraṁ
kuru kṛpayā bhava⋅sāgara⋅pāram 

tava jalamamalaṁ jena nipītaṁ
parama⋅padaṁ khalu tena gṛhītam
mātargaṅge! tvayi jo bhaktaḥ
kila taṁ draṣṭuṁ na jamaḥ śaktaḥ 

patitoddhāriṇi! jāhnavi! gaṅge!
khaṇḍita⋅girivara⋅maṇḍita⋅bhaṅge !
bhīṣma⋅janani! khalu muni⋅vara⋅kanye!
patitoddhāriṇi! tri⋅bhuvana⋅dhanye! 

kalpalatāmiva phaladāṁ loke
praṇamati jastvāṁ na patati śoke
pārāvāra⋅vihāriṇi! gaṅge!
vibudha⋅vādhu⋅kṛta⋅taralāpāṅge! 

tava kṛpayā cet srotaḥsnātaḥ
punarapi jaṭhare so‛pi na jātaḥ
naraka⋅nivāriṇi! jāhnavi! gaṅge!
kaluṣa⋅vināśini! mahimottuṅge! 

parilasadaṅge! puṇya⋅taraṅge!
jaya jaya jāhnavi! karuṇāpāṅge !
indra⋅mukuṭamaṇi⋅rājita⋅caraṇe!
sukhade! śubhade! sevaka⋅śaraṇe! 

rogaṁ śokaṁ tāpaṁ pāpaṁ
hara me bhagavati! kumati⋅kalāpam
tribhuvana⋅sāre! vasudhā⋅hāre!
tvamasi gatirmama khalu saṁsāre 

alakānande! paramānande!
kuru mayi karuṇāṁ kātara⋅vandye !
tava taṭa⋅nikaṭe jasya nivāsaḥ
khalu vaikuṇṭhe tasya nivāsaḥ

varamiha nīre kamaṭho mīnaḥ
kimvā tīre śaraṭaḥ kṣīṇaḥ
athavā gavyūti⋅śvapaco dīna⋅
stava na hi dūre nṛpatiḥ kulīnaḥ

bho bhuvaneśvari! puṇye! dhanye!
devi! dravamayi! munivara⋅kanye !
gaṅgā⋅stavamimamamalaṁ nityaṁ
paṭhati naro jaḥ sa jayati satyam

jeṣāṁ hṛdaye gaṅgā⋅bhakti⋅
steṣāṁ bhavati sadā sukha⋅muktiḥ
madhura⋅manohara⋅pajjhaṭikābhiḥ
