paramānanda⋅kalita⋅lalitābhiḥ

gaṅgāstotramidaṁ bhavasāraṁ
vāñchita⋅phaladaṁ vigalita⋅bhāram
śaṅkara⋅sevaka⋅śaṅkara⋅racitaṁ
paṭhati ca viṣayīdamiti samāptam
IDX.en_translation:
O Devi! O Mistress of the gods! O Goddess! O Gaṅgā! O Deliverer of the three worlds! O You with restless waves! O You who dwell on the head of Śaṅkara! O Pure One! May my mind always rest at Your lotus feet.

O Bhāgīrathī! O Giver of happiness! O Mother! The glory of Your water is famous in the Vedas. I do not know Your glory; O Merciful One! Protect me, who am ignorant.

O Gaṅgā, who sports at the lotus feet of Hari! Your waves are white like snow, the moon, and pearls. Please remove the burden of my sins; by Your mercy, help me cross the ocean of material existence.

He by whom Your pure water is drunk has indeed attained the supreme abode. O Mother Gaṅgā! He who is a devotee of Yours—Yama is unable to even look at him.

O Deliverer of the fallen! O Jāhnavī! O Gaṅgā! Your meandering flow is adorned by the rocks of the King of Mountains (Himālaya). O Mother of Bhīṣma! O Daughter of the great sage (Jahnu)! O Deliverer of the fallen! O You who are glorious in the three worlds!

You are like a wish-fulfilling creeper granting fruits in this world. He who bows to You does not suffer grief. O Gaṅgā, who sports in the ocean! You are gazed upon by the restless eyes of the celestial damsels.

If, by Your mercy, one bathes in Your current, he is not born again in a womb. O Preventer of hell! O Jāhnavī! O Gaṅgā! O Destroyer of sins! O You of towering glory!

O You of shining limbs! O Wave of piety! Glory, glory to You, O Jāhnavī! O You of merciful glance! Your feet are illuminated by the jewels of Indra's crown. O Giver of happiness! O Giver of auspiciousness! O Shelter of Your servants!

O Bhagavatī! Please take away my disease, sorrow, suffering, sin, and heaps of wicked thoughts. O Essence of the three worlds! O Necklace of the Earth! You are indeed my refuge in this material world.

O Alakānandā! O Supreme Bliss! Be merciful to me, O You who are worshipped by the distressed! He who resides near Your banks—his residence is verily in Vaikuṇṭha.

It is better to be a tortoise or a fish in Your waters here, or a tiny lizard on Your bank, or a wretched dog-eater living near You, than to be a king of noble birth far away from You.

O Queen of the Universe! O Pious One! O Blessed One! O Goddess! O Embodiment of water! O Daughter of the great sage! The person who recites this spotless prayer to Gaṅgā daily—he truly becomes victorious.

Those in whose heart devotion to Gaṅgā dwells—for them there is always happiness and liberation. This prayer is adorned with sweet, captivating rhyming verses, filled with supreme bliss.

This Gaṅgā Stotram is the essence of worldly existence, the bestower of desired fruits, and the remover of burdens. Composed by Śaṅkara (achārya), the servant of Śaṅkara (Lord Śiva). Whoever reads it, his materi al entanglement ends.

IDX.first_line: dhana jana deha geha krsne samarpana
IDX.search: dhana jana deha geha krsne samarpana kariyacha suddha citte karaha smarana tabe kena mama suta bali kara duhkha krsna nila nija jana tahe tara sukha
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
dhana, jana, deha, geha kṛṣṇe samarpaṇa
kariyācha, śuddha citte karaha smaraṇa

tabe kena ‘mama suta’ bali’ kara duḥkha ?
kṛṣṇa nila nija⋅jana tāhe tā’ra sukha

kṛṣṇa⋅icchā⋅mate saba ghaṭaya ghaṭanā
tāhe sukha⋅duḥkha⋅jñāna avidyā⋅kalpanā

jāhā icchā kare kṛṣṇa, tāi jāna bhāla
tyajiyā āpana icchā ghucāo jañjāla

deya kṛṣṇa, neya kṛṣṇa, pāle kṛṣṇa sabe
rākhe kṛṣṇa, māre kṛṣṇa, icchā kare jabe

kṛṣṇa⋅icchā⋅viparīta je kare vāsanā
tā’ra icchā nāhi phale, se pāya jātanā

tyajiyā sakala śoka śuna kṛṣṇa⋅nāma
parama ānanda pā’be, pūrṇa ha’be kāma

bhakativinoda māge śrīvāsa⋅caraṇe
ātmanivedana⋅śakti jīvane⋅maraṇe
IDX.en_translation:
Wealth, family, body, and home—all have been surrendered to Kṛṣṇa. You have done this; now remember it with a pure heart.

Then why do you lament, saying, "My son"? Kṛṣṇa has taken His own; in that is His joy.

Everything happens according to Kṛṣṇa's will. The notion of happiness and sorrow in this is simply ignorance and imagination.

Whatever Kṛṣṇa desires, know that to be good. Abandon your own desires and remove this entanglement.

Kṛṣṇa gives, Kṛṣṇa takes, and Kṛṣṇa nourishes all. Kṛṣṇa protects and Kṛṣṇa destroys whenever He desires.

Whoever desires something contrary to Kṛṣṇa's will finds that his desires bear no fruit, and he only experiences suffering.

Abandon all sorrow and listen to Kṛṣṇa's name. You will attain supreme bliss, and your desires will be fulfilled.

Bhaktivinoda begs at the feet of Śrīvāsa for the power of self-surrender in life and in death.

IDX.first_line: dhana mora nityananda pati mora gaura candra
IDX.search: dhana mora nityananda pati mora gaura candra prana mora jugalakisora advaita acarya bala gadadhara mora kula narahari vilasoi mora vaisnavera padadhuli tahe mora snanakeli tarpana mora vaisnavera nama vicara kariya mane bhaktirasa asvadane madhyastha sri bhagavata purana
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
dhana mora nityānanda, pati mora gaura⋅candra,
prāṇa mora jugalakiśora
advaita⋅ācārya bala, gadādhara mora kula,
narahari vilasoi mora

vaiṣṇavera pada-dhūli, tāhe mora snāna-keli,
tarpaṇa mora vaiṣṇavera nāma
vicāra kariyā mane, bhakti-rasa āsvādane,
madhyastha śrī⋅bhāgavata⋅purāṇa

vaiṣṇavera ucchiṣṭa, tāhe mora mano-niṣṭha,
vaiṣṇavera nāmete ullāsa
vṛndāvane cautārā, tāhe mora mano-gherā,
kahe dīna narottama⋅dāsa
IDX.en_translation:
My treasure is Nityānanda. My Lord is Gaura⋅candra. My very life is the Divine Youthful Couple, Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa. My strength is Advaita Ācārya. My family is Gadādhara Paṇḍita. And Narahari Sarakāra is my delight.

The dust from the feet of the devotees is my joyful bath. My sacred offering is the names of the devotees. When I deeply ponder in my mind how to relish the mellow of devotion, the Śrīmad-Bhāgavata Purāṇa serves as my guide.

The remnants of the devotees are my firm conviction. I find great joy in the names of the devotees. My mind is forever encircled by the courtyards of Vṛndāvana. Thus speaks the humble Narottama dāsa.

IDX.first_line: dhana mora nityananda pati mora gaura candraprana mora jugalakisoraadvaita acarya bala gadadhara mora kulanarahari vilasoi mora
IDX.search: dhana mora nityananda pati mora gaura candraprana mora jugalakisoraadvaita acarya bala gadadhara mora kulanarahari vilasoi mora vaisnavera padadhuli tahe mora snanakelitarpana mora vaisnavera namavicara kariya mane bhaktirasa asvadanemadhyastha sri bhagavata purana
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
dhana mora nityānanda, pati mora gaura⋅candra,prāṇa mora jugalakiśoraadvaita⋅ācārya bala, gadādhara mora kula,narahari vilasoi mora

vaiṣṇavera pada-dhūli, tāhe mora snāna-keli,tarpaṇa mora vaiṣṇavera nāmavicāra kariyā mane, bhakti-rasa āsvādane,madhyastha śrī⋅bhāgavata⋅purāṇa

vaiṣṇavera ucchiṣṭa, tāhe mora mano-niṣṭha,vaiṣṇavera nāmete ullāsavṛndāvane cautārā, tāhe mora mano-gherā,kahe dīna narottama⋅dāsa
IDX.en_translation:
My treasure is Nityānanda. My Lord is Gaura⋅candra. My very life is the Divine Youthful Couple, Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa. My strength is Advaita Ācārya. My family is Gadādhara Paṇḍita. And Narahari Sarakāra is my delight.

The dust from the feet of the devotees is my joyful bath. My sacred offering is the names of the devotees. When I deeply ponder in my mind how to relish the mellow of devotion, the Śrīmad-Bhāgavata Purāṇa serves as my guide.

The remnants of the devotees are my firm conviction. I find great joy in the names of the devotees. My mind is forever encircled by the courtyards of Vṛndāvana. Thus speaks the humble Narottama dāsa.

IDX.first_line: dharma bali vede jare eteka prasamsa kare
IDX.search: dharma bali vede jare eteka prasamsa kare adharma baliya ninde jare taha kichu nahi kara dharmadharma parihara hoo rata nigudha vyapare jaci mana dhari tava paya se sakala parihari vrajabhume vasa kari rata hoo jugalasevaya
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
‘dharma’ bali vede jāre, eteka praśaṁsā kare,
‘adharma’ baliyā ninde jā’re
tāhā kichu nāhi kara, dharmādharma parihara,
hoo rata nigūḍha vyāpāre

jāci’ mana, dhari’ tava pāya
se⋅sakala parihari’, vrajabhūme vāsa kari’,
rata hoo jugalasevāya

śrī⋅śacī⋅nandana⋅dhane, śrī⋅nanda⋅nandana⋅sane,
eka kari’ karaha bhajana
śrīmukunda⋅priyajana, gurudeve jāna mana,
tomā’ lāgi’ patita⋅pāvana

jagate prakaṭa, bhāi, tā̐hā vinā gati nāi,
jadi cāo āpana kuśala
tā̐hāra caraṇe dhari’, tadādeśa sadā smari’,
e bhaktivinode deha bala
IDX.en_translation:
That which the Vedas call religious duty and praise so much, and that which they condemn as unrighteousness—do not do any of those. Give up both and engage instead in the confidential activities of devotion.

O my mind! I beg you while holding your feet: give up all of those things and reside in the land of Vraja, and engage yourself in the service of the Divine Couple.

Understand that Lord Caitanya and Lord Kṛṣṇa are one, and worship Them together. Understand in your mind that the Spiritual Master, who is most dear to Lord Kṛṣṇa, is the deliverer of the fallen for your sake.

He is manifest in this world, O brother, and without Him there is no path if you desire your own welfare. Holding His feet and always remembering His instructions, please give strength to this Bhaktivinoda.

IDX.first_line: dharmapathe thaki kara jivana japana bhai
IDX.search: dharmapathe thaki kara jivana japana bhai harinama kara sada ore o bhai hari vina bandhu nai je kona vyavasa dhari jivana nirvaha kari bala mukhe hari hari eimatra bhiksa cai
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
dharmapathe thāki’ kara jīvana jāpana, bhāi
harināma kara sadā (ore o bhāi)
hari vinā bandhu nāi

je⋅kona vyavasā dhari’,
jīvana nirvāha kari’,
bala mukhe ‘hari hari’,
eimātra bhikṣā cāi

gaurāṅgacaraṇe maja’,
anya⋅abhilāṣa tyaja,
vrajendranandane bhaja,
tabe baḍa sukha pāi

āmi cā̐da⋅bāula dāsa,
kari tava kṛpā⋅āśa,
jānāiyā abhilāṣa,
nityānanda⋅ājñā gāi
IDX.en_translation:
O brother, live your life on the path of righteousness. Always chant the Holy Name, for there is no friend other than Hari.

Whatever occupation you may adopt to sustain your life, chant "Hari! Hari!" with your mouth; this is all I beg of you.

Absorb yourself in the lotus feet of Gaurāṅga, give up all other desires, and worship the son of the King of Vraja; then you will find great happiness.

I, Cānd Bāul, hope for your mercy. Expressing my desire, I sing the command of Nityānanda.

IDX.first_line: diksa guru krpa kari mantra upadesa
IDX.search: diksa guru krpa kari mantra upadesa kariya dekhana krsna tattvera nirdesa siksa guru vrnda krpa kariya apara sadhake sikhana sadhanera anga sara
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
dīkṣā⋅guru kṛpā kari’ mantra⋅upadeśa
kariyā dekhāna kṛṣṇa⋅tattvera nirdeśa

śikṣā⋅guru⋅vṛnda kṛpā kariyā apāra
sādhake śikhāna sādhanera aṅga⋅sāra

śikṣā⋅guru⋅gaṇa⋅pāde kariyā praṇati
upadeśa⋅mālā boli nija⋅manaḥ prati
IDX.en_translation:
The initiating spiritual master, by mercifully giving instruction in the sacred sound, reveals the direction regarding the truth about Kṛṣṇa.

The assemblage of instructing spiritual masters, by performing boundless mercy, teach the practitioner the essential limbs of spiritual practice.

Offering obeisances at the feet of the assembly of instructing spiritual masters, I speak this garland of instructions to my own mind.

IDX.first_line: dinabandhu dayitari duhkha na gala mohari
IDX.search: dinabandhu dayitari duhkha na gala mohari helaki nisthura citta nilacale vije kari lakse jojanara gaja dakila he devaraja ta dakaku caturbhuja sunila sravana deri
IDX.author: banamālī
IDX.verses:
dīnabandhu dayitārī, duḥkha na galā moharī
helaki niṣṭhura citta, nīlācale vije kari

lakṣe jojanāra gaja, ḍākilā he devarāja
tā ḍākaku caturbhuja śunila śravaṇa ḍerī

kurupati sabhātaḍe, daupadī ātaṅgakāḍe
koṭī vastra dei heḍe, lajjāru karicha pāri

balavanta prabhu bali, baḍe muye āśrākali
dekhu dekhu bhāsi gali, ke ethu kariba pāri

rakha vā nā rakha mote, śaraṇa to pādagate
kahe vanamālī gīte, basiyāchi mu dhyāna kari
IDX.en_translation:
O Friend of the distressed! O Remover of sorrow! My suffering does not cease. It is a great delusion. O Lord, while You reside in Puruṣottama Kṣetra, my heart remains cruel.

The elephant Gajendra, from hundreds of thousands of miles away, called out to You. You, the four-armed Lord, heard that call with attentive ears.

In the assembly hall of Duryodhana, when Draupadī was in great fear, You saved her from shame by bestowing millions of garments.

Saying "O powerful Lord!", I took great shelter of You. Yet, I see myself drowning in this material world. Who can save me from this?

Whether You save me or not, I have taken refuge at Your feet. Vanamālī sings this song while sitting and meditating upon You.

IDX.first_line: disi disi racayantim sancaran netra laksmi
IDX.search: disi disi racayantim sancaran netra laksmi vilasita khuralibhih khanjari tasya khelam hrdaya madhupa mallim vallavadhisa sunor akhila guna gambhiram radhikam arcayami pitur iha vrsabhanor anvavaya prasastim jagati kila samaste susthu vistarayantim vraja nrpati kumaram khelayantim sakhibhih surabhini nija kunde radhikam arcayami
IDX.author: śrīla rūpa gosvāmī
IDX.verses:
diśi diśi racayantīṁ sañcaran⋅netra⋅lakṣmī⋅
vilasita⋅khuralībhiḥ khañjarī ṭasya khelām
hṛdaya⋅madhupa⋅mallīṁ vallavādhīśa⋅sūnor
akhila⋅guṇa⋅gambhīrāṁ rādhikām arcayāmi

pitur iha vṛṣabhānor anvavāya⋅praśastiṁ
jagati kila samaste suṣṭhu vistārayantīm
vraja⋅nṛpati⋅kumāraṁ khelayantīṁ sakhībhiḥ
surabhiṇi nija⋅kuṇḍe rādhikām arcayāmi

śarad⋅upacita⋅rākā⋅kaumudī⋅nātha⋅kīrti⋅
prakara⋅damana⋅dīkṣā⋅dakṣiṇa⋅smera⋅vaktrām
naṭad⋅aghabhid⋅apāṅgottuṅgitānaṅga⋅raṅgām
kalita⋅ruci⋅taraṅgāṁ rādhikām arcayāmi

vividha⋅kusuma⋅vṛndotphulla⋅dhammilla⋅dhāṭī⋅
vighaṭita⋅mada⋅ghūrṇat keki⋅piccha⋅praśastiṁ
madhuripu⋅mukha⋅bimbodgīrṇa⋅tāmbūla⋅rāga⋅
sphurad⋅amala⋅kapolāṁ rādhikām arcayāmi

amalina⋅lalitāntaḥ sneha⋅siktāntaraṅgām
akhila⋅vidha⋅viśākhā⋅sakhya⋅vikhyāta⋅śīlām
sphurad⋅aghabhid⋅anargha⋅prema⋅māṇikya⋅peṭīṁ
dhṛta⋅madhura⋅vinodāṁ rādhikām arcayāmi

atula⋅mahasi vṛndāraṇya⋅rājye’bhiṣiktāṁ
nikhila⋅samaya⋅bhartuḥ kārtikasyādhi⋅devīm
aparimita⋅mukunda⋅preyasī⋅vṛnda⋅mukhyāṁ
jagad⋅agha⋅hara⋅kīrtiṁ rādhikām arcayāmi

hari⋅pada⋅nakha⋅koṭī⋅pṛṣṭha⋅paryanta⋅sīmā⋅
taṭam api kalayantīṁ prāṇa⋅koṭer abhīṣṭam
pramudita⋅madirākṣī⋅vṛnda⋅vaidagdhya⋅dīkṣā⋅
gurum ati⋅guru⋅kīrtiṁ rādhikām arcayāmi

amala⋅kanaka⋅paṭṭodghṛṣṭa⋅kāśmīra⋅gaurīṁ
madhurima⋅laharībhiḥ samparītāṁ kiśorīm
hari⋅bhuja⋅parirabdhāṁ labdha⋅romāñca⋅pāliṁ
sphurad⋅aruṇa⋅dukūlāṁ rādhikām arcayāmi

tad⋅amala⋅madhurimṇāṁ kāmam ādhāra⋅rūpaṁ
paripaṭhati variṣṭhaṁ suṣṭhu rādhāṣṭakaṁ jaḥ
ahima⋅kiraṇa⋅putrī⋅kūla⋅kalyāṇa⋅candraḥ
sphuṭam akhilam abhīṣṭaṁ tasya tuṣṭas tanoti
IDX.en_translation:
I worship Śrī Rādhikā, who performs the play of a wagtail bird in every direction with the graceful, playful movements of her wandering eyes. She is the jasmine creeper for the bee of the heart of the Son of the king of cowherds, and is profound with all virtues.

I worship Śrī Rādhikā, who beautifully spreads the glory of her father Vṛṣabhānu’s lineage throughout the entire world. She plays with the Prince of Vraja and her sakhīs in her fragrant own pond.

I worship Śrī Rādhikā, whose gently smiling face has taken a vow to surpass the fame of the accumulated moonlight of the full autumn moon. Her sidelong glances towards the killer of Aghāsura heighten the stage of the god of love, and she is endowed with waves of inherent beauty.

I worship Śrī Rādhikā, whose flowing, beautiful braid, adorned with a multitude of blossoming flowers, surpasses the splendor of proud peacock feathers. Her pure cheeks glow brightly with the red color of betel nut ejected from the moon-like face of Kṛṣṇa.

I worship Śrī Rādhikā, whose heart is pure and drenched in unblemished affection, famous for her friendship with all types of sakhīs, including Lalitā and Viśākhā. She is the jewel casket of invaluable love for the killer of Aghāsura, and she delights in sweet pastimes.

I worship Śrī Rādhikā, who is crowned queen in the incomparably glorious kingdom of Vṛndāvana. She is the presiding deity of Kārtika, and the chief among the unlimited beloveds of Mukunda. Her fame removes the sins of the world.

I worship Śrī Rādhikā, who considers even the slightest touch of Hari's toenails to be dearer than millions of lives. She is the initiating teacher of the wisdom of the joyful, intoxicated-eyed sakhīs, and has exceptionally great fame.

I worship Śrī Rādhikā, who is fair like saffron ground on a pure golden slab, a young maiden surrounded by waves of sweetness. Embraced by Hari's arms, she experiences a thrill of ecstasy, wearing a glistening reddish garment.

Whoever properly recites this excellent eight-verse prayer in praise of Rādhā, which is the very embodiment of that pure sweetness, the auspicious moon of the Yamunā riverbank, Kṛṣṇa, being pleased, will clearly fulfill all his desires.

IDX.first_line: dusta mana tumi kisera vaisnava
IDX.search: dusta mana tumi kisera vaisnava pratisthara tare nirjanera ghare tava harinama kevala kaitava jadera pratistha sukarera vistha jana na ki taha mayara vaibhava kanaka kamini divasa yamini bhaviya ki kaja anitya se saba
IDX.author: śrīla bhaktisiddhānta sarasvatī ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
duṣṭa mana, tumi kisera vaiṣṇava ?
pratiṣṭhāra tare, nirjanera ghare,
tava hari-nāma kevala kaitava

jaḍera pratiṣṭhā, śūkarera viṣṭhā,
jāna nā ki tāhā māyāra vaibhava
kanaka⋅kāminī, divasa⋅yāminī,
bhāviyā ki kāja, anitya se⋅saba

tomāra kanaka, bhogera janaka,
kanakera dvāre sevaha mādhava
kāminīra kāma, nahe tava dhāma,
tāhāra mālika kevala jādava

pratiṣṭhāśā⋅taru, jaḍa⋅māyā⋅maru,
nā pela rāvaṇa jujhiyā rāghava
vaiṣṇavī⋅pratiṣṭhā, tā’te kara niṣṭhā,
tāhā nā bhajile labhibe raurava

harijana⋅dveṣa, pratiṣṭhāśā⋅kleśa,
kara kena tabe tāhāra gaurava
vaiṣṇavera kāche, pratiṣṭhāśā āche,
tā’ta kabhu nahe anitya vaibhava

se hari⋅sambandha, śūnya⋅māyāgandha,
tāhā kabhu naya jaḍera kaitava
pratiṣṭhā⋅caṇḍālī, nirjanatā⋅jāli,
ubhaye jāniha māyika raurava

kīrtana chāḍiba, pratiṣṭhā mākhiba,
ki kāja ḍhu̐ḍiyā tādṛśa gaurava
mādhavendra⋅purī, bhāva-ghare curi,
nā karila kabhu sadāi jānaba 

tomāra pratiṣṭhā, śūkarera viṣṭhā,
tāra saha sama kabhu nā mānava
matsaratā⋅vaśe, tumi jaḍarase,
maje’cha chāḍiyā kīrtana⋅sauṣṭhava

tāi duṣṭa mana, nirjana⋅bhajana,
pracāricha chale kuyogī⋅vaibhava
prabhu sanātane, parama jatane,
śikṣā dila jāhā cinta’ sei saba

sei du’ṭī kathā, bhula’ nā sarvathā,
uccaiḥsvare kara hari-nāma⋅raba
phalgu āra jukta, baddha āra mukta,
kabhu nā bhāviha ‘ekākāra’ saba

kanaka⋅kāminī, pratiṣṭhā⋅bāghinī,
chāḍiyāche jā’re sei ta’ vaiṣṇava
sei anāsakta, sei śuddha-bhakta,
saṁsāra tathāya pāya parābhava

jathāyogya bhoga, nāhi tathā roga,
anāsakta sei, ki āra kahaba
āsakti⋅rahita, sambandha⋅sahita,
viṣaya-samūha sakali mādhava

se⋅yuktavairāgya, tāhā ta’ saubhāgya,
tāhāi jaḍete harira vaibhava
kīrtane jāhāra, pratiṣṭhā⋅sambhāra,
tāhāra sampatti kevala kaitava

viṣaya⋅mumukṣu, bhogera bubhukṣu,
du’ye tyaja mana, dui avaiṣṇava
kṛṣṇera sambandha, aprākṛta skandha,
kabhu nahe tāhā jaḍera sambhava

māyāvādī jana, kṛṣṇetara mana,
mukta⋅abhimāne se ninde vaiṣṇava
vaiṣṇavera dāsa, tava bhakti⋅āśa,
kena vā ḍākicha nirjana āhava

je phalgu⋅vairāgī, kahe nije tyāgī,
se nā pāre kabhu haite vaiṣṇava
hari-pada chāḍi’, nirjanatā bāḍi’,
labhiyā ki phala phalgu se⋅vaibhava

rādhā⋅dāsye⋅rahi, chāḍa’ bhoga⋅ahi,
pratiṣṭhāśā nahe kīrtana⋅gaurava
rādhā⋅nityajana, tāhā chāḍi’ mana,
kena vā nirjana⋅bhajana⋅kaitava

vraja-vāsi-gaṇa, pracāraka dhana,
pratiṣṭhā⋅bhikṣuka tā’rā nahe śava
prāṇa āche tā’ra, se⋅hetu pracāra,
pratiṣṭhāśā-hīna kṛṣṇa-gāthā saba

śrī⋅dayita dāsa, kīrtanete āśa,
kara uccaiḥsvare hari-nāma⋅raba
kīrtana⋅prabhāve, smaraṇa hoibe,
se⋅kāle bhajana nirjana sambhava
IDX.en_translation:
O wicked mind, what kind of Vaiṣṇava are you? In a solitary room, for the sake of prestige, your chanting of the holy name is simply cheating.

Material prestige is the stool of a hog; do you not know that it is the opulence of Māyā? Gold, women, day and night—what is the use of thinking about them? They are all temporary.

Your gold is the progenitor of enjoyment; serve Mādhava using that gold. The desire for women is not your abode; their only owner is Yādava.

The tree of the desire for prestige, the desert of material illusion—Rāvaṇa could not obtain it even by fighting with Rāghava. Be fixed in the prestige of a Vaiṣṇava; if you do not worship that, you will attain the Raurava hell.

Envy of the devotees, the misery of desiring prestige—why do you glorify that? There is a desire for prestige in a Vaiṣṇava, but that is never a temporary opulence.

That is related to Hari, devoid of the scent of Māyā; it is never the cheating of the material world. The dog-eating witch of prestige, the net of solitude—know both to be the illusory Raurava.

"I will give up kīrtana and smear myself with prestige"—what is the use of seeking such "glory"? Mādhavendra Purī never committed theft in the storehouse of perception; always know this.

Your prestige is the stool of a hog; never consider it equal to a human being. Controlled by envy, you have drowned in material rasa, abandoning the beauty of kīrtana.

Therefore, O wicked mind, you are deceitfully preaching the opulence of a false yogī as "solitary worship." Contemplate all the instructions that were given to Sanātana Prabhu with great care.

Do not forget those two words at all; chant the holy name of Hari loudly. "Phalgu" (false) and "Yukta" (proper), "bound" and "liberated"—never think of them all as one.

Gold, women, and the tigress of prestige—he who has given them up is indeed a Vaiṣṇava. He is unattached, he is a pure devotee; material existence is defeated there.

Accepting what is appropriate, there is no disease there; he is unattached—what more shall I say? Devoid of attachment, possessing relationship, all objects are Mādhava.

That is "yukta-vairāgya" (proper renunciation), that is indeed good fortune; that is the opulence of Hari within the material world. For one whose kīrtana is a collection of prestige, his wealth is simply cheating.

"Desiring liberation from objects" and "hungry for enjoyment"—O mind, reject both; both are non-Vaiṣṇavas. Relationship with Kṛṣṇa, a transcendental body—that is never possible in matter.

The Māyāvādī, whose mind is not on Kṛṣṇa, criticizes the Vaiṣṇava in his pride of liberation. O servant of the Vaiṣṇava, your hope is devotion; why then do you call for the battle of solitude?

That "phalgu-vairāgī" who calls himself a renunciate can never be a Vaiṣṇava. Abandoning the feet of Hari, increasing solitude—what fruit is gained? That is "phalgu" opulence.

Remaining in the service of Rādhā, give up the snake of enjoyment; the desire for prestige is not the glory of kīrtana. Rādhā's eternal person—abandoning that, O mind, why the cheating of solitary worship?

The residents of Vraja are the wealth of the preachers; they are not beggars for prestige, nor are they dead bodies. They have life, therefore they preach; all the topics of Kṛṣṇa are free from the desire for prestige.

Śrī Dayita-dāsa's hope is in kīrtana; chant the sound of Hari-nāma loudly. By the influence of kīrtana, remembrance will come; at that time, solitary worship is possible.

IDX.first_line: e dusta hrdaye kama adi ripu chaya
IDX.search: e dusta hrdaye kama adi ripu chaya kutinati pratisthasa sathya sada raya hrdaya sodhana ara krsnera vasana nrsiŋha carane more ei ta kamana
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
e duṣṭa hṛdaye kāma⋅ādi ripu chaya
kuṭinaṭi, pratiṣṭhāśā, śāṭhya sadā raya

hṛdaya⋅śodhana āra kṛṣṇera vāsanā
nṛsiŋha⋅caraṇe more ei ta' kāmanā

kā̐diyā nṛsiŋha⋅pade māgiba kakhana
nirāpade navadvīpe jugala⋅bhajana

bhaya, bhaya pāya jā̐ra darśana, se hari
prasanna hoibe kabe more dayā kari’

jadyapi bhīṣaṇa mūrti duṣṭa jīva prati
prahlādādi kṛṣṇabhakta⋅jane bhadra ati

kabe vā prasanna ha’ye sakṛpā⋅vacane
nirbhaya karibe ei mūḍha akiñcane

‘svacchande voisa he vatsa, śrī⋅gaurāṅgadhāme
jugalabhajana hou, rati hou nāme

mama bhakta⋅kṛpābale vighna jābe dūra
śuddhacitte bhaja rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa⋅rasapura’

ei bali’ kabe mora mastaka⋅upara
svīya śrī⋅caraṇa harṣe dharive īśvara

amani jugalapreme sāttbika⋅vikāre
dharāya luṭiba āmi śrī⋅nṛsiŋha⋅dvāre
IDX.en_translation:
In this wicked heart, the six enemies such as lust, crookedness, the desire for fame, and deceit always reside.

My only prayer at the feet of Lord Nṛsiṁhadeva is for the purification of my heart and for a sincere desire to serve Lord Kṛṣṇa.

When will I weep at the feet of Lord Nṛsiṁhadeva and beg to safely perform the worship of the Divine Couple in Navadvīpa?

When will Lord Hari—whose very sight frightens even fear itself—be pleased and show mercy to me?

Although His form is terrifying toward wicked beings, He is extremely kind to devotees of Lord Kṛṣṇa like Prahlāda Mahārāja.

When, being pleased, will He make this foolish and insignificant soul fearless with His merciful words?

"Freely reside, O child, in the holy land of Lord Gaurāṅga. Let there be the worship of the Divine Couple, and let there be attachment to the Holy Name."

"By the mercy of My devotees, all obstacles will be removed. With a pure heart, worship Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa, the source of all nectar."

Saying this, when will the Lord happily place His own divine feet upon my head?

Then, with ecstatic transformations due to the love of the Divine Couple, I will roll on the ground at the door of Lord Nṛsiṁhadeva.

IDX.first_line: e ghora samsare padiya manava
IDX.search: e ghora samsare padiya manava na paya duhkhera sesa sadhusanga kari hari bhaje jadi tabe anta haya klesa visaya anale jbaliche hrdaya anale bade anala aparadha chadi laya krsna nama anale padaye jala
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
e ghora saṁsāre, paḍiyā mānava,
nā pāya duḥkhera śeṣa
sādhusaṅga kari’, hari bhaje jadi,
tabe anta haya kleśa

viṣaya⋅anale, jbaliche hṛdaya,
anale bāḍe anala
aparādha chāḍi’ laya kṛṣṇa⋅nāma,
anale paḍaye jala

nitāi⋅caitanya⋅caraṇa⋅kamale,
āśraya loila jei
kālidāsa bale, jīvane maraṇe,
āmāra āśraya sei
IDX.en_translation:
Having fallen into this terrible saṁsāra, man finds no end to suffering. If one associates with sādhu and worships Hari, then suffering comes to an end.

In the fire of saṁsāra, the heart burns, and fire increases fire. Giving up aparādha and chanting Kṛṣṇa⋅nāma is like water falling into the fire.

Whoever takes refuge at the lotus feet of Nitāi-Caitanya - Kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa says - that is my refuge in life and death.

IDX.first_line: e ghora samsare padiya manavana paya duhkhera sesasadhusanga kari hari bhaje jaditabe anta haya klesa
IDX.search: e ghora samsare padiya manavana paya duhkhera sesasadhusanga kari hari bhaje jaditabe anta haya klesa visaya anale jbaliche hrdayaanale bade analaaparadha chadi laya krsna namaanale padaye jala
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
e ghora saṁsāre, paḍiyā mānava,nā pāya duḥkhera śeṣasādhusaṅga kari’, hari bhaje jadi,tabe anta haya kleśa

viṣaya⋅anale, jbaliche hṛdaya,anale bāḍe analaaparādha chāḍi’ laya kṛṣṇa⋅nāma,anale paḍaye jala

nitāi⋅caitanya⋅caraṇa⋅kamale,āśraya loila jeikālidāsa bale, jīvane maraṇe,āmāra āśraya sei
IDX.en_translation:
Having fallen into this terrible saṁsāra, man finds no end to suffering. If one associates with sādhu and worships Hari, then suffering comes to an end.

In the fire of saṁsāra, the heart burns, and fire increases fire. Giving up aparādha and chanting Kṛṣṇa⋅nāma is like water falling into the fire.

Whoever takes refuge at the lotus feet of Nitāi-Caitanya - Kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa says - that is my refuge in life and death.

IDX.first_line: e mana ara ki manusa habe
IDX.search: e mana ara ki manusa habe bharata bhumete janama labhiye ki kaja karili kabe prathame janani kolete kautuka nahi chila jnana ara sisura sahite khelali bedali pauganda emati para
IDX.author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
e mana! āra ki mānuṣa habe
bhārata⋅bhūmete, janama labhiye,
ki kāja karili kabe

prathame jananī⋅, kolete kautuka,
nāhi chila jñāna āra
śiśura sahite, khelāli beḍāli,
paugaṇḍa emati pāra

prakṛti artha, anartha hoila,
se made hoili bhora
bujhite nāriye, kāminī⋅sāpinī,
mātiye rākhili kroḍa

suta⋅sutā laye, magana rahili,
bhuliye pūrava kathā
māyera udare, kata nā kahili,
jakhana pāili vyathā

caturthe āsiye, jarāya gherila,
sāmarthya hoila hīna
tabu ‘tora’ ‘mora’, nā ghuce vacana,
śamana gaṇiche dina

kubuddhi chāḍiye, ‘hari hari’ bala,
nikaṭe śamana bhāi
kahe premānanda, je⋅nāma loile,
śamana⋅gamana nāi
IDX.en_translation:
O my mind! Will you ever truly become a human being? Having taken birth in this sacred land, what spiritual work have you done so far?

At first, you were in your mother's lap, full of playfulness, without any spiritual knowledge. You played and roamed with other children, and thus your childhood passed.

Then your very nature became a calamity as you became intoxicated by the pursuit of wealth. Unable to understand that the objects of your desire were like a serpent, you remained infatuated.

With your children, you remained engrossed, forgetting the promises you made previously. In your mother's womb, you pledged so much when you were suffering in pain.

As the fourth stage of life arrived, old age enveloped you and your strength diminished. Still, your talk of "yours" and "mine" does not cease, while the God of Death counts your days.

Abandoning your evil intellect, chant "Hari! Hari!" the God of Death is near, O brother. Premānanda says: "By taking that Name, there is no more fear of death."

IDX.first_line: e mana harinama kara sara
IDX.search: e mana harinama kara sara e bhava sagara habe bali cara hatiya hoibi para dharama karama e japa e tapa jnana yoga yaga dhyana nahi nahi nahi kalite kevala upaya govinda nama
IDX.author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
e mana! ‘harināma’ kara sāra
e bhava⋅sāgara, habe bāli⋅cara,
hā̐ṭiyā hoibi pāra

dharama karama, e japa, e tapa,
jñāna⋅yoga⋅yāga⋅dhyāna
nahi nahi nahi, kalite kevala,
upāya ‘govinda’⋅nāma

bhukati mukati, je⋅gati, se⋅gati,
tāhe nā kariha rati
meghera chāyāya, juḍāna jemana,
kaha nā se kon gati

vadana bhariyā, ‘hari hari’ bala,
emana sulabha kabe
bhārata⋅bhūmete, mānuṣa⋅janama,
āra ki emana habe

jateka purāṇa⋅, pramāṇa dekha nā,
nāmera samāna nāi
nāme rati haile, premera udaya,
premete harike pāi

śravaṇa⋅kīrtana, kara anukṣaṇa,
asata pacāla chāḍi’
kahe premānanda, mānuṣa⋅janama,
saphala kara nā bhāḍi
IDX.en_translation:
O my mind! Make the Holy Name of Lord Hari your only essence. This ocean of material existence will then become like a mere sandbank, and you will easily cross it by walking.

Religious duties, worldly actions, chanting, austerity, speculative knowledge, yoga, sacrifices, and meditation—no, no, no! In this Age of Kali, the only means of success is the Name of Govinda.

Whether it be worldly enjoyment or liberation—do not develop any attachment to them. Such destinations are only as permanent as the coolness found in the shadow of a passing cloud.

Filling your mouth, chant "Hari! Hari!" When will such an easy opportunity ever come again? A human birth in this sacred land—will it ever happen again?

See the evidence in all the Purāṇas: there is nothing equal to the Holy Name. When attachment to the Name awakens, divine love arises, and through that love, we attain Lord Hari.

Constantly engage in hearing and chanting His glories, giving up all useless talk. Premānanda Dāsa says: "Make this human birth successful; do not waste it."

IDX.first_line: e mana ki kare varana kula
IDX.search: e mana ki kare varana kula jei kule kena janama na haya kevala bhakati mula kapi kule dhanya vira hanumana sri rama bhakataraja raksasa hoiya vibhisana baise isvara sabhara majha
IDX.author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
e mana! ki kare varaṇa⋅kula
jei kule kena, janama nā haya,
kevala bhakati mūla

kapi⋅kule dhanya, vīra hanumāna,
śrī⋅rāma⋅bhakatarāja
rākṣasa hoiyā, vibhīṣaṇa baise,
īśvara⋅sabhāra mājha

daityera aurase, prahlāda janami,
bhuvane rākhila jaśa
sphaṭika⋅stambhete, prakaṭe nṛhari,
hoiyā jā̐hāra vaśa

caṇḍāla hoiyā, mitāli karilā,
guhaka caṇḍālavara
bala nā ki kula, vidurera chila,
khāila tā̐hāra ghara

dekha nā kemana, sādhana karila,
gokule gopera nārī
jāti⋅kulācāre, tabe ki karila,
se hari je bhaje tāri

śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅bhajane, sabe adhikārī,
kulera garava nāi
kahe premānanda, je kare garava,
nitānta mūrakha bhāi
IDX.en_translation:
O my mind! What do social class and family lineage really accomplish? In whichever family one may be born, devotion is the only essential thing.

In the monkey lineage, the heroic Hanumān is blessed as the foremost devotee of Lord Rāma. Though born a demon, Vibhīṣaṇa sits in the midst of the Lord's assembly.

Though born from a demon, Prahlāda Mahārāja kept his fame throughout the world. Within a crystal pillar, Lord Nṛhari appeared, becoming subservient to him.

Though he was an outcaste, Guhaka became a friend of the Lord. Tell me, what noble family did Vidura have? Yet, the Lord ate in his house.

See how the gopīs of Gokula performed their spiritual practice. What did their caste or family traditions accomplish then? For Lord Hari, only those who serve Him are dear.

Everyone has the right to worship Lord Kṛṣṇa; there is no room for pride of lineage. Premānanda says: "He who takes pride in his family background is utterly foolish, my brother."

IDX.first_line: e mana ki lagi aili bhave
IDX.search: e mana ki lagi aili bhave emana janame hari na bhajili se tui manusa kabe manusa akara hoile ki haya karaha bhutera kama nahile vadane kena na balaha sri krsna govinda nama
IDX.author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
e mana! ki lāgi’ āili bhave
emana janame, ‘hari’ nā bhajili,
se tui mānuṣa kabe

mānuṣa⋅ākāra, hoile ki haya,
karaha bhūtera kāma
nahile vadane, kena nā balaha,
‘śrī⋅kṛṣṇa’ ‘govinda’⋅nāma

pākhīre je⋅nāma, looyāile laya,
śārī śuka ādi kata
tumi je ihāte, ālasya karaha,
e haya kemana mata

divasa⋅rajanī, ābola tābola,
pacāla pāḍite pāra
tāhāra bhitare, kakhana kena ki,
‘govinda’ balite nāra

bhajiba baliye, kahiyā āili,
bhulili ki sukha pāiye
bujhinu ābāra, śamana⋅nagare,
narake majibi jāiye

vadana bhariyā, ‘hari’ bala jadi,
kṣati nā hoibe tāya
kahe premānanda, tabe je nitānta,
eḍābe kṛtānta⋅dāya
IDX.en_translation:
O my mind! For what purpose did you come to this material world? In such a rare human birth, you have not worshipped Lord Hari. When will you truly act as a human being?

What is the use of having a human form if you perform only the acts of a ghost? Otherwise, why do you not utter the Names of Śrī Kṛṣṇa and Govinda with your mouth?

Even birds like parrots and mynahs will chant the Name if taught. Yet you are lazy in this matter—how can this be appropriate?

Day and night you can speak nonsense and engage in idle chatter. In the midst of all that, why can you not utter the Name of Lord Govinda?

You came to this world promising to worship the Lord, but having forgotten that, what happiness have you found? I understand that you will eventually drown in hell in the city of the God of Death.

If you wholeheartedly chant the Name of Lord Hari, it will do you no harm. Premānanda says: "Only then will you certainly escape the grasp of the God of Death."

IDX.first_line: e mana tu bada kalira bhuta
IDX.search: e mana tu bada kalira bhuta kara bala jari sunye diya badi hasaye tapana suta bhutera bapera sraddha kara niti bhutera vegara khata laja nahi mukhe kala kata sukhe calicha jamera bata
IDX.author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
e mana! tu baḍa kalira bhūta
kara bala jāri, śūnye diyā bāḍi,
hāsaye tapana⋅suta

bhūtera bāpera, śrāddha kara niti,
bhūtera vegāra khāṭa
lāja nāhi mukhe, kāla kāṭa’ sukhe,
calicha jamera bāṭa

kāminī⋅kāñcana, hṛdaya⋅rañjana,
tāhāte magana thāka
o dike tomāra, ki daśā ghaṭiche,
tāra kichu khoja rākha

caurāśī narake, jābe eke eke,
patha pariṣkāra prāya
kapālera jora, baḍa baṭe tora,
bāhādurī habe tāya

mūrakha barbvara, suyukati dhara,
jadi taribāre cāo
kahe premānande, manera ānande,
sadā hari-guṇa gāo
IDX.en_translation:
O my mind! You are a great ghost of the Age of Kali. You keep exerting useless force and striking out into the void, while the Son of the Sun-god laughs at you.

You always perform the rituals for the ghost's father and work as a forced laborer for ghosts. You have no shame; you pass your time happily while walking straight on the path to the God of Death.

You remain engrossed in women and gold, which delight your heart. On the other hand, do you keep any track of the terrible condition that is befalling you?

You will go to the eighty-four hells one by one—the path is almost clear. Your fate is truly strong; there will be great "heroism" in that.

O foolish barbarian! Adopt good reasoning if you wish to cross over this ocean. Premānanda says: "With a joyful mind, always sing the glories of Lord Hari."

IDX.first_line: e mana tumi ki bhevecha sukha
IDX.search: e mana tumi ki bhevecha sukha supatha chadiya kupathe gamana e tora kemana buka sthavara yonite krame je janama hoiya vimsati laksa jala jantu majhe nava laksa ara jalei vasati bhaksya
IDX.author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
e mana! tumi ki bhevecha sukha
supatha chāḍiyā, kupathe gamana,
e tora kemana buka

sthāvara⋅yonite, krame je⋅janama,
hoiyā viṁśati lakṣa
jala⋅jantu⋅mājhe, nava lakṣa āra,
jalei vasati bhakṣya

ekādaśa lakṣa, kṛmite janama,
daśa lakṣa joni pakṣa
paśura mājhāre, krame tetriśa lakṣa,
mānava catur lakṣa

mānuṣe āsiyā, kutsit dvi⋅lakṣa,
śūdrādi dviśatavāra
brāhmaṇa⋅kulete, pare ekabāra,
tā⋅sama nāhika āra

kateka kalapa, bhramiyā mānuṣa,
emana janame pāpa
śamane bāndhiyā, puna nā phelābe,
ābāra tomāre bāpa

vadana bhariyā, ‘hari hari’ bala,
asata⋅bhāvanā chāḍa
kahe premānanda, tabe se catura,
e saba jātanā eḍa
IDX.en_translation:
O mind! What have you thought to be happiness? Leaving the good path, going on the bad path—what kind of heart is this of yours?

In the species of unmoving beings (plants), birth after birth, becoming twenty lakhs (2,000,000). Among the water-animals, nine lakhs (900,000) more; residence and food in the water.

Eleven lakhs (1,100,000) birth in worms; ten lakhs (1,000,000) bird species. Among the animals, in sequence thirty-three lakhs (3,300,000); humans four lakhs (400,000).

Coming into human (species), ugly (uncivilized) two lakhs (200,000); Śūdras etc. two hundred times. In the Brāhmaṇa family, afterwards one time; there is nothing equal to that.

Wandering for how many Kalpas, (you attain) such a birth; (if you commit) sin (in this birth). Binding (you), Yamarāja will not drop (you into this birth) again, O dear one.

Filling the mouth, say "Hari! Hari!", give up bad thoughts. Premānanda says: Then he is clever; avoid all this suffering.

IDX.first_line: e mana tumi se kevala bhuta
IDX.search: e mana tumi se kevala bhuta kusanga smasane satata basicha paiya parama juta mala mutra jata asata pacala e tora bhaksana sukhe rama krsna hari gopala govinda balite naricha mukhe
IDX.author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
e mana! tumi se kevala bhūta
kusaṅga⋅śmaśāne, satata basicha,
pāiyā parama juta

mala mūtra jata, asata pacāla,
e tora bhakṣaṇa sukhe
‘rāma kṛṣṇa hari’, ‘gopāla govinda’,
balite nāricha mukhe

je ‘kara’ tomāra, govinda⋅pūjane,
tīratha bhramite pā⋅ya
se dui rākhile, curiye dāriye,
tabe ki ulṭā naya

jata nā karicha, sādhura helana,
se tora anala mukhe
dekha nā tāhāte, āpani dahicha,
emati goṅābi dukhe

kṛṣṇera vasati, sādhura hṛdaye,
sukhera viśrāma⋅bhūmi
emana durdaiva, tā̐hāra paraśa,
karite nāricha tumi

śrī⋅hari⋅caraṇa, karaha śaraṇa,
gayā⋅gaṅgā saba tā’te
kahe premānanda, tabe se uddhāra,
nahile vā habe kā’te
IDX.en_translation:
O my mind! You are merely a ghost, always sitting in the cremation ground of bad company, finding it very suitable there.

All impure and rotten things are your food for pleasure. You cannot even utter the names "Rāma," "Kṛṣṇa," "Hari," "Gopāla," or "Govinda."

Your hands are meant for worshipping Lord Govinda, and your feet are meant for traveling to holy places—if you keep them idle, stealing away their true purpose, then is that not utterly wrong?

All the disrespect you show to the saintly is like fire in your mouth. See how you are burning in it yourself, and thus you will pass your life in misery.

Lord Kṛṣṇa's abode is in the hearts of the saintly—a resting place of true happiness. Such is your misfortune that you are unable to obtain their touch.

Take refuge at the feet of Lord Hari; all holy places like Gayā and the Ganges are contained therein. Premānanda says: "Only then will you be delivered; otherwise, who will achieve it?"

IDX.first_line: e mana tumi va bhulecha kise
IDX.search: e mana tumi va bhulecha kise tomare dekhiya samana kinkara hate tali diya hase ratridine kata asata pacalo sri hari kahite naro emana durlabha janama paiye ki sukhe e ksepa haro
IDX.author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
e mana! tumi vā bhulecha kise
tomāre dekhiyā, śamana⋅kiṅkara,
hāte tāli diyā hāse

rātri-dine kata, asata pacālo,
‘śrī⋅hari’ kahite nāro
emana durlabha, janama pāiye,
ki sukhe e kṣepa hāro

dhana⋅jane jata, āpanā balicha,
ke tora jāibe sāthe
gāyera gumāne, pichu nā gaṇili,
ṭhekāli śamana⋅hāte

dekhiye śuniye, bujhite nārili,
asāre jānili sāra
āpanāra māthā, āpani bhāṅgili,
bala nā e doṣa kā’ra

ekhana takhana, kakhana ki jāni,
hāsite khelite paḍi
e sukha smaribe, galāya jakhana,
caḍibe cāmera daḍi

vadana bhariyā, ‘hari hari’ bala,
śamana taribe sukhe
kahe premānanda, ‘hari’ nā bhajili,
kāli cuṇa tora mukhe
IDX.en_translation:
O my mind! What have you forgotten? Seeing your condition, the servants of the God of Death clap their hands and laugh.

Day and night you waste your time in useless talk and can never utter "Śrī Hari." Having obtained such a rare human birth, for what supposed pleasure do you lose this opportunity?

Of all the wealth and people you consider your own, who among them will go with you in the end? In the pride of your body, you did not consider the end, and now you have fallen into the hands of death.

Even after seeing and hearing the truth, you could not understand it; you considered the temporary to be essential. You have brought this ruin upon yourself—tell me, whose fault is it?

Who knows when, whether now or then, you will suddenly fall while laughing and playing? You will remember your current pleasures when the rope of the God of Death tightens around your neck.

Chant "Hari! Hari!" with a full heart and you will happily cross over the kingdom of death. Premānanda says: "If you do not worship Lord Hari, there will be nothing but disgrace upon your face."

IDX.first_line: e mana vicari kena na cao
IDX.search: e mana vicari kena na cao dekha bhavaroga te kene ghuce na kata na ausadha khao kata na karicha prasada bhaksana carana dhauta jala e saba ausadhi pana kara tabu dhatute nahika bala
IDX.author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
e mana! vicāri kena nā cāo
dekha bhava-roga, te kene ghuce nā,
kata nā auṣadha khāo

kata nā karicha, prasāda bhakṣaṇa,
caraṇa⋅dhauta jala
e saba auṣadhi, pāna kara tabu,
dhātute nāhika bala

jihvāra paraśe, je hari-nāmete,
premete bhāsāya tanu
se nāma loiye, ārdra nā hoili,
lohāra piṇḍa se janu

bhāviyā dekha nā, auṣadhe ki kare,
kupathya chāḍite nāro
kupathya thākile, roga nā chāḍibe,
aruci bāḍibe āro

anupāna jāni, auṣadhi khāo to,
rogera damana habe
ekhano tā jadi, bujhite nā pāra,
tabe se bujhibe kabe

kṣudhāṭī bāḍaye, ruciṭī janame,
khāite ānanda⋅jala
kahe premānanda, tabe se jāniha,
auṣadhi⋅dhāraṇa⋅phala
IDX.en_translation:
O my mind! Why do you not think and see for yourself? Behold the disease of material existence—why does it not disappear, even though you take so many "medicines"?

How much food offered to the Lord do you partake, and how much water that has washed His feet? You drink all these medicines, yet there is no spiritual strength in your constitution.

The Holy Name of Lord Hari, by its very touch upon the tongue, can float the entire body in divine love. Yet taking that Name, your heart has not become softened; such a person is like a lump of iron.

Just think—what can medicine do if you cannot give up unwholesome food? If you continue to eat such things, the disease will not leave you, and your aversion to spiritual life will only increase.

If you take the medicine with the correct knowledge and proper following of instructions, the disease will be suppressed. If you still cannot understand this, then when will you ever understand?

Only when spiritual hunger increases and a taste for divine bliss awakens should you know that the medicine is truly taking effect. Premānanda says: "Only then will you know the true fruit of the medicine."

IDX.first_line: e tina bhuvana majhe avani mandala saje
IDX.search: e tina bhuvana majhe avani mandala saje tahe punah ati anupama soka duhkha tapatraya jara nama santi haya hena sei santipura grama kuvera pandita taya suddha sattva dvijaraya nabha devi tahara grhini santipure kare sthiti krsna puja kare niti bhakti hina dekhiya avani
IDX.author: vaiṣṇava⋅dāsa
IDX.verses: e tina bhuvana mājhe, avanī maṇḍala sāje tāhe punaḥ ati anupāma śoka duḥkha tāpatraya, jāra nāma śānti haya hena sei śāntipura grāma kuvera paṇḍita tāya, śuddha sattva dvijarāya nābhā⋅devī tāhāra gṛhinī śāntipure kare sthiti, kṛṣṇa⋅pūjā kare niti bhakti⋅hīna dekhiyā avanī, kalihata jīva dekhi, mane duḥkha pāya ati bhakte ārādhaye bhagavāna se ārādhana kaje, nābhādevīra garbhājhe mahāviṣṇu hailena adhiṣṭhāna māgha⋅māse śubhakṣaṇe, śuklā saptamī dine avatīrṇa hailena mahāśaya dekhiyā paṇḍita ati, haila haraṣita mati nayane ānanda⋅dhārā vaya ācambite jagajane, ānanda pāila mane ki lāgiyā keha nāhi jāne e vaiṣṇava⋅dāse bole, uddhāra haiba bale patita pāṣaṇḍī dīna hīne
IDX.en_translation:
Within these three worlds, the earthly realm is beautifully adorned, and within it, there is a most incomparable place. It is the village of Śāntipura, where the very name means "peace from grief, sorrow, and the three-fold miseries."

There lived Kuvera Paṇḍita, a pure and saintly king among the twice-born, and his wife was Nābhā-devī. They resided in Śāntipura, always performing the worship of Lord Kṛṣṇa, and they were grieved to see the world devoid of devotion.

Seeing the living entities afflicted by the age of Kali, Kuvera Paṇḍita felt great sorrow in his heart. As a devotee, he worshipped the Supreme Lord. In response to that worship, Lord Mahā-Viṣṇu appeared within the womb of Nābhā-devī.

In the month of Māgha, at an auspicious moment on the seventh day of the bright fortnight, that great personality descended. Seeing Him, the Paṇḍita's heart was filled with joy, and streams of bliss flowed from his eyes.

Suddenly, everyone in the world felt a strange joy in their minds, but no one knew the reason for it. Vaiṣṇava Dāsa says: "By the power of this descent, the fallen, the atheists, and the most distressed will all be delivered."

IDX.first_line: eibara karuna kara caitanya nitai
IDX.search: eibara karuna kara caitanya nitai mo sama pataki ara tribhuvane nai muni ati mudhamati mayara naphara ei saba pape mora tanu jara jara
IDX.author: śrīla locana⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
eibāra karuṇā kara caitanya⋅nitāi
mo⋅sama pātakī āra tri-bhuvane nāi

muñi ati mūḍhamati māyāra naphara
ei saba pāpe mora tanu jara jara

mleccha adhama jata chila anācārī
tā⋅sabā’ hoite mora pāpa ati bhārī

aśeṣa pāpera pāpī jagāi⋅mādhāi
tā⋅dohāre uddhārile tomarā du’ṭī bhāi

locana bale mo⋅adhame dayā naila kene
tumi nā karile dayā ke karibe āne
IDX.en_translation:
O Lord Caitanya and Lord Nitāi! Please show Your mercy this time. There is no sinner like me in all the three worlds.

I am very foolish and a servant of illusion. My body is tormented by all these sins.

Compared to all the most fallen and unrighteous persons, my sins are much heavier.

You two brothers delivered Jagāi and Mādhāi, who were the greatest of sinners.

Locana Dāsa says: "Why is there no mercy for a fallen soul like me? If You do not show mercy, who else will?"

IDX.first_line: eibara karuna kara caitanya nitaimo sama pataki ara tribhuvane nai
IDX.search: eibara karuna kara caitanya nitaimo sama pataki ara tribhuvane nai muni ati mudhamati mayara napharaei saba pape mora tanu jara jara
IDX.author: śrīla locana⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
eibāra karuṇā kara caitanya⋅nitāimo⋅sama pātakī āra tri-bhuvane nāi

muñi ati mūḍhamati māyāra napharaei saba pāpe mora tanu jara jara

mleccha adhama jata chila anācārītā⋅sabā’ hoite mora pāpa ati bhārī

aśeṣa pāpera pāpī jagāi⋅mādhāitā⋅dohāre uddhārile tomarā du’ṭī bhāi

locana bale mo⋅adhame dayā naila kenetumi nā karile dayā ke karibe āne
IDX.en_translation:
O Lord Caitanya and Lord Nitāi! Please show Your mercy this time. There is no sinner like me in all the three worlds.

I am very foolish and a servant of illusion. My body is tormented by all these sins.

Compared to all the most fallen and unrighteous persons, my sins are much heavier.

You two brothers delivered Jagāi and Mādhāi, who were the greatest of sinners.

Locana Dāsa says: “Why is there no mercy for a fallen soul like me? If You do not show mercy, who else will?”

IDX.first_line: eibara karuna kara vaisnava gosanipatitapavana toma vine keha nai
IDX.search: eibara karuna kara vaisnava gosanipatitapavana toma vine keha nai kahara nikate gele papa dure jayaemana dayala prabhu keba kotha paya
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
eibāra karuṇā kara vaiṣṇava⋅gosāñipatita-pāvana tomā vine keha nāi

kāhāra nikaṭe gele pāpa dūre jāyaemana dayāla prabhu kebā kothā pāya

gaṅgāra paraśa hoile paścāte pāvanadarśane pavitra kara⋅ei tomāra guṇa

hari⋅sthāne aparādhe tāre’ harināmatomā’⋅sthāne aparādhe nāhika eḍāna

tomāra hṛdaye sadā govinda viśrāmagovinda kahena,⋅mama vaiṣṇava parāṇa

pratijanme kari āśā caraṇera dhūlinarottame kara dayā āpanāra bali’
IDX.en_translation:
Now bestow your mercy, O Vaiṣṇava, Without you, there is no one who can purify the fallen.

approaching whom do sins go away? Such a merciful master, who can find anywhere else?

Purification from touching the Gaṅgā comes later; You purify by mere sight-this is your quality.

If one offends Hari, the holy name of Hari saves them; But if one offends you, there is no escape.

In your heart, Govinda always rests; Govinda says, “The Vaiṣṇava is My very life”.

In every birth, I hope for the dust of your feet. Show mercy to Narottama, accepting him as your own.

IDX.first_line: eibara karuna kara vaisnava gosanipatitapavana toma vine keha nai
IDX.search: eibara karuna kara vaisnava gosanipatitapavana toma vine keha nai kahara nikate gele papa dure jayaemana dayala prabhu keba kotha paya
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:

kāhāra nikaṭe gele pāpa dūre jāyaemana dayāla prabhu kebā kothā pāya

gaṅgāra paraśa hoile paścāte pāvanadarśane pavitra kara⋅ei tomāra guṇa

hari⋅sthāne aparādhe tāre’ harināmatomā’⋅sthāne aparādhe nāhika eḍāna

pratijanme kari āśā caraṇera dhūlinarottame kara dayā āpanāra bali’

Now bestow your mercy, O Vaiṣṇava, Without you, there is no one who can purify the fallen.

approaching whom do sins go away? Such a merciful master, who can find anywhere else?

Purification from touching the Gaṅgā comes later; You purify by mere sight-this is your quality.

If one offends Hari, the holy name of Hari saves them; But if one offends you, there is no escape.

In your heart, Govinda always rests; Govinda says, “The Vaiṣṇava is My very life”.

In every birth, I hope for the dust of your feet. Show mercy to Narottama, accepting him as your own.

IDX.first_line: eibara paile dekha carana dukhani
IDX.search: eibara paile dekha carana dukhani hiyara majhare rakhi judaba parani tare na dekhiya mora mane bada tapa anale pasiba kimva jale diba jhapa
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
eibāra pāile dekhā caraṇa du’khāni
hiyāra mājhāre rākhi’ juḍā’ba parāṇī

tā’re nā dekhiyā mora mane baḍa tāpa
anale paśiba kiṁvā jale diba jhā̐pa

mukhera muchāba ghāma, khāoyāba pāna guyā
ghāmete vātāsa diba candanādi cūyā

vṛndāvanera phulera gā̐thiyā diba hāra
vināiyā bāndhiba cūḍā kuntalera bhāra

kapāle tilaka diba candanera cā̐da
narottama⋅dāsa kahe viparītera phā̐da
IDX.en_translation:
If I can see Your two lotus feet this time, I will keep them within my heart and soothe my soul.

Not seeing You, there is great burning in my mind. I will enter into fire or jump into the water.

I will wipe the sweat from Your face and feed You betel nuts. When You sweat, I will fan You and apply sandalwood paste and other scents.

I will string a garland of Vṛndāvana flowers and give it to You. I will braid Your hair and tie it into a topknot.

I will place a tilaka mark of sandalwood paste on Your forehead like a moon. Narottama Dāsa says, "This is a trap of the contrary situation (separation)."

IDX.first_line: eirupe nityalila kare duijana
IDX.search: eirupe nityalila kare duijana dina bhede lila bheda kariba varnana radha kunde govardhane lila dinamane ratrilila jamunapuline vrndavane
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
eirūpe nityalīlā kare duijana
dina⋅bhede līlā⋅bheda kariba varṇana

rādhā⋅kuṇḍe govardhane līlā dinamāne
rātrilīlā jāmunapuline vṛndāvane

vṛndāvana ṣola krośa līlā⋅āyatana
mahīdhara caturdike bahu tīrthagaṇa
sarovara⋅rūpe śobhā kare anukṣaṇa

āchena mānasagaṅgā girirāja⋅pāśe
naukālīlā kare kṛṣṇa jathāya ullāse

sujyotsnā mokṣaṇa mālyahāra gaurīsara
sumana vanāridhvaja gandharva⋅sāgara

tathāya ariṣṭakuṇḍa āra rādhā⋅kuṇḍa
sarvatīrtharāja jathā śobhe taruṣaṇḍa

rādhā⋅kuṇḍa⋅uttare je ghāṭa manohara
tannikaṭe aṣṭadala svarūpa catvara

‘anaṅga⋅raṅga ambuja⋅kuñja’ manohara
jā̐hā lalitāra gaṇa seve nirantara

‘lalitānandada’ kuñja āchaye tathāya
tannikaṭe bahu kuñjagaṇa śobhā pāya

tāhāra īśāne kuñja ‘madana⋅sukhada’
‘svānanda⋅sukhada’⋅kuñja baḍa sukhaprada
‘viśākhā⋅ānanda’ nāma kuñja sukhaprada

rādhā⋅kuṇḍa⋅pūrvabhāge ‘citrānanda’ nāma
citrāsakhī⋅kuñja jathā rādhāra viśrāma

rādhā⋅kuṇḍa⋅agnikoṇe ‘pūrṇendu’ saṁjñaka
indulekhā⋅kuñja kṛṣṇa⋅ānandavardhaka

rādhā⋅kuṇḍa⋅dakṣiṇe campakalatā⋅kuñja
parama ānandaprada nāma ‘hemakuñja’

kuṇḍera naiṛta⋅koṇe kuñja manohara
‘śyāmakuñja’ nāma raṅgadevī⋅kuñjavara

rādhā⋅kuṇḍa⋅paścime ‘aruṇa⋅kuñja’ nāma
tuṅgavidyā seve tathā nitya rādhāśyāma

sudevīra kuñja rādhā⋅kuṇḍa⋅vāyukoṇe
‘haritkuñja’ bali khyāta kaila sakhīgaṇe

ei aṣṭasathī⋅kuñje jathā sarvakāla
rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa līlā kare parama rasāla
IDX.en_translation:
In this manner, the Divine Couple perform Their eternal pastimes. I will now describe the distinctions in Their pastimes according to the different times of the day.

During the daytime, Their pastimes take place at Rādhā-kuṇḍa and Govardhana Hill. The nighttime pastimes occur on the banks of the Yamunā in Vṛndāvana.

Vṛndāvana, which is about thirty-two miles in area, is the abode of Their pastimes. On all four sides of Govardhana Hill there are many holy places which constantly shine beautifully in the form of lakes.

Mānasagaṅgā is situated near Govardhana Hill, where Lord Kṛṣṇa joyfully performs His boating pastimes.

There are also the lakes of Sujyotsnā, Mokṣaṇa, Mālyahāra, Gaurīsara, Sumana, and the Gandharva-sāgara.

Furthermore, Ariṣṭa-kuṇḍa and Rādhā-kuṇḍa are located there, where the king of all holy places shines amidst groves of trees.

To the north of Rādhā-kuṇḍa is a charming bathing place, and nearby is an eight-petaled courtyard.

There is the enchanting lotus grove where Cupid plays and where Lalitā's followers render constant service.

The grove that gives bliss to Lalitā is situated there, and near it many other groves of the gopīs shine brightly.

To its northeast is the grove named Madana-sukhad, which is very pleasing to Cupid. The grove of self-bliss is also very pleasing, as is the grove of Viśākhā's bliss.

In the eastern part of Rādhā-kuṇḍa is the grove named Citrānanda, which is the resting place of Rādhā.

In the southeast corner of Rādhā-kuṇḍa is the grove known as Pūrṇendu, which enhances Lord Kṛṣṇa's bliss.

To the south of Rādhā-kuṇḍa is the supremely blissful golden grove of Campakalatā.

In the southwest corner of the lake is the charming dark grove of Raṅgadevī.

To the west of Rādhā-kuṇḍa is the reddish grove where Tuṅgavidyā eternally serves Rādhā and Śyāma.

The green grove of Sudevī is in the northwest corner of Rādhā-kuṇḍa.

In these groves of the eight principal gopīs, Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa perform Their supremely delightful pastimes at all times.

IDX.first_line: eka mukhe ki kahiba goracandera lila
IDX.search: eka mukhe ki kahiba goracandera lila hamagudi nana range jaya sacibala lale mukha jhara jhara dekhite sundara paka bimba phala jini sundara adhara
IDX.author: vasudeva ghoṣa
IDX.verses: eka⋅mukhe ki kahiba goracandera līla hāmāguḍi nānā range jaya śacī-bālā lāle mukha jhara jhara dekhite sundara pākā bimba⋅phala jini sundara adhara angada balayā sobhe su⋅bāhu⋅yugale caraṇe magarā khāḍu bāgha⋅nakha gale sonāra sikali pīthe pātera khopanā vāsudeva ghoṣa kahe nichani āpanā
IDX.en_translation:
With only one mouth, what can I say of the pastimes of Lord Gorācānd? All glories to the Son of Mother Śacī, who is crawling in various playful moods!

His reddish face is captivating and beautiful to behold. His beautiful lips conquer the beauty of the ripe bimba fruit.

Armlets and bracelets adorn His beautiful arms. He wears anklets on His feet and a tiger-claw pendant on His neck.

A golden chain is upon His back, and His hair is in a silken braid. Vāsudeva Ghoṣa says: "I offer my very self as a sacrifice to Him."

IDX.first_line: elo gaura rasa nadi kadambini hayebhasaila gaudadesa premavrsti diye
IDX.search: elo gaura rasa nadi kadambini hayebhasaila gaudadesa premavrsti diye nityananda raya tahe maruta sahayajaha nahi premavrsti taha laye jaya
IDX.author: śrīla locana⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
e’lo gaura⋅rasa⋅nadī kādambinī ha’yebhāsāila gauḍadeśa prema-vṛṣṭi diye

nityānanda⋅rāya tāhe māruta sahāyajā̐hā nāhi prema-vṛṣṭi, tā̐hā la’ye jāya

huḍ huḍ⋅śabde āila śrī⋅advaitacandrajala⋅rasadhārā tāhe rāya⋅rāmānanda

cauṣaṭṭi mahānta āila meghe śobhā kari’śrī⋅rūpa⋅sanātana tāhe hoila vijuri

kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa kavirāja rasera bhāṇḍārījatane rākhila prema hemakumbha bhari’

ebe sei prema la’ye jagajane dilae⋅dāsa locana⋅bhāgye bindu nā milila
IDX.en_translation:
The river of Gaura⋅candra’s divine mellow came as a bank of clouds, inundating the land of Bengal with a rain of divine love.

Lord Nityānanda became the helping wind, carrying it to those places where there was no such rain of love.

With a thunderous roar came Śrī Advaita; Rāya Rāmānanda was a stream of watery mellow accompanying him.

The sixty-four great souls came and beautified the clouds; Śrī Rūpa and Sanātana became the lightning within them.

Kṛṣṇa dāsa Kavirāja, the treasurer of divine mellows, carefully preserved that love, filling a golden pot with it.

Now, taking that divine love, he gave it to the people of the world. But this servant, Locana, by his own destiny, did not receive even a drop.
