IDX.first_line: emana gauranga vina nahi arahena avatara habe ki hayechehena prema paracara
IDX.search: emana gauranga vina nahi arahena avatara habe ki hayechehena prema paracara duramati ati patita pasandiprane na marila kareharinama diya hrdaya sodhilajaci giya ghare ghare
IDX.author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
emana gaurāṅga vinā nāhi āra!hena avatāra, ha’be ki hayeche,hena prema paracāra

duramati ati, patita pāṣaṇḍī,prāṇe nā mārila kā’reharināma diyā, hṛdaya śodhila,jāci’ giyā ghare ghare

bhava⋅viriñcira, vāñchita je⋅prema,jagate phelila ḍhāli’kāṅgāle pāiyā, khāila nāciyā,bājāiyā karatāli

hāsiyā kā̐diyā, preme gaḍāgaḍi,pulake vyāpila aṅgacaṇḍāle brāhmaṇe, kare kolākuli,kabe vā chila e raṅga

ḍākiyā hā̐kiyā, khola⋅karatāle,gāiyā⋅dhāiyā phiredekhiyā śamana, tarāsa pāiyā,kapāṭa hānila dvāre

e tina bhuvana, ānande bharila,uṭhila maṅgala sorakahe premānanda, emana gaurāṅge,rati nā janmila mora
IDX.en_translation:
There is no one like Gaurāṅga! Has such an incarnation ever appeared before, or will one ever appear again, who preaches such divine love?

He did not kill even the most wicked, fallen, or sinful. Instead, He purified their hearts by going door to door, begging them to accept the Holy Name.

He poured out upon the world that divine love desired even by Śiva and Brahmā. The poor and destitute received it, and they danced and clapped their hands in joy.

Laughing, crying, and rolling on the ground in divine love, their bodies were covered in ecstatic thrills. When was such a sight ever seen—outcasts and priests embracing one another?

Shouting and calling out, they roam about singing and running with drums and cymbals. Seeing this, the god of death became frightened and slammed shut his door.

All the three worlds were filled with joy, and auspicious sounds arose. Premānanda says: for such a Gaurāṅga, no love has awakened in my heart.

IDX.first_line: emana sacira nandana vineprema bali nama ati adbhutasruta hoita kara kane
IDX.search: emana sacira nandana vineprema bali nama ati adbhutasruta hoita kara kane sri krsna namera svaguna mahimakeba janaita ara vrnda vipinera maha madhurimapravesa hoita kara
IDX.author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
emana śacīra nandana vine‘prema’ bali’ nāma, ati adbhuta,śruta hoita kā’ra kāṇe

śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅nāmera, svaguṇa mahimā,kebā jānāita āra ?vṛndā⋅vipinera, mahā madhurimā,praveśa hoita kā’ra

kebā jānāita, rādhāra mādhurya,rasa⋅yaśa camatkāra ?tā’ra anubhava, sāttvika vikāra,gocara chila vā kā’ra

vraje je⋅vilāsa, rāsa⋅mahārāsa,prema parakīya tattvagopīra mahimā, vyabhicārī sīmā,kā’ra avagati chila eta

dhanya kali dhanya, nitāi caitanya,parama karuṇā kari’vidhi⋅agocara, je prema⋅vikāra,prakāśe jagata⋅bhari’

uttama adhama, kichu nā bāchila,jāciyā dileka kolakahe premānande, emana gaurāṅge,antare dhariyā dola
IDX.en_translation:
Without the son of Śacī, who would have ever heard of the wondrous name of “prema”?

Who else could have revealed the inherent glories of Śrī Kṛṣṇa’s name? Who would have entered the deep sweetness of the forests of Vṛndāvana?

Who else would have made known the sweetness of Rādhā and her wonderful excellence in rasa? Who else was aware of her ecstatic transformations and experiences?

The pastimes in Vraja—the rāsa and mahā-rāsa dances—the principle of paramour love, the glories of the gopīs, and the heights of their transitory emotions: who understood all this so deeply?

All glories to the age of Kali! All glories to Nitāi and Caitanya, who out of supreme mercy filled the world by revealing the ecstatic symptoms of divine love, which are beyond the reach of ordinary rules.

They did not discriminate between the exalted and the fallen but embraced everyone. Premānanda says: hold such a Gaurāṅga within your heart.

IDX.first_line: eo ta eka kalira cela
IDX.search: eo ta eka kalira cela matha neda kapni para tilaka nake galaya mala dekhte vaisnavera mata asala sakta kajera bela sahaja bhajana karchena mamu sange laye parera bala
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
eo ta’ eka kalira celā
māthā neḍā, kapni parā,
tilaka nāke, galāya mālā

dekhte vaiṣṇavera mata,
āsala śākta kājera belā
sahaja⋅bhajana karchena māmu,
saṅge la’ye parera bālā

sakhībhāve bhajchena tā’re,
nije ha’ye nandalālā
kṛṣṇa⋅dāsera kathāra chale
mahājanake dicchena śalā

nava⋅rasika āpane māni’,
khācchena ābāra mana⋅kalā
bāula bale, dohāi o bhāi,
dūra kara e līlākhelā
IDX.en_translation:
This is indeed a disciple of Kali (the current age). Shaven-headed, wearing a kaupīna (loincloth), With tilaka on the nose, and mālā (beads) around the neck.

Looking like a Vaiṣṇava, But actually a Śākta (worshipper of the Divine Mother) in practice. The uncle is performing sahaja-bhajana (a pseudo-devotional practice), Taking another's woman along.

Worshipping her in the mood of a sakhī (female confidante of Rādhā), thinking himself to be Nandalāla. Under the pretext of Kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa's words, He is giving 'advice' (or subtly criticizing) the mahājana (great souls).

Considering himself a nava-rasika (a new "expert" in divine mellows), He is again indulging in 'mana-kolā' (self-deception/sense gratification). The Baul says, "I beg you, O brother, Abandon this līlā-khelā (pretense)."

IDX.first_line: esa mama hrdi mandire sri gaura hari
IDX.search: esa mama hrdi mandire sri gaura hari panca tattvarupe tomaya dekhaba ami nayana bhari tumi vraje chile vrajera rakhala gaura he nade hale dvijera chaoyala tumi kalo chile gaura hale ki vasana mane kari
IDX.verses:
esa mama hṛdi mandire śrī⋅gaura⋅hari,
pañca⋅tattvarūpe tomāya dekhaba āmi nayana bhari

(tumi) vraje chile vrajera rākhāla (gaura he)
nade ha’le dvijera chāoyāla,
tumi kālo chile gaura ha’le
ki vāsanā mane kari

kālo’rūpa sājita bhāla, gaurarūpa tā ha’teo bhāla
(ogo) śacīra nandana ekhana tomāra e sannyāsīrūpa
herite nāri

gale vanamālā chila (gaura he) ekhana ke tā keḍe nila,
tulasīra mālā galāya dila ūrdhavapuṇḍra nāsā bhari

tumi kāre dile mohana cūḍā kāre dile pītadhaḍā,
māthā neḍā geruyā vasana parale tumi kemana kari

rādhā rādhā rādhā bali’ bājāte mohana muralī,
muralī vinā ai vadane bala’cha sadāi hari hari

(he) nadera cā̐da nade kare, esa mama hṛda mājhāre,
tomāra gaurarūpa nayane here mānava janama saphala kari
IDX.en_translation:
O Lord Gaura⋅hari! Please come into the temple of my heart. I will gaze upon You in the form of the five spiritual truths to my heart's content.

In Vraja, You were a cowherd boy, O Lord Gaura! In Nadīyā, You became the child of a brāhmaṇa. You were dark-complexioned, and now You have become golden—what secret desire do You hold in Your heart?

The dark form of Lord Kṛṣṇa was beautiful, but Your golden form is even more beautiful than that. O Son of Mother Śacī! Now I cannot bear to see Your form as a renunciant.

Around Your neck was a garland of forest flowers—who has taken that away now? They have placed a necklace of Tulasī beads around Your neck and decorated Your nose with the sacred markings of a devotee.

To whom did You give Your enchanting crown? To whom did You give Your yellow garments? How is it that You have a shaven head and wear the saffron garments of a sannyāsī?

Chanting "Rādhā! Rādhā! Rādhā!" You used to play Your enchanting flute, but now, without a flute, You are always chanting "Hari! Hari!" with that very mouth.

O Moon of Nadīyā! Please come into the core of my heart. By seeing Your golden form with my own eyes, I will make my human birth successful.

IDX.first_line: gaite gaite nama ki dasa hoila
IDX.search: gaite gaite nama ki dasa hoila krsna nityadasa muni hrdaye sphurila janilama mayapase e jada jagate govinda virahe duhkha pai nana mate
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
gāite gāite nāma ki daśā hoila
‘kṛṣṇa nityadāsa muñi’ hṛdaye sphurila

jānilāma māyāpāśe e⋅jaḍa jagate
govinda⋅virahe duḥkha pāi nānā mate

āra je saṁsāra mora nāhi lāge bhāla
kā̐hā jāi, kṛṣṇa heri⋅e cintā viśāla

kā̐dite kā̐dite mora ā̐khi variṣaya
varṣādhārā hena cakṣe hoila udaya

nimeṣa hoila mora śatayuga⋅sama
govinda⋅viraha āra sahite akṣama

śūnya dharātala, caudike dekhiye,
parāṇa udāsa haya
ki kari, ki kari, sthira nāhi haya,
jīvana nāhika raya

vrajavāsigaṇa, mora prāṇa rākha,
dekhāo śrī⋅rādhānāthe
bhakativinoda⋅, minati māniyā,
loohe tāhāre sāthe

śrī⋅kṛṣṇaviraha āra sahite nā pāri
parāṇa chāḍite āra dina dui cāri
IDX.en_translation:
As I sang the Holy Name, what a state I reached! "I am Kṛṣṇa's eternal servant" manifested in my heart.

I realized that in this material world, bound by illusion, I suffer in various ways due to separation from Govinda.

This worldly life no longer seems good to me. "Where shall I go to see Kṛṣṇa?"—this thought is vast.

As I weep, my eyes pour down a rain-like torrent.

A moment has become like a hundred eons for me. I am no longer able to bear separation from Govinda.

The earth is empty; looking all around, my heart becomes indifferent. What shall I do? I cannot remain steady, and my life itself seems to depart.

O residents of Vraja, save my life! Show me the Lord of Rādhā. Bhaktivinoda makes this humble request: please take him along with you.

I can no longer tolerate separation from Śrī Kṛṣṇa. My life will depart in just a few days.

IDX.first_line: gaite govinda nama upajilabhavagrama
IDX.search: gaite govinda nama upajilabhavagrama dekhilama jamunara kule vrsabhanusuta sange syama natavara range basari bajaya nipamule dekhiya jugaladhana asthira hoila mana jnanahara hoilu takhana kataksana nahi jani jnana labha haila mani ara nahi bhela darasana
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
gāite govinda nāma, upajilabhāvagrāma,
dekhilāma jamunāra kūle
vṛṣabhānusutā⋅saṅge, śyāma naṭavara⋅raṅge,
bā̐śarī bājāya nīpamūle

dekhiyā jugala-dhana, asthira hoila mana,
jñānahārā hoilu̐ takhana
katakṣaṇa nāhi jāni, jñāna lābha haila māni,
āra nāhi bhela daraśana

sakhi go, kemate dhariba parāṇa
nimeṣa hoila jugera samāna

śrāvaṇera dhārā, ā̐khi variṣaya,
śūnya bhela dharātala
govinda⋅virahe, prāṇa nāhi rahe,
kemane bā̐ciba bala

bhakativinoda, asthira hoiyā,
punaḥ nāmāśraya kari’
ḍāke rādhā-nātha, diyā daraśana,
prāṇa rākha, nahe mari
IDX.en_translation:
While singing the name of Govinda, a multitude of divine emotions arose. I saw on the banks of the Yamunā, along with the daughter of Vṛṣabhānu, Kṛṣṇa—the best of dancers—playing His flute at the base of a kadamba tree.

Seeing that Divine Couple, my mind became restless and I lost all consciousness. I do not know how much time passed before I regained consciousness, but the vision was gone.

O friend, how will I sustain my life? A moment feels like an eon.

A stream of tears like the rains of Śrāvaṇa pours from my eyes; the whole world has become void. In separation from Govinda, my life cannot remain; tell me, how will I survive?

Bhaktivinoda, becoming restless, again takes shelter of the Holy Name. He calls out to the Lord of Rādhā: "By granting Your vision, please save my life, otherwise I will die!"

IDX.first_line: gajendragamane jaya sakaruna dithe caya
IDX.search: gajendragamane jaya sakaruna dithe caya padabhare mahi talamala matta siŋha jini kampamana medini pasandigana suniya vikala aota avadhuta karunara sindhu preme garagara mana kare hari sankirtana patita pavana dinabandhu
IDX.author: balarāma dāsa
IDX.verses:
gajendra-gamane jāya, sakaruṇa diṭhe cāya,
padabhare mahī ṭalamala
matta siŋha jini’, kampa-māna medinī,
pāṣaṇḍi-gaṇa śuniyā vikala

āota avadhūta karuṇāra sindhu
preme garagara mana, kare hari⋅saṅkīrtana,
patita pāvana dīna-bandhu

huṅkāra kariyā cale, acala sacala naḍe,
preme bhāse amara⋅samāja
sahacara-gaṇa saṅge, vividha⋅khelana raṅge,
alakhite kare saba kāja

śeṣaśāyī⋅saṅkarṣaṇa, avatārī⋅nārāyaṇa,
jā̐’ra aṁśa kalāte gaṇana
kṛpā-sindhu bhakti-dātā, jagatera hitakartā,
sei rāma rohiṇī-nandana

jā̐’ra līlā lāvaṇya-dhāma, āgame nigame gāna,
jā̐’ra rūpa madanamohana
ebe akiñcana veśe, phire pa̐hu deśe deśe,
uddhāra karaye tri-bhuvana

vrajera vaidagdhī sāra, jata jata līlā āra,
pāibāre jadi thāke mana
balarāmadāse kaya, manoratha siddhi haya,
bhaja bhaja śrī-pādacaraṇa
IDX.en_translation:
He walks with the gait of a lordly elephant, gazing with compassion. The earth trembles under the weight of His steps. Conquering the pride of a mad lion, He makes the earth shake, and the atheists become distressed upon hearing His roar.

Here comes the Avadhūta, the ocean of mercy! His mind is overwhelmed with divine love, and He performs the congregational chanting of Hari. He is the purifier of the fallen and the friend of the humble.

He walks with a roar, shaking the movable and immovable. The assembly of demigods floats in love. With His companions, in the joy of various pastimes, He accomplishes all tasks unseen.

He is Lord Saṅkarṣaṇa resting on Ananta Śeṣa, the source of all incarnations, Nārāyaṇa. His parts and portions are countless. He is the ocean of mercy, the giver of devotion, and the benefactor of the world. He is Lord Rāma (Balarāma), the son of Rohiṇī.

His pastimes are an abode of beauty, sung in the Āgamas and Vedas. His form captivates even Cupid. Now, in the guise of a humble mendicant, the Lord wanders from land to land, liberating the three worlds.

He is the essence of the charm of Vraja and all other pastimes. If your mind desires to attain them, Balarāma Dāsa says: "Your desires will be fulfilled. Just worship, worship His divine lotus feet."

IDX.first_line: gajendragamane nitai calaye manthare
IDX.search: gajendragamane nitai calaye manthare jare dekhe tare bhasaya premera pathare patita durgata papira ghare ghare giya brahmara durlabha prema dicchena jaciya
IDX.author: devakīnandana⋅dāsa
IDX.verses:
gajendragamane nitāi calaye manthare
jā’re dekhe tā’re bhāsāya premera pāthāre

patita durgata pāpīra ghare ghare giyā
brahmāra durlabha prema dicchena jāciyā

je nā laya, tā’re kaya dante tṛṇa dhari’
‘āmāre kiniyā loo, bala gaura⋅hari

to’ sabāra lāgiyā kṛṣṇera avatāra
śuna bhāi, gaurāṅga-sundara nadīyāra

je pa̐hu gokulapure nandera kumāra
to’ sabāra lāgi’ ebe kaila avatāra’
śuniyā kā̐daye pāpī caraṇe dhariyā
pulake pūrala aṅga gara gara hiyā

tā’re kole kari’ nitāi jāi ānaṭhāma
hena-mate preme bhāsāola pura⋅grāma

devakīnandana bole mui abhāgiyā
ḍubilu̐ viṣaya⋅kūpe nitāi nā bhajiyā
IDX.en_translation:
Lord Nityānanda walks slowly and majestically like a lordly elephant. Whomever He sees, He immerses them in the ocean of divine love.

Going from house to house of the fallen, the distressed, and the sinful, He begs them to accept the divine love that is rare even for Lord Brahmā.

To those who do not accept it, He says, holding a straw between His teeth, "Please purchase Me as your servant by chanting the name of Gaura⋅hari!"

"For the sake of all of you, Lord Kṛṣṇa has incarnated. Listen, O brother! He is Gaurāṅgasundara of Nadiyā."

"That Lord, who was the son of Nanda Mahārāja in Gokula, has now incarnated for all of you." Hearing this, the sinners cry, holding His feet, their bodies filled with ecstasy and their hearts overwhelmed.

Embracing them, Lord Nityānanda goes to another place. In this way, He inundated every town and village with divine love.

Devakīnandana Dāsa says: "I am so unfortunate. I have drowned in the well of material sense gratification, not having worshipped Lord Nityānanda."

IDX.first_line: gangeya campeya tadid vinindi
IDX.search: gangeya campeya tadid vinindi rocih pravaha snapitatmavrnde bandhuka bandhu dyuti divyavaso vrnde numaste caranaravindam bimbadharoditvara mandahasya nasagra muktadyuti dipitasye vicitra ratnabharanasriyadhye vrnde numaste caranaravindam
IDX.author: śrīla viśvanātha cakrabartī ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
gāṅgeya⋅cāmpeya⋅taḍid⋅vinindi⋅
rociḥ⋅pravāha⋅snapitātmavṛnde!
bandhūka⋅bandhu⋅dyuti⋅divyavāso
vṛnde! numaste caraṇāravindam

bimbādharoditvara⋅mandahāsya⋅
nāsāgra⋅muktādyuti⋅dīpitāsye!
vicitra⋅ratnābharaṇāśriyāḍhye!
vṛnde! numaste caraṇāravindam

samasta⋅vaikuṇṭha⋅śiromaṇau śrī⋅
kṛṣṇasya vṛndāvana⋅dhanya⋅dhāmni
dattādhikāre vṛṣabhānu⋅putryā
vṛnde! numaste caraṇāravindam

tvadājñayā pallava⋅puṣpa⋅bhṛṅga⋅
mṛgādibhirmādhava⋅keli⋅kuñjāḥ
madhvādibhirbhānti vibhūṣyamāṇā
vṛnde! numaste caraṇāravindam

tvadīya⋅dūtyena nikuñja⋅yūnoḥ⋅
ratyutkayoḥ keli⋅vilāsa⋅siddhiḥ
tvatsaubhagaṁ kena nirucyatāṁ tad
vṛnde! numaste caraṇāravindam

rāsābhilāṣo vasatiśca vṛndā⋅
vane tvadīśāṅghri⋅saroja⋅sevā
labhyā ca puṁsāṁ kṛpayā tavaiva
vṛnde! numaste caraṇāravindam

tvaṁ kīrtyase sātvata⋅tantravidbhi⋅
rlīlābhidhānā kila kṛṣṇa⋅śaktiḥ
tavaiva mūrtistulasī nṛloke
vṛnde! numaste caraṇāravindam

bhaktyā vihīnā aparādha⋅lakṣaiḥ
kṣiptāśca kāmādi⋅taraṅga⋅madhye
kṛpāmayi! tvāṁ śaraṇaṁ prapannā
vṛnde! numaste caraṇāravindam

vṛndāṣṭakaṁ jaḥ śṛṇuyāt paṭhed vā
vṛndāvanādhīśa⋅padābja⋅bhṛṅgaḥ
sa prāpya vṛndāvana⋅nitya⋅vāsaṁ
tat⋅prema⋅sevāṁ labhate kṛtārthaḥ
IDX.en_translation:
O Vṛndā Devī! Your inner self is bathed by a stream of splendor that surpasses gold, jasmine flowers, and lightning! O You whose divine garments possess the effulgence of a red flower! O Vṛndā Devī, I offer obeisances to Your lotus feet.

O Vṛndā Devī! Your gentle smile emanates from lips like a red fruit, and Your face is illuminated by the luster of the pearls on the tip of Your nose. O You who are enriched by the beauty of various jeweled ornaments! O Vṛndā Devī, I offer obeisances to Your lotus feet.

O Vṛndā Devī! You have been given authority by the daughter of Vṛṣabhānu in Lord Kṛṣṇa's most fortunate abode, Vṛndāvana, which is the crest jewel of all the spiritual planets! O Vṛndā Devī, I offer obeisances to Your lotus feet.

O Vṛndā Devī! By Your order, the pleasure groves of Kṛṣṇa are splendidly decorated with tender leaves, flowers, bees, deer, honey, and other things! O Vṛndā Devī, I offer obeisances to Your lotus feet.

O Vṛndā Devī! By Your ambassadorship, the playful pastimes of the very eager young couple, Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa, in the groves are perfected! Who can describe Your great fortune? O Vṛndā Devī, I offer obeisances to Your lotus feet.

O Vṛndā Devī! The desire for the rāsa dance, residence in Vṛndāvana, and service to the lotus feet of Your Lord are all attainable by people only by Your mercy! O Vṛndā Devī, I offer obeisances to Your lotus feet.

O Vṛndā Devī! You are glorified by those who know the scriptures as the energy of Kṛṣṇa named Līlā! Indeed, Your very form is Tulasi in the human world. O Vṛndā Devī, I offer obeisances to Your lotus feet.

O Vṛndā Devī! We who are devoid of devotion, assailed by millions of offenses and thrown into the midst of the waves of lust and other material desires, have taken shelter of You, O merciful one! O Vṛndā Devī, I offer obeisances to Your lotus feet.

Whoever hears or reads these eight verses glorifying Vṛndā becomes a bee at the lotus feet of the Lord of Vṛndāvana. He attains eternal residence in Vṛndāvana and, having achieved his goal, attains loving devotional service to Him.

IDX.first_line: gati ganjita mattatara dviradam
IDX.search: gati ganjita mattatara dviradam rada nindita sundara kunda madam madanarbuda rupa mada ghna rucim rucira smita manjari manju mukham mukharikrta venu hrta pramadam mada valgita locana tamarasam rasa pura vikasaka keli param paramartha parayana loka gatim
IDX.author: śrīla rūpa gosvāmī
IDX.verses:
gati⋅gañjita⋅mattatara⋅dviradaṁ
rada⋅nindita⋅sundara⋅kunda⋅madam
madanārbuda⋅rūpa⋅mada⋅ghna⋅ruciṁ
rucira⋅smita⋅mañjari mañju⋅mukham

mukharīkṛta⋅veṇu⋅hṛta⋅pramadaṁ
mada⋅valgita⋅locana⋅tāmarasam
rasa⋅pūra⋅vikāsaka⋅keli⋅paraṁ
paramārtha⋅parāyaṇa⋅loka⋅gatim

gati⋅maṇḍita⋅yāmuna⋅tīra⋅bhuvaṁ
bhuvaneśvara⋅vandita⋅cāru⋅padam
padakojjvala⋅komala⋅kaṇṭha⋅rucaṁ
rucakātta⋅viśeṣaka⋅valgutaram

tarala⋅pracalāka⋅parīta⋅śikhaṁ
śikharīndra⋅dhṛti pratipanna⋅bhujam
bhujagendra⋅phaṇāṅgaṇa⋅raṅga⋅dharaṁ
dhara⋅kandara⋅khelana⋅lubdha⋅hṛdam

hṛdayālu⋅suhṛd⋅gaṇa⋅datta⋅mahaṁ
mahanīya⋅kathā⋅kula⋅dhūta⋅kalim
kalitākhila⋅durjaya⋅bāhu⋅balaṁ
bala⋅vallava⋅śāvaka⋅saṁnihitam

hita⋅sādhu⋅samīhita⋅kalpa⋅taruṁ
taruṇī⋅gaṇa⋅nūtana⋅puṣpa⋅śaram
śaraṇāgata⋅rakṣaṇa⋅dakṣatamaṁ
tam asādhu⋅kulotpala⋅caṇḍa⋅karam

kara⋅padma⋅milat⋅kusuma⋅stavakaṁ
baka⋅dānava⋅matta⋅karīndra⋅harim
hariṇī⋅gaṇa⋅hāraka⋅veṇu⋅kalaṁ
kala⋅kaṇṭha⋅ravojjvala⋅kaṇṭha⋅raṇam

raṇa⋅khaṇḍita⋅durjana⋅puṇya⋅janaṁ
jana⋅maṅgala⋅kīrti⋅latā⋅prabhavam
bhava⋅sāgara⋅kumbha⋅janām aguṇaṁ
guṇa⋅saṅga⋅vivarjita⋅bhakta⋅gaṇam

gaṇanātiga⋅divya⋅guṇollasitaṁ
smita⋅raśmi⋅sahodara⋅vaktra⋅varam
vara⋅dṛpta⋅vṛṣāsura⋅dāra⋅ghanaṁ
ghana⋅vibhrama⋅veśa⋅vihāramayam

maya⋅putra⋅tamaḥ⋅kṣaya⋅pūrṇa⋅vidhuṁ
vidhurīkṛta⋅dānava⋅rāja⋅kulam
[...]
kula⋅nandanam atra namāmi harim

urasi parisphurad⋅indibam indindiba⋅mandiba⋅srajollasitam
harim aṅganātimaṅgala⋅maṅgala⋅sac⋅candanaṁ vande
IDX.en_translation:
He whose gait mocks the very intoxicated elephant; whose teeth censure the beautiful jasmine and Kunda flowers; whose luster destroys the pride of the beauty of ten million Cupids; whose charming face has a blossom of a beautiful smile.

He who steals the joy of others by the vocalized flute; whose lotus eyes dance with intoxication; who is devoted to pastimes that expand the flood of rasa; the Goal of the people devoted to the supreme object.

He whose movements adorn the land of the bank of the Yamunā; whose beautiful feet are praised by the Lord of the world; the luster of whose soft neck is bright with a medallion; who is very lovely, having taken a tilaka of pigment.

He whose head is surrounded by trembling peacock feathers; whose arm is engaged in holding the King of Mountains; who holds a festival on the courtyard of the hoods of the King of Snakes; whose heart is greedy for playing in the caves of the Mountain.

To whom a festival is given by His kind-hearted friends; who has shaken off Kali by the multitude of glorious topics; who holds all unconquerable strength of arms; who is accompanied by the strong boys of the cowherds.

The desire tree desired by the beneficial saints; the fresh flower-arrow of the group of young women; most expert in protecting the surrendered; the fierce sun for the lotuses of the assembly of the wicked.

A bunch of flowers meeting in His lotus hand; a lion to the intoxicated elephant of the Baka demon; the low sound of whose flute captivates the herds of deer; whose throat is bright with the low sound of the cuckoos.

Who cut down the wicked people in battle for the sake of the pious people; the source of the creeper of fame auspicious for the people; Agastya for the ocean of material existence; without material qualities; whose group of devotees is devoid of attachment to the modes.

Shining with divine qualities beyond counting; whose excellent face is the brother of the ray of His smile; the cloud for the wife of the excellent proud bull-demon (Ariṣṭa); consisting of pastimes in dense, amorous dress.

The full moon for the destruction of the darkness of the son of Maya (Vyomāsura); who distressed the host of demon kings. I bow down here to Hari, the delight of the family.

I worship Hari, shining on His chest with Indirā (Lakṣmī), shining with a garland like a temple of bees, the true sandalwood of auspiciousness for the very auspicious women.

IDX.first_line: gauranga balite habe pulaka sarira
IDX.search: gauranga balite habe pulaka sarira hari hari balite nayane babe nira ara kabe nitaicadera karuna hoibe samsara vasana mora kabe tuccha habe
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
‘gaurāṅga’ balite habe pulaka śarīra
‘hari hari’ balite nayane ba’be nīra

āra kabe nitāicā̐dera karuṇā hoibe
saṁsāra⋅vāsanā mora kabe tuccha ha’be

viṣaya chāḍiyā kabe śuddha ha’be mana
kabe hāma heraba śrī-vṛndāvana

rūpa⋅raghunātha⋅pade hoibe ākuti
kabe hāma bujhaba se jugala⋅pīriti

rūpa⋅raghunātha⋅pade rahu mora āśa
prārthanā karaye sadā narottama⋅dāsa
IDX.en_translation:
When will my body shiver while saying 'Gaurāṅga'? When will tears flow from my eyes while saying 'Hari Hari'?

When will Nitāi-cānda be merciful? When will my worldly desires become insignificant?

When will my mind be purified by giving up sense objects? When will I see Śrī Vṛndāvana?

When will there be eagerness for the feet of Rūpa and Raghunātha? When will I understand the love of the Divine Couple?

My hope rests at the feet of Rūpa and Raghunātha; Narottama Dāsa always makes this prayer.

IDX.first_line: gauranga karuna kara dina hina jane
IDX.search: gauranga karuna kara dina hina jane mo sama patita prabhu nahi tri bhuvane dante trna dhari gaura daki he tomara krpa kari eso amara hrdaye mandire
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses: gaurāṅga karuṇā kara, dīna hīna jane mo⋅sama patita prabhu, nāhi tri⋅bhuvane dante tṛṇa dhari’ gaura, ḍāki he tomāra kṛpā kari’ eso āmāra, hṛdaye mandire jadi dayā nā karibe, patita dekhiyā patita⋅pāvana nāma, kisera lāgiyā paḍechi bhava tuphāne, nāhika nistāra śrī caraṇa taraṇī dāne, dāse kara pāra śrī kṛṣṇa caitanya prabhu, dāsera anudāsa prārthanā karaye sadā, narottama⋅dāsa
IDX.en_translation:
O Gaurāṅga! Be merciful to the lowly and destitute person. O Lord, there is no fallen soul like me in the three worlds.

Holding a straw in my teeth, O Gaura, I call to You: please be merciful and come into the temple of my heart.

If You do not give mercy seeing the fallen, then what is the name 'Patita-pāvana' for?

I have fallen into the storm of material existence; there is no escape. Giving the boat of Your holy feet, take the servant across.

Narottama Dāsa, the servant of the servant of Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya Prabhu, always makes this prayer.

IDX.first_line: gauranga tumi more daya na chadiha
IDX.search: gauranga tumi more daya na chadiha apana kariya ranga carane rakhiha tomara carana lagi saba teyaginu sitala carana pana sarana loinu
IDX.author: vasudeva ghoṣa
IDX.verses:
gaurāṅga tumi more dayā nā chāḍiha
āpana kariyā, rāṅgā caraṇe rākhiha

tomāra caraṇa lāgi saba teyāginu
śītala caraṇa pāñā śaraṇa loinu

ekule okule muñi, dilu tilāñjali
rākhiha caraṇe more, āpanāra bali

vāsudeva ghoṣa kahe caraṇe dhariyā
kṛpā kari rākha more pada-chāyā diyā
IDX.en_translation:
Gaurāṅga, please do not abandon your mercy on me. Make me your own, and keep me at your red lotus feet.

For the sake of your feet, I have given up everything. Having found your cooling feet, I have taken shelter.

I have offered oblations (renounced all ties) to both this world and the next. Keep me at your feet, accepting me as your own.

Vāsudeva Ghoṣa says, holding onto your feet, Please mercifully keep me by giving me the shade of your feet.

IDX.first_line: gauranga tumi more daya na chadihaapana kariya ranga carane rakhiha
IDX.search: gauranga tumi more daya na chadihaapana kariya ranga carane rakhiha tomara carana lagi saba teyaginusitala carana pana sarana loinu
IDX.author: vasudeva ghoṣa
IDX.verses:
gaurāṅga tumi more dayā nā chāḍihaāpana kariyā, rāṅgā caraṇe rākhiha

tomāra caraṇa lāgi saba teyāginuśītala caraṇa pāñā śaraṇa loinu

IDX.en_translation:
Gaurāṅga, please do not abandon your mercy on me. Make me your own, and keep me at your red lotus feet.

For the sake of your feet, I have given up everything. Having found your cooling feet, I have taken shelter.

I have offered oblations (renounced all ties) to both this world and the next. Keep me at your feet, accepting me as your own.

Vāsudeva Ghoṣa says, holding onto your feet, Please mercifully keep me by giving me the shade of your feet.

IDX.first_line: gaurangera duti pada jara dhana sampadase jane bhakati rasasaragaurangera madhura lila jara karne pravesilahrdaya nirmala bhela tara
IDX.search: gaurangera duti pada jara dhana sampadase jane bhakati rasasaragaurangera madhura lila jara karne pravesilahrdaya nirmala bhela tara je gaurangera nama laya tara haya premodayatare muni jai baliharigauranga gunete jhure nityalila tare sphurese jana bhakati adhikari
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
gaurāṅgera du’ṭī pada, jā’ra dhana sampada,se jāne bhakati⋅rasasāragaurāṅgera madhura līlā, jā’ra karṇe praveśilā,hṛdaya nirmala bhela tā’ra

je gaurāṅgera nāma laya, tā’ra haya premodaya,tā’re muñi jāi balihārigaurāṅga⋅guṇete jhure, nityalīlā tā’re sphure,se⋅jana bhakati⋅adhikārī

gaurāṅgera saṅgi-gaṇe, nitya-siddha kari’ māne,se jāya vrajendrasuta⋅pāśaśrī⋅gauḍamaṇḍala⋅bhūmi, jebā jāne cintāmaṇi,tā’ra haya vraja-bhūme vāsa

gauraprema⋅rasārṇave, se taraṅge jebā ḍube,se rādhā⋅mādhava⋅antaraṅgagṛhe vā vanete thāke, ‘hā gaurāṅga’ ba’le ḍāke,narottama māge tā’ra saṅga
IDX.en_translation:
He who considers Gaurāṅga’s lotus feet as his treasure knows the essence of devotional mellows. He whose ears Gaurāṅga’s sweet pastimes enter finds his heart becoming pure.

Love awakens in whoever chants Gaurāṅga’s name; I adore such a person. He who weeps, overwhelmed by Gaurāṅga’s qualities, and in whom the eternal pastimes become manifest, is truly qualified for devotion.

He who considers Gaurāṅga’s associates to be eternally perfect goes to the side of Kṛṣṇa. He who knows the land of Bengal to be a wish-fulfilling jewel resides in the spiritual world of Vraja.

He who dives into the waves of the ocean of Gaurāṅga’s love and mellows becomes intimate with Rādhā and Mādhava. Whether he stays at home or in the forest, if he cries out “O Gaurāṅga!”, Narottama begs for his association.

IDX.first_line: gaya gora madhura svarehare krsna hare krsna krsna krsna hare harehare rama hare rama rama rama hare hare
IDX.search: gaya gora madhura svarehare krsna hare krsna krsna krsna hare harehare rama hare rama rama rama hare hare grhe thaka vane thaka sada hari bale dakasukhe duhkhe bhula nakavadane harinama kara re
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
gāya gorā madhura svarehare kṛṣṇa hare kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa hare harehare rāma hare rāma rāma rāma hare hare

gṛhe thāka, vane thāka, sadā ‘hari’ ba’le ḍāka,sukhe duḥkhe bhula nā’ka,vadane harināma kara re

māyājāle baddha ha’ye, ācha miche kāja la’ye,ekhanoo cetana pe’ye,rādhā⋅mādhava nāma bala re

jīvana hoila śeṣa, nā bhajile hṛṣīkeśa,bhaktivinoda⋅(ei) upadeśa,ekabāra nāmarase mātare
IDX.en_translation:
Gaura⋅candra sings in a sweet voice: Hare Kṛṣṇa Hare Kṛṣṇa Kṛṣṇa Kṛṣṇa Hare Hare, Hare Rāma Hare Rāma Rāma Rāma Hare Hare.

Whether you stay at home or in the forest, always call out “Hari!” In happiness or sorrow, never forget Him; chant the Holy Name with your mouth.

Bound by the net of illusion, you are engaged in useless work. Now that you have regained consciousness, chant the names of Rādhā and Mādhava.

Life will come to an end if you do not worship the Lord of the senses. This is the instruction of Bhaktivinoda: become intoxicated just once in the sweetness of the Holy Name.

IDX.first_line: gaya goracada jivera tarehare krsna hare
IDX.search: gaya goracada jivera tarehare krsna hare hare krsna hare krsna krsna krsna hare harehare rama hare rama rama rama hare hare
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
gāya gorācā̐da jīvera tarehare kṛṣṇa hare

hare kṛṣṇa hare kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa hare hare,hare rāma hare rāma rāma rāma hare hare

ekabāra bala rasanā uccaiḥsvare(bala) nandera nandana, jaśodā⋅jīvana,śrī-rādhā-ramaṇa prema⋅bhare

(bala) śrī-madhusūdana, gopī⋅prāṇadhana,muralīvadana nṛtya ka’re(bala) agha⋅nisūdana, pūtanā⋅ghātana,brahma⋅vimohana, ūrdhva⋅kare
IDX.en_translation:
Gaura⋅candra sings for the sake of all souls: “Hare Kṛṣṇa!”

Hare Kṛṣṇa Hare Kṛṣṇa Kṛṣṇa Kṛṣṇa Hare Hare, Hare Rāma Hare Rāma Rāma Rāma Hare Hare

O tongue, just once call out loudly: “Chant the names of the son of Nanda, the life of Yaśodā, and the enjoyer of Śrī Rādhā with great love!

“Chant the names of the slayer of Madhu, the treasure of the gopīs’ lives, and the flute-player, while dancing. Chant the names of the slayer of Agha, the killer of Pūtanā, and the one who bewildered Brahmā, with Your arms raised high!”

IDX.first_line: gayata radha madhava lilam
IDX.search: gayata radha madhava lilam kuruta matim rasaranjita silam vrndaranya caracara vrndam srayata maha rasa vaibhava vandam
IDX.author: śrīla bhaktisiddhānta sarasvatī ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
gāyata rādhā mādhava⋅līlaṁ,
kuruta matiṁ rasarañjita⋅śīlām

vṛndāraṇya carācara vṛndaṁ
śrayata mahā⋅rasa vaibhava⋅vandam

paśyata rādhā keli nikuñjaṁ
prakaṭa mahādbhuta⋅rati⋅rasa⋅puñjam

puline puline tapana⋅sutāyāḥ
bhramatha na jatra prasarati⋅māyā

paramānanda rasāmṛta⋅sāre
nayata mano vraja⋅rāja⋅kumāre

muñcata viṣaya⋅viṣama rasa⋅gandhaṁ,
ghaṭayata haripade dṛḍha⋅rati⋅bandham

mṛgayata rādhā⋅pada⋅rasa⋅bhājaṁ,
paricinūtojvalara⋅rasa bāram

iti hita⋅sāra sarasvatī⋅gītaṁ,
janayata kañcana bhāvam⋅adhitama
IDX.en_translation:
Sing of the pastimes of Rādhā and Mādhava. Make your mind joyful with pure transcendental mellows.

In the Vṛndāvana forest, the multitude of all living beings takes shelter in the adoration of its great opulent mellows.

Behold Rādhā's playful grove, where an abundance of wonderful divine love is manifested.

On every sandy bank of the Yamunā, wander where illusion does not spread.

Bring your mind to the Son of Nanda Mahārāja, who is the essence of the nectar of supreme bliss.

Give up the poisonous scent of material objects and fix a firm attachment at the feet of Lord Hari.

Seek those devoted to the mellow of Śrīmati Rādhā⋅rāṇī's feet, realizing the brilliant essence of divine love.

Thus this song by Bhaktisiddhānta Sarasvatī, ardently presented, generates some spiritual emotion.

IDX.first_line: gopinatha 1 mama nivedana sunavisayi durjana sada kamaratakichu nahi mora guna
IDX.search: gopinatha 1 mama nivedana sunavisayi durjana sada kamaratakichu nahi mora guna gopinatha amara bharasa tumitomara carane loinu saranatomara kinkara ami
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
gopīnātha (1), mama nivedana śunaviṣayī durjana, sadā kāma-rata,kichu nāhi mora guṇa

gopīnātha, āmāra bharasā tumitomāra caraṇe, loinu śaraṇa,tomāra kiṅkara āmi

gopīnātha, kemane śodhibe morenā jāni bhakati, karme jaḍamati,paḍechi saṁsāra⋅ghore

gopīnātha, sakali tomāra māyānāhi mama bala, jñāna sunirmala,svādhīna nahe e kāyā

gopīnātha, niyata caraṇe sthānamāge e pāmara, kā̐diyā kā̐diyā,karahe karuṇā dāna

gopīnātha, tumi ta’ sakali pāradurjane tārite, tomāra śakati,ke āche pāpīra āra

gopīnātha, tumi kṛpā⋅pārābārajīvera kāraṇe, āsiyā prapañce,līlā kaile suvistāra

gopīnātha, āmi ki doṣe doṣīasura⋅sakala, pāila caraṇa,vinoda thākila vasi’
IDX.en_translation:
Gopīnātha, please hear my appeal. I am a worldly, wicked person, always engrossed in material desires. I have no good qualities.

Gopīnātha, You are my only hope. I take refuge at Your feet; I am Your servant.

Gopīnātha, how will You purify me? I know no devotion, my mind is dull in action, and I have fallen into the whirlpool of material existence.

Gopīnātha, everything is Your illusion. I have no strength, nor pure knowledge; this body is not independent.

Gopīnātha, this fallen one constantly begs for a place at Your feet; weeping, I ask You to please bestow Your mercy.

Gopīnātha, You can do everything. It is Your power to deliver the wicked; who else is there for a sinner like me?

Gopīnātha, You are an ocean of mercy. For the sake of all souls, You came into this material world and extensively performed Your divine pastimes.

Gopīnātha, what fault have I committed? All the demons received the shelter of Your feet, yet Bhaktivinoda remains left behind.

IDX.first_line: gopinatha 2 ghucao samsara jvalaavidya yatana ara nahi sahejanama marana mala
IDX.search: gopinatha 2 ghucao samsara jvalaavidya yatana ara nahi sahejanama marana mala gopinatha ami ta kamera dasavisaya vasana jagiche hrdayephadiche karama phasa
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
gopīnātha (2), ghucāo saṁsāra⋅jvālāavidyā⋅yātanā, āra nāhi sahe,janama⋅maraṇa⋅mālā

gopīnātha, āmi ta’ kāmera dāsaviṣaya⋅vāsanā, jāgiche hṛdaye,phā̐diche karama phā̐sa

gopīnātha, kabe vā jāgiba āmikāma-rūpa ari, dūre teyāgiba,hṛdaye sphuribe tumi

gopīnātha, āmi ta’ tomāra janatomāre chāḍiyā, saṁsāra bhajinu,bhuliyā āpana dhana

gopīnātha, tumi ta’ sakali jānaāpanāra jane, daṇḍiyā ekhana,śrī-caraṇe deha sthāna

gopīnātha, ei ki vicāra tavavimukha dekhiyā, chāḍa nija⋅jane,nā kara’ karuṇā⋅laba

gopīnātha, āmi ta’ mūrakha atikise bhāla haya, kabhu nā bujhinu,tāi hena mama gati

gopīnātha, tumi ta’ paṇḍitavaramūḍhera maṅgala, tumi anveṣibe,e dāse nā bhāva’ para
IDX.en_translation:
Gopīnātha, dispel the pains of material existence. I can no longer tolerate the agony of ignorance and the cycle of birth and death.

Gopīnātha, I am truly a servant of lust. Material desires awaken in my heart, setting the trap of actions and reactions.

Gopīnātha, when will I awaken? I will cast away the enemy of lust, and You will manifest in my heart.

Gopīnātha, I am indeed Your person. Leaving You, I worshipped the material world, forgetting my own true treasure You.

Gopīnātha, You know everything. Punishing Your own person now, please grant me a place at Your lotus feet.

Gopīnātha, is this Your judgment? Seeing my aversion, You abandon Your own person, showing no drop of mercy.

Gopīnātha, I am indeed very foolish. I never understood what is good for me, therefore such is my condition.

Gopīnātha, You are indeed the wisest. You will seek the welfare of this fool; do not consider this servant a stranger.

IDX.first_line: gopinatha 3 amara upaya naitumi krpa kari amare loilesamsare uddhara pai
IDX.search: gopinatha 3 amara upaya naitumi krpa kari amare loilesamsare uddhara pai gopinatha padechi mayara pheredhana dara suta ghireche amarekamete rekheche jere
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
gopīnātha (3), āmāra upāya nāitumi kṛpā kari’, āmāre loile,saṁsāre uddhāra pāi

gopīnātha, paḍechi māyāra pheredhana⋅dārā⋅suta, ghireche āmāre,kāmete rekheche jere

gopīnātha, mana je pāgala moranā māne śāsana, sadā acetana,viṣaye ra’yeche ghora

gopīnātha, hāra je menechi āmianeka jatana, hoila viphala,ekhana bharasā tumi

gopīnātha, kemane hoibe gatiprabala indriya⋅, vaśībhūta mana,nā chāḍe viṣaya⋅rati

gopīnātha, hṛdaye basiyā moramanake śamiyā, laha nija⋅pāne,ghucibe vipada ghora

gopīnātha, anātha dekhiyā moretumi hṛṣīkeśa, hṛṣīka damiyā,tāra’ he saṁsṛti⋅ghore

gopīnātha, galāya legeche phā̐sakṛpā⋅asi dhari’, bandhana chediyā,vinode karaha dāsa
IDX.en_translation:
Gopīnātha, I have no other recourse. If You mercifully accept me, I will find deliverance from material existence.

Gopīnātha, I have fallen into the trap of illusion. Wealth, wife, and children have surrounded me, and desire has kept me entangled.

Gopīnātha, my mind is truly mad. It does not obey any discipline, is always bewildered, and remains deeply absorbed in worldly affairs.

Gopīnātha, I have accepted defeat. Many efforts have been in vain. Now You are my only hope.

Gopīnātha, how will I make any spiritual progress? The powerful senses and my subjugated mind do not give up their attachment to sense objects.

Gopīnātha, sitting in my heart and pacifying my mind, please draw me toward Yourself. Then this terrible danger will cease.

Gopīnātha, seeing me as helpless, You—the Lord of the senses—please control my senses and deliver me from the dreadful cycle of material existence.

Gopīnātha, a noose has been tied around my neck. Taking the sword of Your mercy and cutting this bondage, please make Bhaktivinoda Your servant.

IDX.first_line: gopya ucuhjayati tedhikam janmana vrajahsrayate indira sasvadatra hidayita drsyatam diksu tavaka stvayi dhrtasavastvam vicinvate
IDX.search: gopya ucuhjayati tedhikam janmana vrajahsrayate indira sasvadatra hidayita drsyatam diksu tavaka stvayi dhrtasavastvam vicinvate saradudasaye sadhujatasatsarasijodara srimusa drsasurata natha tesulka dasikavarada nighnato neha kim vadhah
IDX.author: śrī⋅vedavyāsa
IDX.verses:
gopya ūcuḥjayati te‛dhikaṁ janmanā vrajaḥśrayate indirā śaśvadatra hidayita dṛśyatāṁ dikṣu tāvakā⋅stvayi dhṛtāsavastvāṁ vicinvate

śaradudāśaye sādhujātasatsarasijodara śrīmuṣā dṛśāsurata⋅nātha te‛śulka⋅dāsikāvarada nighnato neha kiṁ vadhaḥ

viṣa⋅jalāpyayād vyāla⋅rākṣasād⋅varṣa⋅mārutād vaidyutānalātvṛṣamayātmajād viśvato bhayād⋅ṛṣabha te vayaṁ rakṣitā muhuḥ

na khalu gopīkānandano bhavānakhila⋅dehinām antarātmadṛkvikhanasārthito viśva⋅guptayesakha udeyivān sātvatāṁ kule

viracitābhayaṁ vṛṣṇi⋅dhūrya tecaraṇamīyuṣāṁ saṁsṛterbhayātkara⋅saroruhaṁ kānta kāmadaṁśirasi dhehi naḥ śrī⋅kara⋅graham

vraja⋅janārtihan vīra joṣitāṁnija⋅jana⋅smaya⋅dhvaṁsana⋅smitabhaja sakhe bhavatkiṅkarīḥ sma nojalaruhānanaṁ cāru darśaya

praṇata⋅dehināṁ pāpa⋅karṣaṇaṁtṛṇa⋅carānugaṁ śrī⋅niketanamphaṇi⋅phaṇārpitaṁ te padāmvujaṁkṛṇu kuceṣu naḥ kṛndhi hṛcchayam

madhurayā girā valgu⋅vākyayābudha⋅manojñayā puṣkarekṣaṇavidhikarīrimā vīra muhyatī⋅radhara⋅sīdhunāpyāyayasva naḥ

tava kathāmṛtaṁ tapta⋅jīvanaṁkavibhirīḍitaṁ kalmaṣāpahamśravaṇa⋅maṅgalaṁ śrīmadātataṁbhuvi gṛṇanti te bhūridā janāḥ

prahasitaṁ priya prema⋅vīkṣaṇaṁviharaṇañca te dhyāna⋅maṅgalamrahasi saṁvido jā hṛdi spṛśaḥkuhaka no manaḥ kṣobhayanti hi

calasi jadvrajāccārayan paśūnnalina⋅sundaraṁ nātha te padamśila⋅tṛṇāṅkuraiḥ sīdatīti naḥkalilatāṁ manaḥ kānta gacchati

dinaparikṣaye nīla⋅kuntalai⋅rvanaruhānanaṁ vibhradāvṛtamdhanarajasvalaṁ darśayan muhu⋅rmanasi naḥ smaraṁ vīra jacchasi

praṇata⋅kāmadaṁ padmajārcitaṁdharaṇī⋅maṇḍanaṁ dhyeyamāpadicaraṇa⋅paṅkajaṁ śantamañca teramaṇa naḥ staneṣvarpayādhihan

surata⋅vardhanaṁ śoka⋅nāśanaṁsvarita⋅veṇunā suṣṭhu cumbitamitara⋅rāga⋅vismāraṇaṁ nṛṇāṁvitara vīra naste‛dharāmṛtam

aṭati jadbhavānahni kānanaṁtruṭiryugāyate tvāmapaśyatāmkuṭila⋅kuntalaṁ śrīmukhañca tejaḍa udīkṣatāṁ pakṣmakṛd dṛśām

pati⋅sutānvaya⋅bhrātṛ⋅vāndhavānativilaṅghya te‛ntyacyutāgatāḥgatividastavodgītamohitāḥkitava joṣitaḥ kastyajenniśi

rahasi saṁvidaṁ hṛcchayodayaṁprahasitānanaṁ prema⋅vīkṣaṇamvṛhaduraḥśriyo vīkṣya dhāma temuhuratispṛhā muhyate manaḥ

vraja⋅vanaukasāṁ vyaktiraṅga tevṛjinahantryalaṁ viśva⋅maṅgalamtyaja manāk ca nastvatspṛhātmanāṁsvajana⋅hṛdrujāṁ janniṣūdanam

jat te sujātacaraṇāmvuruhaṁ staneṣubhītāḥ śanaiḥ priya dadhīmahi karkaśeṣutenāṭavīmaṭasi tadvyathate na kiṁ svitkūrpādibhir bhramati dhīrbhavadāyuṣāṁ naḥ

śrī⋅śuka uvācaiti gopyaḥ pragāyantyaḥpralapantyaśca citradhāruruduḥ susvaraṁ rājankṛṣṇadarśanalālasāḥ

tāsāmāvirabhūcchauriḥsmayamānamukhāmbujaḥpītāmbara⋅dharaḥ sragvīsākṣānmanmatha⋅manmathaḥ
IDX.translation_intro: Translation from Śrīla Bhaktivedānta Swami’s Śrīmad Bhāgavatam (10.31.1⋅19, 10.32.1⋅2)
IDX.en_translation:
The gopīs said: O beloved, Your birth in the land of Vraja has made it exceedingly glorious, and thus Indirā, the goddess of fortune, always resides here. It is only for Your sake that we, Your devoted servants, maintain our lives. We have been searching everywhere for You, so please show Yourself to us.

O Lord of love, in beauty Your glance excels the whorl of the finest, most perfectly formed lotus within the autumn pond. O bestower of benedictions, You are killing the maidservants who have given themselves to You freely, without any price. Isn’t this murder?

O greatest of personalities, You have repeatedly saved us from all kinds of danger - from poisoned water, from the terrible man-eater Agha, from the great rains, from the wind demon, from the fiery thunderbolt of Indra, from the bull demon and from the son of Maya Dānava.

You are not actually the son of the gopī Yaśodā, O friend, but rather the indwelling witness in the hearts of all embodied souls. Because Lord Brahmā prayed for You to come and protect the universe, You have now appeared in the Sātvata dynasty.

O best of the Vṛṣṇis, Your lotuslike hand, which holds the hand of the goddess of fortune, grants fearlessness to those who approach Your feet out of fear of material existence. O lover, please place that wish-fulfilling lotus hand on our heads.

O You who destroy the suffering of Vraja’s people, O hero of all women, Your smile shatters the false pride of Your devotees. Please, dear friend, accept us as Your maidservants and show us Your beautiful lotus face.

Your lotus feet destroy the past sins of all embodied souls who surrender to them. Those feet follow after the cows in the pastures and are the eternal abode of the goddess of fortune. Since You once put those feet on the hoods of the great serpent Kāliya, please place them upon our breasts and tear away the lust in our hearts.

O lotus-eyed one, Your sweet voice and charming words, which attract the minds of the intelligent, are bewildering us more and more. Our dear hero, please revive Your maidservants with the nectar of Your lips.

The nectar of Your words and the descriptions of Your activities are the life and soul of those suffering in this material world. These narrations, transmitted by learned sages, eradicate one’s sinful reactions and bestow good fortune upon whoever hears them. These narrations are broadcast all over the world and are filled with spiritual power. Certainly those who spread the message of Godhead are most munificent.

Your smiles, Your sweet, loving glances, the intimate pastimes and confidential talks we enjoyed with You - all these are auspicious to meditate upon, and they touch our hearts. But at the same time, O deceiver, they very much agitate our minds.

Dear master, dear lover, when You leave the cowherd village to herd the cows, our minds are disturbed with the thought that Your feet, more beautiful than a lotus, will be pricked by the spiked husks of grain and the rough grass and plants.

At the end of the day You repeatedly show us Your lotus face, covered with dark blue locks of hair and thickly powdered with dust. Thus, O hero, You arouse lusty desires in our minds.

Your lotus feet, which are worshiped by Lord Brahmā, fulfill the desires of all who bow down to them. They are the ornament of the earth, they give the highest satisfaction, and in times of danger they are the appropriate object of meditation. O lover, O destroyer of anxiety, please put those lotus feet upon our breasts.

O hero, kindly distribute to us the nectar of Your lips, which enhances conjugal pleasure and vanquishes grief. That nectar is thoroughly relished by Your vibrating flute and makes people forget any other attachment.

When You go off to the forest during the day, a tiny fraction of a second becomes like a millennium for us because we cannot see You. And even when we can eagerly look upon Your beautiful face, so lovely with its adornment of curly locks, our pleasure is hindered by our eyelids, which were fashioned by the foolish creator.

Dear Acyuta, You know very well why we have come here. Who but a cheater like You would abandon young women who come to see Him in the middle of the night, enchanted by the loud song of His flute? Just to see You, we have completely rejected our husbands, children, ancestors, brothers and other relatives.

Our minds are repeatedly bewildered as we think of the intimate conversations we had with You in secret, feel the rise of lust in our hearts and remember Your smiling face, Your loving glances and Your broad chest, the resting place of the goddess of fortune. Thus we experience the most severe hankering for You.

O beloved, Your all-auspicious appearance vanquishes the distress of those living in Vraja’s forests. Our minds long for Your association. Please give to us just a bit of that medicine, which counteracts the disease in Your devotees’ hearts.

O dearly beloved! Your lotus feet are so soft that we place them gently on our breasts, fearing that Your feet will be hurt. Our life rests only in You. Our minds, therefore, are filled with anxiety that Your tender feet might be wounded by pebbles as You roam about on the forest path.

Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: O King, having thus sung and spoken their hearts out in various charming ways, the gopīs began to weep loudly. They were very eager to see Lord Kṛṣṇa.

Then Lord Kṛṣṇa, a smile on His lotus face, appeared before the gopīs. Wearing a garland and a yellow garment, He directly appeared as one who can bewilder the mind of Cupid, who himself bewilders the minds of ordinary people.

IDX.first_line: gora bhaja gora bhaja gora bhaja bhai
IDX.search: gora bhaja gora bhaja gora bhaja bhai gora vina e jagate guru ara nai jadi bhajibe gora sarala kara nija mana kutinati chadi bhaja gorara carana
IDX.author: śrī⋅prema⋅vivarta
IDX.verses:
gorā bhaja, gorā bhaja, gorā bhaja bhāi
gorā vinā e jagate guru āra nāi

jadi bhajibe gorā, sarala kara nija⋅mana
kuṭināṭi chāḍi’ bhaja gorāra caraṇa

manera kathā gorā jāne, phā̐ki kemane dibe
sarala ha’le gorāra śikṣā bujhiyā laibe

ānera mana rākhite giyā āpanāke dibe phā̐ki
manera kathā jāne gorā, kemane hṛdaya ḍhāki

gorā bale,⋅āmāra mata karaha carita
āmāra ājñā pālana kara, cāha jadi hita

‘gorāra āmi, gorāra āmi’, mukhe balile nāhi cale
gorāra ācāra, gorāra vicāra, laile phala phale

loka dekhāna gorā⋅bhajā tilaka mātra dhari’
gopanete atyācāra gorā dhare curi

adhaḥ⋅patana ha’be bhāi kaile kuṭināṭi
nāma⋅aparādhe tomāra bhajana ha’be māṭi

nāma loo je kare pāpa, haya aparādha
era mata āra kibā āche bhakti⋅bādha?

nāma karite kaṣṭa nāi, nāma sahaja dhana
oṣṭha⋅spandana⋅mātre haya nāmera kīrtana
tāhāo nā haya jadi haya nāmera smaraṇa

tuṇḍa vandhe, cita⋅braṁśe, śravaṇa tavu haya
sarvapāpa⋅kṣaye jīvera mukhya phalodaya

bahu⋅janma arcanete ei phala dhare
kṛṣṇa⋅nāma nirantara tuṇḍe nṛtya kare

karma⋅jñāna⋅yogādira sei śakti nahe
vidhi⋅bhaṅga⋅doṣe phala⋅hīna, śāstre kahe

se⋅saba chāḍa bhāi, nāma kara sāra
ati alpadine tabe jinibe saṁsāra
IDX.en_translation:
Worship Lord Gaura, worship Lord Gaura, worship Lord Gaura, O brother! Without Lord Gaura, there is no other Guru in this world.

If you wish to worship Lord Gaura, then make your own mind simple. Abandoning all duplicity, worship the lotus feet of the Lord.

Lord Gaura knows the thoughts of your mind—how can you deceive Him? If you become simple, you will understand the Lord's teachings.

Trying to please others, you only deceive yourself. Lord Gaura knows the thoughts of your mind—how can you hide your heart?

Lord Gaura says: "Act according to My example. Obey My command, if you desire your own well-being."

Just saying "I belong to Lord Gaura" with your mouth does not work. Taking up His behavior and His philosophy bears true fruit.

If you only display worship for others by wearing forehead markings while secretly committing atrocities, the Lord will catch your theft.

You will fall down, brother, if you act with duplicity. Due to offenses against the Holy Name, your worship will be ruined.

To chant the Name while committing sins is an offense. What greater impediment to devotion is there than this?

There is no difficulty in chanting the Holy Name; it is an easy treasure. Just by the vibration of the lips, the chanting of the Name occurs. If even that is not possible, then simply remember the Name.

Even if the mouth is closed and the mind is disturbed, hearing still occurs. The destruction of all sins is the primary result for the living entity.

This fruit is achieved after many births of Deity worship. But the Name of Lord Kṛṣṇa constantly dances on the tongue.

The processes of fruitive action, speculative knowledge, and mystic yoga do not have that power. Due to the fault of breaking regulations, they become fruitless, as the scriptures say.

Give up all these other paths, O brother, and make the Holy Name your only essence. Then, in a very short time, you will conquer this material world.

IDX.first_line: gora pahu na bhajiya mainu
IDX.search: gora pahu na bhajiya mainu prema ratana dhana helaya harainu adhane jatana kari dhana teyaginu apana karama dose apani dubinu
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
gorā pa̐hu nā bhajiyā mainu
prema⋅ratana⋅dhana helāya hārāinu

adhane jatana kari’ dhana teyāginu
āpana karama⋅doṣe āpani ḍubinu

satsaṅga chāḍi’ kainu asate vilāsa
te⋅kāraṇe lāgila je karmabandha⋅phā̐sa

viṣaya viṣama viṣa satata khāinu
gaurakīrtana⋅rase magana nā hainu

kena vā āchaye prāṇa ki sukha pāiyā
narottama⋅dāsa kena nā gela mariyā
IDX.en_translation:
I died without worshiping Gaurāṅga Prabhu. I carelessly lost the treasure of the jewel of divine love.

Caring for what is worthless, I gave up true wealth. By my own faults, I have drowned myself.

Giving up the association of devotees, I indulged in bad company. For that reason, the noose of karmic bondage has caught me.

I constantly consumed the deadly poison of material enjoyment. I did not immerse myself in the nectar of chanting the glories of Gaura.

Why do I still have life? What happiness have I gained? Why did Narottama dāsa not simply die?

IDX.first_line: goracadera ajna peye grhavasigana
IDX.search: goracadera ajna peye grhavasigana mrta sute anganete ane tataksana kali malahari gora jijnase takhana srivase chadiya sisu jao ki karana
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
gorācā̐dera ājñā pe’ye, gṛhavāsigaṇa
mṛta sute aṅganete āne tatakṣaṇa

kali⋅malahārī gorā jijñāse takhana
śrīvāse chāḍiyā, śiśu, jāo ki kāraṇa

mṛta śiśumukhe jīva kare nivedana⋅
“loka⋅śikṣā lāgi’ prabhu, tava ācaraṇa

tumi ta’ paramatattva ananta advaya
parāśakti tomāra abhinna tattva haya

sei parā śakti tridhā hoiyā prakāśa
tava icchāmata karāya tomāra vilāsa

cicchakti⋅svarūpe nityalīlā prakāśiyā
tomāre ānanda dena hlādinī hoiyā

jīvaśakti hañā tava citkiraṇacaye
taṭastha⋅svabhāve jīva-gaṇe prakaṭaye

māyāśakti hañā kare prapañca sṛjana
bahirmukha⋅jīve tāhe karaya bandhana”

bhakativinoda bale aparādhaphale
bahirmukha ha’ye āchi prapañca⋅kavale
IDX.en_translation:
Receiving Lord Caitanya's order, the household residents immediately bring the dead son into the courtyard.

Then Gaura⋅hari, the remover of the impurities of the Kali age, asks, "Child, why are you leaving the home of Śrīvāsa?"

From the dead child's mouth, the soul submits: "Lord, Your actions are for the education of the people.

You are indeed the Supreme Truth, infinite and non-dual. Your supreme energy is Your non-different principle.

That supreme energy, manifesting in three ways, performs Your pastimes according to Your will.

In the form of internal potency, manifesting eternal pastimes, She gives You joy as the bliss-giving potency.

Becoming the marginal potency, Your rays of consciousness manifest the living entities through their marginal nature.

Becoming the external potency, She creates the material world and binds the averted souls within it."

Bhaktivinoda says: "Due to the fruit of offenses, I have become averse and am in the clutches of the material world."

IDX.first_line: gorapreme garagara nitai amara
IDX.search: gorapreme garagara nitai amara aruna nayane vahe suradhuni dhara vipula pulakavali sohe hema gaya gajendragamane heli duli cali jaya
IDX.author: narahari⋅dāsa
IDX.verses:
gorā-preme garagara nitāi āmāra
aruṇa nayāne vahe suradhunī dhāra

vipula pulakāvāli śohe hema gāya
gajendra-gamane heli duli cali jāya

patitere nirakhiyā dubāhu pasāri
kore kari saghane bolāya hari hari

emana dayāra nidhi ke hoibe āra
narahari adhame tārite avatāra
IDX.en_translation:
My Nitāi is overwhelmed with Gaura-prema. From His reddish eyes, a stream like the Ganges flows.

Extensive hairs standing on end beautify His golden body. He walks swaying to and fro with the gait of the king of elephants.

Seeing the fallen souls, He spreads His two arms; taking them in His lap, He makes them deeply call out "Hari! Hari!".

Who else will be such an ocean of mercy? Narahari says: He is the Avatāra to deliver the lowest.

IDX.first_line: govardhana girivara kevala nirjana sthala
IDX.search: govardhana girivara kevala nirjana sthala rai kanu karibe sayane lalita visakha sange sevana kariba range sukhamaya ratula carane kanaka samputa kari karpura tambula bhari jogaiba vadanakamale manimaya kinkini ratananupura ani paraiba caranayugale
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
govardhana girivara, kevala nirjana sthala,
rāi kānu karibe śayane
lalitā⋅viśākhā saṅge, sevana kariba raṅge,
sukhamaya rātula caraṇe

kanaka sampūṭa kari, karpūra tāmbula bhari,
jogāiba vadanakamale
maṇimaya kiṅkiṇī, ratananūpura āni’,
parāiba caraṇayugale

kanaka kaṭorā pūri’, sugandhi candana buri,
dohākāra śrīaṅge ḍhāliba
gururūpā sakhā bāme, tribhaṅga bhaṅgima ṭhāme,
cāmarera vātāsa kariba

dohāra kamala ā̐khi, pulaka hoiyā dekhi’,
duhu̐ pada paraśiba kare
caitanya-dāsera dāsa, mane mātra abhilāṣa,
narottama⋅dāse sadā sphūre
IDX.en_translation:
Govardhana is the best of hills, a completely solitary place; Rāi and Kānu will be lying down. Along with Lalitā and Viśākhā, I will serve joyfully at Their blissful reddish feet.

Making a golden box, filling it with camphor and betel nuts, I will supply it to Their lotus mouths. Bringing a jeweled girdle and jeweled anklets, I will put them on Their feet.

Filling a golden cup, making a paste of fragrant sandalwood, I will pour it on the beautiful bodies of Both. With the guru-rūpā sakhī on the left, I will fan Them with a whisk in Their three-fold bending posture.

Seeing the lotus eyes of Both with hairs standing on end, I will touch Both of Their feet with my hands. The servant of the servant of Caitanya—this only desire in the mind always manifests in Narottama Dāsa.

IDX.first_line: grhastha vairagi duhe bale goraraya
IDX.search: grhastha vairagi duhe bale goraraya dekha bhai nama vina jena dina nahi jaya bahu anga sadhane bhai nahi prayojana krsna namasraye suddha karaha jivana
IDX.verses:
gṛhastha⋅vairāgī⋅duhe bale gorārāya
dekha bhāi! nāma vinā jena dina nāhi jāya

bahu aṅga⋅sādhane bhāi nāhi prayojana
kṛṣṇa⋅nāmāśraye śuddha karaha jīvana

baddha⋅jīve kṛpā kari’ kṛṣṇa haila ‘nāma’
kali⋅jīve dayā kari’, kṛṣṇa haila gaura⋅dhāma

ekānta sarala⋅bhāve bhaja gaura⋅jana
tabe ta’ pāibe bhāi śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅caraṇa

gaura⋅jana⋅saṅga kara ‘gaurāṅga’ baliyā
‘hare kṛṣṇa’ nāma bala nāciyā nāciyā

acire pāibe bhāi nāma⋅prema⋅dhana
jāhā vilāite prabhura nade āgamana
IDX.en_translation:
Lord Gorārāya says to both the householder and the renunciate: "See, O brother! Let not a single day pass without the Holy Name."

"O brother, there is no need for many elaborate spiritual practices. Make your life pure by taking shelter of the Holy Name of Kṛṣṇa."

"Showing mercy to the conditioned souls, Lord Kṛṣṇa became the Holy Name. Showing compassion to the souls of Kali-yuga, Lord Kṛṣṇa became the abode of Gaura."

"Exclusively worship the followers of Lord Caitanya with a simple heart. Then, O brother, you will certainly attain the lotus feet of Śrī Kṛṣṇa."

"Associate with the followers of Lord Caitanya, chanting 'Gaurāṅga', and enthusiastically chant the 'Hare Kṛṣṇa' name while dancing."

"Very soon, O brother, you will attain the treasure of love for the Holy Name, which the Lord came to Nadīyā to distribute."

IDX.first_line: gurau gosthe gosthalayisu sujane bhusuraganesvamantre sri namni vraja nava yuva dvandva saranesada dambham hitva kuru ratimapurvamatitara maye svantarbhratascatubhirabhiyace dhrta padah
IDX.search: gurau gosthe gosthalayisu sujane bhusuraganesvamantre sri namni vraja nava yuva dvandva saranesada dambham hitva kuru ratimapurvamatitara maye svantarbhratascatubhirabhiyace dhrta padah na dharmam nadharmam srutigana niruktam kila kuruvraje radha krsna pracura paricaryamiha tanusaci sunum nandisvara pati sutattve guruvarammukunda presthatve smara paramajasram nanu manah
IDX.author: śrīla raghunāthadāsa gosvāmī
IDX.verses:
gurau goṣṭhe goṣṭhālayiṣu sujane bhūsuragaṇesvamantre śrī⋅nāmni vraja⋅nava⋅yuva⋅dvandva⋅śaraṇesadā dambhaṁ hitvā kuru ratimapūrvāmatitarā⋅maye svāntarbhrātaścaṭubhirabhiyāce dhṛta⋅padaḥ

na dharmaṁ nādharmaṁ śrutigaṇa⋅niruktaṁ kila kuruvraje rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa⋅pracura⋅paricaryāmiha tanuśacī⋅sūnuṁ nandīśvara⋅pati⋅sutattve guruvaraṁmukunda⋅preṣṭhatve smara paramajasraṁ nanu manaḥ

jadīccherāvāsaṁ vrajabhuvi sarāgaṁ pratijanu⋅ryuva⋅dvandvaṁ taccet paricaritumārādabhilaṣeḥsvarūpaṁ śrī⋅rūpaṁ sagaṇamiha tasyāgrajamapisphuṭaṁ premṇā nityaṁ smara nama tadā tvaṁ śṛṇu manaḥ

asadvārtā⋅veśyā visṛja mati⋅sarvasva⋅haraṇīḥkathā mukti⋅vyāghrā na śṛṇu kila sarvātma⋅gilanīḥapi tyaktva lakṣmī⋅pati⋅ratimito vyoma⋅nayanīṁvraje rādhā⋅kṛṣṇau sva⋅rati⋅maṇidau tvaṁ bhaja manaḥ

asacceṣṭā⋅kaṣṭa⋅prada⋅vikaṭa⋅pāśālibhirihaprakāmaṁ kāmādi⋅prakaṭa⋅patha⋅pāti⋅vyatikaraiḥgale vaddhvā hanye‛hamiti vakabhid⋅vartmapa⋅gaṇekuru tvāṁ phutkārānavati sa jathā tvāṁ mana itaḥ

are cetaḥ prodyat⋅kapaṭa⋅kuṭi⋅nāṭī⋅bhara⋅khara⋅kṣaranmūtre snātvā dahasi kathamātmānamapi māmsadā tvaṁ gāndharvā⋅giridhara⋅pada⋅prema⋅vilasat⋅sudhāmbhodhau snātvā svamapi nitarāṁ māñca sukhaya

pratiṣṭhāśā dhṛṣṭā śvapacaramaṇī me hṛdi naṭetkathaṁ sādhuḥ premā spṛśati śuciretannanu manaḥsadā tvaṁ sevasva prabhu⋅dayita⋅sāmantamatulaṁjathā tāṁ niṣkāśya tvaritamiha taṁ veśayati saḥ

jathā duṣṭatvaṁ me davayati śaṭhasyāpi kṛpayājathā mahyaṁ premāmṛtamapi dadātyujjvalamasaujathā śrī⋅gāndharvā⋅bhajana⋅vidhaye prerayati māṁtathā goṣṭhe kākvā giridharamiha tvaṁ bhaja manaḥ

madīśānāthatve vraja⋅vipina⋅candraṁ vrajavane⋅śvarīṁ tannāthatve tadatula⋅sakhītve tu lalitāmviśākhāṁ śikṣālī⋅vitaraṇa⋅gurutve priya⋅saro⋅girīndrau tatprekṣā⋅lalita⋅rati⋅datve smara manaḥ

ratiṁ gaurī⋅līle api tapati saundarya⋅kiraṇaiḥśacī⋅lakṣmī⋅satyāḥ paribhavati saubhāgya⋅valanaiḥvaśīkāraiścandrāvalī⋅mukha⋅navīna⋅vrajasatīḥkṣipatyārād jā tāṁ hari⋅dayita⋅rādhāṁ bhaja manaḥ

samaṁ śrī⋅rūpeṇa smara⋅vivaśa⋅rādhā⋅giribhṛto⋅rvraje sākṣāt⋅sevā⋅labhana⋅vidhaye tad⋅gaṇa⋅yujoḥtadijyākhyā⋅dhyāna⋅śravaṇa⋅nati pañcāmṛtamidaṁdhayannītyā govardhanamanudinaṁ tvaṁ bhaja manaḥ

manaḥśikṣaikādaśaka⋅varametanmadhurayāgirā gāyatyuccaiḥ samadhigata⋅sarvārtha⋅tati jaḥsayūthaḥ śrī⋅rūpānuga iha bhavan gokulavanejano rādhā⋅kṛṣṇātula⋅bhajana⋅ratnaṁ sa labhate
IDX.en_translation:
O my mind, my brother! I entreat you with folded hands: always give up pride and develop unprecedented, intense attachment for your spiritual master, the village of Vraja and its residents, all good people, the assembly of priests, your own mantra, the Holy Name, and the shelter of the youthful Divine Couple of Vraja.

O mind, do not perform the pious or impious acts described in the Vedas. Instead, engage in abundant service to Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa here in Vraja. Always remember the son of Śacī as non-different from Kṛṣṇa, the son of the Lord of Vraja, and as the topmost spiritual master. Remember Him as the dearmost of Kṛṣṇa here eternally.

O mind, listen then! If you desire to reside in the land of Vraja with great affection in every birth, and if you long to serve the youthful Divine Couple intimately, then clearly and perpetually remember with love Svarūpa Dāmodara, Śrī Rūpa Gosvāmī along with his associates, and his elder brother Sanātana Gosvāmī, and offer obeisances to them.

O mind, abandon the prostitute of unholy talk, which steals all intelligence. Indeed, do not listen to the tiger of impersonal liberation, which devours the entire self. Even giving up the attachment to the Lord of Vaikuṇṭha in the spiritual sky, worship the couple Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa in Vraja, who are the givers of the jewel of Their own love.

O mind, here I feel as if I am being strangled and killed, entangled by the ugly nooses of sinful acts that cause suffering and the manifest entanglements of lust and other vices. In the path of Kṛṣṇa—the killer of Bakāsura—cry out so that He may protect you from this path.

O mind! How can you burn yourself and me by bathing in the pungent urine of increasing deceit and crooked drama? Always bathe in the nectarine ocean of love for the lotus feet of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa, and thus please yourself and me greatly.

O mind, how can pure divine love touch this heart of mine where the shameless desire for fame—like a dog-eating woman—dances? Always serve the incomparable beloved associate of the Lord—devotional service—so that he may quickly evict that desire for fame and enthrone pure love here.

O mind, worship Kṛṣṇa here in the cowherd village with fervent prayers in such a way that by His mercy He may burn away my wickedness, even though I am deceitful; that He may bestow upon me the brilliant nectar of prema (divine love); that He may inspire me in the method of worshiping Śrī Rādhā.

O mind, remember Kṛṣṇa—the moon of Vraja—as the Lord of my Lord. Remember the Queen of Vraja, Rādhā, as His Lord. Remember Lalitā as Her incomparable friend. Remember Viśākhā as the teacher who bestows instructions. Remember Govardhana Hill and Rādhā-kuṇḍa as the givers of beautiful love by Their mere sight.

O mind, worship the beloved of Hari, Rādhā, who by the rays of Her beauty eclipses even the pastimes of Pārvatī; who by the abundance of Her good fortune defeats Śacī, Lakṣmī, and Satyabhāmā; and who by Her captivating power throws away the chaste new gopīs headed by Candrāvalī.

O mind, desiring to attain direct service to Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa—who are overwhelmed by transcendental love—in Vraja, along with Śrī Rūpa and his associates, always drink these five nectars: worshiping Them, chanting Their names, meditating on Them, hearing about Them, and offering obeisances to Them. In this way, daily worship Govardhana Hill.

Whoever, becoming a follower of Śrī Rūpānuga along with his associates here in the forest of Gokula, sings aloud these excellent eleven-verse instructions to the mind with sweet words and thus comprehends all meanings, will attain the incomparable jewel of devotional service to Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa.

IDX.first_line: gurau mantre namni prabhuvara sacigarbhaja pade
IDX.search: gurau mantre namni prabhuvara sacigarbhaja pade svarupe sri rupe ganayuji tadiyaprathamaje girindre gandharvasarasi madhurpuyyam vrajavane vraje bhakte gosthalayisu paramastam mama ratih na canyatra ksetre haritanusanathehapi sujanad rasasvadam premna dadhadapi vasami ksanamapi samam tvetadgramyavalibhirabhi tanvannapi katham vidhasye samvasam vrajabhuvana eva pratibhavam
IDX.author: śrīla raghunāthadāsa gosvāmī
IDX.verses:
gurau mantre nāmni prabhuvara⋅śacīgarbhaja⋅pade
svarūpe śrī⋅rūpe gaṇayuji tadīyaprathamaje
girīndre gāndharvāsarasi madhurpuyyāṁ vrajavane
vraje bhakte goṣṭhālayiṣu paramāstāṁ mama ratiḥ

na cānyatra kṣetre haritanusanāthehapi sujanād
rasāsvādaṁ premnā dadhadapi vasāmi kṣaṇamapi
samaṁ tvetadgrāmyāvalibhirabhi tanvannapi kathāṁ
vidhāsye saṁvāsaṁ vrajabhuvana eva pratibhavam

sadā rādhā⋅kṛṣṇocchaladatula⋅khelāsthalayujaṁ
vrajaṁ santyajyaitad⋅yugavirahito‛pi kraṭimapi
punardvārāvatyāṁ jadupatimapi prauḍhavibhavaiḥ
sphurantaṁ tadvācāpi hi nahi calāmīkṣitumapi

gatonmādai rādhā sphurati hariṇā śliṣṭahṛdayā
sphuṭaṁ dvārāvatyāmiti jadi śṛṇomi śrutitaṭe
tadāhaṁ tatraivoddhatamati patāmi vrajapurāt
samuḍḍīya svāntādhikagati⋅khagendrādapi javāt

anādiḥ sādirvā paṭuratimṛdurvā pratipada⋅
pramīlatkāruṇyaḥ praguṇakaruṇāhīna iti vā
mahā-vaikuṇṭheśādhika iha naro vā vrajapate⋅
rayaṁ sūnurgoṣṭhe prati⋅jani mamāstāṁ prabhuvaraḥ

anādṛtyodagītāmapi munigaṇairvaiṇikamukhaiḥ
pravīṇāṁ gāndharvāmapi ca nigamaistat priyatamām
ja ekaṁ govindaṁ bhajati kapaṭī dāmbhikatayā
tadabhyarṇe śīrṇe kṣaṇamapi na jāmi vratamidam

ajāṇḍe rādheti⋅sphuradabhidhayāsiktajanayā⋅
‛nayā sākaṁ kṛṣṇaṁ bhajati ja iha premanamitaḥ
paraṁ prakṣālyetaccaraṇa⋅kamale tajjalamaho
mudā pītvā śaśvacchirasi ca vahāmi pratidinam

parityaktaḥpreyojanasamudayairvāḍhamasudhī⋅
rdurandho nīrandhraṁ kadanabharavārdhau nipatitaḥ
tṛṇaṁ dantairdaṣṭvā caṭubhirabhiyācehadya kṛpayā
svayaṁ śrī⋅gāndharvā svapadanalināntaṁ nayatu mām

vrajotpanna⋅kṣīrāśana⋅vasanapātrādibhirahaṁ
padārthairnirvāhya vyavahṛtimadambhaṁ saniyamaḥ
vasāmīśākuṇḍe giri-kula-vare caiva samaye
mariṣye tu preṣṭhe sarasi jīvādipurataḥ

sphurallakṣmīlakṣmī ⋅vrajavijayilakṣmībhara⋅lasad⋅
vapuḥ śrī⋅gāndharvāsmaranikara⋅divyad giribhṛtoḥ
vidhāsye kuñjādau vividha-varivasyāḥ sa-rabhasaṁ
rahaḥ śrī⋅rūpākhyapriyatamajanasyaiva caramaḥ

kṛtaṁ kenāpyetannīja⋅niyamaśaṁsi⋅stavamimaṁ
paṭhet jo visravdhaḥ priya⋅yugala⋅rūpe‛rpita⋅manāḥ
dṛḍhaṁ goṣṭhe hṛṣṭo vasati⋅vasatiṁ prāpya samaye
mudā rādhā⋅kṛṣṇau bhajati sa hi tenaiva sahitaḥ
IDX.en_translation:
Let my supreme love be for the Spiritual Master, the mantra, the Holy Name, and the lotus feet of Lord Caitanya. Let it be for Svarūpa Dāmodara, Śrīla Rūpa Gosvāmī and his associates, and his elder brother Sanātana Gosvāmī. Let it be for Govardhana Hill, Rādhā-kuṇḍa, Mathurā, and the forest of Vraja. Let it be for the devotees and for all the residents of Vraja.

I will not reside for even a moment in any other place, even if it is a holy place of Lord Hari, or even if I am tasting divine mellows with love from saintly persons there. Rather, I will always make my residence, in every birth, only in the land of Vraja, associating with its inhabitants.

Abandoning Vraja, where the peerless pastimes of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa always overflow, I will not go to see even for a moment the Lord of the Yadus resplendent with great opulence in Dvārakā, even if invited.

If I hear it rumored that Rādhā is appearing in Dvārakā with Lord Hari, then with an agitated mind, I will immediately fly there from Vraja, even faster than Garuḍa.

Whether He is beginningless or has a beginning, whether He is expert or very gentle, whether His compassion blossoms at every step or is seemingly absent, whether He is greater than the Lord of Vaikuṇṭha or just a human being here—let this Son of Nanda Mahārāja in Vraja be my only Lord in every birth.

This is my vow: I will not go for even a moment near that deceitful and arrogant person who worships Lord Govinda alone, disregarding Śrīmati Rādhā⋅rāṇī, who is most dear to the Vedas and is sung by all the sages.

Whoever worships Lord Kṛṣṇa along with Rādhā, bowing down with love—I will wash his lotus feet, drink that water with joy, and always carry it on my head every day.

Utterly bewildered, having abandoned all beneficial purposes, and deeply fallen into an ocean of misery, I bite a blade of grass between my teeth and humbly beg today: "Let Śrīmati Rādhā⋅rāṇī herself lead me to Her lotus feet!"

Sustaining my life without pride with the milk, food, and clothes produced in Vraja, I will reside at Rādhā-kuṇḍa and Govardhana Hill. And I will die at that most beloved lake in front of the devotees.

As the ultimate follower of the most beloved Śrīla Rūpa Gosvāmī, I will eagerly and secretly perform various services in the groves for Śrīmati Rādhā⋅rāṇī and for Lord Kṛṣṇa, who is resplendent with the beauty of many Cupids.

He who reads with faith this hymn, which proclaims his own vow, with his mind offered to the Divine Couple, will certainly attain a firm and joyful residence in Vraja and will worship Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa along with Their associates.

IDX.first_line: gurau sri gaurange tad udita su bhakti prakarane
IDX.search: gurau sri gaurange tad udita su bhakti prakarane saci sunor lila vikasita su tirthe nija manau harer namni presthe hari tithisu rupanuga jane suka prokte sastre prati jani mamastam khalu ratih sada vrndaranye madhura rasa dhanye rasa mayah param saktim radham parama rasa murtim ramayati sa caivayam krsno nija bhajana mudram upadisan saci sunur gaude prati jani mamastam prabhu varah
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
gurau śrī⋅gaurāṅge tad⋅udita⋅su⋅bhakti⋅prakaraṇe
śacī⋅sunor⋅līlā⋅vikasita⋅su⋅tīrthe nija⋅manau
harer namni preṣṭhe hari⋅tithiṣu rupānuga⋅jane
śuka⋅prokte śāstre prati⋅jani mamāstāṁ khalu ratiḥ

sadā vṛndāranye madhura⋅rasa⋅dhanye rasa⋅mayaḥ
parāṁ śaktiṁ rādhāṁ parama⋅rasa⋅mūrtiṁ ramayati
sa caivāyam kṛṣṇo nija⋅bhajana⋅mudrām⋅upadiśan
śacī⋅sūnur gauḍe prati⋅jani mamāstāṁ prabhu⋅varaḥ

na vairāgyaṁ grāhyaṁ bhavati na hi jad bhakti⋅janitaṁ
tathā jñānaṁ bhānaṁ citi jadi viśeṣaṁ na manute
spṛhā me nāṣṭaṅge hari⋅bhajana⋅saukhyaṁ na hi jatas
tato rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa⋅pracura⋅paricaryā bhavatu me

kuṭīre ‘pi kṣudre vraja⋅bhajana⋅yogye taru⋅tale
śacī⋅sūnos tīrthe bhavatu nitarāṁ me nivasatiḥ
na cānyatra kṣetre vibudha⋅gaṇa⋅sevye pulakito
vasāmi prāsāde vipula⋅dhana⋅rājyānvita iha

na varṇe saktir me na khalu mamatā hyāśrama⋅vidhau⋅
na dharme nddharme mama ratir ihāste kvacid api
param tat⋅tad⋅dharme mama jaḍa⋅śarīram dhṛtam idam
ato dharmān sarvān su⋅bhajana⋅sahāyānn abhilaṣe

su⋅dainyaṁ sāralyaṁ sakala⋅sahanaṁ mānada⋅danaṁ
dayāṁ svīkṛtya śrī⋅hari⋅carana⋅sevā mama tapaḥ
sadācāro ‘sau me prabhu⋅pada⋅parair jaḥ samuditaḥ
prabhos caitanyasyākṣaya⋅carita⋅pīyūṣa⋅kṛtiṣu

na vaikuṇṭhe rājye na ca viṣaya⋅kārye mama ratir
na nirvāṇe mokṣe mama matir ihāste kṣaṇam api
vrajānandād anyad hari⋅vilasitaṁ pāvanam api
kathañcin māṁ rādhānvaya⋅virahitaṁ no sukhayati

na me patnī⋅kanyā⋅tanaya⋅jananī⋅bandhu⋅nicayā
harau bhakte bhaktau na khalu jadi teṣāṁ su⋅mamatā
abhaktānām⋅anna⋅grahaṇam api dośo viṣayiṇāṁ
kathaṁ teṣāṁ saṅgād hari⋅bhajana⋅siddhir bhavati me?

asat⋅tarkair⋅andhān jaḍa⋅sukha⋅parān kṛṣṇa⋅vimukhān
ku⋅nirvāṇāsaktān satatam ati⋅dūre pariharan
arādhāṁ govindaṁ bhajati nitarāṁ dāmbhikatayā
tad⋅abhyāse kintu kṣaṇam api na jāmi vratam idam

prasādānna⋅kṣīrāśana⋅vasana⋅pātrādibhir ahaṁ
padārthair nirvāhya vyavahṛtim asaṅgaḥ ku⋅viṣaye
vasann īśa⋅kṣetre jugala⋅bhajanānandita⋅manas
tanuṁ mokṣye kāle juga⋅pada⋅parāṇāṁ pada⋅tale

śacī⋅sūnor⋅ājñā⋅grahaṇa⋅caturo jo vraja⋅vane
parārādhyāṁ rādhām bhajati nitarām kṛṣṇa⋅rasikām
ahaṁ tvetat⋅pādāmṛtam anudinaṁ naiṣṭhika⋅manā
vaheyaṁ vai pītvā śirasi ca mudā sannati⋅yutaḥ

harer dāsyaṁ dharmo mama tu cira⋅kālaṁ prakṛtito
mahā⋅māyā⋅yogād⋅abhinipatitaḥ duḥkha⋅jaladhau
ito jāsyāmy ūrdhvaṁ sva⋅niyama⋅su⋅ratya prati⋅dinaṁ
sahāyo me mātraṁ vitatha⋅dalanī vaiṣṇava⋅krpā ⋅

kṛtaṁ kenāpyetat sva⋅bhajana⋅vidhau svaṁ niyamakāṁ
paṭhed jo viśraddhaḥ priya⋅yugala⋅rūpe ‘rpita manaḥ
vraje rādhā⋅kṛṣṇau bhajati kila saṁprāpya nilayaṁ
sva⋅mañjaryāḥ paścād vividha⋅varivasyāṁ sa kurute
IDX.en_translation:
May my attachment, in every birth, be to the Spiritual Master, to Lord Gaurāṅga, to the practices of pure devotion taught by Him, to the holy places where the Son of Mother Śacī performed His pastimes, to my own mantra, to the Holy Name of Lord Hari, to the holy days of the Lord, to the followers of Śrīla Rūpa Gosvāmī, and to the scriptures spoken by Śukadeva Gosvāmī.

May that same Lord Kṛṣṇa, who always enjoys in the forest of Vṛndāvana with Rādhā—the supreme potency and personification of the highest mellows—and who has appeared as the Son of Mother Śacī in the land of Gauḍa teaching His own method of worship, be my supreme Lord in every birth.

Renunciation that is not born of devotion is not acceptable. Similarly, knowledge and realization are of no special value if they do not lead to spiritual consciousness. I have no desire for the eight-fold yoga process, because there is no happiness of worshipping Lord Hari within it. Therefore, may I be blessed with abundant service to Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa.

May my residence always be in the holy land of the Son of Mother Śacī, even if it is in a tiny cottage suitable for the worship of Vraja, or under a tree. I have no desire to reside even in a palace endowed with vast wealth and a kingdom in any other place, even if it were served by the demigods.

I have no attachment to social class or the regulations of the spiritual orders of life. My attachment is not at all in pious or impious deeds. But since this material body is sustained by those duties, I desire all such duties only as they serve as aids to pure devotional service.

Great humility, simplicity, tolerance of all things, giving respect to others, and accepting mercy—the service of the lotus feet of Lord Hari is my only austerity. This good conduct of mine has been spoken of by those devoted to the lotus feet of the Lord in their descriptions of the nectarean character of Lord Caitanya.

I have no attachment to the spiritual world of Vaikuṇṭha, nor to worldly activities. My mind is not interested even for a moment in impersonal liberation. Other than the bliss of Vraja, even the pure pastimes of Lord Hari do not make me happy if they are without a connection to Rādhā.

I do not care for my wife, children, mother, or any relatives if they do not have deep affection for Lord Hari, His devotees, and the path of devotion. Even accepting food from non-devotees and materialistic people is a fault. How then, through their association, could I ever attain perfection in the worship of Lord Hari?

I will always keep far away from those who are blinded by false arguments, those devoted to material happiness, those averse to Lord Kṛṣṇa, and those who worship Lord Govinda without Rādhā out of great pride. I will not follow their practices for even a single moment; this is my vow.

Managing my life with the food, milk, clothing, and utensils produced in the holy land, and remaining unattached to worldly affairs, I will reside in the land of the Lord. With a mind delighted by the worship of the Divine Couple, I will give up my body at the lotus feet of those who are devoted to Them.

With a firm mind, I will daily carry upon my head and drink the nectar of the lotus feet of that person who is expert in following the orders of the Son of Mother Śacī, and who worships with great devotion the supremely worshipable Rādhā in the forests of Vraja.

Servitude to Lord Hari is my natural and eternal duty. I have fallen into the ocean of misery due to the influence of the great illusory energy. From this state I will rise daily with my own regulated practice and great attachment. My only helper is the mercy of the devotees, which destroys all illusion.

Whoever reads this hymn, which was composed as a set of regulations for the method of worship, with great faith and a mind offered to the Divine Couple, will surely attain Their abode and worship Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa in Vraja, performing various services as a maidservant.

IDX.first_line: gurudeva
IDX.search: gurudeva bada krpa kari gauda vana majhe godrume diyacha sthana ajna dila more ei vraje vasi harinama kara gana kintu kabe prabhu jogyata arpibe e dasere daya kari citta sthira habe sakala sahiba ekante bhajiba hari
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
gurudeva!
baḍa kṛpā kari’, gauḍa⋅vana⋅mājhe,
godrume diyācha sthāna
ājñā dilā more, ei vraje vasi’,
harināma kara gāna

kintu kabe prabhu, jogyatā arpibe,
e dāsere dayā kari’
citta sthira ha’be, sakala sahiba,
ekānte bhajiba hari

śaiśave⋅yauvane, jaḍasukha⋅saṅge,
abhyāsa hoila manda
nija karmadoṣe, e deha hoila,
bhajanera pratibandha

vārdhakye ekhana, pañcaroge hata,
kemane bhajiba bala
kā̐diyā kā̐diyā, tomāra caraṇe,
paḍiyāchi suvihvala
IDX.translation_intro: From The Harmonist, June 1930. Edited by Bhaktisiddhanta Sarasvati.
IDX.en_translation:
Gurudeva! Out of thy great mercy, ‘Mid the wood-land of Gauda, Thou hast given me a place in Godruma. Thou hast given me thy command,- Abiding here in Braja You sing the Name of Hari.

But when, O revered one! Wilt thou, out of thy mercy, Bestow the fitness on this slave ? When will my mind be tranquil, I will bear up with all, And serve Hari with singleness of heart ?

In childhood and youth, By contact with worldly pleasure, My habit has grown corrupt. By my own evil deeds This body has become A hindrance to the service of God.

Now in old age Smitten with the five-fold malady, Teach me how I am to serve, Crying with anguish, At thy feet I fall in this great distress.

IDX.first_line: gurudeva
IDX.search: gurudeva krpa bindu diya kara ei dase trnapeksa ati hina sakala sahane bala diya kara nija mane sprhahina sakale sammana karite sakati deha natha jathayatha tabe ta gaiba harinama sukhe aparadha habe hata
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
gurudeva!
kṛpā⋅bindu diyā, kara ei dāse,
tṛṇāpekṣā ati hīna
sakala⋅sahane, bala diyā kara,
nija⋅māne spṛhā-hīna

sakale sammāna, karite śakati,
deha nātha! jathāyatha
tabe ta’ gāiba, harināma⋅sukhe,
aparādha ha’be hata

kabe hena kṛpā, labhiyā e jana,
kṛtārtha hoibe nātha!
śakti⋅buddhi⋅hīna, āmi ati dīna,
kara more ātma⋅sātha

jogyatā⋅vicāre, kichu nāhi pāi,
tomāra karuṇā sāra
karuṇā nā haile, kā̐diyā kā̐diyā,
prāṇa nā rākhiba āra
IDX.translation_intro: From The Harmonist, June 1930. Edited by Bhaktisiddhanta Sarasvati.
IDX.en_translation:
