Gurudeva ! By administering a particle of thy mercy, Make this servant of Thine Infinitely more humble than a blade of grass. Imparting strength To bear all trials Make me unsolicitous of my own honour.

Give me, my Lord, The strength to honour all In the manner that is due. It is only then that with joy I will sing the Name of Hari, And all offence will end.

When will this person, Gaining such mercy, Be blessed, indeed, O Lord ? Destitute of strength and wit, I am very low; May thou make me thine.

To judge of my fitness, I find T have none, Thy mercy is essential. If thou art not merciful, I will not cease weeping Till life itself is spent out.

IDX.first_line: gurudeva
IDX.search: gurudeva kabe mora sei dina habe mana sthira kari nirjane basiya krsna nama gaba jabe samsara phukara kane na pasibe deha roga dure rabe hare krsna bali gahite gahite nayane bahibe lora dehete pulaka udita hoibe premete karibe bhora
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
gurudeva!
kabe mora sei dina habe?
mana sthira kari’, nirjane basiyā,
kṛṣṇa⋅nāma gā’ba jabe
saṁsāra⋅phukāra, kāṇe nā paśibe,
deha⋅roga dūre ra’be

‘hare kṛṣṇa’ bali’, gāhite gāhite,
nayane bahibe lora
dehete pulaka, udita hoibe,
premete karibe bhora

gada⋅gada vāṇī, mukhe bāhiribe,
kā̐pibe śarīra mama
gharma muhurmuhuḥ, vivarṇa hoibe,
stambhita pralaya sama

niṣkapaṭe hena, daśā kabe ha’be,
nirantara nāma gā’ba
āveśe rahiyā, dehayātrā kari’,
tomāra karuṇā pā’ba
IDX.translation_intro: From The Harmonist, October 1930. Edited by Bhaktisiddhanta Sarasvati.
IDX.en_translation:
Gurudeva ! When will that day be mine ! When composing my mind Seated in seclusion, The Name of Krishna T will sing. The tumult of the world Will not penetrate the ear And malady of body will keep afar.

As I say “Hare-Krishna”, By chanting the song Tears will flow in my eyes; The horripilation of joy Will manifest on the body And overwhelm with love.

Voice pressed at the throat Will issue from the mouth, My body will tremble; Sweating every moment Pallor will appear And death like stupor.

Oh! When without guile, Such will be any state I will constantly sing the Name! Remaining pervaded within, Performing the functions of body, I will gain thy mercy;

IDX.first_line: gurudeva
IDX.search: gurudeva ami svarupatah sri krsna yosita nivasa sri vraja dhama hoiya tomara jugala sevara sahayata mora kama kotha sei karya kotha mui anarya jada svarthe sada rata tuccha sukha svacchandya sarvatha amanda rahe jadi hai krtartha
IDX.verses:
gurudeva!
āmi svarūpataḥ, śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅yoṣita,
nivāsa⋅śrī⋅vraja⋅dhāma
hoiyā tomāra, jugala⋅sevāra,
sahāyatā⋅mora kāma

kothā sei kārya, kothā mui anārya,
jaḍa svārthe sadā ratā
tuccha sukha⋅svācchandya, sarvathā amanda,
rahe jadi hai kṛtārtha

bhāvi⋅kṛpā haila, janama saphala,
natubā hai je kātara
kṛṣṇa⋅vimukhatāya, kaṣṭa nāhi pāya,
citta kibā se pāthara

aho ki durgati ! kemana kumati !
jagate rahite daḍha
nija ṭhikānāya, mati nāhi dhāya,
mui chiḥ ki śocyatara !!

svarūpānubandhi⋅, sevā prati jadi,
śuddha lobha nāhi haya
koṭī janma dhari’, sukṛti vā kari,
tathāpi siddhi je naya

prabhu! ei nirbandha, jehetu sambandha,
āche mora tava sātha
jaḍa ākarṣaṇa, nāśi’ vikarṣaṇa,
deha’ sei lobha nātha !!

jāte vyākulatā, jāge mane sadā,
nityasevāya jogadāne
tabe je bhajana, habe anukṣaṇa,
druta gati vraja pāne

jogyatā⋅rahita, tabu āśā dīpta,
pā’ba ei kṛpā⋅dhana
bharasā āmāra, ‘śrī’⋅jana tomāra,
‘śrī’⋅sama navanī mana
IDX.en_translation:
O Gurudeva! By my original nature, I am a maidservant of Lord Kṛṣṇa, and my true home is the divine land of Vraja. Having become your follower, my only desire is to assist in the service of the Divine Couple.

Where is that divine service, and where am I—a low-born soul always absorbed in material self-interest? I would be fulfilled even by insignificant happiness and comfort, completely devoid of goodness.

I think that if mercy is granted, my life will be successful; otherwise, I will remain distressed. In my aversion to Lord Kṛṣṇa, I feel no pain—is my heart made of stone?

Oh, what a wretched state! What evil intelligence! I am firmly established in this world. My mind does not run toward my true address. Alas! How much more pitiable can I be?

If a pure greed for service, following one's true nature, does not arise, then even after countless births and performing many pious deeds, perfection is never attained.

O Lord! This persistent prayer is because of the relationship I have with you. Destroying my material attraction and repulsion, please grant me that spiritual greed, O Master!

May eagerness always awaken in my mind to participate in your eternal service. Then my devotional practice will be constant, making swift progress toward Vraja.

Though I am devoid of all qualifications, my hope shines brightly that I will obtain this treasure of mercy. My only hope is in your dear ones, whose minds are as soft as fresh butter.

IDX.first_line: gurudeva dayamayapranera jatana janaba ki tomahayeche jivana jantranamaya
IDX.search: gurudeva dayamayapranera jatana janaba ki tomahayeche jivana jantranamaya sri krsne bhajite nahi cahe mativisaya bhogete prabala asaktivisayera asa nahi chade manavisayete sada dhaya
IDX.author: śrīmadbhaktikumuda santa gosvāmī mahārāja
IDX.verses:
gurudeva! dayāmaya!prāṇera jātanā jānāba ki tomāhayeche, jīvana jantraṇā-maya

śrī⋅kṛṣṇe bhajite nāhi cāhe mati,viṣaya⋅bhogete prabalā āsakti,viṣayera āśā nāhi chāḍe manaviṣayete sadā dhāya

kṛṣṇa⋅dāsya bhuli’ māyāre bhajinu,āpana svarūpa kabhu nā cintinu,virūpe svarūpa bhāvi’ mūḍha manamāyāte ākṛṣṭa haya

duṣṭa⋅saṅgaphala nā bujhinu hāya,sādhu kāche jete citta nāhi cāya,asatera saṅge thākiyā satatacitta ha’la vajraprāya

kanaka⋅kāminī⋅lābha⋅pūjā⋅āśā,cāhe mora citta āra pratiṣṭhāśā,kirūpe śodhita ha’be mora citaei cintā sadā haya

tava kṛpākaṇa āmāra sambala,tava kṛpā vinā nāhi anya bala,kṛpā kara prabho diyā cidbala,dāsa tomā praṇamaya

sādhusaṅge thāki’ chaya vega dami’śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅caraṇa sevi jena āmi;hena mati jāce tava dāsādhamabandi tava rāṅgā pāya

ohe gurudeva! tava śrī⋅caraṇa,sevi jena āmi janama janama,ei āśīrbāda jāci’ abhājanatava pade sthāna cāya
IDX.en_translation:
O spiritual master! O merciful one! How can I tell you the anguish of my heart? My life has become full of suffering.

My mind does not wish to worship Śrī Kṛṣṇa; it has a strong attachment to worldly enjoyments. My mind does not give up the hope for material things but constantly runs after them. 

Forgetting my service to Kṛṣṇa, I have worshiped illusion and never contemplated my true spiritual identity. Thinking my false identity to be my real self, my foolish mind is attracted to illusion.

Alas, I did not understand the result of bad association; my mind does not want to go near the saintly. Constantly remaining with bad company, my heart has become hardened like a thunderbolt. 

My mind desires wealth, women, gain, worship, and prestige. How will my heart ever be purified? This thought is always present. 

A drop of your mercy is my only asset; without it, I have no other strength. Please bestow your mercy, O Lord, by giving me spiritual strength; your servant offers obeisances to you.

Remaining in the association of the saintly and controlling the six urges of the senses, may I serve the lotus feet of Śrī Kṛṣṇa. The lowest of your servants begs for such a disposition, offering homage at your reddish feet.

O spiritual master! May I serve your holy lotus feet birth after birth. Begging for this blessing, this unworthy soul desires a place at your feet.

IDX.first_line: gurudeva krpa karake mujhako apana lena
IDX.search: gurudeva krpa karake mujhako apana lena mai sarana pada teri carano me jagaha dena karunanidhi nama tera karuna barasao tuma soye hue bhagyako he natha jagao tuma meri nava bhavara dole use para laga dena
IDX.verses:
gurudeva kṛpā karake mujhako apanā lenā
mai̐ śaraṇa paḍā terī, caraṇo me̐ jagaha denā

karuṇānidhi nāma terā, karuṇā barasāo tuma
soye hue bhāgyako, he nātha jagāo tuma
merī nāva bha̐vara ḍole, use pāra lagā denā

tuma sukhake sāgara ho, bhaktike sahāre ho
mere maname̐ samāye ho, mujhe prāṇoṁse pyāre ho
nita mālā japū̐ terī, mere doṣa bhulā denā

mai̐ santo kā sevaka hū̐, guru caraṇo kā dāsa hū̐
nahī̐ nātha bhulānā mujhe, isa jagame̐ akelā hū̐
tere dvāra kā bhikhārī hū̐, nahī̐ dilase bhulā denā
IDX.en_translation:
Gurudeva, be merciful and accept me as your own. I have fallen at your shelter, give me a place at your feet.

Your name is the Ocean of Mercy, shower mercy upon me. O Lord, awaken my sleeping fortune. My boat is tossing in the whirlpool, take it across.

You are the ocean of happiness, you are the support of devotion. You are contained in my mind, you are dearer to me than life. I chant your beads constantly, make me forget my faults.

I am a servant of the saints, I am a servant of the Guru's feet. Do not forget me, O Lord, I am alone in this world. I am a beggar at your door, do not forget me from your heart.

IDX.first_line: gurudevakabe tava karuna prakasesri gauranga lila haya nitya tattvaei sudrdha visvasehari hari bali godruma kananebhramiba darsana ase
IDX.search: gurudevakabe tava karuna prakasesri gauranga lila haya nitya tattvaei sudrdha visvasehari hari bali godruma kananebhramiba darsana ase nitai gauranga advaita srivasagadadhara pancajanakrsna nama rase bhasabe jagatkari mahasankirtana
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
gurudeva!kabe tava karuṇā prakāśeśrī⋅gaurāṅga⋅līlā, haya nitya⋅tattva,ei sudṛḍha viśvāse‘hari hari’ bali’, godruma⋅kānane,bhramiba darśana⋅āśe

 nitāi, gaurāṅga, advaita, śrīvāsa,gadādhara⋅pañcajanakṛṣṇa⋅nāma⋅rase, bhāsā’be jagat,kari’ mahāsaṅkīrtana

 nartana⋅vilāsa, mṛdaṅga⋅vādana,śuniba āpana kāṇedekhiyā dekhiyā, se⋅līlā⋅mādhurī,bhāsiba premera vāṇe

 nā dekhi ābāra, se⋅līlā⋅ratana,kā̐di ‘hā gaurāṅga’ bali’āmāre viṣayī, pāgala baliyā,aṅgete dibeka dhūli
IDX.translation_intro: From The Harmonist, October 1930. Edited by Bhaktisiddhanta Sarasvati.
IDX.en_translation:
Gurudeva! When by the manifestation of thy mercy, 'The Pastimes of Sri Gauranga Are eternal varities In this firm conviction, Reciting the Name of Hari, 'Mid the garden of Godruma, I will roam for their sight.

Nitai, Gauranga, Advaita, Sribas, Gadadhar,--all five, By the liquid sweetness of the Name of Krishna,  Overflood the world, Singing the high chant in unison,

The Pastime of dance, Playing on the Mridanga, I will hear with my own ears; By the constant vision Of the Beauty of that Leela, I will be borne on the flood-tide of love.

And presently again missing the sight Of the treasure of that Pastime As I weep and cry aloud the Name of Gauranga, The worldlings Proclaiming me mad Will throw the dust at my body.

IDX.first_line: gurudeve vrajavane vrajabhumivasi jane
IDX.search: gurudeve vrajavane vrajabhumivasi jane suddhabhakte ara vipragane istamantre hariname jugala bhajana kame kara rati apurva jatane dhari mana carane tomara janiyachi ebe sara krsnabhakti vina ara nahi ghuce jivera samsara
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
gurudeve, vraja-vane, vraja-bhūmi-vāsī jane,
śuddhabhakte, āra vipragaṇe
iṣṭamantre, hari-nāme, jugala⋅bhajana⋅kāme,
kara rati apūrva jatane

dhari, mana, caraṇe tomāra
jāniyāchi ebe sāra, kṛṣṇa-bhakti vinā āra,
nāhi ghuce jīvera saṁsāra

karma jñāna tapaḥ joga, sakaloi ta’ karma-bhoga,
karma chāḍāite keha nāre
sakala chāḍiyā bhāi, śraddhā-devīra guṇa gāi,
jā̐’ra kṛpā bhakti dite pāre

chāḍi’ dambha anukṣaṇa, smara aṣṭatattva, mana,
kara tāhe niṣkapaṭa rati
sei rati⋅prārthanāya, śrī⋅dāsagosvāmi⋅pāya,
e bhaktivinoda kare nati
IDX.en_translation:
To Gurudeva, in forests of Vraja, to the residents of Vraja, to pure devotees, and to the brāhmaṇas, With your chosen mantra, holy names of Hari, and with the desire for devotional service to the Divine Couple, apply unprecedented care and affection.

Holding, O mind, to your feet, I have now understood the essence: without Kṛṣṇa bhakti, the cycle of birth and death of the living entity cannot cease.

Fruitive action, knowledge, austerity, yoga - all are indeed reactions of karma. No one can escape karma. Leaving all else, O brother, I sing the glories of the goddess of faith, by whose mercy devotion can be given.

Constantly abandoning pride, O mind, remember the eight principles and render sincere affection unto them. With that prayer for affection, this Bhaktivinoda Ṭhākura offers obeisances at the feet of Śrī Raghunātha Dāsa Gosvāmī.

IDX.first_line: ha ha kabe gaura nitai
IDX.search: ha ha kabe gaura nitai e patita jane uru krpa kari dekha dibe duti bhai duhu krpa bale navadvipa dhame dekhiba vrajera sobha ananda sukhada kunja manohara heriba nayana lobha
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hā hā kabe gaura⋅nitāi
e patita jane, uru kṛpā kari’,
dekhā dibe du’ṭī bhāi

du̐hu kṛpā⋅bale, navadvīpa⋅dhāme,
dekhiba vrajera śobhā
ānanda⋅sukhada⋅, kuñja manohara,
heriba nayana⋅lobhā

tāhāra nikaṭe, śrīlalitā⋅kuṇḍa,
ratnavedī kata śata
jathā rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa, līlā vistāriyā,
viharena avirata

sakhīgaṇa jathā, līlāra sahāya,
nānā⋅sevā⋅sukha pāya
e dāsī tathāya, sakhīra ājñāte,
kārye iti⋅uti dhāya

mālatīra mālā, gā̐thiyā āniba,
diba tabe sakhī⋅kare
rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa⋅gale, sakhī parāibe,
nāciba ānandabhare
IDX.en_translation:
Alas, alas! When will Gaura-Nitāi, the two brothers, showing great mercy, appear to this fallen person?

By the strength of Their mercy, in Navadvīpa-dhāma, I will see the beauty of Vraja. I will behold the delightful, blissful, charming groves that captivate the eyes.

Near there is Śrī Lalitā-kuṇḍa, and hundreds of jeweled altars, where Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa continuously enjoy, expanding Their pastimes.

Where the sakhīs assist in the pastimes and receive the happiness of various services, this maidservant, by the command of the sakhīs, runs here and there for service.

I will string a garland of mālatī flowers and bring it, then place it in the hands of the sakhī. The sakhī will place it around the necks of Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa, and I will dance with great joy.

IDX.first_line: ha ha mora gaura kisora
IDX.search: ha ha mora gaura kisora kabe daya kari srigodrumavane dekha dibe manahcora ananda sukhada kunjera bhitare gadadhare bame kari kancana varana cacara cikura natana suvesa dhari
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hā hā mora gaura⋅kiśora
kabe dayā kari’, śrī-godruma-vane,
dekhā dibe manaḥcora

ānanda⋅sukhada⋅, kuñjera bhitare,
gadādhare bāme kari’
kāñcana⋅varaṇa, cā̐cara cikura,
naṭana suveśa dhari’

dekhite dekhite, śrī⋅rādhā⋅mādhava,
rūpete karibe ālā
sakhīgaṇa⋅saṅge, karibe naṭana,
galete mohana-mālā

anaṅga⋅mañjarī, sadaya hoiyā,
e dāsī⋅karete dhari’
du̐he nivedibe, du̐hāra mādhurī,
heriba nayana bhari’
IDX.en_translation:
Oh, my Gaura-kiśora! When, out of mercy, will You, the thief of my heart, appear in Śrī-godruma-vana?

Inside the joyful and pleasant grove, with Gadādhara on Your left, with a golden complexion and curly hair, dressed beautifully for dancing.

As I gaze, Śrī Rādhā-Mādhava will shine forth in a beautiful form, dancing with the sakhīs, a charming garland around Your neck.

Anaṅga-Mañjarī, being merciful, will hold this maidservant's hand and present the sweetness of both to me. I will behold them with my eyes completely filled.

IDX.first_line: hamare vraja ke rakhavalekanhaiya radhikaranikanhaiya radhikaranikanhaiya radhikarani
IDX.search: hamare vraja ke rakhavalekanhaiya radhikaranikanhaiya radhikaranikanhaiya radhikarani hamare nayano ke tarekanhaiya radhikaranisahara be saharo kekanhaiya radhikarani
IDX.verses:
hamāre vraja ke rakhavāle,kanhaiyā rādhikārānīkanhaiyā rādhikārānī,kanhaiyā rādhikārānī

hamāre nayano ke tāre,kanhaiyā rādhikārānīsahārā be⋅sahāro ke,kanhaiyā rādhikārānī
IDX.en_translation:
Our protectors of Vraja are Lord Kanhaiyā and Queen Rādhikā. They are Kanhaiyā and Queen Rādhikā.

They are the stars of our eyes, Kanhaiyā and Queen Rādhikā. They are the support of the helpless, Kanhaiyā and Queen Rādhikā.

IDX.first_line: hare haraye namah krsna jadavaya namah
IDX.search: hare haraye namah krsna jadavaya namah jadavaya madhavaya kesavaya namah gopala govinda rama srimadhusudana radha govinda balo x4 govinda govinda govinda balo radha govinda balo x4 guru krpa jale nasi visaya anala radha govinda balo x4
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
(hare) haraye namaḥ kṛṣṇa jādavāya namaḥ
jādavāya mādhavāya keśavāya namaḥ
gopāla govinda rāma śrīmadhusūdana
rādhā⋅govinda balo (x4)
 
govinda govinda govinda balo
rādhā⋅govinda balo (x4)
guru⋅kṛpā jale nāśi’ viṣaya⋅anala
rādhā⋅govinda balo (x4)
 
kṛṣṇete arpiyā deha⋅gehādi sakala
rādhā⋅govinda balo (x4)
ananya⋅bhāvete citta kariyā sarala
rādhā⋅govinda balo (x4)
 
rūpānuga vaiṣṇavera piyā pada⋅jala
rādhā⋅govinda balo (x4)
daśa aparādha tyaji’ bhukti⋅mukti⋅phala
rādhā⋅govinda balo (x4)
 
sakhīra caraṇa⋅reṇu kariyā sambala
rādhā⋅govinda balo (x4)
svarūpete vraja⋅vāse hoiyā śītala
rādhā⋅govinda balo (x4)
IDX.en_translation:
Salutations to Hari, Kṛṣṇa, and the descendant of Yadu. Salutations to Mādhava and Keśava. All glories to Gopāla, Govinda, Rāma, and Śrī Madhusūdana. Chant "Rādhā-Govinda!"

Chant "Govinda, Govinda, Govinda!" Chant "Rādhā-Govinda!" Destroy the fire of material enjoyment with the water of the Guru's mercy. Chant "Rādhā-Govinda!"

Offering all things, including body and home, to Kṛṣṇa, chant "Rādhā-Govinda!" Making the mind pure with exclusive devotion, chant "Rādhā-Govinda!"

Drinking the foot-water of the followers of Śrī Rūpa, chant "Rādhā-Govinda!" Abandoning the ten offenses and the desires for material enjoyment and liberation, chant "Rādhā-Govinda!"

Making the dust of the feet of the gopīs one's only treasure, chant "Rādhā-Govinda!" Becoming peaceful by residing in Vraja in one's true spiritual form, chant "Rādhā-Govinda!"

IDX.first_line: hare hare govinda harekaliya mardana kamsa nisudanadevaki nandana rama hare
IDX.search: hare hare govinda harekaliya mardana kamsa nisudanadevaki nandana rama hare matsya kacchapa vara sukara naraharivamana bhrgusuta raksakularesri baladeva buddha kalki narayanadeva janardana sri kamsare
IDX.verses:
hare hare govinda harekāliya⋅mardana kaṁsa⋅nisūdanadevakī⋅nandana rāma hare

matsya kacchapa⋅vara śūkara naraharivāmana bhṛgusuta rakṣakulāreśrī baladeva buddha kalki nārāyaṇadeva janārdana śrī⋅kaṁsāre

keśava mādhava jādava jadu⋅patidaitya⋅dalana duḥkha⋅bhañjana śauregolokaindu gokula⋅candra gadādharagaruḍa⋅dhvaja gaja⋅mocana murāre

śrī⋅puruṣottama parameśvara prabhuparama⋅brahma parameṣṭhī aghāreduḥkhite dayāṁ kuru deva devakī⋅sutadurmati⋅paramānanda parihāre
IDX.en_translation:
O Lord Hari! O Govinda! All glories to You! O Subduer of the Kāliya serpent! O Slayer of Kaṁsa! O Son of Devakī! O Balarāma! O Lord Hari!

You are the fish, tortoise, boar, and man-lion incarnations. You are Vāmana, the Son of Bhṛgu, and the Enemy of the demon race. You are Balarāma, Buddha, Kalki, and Nārāyaṇa. O Lord Janārdana! O Enemy of Kaṁsa!

O Keśava! O Mādhava! O Yādava! O Lord of the Yadus! O Destroyer of the demons! O Remover of sorrow! O Son of Śūra! O Moon of Goloka! O Moon of Gokula! O Bearer of the mace! O You whose flag bears Garuḍa! O Savior of the elephant! O Enemy of Mura!

O Supreme Person! O Supreme Controller! O Master! O Supreme Brahman! O Supreme Being! O Enemy of the Agha demon! Have mercy on the distressed, O Son of Devakī! Please remove my evil thoughts and grant me supreme bliss.

IDX.first_line: hare krsna hare
IDX.search: hare krsna hare nitai ki nama eneche renitai nama eneche namera hatesraddha mulye nama diteche re
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hare kṛṣṇa hare

nitāi ki nāma eneche re(nitāi) nāma eneche, nāmera hāṭe,śraddhā⋅mūlye nāma diteche re

hare kṛṣṇa hare kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa hare hare rehare rāma hare rāma rāma rāma hare hare re

(nitāi) jīvera daśā, malina dekhe,nāma eneche vraja theke ree nāma, śiva jape pañcamukhe re,

(madhura ei harināma)e nāma, brahmā jape caturmukhe re(madhura ei harināma)e nāma, nārada jape vīṇāyantre re

(madhura ei harināma)e nāmābhāse ajāmila vaikuṇṭhe gela ree nāma balte balte vraje cala re
IDX.en_translation:
Hare Kṛṣṇa Hare (Refrain)

Oh, what a Name Nitāi has brought! Nitāi has brought the Name to the marketplace of the Name, And He is giving that Name for the price of faith.

Hare Kṛṣṇa Hare Kṛṣṇa Kṛṣṇa Kṛṣṇa Hare Hare Hare Rāma Hare Rāma Rāma Rāma Hare Hare

Nitāi, seeing the fallen condition of the souls to be so wretched, Has brought the Name all the way from Vraja. This Name is chanted by Śiva with his five mouths.

(This sweet Hari-nāma) This Name is chanted by Brahmā with his four mouths. (This sweet Hari-nāma) This Name is chanted by Nārada on his vīṇā.

(This sweet Hari-nāma) By a mere reflection of this Name (nāmābhāsa), Ajāmila went to Vaikuṇṭha. While constantly chanting this Name, let us go to Vraja!

IDX.first_line: hare krsna hare krsna
IDX.search: hare krsna hare krsna krsna krsna hare hare hare rama hare rama rama rama hare hare iti sodasakam namnam kalikalmasanasanam natah parataropayah sarvavedesu drsyate
IDX.author: kali-santaraṇa upaniṣad
IDX.verses:
hare kṛṣṇa hare kṛṣṇa
kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa hare hare
hare rāma hare rāma
rāma rāma hare hare

iti ṣoḍaśakaṁ nāmnāṁ
kali-kalmaṣa-nāśanaṁ
nātaḥ parataropāyaḥ
sarva-vedeṣu dṛśyate
IDX.translation_intro: Śrīla A.C. Bhaktivedānta Swami Prabhupāda, Mahā-mantra essay written in December 1966 in New York City
IDX.en_translation:
This transcendental vibration by chanting of: Hare Kṛṣṇa Hare Kṛṣṇa Kṛṣṇa Kṛṣṇa Hare Hare Hare Rāma Hare Rāma Rāma Rāma Hare Hare, is the sublime method of reviving our Kṛṣṇa consciousness. As living spiritual souls we are all originally Kṛṣṇa conscious entities, but due to our association with matter from time immemorial, our consciousness is now polluted by the material atmosphere.
In this polluted concept of life, we are all trying to exploit the resources of material nature, but actually we are becoming more and more entangled in her complexities. This illusion is called māyā — or hard struggle for existence for winning over the stringent laws of material nature.
This illusory struggle against the material nature can at once be stopped by the revival of our Kṛṣṇa consciousness. Kṛṣṇa consciousness is not an artificial imposition on the mind. This consciousness is the original energy of the living entity. When we hear the transcendental vibration, this consciousness is revived, and the process is recommended by authorities for this age.
By practical experience, also, we can perceive that by chanting this maha-mantra, or “the great chanting for deliverance,” one can at once feel transcendental ecstasy from the spiritual stratum. When one is factually on the plane of spiritual understanding, surpassing the stages of sense, mind, and intelligence, one is situated on the transcendental plane.
This chanting of Hare Kṛṣṇa, Hare Kṛṣṇa, Kṛṣṇa Kṛṣṇa, Hare Hare, Hare Rāma, Hare Rāma, Rāma Rāma, Hare Hare is directly enacted from the spiritual platform, surpassing all lower stages of consciousness, namely sensual, mental, and intellectual. There is no need of understanding the language of the mantra, nor there is any need of mental speculation, nor an intellectual adjustment for chanting this Mahā-mantra. It springs automatically from the spiritual platform, and as such anyone can take part in this transcendental sound vibration without any previous qualification and feel the ecstasy. We have seen it practically —even a child can take part in the chanting, or even a dog can take part in it.
The chanting should be heard from the lips of a pure devotee of the Lord so that immediate effect can be achieved. As far as possible chanting from the lips of pure devotee should be avoided as much as milk touched by lips of a serpent causes poisonous effect.
The word Harā is a form of addressing the energy of the Lord. Kṛṣṇa and Rāma are forms of addressing directly the Lord, and They mean “the highest pleasure-eternal.” Harā is the supreme pleasure potency of the Lord. This potency, when addressed as Hare, helps us in reaching the supreme Lord.
The material energy, called as māyā, is also one of the multipotencies of the Lord as much as we are also marginal potency of the Lord. The living entities are described as superior energy than matter. When the superior energy is in contact with inferior energy, it becomes an incompatible situation. But when the supreme marginal potency is in contact with the spiritual potency, Harā, it becomes the happy, normal condition of the living entity.
The three words, namely Harā, Kṛṣṇa, and Rāma, are the transcendental seeds of the Mahā-mantra, and the chanting is the spiritual call for the Lord and His internal energy, Harā, for giving protection to the conditioned soul. The chanting is exactly like genuine crying by the child for mother. Mother Harā helps in achieving the grace of the supreme father Hari, or Kṛṣṇa and the Lord reveals Himself to such a sincere devotee. No other means therefore of spiritual relization is as effective in this age as chanting the Mahā-mantra: Hare Rāma, Hare Rāma, Rāma Rāma, Hare HareHare Kṛṣṇa Hare Kṛṣṇa Kṛṣṇa Kṛṣṇa Hare Hare Hare Rāma Hare Rāma Rāma Rāma Hare Hare. The sixteen words Hare Kṛṣṇa, Hare Kṛṣṇa, Kṛṣṇa Kṛṣṇa, Hare Hare Hare Rāma, Hare Rāma, Rāma Rāma, Hare Hare are especially meant for counteracting the contaminations of Kali. After searching through all the Vedas, you will not find a better means of self-realization for this age.

IDX.first_line: hari bala hari bala hari bala bhai re
IDX.search: hari bala hari bala hari bala bhai re harinama aniyache gauranga nitai re modera duhkha dekhe re harinama vina jivera anya dhana nai re hariname suddha hala jagai madhai re
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
‘hari’ bala, ‘hari’ bala, ‘hari’ bala, bhāi re
harināma āniyāche gaurāṅga nitāi re

(modera duḥkha de’khe re)
harināma vinā jīvera anya dhana nāi re
harināme śuddha ha’la jagāi⋅mādhāi re

(baḍa pāpī chila re)
miche māyābaddha haye jīvana⋅kāṭāi re
(āmi āmāra bole re)
āśāvaśe ghure’ ghure’ āra kothā jāi re

(āśāra śeṣa nāi re)
‘hari’ ba’le deo bhāi āśāra mukhe chāi re
(nirāśa to sukha re)
bhoga⋅mokṣa⋅vāñchā chāḍi’ harināma gāi re

(śuddhasattva haye re)
nā ce’yeo nāmera guṇe o saba phala pāi re
(tuccha phale prayāsa che’ḍe re)
vinoda bale, jāi la’ye nāmera bālāi re

(nāmera bālāi che’ḍe re)
śabdārthaḥ⋅ bālāi⋅pratibandhaka, esthale daśa nāmāparādha
IDX.en_translation:
Chant "Hari! Hari! Hari!" O brother! Gaurāṅga and Nitāi have brought the Holy Name.

Seeing our distress, they brought this Name, for without the Holy Name, the soul has no other wealth. Even the great sinners Jagāi and Mādhāi were purified by it.

Falsely bound by illusion, we spend our lives; saying "I" and "mine" and driven by desire, where else shall we go?

There is no end to desire! Therefore, chant "Hari!" and throw ashes on the face of desire. Freedom from desire is true happiness; giving up the quest for enjoyment and liberation, we sing the Holy Name.

Becoming pure in being, we attain all fruits through the power of the Name without even asking. Giving up the effort for trivial results, Bhaktivinoda says: let us go, casting away the offenses to the Holy Name.

(Casting aside the offenses to the Name!)

IDX.first_line: hari balaba ara madanamohana heriba goei rupete vrajera pathe caliba go
IDX.search: hari balaba ara madanamohana heriba goei rupete vrajera pathe caliba go jaba go vrajendrapura haba gopikara nupuratadera carane madhura madhura bajiba go
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari ba’laba āra madanamohana heriba goei rūpete vrajera pathe caliba go

jā’ba go vrajendrapura, ha’ba gopīkāra nūpura,tādera caraṇe madhura⋅madhura bājiba go

rādhā⋅kṛṣṇera rūpamādhurī, heriba du’nayana bhari’,nikuñjera dvārī dvārī rahiba go

vipine vinoda khelā, saṅgete rākhālera melā,tā̐dera caraṇera dhūlā mākhiba go

tomarā sabe vrajavāsī, purāo manera abhilāṣī,āra kabe śrī⋅kṛṣṇera bā̐śī śuniba go

ei deha antimakāle, rākhiba śrī⋅yamunāra jale,jaya rādhā⋅govinda ba’le bhāsiba go

kahe narottama⋅dāsa, nā pūrila abhilāṣa,(āra) kabe hāma vrajavāsa kariba go
IDX.en_translation:
I will chant the name of Hari and behold the Lord who enchants Cupid. In this way, I will walk on the path to Vraja.

I will go to the city of the King of Vraja and become the ankle bells of the cowherd girls, sweetly jingling at their feet.

I will behold the sweet beauty of the forms of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa, filling my eyes, and I will remain at every entrance of the groves.

In the forest are joyful pastimes with a gathering of cowherd boys; I will smear myself with the dust of their feet.

O residents of Vraja, please fulfill my heart’s desire! When will I hear the flute of Śrī Kṛṣṇa again?

At the time of death, I will place this body in the waters of the Yamunā and float away while chanting, “All glories to Rādhā and Govinda!”

Narottama dāsa says: “My desires are not yet fulfilled. When will I truly reside in Vraja?”

IDX.first_line: hari bale modera gaura elo
IDX.search: hari bale modera gaura elo ela re gaurangacada preme elo thelonitai advaita sange godrume pasila
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari ba’le modera gaura elo

ela re gaurāṅgacā̐da preme elo⋅thelonitāi⋅advaita⋅saṅge godrume paśila

saṅkīrtana⋅rase mete nāma vilāilanāmera hāṭe ese preme jagat bhāsāila

godrumavāsīra āja duḥkha dūre gelabhaktavṛnda⋅saṅge āsi’ hāṭa jāgāila

nadīyā bhramite gorā ela nāmera hāṭegaura ela hāṭe, saṅge nitāi ela hāṭe

nāce mātoyārā nitāi godrumera māṭhejagat mātāya nitāi premera mālasāṭe

(torā dekhe jāre)advaitādi bhaktavṛnda nāce ghāṭe ghāṭepalāya duranta kali paḍiyā vibhrāṭe

ki sukhe bhāsila jīva gorācā̐dera nāṭedekhiyā śuniyā pāṣaṇḍīra buka phāṭe
IDX.en_translation:
Our Gaura has arrived, chanting Hari.

Here comes Gaurāṅgacānda, disheveled in ecstatic love. With Nitāi and Advaita, He entered Godruma.

Intoxicated by the mellow of saṅkīrttana, He distributed the holy name. Coming to the market of the holy name, He inundated the world with love.

Today, the sorrow of Godruma’s residents is gone. Coming with the bhaktavṛnda, He awakened the market.

As Gorā traveled Nadīyā, He came to the market of the holy name. Gaura came to the market, and Nitāi came with Him.

Nitāi dances ecstatically in Godruma’s fields. Nitāi intoxicates the world with an abundance of love.

(Go and see for yourselves!) Advaita and the bhaktavṛnda dance at every ghāṭa. The troublesome Kali flees in confusion.

How much joy the jīva is immersed in, in the play of Gorācānda! Seeing and hearing this, the pāṣaṇḍī’s heart breaks.

IDX.first_line: hari e ohari rama
IDX.search: hari e ohari rama adhamaka uddhara karahe narayana dhanajana namago moi moksa parasada krpa kari kara prabhu moka atmasat
IDX.verses:
hari⋅e ohari rāma
adhamaka uddhāra karāhe nārāyaṇa

dhanajana nāmāgo moi mokṣa parasāda
kṛpā kari karā prabhu moka ātmasāt

tumi jagatara svāmī sakalo tomāra
tumi mātra dātā prabhu, dātā kon āra

mora buli bhāvi karo mithyā abhimāna
dāsa kari la’le haya tāra avasāna

karmadoṣe jate thāko svarga vā narake
ei kṛpā karā jena nubhulo tomāke

bhakati parama dhana tayu bharālata
nukhujoho āna eko tāka diyā moka

tayu bhakatara saṅga diyā sarvakṣaṇe
tayu nāma⋅guṇa śuno sadāya jena kāne
IDX.en_translation:
O Hari! O Rāma! O Nārāyaṇa, please deliver this fallen soul.

I do not seek wealth, followers, fame, nor even the blessing of liberation. By Your grace, O Lord, please make me Your own.

You are the Master of the universe, and everything belongs to You. You alone are the Giver, O Lord; who else can truly give?

Thinking "mine," I am full of false ego. If You make me Your servant, that ego will come to an end.

Due to the faults of my past actions, wherever I may reside—whether in heaven or in hell—please grant me this grace: that I may never forget You.

Devotion is the supreme treasure in Your treasury. I do not ask for anything else; please give that to me.

Grant me the company of Your devotees at all times, so that I may always hear Your Holy Names and glories with my ears.

IDX.first_line: hari haraye namah krsna jadavaya namah
IDX.search: hari haraye namah krsna jadavaya namah jadavaya madhavaya kesavaya namah gopala govinda rama srimadhusudana giridhari gopinatha madanamohana
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
(hari) haraye namaḥ kṛṣṇa jādavāya namaḥ
jādavāya mādhavāya keśavāya namaḥ

gopāla govinda rāma śrīmadhusūdana
giridhārī gopī-nātha madana-mohana

śrī⋅caitanya nityānanda śrī⋅advaita goptā
hari, guru, vaiṣṇava, bhāgavata, gītā

śrī⋅rūpa, śrī⋅sanātana, bhaṭṭa⋅raghunātha
śrī-jīva, gopāla-bhaṭṭa, dāsa⋅raghunātha

ei chaya gosāñira kari caraṇa vandana
jāhā haite vighna-nāśa abhīṣṭapūraṇa

ei chaya gosāñī jā̐’ra, mui tā̐’ra dāsa
tā̐’ sabāra pada-reṇu mora pañca-grāsa

tā̐’dera caraṇa sevi bhaktasane vāsa
janame janame haya, ei abhilāṣa

ei chaya gosāñī jabe vraje kailā vāsa
rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa⋅nityalīlā karilā prakāśa

ānande bala hari, bhaja vṛndāvana
śrī⋅guru⋅vaiṣṇava⋅pade majāiyā mana

śrī⋅guru⋅vaiṣṇava⋅pāda⋅padma kari’ āśa
nāma⋅saṅkīrtana kahe narottama⋅dāsa
IDX.en_translation:
Salutations to Hari, Kṛṣṇa, and the descendant of Yadu! Salutations to Mādhava and Keśava!

Gopāla, Govinda, Rāma, and Śrī Madhusūdana! The lifter of Govardhana Hill, the Lord of the gopīs, and the one who enchants Cupid!

Śrī Caitanya, Nityānanda, Śrī Advaita the protector! Hari, the spiritual master, the devotee, the scripture, and the Gītā!

Śrī Rūpa, Śrī Sanātana, Raghunātha Bhaṭṭa, Śrī Jīva, Gopāla Bhaṭṭa, and Raghunātha dāsa.

I offer my obeisances to the lotus feet of these six spiritual masters, by which the destruction of obstacles and the fulfillment of desires occur.

Whoever follows these six masters—I am their servant. The dust of their lotus feet is my very sustenance.

To serve their feet and reside with the devotees—this is my desire, birth after birth.

When these six masters resided in Vraja, they revealed the eternal pastimes of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa.

Joyfully chant the name of Hari and worship Vṛndāvana, immersing your mind in the lotus feet of the spiritual master and the devotees.

Taking hope in the lotus feet of the spiritual master and the devotees, Narottama dāsa sings this congregational chanting.

IDX.first_line: hari hari ara kabe palatibe dasa
IDX.search: hari hari ara kabe palatibe dasa e dasa kariya bame jaba vrndavana dhame ei mane kariyachi asa dhana jana putra dare esaba kariya dure ekanta hoiya kabe jaba saba duhkha parihari vrndavane vasa kari madhukari magiya khaiba
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari hari! āra kabe pālaṭibe daśā
e⋅daśā kariyā bāme, jā’ba vṛndāvana⋅dhāme,
ei mane kariyāchi āśā

dhana⋅jana putra⋅dāre, esaba kariyā dūre,
ekānta hoiyā kabe jāba
saba duḥkha parihari’, vṛndāvane vāsa kari’,
mādhukarī māgiyā khāiba

jamunāra jala jena, amṛta samāna hena,
kabe piba udara pūriyā
kabe rādhā⋅kuṇḍa⋅jale, snāna kari’ kutūhale,
śyāma-kuṇḍe rahiba paḍiyā

bhramiba dvādaśa vane, rasakeli je je sthāne,
premāveśe gaḍāgaḍi diyā
śudhāiba jane jane, vrajavāsigaṇa⋅sthāne,
nivediba caraṇa dhariyā

bhajanera sthāna kabe, nayana-gocara habe,
āra jata āche upavana
tā’ra madhye vṛndāvana, narottama dāsera mana,
āśā kare jugala⋅caraṇa
IDX.en_translation:
Hari Hari! When will my condition change? Having left this state behind, I will go to the abode of Vṛndāvana—this is the hope I have cherished in my mind.

Abandoning wealth, followers, children, and wife far away, when will I go, becoming single-minded? Giving up all distress, residing in Vṛndāvana, I will eat by begging alms.

When will I fill my belly drinking the water of the Yamunā, which is like nectar? When, bathing with curiosity in the waters of Rādhā-kuṇḍa, will I lie down at Śyāma-kuṇḍa?

I will wander in the twelve forests, in all the places of playful pastimes, rolling on the ground in the ecstasy of love. I will ask each and every person at the places of the residents of Vraja, and I will submit my petition holding their feet.

When will the place of worship become visible to my eyes, along with all the gardens that exist? Among them is Vṛndāvana; Narottama Dāsa's mind hopes for the lotus feet of the Divine Couple.

IDX.first_line: hari hari ara ki emana dasa haba
IDX.search: hari hari ara ki emana dasa haba e bhava samsara tyaji parama anande maji ara kabe vrajabhume jaba sukhamaya vrndavana kabe habe darasana se dhuli lagibe kabe gaya preme gadagada haina radha krsna nama laiya kandiya bedaba ubharaya
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari hari! āra ki emana daśā haba
e⋅bhava⋅saṁsāra tyaji’, parama⋅ānande maji’,
āra kabe vraja-bhūme jā’ba

sukha-maya vṛndāvana, kabe ha’be daraśana,
se⋅dhūli lāgibe ka’be gāya
preme gadagada haiñā, rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa⋅nāma laiyā,
kāndiyā beḍāba ubharāya

nibhṛte nikuñje jāñā, aṣṭāṅge praṇāma haiyā,
ḍākiba ‘hā rādhānātha’ bali’
kabe jamunāra tīre, paraśa kariba nīre,
kabe piba karapuṭe tuli’

āra kabe emana haba, śrī-rāsa-maṇḍale jā’ba,
kabe gaḍāgaḍi diba tāya
vaṁśīvaṭa⋅chāyā pāñā, parama⋅ānanda hañā,
paḍiyā rahiba tā’ra chāya

kabe govardhana giri, dekhiba nayana bhari’,
kabe habe rādhā⋅kuṇḍe vāsa
bhramite bhramite kabe, e⋅deha patana habe,
kahe dīna narottama⋅dāsa
IDX.en_translation:
O Lord Hari! Will such a state ever be mine? Abandoning this material existence and immersed in supreme bliss, when will I go to Vrajabhūmi?

When will I behold blissful Vṛndāvana? When will its dust touch my body? Overwhelmed with divine love and chanting the names of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa, I will wander about weeping loudly.

Going to a secluded grove and offering my full obeisances, I will call out, "O Lord of Rādhā!" When will I touch the water on the bank of the Yamunā? When will I drink it from my cupped hands?

When will such a state be mine again? I will go to the place of the rāsa dance. When will I roll on the ground there? Finding the shade of the Vaṁśīvaṭa tree and becoming supremely joyful, I will remain there.

When will I see Govardhana Hill with my eyes full? When will I reside at Rādhā-kuṇḍa? While wandering around, when will this body fall? Thus says the humble Narottama dāsa.

IDX.first_line: hari hari ara ki emana dasa haba
IDX.search: hari hari ara ki emana dasa haba kabe vrsabhanupure ahiri gopera ghare tanaya hoiya janamiba javate amara kabe e pani grahana habe vasati kariba kabe taya sakhira parama srestha je tahara haya prestha sevana kariba tara paya
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari hari! āra ki emana daśā haba
kabe vṛṣabhānupure, āhirī gopera ghare,
tanayā hoiyā janamiba

jāvaṭe āmāra kabe, e pāṇi⋅grahaṇa habe,
vasati kariba kabe tāya
sakhīra parama śreṣṭha, je tāhāra haya preṣṭha,
sevana kariba tā̐ra pāya

te̐ha kṛpāvān haiñā, rātula caraṇe lañā,
āmāre karibe samarpaṇa
saphala hoibe daśā, pūribe manere āśā,
se du̐hāra jugala caraṇa

vṛndāvane duijana, caturdike sakhīgaṇa,
sevana kariba avaśeṣe
sakhīgaṇa cāribhite, nānā jantra laiñā hāte,
dekhiba manera abhilāṣe

du̐hu cā̐damukha dekhi’, juḍābe tāpita ā̐khi,
nayane bahibe aśrudhāra
vṛndāra nideśa pāba, dohāra nikaṭe jāba,
hena dina hoibe āmāra

śrī⋅rūpamañjarī sakhī, more anāthinī dekhi,
rākhibe rātula duṭī pāya
narottama⋅dāsa bhaṇe, priyanarmasakhīgaṇe,
kabe dāsī karibe āmāya
IDX.en_translation:
O Lord Hari! Will such a state ever come for me again? When will I be born as a daughter in the home of a cowherd in the village of Vṛṣabhānu?

When will my life be accepted in the village of Yāvaṭa, and when will I reside there? I will serve at the feet of the greatest of the companions, who is most dear to Rādhā.

She, becoming merciful and taking me to her reddish feet, will surrender me to the Divine Couple. Then my life will be successful, and my heart's desires will be satisfied at Their twin feet.

In Vṛndāvana, I will finally serve the Divine Couple, who are surrounded by companions on all sides. I will see the companions all around, holding various instruments in their hands, fulfilling the desire of my heart.

Seeing Their moon-like faces, my burning eyes will be cooled, and streams of tears will flow. I will receive the order of Vṛndā and go near the Two. Will such a day come for me?

Śrī Rūpa Mañjarī, seeing me as a helpless orphan, will keep me at her two reddish feet. Narottama dāsa says: when will she make me a maidservant among the dear intimate companions?

IDX.first_line: hari hari ara ki emana dasa haba
IDX.search: hari hari ara ki emana dasa haba chadiya purusadeha kabe va prakrti haba duhu ange candana paraba taniya badhiba cuda nava gunjahare beda nana phule gathi diba hara pitavasana ange paraiba sakhi sange vadane tambula diba ara
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari hari! āra ki emana daśā haba
chāḍiyā puruṣadeha, kabe vā prakṛti haba,
du̐hu aṅge candana parāba

ṭāniyā bā̐dhiba cūḍā, nava guñjāhāre beḍā,
nānā phule gā̐thi diba hāra
pītavasana aṅge, parāiba sakhī⋅saṅge,
vadane tāmbūla diba āra

du̐hu rūpa manohārī, heriba nayana bhari’,
nīlāmbare rāi sājāiyā
navaratna⋅jari āni, bāndhiba vicitra veṇī,
tāte phula mālatī gā̐thiyā

sei rūpamādhurī, dekhiba nayana bhari’,
ei kari mane abhilāṣa
jaya rūpa sanātana, deha more ei dhana,
nivedaye narottama⋅dāsa
IDX.en_translation:
O Lord Hari! Will such a state ever be mine? Giving up this male body and becoming a female attendant, I will adorn the bodies of both Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa with sandalwood paste.

I will tightly bind Their hair into a topknot, encircling it with a fresh garland of berries. I will weave garlands of various flowers. Along with the companions, I will dress Them in yellow garments and offer betel to Their mouths.

I will gaze to my heart's content upon Their enchanting forms, dressing Rādhā in blue garments. Bringing lace of nine jewels, I will braid Her hair into an exquisite braid, weaving jasmine flowers into it.

I will behold that beautiful sweetness to my heart's content; this is the ardent desire within my heart. All glories to Rūpa and Sanātana! Bestow this treasure upon me—thus prays Narottama dāsa.

IDX.first_line: hari hari hena dina hoibe amara
IDX.search: hari hari hena dina hoibe amara duhu anga parasiba duhu anga nirakhiba sevana kariba dohakara mala gathi diba nana phule kanakasamputa kari karpura tambula puri jogaiba adharayugale
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari hari! hena dina hoibe āmāra
du̐hu aṅga paraśiba, du̐hu aṅga nirakhiba,
sevana kariba dohākāra

mālā gā̐thi’ diba nānā phule
kanaka-sampūṭa kari’, karpūra tāmbula pūri’,
jogāiba adharayugale

rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa vṛndāvana, ei mora prāṇa-dhana,
ei mora jīvana⋅upāya
jaya patita-pāvana, deha more ei dhana,
tomā vine anya nāhi bhāya

śrī⋅guru karuṇā⋅sindhu, adhama janāra bandhu,
lokanātha lokera jīvana
hā hā! prabhu kara dayā, deha more padachāyā,
narottama loila śaraṇa
IDX.en_translation:
Hari Hari! Such a day will be mine. I will touch the limbs of Both, I will see the limbs of Both, I will serve Both of Them.

Stringing garlands of various flowers, I will give them. Making a golden box, filling it with camphor and betel nuts, I will supply it to Their lips.

Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa, Vṛndāvana—this is the treasure of my life, this is the means of my life. Glory to the Savior of the Fallen! Give me this wealth; nothing else shines for me without You.

Śrī Guru, ocean of mercy, friend of the fallen people, Lokanātha—the life of the people. Alas, alas! Master, be merciful, give me the shade of your feet; Narottama has taken shelter.

IDX.first_line: hari hari kabe haba vrndavanavasi
IDX.search: hari hari kabe haba vrndavanavasi nirakhiba nayane jugala ruparasi tyajiya sayanasukha vicitra palanka kabe vrajera dhulaya dhusara habe anga
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari hari! kabe haba vṛndāvanavāsī
nirakhiba nayane jugala⋅rūparāśi

tyajiyā śayanasukha vicitra pālaṅka
kabe vrajera dhūlāya dhūsara ha’be aṅga

ṣaḍrasa bhojana dūre parihari
kabe vraje māgiyā khāiba mādhukarī

parikramā kariyā beḍāba vane vane
viśrāma kariyā jāi jamunā⋅puline

tāpa dūra kariba śītala vaṁśīvaṭe
(kabe) kuñje baiṭhaba hāma vaiṣṇava⋅nikaṭe

narottama⋅dāsa kahe kari’ parihāra
kabe vā emana daśā hoibe āmāra
IDX.en_translation:
Hari Hari! When will I become a resident of Vṛndāvana? I will behold with my eyes the beautiful forms of the Divine Couple.

Forsaking the comfort of sleeping on an elaborate bed, when will my body be covered in the dust of Vraja?

Leaving aside meals with six tastes (sweet, sour, salty, pungent, bitter, and astringent), when will I eat by begging for mādhukarī (alms) in Vraja?

I will wander from forest to forest performing parikramā, and I will rest on the banks of the Yamunā.

I will relieve my distress in the cool Vaṁśīvaṭa, when will I sit in a kuñja (grove) in the company of Vaiṣṇavas?

Narottam Dāsa says, making this humble plea, when will such a state be mine?

IDX.first_line: hari hari kabe haba vrndavanavasinirakhiba nayane jugala ruparasi
IDX.search: hari hari kabe haba vrndavanavasinirakhiba nayane jugala ruparasi tyajiya sayanasukha vicitra palankakabe vrajera dhulaya dhusara habe anga
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari hari! kabe haba vṛndāvanavāsīnirakhiba nayane jugala⋅rūparāśi

tyajiyā śayanasukha vicitra pālaṅkakabe vrajera dhūlāya dhūsara ha’be aṅga

ṣaḍrasa bhojana dūre pariharikabe vraje māgiyā khāiba mādhukarī

parikramā kariyā beḍāba vane vaneviśrāma kariyā jāi jamunā⋅puline

tāpa dūra kariba śītala vaṁśīvaṭe(kabe) kuñje baiṭhaba hāma vaiṣṇava⋅nikaṭe

narottama⋅dāsa kahe kari’ parihārakabe vā emana daśā hoibe āmāra
IDX.en_translation:
Hari Hari! When will I become a resident of Vṛndāvana? I will behold with my eyes the beautiful forms of the Divine Couple.

Forsaking the comfort of sleeping on an elaborate bed, when will my body be covered in the dust of Vraja?

Leaving aside meals with six tastes (sweet, sour, salty, pungent, bitter, and astringent), when will I eat by begging for mādhukarī (alms) in Vraja?

I will wander from forest to forest performing parikramā, and I will rest on the banks of the Yamunā.

I will relieve my distress in the cool Vaṁśīvaṭa, when will I sit in a kuñja (grove) in the company of Vaiṣṇavas?

Narottam Dāsa says, making this humble plea, when will such a state be mine?

IDX.first_line: hari hari kabe hena dasa habe mora
IDX.search: hari hari kabe hena dasa habe mora seviba dohara pada anande vibhora bhramara hoiya sada rahiba carane sricaranamrta sada kariba asvadane
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari hari! kabe hena daśā habe mora
seviba dohāra pada ānande vibhora

bhramara hoiyā sadā rahiba caraṇe
śrī-caraṇāmṛta sadā kariba āsvādane

ei āśā kari āmi⋅yata sakhīgaṇa
tomādera kṛpāya haya vāñchita pūraṇa

bahudina vāñchā kari’ pūrṇa jāte haya
sabe meli dayā kara hoiyā sadaya

sevā āśe narottama kānde divāniśi
kṛpā kari’ kara more anugata dāsī
IDX.en_translation:
Hari Hari! When will such a state be mine? Overwhelmed with joy, I will serve the feet of the Couple.

Becoming a bee, I will always remain at the feet; I will always taste the nectar of the holy feet.

I cherish this hope—all you sakhīs! By your mercy, the desire is fulfilled.

That which I have desired for many days may be fulfilled—all of you meeting together, be kind, becoming merciful.

In the hope of service, Narottama weeps day and night; mercifully make me a follower maidservant.

IDX.first_line: hari hari kabe mora habe hena dinavimala vaisnave rati upajibevasana hoibe ksina
IDX.search: hari hari kabe mora habe hena dinavimala vaisnave rati upajibevasana hoibe ksina antara bahire sama vyavaharaamani manada habakrsna sankirtane sri krsna smaranesatata majiya raba
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari hari kabe mora habe hena dinavimala vaiṣṇave, rati upajibe,vāsanā hoibe kṣīṇa

antara⋅bāhire, sama vyavahāra,amānī mānada ha’bakṛṣṇa⋅saṅkīrtane, śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅smaraṇe,satata majiyā ra’ba

e dehera kriyā, abhyāse kariba,jīvana jāpana lāgi’śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅bhajane, anukūla jāhā,tāhe ha’ba anurāgī

bhajanera jāhā, pratikūla tāhā,dṛḍhabhāve teyāgibabhajite bhajite, samaya āsile,e deha chāḍiyā diba

bhakativinoda, ei āśā kari’,basiyā godruma⋅vaneprabhu⋅kṛpā lāgi’, vyākula antare,sadā kā̐de saṅgopane
IDX.en_translation:
Hari Hari, when will that day be mine? When affection for pure Vaiṣṇavas will arise, And desires will become weak.

Inside and outside, my behavior will be consistent, I will become one who desires no respect for myself but offers all respect to others. In congregational chanting of Kṛṣṇa’s names, and in remembering Śrī Kṛṣṇa, I will always remain immersed.

The activities of this body, I will perform out of habit, For the sake of maintaining life. That which is favorable to devotional service to Śrī Kṛṣṇa, To that I will become attached.

That which is unfavorable to devotional service, That I will firmly give up. While performing devotional service, when the time comes, I will give up this body.

Bhaktivinoda, cherishing this hope, Sitting in Godruma-vana, For the Lord’s grace, with an anxious heart, He always weeps secretly.

IDX.first_line: hari hari kabe mora hoibe sudina
IDX.search: hari hari kabe mora hoibe sudina govardhana girivare parama nibhrte ghare rai kanu karaiba sayana bhrngarera jale ranga carana dhoyaiba muchaba apana cikure kanakasamputa kari karpura tambula puri jogaiba duhuka adhare
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari hari! kabe mora hoibe sudina
govardhana girivare, parama nibhṛte ghare,
rāi kānu karāiba śayana

bhṛṅgārera jale rāṅgā⋅, caraṇa dhoyāiba,
muchaba āpana cikure
kanakasampuṭa kari’, ‘karpūra tāmbūla pūri’
jogāiba du̐huka adhare

priyasakhīgaṇa⋅saṅge, sevana kariba raṅge,
caraṇa seviba nija kare
du̐huka kamala⋅diṭhi, kautuke heraba,
du̐hu aṅga pulaka antare

mallikā mālatī jūthi, nānā phule mālā gā̐thi,
kabe diba dohāra galāya
soṇāra kaṭorā kari, kārpūra candana bhari’,
kabe diba dohākāra gāya

āra kabe emana habe, du̐hu⋅mukha⋅nirakhiba,
līlārasa nikuñjaśayane
śrī⋅kundalatāra saṅge, keli⋅kautuka raṅge,
narottama karibe śravaṇe
IDX.en_translation:
O Lord Hari! When will my auspicious day come? On Govardhana Hill, in a most secluded place, I will make Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa lie down to rest.

With water from a jeweled pitcher I will wash Their reddish lotus feet and wipe them with my own hair. Taking a golden casket and filling it with camphor and betel, I will offer it to Their lips.

With my dear companions I will serve Them joyfully, serving Their lotus feet with my own hands. I will behold Their lotus-like glances with delight, and Their forms will cause ecstasy within my heart.

When will I string garlands of jasmine and various other flowers and place them around Their necks? Taking a golden cup and filling it with camphor and sandalwood paste, when will I anoint Their bodies?

And when will it happen that I will gaze upon Their faces, reveling in the nectar of Their pastimes as They recline in the secluded grove? With Śrī Kundalatā, in the joy of playful sport, Narottama will listen.

IDX.first_line: hari hari kabe mora hoibe sudine
IDX.search: hari hari kabe mora hoibe sudine kelikautuka range kariba sevane lalita visakha sane jateka sangira gane mandali kariba doha meli rai kanu kare dhari nrtya kare phiri phiri nirakhi gonaba kutuhali
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari hari! kabe mora hoibe sudine
kelikautuka raṅge kariba sevane

lalitā⋅viśākhā sane, jateka saṅgīra gaṇe,
maṇḍalī kariba doha meli’
rāi kānu⋅kare dhari’, nṛtya kare phiri’ phiri’,
nirakhi’ goṅā’ba kutūhalī

alasa⋅viśrāma⋅ghare, govardhana⋅girivare,
rāikānu karibe śayana
narottama⋅dāsa kaya, ei jena mora haya,
anukṣaṇa caraṇa sevana
IDX.en_translation:
Hari Hari! When will I have that auspicious day when I will perform service in the playful pastimes?

With Lalitā and Viśākhā, and all the group of companions, I will make a circle, bringing the Two together. Holding the hands of Rāi and Kānu, They will dance round and round; I will pass the time watching with curiosity.

In the house of leisurely rest, on the best of hills, Govardhana, Rāi and Kānu will lie down. Narottama Dāsa says: May this be mine—service to the feet at every moment.

IDX.first_line: hari hari kata dine hena dasa haba
IDX.search: hari hari kata dine hena dasa haba sri rasa manjari sange sri mani manjari range sri rupera anuga haiba susitala vrndavana ratnavedi susobhana tahe manimaya siŋhasana hema nila kantidhara rai kanu sundara tahe basaiba duijana
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses: hari hari! kata dine hena daśā haba śrī⋅rāsa⋅mañjarī saṅge śrī⋅maṇi⋅mañjarī rāṅge śrī⋅rūpera anugā haiba suśītala vṛndāvana ratnavedī suśobhana tāhe maṇimaya siŋhāsana hema nīla⋅kāntidhara rāi⋅kānu sundara tāhe basāiba duijana sakhīra ādeśa habe cāmara ḍhulāba kabe tāmbula jogāba cāṅda⋅mukhe ānandita ha’ba sadā śuddha⋅bhāve prema⋅kathā duhāra pīriti rasa⋅sukhe mallikā mālatī jūthī nānā phule mālā gā̐thi parāiba duhāra galāye rasera ālasa kāle basiyā caraṇa tale sevana kariba duhāra pāye rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa prāṇapati jīvane maraṇe gati ihā vine āra nāhi mane śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅caitanya⋅prāṇa svarūpa⋅rūpa⋅sanātana narottama ei nivedana
IDX.en_translation:
O Lord Hari! When will I achieve such a state? In the company of Śrī Rāsa-mañjarī and Śrī Maṇi-mañjarī, I will become a follower of Śrīla Rūpa Gosvāmī.

In the very cool and beautiful Vṛndāvana, on a beautiful, jewel-studded altar, I will see a jewel-studded throne. Upon that throne, I will seat the beautiful Divine Couple, who have golden and dark-blue complexions.

When will I receive the order of the gopī friends to fan Them with a whisk? When will I offer betel nuts to Their moon-like faces? I will always be joyful, purely devoted to topics of divine love and the happiness of Their mutual affection.

Stringing garlands of various jasmine flowers, I will place them around Their necks. When They are resting, I will sit at Their lotus feet and render service to both of Them.

Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa are the Lords of my life, my only shelter in life and in death. Without Them, nothing else remains in my mind. Svarūpa Dāmodara, Rūpa Gosvāmī, and Sanātana Gosvāmī are the very life of Lord Caitanya. This is the humble prayer of Narottama Dāsa.

IDX.first_line: hari hari ki mora karama abhaga
IDX.search: hari hari ki mora karama abhaga viphale jivana gela hrdaye rahila sela nahi bhela hari anuraga jajna dana tirtha snana punyakarma japa dhyana akarane saba gela mohe bujhilama mane hena upahasa haya jena vastrahina alankara dehe
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari hari! ki mora karama abhāga
viphale jīvana gela, hṛdaye rahila śela,
nāhi bhela hari⋅anurāga

jajña, dāna, tīrtha⋅snāna, puṇya-karma, japa, dhyāna,
akāraṇe saba gela mohe
bujhilāma mane hena, upahāsa haya jena,
vastrahīna alaṅkāra dehe

sādhu-mukhe kathāmṛta, śuniyā vimala cita,
nāhi bhela aparādha⋅kāraṇa
satata asatsaṅga, sakali hoila bhaṅga,
ki kariba āile śamana

śruti⋅smṛti sadā rabe, śuniyāchi ei sabe,
haripada abhaya śaraṇa
janama loiyā sukhe, kṛṣṇa nā balinu mukhe,
nā karinu se⋅rūpa bhāvana

rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa du̐hu pāya, tanu mana rahu tāya,
āra dūre jāuka vāsanā
narottama dāse kaya, āra mora nāhi bhaya,
tanu⋅mana sa̐pinu āpanā
IDX.en_translation:
O Lord Hari! What unfortunate destiny is mine! My life passed in vain, and a spear remained in my heart, for I did not attain affection for You.

Sacrifice, charity, bathing in holy places, pious activities, chanting, and meditation—all were lost fruitlessly due to illusion. I realize now that it was like a mockery, like wearing decorations on a body without clothes.

From the mouths of the saints I heard the nectar of Your words, yet with my impure heart, I did not avoid committing offenses. Constant association with the impious broke everything; what will I do when the messenger of death arrives?

The scriptures will always declare that Your feet are a fearless shelter—I have heard all of this. Though born in comfort, I did not utter Kṛṣṇa's name, nor did I meditate on His form.

May my body and mind remain at the feet of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa, and may all other desires depart far away. Narottama dāsa says: I have no more fear, for I have surrendered my body and mind completely.

IDX.first_line: hari hari krpa kari rakha nija padekama krodha chaya jane lana phire nanasthanevisaya bhunjaya nanamate
IDX.search: hari hari krpa kari rakha nija padekama krodha chaya jane lana phire nanasthanevisaya bhunjaya nanamate hoiya mayara dasa kari nana abhilasatomara smarana gela durearthalabha ei ase kapata vaisnava vesebhramiya bulaye ghare ghare
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari hari! kṛpā kari’ rākha nija padekāma⋅krodha chaya jane, lañā phire nānāsthāne,viṣaya⋅bhuñjāya nānāmate

hoiyā māyāra dāsa, kari’ nānā abhilāṣa,tomāra smaraṇa gela dūrearthalābha ei āśe, kapaṭa vaiṣṇava⋅veśe,bhramiyā bulaye ghare ghare

aneka duḥkhera pare, layechile vrajapure,kṛpāḍora galāya bāndhiyādaivamāyā balātkāre, khasāiyā sei ḍore,bhava-kūpe dileka ḍāriyā

punaḥ jadi kṛpā kari’, e janāra keśe dhari’,ṭāniyā tulaha vraja⋅dhāmetabe se dekhiye bhāla, natubā parāṇa gela,kahe dīna dāsa narottame
IDX.en_translation:
O Lord Hari! Please bestow Your mercy and keep me at Your feet. Lust, anger, and the other four enemies take me and drag me to various places, making me enjoy material things in various ways.

Becoming a servant of illusion and following various desires, Your remembrance went far away. In the hope of gaining wealth and wearing the guise of a deceitful devotee, I wander from house to house.

After much suffering, You had taken me to the town of Vraja, tying a rope of mercy around my neck. But the divine energy of illusion forcefully untied that rope and threw me back into the well of material existence.

Again, if You bestow mercy, catching me by the hair and pulling me up to Vrajadhāma—then it will be good. Otherwise, my life is lost. So says the humble servant Narottama.

IDX.first_line: hari hari krpa kari rakha nija padekama krodha chaya jane lana phire nanasthanevisaya bhunjaya nanamate
IDX.search: hari hari krpa kari rakha nija padekama krodha chaya jane lana phire nanasthanevisaya bhunjaya nanamate hoiya mayara dasa kari nana abhilasatomara smarana gela durearthalabha ei ase kapata vaisnava vesebhramiya bulaye ghare ghare
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari hari! kṛpā kari’ rākha nija padekāma⋅krodha chaya jane, lañā phire nānāsthāne,viṣaya⋅bhuñjāya nānāmate

hoiyā māyāra dāsa, kari’ nānā abhilāṣa,tomāra smaraṇa gela dūrearthalābha ei āśe, kapaṭa vaiṣṇava⋅veśe,bhramiyā bulaye ghare ghare

aneka duḥkhera pare, layechile vrajapure,kṛpāḍora galāya bāndhiyādaivamāyā balātkāre, khasāiyā sei ḍore,bhava-kūpe dileka ḍāriyā

punaḥ jadi kṛpā kari’, e janāra keśe dhari’,ṭāniyā tulaha vraja⋅dhāmetabe se dekhiye bhāla, natubā parāṇa gela,kahe dīna dāsa narottame
IDX.en_translation:
O Lord Hari! Please bestow Your mercy and keep me at Your feet. Lust, anger, and the other four enemies take me and drag me to various places, making me enjoy material things in various ways.

Becoming a servant of illusion and following various desires, Your remembrance went far away. In the hope of gaining wealth and wearing the guise of a deceitful devotee, I wander from house to house.

After much suffering, You had taken me to the town of Vraja, tying a rope of mercy around my neck. But the divine energy of illusion forcefully untied that rope and threw me back into the well of material existence.

Again, if You bestow mercy, catching me by the hair and pulling me up to Vrajadhāma—then it will be good. Otherwise, my life is lost. So says the humble servant Narottama.

IDX.first_line: hari hari viphale janama gonainumanusyajanama paiya radha krsna na bhajiyajaniya suniya visa khainu
IDX.search: hari hari viphale janama gonainumanusyajanama paiya radha krsna na bhajiyajaniya suniya visa khainu golokera premadhana harinama sankirtanarati na janmila kene tayasamsara visanale divanisi hiya jvalejudaite na kainu upaya
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari hari! viphale janama goṅāinumanuṣya-janama pāiyā, rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa nā bhajiyā,jāniyā śuniyā viṣa khāinu

golokera prema-dhana, harināma⋅saṅkīrtana,rati nā janmila kene tāyasaṁsāra⋅viṣānale, divāniśi hiyā jvale,juḍāite nā kainu upāya

vrajendra-nandana jei, śacī⋅suta haila sei,balarāma hoila nitāidīna hīna jata chila, hari-nāme uddhārila,tā’ra sākṣī jagāi⋅mādhāi

hā hā prabhu nandasuta, vṛṣabhānu⋅sutāyuta,karuṇā karaha eibāranarottama⋅dāsa kaya, nā ṭhelio rāṅgāpāya,tomā vinā ke āche āmāra
IDX.en_translation:
Hari Hari! I spent my life in vain. Having obtained a human birth, not worshipping Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa, I knowingly drank poison.

The treasure of Goloka’s love, the saṅkīrtana of Hari’s Name—why did I not develop attachment for it? My heart burns day and night in the poisonous fire of worldly existence; I did not make a way to cool it.

He who is Vrajendra-nandana became Śacī-suta; Balarāma became Nitāi. He delivered all the lowly and destitute by the Holy Name; Jagāi and Mādhāi are the witnesses to that.

Alas, alas! O Lord, Son of Nanda, along with the daughter of Vṛṣabhānu, be merciful this time. Narottama Dāsa says: Do not push me away from Your reddish feet; who do I have except You?

IDX.first_line: hari he
IDX.search: hari he prapance padiya agati hoiya na dekhi upaya ara agatira gati carane sarana tomaya karinu sara karama geyana kichu nahi mora sadhana bhajana nai tumi krpamaya ami ta kangala ahaituki krpa cai
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari he!
prapañce paḍiyā, agati hoiyā,
nā dekhi’ upāya āra
agatira gati, caraṇe śaraṇa,
tomāya karinu sāra

karama geyāna, kichu nāhi mora,
sādhana⋅bhajana nāi
tumi kṛpāmaya, āmi ta’ kāṅgāla,
ahaitukī kṛpā cāi

vākya⋅manovega, krodha⋅jihvā⋅vega,
udara⋅upastha⋅vega
miliyā e saba, saṁsāre bhāsāye,
diteche paramodvega

aneka jatane, se⋅saba damane,
chāḍiyāchi āśā āmi
anāthera nātha, ḍāki tava nāma,
ekhana bharasā tumi
IDX.translation_intro: From The Harmonist, March 1929. Edited by Bhaktisiddhanta Sarasvati.
IDX.en_translation:
Hari! Fallen into this world, Rendered helpless, I find no other way of deliverance, Guide of the guideless! The Refuge of Thy feet I seek as essential.

Capacity for work or Knowledge I have none; I neither serve Thee nor try. Thou art full of mercy, I am destitute, indeed; Thy causeless mercy I crave.

The activities of speach and mind, Anger, palate, The stomach and organ of generation,- All these joining together, Casting me adrift on the stream of the world, Are giving me great trouble.

The hope of subduing them, By using every care, I have given up. Lord of the lordless! I call upon Thy Name. Thou art now the Hope.

IDX.first_line: hari he
IDX.search: hari he arthera sancaye visaya prayase ana katha prajalpane ana adhikara niyama agrahe asatsanga saŋghatane asthira siddhante rahinu majiya haribhakti raila dure e hrdaye matra parahimsa mada pratistha sathata sphure
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari he!
arthera sañcaye, viṣaya⋅prayāse,
āna kathā prajalpane
āna adhikāra, niyama⋅āgrahe,
asatsaṅga⋅saŋghaṭane

asthira siddhānte, rahinu majiyā,
haribhakti raila dūre
e hṛdaye mātra, parahiṁsā⋅mada,
pratiṣṭhā, śaṭhatā, sphure

e saba āgraha, chāḍite nārinu,
āpana doṣete mari’
janama viphala, hoila āmāra,
ekhana ki kari’ hari

āmi ta’ patita, patita⋅pāvana,
tomāra pavitra nāma
se⋅sambandha dhari’, tomāra caraṇe,
śaraṇa loinu hāma
IDX.translation_intro: From The Harmonist, April 1929. Edited by Bhaktisiddhanta Sarasvati.
IDX.en_translation:
Hari! Gathering wealth, Striving after things of this world, Idle talk on topics of the hour, Misunderstanding the sphere of my duties, Adherence to the letter of the law, Contriving to mix with worldly people,

Uncertainty of principle, - Continue to enamour me still. Devotion to Hari is afar off. This heart only breeds Malice against others, pride, Desire for fame and cunning,

All these inclinations I could not leave. I am ruined by my own fault, This birth of mine Is rendered useless Hari! what now I am to do?

I am fallen, indeed, Thy Holy Name Is Saviour of those gone astray. By this relationship At Thy feet I take Refuge.

IDX.first_line: hari he
IDX.search: hari he bhajane utsaha bhaktite visvasa premalabhe dhairya dhana bhakti anukula karma pravartana asatsanga visarjana bhakti sadacara ei chaya guna nahila amara natha kemane bhajiba tomara carana chadiya mayara satha
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari he!
bhajane utsāha, bhaktite viśvāsa,
premalābhe dhairya⋅dhana
bhakti⋅anukūla, karma⋅pravartana,
asatsaṅga visarjana

bhakti⋅sadācāra, ei chaya guṇa,
nahila āmāra nātha
kemane bhajiba, tomāra caraṇa,
chāḍiyā māyāra sātha

garhita ācāre, rahilāma maji’,
nā karinu sādhusaṅga
la’ye sādhu⋅veśa, āne upadeśi,
e baḍa māyāra raṅga

e hena daśāya, ahaitukī kṛpā,
tomāra pāiba, hari
śrī⋅guru⋅āśraye, ḍākiba tomāya,
kabe vā minati kari’
IDX.translation_intro: From The Harmonist, April 1929. Edited by Bhaktisiddhanta Sarasvati.
IDX.en_translation:
Hari ! Zeal in serving, Faith in devotion, The treasure of patience in attainment of love, Devising act That conduces to devotion, Abandonment of worldly association,

Approved devotional practice, These six good qualities, Lord, have never been mine. How worship Thy feet, Giving up all affinity with Thy delusive power?

Of improper activities I am so enamoured; Association with sadhus I have not practised. Assuming the garb of a sadhu I instruct others. This is a funny contrivance of Maya!

In such condition I shal! receive, Hari, Thy cause-less mercy. Under the guidance of the good preceptor, O, when shall I call upon Thee With humble entreaty ?

IDX.first_line: hari he
IDX.search: hari he dana pratigraha mitho gupta katha bhaksana bhojana dana sangera laksana ei chaya haya ihate bhaktira prana tattva na bujhiye jnane va ajnane asate e saba kari bhakti harainu samsari hoinu sudure rahile hari
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari he!
dāna⋅pratigraha, mitho gupta⋅kathā,
bhakṣaṇa, bhojana⋅dāna
saṅgera lakṣaṇa, ei chaya haya,
ihāte bhaktira prāṇa

tattva nā bujhiye, jñāne vā ajñāne,
asate e saba kari’
bhakti hārāinu, saṁsārī hoinu,
sudūre rahile hari

kṛṣṇabhakta⋅jane, e saṅga⋅lakṣaṇe,
ādara kariba jabe
bhakti⋅mahādevī, āmāra hṛdaya⋅,
āsane basibe tabe

joṣitsaṅgī jana, kṛṣṇābhakta āra,
du̐hu⋅saṅga parihari’
tava bhaktajana⋅, saṅga anukṣaṇa,
kabe vā hoibe hari
IDX.translation_intro: From The Harmonist, August 1929. Edited by Bhaktisiddhanta Sarasvati.
IDX.en_translation:
Hari! Making and receiving gifts, Mutual confidential talk, Accepting and offering food, These six are indications Of intimate association; These are the life of devotion.

Not understanding the truth,- Consciously or unconsciously, Enacting these with sinners I have missed devotion, Am become a worldling; Thou, Hari, art left afar.

When I shall cherish those Who are devoted to Krishna By these indications of intimacy, The high goddess of devotion Will then take her seat On the throne of my heart.

Those who are addicted to women, Those who are averse to Krishna,- Abandoning the society of both, When shall I cultivate, O Hari, Constant association with Thy devotees?

IDX.first_line: hari he
IDX.search: hari he sangadosa sunya diksitadiksita jadi tava nama gaya manase adara kariba tahare jani nija jana taya diksita hoiya bhaje tuya pada tahare pranati kari ananya bhajane vijna jei jana tahare seviba hari
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari he!
saṅgadoṣa⋅śūnya, dīkṣitādīkṣita,
jadi tava nāma gāya
mānase ādara, kariba tā̐hāre,
jāni’ nija⋅jana tā’ya

dīkṣita hoiyā, bhaje tuyā pada,
tā̐hāre praṇati kari
ananya⋅bhajane, vijña jei jana,
tā̐hāre seviba, hari

sarvabhūte sama, je bhaktera mati,
tā̐hāra darśane māni
āpanāke dhanya, se⋅saṅga pāiyā,
caritārtha hoilu̐ jāni

niṣkapaṭa mati, vaiṣṇavera prati,
ei dharma kabe pā’ba
kabe e saṁsāra⋅, sindhu pāra ha’ye,
tava vrajapure jā’ba
IDX.translation_intro: From The Harmonist, August 1929. Edited by Bhaktisiddhanta Sarasvati.
IDX.en_translation:
Hari- If one, initiated, or no, who is free From the offence of evil association, Sing Thy Name,- In my mind I will him esteem, Knowing him to be my kin.

To one who serves Thy Feet Being initiated, I make my obeisance. One who is an adept In exclusive devotion to Thee, Hari, him I’ll serve.

By the sight of the devotee Whose mind regards with equanimity All animate beings I feel myself exalted, By obtaining his society I know that my want is fulfilled.

O, when shall I have the quality Of a guileless mind Towards the Vaishnava? O, when shall I hie To Thy abode of Braja After crossing the ocean of this world?

IDX.first_line: hari he
IDX.search: hari he niradharma gata jahnavi salile panka phena drsta haya tathapi kakhana brahmadrava dharma se salila na chadaya vaisnava sarira aprakrta sada svabhava vapura dharme kabhu nahe jada tathapi je ninde pade se visamadharme
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari he!
nīradharma⋅gata, jāhnavī⋅salile,
paṅka⋅phena dṛṣṭa haya
tathāpi kakhana, brahmadrava⋅dharma,
se salila nā chāḍaya

vaiṣṇava⋅śarīra, aprākṛta sadā,
svabhāva⋅vapura dharme
kabhu nahe jaḍa, tathāpi je ninde,
paḍe se viṣamādharme

sei aparādhe, jamera jātanā,
pāya jīva avirata
he nanda⋅nandana! sei aparādhe,
jena nāhi hoi hata

tomāra vaiṣṇava, vaibhava tomāra,
āmāre karuna dayā
tabe mora gati, habe tava prati,
pā’ba tava padachāyā
IDX.translation_intro: From The Harmonist, November 1929. Edited by Bhaktisiddhanta Sarasvati.
IDX.en_translation:
Hari! In the stream of the Ganges Are found mud and froth Inherent in the nature of water. Yet that fluid Never is divested Of the quality of its Divine solvency.

The body of the Vaishnava Is ever super sensuous By its nature. It is never a dead thing; Those who yet malign, Thereby fall into mortal sin.

For this offence To the sufferings of death The fallen snul is for ever doomed. Darling Son of Nanda! May T not be killed By committing sueh transgression!

The Vaishnava is Thy own, An expression of Thy Divine power. May I obtain bis mercy ! Then my steps Will turn toward Thee And I shall attain the shade of Thy feet.

IDX.first_line: hari he
IDX.search: hari he tomare bhuliya avidya pidaya pidita rasana mora krsna nama sudha bhala nahi lage visaya sukhete bhora pratidina jadi adara kariya se nama kirtana kari sitapala jena nasi roga mula krame svadu haya hari
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari he!
tomāre bhuliyā, avidyā⋅pīḍāya,
pīḍita rasanā mora
kṛṣṇa⋅nāma⋅sudhā, bhāla nāhi lāge,
viṣaya⋅sukhete bhora

pratidina jadi, ādara kariyā,
se⋅nāma kīrtana kari
sitapala jena, nāśi’ roga⋅mūla,
krame svādu haya, hari

durdaiva āmāra, se nāme ādara,
nā hoila dayāmaya!
daśa aparādha, āmāra durdaiva,
kemane hoibe kṣaya

anudina jena, tava nāma gāi,
kramete kṛpāya tava
aparādha jā’be, nāme ruci ha’be,
āsvādiva nāmāsava
IDX.translation_intro: From The Harmonist, November 1929. Edited by Bhaktisiddhanta Sarasvati.
IDX.en_translation:
Hari ! Forgetting Thee The tip of my tongue Is embittered by the distemper of un-truth; On it the nectar of the Name Krishna Does not taste sweet; I am delirious with the taste of worldly pleasures.

If perchance daily With warm affection I sing that Name, Hari, then, like the lump of sugar, Rooting out billiousness that makes it taste bitter, It turns mellow by degrees.

But such is my lot, Most merciful Lord! I feel no attraction for that Name. The ten offences, My calamitous fate, How are they to cease?

May I daily without fail Sing Thy Name; Then in proper sequence, by Thy mercy, Offence will vanish, Liking for the Name appear, And T shall taste the nectarine sweetness of the Name!

IDX.first_line: hari he
IDX.search: hari he sri rupagosani sri guru rupete siksa dila mora kane jana mora katha namera kangala rati pabe nama gane krsna nama rupa guna sucarita parama jatane kari rasana manase karaha niyoga krama vidhi anusari
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari he!
śrī⋅rūpagosāñi, śrī⋅guru⋅rūpete,
śikṣā dilā mora kāṇe
“jāna mora kathā, nāmera kāṅgāla!
rati pā’be nāma⋅gāne

kṛṣṇa⋅nāma⋅rūpa⋅, guṇa⋅sucarita,
parama jatane kari’
rasanā⋅mānase, karaha niyoga,
krama⋅vidhi anusari’

vraje kari’ vāsa, rāgānugā hañā,
smaraṇa, kīrtana kara
e nikhila kāla, karaha jāpana,
upadeśa⋅sāra dhara”

hā! rūpagosāñi, dayā kari’ kabe,
dibe dīne vrajavāsā
rāgātmika tumi, tava padānuga,
hoite dāsera āśā
IDX.translation_intro: From The Harmonist, June 1930. Edited by Bhaktisiddhanta Sarasvati.
IDX.en_translation:
Hari! Sri Rupa Goswami In the form of Sree Guru Taught in my ear,- Learn the meaning of my words, You who beg for the gift of the Nameo ; You will then find attraction in chanting the Name.

The Name, Form, Quglity, The excellent Deeds of Krishna, With the utmost care, Apply to thy tongue And mind, in order Following the Scriptural injunction.

Dwelling in Braja, Following the spontaneous bent of love Practise chanting and recollection. In this manner employ Thy whole time, Accept this as the essence of all teaching.

Ah! Rupa Gosain! When wilt thou, out of mercy, Grant this wretch to live in Braja ! Thy soul is love; To be the follower of thy feet Is the hope of thy servant.

IDX.first_line: hari he
IDX.search: hari he ohe prabhu dayamaya tomara caranadvaya sruti siropari sobha paya gurujana sire punah sobha paya sata guna dekhi amara parana judaya jiva manoratha patha tahi saba anugata jiva vanchakalpataru jatha jivera se kuladhana ati pujya sanatana jivera caramagati tatha
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari he!
ohe prabhu dayāmaya, tomāra caraṇadvaya,
śruti⋅śiropari śobhā pāya
gurujana⋅śire punaḥ, śobhā pāya śata guṇa,
dekhi’ āmāra parāṇa juḍāya

jīva⋅manoratha⋅patha, ta̐hi saba anugata,
jīva⋅vāñchākalpataru jathā
jīvera se kuladhana, ati pūjya sanātana,
jīvera caramagati tathā

kamalākṣa⋅padadvaya, parama⋅ānandamaya,
niṣkapaṭe seviyā satata
e bhaktivinoda cāya, satata tuṣite tāya,
bhaktajanera ha’ye anugata
IDX.en_translation:
O Hari! O merciful Lord, Your two lotus feet adorn the crown of the Vedas. And upon the heads of the spiritual masters, they are a hundred times more glorious; seeing this, my heart is soothed.

They are the goal of every soul's desires, just like a wish-fulfilling tree. They are the ancestral wealth of the soul, eternally worshipable, and the ultimate destination.

The two feet of the lotus-eyed Lord are supremely blissful. By serving them sincerely and constantly, Bhaktivinoda desires to always satisfy them by following in the footsteps of the devotees.

IDX.first_line: hari he
IDX.search: hari he tomara iksane haya sakala utpatti laya caturdasa bhuvanete jata jada jiva adi kari tomara krpaya hari labhe janma ara kaba kata tahadera vrtti jata tomara iksane svatah janme prabhu tumi sarvesvara sakala jantura tumi svabhavika nityasvami suhrnmitra pranera isvara
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari he!
tomāra īkṣaṇe haya, sakala utpatti laya,
caturdaśa bhuvanete jata
jaḍa jīva ādi kari’, tomāra kṛpāya hari,
labhe janma, āra ka’ba kata

tāhādera vṛtti jata, tomāra īkṣaṇe svataḥ,
janme, prabhu tumi sarveśvara
sakala jantura tumi, svābhāvika nityasvāmī,
suhṛnmitra⋅prāṇera īśvara

e bhaktivinoda kaya, śuna, prabhu dayāmaya,
bhaktaprati vātsalya tomāra
naisargika dharma haya, aupādhika kabhu naya,
dāse dayā hoiyā udāra
IDX.en_translation:
O Lord! By Your glance, all creation and dissolution occur in the fourteen planetary systems. Starting with inert matter and moving up to living beings, all obtain birth by Your mercy, O Hari. How much more can I say?

All their activities arise spontaneously by Your glance, O Lord, for You are the controller of all. You are the natural and eternal master of all living entities, a well-wishing friend, and the Lord of their lives.

Bhaktivinoda says: Listen, O merciful Lord! Your affection for Your devotees is a natural quality, never temporary or conditional. Please be generous with Your mercy to Your servant.

IDX.first_line: hari he
IDX.search: hari he paratattva vicaksana vyasa adi munigana sastra vicariya bara bara prabhu tava nityarupa gunasila anurupa tomara caritra sudhasara suddhasattvamayi lila mukhyasastre prakasila jivera kusala suvidhane rajastamoguna andha asura prakrti manda jane taha bujhite na jane
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari he!
paratattva vicakṣaṇa, vyāsa⋅ādi munigaṇa,
śāstra vicāriyā bāra bāra
prabhu, tava nityarūpa, guṇaśīla anurūpa,
tomāra caritra sudhāsāra

śuddhasattvamayī līlā, mukhyaśāstre prakāśilā,
jīvera kuśala suvidhāne
rajastamoguṇa⋅andha, asura⋅prakṛti manda,
jane tāhā bujhite nā jāne

nāhi māne nityarūpa, bhajiyā maṇḍbandhakūpa,
rahe tāhe udāsīnaprāya
e bhaktivinoda gāya, ki durdaiva hāya hāya,
haridāsa hari nāhi pāya
IDX.en_translation:
O Hari! Sages like Vyāsa and others, experts in the Supreme Truth, repeatedly deliberate the scriptures. O Lord, Your eternal form, with its corresponding divine qualities and characteristics, and Your pastimes are the essence of nectar.

These pastimes consisting of pure goodness are revealed in the prominent scriptures for the ultimate welfare of the souls. But those blinded by the modes of passion and ignorance, with a demoniac nature, are unable to understand this.

They do not accept the eternal form, and resorting to the dark well of material existence, they remain almost indifferent. Bhaktivinoda sings: Alas, what great misfortune! A servant of Hari does not attain Hari!

IDX.first_line: hari he
IDX.search: hari he jagatera vastu jata baddha saba svabhavatah desa kala vastu simasraye tumi prabhu sarvesvara naha sima vidhipara vidhi saba kape tava bhaye sama va adhika tava svabhavatah asambhava vidhi langhi tava avasthana svatantra svabhava dhara apane gopana kara mayabale kari adhisthana
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari he!
jagatera vastu jata, baddha saba svabhāvataḥ,
deśa⋅kāla⋅vastu⋅sīmāśraye
tumi prabhu sarveśvara, naha sīmā⋅vidhipara,
vidhi saba kā̐pe tava bhaye

sama vā adhika tava, svabhāvataḥ asambhava,
vidhi laṅghi’ tava avasthāna
svatantra⋅svabhāva dhara, āpane gopana kara,
māyābale kari’ adhiṣṭhāna

tathāpi ananya⋅bhakta, tomāre dekhite śakta,
sadā dekhe svarūpa tomāra
e bhaktivinoda dīna, ananyabhajana⋅hīna,
bhakta⋅pada⋅reṇu mātra sāra
IDX.en_translation:
Hari! All things in the world are inherently bound, limited by space, time, and material objects. You, Lord, are the master of all, not subject to any limits or rules; all regulations tremble in fear of You.

An equal to or greater than You is naturally impossible; Your existence transcends all rules. You possess an independent nature, You conceal Yourself, manifesting through the power of Māyā.

Yet, exclusive devotees are capable of seeing You, always perceiving Your true form. This Bhaktivinoda, humble and devoid of exclusive worship, considers only the dust of the devotees' feet as his true essence.

IDX.first_line: hari he
IDX.search: hari he tumi sarvagunayuta sakti tava vasibhuta vadanya sarala suci dhira dayalu madhura sama krti sthira sarvottama krtajna laksane punah vira samasta kalyana guna ganamrta sambhavana samudra svarupa bhagavan bindu bindu guna tava sarvajiva suvaibhava tumi purna sarvasaktiman
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari he!
tumi sarvaguṇayuta, śakti tava vaśībhūta,
vadānya, sarala, śuci, dhīra
dayālu, madhura, sama, kṛtī, sthira, sarvottama,
kṛtajña⋅lakṣaṇe punaḥ vīra

samasta kalyāṇa⋅guṇa⋅, gaṇāmṛta⋅sambhāvana,
samudra⋅svarūpa bhagavān
bindu bindu guṇa tava, sarvajīva⋅suvaibhava,
tumi pūrṇa sarvaśaktimān

e bhaktivinoda chāra, kṛtāñjali bāra bāra,
kare cittakathā vijñāpana
