tava dāsagaṇa⋅saṅge, tava līlākathā⋅raṅge,
jāya jena āmāra jīvana
IDX.en_translation:
O Hari! You are endowed with all good qualities, and all power is under Your control. You are generous, simple, pure, steadfast, merciful, sweet, impartial, successful, unwavering, and supreme. You are valiant and possess a grateful nature.

O Lord, You are the ocean-like embodiment of all auspiciousness and nectar-like virtues! Every drop of Your qualities is the supreme wealth for all living entities. You are complete and all-powerful.

This insignificant Bhaktivinoda, with folded hands again and again, makes this heartfelt request: May my life pass in the company of Your servants, in the joy of the narratives of Your divine pastimes.

IDX.first_line: hari he
IDX.search: hari he tomara gambhira mana nahi bujhe anya jana sei mana anusari saba jagat udbhava sthiti pralaya samsaragati mukti adi saktira vaibhava e saba vaidika lila icchamatra prakasila jivera vasana anusare tomate vimukha haye majila avidya laye sei jiva karma parabare
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari he!
tomāra gambhīra mana, nāhi bujhe anya jana,
sei mana⋅anusāri’ saba
jagat⋅udbhava⋅sthiti, pralaya⋅saṁsāragati,
mukti⋅ādi śaktira vaibhava

e saba vaidika līlā, icchāmātra prakāśilā,
jīvera vāsanā⋅anusāre
tomāte vimukha ha’ye, majila avidyā la’ye,
sei jīva karma⋅pārābāre

punaḥ jadi bhakti kari’, bhaje bhaktasaṅga dhari’,
tabe pāya tomāra caraṇa
antaraṅga⋅līlārase, bhāse, māyā nā paraśe,
bhaktivinodera phire mana
IDX.en_translation:
O Lord Hari! No other person comprehends Your profound will. Following that will, all the creation, maintenance, and destruction of the universe, the course of transmigration, and the opulence of powers like liberation originate.

All these Vedic pastimes You manifest by Your mere will, in accordance with the desires of the individual soul. Becoming averse to You, that soul becomes absorbed in ignorance in the ocean of karma.

Again, if one performs devotion and worships by taking the association of devotees, then they attain Your lotus feet. They float in the mellow of Your intimate pastimes, and illusion does not touch them. Bhaktivinoda's mind returns.

IDX.first_line: hari he
IDX.search: hari he mayabaddha jataksana thake ta jivera mana jadamajhe kare vicarana paravyoma jnanamaya tahe tava sthiti haya mana nahi paya darasana bhaktikrpa khadgaghate jadabandha cheda tate jaya mana prakrtira para tomara sundara rupa here tava aparupa jadavastu karaye dhikkara
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari he!
māyābaddha jatakṣaṇa, thāke ta’ jīvera mana,
jaḍamājhe kare vicaraṇa
paravyoma jñānamaya, tāhe tava sthiti haya,
mana nāhi pāya daraśana

bhaktikṛpā⋅khaḍgāghāte, jaḍabandha cheda tā’te,
jāya mana prakṛtira pāra
tomāra sundara rūpa, here’ tava aparūpa,
jaḍavastu karaye dhikkāra

ananta vibhūti jā̐’ra, jini dayā⋅pārābāra,
sei prabhu jīvera īśvara
e bhaktivinoda hīna, sadā śuddhabhaktihīna,
śuddhabhakti māge nirantara
IDX.en_translation:
O Hari! As long as the mind remains bound by illusion, it wanders in the material world. You reside in the spiritual sky, which is full of knowledge, but the mind cannot perceive You.

By the sword of devotion and mercy, that material bondage is severed, and the mind transcends material nature. Upon seeing Your beautiful form and unparalleled beauty, the soul then condemns all material things.

He whose opulences are endless and who is an ocean of mercy is the Lord of all souls. Bhaktivinoda, who is fallen and devoid of pure devotion, constantly begs for that pure devotion.

IDX.first_line: hari he
IDX.search: hari he dharmanistha nahi mora atmabodha va sundara bhakti nahi tomara carane atoeva akincana gatihina dustajana rata sada apana vancane patita pavana tumi patita adhama ami tumi mora ekamatra gati tava padamule painu tomara sarana lainu ami dasa tumi mora pati
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari he!
dharmaniṣṭhā nāhi mora, ātmabodha vā sundara,
bhakti nāhi tomāra caraṇe
atoeva akiñcana, gatihīna duṣṭajana,
rata sadā āpana vañcane

patita⋅pāvana tumi, patita adhama āmi,
tumi mora ekamātra gati
tava pādamūle painu, tomāra śaraṇa lainu,
āmi dāsa, tumi mora pati

e bhaktivinoda kā̐de, hṛde dhairya nāhi bā̐dhe,
bhūme paḍi’ bale ataḥpara
ahaitukī kṛpā kari’, ei duṣṭajane hari,
deha pada⋅chāyā nirantara
IDX.en_translation:
O Hari! I have no religious steadfastness, nor self-realization, nor devotion at Your lotus feet. Therefore, I am a wicked person without possessions or refuge, always engaged in self-deception.

You are the purifier of the fallen, and I am a fallen, lowly soul. You are my only shelter. I have fallen at Your lotus feet and taken shelter. I am Your servant, and You are my master.

Bhaktivinoda cries, no patience remains in his heart; falling to the ground, he says: granting causeless mercy, O Hari, to this wicked person, always give the shade of Your lotus feet.

IDX.first_line: hari he
IDX.search: hari he hena dusta karma nai jaha ami kari nai sahasra sahasrabara hari sei saba karmaphala peye avasara bala amaya pisiche jantropari gati nahi dekhi ara kadi hari anivara tomara agrete ebe ami ja tomara haya mane danda deo akincane tumi mora dandadhara svami
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari he!
hena duṣṭa karma nāi, jāhā āmi kari nāi,
sahasra sahasrabāra hari
sei saba karmaphala, peye avasara⋅bala,
āmāya piśiche jantropari

gati nāhi dekhi’ āra, kā̐di hari anivāra,
tomāra agrete ebe āmi
jā tomāra haya mane, daṇḍa deo akiñcane,
tumi mora daṇḍadhara svāmī

kleśa⋅bhoga bhāgye jata, bhoga mora hou tata,
kintu eka mama nivedana
je je daśā bhogi āmi, āmāke nā chāḍa svāmi!
bhaktivinodera prāṇadhana
IDX.en_translation:
O Hari! There is no wicked action that I have not committed, thousands upon thousands of times. The results of all those actions, having gained strength from opportunity, are crushing me within the machine of material existence.

Seeing no other path, I cry incessantly before You. Whatever You deem fit, bestow punishment upon this humble, destitute soul. You are my disciplinarian and my Master.

Whatever suffering is destined for me, let me experience it. But I have one humble request: In whatever condition I may suffer, do not abandon me, my Master! You are Bhaktivinoda's most precious treasure.

IDX.first_line: hari he
IDX.search: hari he nijakarma dosa phale padi bhavarnava jale habudubu khai katakala satari satari jai sindhu anta nahi pai bhavasindhu ananta visala nimagna hoinu jabe dakinu katara rabe keha more karaha uddhara seikale aile tumi toma jani kulabhumi asabija hoila amara
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari he!
nijakarma⋅doṣa⋅phale, paḍi’ bhavārṇava⋅jale,
hābuḍubu khāi katakāla
sā̐tāri’ sā̐tāri’ jāi, sindhu⋅anta nāhi pāi,
bhavasindhu ananta viśāla

nimagna hoinu jabe, ḍākinu kātara rabe,
keha more karaha uddhāra
seikāle āile tumi, tomā jāni’ kūlabhūmi,
āśābīja hoila āmāra

tumi hari dayāmaya, pāile more suniścaya,
sarvottama dayāra viṣaya
tomāke nā chāḍi āra, e bhaktivinoda chāra,
dayāpātra pāile dayāmaya
IDX.en_translation:
O Hari! As a result of my own faulty deeds, I have fallen into the ocean of material existence. How long will I continue to drown and float? Swimming and swimming, I find no end to this infinitely vast ocean.

When I was submerged, I called out with a distressed cry, "Someone please save me!" At that moment, You came. Knowing You to be my shore, the seed of hope sprouted within me.

You, Hari, are merciful. You have surely found me to be the most deserving subject of Your mercy. I will not leave You anymore; this wretched Bhaktivinoda has found the object of Your compassion, O merciful one!

IDX.first_line: hari he
IDX.search: hari he anya asa nahi jara tava pada padma tara chadibara jogya nahi haya tava padasraye natha kare sei dinapata tava pade tahara abhaya stanyapayi sisujane mata chade krodhamane sisu tabu nahi chade maya jehetu tahara ara e jivana dharibara mata vina nahika upaya
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari he!
anya āśā nāhi jā’ra, tava pāda⋅padma tā’ra,
chāḍibāra jogya nāhi haya
tava padāśraye nātha, kare sei dinapāta,
tava pade tāhāra abhaya

stanyapāyī śiśujane, mātā chāḍe krodhamane,
śiśu tabu nāhi chāḍe māya
jehetu tāhāra āra, e jīvana dharibāra,
mātā vinā nāhika upāya

e bhaktivinoda kaya, tumi chāḍa dayāmaya,
dekhiyā āmāra doṣagaṇa
āmi ta’ chāḍite nāri, tomā vinā nāhi pāri,
kakhana dharite e jīvana
IDX.en_translation:
Hari! He who has no other hope, he is not worthy of leaving Your lotus feet. O Lord, in the shelter of Your feet, he passes his days; in Your feet, he finds fearlessness.

To a suckling child, the mother leaves him in anger, but the child still does not leave the mother. Because for him, to sustain this life, there is no other way without the mother.

This Bhaktivinoda says, You may leave me, O merciful one, seeing my faults. But I cannot leave You; without You, I cannot ever sustain this life.

IDX.first_line: hari he
IDX.search: hari he tava pada pankajini jivamrta sancarini atibhagye jiva taha paya se amrta pana kari mugdha haya tahe hari ara taha chadite na caya nivista hoiya taya anya sthane nahi jaya anya rasa tuccha kari mane madhupurna padmasthita madhuvrata kadacita nahi caya iksudanda pane
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari he!
tava pada⋅paṅkajinī, jīvāmṛta⋅sañcāriṇī,
atibhāgye jīva tāhā pāya
se⋅amṛta pāna kari’, mugdha haya tāhe hari,
āra tāhā chāḍite nā cāya

niviṣṭa hoiyā tāya, anya sthāne nāhi jāya,
anya rasa tuccha kari’ māne
madhupūrṇa padmasthita, madhuvrata kadācita,
nāhi cāya ikṣudaṇḍa⋅pāne

e bhaktivinoda kabe, se⋅paṅkajasthita ha’be,
nāhi jā’be saṁsārābhimukhe
bhaktakṛpā, bhaktibala, ei duiṭī susambala,
pāile se⋅sthiti ghaṭe sukhe
IDX.en_translation:
O Hari! A living being obtains Your lotus feet, which distribute the nectar of life, only by great fortune. Drinking that nectar, the soul becomes enchanted and never wishes to leave.

Becoming absorbed there, one never goes anywhere else, considering all other tastes as insignificant. A bee residing in a lotus full of honey never desires sugarcane.

When will Bhaktivinoda become situated in those lotus feet and never again turn toward the material world? That state occurs happily when the mercy of devotees and the strength of devotion are received.

IDX.first_line: hari he
IDX.search: hari he bhramite samsara vane kabhu daiva saŋghatane konamate kona bhagyavan tava pada uddesiya thake krtanjali hana ekabara ohe bhagavan seiksane tara jata amangala haya hata sumangala haya pusta ati ara nahi ksaya haya krame tara subhodaya tare deya sarvottama gati
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari he!
bhramite saṁsāra⋅vane, kabhu daiva⋅saŋghaṭane,
konamate kona bhāgyavān
tava pada uddeśiyā, thāke kṛtāñjali hañā,
ekabāra ohe bhagavān

seikṣaṇe tā’ra jata, amaṅgala haya hata,
sumaṅgala haya puṣṭa ati
āra nāhi kṣaya haya, krame tā’ra śubhodaya,
tā’re deya sarvottama⋅gati

emana dayālu tumi, emana durbhāgā āmi,
kabhu nā karinu paraṇāma
tava pāda⋅padma⋅prati, nā jāne e duṣṭamati,
bhaktivinodera pariṇāma
IDX.en_translation:
O Hari! While wandering in the forest of material existence, by some divine arrangement, some fortunate soul, somehow aiming for Your feet, stands with folded hands just once, O Bhagavān!

At that very moment, all his misfortunes are destroyed, and great auspiciousness becomes extremely strong. There is no more loss for him; gradually his good fortune appears, granting him the supreme destination.

You are so merciful, and I am so unfortunate; I never offered my obeisances to Your lotus feet. This wicked mind does not know what will become of Bhaktivinoda.

IDX.first_line: hari he
IDX.search: hari he tomara caranapadma anuraga sudhasadma sagarasikara jadi paya kona bhagyavan jane kona karya saŋghatane tara saba duhkha dure jaya se sudha samudrakana samsaragni nirvapana ksaneke kariya phele tara parama nirvrti diya tomara carane lana deya tabe ananda apara
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari he!
tomāra caraṇapadma, anurāga⋅sudhāsadma⋅,
sāgaraśīkara jadi pāya
kona bhāgyavān jane, kona kārya⋅saŋghaṭane,
tā’ra saba duḥkha dūre jāya

se⋅sudhā⋅samudrakaṇa, saṁsārāgni⋅nirvāpaṇa,
kṣaṇeke kariyā phele tā’ra
parama⋅nirvṛti diyā, tomāra caraṇe lañā,
deya tabe ānanda apāra

e bhaktivinoda kā̐de, paḍiyā saṁsāra⋅phā̐de,
bale, nāhi kona bhāgya mora
e ghaṭanā nā ghaṭila, āmāra janama gela,
vṛthā rainu ha’ye ātmabhora
IDX.en_translation:
Hari! Your lotus feet, a dwelling of the nectar of divine love, if a fortunate soul obtains a drop from that ocean, through some course of action, all his sorrows vanish.

That particle of the ocean of nectar, extinguishes the fire of material existence, it does so in an instant. Giving supreme peace, taking him to Your feet, it then bestows boundless joy.

This Bhaktivinoda cries, having fallen into the trap of material existence, says, "I have no fortune. This event of receiving that nectar did not occur, my life has passed, I remained uselessly engrossed in myself."

IDX.first_line: hari he
IDX.search: hari he tavanghri kamaladvaya vilasa vikramamaya paravara jagat vyapiya sarvaksana vartamana bhaktaklesa avasana lagi sada prastuta hoiya jagatera sei dhana ami jagamadhya jana atoeva sama adhikara ami kiba bhagyahina sadhane vancita dina ki kaja jivane ara chara
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari he!
tavāṅghri kamaladvaya, vilāsa⋅vikramamaya,
parāvara jagat vyāpiyā
sarvakṣaṇa vartamāna, bhaktakleśa⋅avasāna,
lāgi’ sadā prastuta hoiyā

jagatera sei dhana, āmi jagamadhya jana,
atoeva sama adhikāra
āmi kibā bhāgyahīna, sādhane vañcita dīna,
ki kāja jīvane āra chāra

kṛpā vinā nāhi gati, e bhaktivinoda ati,
dainya kari’ bale prabhu⋅pāya
kabe tava kṛpā pe’ye, uṭhiba sabale dhe’ye,
heriba se padayuga hāya
IDX.en_translation:
Hari! Your two lotus feet, full of joyous might, pervading the upper and lower worlds, Are always present, ever ready to end the devotees' suffering.

That treasure of the world, I am a person within the world, therefore I have equal right. How unfortunate am I, deprived and wretched in spiritual practice, what good is this worthless life then?

Without Your mercy, there is no way to progress. This Bhaktivinoda says with great humility at the feet of the Lord: When, receiving Your mercy, will I rise with full strength and rush to see those two feet?

IDX.first_line: hari he
IDX.search: hari he ami sei dustamati na dekhiya anya gati tava pade layechi sarana janilama ami natha tumi prabhu jagannatha ami tava nitya parijana seidina kabe habe aikantikabhave jabe nityadasa bhava laye ami manorathantara jata nihsesa kariya svatah seviba amara nityasvami
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari he!
āmi sei duṣṭamati, nā dekhiyā anya gati,
tava pade la’yechi śaraṇa
jānilāma āmi nātha, tumi prabhu jagannātha,
āmi tava nitya parijana

seidina kabe ha’be, aikāntikabhāve jabe,
nityadāsa⋅bhāva la’ye āmi
manorathāntara jata, niḥśeṣa kariyā svataḥ,
seviba āmāra nityasvāmī

nirantara sevā⋅mati, bahibe cittete satī,
praśānta hoibe ātmā mora
e bhaktivinoda bale, kṛṣṇasevā⋅kutūhale,
ciradina thāki jena bhora
IDX.en_translation:
O Hari! I am that wicked-minded person who, seeing no other recourse, has taken shelter at Your feet. I have come to know, O Lord, that You are the Lord of the Universe and I am Your eternal associate.

When will that day come when, adopting the mood of an eternal servant, I will automatically end all other desires and serve my eternal Master?

May constant service-mindedness flow purely in my heart, and may my soul become peaceful. Bhaktivinoda says: May I remain absorbed forever in the eagerness of serving Kṛṣṇa.

IDX.first_line: hari he
IDX.search: hari he ami aparadhi jana sada dandya durlaksana sahasra sahasra dose dosi bhima bhavarnavodare patita visama ghore gatihina gati abhilasi hari tava padadvaye sarana loinu bhaye krpa kari kara atmasatha tomara pratijna ei sarana loibe jei tumi tara raksakarta natha
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari he!
āmi aparādhi⋅jana, sadā daṇḍya, durlakṣaṇa,
sahasra sahasra doṣe doṣī
bhīma bhavārṇavodare, patita viṣama ghore,
gatihīna gati⋅abhilāṣī

hari! tava pādadvaye, śaraṇa loinu bhaye,
kṛpā kari’ kara ātmasātha
tomāra pratijñā ei, śaraṇa loibe jei,
tumi tā’ra rakṣākartā nātha

pratijñāte kari’ bhara, o mādhava prāṇeśvara!
śaraṇa loila ei dāsa
e bhaktivinoda gāya, tomāra se rāṅgāpāya,
deha dāse sevāya vilāsa
IDX.en_translation:
O Hari! I am a sinful person, always punishable, possessing bad qualities, guilty of thousands and thousands of faults. In the terrible ocean of material existence, I have fallen into a dreadful, difficult situation, without shelter, yet desiring to find shelter.

Hari! At Your two feet, I take shelter with fear; please mercifully accept me as Your own. This is Your promise: whoever takes Your shelter, You are their protector, my Lord.

Relying on that promise, O Mādhava, Lord of my life! This servant has taken shelter. Bhaktivinoda sings: at Your reddish lotus feet, please grant this servant the joy of service.

IDX.first_line: hari he
IDX.search: hari he avivekarupa ghana tahe dik acchadana haila tate andhakara ghora tahe duhkha vrsti haya dekhi caridike bhaya pathabhrama hoiyache mora nija aviveka dose padi durdinera rose prana jaya samsara kantare pathapradarsaka nai e durdaive mara jai daki tai acyuta tomare
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari he!
avivekarūpa ghana, tāhe dik ācchādana,
haila tā’te andhakāra ghora
tāhe duḥkha⋅vṛṣṭi haya, dekhi’ cāridike bhaya,
pathabhrama hoiyāche mora

nija aviveka⋅doṣe, paḍi’ durdinera roṣe,
prāṇa jāya saṁsāra⋅kāntāre
pathapradarśaka nāi, e durdaive mārā jāi,
ḍāki tāi, acyuta, tomāre

ekabāra kṛpādṛṣṭi, kara āmā⋅prati vṛṣṭi,
tabe mora ghucibe durdina
viveka sabala ha’be, e bhaktivinode tabe,
dekhāibe patha samīcīna
IDX.en_translation:
O Hari! Clouds of ignorance cover the directions, creating dense darkness. Misery rains down, and I see fear on all sides. I have lost my way.

By my own lack of discrimination and by the force of misfortune, my life is being lost in the forest of the material world. There is no guide, and in this great calamity, I am dying. Therefore, I call out to You, Acyuta!

Please bestow just one merciful glance upon me. Then my days of misfortune will end. My discrimination will become strong, and then Bhaktivinoda will be shown the proper path.

IDX.first_line: hari he
IDX.search: hari he agre eka nivedana kari madhu nisudana suna krpa kariya amaya nirarthaka katha naya nigudharthamaya haya hrdaya hoite bahiraya ati apakrsta ami parama dayalu tumi tava daya mora adhikara je jata patita haya tava daya tata taya tate ami supatra dayara
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari he!
agre eka nivedana, kari madhu⋅nisūdana,
śuna kṛpā kariyā āmāya
nirarthaka kathā naya, nigūḍhārthamaya haya,
hṛdaya hoite bāhirāya

ati apakṛṣṭa āmi, parama dayālu tumi,
tava dayā mora adhikāra
je jata patita haya, tava dayā tata tāya,
tā’te āmi supātra dayāra

more jadi upekṣibe, dayā⋅pātra kothā pā’be,
‘dayāmaya’⋅nāmaṭī ghucā’be
e bhaktivinoda kaya, dayā kara dayāmaya,
jaśaḥkīrti ciradina pā’be
IDX.en_translation:
O Hari! First, I make a humble submission, O Madhu-nisūdana: Please listen mercifully to me. These are not meaningless words; they are full of profound meaning, and they emanate from my heart.

I am most fallen, and You are supremely merciful; Your mercy is my right. The more fallen one is, the more Your mercy is upon them; therefore, I am a fit recipient for Your mercy.

If You neglect me, where will You find a recipient of mercy? You will lose Your name, ‘Dayāmaya’. Bhaktivinoda says: bestow mercy, O Dayāmaya, and You will receive eternal fame.

IDX.first_line: hari he
IDX.search: hari he toma chadi ami kabhu sanatha na hoi prabhu prabhuhina dasa nirasraya amake na nile satha kaiche tumi habe natha dayaniya ke tomara haya amadera e sambandha vidhikrta sunirbandha savidhi tomara gunadhama atoeva nivedana suna he madhumathana chada chadi nahe kona kama
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari he!
tomā chāḍi’ āmi kabhu, sanātha nā hoi prabhu,
prabhuhīna dāsa nirāśraya
āmāke nā nile sātha, kaiche tumi ha’be nātha,
dayanīya ke tomāra haya

āmādera e sambandha, vidhikṛta sunirbandha,
savidhi tomāra guṇadhāma
atoeva nivedana, śuna he madhumathana,
chāḍā⋅chāḍi nahe kona kāma

e bhaktivinoda gāya, rākha more tava pāya,
pāla’ more nā chāḍa kakhana
jabe mama pāo doṣa, kariyā ucita roṣa,
daṇḍa diyā deo śrī⋅caraṇa
IDX.en_translation:
O Hari! Leaving You, I will never be protected; a servant without a Master is shelterless. If You do not take me along, how will You be the Protector? Who else is there for You to show mercy to?

This relationship of ours is ordained and firmly established by Your divine nature. Therefore, please hear my submission, O Slayer of Madhu: separation is not desired at all.

Bhaktivinoda sings: keep me at Your feet, nourish me, and never abandon me. When You find my faults, with appropriate anger, give me punishment and bestow Your divine feet.

IDX.first_line: hari he
IDX.search: hari he stri purusa dehagata varna adi dharma jata tahe punah dehagata bheda sattvarajastamoguna asrayete bheda punah eirupa sahasra prabheda je kona sarire thaki je kona avastha rakhi se saba ekhana tava pa ya sapilama pranesvara mama bali atahpara ara kichu na rahila daya
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari he!
strī⋅puruṣa⋅dehagata, varṇa⋅ādi dharma jata,
tāhe punaḥ dehagata bheda
sattvarajastamoguṇa, āśrayete bheda punaḥ,
eirūpa sahasra prabheda

je⋅kona śarīre thāki, je⋅kona avasthā rākhi,
se⋅saba ekhana tava pā⋅ya
sa̐pilāma, prāṇeśvara! mama bali’ ataḥpara,
āra kichu nā rahila dāya

tumi, prabhu! rākha māra, saba tava adhikāra,
āchi āmi tomāra kiṅkara
e bhaktivinoda bale, tava dāsya kautūhale,
thāki jena sadā sevāpara
IDX.en_translation:
O Hari! All the duties related to male and female bodies, social orders, and so forth; the distinctions based on the body itself; and further differences due to the qualities of goodness, passion, and ignorance—in this way, there are thousands of divisions.

Whatever body I may inhabit, whatever state I may be in, all of that I now surrender at Your feet, O Prāṇeśvara! From now on, nothing remains for me to call "mine" or to be my responsibility.

You, my Lord! You may protect or destroy; everything is under Your control. I am Your servant. Bhaktivinoda says: out of eagerness for Your service, may I always remain engaged in serving You.

IDX.first_line: hari he
IDX.search: hari he vedavidhi anusare karma kari e samsare punah punah jiva janma paya purvakrta karmaphale tomara va icchabale janma jadi labhi punaraya tabe eka katha mama suna he purusottama tava dasa sangijana ghare kita janma jadi haya tahateo dayamaya rahiba he santusta antare
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari he!
vedavidhi⋅anusāre, karma kari’ e saṁsāre,
punaḥ punaḥ jīva janma pāya
pūrvakṛta⋅karmaphale, tomāra vā icchābale,
janma jadi labhi punarāya

tabe eka kathā mama, śuna he puruṣottama,
tava dāsa⋅saṅgijana⋅ghare
kīṭa⋅janma jadi haya, tāhāteo dayāmaya,
rahiba he santuṣṭa⋅antare

tava dāsa⋅saṅgahīna, je⋅gṛhastha arvācīna,
tā’ra gṛhe caturmukha⋅bhūti
nā hou kakhana, hari! karadvaya joḍa kari’,
kare bhaktivinoda minati
IDX.en_translation:
Hari! According to Vedic injunctions, performing actions in this material world, the soul repeatedly takes birth. By the results of past deeds, or by Your will, if I take birth again,

Then I have one request, please listen, O Supreme Person: Even if I am born as an insect in the home of Your devotee companions, O Merciful Lord, I will remain content in my heart.

But to be devoid of the company of Your devotees, in the home of such an ignorant householder- may I never attain the opulence of Brahmā, O Hari! With folded hands, Bhaktivinoda offers this prayer.

IDX.first_line: hari he
IDX.search: hari he tomara je suddhabhakta tomate se anurakta bhukti mukti tuccha kari jane bareka dekhite tava cidakara sri vaibhava trna bali anya sukha mane se saba bhaktera sange lila kara nanarange viraha sahite nahi para krpa kari akincane dekhao mahatmagane sadhu vina gati nahi ara
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari he!
tomāra je śuddhabhakta, tomāte se anurakta,
bhukti⋅mukti tuccha kari’ jāne
bāreka dekhite tava, cidākāra⋅śrī⋅vaibhava,
tṛṇa bali’ anya sukha māne

se saba bhaktera saṅge, līlā kara nānāraṅge,
viraha sahite nāhi pāra
kṛpā kari’ akiñcane, dekhāo mahātmagaṇe,
sādhu vinā gati nāhi āra

se⋅bhaktacaraṇa⋅dhana, kabe pā’ba daraśana,
śodhiba āmāra duṣṭa mana
e bhaktivinoda bhaṇe, kṛpā ha’be jatakṣaṇe,
mahātmāra ha’be daraśana
IDX.en_translation:
O Hari! Your pure devotee, being deeply attached to You, considers material enjoyment and liberation to be insignificant. Having seen Your spiritual opulence even once, they consider all other pleasures as mere grass.

You perform various pastimes with such devotees and cannot tolerate separation from them. Out of mercy, please show such great souls to this destitute person; without the saintly, there is no other path.

When will I behold the wealth of the lotus feet of those devotees and thus purify my wicked mind? Bhaktivinoda says: only when mercy descends will I have the vision of those great souls.

IDX.first_line: hari he
IDX.search: hari he suna he madhumathana mama eka vijnapana visesa kariya bali ami tomara sesatva mama svakiya vaibhavottama ami dasa tumi mora svami se vibhava bahirbhuta haite haile he acyuta ksanamatra sahite na pari deha prana sukha asa atmaprati bhalabasa sarvatyaga karite vicari
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari he!
śuna he madhumathana! mama eka vijñāpana,
viśeṣa kariyā bali āmi
tomāra śeṣatva mama, svakīya vaibhavottama,
āmi dāsa, tumi mora svāmī

se⋅vibhava⋅bahirbhūta, haite haile, he acyuta,
kṣaṇamātra sahite nā pāri
deha⋅prāṇa⋅sukha āśā, ātmaprati bhālabāsā,
sarvatyāga karite vicāri

e saba jāuka nāśa, śatabāra śrīnivāsa,
tabu thāku dāsatva tomāra
e bhaktivinoda kaya, kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa jīva haya,
dāsya vinā kibā āche āra
IDX.en_translation:
O Hari! Listen, O Madhumathana! I have one submission, which I especially say: I am subordinate to You, a part of Your own supreme opulence. I am Your servant, and You are my master.

O Acyuta, if I were to be excluded from that opulence, I could not tolerate it for even a moment. I resolve to give up everything: the hope for bodily and vital pleasures, and love for myself.

Let all these perish a hundred times, O Śrīnivāsa, but let my servitude to You remain. Bhaktivinoda says: the soul is a servant of Kṛṣṇa; what else is there without servitude?

IDX.first_line: hari he
IDX.search: hari he ami narapasupraya acaravihina taya anadi ananta suvistara atikaste pariharya sahajete anivarya asubhera aspada abara tumi ta dayara sindhu tumi ta jagadbandhu asima vatsalya payonidhi tava gunagana smari bhavabandha cheda kari nirbhika hoiba niravadhi
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari he!
āmi narapaśuprāya, ācāravihīna tāya,
anādi ananta suvistāra
atikaṣṭe parihārya, sahajete anivārya,
aśubhera āspada ābāra

tumi ta’ dayāra sindhu, tumi ta’ jagadbandhu,
asīma vātsalya⋅payonidhi
tava guṇagaṇa smari’, bhavabandha cheda kari’,
nirbhīka hoiba niravadhi

ei icchā kari’ mane, śrī⋅yāmuna⋅caraṇe,
gāya bhaktivinoda ekhana
jāmuna⋅vipina⋅vidhu, śrī⋅caraṇāmbuja⋅sīdhu,
tā’ra śire karuna arpaṇa
IDX.en_translation:
O Hari! I am like a human animal, devoid of proper conduct, trapped in an endless, beginningless expanse of material existence. Misfortune is exceedingly difficult to avoid, yet easily becomes inevitable, and I am the abode of inauspiciousness.

But You are indeed an ocean of mercy, You are indeed the friend of the universe, an unlimited ocean of parental affection. Remembering Your many qualities and severing the bonds of material existence, I shall become fearless forever.

With this desire in my heart, at the lotus feet of Śrī Yāmuna, Bhaktivinoda now sings: O moon of the Yamunā forest, may the nectar of Your lotus feet be offered upon my head.

IDX.first_line: hari he
IDX.search: hari he tumi jagatera pita tumi jagatera mata dayita tanaya hari tumi tumi suhrnmitra guru tumi gati kalpataru tvadiya sambandhamatra ami tava bhrtya parijana gatiprarthi akincana prapanna tomara sri carane tava sattva tava dhana tomara palita jana amara mamata tava jane
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari he!
tumi jagatera pitā, tumi jagatera mātā,
dayita, tanaya, hari tumi
tumi suhṛnmitra guru, tumi gati kalpataru,
tvadīya sambandhamātra āmi

tava bhṛtya, parijana, gatiprārthī, akiñcana,
prapanna tomāra śrī⋅caraṇe
tava sattva, tava dhana, tomāra pālita jana,
āmāra mamatā tava jane

e bhaktivinoda kaya, ahaṁtā⋅mamatā naya,
śrī⋅kṛṣṇasambandha⋅abhimāne
sevāra sambandha dhari’, ahaṁtā⋅mamatā kari’,
taditara prākṛta vidhāne
IDX.en_translation:
O Hari! You are the father of the world, and You are the mother of the world. You are the beloved, the son, and God Yourself. You are the sincere friend, the teacher, the shelter, and the wish-fulfilling tree. My only connection is with You.

I am Your servant, Your associate, seeking shelter, destitute, and surrendered at Your lotus feet. I am Your property, Your wealth, maintained by You; my affection is for Your people.

Thus Bhaktivinoda says: My sense of 'I' and 'mine' is not worldly but is derived from my relationship with Śrī Kṛṣṇa. Embracing the relationship of service, I cultivate this identity in a spiritual manner, unlike the material way.

IDX.first_line: hari he
IDX.search: hari he ami ta cancalamati amaryada ksudra ati asuya prasava sada mora papistha krtaghna mani nrsamsa vancane jnani kamavase thaki sada ghora e hena durjana haye e duhkha jaladhi vaye caritechi samsara sagare kemane e bhavamvudhi para haye niravadhi tava padaseva mile more
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hari he!
āmi ta’ cañcalamati, amaryāda, kṣudra ati,
asūyā⋅prasava sadā mora
pāpiṣṭha, kṛtaghna, mānī, nṛśaṁsa, vañcane jñānī,
kāmavaśe thāki’ sadā ghora

e hena durjana ha’ye, e duḥkha⋅jaladhi va’ye,
caritechi saṁsāra⋅sāgare
kemane e bhavāmvudhi, pāra ha’ye niravadhi,
tava pādasevā mile more

tomāra karuṇā pāi, tabe ta’ tariyā jāi,
āmi ei duranta sāgara
tumi prabhu, śrī⋅caraṇe, rākha dāse dhūlisane,
nahe bhaktivinoda kātara
IDX.en_translation:
O Hari! Indeed, I am fickle-minded, disrespectful, very insignificant, and my heart always gives birth to envy. I am sinful, ungrateful, proud, cruel, expert in deception, and always deeply immersed in lust.

Having become such a wicked person, bearing this ocean of sorrow, I am wandering in the ocean of material existence. How can I cross this ocean of material existence and incessantly attain service to Your lotus feet?

If I receive Your mercy, then indeed I can cross this formidable ocean. You, my Lord, please keep this servant with the dust at Your beautiful feet; otherwise Bhaktivinoda will be distressed.

IDX.first_line: hari he dayala mora jaya radhanatha
IDX.search: hari he dayala mora jaya radhanatha barabara ei bara laha nija satha bahu joni bhrami natha loinu sarana nijagune krpa kara adhamatarana
IDX.verses:
hari he dayāla mora jaya rādhānātha
bārabāra ei bāra laha nija sātha

bahu joni bhrami’ nātha, loinu śaraṇa
nija-guṇe kṛpā kara adhamatāraṇa

jagata⋅kāraṇa tumi jagata⋅jīvana
tomā chāḍā kā’ra nahi, he rādhā-ramaṇa

bhuvanamaṅgala tumi bhuvanera pati
tumi upekṣile nātha ki hoibe gati

bhāviyā dekhinu ei jagata⋅mājhāre
tomā vinā keha nāhi e dāse uddhāre
IDX.en_translation:
O Lord Hari, my merciful one! All glories to the Lord of Rādhā! This time, please take me as Your own companion.

Having wandered through many species, O Lord, I have finally taken Your shelter. By Your own mercy, please be kind, O deliverer of the fallen.

You are the cause of the universe, and You are the life of the universe. Without You, I belong to no one, O enjoyer of Rādhā.

You are the welfare of all worlds, and You are their Lord. If You neglect me, O Lord, what will be my fate?

Having thought it over, I have realized that in this world, no one can deliver this servant but You.

IDX.first_line: hari he dayala mora jaya radhanathabarabara ei bara laha nija satha

bhuvanamaṅgala tumi bhuvanera patitumi upekṣile nātha ki hoibe gati

Having wandered through many species, O Lord, I have finally taken Your shelter. By Your own mercy, please be kind, O deliverer of the fallen.

You are the cause of the universe, and You are the life of the universe. Without You, I belong to no one, O enjoyer of Rādhā.

You are the welfare of all worlds, and You are their Lord. If You neglect me, O Lord, what will be my fate?

Having thought it over, I have realized that in this world, no one can deliver this servant but You.

IDX.first_line: harinama tuya aneka svarupa
IDX.search: harinama tuya aneka svarupa jasoda nandana ananda vardhana nandatanaya rasakupa putana ghatana trnavartahana sakata bhanjana gopala murali vadana agha baka mardana govardhanadhari rakhala
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
harināma tuyā aneka svarūpa
jaśodā⋅nandana, ānanda⋅vardhana,
nandatanaya rasakūpa

pūtanā⋅ghātana, tṛṇāvartahana,
śakaṭa⋅bhañjana gopāla
muralī⋅vadana, agha⋅baka⋅mardana,
govardhanadhārī rākhāla

keśi⋅mardana, brahma⋅vimohana,
surapati⋅darpa⋅vināśī
ariṣṭa⋅pātana, gopī⋅vimohana,
jāmunapulina⋅vilāsī

rādhikā⋅rañjana, rāsa⋅rasāyana,
rādhā⋅kuṇḍa⋅kuñjavihārī
rāma⋅kṛṣṇa⋅hari, mādhava⋅narahari,
matsyādi⋅gaṇe avatari

govinda⋅vāmana, śrīmadhusūdana,
jādavacandra⋅vanamālī
kālīya⋅śātana, gokula⋅rañjana,
rādhābhajana⋅sukhaśālī

ityādika nāma, svarūpe prakāma,
bāḍuka mora rati rāge
rūpa⋅svarūpa⋅pada, jāni’ nija⋅sampada,
bhaktivinoda dhari’ māge
IDX.en_translation:
Your Holy Name has many manifestations: the son of Yaśodā, the bestower of joy, the son of Nanda, and a wellspring of divine mellows.

He is the killer of Pūtanā, the killer of Tṛṇāvarta, the breaker of the cart demon, and the protector of the cows. With His flute at His lips, He is the subduer of Agha and Baka and the cowherd boy who lifted Govardhana Hill.

He is the subduer of Keśī, the bewilderer of Brahmā, and the destroyer of Indra's pride. He is the killer of Ariṣṭa, the enchanter of the gopīs, and the enjoyer of pastimes on the banks of the Yamunā.

He is the delighter of Rādhā, the elixir of the rāsa dance, and the enjoyer of pastimes in the groves of Rādhā-kuṇḍa. He is Rāma, Kṛṣṇa, and Hari; He is Mādhava and Lord Narasiṁha, incarnating in forms like the fish and others.

He is Govinda, Vāmana, and the slayer of Madhu; He is the moon of the Yadu dynasty and the wearer of a forest garland. He is the subduer of Kāliya and the delighter of Gokula, and He finds happiness in the worship of Rādhā.

May my deep affection and attachment increase for all these Names, which are fully manifested in Their true forms. Bhaktivinoda prays, holding the lotus feet of Rūpa Gosvāmī and Svarūpa Dāmodara as his true treasure.

IDX.first_line: harir drstva gosthe mukura gatam atmanam atulam
IDX.search: harir drstva gosthe mukura gatam atmanam atulam sva madhuryam radha priyatara sakhivaptum abhitah aho gaude jatah prabhur apara gauraika tanu bhak saci sunuh kim me nayana saranim jasyati punah puri devasyantah pranaya madhuna snana madhuro muhur govindodyad visada paricaryarcita padah svarupasya pranarbuda kamala nirajita mukhah saci sunuh kim me nayana saranim jasyati punah
IDX.author: śrīla raghunāthadāsa gosvāmī
IDX.verses:
harir dṛṣṭvā goṣṭhe mukura⋅gatam ātmānam atulaṁ
sva⋅mādhuryaṁ rādhā⋅priyatara⋅sakhīvāptum abhitaḥ
aho gauḍe jātaḥ prabhur apara⋅gauraika⋅tanu⋅bhāk
śacī⋅sūnuḥ kiṁ me nayana⋅śaraṇīṁ jāsyati punaḥ

purī⋅devasyāntaḥ praṇaya⋅madhunā snāna⋅madhuro
muhur govindodyad⋅viśada⋅paricaryārcita⋅padaḥ
svarūpasya prāṇārbuda⋅kamala⋅nīrājita⋅mukhaḥ
śacī⋅sūnuḥ kiṁ me nayana⋅śaraṇīṁ jāsyati punaḥ

dadhānaḥ kaupīnaṁ tad⋅upari bahir⋅vastram aruṇaṁ
prakāṇḍo hemādri dyutibhir abhitaḥ sevita⋅tanuḥ
mudā gāyann uccair nija⋅madhura⋅nāmāvalim asau
śacī⋅sūnuḥ kiṁ me nayana⋅śaraṇīṁ jāsyati punaḥ

anāvedyāṁ pūrvair api muni⋅gaṇair bhakti⋅nipuṇaiḥ
śruter gūḍhāṁ premojvala⋅rasa⋅phalāṁ bhakti⋅latikām
kṛpālus tāṁ gauḍe prabhur ati⋅kṛpābhiḥ prakaṭayan
śacī⋅sūnuḥ kiṁ me nayana⋅śaraṇīṁ jāsyati punaḥ

nijatve gauḍīyān jagati parigṛhya prabhur imān
hare⋅kṛṣṇety evaṁ gaṇana⋅vidhinā kīrtayata bhoḥ
iti prāyāṁ śikṣāṁ janaka iva tebhyaḥ paridiśan
śacī⋅sūnuḥ kiṁ me nayana⋅śaraṇīṁ jāsyati punaḥ

puraḥ paśyan nīlācala⋅patim uru⋅prema⋅nivahaiḥ
kṣaran netrāmbhobhiḥ snapita⋅nija⋅dīrghojvala⋅tanuḥ
sadā tiṣṭhan deśe praṇayi⋅garuḍa⋅stambha⋅carame
śacī⋅sūnuḥ kiṁ me nayana⋅śaraṇīṁ jāsyati punaḥ

mudā dantair daṣṭvā dyuti⋅vijita⋅bandhūkam adharaṁ
karaṁ kṛtvā vāmaṁ kaṭi⋅nihitam anyaṁ parilasan
samutthāpya premṇāgaṇita⋅pulako nṛtya⋅kutukī
śacī⋅sūnuḥ kiṁ me nayana⋅śaraṇīṁ jāsyati punaḥ

sarit⋅tīrārāme viraha⋅vidhuro gokula⋅vidhor
nadīm anyāṁ kurvan nayana⋅jala⋅dhārā⋅vitatibhiḥ
muhur mūrchāṁ gacchan mṛtakam iva viśvaṁ viracayan
śacī⋅sūnuḥ kiṁ me nayana⋅śaraṇīṁ jāsyati punaḥ

śacī⋅sūnor asyāṣṭakam idam abhīṣṭaṁ viracayat
sadā dainyodrekād ati⋅viśada⋅buddhiḥ paṭhati jaḥ
prakāmaṁ caitanyaḥ prabhur ati⋅kṛpāveśa⋅vivaśaḥ
pṛthu premāmbhodhau prathita⋅rasade majayati tama
IDX.en_translation:
Lord Hari, seeing His own incomparable self reflected in a mirror in Vraja and desiring to taste His own sweetness completely—just as Rādhā or Her dearmost companions do—took another golden form and was born in the land of Bengal. Will the Son of Mother Śacī ever again come before my eyes?

He is sweet like a bath in the honey of love within Advaita Ācārya's heart. His feet are repeatedly worshipped by Govinda Dāsa's excellent service. His face is offered ārati by Svarūpa Dāmodara's millions of life-breaths. Will the Son of Mother Śacī ever again come before my eyes?

Wearing a loincloth and over it an outer saffron cloth, His grand body, like a golden mountain, is completely served by its own effulgence. Joyfully and loudly singing His own sweet Holy Names, will the Son of Mother Śacī ever again come before my eyes?

That which was unknown even to previous sages—the creeper of devotion, hidden in the Vedas and bearing the fruit of pure, brilliant ecstatic love—the merciful Lord manifested that with great mercy in Bengal. Will the Son of Mother Śacī ever again come before my eyes?

The Lord accepted the devotees from Bengal as His own. Instructing them like a father, He said: "Chant the Names of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa according to a counting method, O devotees!" Will the Son of Mother Śacī ever again come before my eyes?

Seeing the Lord of Purī in front of Him with great streams of love, His tall and effulgent body is bathed by the flowing tears from His eyes. He always stands at the Garuḍa pillar in the temple. Will the Son of Mother Śacī ever again come before my eyes?

Joyfully biting His lower lip with His teeth and placing His left hand on His hip, He shines beautifully. With countless ecstatic shivers and eager to dance, He rises up in love. Will the Son of Mother Śacī ever again come before my eyes?

Overwhelmed by separation from the Moon of Gokula on the banks of a river, He creates another river with the incessant flow of His tears. Repeatedly falling into unconsciousness, He makes the entire world appear like a corpse. Will the Son of Mother Śacī ever again come before my eyes?

This eight-verse prayer of the Son of Mother Śacī bestows all desires. He who recites this with a very clear mind due to increasing humility—Lord Caitanya, being overwhelmed by intense mercy, will immerse him in the great ocean of love, which is famous for bestowing divine nectar.

IDX.first_line: harise bada harika namaprabhuse bada prabhuka namaantame nikala ye parinama
IDX.search: harise bada harika namaprabhuse bada prabhuka namaantame nikala ye parinama sumiro nama rupa vina dekhekaudi lage na damanamake badhe khinca ayengeakhira eka dina syama
IDX.verses:
harise baḍā harikā nāma,prabhuse baḍā prabhukā nāmaantame̐ nikalā ye pariṇāma

sumiro nāma rūpa vina dekhe,kauḍī lage na dāmanāmake bā̐dhe khiñca āyeṅge,ākhira eka dina śyāma

draupadīne jaba nāma pukārā,jhaṭa ā goe ghanaśyāmasāḍī khaiñcata hārā duḥśāsana,sāḍī baḍhāī śyāma

jala ḍūbata gajarāja pukāro,āye ādhe nāmanāmīko cintā rahatī hai,nāma na ho badanāma

jisa sāgarako lāṅgha sake nā,vinā pulake rāmakūda goe hanumāna usīko,leke harikā nāma

vo dila vāle ḍūba jāyeṅge,jiname̐ nahī̐ hai nāmavo patthara bhī tereṅge jina para,likhā rāmakā nāma
IDX.en_translation:
Lord Hari is great, but His Name is greater. The Lord is great, but the Lord’s Name is greater. In the end, this is the ultimate conclusion.

Remember the Name without seeing the Form. It costs not a single penny. Bound by the Name, He will be drawn to you. In the end, one day Lord Śyāma will come.

When Draupadī called out the Name, Lord Ghanaśyāma immediately came. Duḥśāsana was defeated while pulling her sari; Lord Śyāma extended the sari endlessly.

The elephant king Gajendra called out while drowning in the water. The Lord came even before half the Name was uttered. The one who bears the Name is worried that His Name should not be dishonored.

The ocean which Lord Rāma could not cross without a bridge—Hanumān leaped across that very same ocean by taking the Name of Lord Hari.

Those who have hearts but no Name of God within them will drown. But even stones will float if the Name of Lord Rāma is written upon them.

IDX.first_line: he govinda he gopala
IDX.search: he govinda he gopala kesava madhava dina dayala tumi parama dayala prabhu parama dayala kesava madhava dina dayala
IDX.author: śrī⋅jayadeva gosvāmī
IDX.verses:
he govinda he gopāla
keśava mādhava dīna⋅dayāla

tumi parama dayāla prabhu, parama dayāla
keśava mādhava dīna dayāla

pīta⋅vasana pari mayūrera śikha dhari
muralīra vāṇī⋅tule bole rādhā⋅nāma

tumi moder gopāla prabhu, moder gopāla
keśava mādhava dīna⋅dayāla

bhava⋅bhaya⋅bhañjana śrī madhusūdana
vipada⋅bhañjana tumi nārāyaṇa
IDX.en_translation:
O Govinda! O Gopāla! O Keśava! O Mādhava! O You who are compassionate to the fallen!

You are supremely merciful, my Lord, supremely merciful. O Keśava! O Mādhava! O You who are compassionate to the fallen!

Wearing yellow garments and holding a peacock feather, He plays His flute and utters the Name of Rādhā.

You are our Gopāla, my Lord, our Gopāla! O Keśava! O Mādhava! O You who are compassionate to the fallen!

He is the destroyer of the fear of material existence, Lord Madhusūdana. You are the dispeller of all dangers, Lord Nārāyaṇa.

jāgo uṭho mohana naina kholo
dvāre khaḍe gvāla bulā rahe hai̐,
govinda dāmodara mādhaveti

vidyānurāgī nija pustakomme̐,
arthānurāgī dhana sañcayomme̐
ye hī nirālī dhvani gā rahe hai̐,
govinda dāmodara mādhaveti

le ke karomme̐ dohani anokhī,
gau dugdha kāḍhe avalā navelī
gau dugdha dhārā saṅga gā rahī hai,
govinda dāmodara mādhaveti

jāge pujārī hari mandiromme̐,
jāke jagāve̐ hariko sabere
he kṣīrasindhu ava netra kholo,
govinda dāmodara mādhaveti

soyā kisīkā suta pālaneme̐,
ḍorī karo se jaba kheñcatī hai
ho prema magnā usane pukārā,
govinda dāmodara mādhaveti

royā kisīkā suta pālaneme̐,
ho prema magnā usane pukārā
rovo na gāvo prabhu saṅga mere,
govinda dāmodara mādhaveti

koī navelī patiko jagāve,
prāṇeśa jāgo ava nīnda tyāgo
belā yahī hai hari gīta gāvo,
govinda dāmodara mādhaveti
IDX.en_translation:
O Kṛṣṇa! O Yādava! O friend! O Govinda! O Dāmodara! O Mādhava! When someone drops the churning rod into the yogurt, the thought of the butter-thief Kṛṣṇa immediately comes to mind.

With a choked voice she calls out: "O Govinda! O Dāmodara! O Mādhava!" O Kṛṣṇa! O Yādava! O friend! O Govinda! O Dāmodara! O Mādhava!

A woman cleaning her courtyard prays: "May Lord Govinda come and play in my house." While meditating, she sings these words: "O Govinda! O Dāmodara! O Mādhava!"

Mother Yaśodā awakens Lord Hari: "Wake up, get up, O Mohana! Open Your eyes! The cowherd boys are standing at the door calling for You: O Govinda! O Dāmodara! O Mādhava!"

The one devoted to knowledge in his books, and the one devoted to wealth while accumulating riches—they too are singing this unique sound: "O Govinda! O Dāmodara! O Mādhava!"

Holding a milking pot in her hands, a young woman milks the cow. Along with the stream of milk, she sings: "O Govinda! O Dāmodara! O Mādhava!"

The priests wake up in the Lord's temples and go to awaken Him in the morning: "O Ocean of milk! Now please open Your eyes! O Govinda! O Dāmodara! O Mādhava!"

When a mother pulls the string of her child's cradle, she calls out in love: "O Govinda! O Dāmodara! O Mādhava!"

When a child cries in the cradle, the mother calls out in love: "Do not cry! Sing with me, O Lord: O Govinda! O Dāmodara! O Mādhava!"

A young woman awakens her husband: "O Lord of my life! Wake up and abandon sleep! This is the time to sing the Lord's song: O Govinda! O Dāmodara! O Mādhava!"

IDX.first_line: hede he nagaravara suna he muralidhara
IDX.search: hede he nagaravara suna he muralidhara nivedana kari tuya paya carana nakhara mani jena cadera gathani bhala sobhe amara galaya sridama sudama sange jakhana vane jao range takhana ami duyare dadaye mane kari sange jai gurujanara bhaya pai akhi rahe tuya pane ceye
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hede he nāgaravara, śuna he muralīdhara,
nivedana kari tuyā pāya
caraṇa⋅nakhara⋅maṇi, jena cā̐dera gā̐thani,
bhāla śobhe āmāra galāya

śrīdāma⋅sudāma⋅saṅge, jakhana vane jāo raṅge,
takhana āmi duyāre dā̐ḍāye
mane kari saṅge jāi, gurujanāra bhaya pāi,
ā̐khi rahe tuyā pāne ceye

cāi navīna meghapāne, tuyā ba̐dhu paḍe mane!
elāile keśa nāhi bā̐dhi
randhana-śālāte jāi, tuyā vadhu guṇa gāi,
dhu̐yāra chalanā kari' kāndi

maṇi noo, māṇika noo, ā̐cale bā̐dhile roo,
phula noo je keśe kari veśe
nārī nā karita vidhi, tuyā hena guṇanidhi,
loiyā phiritāma deśe deśe

aguru candana hoitāma, tuyā aṅge mākhā roitāma,
ghāmiyā paḍitāma rāṅgā pāya
ki mora manera sādha, vāmana haye cā̐de hāta,
vidhi ki sādha pūrābe āmāya

narottama⋅dāse kaya, śuna ohe dayāmaya,
tumi āmāya nā chāḍiha dayā
je dina tomāra bhāve, āmāra parāṇa jābe,
sei dina dio padachāyā
IDX.en_translation:
O best among the charming lovers! Listen, O flute-player! I offer this plea at Your feet. The jewels of Your toenails are like a string of moons; how well they would adorn my neck!

When You go to the forest with joy in the company of Śrīdāma and Sudāma, I stand at my doorway. I think of going with You, but I fear my elders; thus my eyes remain fixed upon You.

I look at the new clouds and remember You, my beloved! My hair has come loose, yet I do not tie it. I go to the kitchen and sing Your glories, pretending that my tears are caused by the smoke.

You are not a jewel or a ruby that I can tie in the end of my cloth, nor are You a flower to adorn my hair. If fate had not made me a woman, I would take such a treasure of virtues as You and wander from country to country.

I wish I were aloe wood or sandalwood paste, so I could remain smeared on Your body. Sweating, I would fall upon Your reddish feet. What a desire is in my mind—like a dwarf reaching for the moon! Will fate ever fulfill this desire for me?

Narottama dāsa says: listen, O compassionate Lord! Please do not withdraw Your mercy from me. On the day my life departs, immersed in Your love, please grant me the shade of Your feet.

IDX.first_line: hemabha sankasa sucaru gatram
IDX.search: hemabha sankasa sucaru gatram satyasthitam puta caritra vantam karunya purnam hi sada praphullam vande prabhum bhakti vilasa tirtham samsara santapa vinasa daksam susnigdha hasya sphuta sundarasyam sad bhakti tattvanvita manju bhasam vande prabhum bhakti vilasa tirtham

IDX.verses:
hemābha⋅saṅkāśa⋅sucāru gātraṁ

sad⋅bhakti⋅tattvānvita⋅mañju⋅bhāṣaṁ
vande prabhuṁ bhakti⋅vilāsa⋅tīrtham

śrīdhāma⋅māyāpura⋅vaibhavāḍhyaṁ
śrī⋅vigrahārādhana⋅vīthi⋅vijñam
prakhyāta caitanya⋅maṭhābja⋅śobhaṁ
vande prabhuṁ bhakti⋅vilāsa⋅tīrtham

“caitanya⋅vāṇī⋅sadanaṁ” suramyaṁ
saṁsthāpitaṁ jena mahodayena
nirgūḍha⋅māhātmya⋅vibhūṣitaṁ taṁ
vande prabhuṁ bhakti⋅vilāsa⋅tīrtham

tāpa⋅trayonmukta suśānta⋅cittaṁ
dīptaṁ mahā⋅puruṣa⋅cihna⋅lagnam
lokottara⋅nyāsikula⋅pramodaṁ
vande prabhuṁ bhakti⋅vilāsa⋅tīrtham

ātyantika⋅kṣema⋅nikāya⋅nivāsaṁ
śrī⋅rūpa⋅vartmanugataṁ manojñam
śrī⋅gaura⋅līlāmṛta⋅pāna⋅mattaṁ
vande prabhuṁ bhakti⋅vilāsa⋅tīrtham

atyujjvala⋅prema⋅sudhābhiṣiktaṁ
gūḍha⋅vrajāraṇya⋅rahasya⋅prajñam
śrī⋅rādhikā⋅mādhava⋅nāma⋅mugdhaṁ
vande prabhuṁ bhakti⋅vilāsa⋅tīrtham

o viṣṇu⋅pādaṁ nitarāṁ śaraṇyaṁ
śābde pare cātiśaya⋅pravīṇam
nityaṁ suvandyaṁ mama jīvanaṁ taṁ
vande prabhuṁ bhakti⋅vilāsa⋅tīrtham
IDX.en_translation:
I offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Śrīla Bhakti Vilāsa Tīrtha Gosvāmī Mahārāja. His beautiful body is like molten gold, he is established in truth, and he possesses a pure character. He is full of compassion and is always joyful.

I offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Śrīla Tīrtha Mahārāja. He is expert in destroying the miseries of material existence. His beautiful face is adorned with a gentle smile, and his charming speech is filled with the true principles of devotional service.

I offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Śrīla Tīrtha Mahārāja. He enriched the glories of Śrīdhāma Māyāpura and was very knowledgeable in the path of Deity worship. He graced the renowned Caitanya Maṭha like a blooming lotus.

I offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Śrīla Tīrtha Mahārāja. He established the beautiful house of Lord Caitanya's teachings and was adorned with profound spiritual glories.

I offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Śrīla Tīrtha Mahārāja. His peaceful mind is free from the three-fold miseries, and he shines with the marks of a great personality. He is the joy of the transcendental sannyāsī community.

I offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Śrīla Tīrtha Mahārāja. He is the abode of ultimate welfare and follows the path of Śrīla Rūpa Gosvāmī. He is intoxicated by drinking the nectar of Lord Gaura's pastimes.

I offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Śrīla Tīrtha Mahārāja. He is bathed in the most brilliant nectar of divine love and is knowledgeable in the confidential mysteries of the Vṛndāvana forest. He is enchanted by the Names of Śrīmati Rādhikā and Lord Mādhava.

I offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Śrīla Tīrtha Mahārāja. He is supremely worthy of refuge and is exceedingly expert in the knowledge of transcendental sound. He is my very life.

IDX.first_line: hena kale kabe vilasa manjari
IDX.search: hena kale kabe vilasa manjari ananga manjari ara amare heriya ati krpa kari balibe vacana sara esa esa sakhi srilalita gane janiba tomare aja grhakatha chadi radha krsna bhaja tyajiya dharama laja
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
hena kāle kabe, vilāsa mañjarī,
anaṅga mañjarī āra
āmāre heriyā, ati kṛpā kari’,
balibe vacana sāra

esa, esa, sakhi! śrīlalitā⋅gaṇe,
jāniba tomāre āja
gṛha-kathā chāḍi’, rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa bhaja,
tyajiyā dharama⋅lāja

se madhura vāṇī, śuniyā e jana,
se du̐hāra śrī⋅caraṇe
āśraya loibe, du̐he kṛpā kari’,
loibe lalitā⋅sthāne

lalitā sundarī, sadaya hoiyā,
karibe āmāre dāsī
svakuñja⋅kuṭīre, dibena vasati,
jāni’ sevā⋅abhilāṣī
IDX.en_translation:
When will that time come, when Vilāsa Mañjarī and Anaṅga Mañjarī, seeing me, will with great mercy speak these essential words:

"Come, come, friend! We will acknowledge you today among Śrī Lalitā's associates. Leaving aside household concerns, worship Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa, abandoning worldly shame."

Hearing those sweet words, I will take shelter at their lotus feet, and both of them, with mercy, will take me to Lalitā's abode.

Beautiful Lalitā, becoming merciful, will make me her maidservant. In her own bower's cottage, she will give me residence, knowing my desire to serve.

IDX.first_line: indranilamani manjula varnahphullanipa kusumancita karnahkrsnalabhirakrsorasi harisundaro jayati kunjavihari
IDX.search: indranilamani manjula varnahphullanipa kusumancita karnahkrsnalabhirakrsorasi harisundaro jayati kunjavihari radhika vadanacandra cakorahsarvavallavavadhu dhrticaurahcarcari caturatancita cari caruto jayati kunjavihari

indranīlamaṇi⋅mañjula⋅varṇaḥphullanīpa⋅kusumāñcita⋅karṇaḥkṛṣṇalābhirakṛśorasi hārīsundaro jayati kuñjavihārī

rādhikā⋅vadanacandra⋅cakoraḥsarvavallavavadhū⋅dhṛticauraḥcarcarī⋅caturatāñcita⋅cārī⋅cāruto jayati kuñjavihārī

sarvataḥ prathita⋅kaulikaparva⋅dhvaṁsanena hṛta⋅vāsava⋅garvaḥgoṣṭha⋅rakṣaṇa⋅kṛte giridhārīlīlayā jayati kuñjavihārī

rāgamaṇḍala⋅vibhūṣita⋅vaṁśī⋅vibhrameṇa madanotsava⋅śaṁsīstūyamāna⋅caritaḥ śuka-śārī⋅śreṇibhirjayati kuñjavihārī

śātakumbha⋅rucihāri⋅dukūlaḥkeki⋅candraka⋅virājita⋅cūlaḥnavyayauvana⋅lasad vrajanārī⋅rañjano jayati kuñjavihārī

sthāsakīkṛta⋅sugandhi⋅paṭīraḥsvarṇakañci⋅pariśobhi⋅kaṭīraḥrādhikonnata⋅payodhara⋅vārī⋅kuñjaro jayati kuñjavihārī

gauradhātu⋅tilakojjvala⋅bhālaḥkeli⋅cañcalita⋅campaka⋅mālaḥadri⋅kandara⋅gṛheṣvabhisārīsubhruvāṁ jayati kuñjavihārī

vibhramoccala⋅dṛgañcala⋅nṛtya⋅kṣipta⋅gopa⋅lalanākhila⋅kṛtyaḥpremamatta⋅vṛṣabhānu⋅kumārī⋅nāgaro jayati kuñjavihārī

aṣṭakaṁ madhura⋅kuñjavihāri⋅krīḍayā paṭhati jaḥ kila hārisa prayāti vilasat parabhāgaṁtasya pāda⋅kamalārcana⋅rāgam
IDX.en_translation:
All glories to the beautiful Lord who sports in the groves! His complexion is like a sapphire, His ears are adorned with fully blossomed nīpa flowers, and He wears a garland of berries on His broad chest.

All glories to the charming Lord who sports in the groves! He is like the chakora bird for the moon-like face of Rādhā, the thief of the steadfastness of all the cowherd girls, and skillful in His dancing.

All glories to the Lord who sports in the groves! He is renowned for destroying the festival of Indra, thereby removing his pride, and for lifting a mountain to protect the cowherd community with His pastimes.

All glories to the Lord who sports in the groves! With the enchanting movements of His flute, He declares the festival of Cupid. His character is praised by flocks of parrots and mynas.

All glories to the Lord who sports in the groves! His lower garment surpasses the luster of pure gold, His topknot is adorned with peacock feathers, and He delights the young women of Vraja with His fresh youth.

All glories to the Lord who sports in the groves! He is smeared with fragrant sandalwood paste for decoration, His waist is resplendent with a golden belt, and He is like an elephant playing with the beauty of Rādhā.

All glories to the Lord who sports in the groves! His forehead shines brightly with sacred markings, His garland of jasmine flowers sways playfully, and He secretly meets the beautiful-browed maidens in mountain caves.

All glories to the Lord who sports in the groves! With the dance of His restless and charming sidelong glances, He disrupts all the duties of the cowherd women, and He is the lover of the love-intoxicated daughter of Vṛṣabhānu.

Whoever recites this charming eight-verse song about the sweet pastimes of the Lord who sports in the groves will attain great fortune and devotion to the worship of His lotus feet.

IDX.first_line: jadi te hari pada saroja sudha
IDX.search: jadi te hari pada saroja sudha rasa pana param hrdayam satatam parihrtya grham kalibhavamayam bhaja godruma kanana kunja vidhum dhana yauvana jivana rajya sukham na hi nityamanuksana nasa param tyaja gramya katha sakalam viphalam bhaja godruma kanana kunja vidhum

iha copaniṣat⋅parigīta⋅vibhuḥ

vada jādava mādhava kṛṣṇa hare

vada rāsa-rasāyana gaura-hare
bhaja godruma⋅kānana⋅kuñja⋅vidhum

cala gaura-vanaṁ nava-khaṇḍa-mayaṁ
paṭha gaura-hareścaritāni mudā
luṭha gaura⋅padāṅkita⋅gāṅga⋅taṭaṁ
bhaja godruma⋅kānana⋅kuñja⋅vidhum

smara gaura⋅gadādhara⋅keli⋅kalāṁ
bhava gaura⋅gadādhara⋅pakṣa⋅caraḥ
śṛṇu gaura⋅gadādhara⋅cāru⋅kathāṁ
bhaja godruma⋅kānana⋅kuñja⋅vidhum
IDX.en_translation:
If your heart is always intent on drinking the nectar-like taste of Hari's lotus feet, abandoning the home full of the mood of Kali-yuga, worship the splendor of the groves in Godruma forest.

Wealth, youth, life, and the happiness of a kingdom are not eternal; they are constantly perishing. Give up all mundane talks, which are useless. Worship the splendor of the groves in Godruma forest.

And O friend, the happiness of association with women, in the end, is fearful and takes away one's human goals. Being intoxicated by the nectar-like taste of Hari-nāma, worship the splendor of the groves in Godruma forest.

The taste of mundane poetry is not true poetic taste. The taste of Gaura, who purifies Kali-yuga, is indeed the true taste. Enough of cultivating other topics. Worship the splendor of the groves in Godruma forest.

Whose beautiful left side is accompanied by Vṛṣabhānu-sutā; the hero on the bank of the Yamunā, son of Nanda; engaged in the joyous sweet music of the flute- Worship the splendor of the groves in Godruma forest.

Situated in the midst of Hari-kīrttana with His own associates, surrounded by them, Hari with a golden luster; the sole ocean of mercy for the people of Gaura-deśa- Worship the splendor of the groves in Godruma forest.

Whose home is surrounded by Girirājasutā; the master of the nine continents, captivating the minds of ascetics; praised by hosts of demigods, with His beloved- Worship the splendor of the groves in Godruma forest.

Holding the mood of a club for the dogs of Kali; intent on giving the great medicine of Hari-nāma; embodying a beautiful form, full of compassion for the distressed fallen- Worship the splendor of the groves in Godruma forest.

Whose mercy makes no distinction between enemy and friend, constantly arising from His lotus-like face; that Akṛṣṇa here, the son of Vraja⋅rāja- Worship the splendor of the groves in Godruma forest.

Here, the all-pervading Lord praised in the Upaniṣads; the son of Dvijarāja, Hari with a golden luster; plays in His own abode with His friends- Worship the splendor of the groves in Godruma forest.

The best of all avatāras, fully complete; the Supreme Truth, full of self-manifested pastimes here; the hidden rasa of the ocean of Vraja-dhāma- Worship the splendor of the groves in Godruma forest.

Whose mercy is not dependent on Vedic knowledge, social status, or wealth, powerful in generating devotion without these. O friend, He is indeed the path of causeless ecstatic love- Worship the splendor of the groves in Godruma forest.

Even when in the grip of a crocodile, in the middle of a lake, He delivered that distressed living entity. He is unborn; whose strength is inconceivable, a desire-tree of auspiciousness- Worship the splendor of the groves in Godruma forest.

With a mind pleased by the penance of Surabhīndra, Hari, possessing a beautiful complexion, appeared. That eternal happiness, who steals the patience of the sages- Worship the splendor of the groves in Godruma forest.

All desires, and the mentality of non-difference; give up all of this, both inauspicious and auspicious. With favor and beloved service, worship the splendor of the groves in Godruma forest.

Intent on the duty of serving Hari's servants; delighted in drinking the nectar of Hari-nāma's rasa; endowed with humility, meekness, and compassion- Worship the splendor of the groves in Godruma forest.

Chant Yādava, Mādhava, Kṛṣṇa, Hare! Chant Rāma, Janārddana, Keśava, O! Always the beloved Lord of Vṛṣabhānu-sutā- Worship the splendor of the groves in Godruma forest.

Chant "Lord of the forest and mountains on the bank of the Yamunā!" Chant "Sound of the clusters of groves in Gokula forest!" Chant "O Gaura-Hari, the elixir of Rāsa!"- Worship the splendor of the groves in Godruma forest.

Go to the nine-part forest of Gaura. Read the pastimes of Gaura-Hari with joy. Roll on the bank of the Gaṅgā marked with Gaura's footsteps- Worship the splendor of the groves in Godruma forest.

Remember the playful arts of Gaura and Gadādhara. Become a follower of Gaura and Gadādhara. Hear the charming narrations of Gaura and Gadādhara- Worship the splendor of the groves in Godruma forest.

IDX.first_line: jamalarjuna bhanjanam asrita ranjanam ahiganjana ghana lasya bharam
IDX.search: jamalarjuna bhanjanam asrita ranjanam ahiganjana ghana lasya bharam pasupala purandaram abhisrta kandaram atisundaram aravinda karam vara gopa vadhu jana viracita pujanam uru kujana nava venu dharam smara narma vicaksanam akhila vilaksana tanu laksanam atidaksataram pranatasani panjaram namvara pinjaram arikunjara harim indumukham go mandala raksinam anukrta paksinam atidaksinam amitatma sukham guru gairika manditam anunaya panditam avakhandita puruhuta makham vraja kamala virocanam alika surocana gorocanam atitamra nakham
IDX.author: śrīla rūpa gosvāmī
IDX.verses:
jamalārjuna⋅bhañjanam āśrita⋅rañjanam ahigañjana⋅ghana⋅lāsya⋅bharaṁ
paśupāla⋅purandaram abhisṛta⋅kandaram atisundaram aravinda⋅karam
vara⋅gopa⋅vadhū⋅jana⋅viracita⋅pūjanam uru⋅kūjana⋅nava⋅veṇu⋅dharaṁ
smara⋅narma⋅vicakṣaṇam akhila⋅vilakṣaṇa⋅tanu⋅lakṣaṇam atidakṣataram

praṇatāśani⋅pañjaram namvara⋅piñjaram arikuñjara⋅harim indumukhaṁ
go⋅maṇḍala⋅rakṣiṇam anukṛta⋅pakṣiṇam atidakṣiṇam amitātma⋅sukham
guru⋅gairika⋅maṇḍitam anunaya⋅paṇḍitam avakhaṇḍita⋅puruhūta⋅makhaṁ
vraja⋅kamala⋅virocanam alika⋅surocana⋅gorocanam atitāmra⋅nakham

unmada⋅rati⋅nāyaka⋅śāṇita⋅sāyaka⋅vinidhāyaka⋅cala⋅cilli⋅latam
uddhata⋅saṅkocanam ambuja⋅locanam agha⋅mocanam amarāli⋅natam
nikhilādhika⋅gauravam ujjvala⋅saurabham atigaurabha⋅paśupīṣu rataṁ
mṛdu⋅pada⋅pallavam abhram u vallabha⋅ruci⋅durlabha⋅sa⋅vilāsa⋅gatam

bhuja⋅mūrdhni viśaṅkaṭam adhigata⋅śaṅkaṭa⋅nata⋅kaṅkaṭam aṭavīṣu calaṁ
navanīpa⋅karambita⋅vana⋅rolambitam avalambita⋅kala⋅kaṇṭha⋅kalam
durjana⋅tṛṇa⋅pāvakam anucara⋅śāvaka⋅nikarāvakam aruṇoṣṭha⋅dalaṁ
nija⋅vikrama⋅carcita⋅bhuja⋅guru⋅garvita⋅gandharvita⋅danujārdi⋅balam

śruti⋅ratna⋅vibhūṣaṇa⋅ruci⋅jita⋅pūṣaṇam alidūṣaṇa⋅nayanānta⋅gatiṁ
jamunā⋅taṭa⋅talpita⋅puṣpam analpita⋅mada⋅jalpita⋅dayitāpta⋅ratim
vandemahi vandita⋅nandam amandita⋅kulam andhita⋅khala⋅kaṁsa⋅matiṁ
tvām iha dāmodara haladhara⋅sodara hara no daram anubaddha⋅ratim
IDX.en_translation:
Breaker of the twin Arjuna trees, You delight those who take shelter of You. Your heavy dancing shames the dark clouds. You are the best among the protectors of the cowherds; approaching the caves, You are exceedingly beautiful, with lotus-like hands. Worshiped by excellent cowherd girls and holding a new flute with abundant sweet sounds, You are an expert in amorous pastimes and possess all extraordinary bodily characteristics.

You are a fortress for the surrendered, wearing yellow garments—a lion to Your elephant-like enemies, with a moon-like face. Protector of the cowherd community, imitating the birds, You are very benevolent and possess unlimited inner happiness. Adorned with red chalk and expert in persuasion, You thwarted Indra's sacrifice. You illuminate the lotus of Vṛndāvana with an excellent vermilion mark and exceedingly reddish nails.

Your moving, creeper-like eyebrows set the sharpened arrows of the god of intoxicated love in motion. Humbling the arrogant, with lotus-like eyes, You are the liberator from sins, bowed to by hosts of demigods. You possess supreme glory and a brilliant fragrance, greatly delighting in the cowherd girls. With tender sprout-like feet, Your playful gait and dark beauty are difficult to attain even by the desires of Your most beloved devotees.

With broad shoulders and strong arms adorned with captivating bracelets, You move through the forests. Playing amidst forests intertwined with fresh kadamba flowers and surrounded by buzzing bees, Your sweet voice is echoed by the sweet-throated birds. You are a fire to the grass-like wicked and the protector of Your young followers, with reddish petal-like lips. Your arms are anointed with Your own valor, and Your strength torments the proud demons.

Adorned with the jewels of the Vedas, Your splendor conquers the sun-god, and Your sidelong glances shame the bees. Your beloveds attain deep love through Your intoxicating words, and flowers are spread for You on the banks of the Yamunā. We worship You, who are worshiped by Nanda, whose family is glorious, and who blinded the intelligence of the wicked king Kaṁsa. O Dāmodara, brother of Balarāma, remove our fear and grant us constant devotion.

IDX.first_line: jamuna puline kadamba kananeki herinu sakhi ajasyama vamsidhari manimancoparikare lila rasaraja
IDX.search: jamuna puline kadamba kananeki herinu sakhi ajasyama vamsidhari manimancoparikare lila rasaraja krsnakeli sudha prasravanaastadalopari sriradha srihariastasakhi parijana
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
jamunā⋅puline, kadamba⋅kānane,ki herinu sakhi! ājaśyāma vaṁśī-dhārī, maṇi-mañcopari,kare līlā rasa-rāja

kṛṣṇakeli sudhā⋅prasravaṇaaṣṭadalopari, śrī-rādhā śrī-hari,aṣṭasakhī parijana

sugīta⋅nartane, saba sakhī-gaṇe,tuṣiche jugaladhanekṛṣṇa-līlā heri’, prakṛti sundarī,vistāriche śobhā vane

ghare nā jāiba, vane praveśiva,o līlā⋅rasera taretyaji’ kulalāja, bhaja vraja⋅rāja,vinoda minati kare’
IDX.en_translation:
On the banks of the Yamunā, in a kadamba forest—what did I see today, my friend! Śyāma, the flute-holder, upon a jeweled platform, performs His pastimes as the King of Mellows.

Kṛṣṇa’s pastimes are a fountain of nectar. Upon an eight-petaled lotus are Śrī Rādhā and Śrī Hari, with their eight principal companions as attendants.

With beautiful singing and dancing, all the companions please the Divine Couple. Seeing Kṛṣṇa’s pastimes, beautiful nature spreads its splendor through the forest.

I will not go home; I will enter the forest for the sake of those pastimes and their nectar. Giving up family honor, worship the King of Vraja—this is Bhaktivinoda’s humble prayer.

IDX.first_line: janama saphala tara krsna darasana jarabhagye hoiyache ekabaravikasiya hrnnayana kari krsna darasanachade jiva cittera vikara
IDX.search: janama saphala tara krsna darasana jarabhagye hoiyache ekabaravikasiya hrnnayana kari krsna darasanachade jiva cittera vikara vrndavana kelicatura vanamalitribhanga bhangima rupa vamsidhari aparuparasamayanidhi gunasali
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
janama saphala tā’ra, kṛṣṇa daraśana jā’ra,bhāgye hoiyāche ekabāravikaśiyā hṛnnayana, kari’ kṛṣṇa⋅daraśana,chāḍe jīva cittera vikāra

vṛndāvana⋅kelicatura vanamālītribhaṅga⋅bhaṅgima rūpa, vaṁśīdhārī aparūpa,rasa-mayanidhi guṇaśālī

varṇa nava-jaladhara, śire śikhi-picchavara,alakā tilaka śobhā pāyaparidhāne pītavāsa, vadane madhura hāsa,hena rūpa jagata mātāya

indranīla jini’, kṛṣṇarūpakhāni,heriyā kadambamūlemana ucāṭana, nā cale caraṇa,saṁsāra gelāma bhule

(sakhi he) sudhāmaya, se rūpamādhurīdekhile nayana, haya acetana,jhare prema-maya vāri

kibā cūḍā śire, kibā vaṁśī kare,kibā se tribhaṅga⋅ṭhāmacaraṇa-kamale, amiyā uchale,tāhāte nūpuradāma

sadā āśā kari’, bhṛṅga-rūpa dhari’,caraṇa-kamale sthānaanāyāse pāi, kṛṣṇa-guṇa gāi,āra nā bhajiba āna
IDX.en_translation:
Successful is the birth of one who has had the vision of Kṛṣṇa even once by great fortune. By opening the eyes of the heart and beholding Kṛṣṇa, the soul abandons all mental perversions.

He is the expert lover adorned with forest flowers in His Vṛndāvana pastimes; with His unparalleled threefold-bending form and holding His flute, He is a treasure-trove of mellows endowed with all good qualities.

His complexion is like a fresh rain cloud, and on His head is a beautiful peacock feather; curly locks and a sacred tilaka adorn Him. Dressed in yellow garments with a sweet smile on His face, such a form enchants the world.

Surpassing the beauty of sapphire, gazing at Kṛṣṇa’s beautiful form at the base of a kadamba tree, my mind becomes agitated, my feet cannot move, and I forget the material world.

O my friend! Nectarine and sweet is that beautiful form. Upon seeing it, the eyes become unconscious and tears of divine love flow.

Oh, what a crown on His head! Oh, what a flute in His hand! Oh, what a charming threefold-bending posture! Nectar overflows from His lotus feet, which are adorned with anklets.

I always hope to assume the form of a bee and easily attain a place at His lotus feet. I will sing the glories of Kṛṣṇa and serve no one else.
