IDX.first_line: jara nama sravane samsara bandha ghuce
IDX.search: jara nama sravane samsara bandha ghuce hena prabhu avatari kaliyuge nace jara nama loi suka narada bedaya sahasra vadana prabhu jara guna gaya
IDX.author: śrīla vṛndāvana⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
jā̐ra nāma śravaṇe saṁsāra⋅bandha ghuce
hena prabhu avatari kaliyuge nāce

jā̐ra nāma loi śuka⋅nārada beḍāya
sahasra vadana prabhu jāra guṇa gāya

sarva⋅mahā⋅prāyaścitta je prabhura nāma
se prabhu nācaye dekhe jata bhāgyavāna

khrīkṛṣṇacaitanya nityānandaṭāda jāna
vṛndāvana dāsa tachu padayuge gāna
IDX.en_translation:
By hearing whose Name the bondage of material existence is removed—such a Lord has incarnated and dances in this Age of Kali.

Taking whose Name, the great sages Śukadeva and Nārada wander and preach; the Lord with a thousand mouths sings whose glories.

The Name of that Lord is the greatest atonement for all sins; that Lord now dances, and only the fortunate ones are able to see this.

Know Them to be Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya and Lord Nityānanda. Vṛndāvana Dāsa sings at Their lotus feet.

IDX.first_line: jasomati nandana vrajavara nagara
IDX.search: jasomati nandana vrajavara nagara gokula ranjana kana gopi paranadhana madana manohara kaliya damana vidhana amala harinama amiya vilasa vipina purandara navina nagaravara vamsivadana suvasa
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
jaśomatī⋅nandana, vrajavara⋅nāgara,
gokula⋅rañjana kāna
gopī⋅parāṇadhana, madana⋅manohara,
kālīya⋅damana⋅vidhāna

amala harināma amiya⋅vilāsā
vipina⋅purandara, navīna⋅nāgaravara,
vaṁśī-vadana, suvāsā

vraja⋅jana⋅pālana, asurakula⋅nāśana,
nanda⋅godhana⋅rākhooyālā
govinda, mādhava, navanīta⋅taskara,
sundara nanda-gopālā

jāmuna⋅taṭacara, gopī⋅vasanahara,
rāsa⋅rasika kṛpāmaya
śrī-rādhā-vallabha, vṛndāvana⋅naṭavara,
bhakativinoda⋅āśraya
IDX.en_translation:
The son of Yaśomatī, the chief lover of Vraja, Kṛṣṇa, who delights Gokula. The wealth of the gopīs' lives, the enchanter of Cupid, and the subduer of the serpent Kāliya.

Pure Harināma is a nectarine pastime. He is the Lord of the forests, the ever-fresh supreme lover, the flute-player adorned with beautiful garments.

The protector of the people of Vraja, the destroyer of the demon dynasty, and the protector of Nanda's cows. He is Govinda, Mādhava, the butter thief, and the beautiful cowherd boy of Nanda.

He roams on the banks of the Yamunā, stole the garments of the gopīs, and enjoys the rāsa dance, full of mercy. He is the beloved of Śrī Rādhā, the supreme dancer of Vṛndāvana, and the shelter of Bhaktivinoda.

IDX.first_line: jat kinkarisu bahusah khalu kaku vani
IDX.search: jat kinkarisu bahusah khalu kaku vani nityam parasya purusasya sikhanda mauleh tasyah kada rasa nidher vrsabhanu jayas tat keli kunja bhavanangana marjani syam jo brahma rudra suka narada bhisma mukhyair alaksito na sahasa purusasya tasya sadyo vasi karana curnam ananta saktim tam radhika carana renum anusmarami
IDX.verses:
jat⋅kiṅkarīṣu bahuśaḥ khalu kāku⋅vāṇī
nityaṁ parasya puruṣasya śikhaṇḍa⋅mauleḥ
tasyāḥ kadā rasa⋅nidher vṛṣabhānu⋅jāyās
tat⋅keli⋅kuñja⋅bhavanāṅgana⋅marjanī syām

jo brahma⋅rudra⋅śuka⋅nārada⋅bhīṣma⋅mukhyair
ālakṣito na sahasā puruṣasya tasya
sadyo⋅vaśī⋅karaṇa⋅cūrṇam ananta⋅śaktiṁ
taṁ rādhikā⋅caraṇa⋅reṇum anusmarāmi

jasyāḥ kadāpi vasanāñcala⋅khelanottha⋅
dhanyāti⋅dhanya⋅pavanena kṛtārtha⋅mānī
jogīndra⋅durgama⋅gatir madhusūdano ‘pi
tasyā namo ‘stu vṛṣabhānu⋅bhuvo diśe ‘pi

pādābjayos tava vinā vara⋅dāsyam eva
nānyat kadāpi samaye kila devi jāce
sakhyāya te mama namo ‘stu namo ‘stu nityaṁ
dāsyāya te mama raso ‘stu raso ‘stu satyam

hā devi kāku⋅bhara⋅gadgadayādya vācā
jāce nipatya bhuvi daṇḍavad udbhaṭārtiḥ
asya prasādam abudhasya janasya kṛtvā
gāndharvike nija⋅gaṇe gaṇanāṁ vidhehi

anārādhya rādhā⋅padāmbhoja⋅reṇum
anāśritya vṛndāṭavīṁ tat⋅padāṅkam
asambhāṣya tad⋅bhāva⋅gambhīra⋅cittān
kutaḥ śyāma⋅sindho rasasyāvagāhaḥ

bhajāmi rādhām aravinda⋅netrāṁ
smarāmi rādhāṁ madhura⋅smitāsyām
vadāmi rādhāṁ karuṇa⋅bharārdrāṁ
tato mamānyāsti gatir na kāpi

mahā⋅bhāva⋅svarūpā tvaṁ kṛṣṇa⋅priyā⋅varīyasi
prema⋅bhakti⋅prade devi rādhike tvāṁ namāmy aham

rādhe vṛndāvanādhīśe karuṇāmṛta⋅vāhini
kṛpayā nija⋅pādābja⋅dāsyaṁ mahyaṁ pradīyatām

vande nanda⋅vraja⋅strīṇāṁ, pāda⋅reṇum abhīkṣṇaśaḥ
jāsāṁ hari⋅kathodgītaṁ, punāti bhuvana⋅trayam

tavaivāsmi tavaivāsmi, na jīvāmi tvayā vinā
iti vijñāya devi tvaṁ, naya māṁ caraṇāntikama
IDX.en_translation:
When will I be the sweeper of the courtyard of the play-grove of the daughter of Vṛṣabhānu, the ocean of rasa? The Supreme Person with a peacock-feather crown (Kṛṣṇa) always speaks many plaintive words to Her maidservants.

I remember that dust of Rādhikā's feet, which is the infinite power, the powder that instantly subdues that Person who is not easily perceived by Brahmā, Rudra, Śuka, Nārada, Bhīṣma, and others.

Even Madhusūdana, whose path is difficult for the best of yogīs to attain, considers Himself successful by the most fortunate breeze arising from the playful movement of the corner of Her cloth. Obeisances to Her, and even to the direction of Vṛṣabhānu's daughter.

O Devi! I indeed never beg for anything else at any time except for the excellent service of Your lotus feet. Obeisances to Your friendship; obeisances always. May my taste be for Your service; may it be true.

Alas, O Devi! Today, with a voice choked with piteous words, falling on the ground like a stick, I beg with intense distress: bestowing mercy on this ignorant person, O Gāndharvikā, please count me among Your own people.

Without worshipping the dust of Rādhā's lotus feet, without taking shelter of the forest of Vṛndā which bears Her footprints, without conversing with those whose hearts are deep with Her mood—how can one enter the ocean of the rasa of Śyāma?

I worship Rādhā who has lotus eyes; I remember Rādhā who has a sweet smile on Her face; I speak of Rādhā who is melted by the burden of compassion. Therefore, I have no other goal.

You are the form of Mahābhāva, the best of Kṛṣṇa's beloveds. O Devi Rādhikā, bestower of loving devotion, I bow to You.

O Rādhe! Queen of Vṛndāvana! River of the nectar of mercy! Please mercifully give me the service of Your lotus feet.

I repeatedly offer obeisances to the dust of the feet of the women of Nanda's Vraja, whose loud singing of the topics of Hari purifies the three worlds.

I am Yours alone, I am Yours alone, I cannot live without You. Knowing this, O Devi, lead me to the vicinity of Your feet.

IDX.first_line: jatane jateka dhana pape vataralo
IDX.search: jatane jateka dhana pape vataralo meli meli parijana khaya maranaka beri koi na puchoi karama sange cali jaya e hari vando tuya pada naya tuya pada parihari papa payonidhi para haba kon upaya
IDX.author: śrī⋅vidyāpati
IDX.verses:
jatane jateka dhana, pāpe vaṭāralo,
meli meli parijana khāya
maraṇaka beri, koi nā puchoi,
karama saṅge cali jāya

e hari vando tuyā pada⋅nāya
tuyā pada parihari, pāpa⋅payonidhi,
pāra haba kon upāya

jāvata janama hāma, tuyā pada nā sevinu,
juvatī matimaya meli
amṛta tyaji kiye, halāhala piyanu,
sampade vipadahi bheli

bhaṇahu̐ vidyāpati, leha mane gaṇi,
kahile ki bāḍhaba kāje
sā̐jhaka beri, seva koi māgoi,
heroite tuyā pada lāje
IDX.en_translation:
Whatever wealth I accumulated with effort, I earned through sin, and my family consumed it. At the time of death, no one will care; only my past actions will go with me.

O Lord Hari! I worship Your lotus feet. Having abandoned Your feet, how can I cross this ocean of sin?

Throughout my life, I did not serve Your feet, being absorbed in the intoxication of youth and delusion. Abandoning nectar, I drank poison, and my prosperity has turned into misfortune.

Vidyāpati says: "Consider this in your heart—what more will be gained by speaking? At the end of my life, who will ask for service? I am ashamed to even look at Your lotus feet."

IDX.first_line: jaya godrumapati gora
IDX.search: jaya godrumapati gora nitai jivana advaitera dhana vrndavana bhava vibhora
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
jaya godrumapati gorā

nitāi⋅jīvana, advaitera dhana,
vṛndāvana⋅bhāva⋅vibhorā

gadādhara⋅prāṇa, śrīvāsa⋅śaraṇa,
kṛṣṇabhakta⋅mānasa⋅corā
IDX.en_translation:
All glories to Gaura⋅candra, the Lord of Godruma!

He is the life of Nitāi and the treasure of Advaita, absorbed in the mood of Vṛndāvana.

He is the life of Gadādhara and the shelter of Śrīvāsa—the thief of the minds of the devotees of Kṛṣṇa.

IDX.first_line: jaya govinda jaya gopalakesava madhava dinadayalasyamasundara kanhaiyalalagirivaradhari nandadulala
IDX.search: jaya govinda jaya gopalakesava madhava dinadayalasyamasundara kanhaiyalalagirivaradhari nandadulala acyuta kesava sridhara madhavagopala govinda hariyamuna puliname vamsi bajavenatavara vesadhari
IDX.verses:
jaya govinda, jaya gopāla,keśava, mādhava, dīnadayālaśyāma-sundara kanhaiyālāla,girivara-dhārī nanda-dulāla

acyuta, keśava, śrī-dhara mādhava,gopāla govinda, hariyamunā puliname̐ vaṁśī bajāve,naṭavara veśadhārī
IDX.en_translation:
All glories to Govinda, all glories to Gopāla! Keśava, Mādhava, the Friend of the distressed! Śyāmasundara, the beloved Kanhaīyālāla, the Lifter of Govardhana Hill, the beloved Son of Nanda!

Acyuta, Keśava, Śrīdhara, Mādhava, Gopāla, Govinda, Hari! On the banks of the Yamunā, He plays the flute, appearing as the supreme dancer.

IDX.first_line: jaya jaga tarana karana dhama
IDX.search: jaya jaga tarana karana dhama ananda kanda nityananda nama dagamaga locana kamala dhulayata sahaje athira gati jiti matoyara bhaiya abhirama bali ghana ghana daka i gaura prema bhare cala i na para
IDX.author: govinda dāsa
IDX.verses: jaya jaga⋅tāraṇa⋅kāraṇa dhāma ānanda⋅kanda nityānanda nāma ḍagamaga locana⋅kamala ḍhulāyata sahaje athira gati jiti matoyāra bhāiyā abhirāma bali ghana ghana ḍāka⋅i gaura prema⋅bhare cala⋅i nā pāra gada gada ādhamadhura vacanāmṛta lahu lahu hāsa⋅vikaśita ganta pāṣaṇḍa khaṇḍana śrī⋅bhuja⋅maṇḍana kanaka⋅ khacita avalamvana daṇḍa kali⋅yuga kālabhujaṅgama daṁśala dagadhala sthāvara jāṅgama dekhi prema⋅sudhā⋅rasa jaga bhari barikhala govinda⋅dāsa ke kāŋhe upekhi
IDX.en_translation:
All glories to the abode that is the cause of the world's deliverance, the root of all bliss, whose Name is Nityānanda.

His lotus-like eyes sway unsteadily with ease. His wavering gait outdoes even an intoxicated person. Calling out "O brother Abhirāma!" again and again, Gaura is unable to walk, overwhelmed with divine love.

Faltering, half-sweet, nectarous words gently flow from His mouth, His face blossoming with a soft smile. The destroyer of atheists, He holds a golden staff inlaid with gold, which adorns His beautiful arms.

Seeing all beings, both moving and non-moving, bitten and scorched by the serpent of the Age of Kali, He showered the entire world with the nectarean essence of divine love. Why then does He neglect Govinda Dāsa?

IDX.first_line: jaya jasoda nandana krsnagopala govindajaya madanamohana hareananta mukunda
IDX.search: jaya jasoda nandana krsnagopala govindajaya madanamohana hareananta mukunda jaya acyuta madhava ramavrndavana candrajaya muralivadana syamagopi janananda
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
jaya jaśodā⋅nandana kṛṣṇagopāla govindajaya madanamohana hareananta mukunda

jaya acyuta mādhava rāmavṛndāvana⋅candrajaya muralīvadana śyāmagopī⋅janānanda
IDX.en_translation:
All glories to Yaśodā-nandana (son of Yaśodā), Kṛṣṇa. Gopāla, Govinda. All glories to Madanamohana (he who enchants Cupid), Hari (the remover of suffering). Ananta (limitless), Mukunda (giver of liberation).

All glories to Acyuta (infallible), Mādhava, Rāma (the supreme enjoyer). Vṛndāvanacandra (moon of Vṛndāvana). All glories to Muralīvadana (one whose face holds a flute), Śyāma. Gopījanānanda (the delight of the gopīs).

IDX.first_line: jaya jaya adbhuta so pahu advaita
IDX.search: jaya jaya adbhuta so pahu advaita suradhuni sannidhane akhi mudi rahe prema nadi vahe vasana titila ghame nija pahu mane ghana garajane uthe jode jode lampha dake bahu tuli kade phuli phuli dehe viparita kampa
IDX.author: śrīla vṛndāvana⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
jaya jaya adbhuta, so pahu̐ advaita,
suradhunī sannidhāne
ā̐khi mudi’ rahe, prema⋅nadī vahe,
vasana titila ghāme

nija pahu̐ mane, ghana garajane,
uṭhe joḍe joḍe lampha
ḍāke bāhu tuli’, kā̐de phuli’ phuli’,
dehe viparīta kampa

advaita⋅huṅkāre, suradhunī⋅tīre,
āilā nāgara⋅rāja
tā̐hāra pīrite, āilā tvarite,
udaya nadīyā⋅mājha

jaya sītānātha, karala vekata,
nandera nandana hari
kahe vṛndāvana, advaita⋅caraṇa,
hiyāra mājhāre dhari
IDX.en_translation:
All glories, all glories to the amazing one! There, the master Advaita, near the Suradhunī, remains with closed eyes. A river of love flows, his clothes are drenched in sweat.

In his own mind, the master, with a deep roar, leaps again and again. He calls with raised arms, he weeps profusely, his body trembles violently.

By the roar of Advaita, on the bank of the Suradhunī, the Nāgara-rāja arrived. Out of love for him, he quickly came, appearing in Nadīyā.

All glories to Sītānātha! He made manifest Hari, the son of Nanda. Vṛndāvana says, holding the lotus feet of Advaita within my heart.

IDX.first_line: jaya jaya advaita acarya dayamaya
IDX.search: jaya jaya advaita acarya dayamaya jara huhunkare gaura avatara haya premadata sitanatha karuna sagara jara premarase aila vrajera nagara
IDX.author: śrīla locana⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
jaya jaya advaita ācārya dayāmaya
jā’ra huhuṅkāre gaura⋅avatāra haya

premadātā sītānātha karuṇā⋅sāgara
jā’ra prema-rase āilā vrajera nāgara

jāhāre karuṇā kari’ kṛpā dite cāya
prema⋅rase se⋅jana caitanya⋅guṇa gāya

tāhāra padete jebā loila śaraṇa
se⋅jana pāila gauraprema⋅mahādhana

emana dayāra nidhi kene nā bhajilu
locana bale nija⋅māthe vajara pāḍilu
IDX.en_translation:
Glory, glory to the merciful Advaita Ācārya! By whose roaring call the Avatāra Gaura appeared.

The giver of love, the Lord of Sītā, the ocean of compassion. By the nectar of whose love, the Hero of Vraja came.

To whomsoever He desires to give mercy, that person sings the qualities of Caitanya in the mellows of love.

Whoever took shelter at His feet, that person attained the great treasure of Gaura-prema.

Why did I not worship such a reservoir of mercy? Locana says, "I have struck a thunderbolt on my own head."

IDX.first_line: jaya jaya gadadhara pandita gosani
IDX.search: jaya jaya gadadhara pandita gosani jara krpabale se caitanya guna gai hena se gaurangacandre jahara piriti gadadhara prananatha jahe lage khyati
IDX.author: śivānanda
IDX.verses:
jaya jaya gadādhara paṇḍita gosāñi
jā̐’ra kṛpā-bale se caitanya⋅guṇa gāi

hena se gaurāṅgacandre jā̐hāra pirīti
‘gadādhara⋅prāṇanātha’ jāhe lāge khyāti

gauragata⋅prāṇa, prema ke bujhite pāre
kṣetra-vāsa, kṛṣṇa-sevā jā̐’ra lāgi’ chāḍe

gadāira gaurāṅga, gaurāṅgera gadādhara
śrī⋅rāma⋅jānakī jena eka kalevara

jena ekaprāṇa rādhā⋅vṛndāvana⋅candra
tena gaura⋅gadādhara premera taraṅga

kahe śivānanda pa̐hu jā̐’ra anurāge
śyāma-tanu gaurāṅga hoiyā prema māge
IDX.en_translation:
All glories, all glories to Śrīla Gadādhara Paṇḍita Gosvāmī! By the power of his mercy, I sing the glories of Lord Caitanya.

Such is his love for Lord Gaurāṅgacandra that he is famously known as 'Gadādhara-prāṇanātha' (the Lord of Gadādhara's life).

His life is completely absorbed in Lord Gaura. Who can truly understand his love? For the sake of Lord Gaura, he gave up his residence in Jagannātha Purī and even his direct service to Lord Kṛṣṇa.

Gaurāṅga belongs to Gadādhara, and Gadādhara belongs to Gaurāṅga. They are just like Śrī Rāma and Śrīmati Sītā-devī, appearing in one divine form.

Just as Śrīmati Rādhā⋅rāṇī and Vṛndāvanacandra are one soul, so are Lord Gaura and Śrīla Gadādhara Paṇḍita—a vast wave of divine love.

Śivānanda Sena says: "In whose deep affection Lord Kṛṣṇa, whose body is the color of a dark cloud, became Gaurāṅga and now begs for divine love."

IDX.first_line: jaya jaya gadadhara premera sagara
IDX.search: jaya jaya gadadhara premera sagara gaurangera priyottama pandita pravara madhava misrera putra sri gadadhara nama sisu haite samsare virakta bhagyavan
IDX.author: śrī⋅caitanyabhāgabata hoite uddhṛta ~ caitanya⋅bhāgavata
IDX.verses:
jaya jaya gadādhara premera sāgara
gaurāṅgera priyottama paṇḍita pravara

“mādhava miśrera putra⋅śrī⋅gadādhara nāma
śiśu haite saṁsāre virakta bhāgyavān

viṣṇu⋅bhakti⋅tejomaya divya kalevara
ākṛti, prakṛti⋅dui parama sundara

āpane caitanya baliyāche bāra bāra
‘gadādhara mora vaikuṇṭhera paribāra’

niravadhi gadādhara thākena saŋhati
prabhu⋅gadādharera viccheda nāhi kati

ki bhojane, ki śayane, paryaṭane
gadādhara prabhure sevena anukṣaṇe

gadādhara sammukhe paḍena bhāgavata
śuni prabhu hana premarase mahāmatta

gadādhara⋅vākye mātra prabhu sukhī haya
bhrame gadādhara saṅge vaiṣṇava⋅ālaya

paṇḍite prabhura prasāda kahana nā jāya
‘gadādhara⋅prāṇanātha’ nāma haila jāya

gadādhara⋅pāda⋅padme mora namaskāra
gaura⋅candra⋅saṅge jā̐ra kīrtana vihāra”
IDX.en_translation:
Glory, glory to Gadādhara, the ocean of love! The dearest of Gaurāṅga, the best of Paṇḍitas.

The son of Mādhava Miśra, named Śrī Gadādhara. From childhood, he was detached from the world and fortunate.

A divine body full of the brilliance of devotion to Viṣṇu. Both his form and nature were supremely beautiful.

Caitanya Himself has said again and again: "Gadādhara is My family from Vaikuṇṭha."

Gadādhara remains constantly in His company. There is never any separation between the Lord and Gadādhara.

Whether eating or sleeping or travelling, Gadādhara serves the Lord at every moment.

Gadādhara reads the Bhāgavata in front of Him. Hearing it, the Lord becomes greatly maddened in the rasa of love.

The Lord becomes happy only by the words of Gadādhara. He wanders to the homes of Vaiṣṇavas with Gadādhara.

The Lord's mercy on the Paṇḍita cannot be described. His name became 'Gadādhara-Prāṇanātha' (Lord of Gadādhara's life).

My obeisances to the lotus feet of Gadādhara, whose kīrtana-pastime is with Gaura⋅candra.

IDX.first_line: jaya jaya goracadera aratiko sobhajahnavi tatavane jagamana lobha
IDX.search: jaya jaya goracadera aratiko sobhajahnavi tatavane jagamana lobha daksine nitaicada bame gadadharanikate advaita srinivasa chatradhara
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
jaya jaya gorācā̐dera āratiko śobhājāhnavī⋅taṭavane jagamana⋅lobhā

dakṣiṇe nitāicā̐da, bāme gadādharanikaṭe advaita, śrīnivāsa chatra-dhara

basiyāche gorācā̐da ratna⋅siŋhāsaneārati karena brahmā⋅ādi deva-gaṇe

narahari⋅ādi kari’ cāmara ḍhulāyasañjaya⋅mukunda⋅vāsughoṣa⋅ādi gāya

śaṅkha bāje, ghaṇṭā bāje, bāje karatālamadhura mṛdaṅga bāje parama rasāla

bahukoṭī candra jini’ vadana ujjvalagaladeśe vanamālā kare jhalamala

śiva⋅śuka⋅nārada preme gadagadabhakativinoda dekhe gorāra sampada
IDX.en_translation:
Glory, glory to the beauty of Gorācānd’s āratī ceremony! In the forest on the bank of the Ganges, captivating the minds of all beings.

On the right, Nitāicānd; on the left, Gadādhara. Nearby is Advaita, and Śrīnivāsa Paṇḍita holds the umbrella.

Gorācānd is seated on a jeweled throne. Lord Brahmā and other demigods perform His āratī.

Narahari Sarkār and others wave the whisks. Sañjaya, Mukunda Datta, Vāsughoṣa and others sing.

The conch shell blows, the bell rings, and the cymbals clash. The sweet drum sounds, supremely delightful.

His face is bright, surpassing millions of moons. A garland of forest flowers glitters around His neck.

Lord Śiva, Śukadeva Gosvāmī, and Nārada Muni are overwhelmed with divine love. Bhakativinoda beholds the spiritual opulence of Gorā.

IDX.first_line: jaya jaya gurudeva sri bhaktiprajnanaparama mohana rupa arta vimocana
IDX.search: jaya jaya gurudeva sri bhaktiprajnanaparama mohana rupa arta vimocana murtimanta sri vedanta asubha nasanabhaktigrantha srivedanta tava vighosana
IDX.author: śrīmadbhaktivedānta trivikrama gosvāmī mahārāja
IDX.verses:
jaya jaya gurudeva śrī⋅bhaktiprajñānaparama mohana rūpa ārta⋅vimocana

mūrtimanta śrī⋅vedānta aśubha⋅nāśana“bhakti-grantha śrī-vedānta” tava vighoṣaṇa

vedānta samiti⋅dīpe śrī⋅siddhānta⋅jyotiārati tomāra tāhe haya niravadhi

śrī⋅vinodadhārā⋅taile dīpa prapūritarūpānuga⋅dhūpe daśa-dik āmodita

sarvaśāstra sugambhīra karuṇā⋅komalajugapat suśobhana vadana⋅kamala

svarṇakānti vinindita śrīaṅga⋅śobhanajativāsa paridhāne jagat⋅kalyāṇa

nānā chā̐de sajjana cāmara ḍhulāyagaura-jana uccakaṇṭhe sumadhura gāya

sumaṅgala nīrājana kare bhaktagaṇaduramati dūra haite dekhe trivikrama
IDX.en_translation:
All glories, all glories to Śrī Gurudeva Bhakti-prajñāna! Your supremely enchanting form delivers the distressed.

You are the personification of Śrī Vedānta, the destroyer of inauspiciousness. You proclaimed, “Bhakti is the scripture, Śrī Vedānta.”

In the lamp of the Vedānta Samiti, the light of siddhānta (philosophical conclusion) shines; Your ārati is performed there incessantly.

The lamp is filled with the oil of the current of Śrī Vinoda; The ten directions are perfumed by the incense of the followers of Rūpa.

Grave in all scriptures, soft with mercy; simultaneously, Your lotus face is very beautiful.

The beauty of Your divine limbs defeats the luster of gold; Wearing the dress of a sannyāsī, You bring auspiciousness to the world.

In various styles, the saintly persons wave the cāmara; The associate of Gaura sings very sweetly in a loud voice.

The devotees perform the auspicious nīrājana; From a distance, the wicked-minded Trivikrama watches.

IDX.first_line: jaya jaya harinama cidanandamrta dhama
IDX.search: jaya jaya harinama cidanandamrta dhama paratattva aksara akara nija jane krpa kari namarupe avatari jive daya karile apara jaya hari krsna nama jagajana suvisrama sarvajana manasa ranjana munivrnda nirantara je namera samadara kari gaya bhariya vadana
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
jaya jaya harināma, cidānandāmṛta dhāma,
paratattva akṣara⋅ākāra
nija⋅jane kṛpā kari’, nāmarūpe avatari’,
jīve dayā karile apāra

jaya hari⋅kṛṣṇa⋅nāma, jagajana suviśrāma,
sarvajana⋅mānasa⋅rañjana
munivṛnda nirantara, je nāmera samādara,
kari’ gāya bhariyā vadana

ohe kṛṣṇa⋅nāmākṣara, tumi sarvaśaktidhara,
jīvera kalyāṇa⋅vitaraṇe
tomā vinā bhavasindhu, uddhārite nāhi bandhu,
āsiyācha jīva⋅uddhāraṇe

āche tāpa jīve jata, tumi saba kara hata,
helāya tomāre ekabāra
ḍāke jadi kona jana, ha’ye dīna akiñcana,
nāhi dekhi’ anya pratikāra

tava svalpasphūrti pāya, ugratāpa dūre jāya,
liṅga⋅bhaṅga haya anāyāse
bhakativinoda kaya, jaya harināma jaya,
pa’ḍe thāki tuyā pada⋅āśe
IDX.en_translation:
All glories, all glories to the Holy Name, the abode of conscious bliss and nectar, the Supreme Truth in the form of syllables! Out of mercy for Your own people, You have descended in the form of the Name and bestowed unlimited compassion upon the souls.

All glories to the names of Hari and Kṛṣṇa, the supreme rest for all living beings and the delight of everyone's mind! Groups of sages constantly honor this Name and sing it with a full voice.

O imperishable Name of Kṛṣṇa, You possess all potencies for bestowing welfare upon the souls. Without You, there is no friend to deliver us from the ocean of material existence; You have come for the soul's deliverance.

Whatever miseries exist for the soul, You destroy them all if a person calls out to You just once, becoming humble and destitute, seeing no other remedy.

Upon receiving Your slightest manifestation, fierce miseries vanish and the subtle body is easily destroyed. Bhaktivinoda says: victory to the Holy Name! I remain in the hope of Your feet.

IDX.first_line: jaya jaya jadu kula jalanidhi candra
IDX.search: jaya jaya jadu kula jalanidhi candra vraja kula gokula ananda kanda jaya jaya jaladhara syamara anga helana kalpa taru lalita tri bhanga
IDX.author: govinda dāsa
IDX.verses: jaya jaya jadu⋅kula⋅jalanidhi⋅candra vraja⋅kula gokula ānanda⋅kanda jaya jaya jaladhara śyāmara aṅga helana kalpa⋅taru lalita tri⋅bhaṅga sudha⋅i sudhāmaya murali⋅vilāsa jaga⋅jana⋅mohana madhurima hāsa avanī vilambita vani vana⋅bāla madhukara jhaṅkaru tatahi rasāla taruṇa aruṇa ruci pada aravinda nakha⋅maṇi nichani dāsa⋅govinda
IDX.en_translation:
All glories, all glories to the Moon of the Yadu dynasty, the very root of joy for the residents of Vraja and Gokula!

All glories, all glories to Him whose body is dark like a cloud, who effortlessly surpasses the wish-fulfilling tree, and who stands in a charming threefold bending posture!

Nectarean are His pastimes with the flute, and His sweet smile enchants everyone in the world.

His forest garland sways low to the earth, and the bees hum there, attracted by the sweet aroma.

Govinda Dāsa offers his adoration to the jewel-like nails of His lotus feet, which are as radiant as the rising sun.

IDX.first_line: jaya jaya jaga jana locana phanda
IDX.search: jaya jaya jaga jana locana phanda radha ramana vrndavana canda abhinava nila jalada tanu dhala dhala pincha mukuta sire sajani re kancana vasanaratanamaya abharana nupura ranarani bajani re
IDX.author: govinda dāsa
IDX.verses: jaya jaya jaga⋅jana⋅locana⋅phānda rādhā⋅ramaṇa vṛndāvana⋅cānda abhinava nīla jalada tanu ḍhala ḍhala piñcha mukuṭa śire sājani re kāñcana vasanaratanamaya ābharaṇa nūpura raṇaraṇi bājanī re indīvara⋅yuga subhaga vilocana añcala cañcala kusuma⋅śare avicala kula ramaṇī⋅gaṇa mānasa jara jara antare madana⋅bhare vani vana⋅māloājānu⋅lambita parimale ali⋅kula māti rahu bimbādhara pare mohana muralī gā⋅ota govinda⋅dāsa⋅pahu
IDX.en_translation:
All glories, all glories to the moon of Vṛndāvana, Rādhā-ramaṇa, who captivates the eyes of all the people in the world!

His fresh, blue body is dazzling like a cloud, and a peacock-feather crown adorns His head. He wears golden garments and jeweled ornaments, and His anklets make a jingling sound.

His beautiful lotus-like eyes dart restless glances like Cupid's flower-arrows. The hearts of all the ladies of Vraja are overwhelmed with love by His glances.

A forest garland hangs down to His knees, and its fragrance intoxicates the bees. Upon His red lips, an enchanting flute sings—so says the Master of Govinda Dāsa.

IDX.first_line: jaya jaya jagannatha sacira nandanatribhuvana kare jara carana vandana
IDX.search: jaya jaya jagannatha sacira nandanatribhuvana kare jara carana vandana nilacale sankha cakra gada padmadharanadiya nagare danda kamandalu kara
IDX.author: vasudeva ghoṣa
IDX.verses:
jaya jaya jagannātha śacīra nandanatri-bhuvana kare jā̐’ra caraṇa vandana

nīlācale śaṅkha⋅cakra⋅gadā⋅padmadharanadīyā nagare daṇḍa⋅kamaṇḍalu⋅kara

keha bale, ⋅‘pūravete rāvaṇa vadhilā’golokera vaibhava līlā prakāśa karilā

śrī⋅rādhāra bhāve ebe gorā avatāra‘hare kṛṣṇa’⋅nāma gaura karilā pracāra

vāsudeva ghoṣa bale kari’ joḍa hātajei gaura, sei kṛṣṇa, sei jagannātha
IDX.en_translation:
All glories to Jagannātha, the Son of Śacī! The three worlds worship His feet.

In Nīlācala, He is the Holder of conch, disc, club, and lotus; in the town of Nadīyā, He is the Holder of the staff and water-pot.

Someone says, “Previously He killed Rāvaṇa.” He manifested the opulent pastimes of Goloka.

Now, in the mood of Śrī Rādhā, He is the Golden Avatar; Gaura preached the Name “Hare Kṛṣṇa”.

Vāsudeva Ghoṣa says with folded hands: He who is Gaura is Kṛṣṇa, is Jagannātha.

IDX.first_line: jaya jaya mahaprabhu jaya gaura candra
IDX.search: jaya jaya mahaprabhu jaya gaura candra aya visvambhara jaya karunara sindhu jaya sacisuta jaya pandita nimani jaya misra purandara jaya saci ai
IDX.author: kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa
IDX.verses:
jaya jaya mahāprabhu jaya gaura⋅candra
aya viśvambhara jaya karuṇāra sindhu

jaya śacīsūta jaya paṇḍita nimāñi
jaya miśra purandara jaya śacī āi

jaya jaya navadvīpa jaya suradhunī
jaya lakṣmī viṣṇupriyā prabhura gṛhiṇī

jaya jaya navadvīpavāsī⋅bhaktagaṇa
jaya jaya nityānanda advaita⋅caraṇa

nityānanda pada dvandva sadā kari āśa
nāma saŋkīrtana gāila kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa
IDX.en_translation:
All glories to Mahāprabhu! All glories to Gaura⋅candra! All glories to Viśvambhara, the maintainer of the universe and the ocean of mercy!

All glories to the Son of Mother Śacī, the learned Paṇḍita Nimāi! All glories to Jagannātha Miśra and Mother Śacī!

All glories to Navadvīpa and the River Ganges! All glories to Lakṣmī-devī and Viṣṇupriyā-devī, the wives of the Lord.

All glories to the residents of Navadvīpa and all the devotees! All glories to the lotus feet of Lord Nityānanda and Advaita Ācārya!

I always desire the twin lotus feet of Lord Nityānanda. Kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa sings this congregational chanting of the Holy Name.

IDX.first_line: jaya jaya nandasuta vrajakulapati
IDX.search: jaya jaya nandasuta vrajakulapati jaya jaya jadunatha tribhuvana gati jaya jaya jagatanivasa hrsikesa jaya jaya bhaktakula nalini dinesa
IDX.author: śrīla raghunātha bhāgabatācārya
IDX.verses:
jaya jaya nandasuta vrajakulapati
jaya jaya jadunātha tribhuvana⋅gati

jaya jaya jagatanivāsa hṛṣīkeśa
jaya jaya bhaktakula⋅nalinī⋅dineśa

jaya jaya kamalā⋅lālita⋅padadvandva
jaya jaya munīndra⋅mānasa⋅sukhānanda

jaya jaya guṇanidhi, jaya dayāmaya
jaya jaya bhakatavatsala rasamaya

jaya jaya mahābhaya⋅durita⋅bhañjana
jaya jaya paracaṇḍa, pāṣaṇḍa⋅mardana

jaya jaya asura⋅kuñjara⋅mahāsiŋha
jaya jaya vrajavadhu⋅mukhapadma⋅bhṛṅga

jaya jaya jogendra⋅mānasa⋅paramahaṁsa
jaya bhakta⋅bhavapatha⋅parikrama⋅dhvaṁsa

jaya jaya jagatamaṅgala guṇadhāma
jaya jaya śrutivāṇī⋅agocara nāma

jaya pūrṇabrahma kṛṣṇa vicitra⋅vihāra
jaya jagannātha nīlācala⋅avatāra

jaya jaya śrī⋅gaurāṅga caitanya⋅mūrati
premabhaktidātā prabhu bhakatera gati
IDX.en_translation:
All glories to the Son of Nanda, the Master of the family of Vraja! All glories to the Lord of the Yadus, the ultimate destination of the three worlds!

All glories to the Abode of the universe, the Master of the senses! All glories to Him who is like the sun for the lotus-like devotees!

All glories to Him whose lotus feet are caressed by the Goddess of Fortune! All glories to the blissful joy of the minds of the great sages!

All glories to the Ocean of good qualities! All glories to the Merciful One! All glories to Him who is affectionate to His devotees and full of divine mellows!

All glories to the Destroyer of great fear and sin! All glories to the fierce Subduer of atheists!

All glories to the great Lion who defeats the elephant-like demons! All glories to the Honeybee for the lotus-like faces of the gopīs!

All glories to the Swan in the minds of the great yogīs! All glories to the Destroyer of the cycle of material existence for the devotees!

All glories to the Auspiciousness of the universe, the Abode of all good qualities! All glories to Him whose Name is beyond the reach of the Vedas!

All glories to the complete Absolute Truth, Lord Kṛṣṇa, who performs wonderful pastimes! All glories to Lord Jagannātha, the incarnation of Nīlācala!

All glories to Śrī Gaurāṅga, the personification of consciousness! He is the Bestower of loving devotion and the only shelter of the devotees.

IDX.first_line: jaya jaya nityananda rohini kumara
IDX.search: jaya jaya nityananda rohini kumara patita uddhara lagi dubahu pasara gada gada madhura madhura adho bola jare dekhe tare preme dhari deya kola
IDX.author: śrīla vṛndāvana⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
jaya jaya nityānanda rohiṇī⋅kumāra
patita uddhāra lāgi’ du’bāhu pasāra

gada gada madhura madhura ādho bola
jā’re dekhe tā’re preme dhari’ deya kola

ḍagamaga locana ghuraye nirantara
sonāra kamale jena phiraye bhramara

dayāra ṭhākura nitāi para⋅duḥkha jāne
hari-nāmera mālā gā̐thi’ dila jagajane

pāpī⋅pāṣaṇḍī jata karila dalana
dīna⋅hīna⋅jane kailā prema vitaraṇa

‘āhā re gaurāṅga’⋅bali’ paḍe bhūmitale
śarīra bhijila nitāira nayanera jale

vṛndāvana⋅dāsa mane ei vicārila
dharaṇī upare kibā sumeru paḍila
IDX.en_translation:
Glory, glory to Nityānanda, the Son of Rohiṇī! He spreads His two arms to deliver the fallen.

Choked up, sweet, sweet broken words; whomever He sees, He holds him with love and embraces him.

His overwhelmed eyes roll constantly; as if a bee wanders in a golden lotus.

The merciful Lord Nitāi knows the pain of others; He strung a garland of the Holy Name and gave it to the people of the world.

He crushed all the sinners and atheists; He distributed love to the lowly and destitute people.

Saying "Alas! O Gaurāṅga!", He falls to the ground; Nitāi's body became wet with the water of His eyes.

Vṛndāvana Dāsa considered this in his mind: Has Sumeru fallen upon the earth?

IDX.first_line: jaya jaya nityanandadvaita gauranganitai gauranga jaya jaya nitai gauranga
IDX.search: jaya jaya nityanandadvaita gauranganitai gauranga jaya jaya nitai gauranga jaya jasoda nandana saci suta gaura candrajaya rohininandana balarama nityananda
IDX.verses:
jaya jaya nityānandādvaita gaurāṅganitāi gaurāṅga jaya, jaya nitāi gaurāṅga

(jaya) jaśodā⋅nandana śacī⋅suta gaura⋅candra(jaya) rohiṇī-nandana balarāma nityānanda

(jaya) mahāviṣṇura avatāra śrī⋅advaitacandra(jaya) gadādhara, śrīvāsādi gaura-bhakta-vṛnda

(jaya) svarūpa, rūpa, sanātana, rāya⋅rāmānanda(jaya) khaṇḍa-vāsī narahari, murāri, mukunda

(jaya) pañcaputra saṅge nāce rāya⋅bhavānanda(jaya) tina putra saṅge nāce sena⋅śivānanda

(jaya) dvādaśa gopāla āra cauṣaṭṭi mahānta(tomarā) kṛpā kari’ deha gaura⋅caraṇāravinda
IDX.en_translation:
All glories, all glories to Nityānanda, Advaita, and Gaurāṅga! Glory to Nitāi-Gaurāṅga! Glory to Nitāi-Gaurāṅga!

Glory to the Son of Yaśodā, the Son of Śacī, Gaura⋅candra! Glory to the Son of Rohiṇī, Balarāma, Nityānanda!

Glory to the Avatar of Mahāviṣṇu, Śrī Advaitacandra! Glory to Gadādhara, Śrīvāsa, and the group of Gaura’s devotees!

Glory to Svarūpa, Rūpa, Sanātana, Rāya Rāmānanda! Glory to the residents of Khaṇḍa—Narahari, Murāri, Mukunda!

Glory to Rāya Bhavānanda, who dances with five sons! Glory to Sena Śivānanda, who dances with three sons!

Glory to the twelve Gopālas and the sixty-four Mahantas! All of you, mercifully give the lotus feet of Gaura.

IDX.first_line: jaya jaya prabhupadera arati neharijogamayapura nityaseva danakari
IDX.search: jaya jaya prabhupadera arati neharijogamayapura nityaseva danakari sarvatra pracara dhupa saurabha manoharabaddha mukta alikula mugdha caracara
IDX.author: śrīmadbhaktiprajñāna keśava gosvāmī mahārāja
IDX.verses:
jaya jaya prabhupādera ārati nehārijogamāyāpura⋅nityasevā⋅dānakārī

sarvatra pracāra⋅dhūpa saurabha manoharabaddha⋅mukta alikula mugdha carācara

bhakati⋅siddhānta⋅dīpa jvāliyā jagatepañcarasa⋅sevā⋅śikhā pradīpta tāhāte

pañca mahādīpa jathā pañca mahājyotiḥtriloka⋅timira nāśe avidyā⋅durmati

bhakativinoda⋅dhārā jala⋅śaṅkha⋅dhāraniravadhi vahe tāhā rodha nāhi āra

sarvavādyamayī ghaṇṭā bāje sarvakālabṛhat⋅mṛdaṅga⋅vādya parama rasāla

viśāla lalāṭe śobhe tilaka ujjvalagaladeśe tulasī-mālā kare jhalamala

ājānulambita bāhu dīrgha kalevarataptakāñcana⋅varaṇa parama sundara

lalita⋅lāvaṇyamukhe sneha⋅bharā hāsiaṅgakānti śobhe jaiche nitya⋅pūrṇaśaśī

jatidharme paridhāne aruṇa vasanamukta kaila meghāvṛta gauḍīya⋅gagana

bhakati⋅kusume kata kuñja viracitasaundarya⋅saurabhe tāra viśva⋅vimohita

sevādarśe narahari cāmara ḍhulāyakeśava ati ānande nīrājana gāya
IDX.en_translation:
All glories, all glories! Beholding the ārati of Prabhupāda, who bestows eternal service at Yogapīṭha in Māyāpura.

The enchanting fragrance of his preaching acts like incense, captivating the bees of the bound and liberated souls, and the entire moving and non-moving world.

Igniting the lamp of Bhaktisiddhānta (devotional conclusions) in the world, the flame of service in the five mellows shines brightly within it.

Just as the five great lamps serve as five great lights, they destroy the darkness of the three worlds—ignorance and wicked intelligence.

The current of Bhaktivinoda flows like water from a conchshell; it flows incessantly, with no obstruction.

The bell, consisting of all instruments, rings at all times; the great mṛdaṅga plays, supremely sweet.

On his broad forehead shines a bright tilaka, and a necklace of tulasī beads glitters on his neck.

His long arms reach down to his knees, his body is tall, and his complexion is like molten gold—supremely beautiful.

A smile filled with affection is on his charming, graceful face; his bodily effulgence shines like the eternal full moon.

Wearing the saffron cloth of the renounced order, he cleared the cloud-covered Gauḍīya sky.

How many groves he has created with the flowers of devotion; the whole universe is enchanted by their beauty and fragrance.

In the vision of service, Narahari waves the cāmara, and Keśava sings the nīrājana with great joy.

IDX.first_line: jaya jaya radha krsna yugala milanaarati karaye lalitadi sakhigana
IDX.search: jaya jaya radha krsna yugala milanaarati karaye lalitadi sakhigana madanamohana rupa tribhangasundarapitambara sikhipuccha cuda manohara
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
jaya jaya rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa⋅yugala milanaārati karaye lalitādi sakhī-gaṇa

madanamohana⋅rūpa tribhaṅgasundarapītāmbara śikhipuccha cūḍā⋅manohara

lalitamādhava⋅bāme vṛṣabhānu⋅kanyānīlavasanā gaurī rūpe guṇe dhanyā

nānāvidha alaṅkāra kare jhalamalaharimano⋅vimohana vadana ujjvala

viśākhādi sakhī-gaṇa nānā rāge gāyapriya-narma-sakhī jata cāmara ḍhulāya

śrī⋅rādhā⋅mādhava⋅pada⋅sarasija⋅āśebhakativinoda sakhī-pade sukhe bhāse
IDX.en_translation:
All glories, all glories to the union of the Divine Couple, Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa! Lalitā and the other friends perform the āratī.

Kṛṣṇa, whose form enchants even Cupid, is beautiful with His three-fold bending posture. He is adorned with yellow garments and a charming crest of peacock feathers.

To the left of charming Mādhava stands the daughter of Vṛṣabhānu. Dressed in blue garments, fair-complexioned, she is blessed with beauty and excellent qualities.

Various kinds of ornaments glitter. Their radiant faces captivate Hari’s mind.

Viśākhā and the other friends sing in various melodies. All the intimate joking friends wave the whisks.

With hope for the lotus feet of Śrī Rādhā and Mādhava, Bhaktivinoda joyfully floats in the mood of a female friend.

IDX.first_line: jaya jaya radha madhava
IDX.search: jaya jaya radha madhava radha madhava radhe jayadevera pranadhana he jaya jaya radha madanagopala radha madanagopala radhe sitanathera pranadhana he
IDX.verses:
jaya jaya rādhā⋅mādhava
rādhā⋅mādhava rādhe
(jayadevera prāṇa-dhana he)

jaya jaya rādhā⋅madanagopāla
rādhā⋅madanagopāla rādhe
(sītānāthera prāṇa-dhana he)

jaya jaya rādhā⋅govinda
rādhā⋅govinda rādhe
(rūpa gosvāmīra prāṇa-dhana he)

jaya jaya rādhā⋅madanamohana
rādhā⋅madanamohana rādhe
(sanātanera prāṇa-dhana he)

jaya jaya rādhā⋅gopīnātha
rādhā⋅gopīnātha rādhe
(madhu paṇḍitera prāṇa-dhana he)

jaya jaya rādhā⋅dāmodara
rādhā⋅dāmodara rādhe
(jīva gosvāmīra prāṇa-dhana he)

jaya jaya rādhā⋅ramaṇa
rādhā⋅ramaṇa rādhe
(gopālabhaṭṭera prāṇa-dhana he)

jaya jaya rādhāvinoda
rādhāvinoda rādhe
(lokanāthera prāṇa-dhana he)

jaya jaya rādhā⋅giridhārī
rādhā⋅giridhārī rādhe
(dāsa gosvāmīra prāṇa-dhana he)

jaya jaya rādhā⋅śyāmasundara
rādhā⋅śyāmasundara rādhe
(śyāmānandera prāṇa-dhana he)

jaya jaya rādhākānta
rādhākānta rādhe
(vakreśvarera prāṇa-dhana he)

jaya jaya gāndharvikā⋅giridhārī
gāndharvikā⋅giridhārī rādhe
(śrīla bhaktisiddhānta sarasvatī prabhupādera prāṇa-dhana he)

jaya jaya rādhā⋅vinoda⋅vihārī
rādhā⋅vinoda⋅vihārī rādhe
(śrīla bhaktiprajñāna keśava gosvāmī mahārājera prāṇa-dhana he)

jaya jaya rādhā⋅ramaṇa⋅vihārī
rādhā⋅ramaṇa⋅vihārī rādhe
(śrīla bhaktivedānta nārāyaṇa gosvāmī mahārājera prāṇa-dhana he)
IDX.en_translation:
All glories to Rādhā-Mādhava, the life-treasure of Jayadeva Gosvāmī! All glories to Rādhā-Mādhava!

All glories to Rādhā-Madanagopāla, the life-treasure of Advaita Ācārya! All glories to Rādhā-Madanagopāla!

All glories to Rādhā-Govinda, the life-treasure of Rūpa Gosvāmī! All glories to Rādhā-Govinda!

All glories to Rādhā-Madanamohana, the life-treasure of Sanātana Gosvāmī! All glories to Rādhā-Madanamohana!

All glories to Rādhā-Gopīnātha, the life-treasure of Madhu Paṇḍita! All glories to Rādhā-Gopīnātha!

All glories to Rādhā-Dāmodara, the life-treasure of Jīva Gosvāmī! All glories to Rādhā-Dāmodara!

All glories to Rādhā-Ramaṇa, the life-treasure of Gopāla Bhaṭṭa Gosvāmī! All glories to Rādhā-Ramaṇa!

All glories to Rādhā-Vinoda, the life-treasure of Lokanātha Gosvāmī! All glories to Rādhā-Vinoda!

All glories to Rādhā-Giridhārī, the life-treasure of Raghunātha Dāsa Gosvāmī! All glories to Rādhā-Giridhārī!

All glories to Rādhā-Śyāmasundara, the life-treasure of Śyāmānanda Prabhu! All glories to Rādhā-Śyāmasundara!

All glories to Rādhākānta, the life-treasure of Vakreśvara Paṇḍita! All glories to Rādhākānta!

All glories to Gāndharvikā-Giridhārī, the life-treasure of Śrīla Bhaktisiddhānta Sarasvatī Prabhupāda!

All glories to Rādhā-Vinoda-Bihārī, the life-treasure of Śrīla Bhakti Prajñāna Keśava Gosvāmī Mahārāja!

All glories to Rādhā-Ramaṇa-Bihārī, the life-treasure of Śrīla Bhaktivedānta Nārāyaṇa Gosvāmī Mahārāja!

IDX.first_line: jaya jaya radha ramana haribolajaya jaya radha ramana haribolanata nagara navala chaila rasiyapyaro kanha hai mero mana basiyakare kalindi kula kilolajaya jaya radha ramana haribola
IDX.search: jaya jaya radha ramana haribolajaya jaya radha ramana haribolanata nagara navala chaila rasiyapyaro kanha hai mero mana basiyakare kalindi kula kilolajaya jaya radha ramana haribola
IDX.verses: jaya jaya rādhā⋅ramaṇa haribola,jaya jaya rādhā⋅ramaṇa haribolanaṭa nāgara navala chaila rasiyā,pyāro kānhā hai mero mana basiyākare kālindī kūla kīlola,jaya jaya rādhā⋅ramaṇa haribola
IDX.en_translation: Victory, victory to Rādhā-ramaṇa! Chant the Holy Name of Lord Hari! Victory, victory to Rādhā-ramaṇa! Chant the Holy Name of Lord Hari! He is the expert dancer, the clever lover, the ever-fresh youth, and the enjoyer of divine mellows. My beloved Lord Kṛṣṇa resides in my heart. He performs playful pastimes on the banks of the Yamunā. Victory, victory to Rādhā-ramaṇa! Chant the Holy Name of Lord Hari!

IDX.first_line: jaya jaya radhe krsna govinda
IDX.search: jaya jaya radhe krsna govinda radhe govinda radhe govinda jaya jaya syamasundara madanamohana vrndavana candra jaya jaya radha ramana rasavihari sri gokulananda
IDX.verses:
jaya jaya rādhe kṛṣṇa govinda
rādhe govinda rādhe govinda

jaya jaya śyāmasundara madanamohana
vṛndāvana⋅candra
jaya jaya rādhā⋅ramaṇa rāsavihārī
śrī⋅gokulānanda

jaya jaya rāseśvarī vinodinī
bhānukūlacandra
jaya jaya lalitā⋅viśākhā ādi
jata sakhīvṛnda

jaya jaya śrī⋅rūpamañjarī rati⋅
mañjarī⋅anaṅga
jaya jaya paurṇamāsī jogamāyā
jaya vīrāvṛnda

sabe mili’ kara kṛpā
āmi ati manda
(tomarā) kṛpā kari’ deha’ jugala⋅
caraṇāravinda
IDX.en_translation:
All glories, all glories to Rādhe, Kṛṣṇa, Govinda! Rādhe Govinda, Rādhe Govinda!

All glories, all glories to Śyāmasundara, Madana-mohana, the Moon of Vṛndāvana! All glories, all glories to Rādhā-ramaṇa, Rāsa-vihārī, the bliss of Gokula!

All glories, all glories to the Queen of the Rāsa, Vinodinī, the Moon of the family of Bhānu! All glories, all glories to the multitude of sakhīs headed by Lalitā and Viśākhā!

All glories, all glories to Śrī Rūpa Mañjarī, Rati Mañjarī, Anaṅga Mañjarī! All glories, all glories to Paurṇamāsī, Yogamāyā! Glory to Vīrā and her group!

All of you together be merciful; I am very wicked. Please be merciful and give me the lotus feet of the Couple.

IDX.first_line: jaya jaya sri advaita patita pavana
IDX.search: jaya jaya sri advaita patita pavana mui saba patitera karaha mocana krsna na karena jara sankalpa anyatha je karite pare krsna saksat sarvatha
IDX.author: śrīla vṛndāvana⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
“jaya jaya śrī⋅advaita patita⋅pāvana
mui saba patitera karaha mocana

kṛṣṇa nā karena jā̐ra saṅkalpa anyathā
je karite pāre kṛṣṇa⋅sākṣāt sarvathā

kṛṣṇa⋅candre jā̐ra vākya karena pālana
se tomā⋅smaraṇe sarvabandha⋅vimocana

jama, kāla, mṛtyu jā̐ra ājñā śire dhare
jā̐ra pada vāñche jogeśvare munīśvare

sei tomā hena jane ke jāne saṁsāre
tumi kṛpā karile se bhaktiphala dhare

bhaktira bhāṇḍārī tumi, vine bhakti dile
kṛṣṇa⋅bhakti, kṛṣṇabhakta, kṛṣṇa kāre mile

amāyāya kṛṣṇa⋅bhakti deha he⋅āmāre
janma janma āra jena kṛṣṇa pāsare

advaita⋅caraṇe mora ei namaskāra
tāna priya, tā̐he mati rahuka āmāra”
IDX.en_translation:
All glories, all glories to Śrī Advaita, the patita-pāvana! Please deliver all of us who are fallen.

Kṛṣṇa never allows His resolve to be otherwise; He can directly manifest Kṛṣṇa in all respects.

Whose words Kṛṣṇa⋅candra obeys; by remembering You, all bondage is released.

Whose command Yama, Kāla, and Mṛtyu obey; whose lotus feet are desired by the greatest yogeśvaras and munīśvaras.

Who in this world can truly know such a person as You? Only by Your mercy can one attain the fruit of devotion.

You are the treasurer of devotion; without Your giving devotion, who can attain Kṛṣṇa-bhakti, a Kṛṣṇa-bhakta, or Kṛṣṇa Himself?

Please sincerely bestow upon me Kṛṣṇa-bhakti, so that in birth after birth, I may never forget Kṛṣṇa.

To the lotus feet of Advaita, I offer this namaskāra; may my mind remain fixed on Him, who is so dear.

IDX.first_line: jaya jaya sri caitanya patita pavana
IDX.search: jaya jaya sri caitanya patita pavana jaya nityananda prabhu anatha tarana jaya jayadvaitacandra krpara sagara jaya rupa sanatana jaya gadadhara
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
jaya jaya śrī⋅caitanya patita⋅pāvana,
jaya nityānanda prabhu anātha⋅taraṇa

jaya jayādvaitacandra kṛpāra sāgara
jaya rūpa⋅sanātana jaya gadādhara

śrī⋅jīva gopāla⋅bhaṭṭa raghunātha⋅dvaya
jaya vraja⋅dhāma⋅vāsī vaiṣṇava⋅nicoya

jaya jaya navadvīpa⋅vāsī bhakta⋅gaṇa
sabe mili’ kṛpā more kara vitaraṇa

nikhila⋅vaiṣṇava⋅jana dayā prakāśiyā
śrī⋅jāhnavā⋅pade more rākhaha ṭāniyā

āmi to’ durbhaga ati vaiṣṇava nā cini
more kṛpā karibena vaiṣṇava āpani

śrī⋅guru⋅caraṇe more bhakti kara dāna
je caraṇa⋅bale pāi tattvera sandhāna

brāhmaṇa sakala kari’ kṛpā mora prati
vaiṣṇava⋅caraṇe more deha dṛḍha⋅mati

ucca nīca sarva⋅jīva caraṇe śaraṇa
loilāma āmi dīna hīna akiñcana

sakalake kariyā kṛpā deha’ more vara
vaiṣṇave karuṇā ei granthera ādara

grantha⋅dvārā vaiṣṇava⋅janera kṛpā pāi
vaiṣṇava⋅kṛpāya kṛṣṇa⋅lābha haya bhāi

vaiṣṇava⋅vimukha jā’re tāhāra jīvana
nirarthaka jāna’ bhāi prasiddha vacana

śrī⋅vaikuṇṭha⋅dhāme āche niḥśreyasa vana
tāhe śobhā pāya kalpa⋅taru agaṇana

tāhā mājhe e kalyāṇa⋅kalpa⋅taru⋅rāja
nitya⋅kāla nitya⋅dhāme karena virāja

skandha⋅traya āche tā’ra apūrva darśana
upadeśa upalabdhi ucchvāsa gaṇana

su⋅bhakti⋅prasūna tāhe ati śobhā pāya
kalyāṇa nāmaka phala agaṇana tāya

je sujana e viṭapī karena āśraya
kṛṣṇa⋅sevā⋅su⋅kalyāṇa⋅phala tā̐’ra haya

śrī⋅guru⋅caraṇa⋅kṛpā⋅sāmarthya labhiyā
e hena apūrva vṛkṣa dilāma āniyā

ṭāniyā ānite vṛkṣa e karkaśa mana
naśilo ihāra śobhā śuno sādhu⋅jana

tomāra sakala ha⋅o e vṛkṣera mālī
śraddhā⋅vari diyā punaḥ kara rūpaśālī

phalibe kalyāṇa⋅phala jugala⋅sevana
kariba sakale mili’ tāhā āsvādana

nṛtya kari’ hari bala’ khāo sevā⋅phala
bhakti⋅bale kara’ dūra kutarka⋅anala
IDX.en_translation:
All glories, all glories to Śrī-caitanya, the savior of the fallen! All glories to Nityānanda Prabhu, the deliverer of the helpless!

All glories, all glories to Advaitacandra!

All glories to Śrī Jīva residing in Braja-dhāma!

All glories, all glories to the devotee community residing in Navadvīpa! All of you together, please bestow your mercy upon me.

By manifesting mercy, all Vaiṣṇavas, Please pull me and keep me at the feet of Śrī Jāhnavā Devī.

I am very unfortunate and do not recognize any Vaiṣṇava; May you, the Vaiṣṇavas, show mercy upon me.

Please bestow devotion upon me at the feet of my Śrī-guru, By the strength of those feet, I obtain the understanding of truth.

May all Brāhmaṇas show mercy towards me, And grant me firm faith at the feet of the Vaiṣṇavas.

High and low, all living entities take shelter at their feet, I, who am poor, fallen, and insignificant, have taken that shelter.

All of you, showing mercy, please grant me this boon: Compassion towards Vaiṣṇavas, and adoration for this book.

Through this book, I receive the mercy of the Vaiṣṇavas, the attainment of Kṛṣṇa occurs, O brother!

Know, O brother, that the life of one who is averse to Vaiṣṇavas Is useless; this is a famous saying.

In Śrī-Vaikuṇṭha-dhāma there is a forest of supreme welfare, In which innumerable wish-fulfilling trees shine beautifully.

Among them, this king of wish-fulfilling trees named Kalyāṇa, Eternally resides in the eternal abode, shining brightly.

It has three branches, a wonderful sight: Instruction, realization, and expression of ecstasy.

Beautiful flowers of devotion greatly enhance its beauty, And innumerable fruits named Kalyāṇa are found there.

For any good person who takes shelter of this tree, The excellent fruit of service to Kṛṣṇa becomes his.

Having obtained the power of the mercy of the feet of my Śrī-guru, I have brought such an uncommon tree here.

To pull and bring this tree, my harsh mind Destroyed its beauty; please listen, O saintly people!

May all of you become the gardeners of this tree, And by offering the water of faith, make it beautiful again.

It will bear the auspicious fruit of yugala-sevana; We will all together relish that.

Dance, chant 'Hari', and partake of the fruit of service; By the strength of devotion, drive far away the fire of bad arguments.

IDX.first_line: jaya jaya sri krsnacaitanya dayasindhu
IDX.search: jaya jaya sri krsnacaitanya dayasindhu patita uddhara hetu jaya dinabandhu jaya premabhaktidata daya kara more dante trna dhari dake e dasa pamare
IDX.author: rādhāmohana dāsa
IDX.verses:
jaya jaya śrī⋅kṛṣṇacaitanya dayāsindhu
patita uddhāra hetu jaya dīna-bandhu

jaya premabhaktidātā, dayā kara more
dante tṛṇa dhari’ ḍāke e⋅dāsa pāmare

pūrvete sākṣāt jata pātakī tārilā
se vicitra nahe jāte avatāra kailā

mo⋅hena pāpiṣṭhe ebe karaha uddhāra
āścarya dayāla⋅guṇa ghuṣuka saṁsāra

vicāra karile muñi nahe dayāpātra
āpana svabhāva⋅guṇe karaha kṛtārtha

viśeṣa pratijñā śuni’ ei kaliyuge
ei bharasāya rādhāmohana dayā māge
IDX.en_translation:
All glories, all glories to Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya, an ocean of mercy! All glories to the Friend of the distressed, for delivering the fallen!

All glories to the giver of loving devotion! Please bestow Your mercy upon me. This fallen servant calls out, holding a blade of grass in his teeth.

In the past, You directly delivered many sinful persons. This is not surprising, for You have appeared as an incarnation specifically for that purpose.

Now, please deliver a wretch as sinful as me. Let Your astonishingly merciful qualities be proclaimed throughout the entire world.

Upon reflection, I am not worthy of Your mercy. Please fulfill me by Your own merciful nature and divine qualities.

Hearing Your special promise in this Age of Kali, and with this hope alone, Rādhāmohana begs for Your mercy.

IDX.first_line: jaya jaya sri krsnacaitanya nityanandajayadvaitacandra jaya gaurabhaktavrnda
IDX.search: jaya jaya sri krsnacaitanya nityanandajayadvaitacandra jaya gaurabhaktavrnda krpa kari sabe mili karaha karunaadhama patitajane na kariha ghrna
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
jaya jaya śrī⋅kṛṣṇacaitanya⋅nityānandajayādvaita-candra jaya gaura-bhakta-vṛnda

kṛpā kari’ sabe mili karaha karuṇāadhama patita-jane nā kariha ghṛṇā

e⋅tina saṁsāra⋅mājhe tuyā⋅pada sārabhāviyā dekhinu mane⋅gati nāhi āra

se pada pā’bāra āśe kheda uṭhe manevyākula hṛdaye sadā kariye krandane

kirūpe pāiba, kichu nā pāi sandhānaprabhu lokanātha⋅pada nāhika smaraṇa

tumi ta’ dayāla prabhu, cāha ekabāranarottama⋅hṛdayera ghucāo andhakāra
IDX.en_translation:
All glories to Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya and Nityānanda! All glories to Advaitacandra, and all glories to the assembly of Lord Gaura’s devotees!

Please, all of you together, bestow your mercy; do not disdain this fallen and degraded soul.

In these three worlds, Your lotus feet are the essence; reflecting in my mind, I see that there is no other shelter.

In the hope of attaining those feet, distress arises in my mind; with an anxious heart, I always lament.

How will I attain them? I find no way. I cannot even remember the lotus feet of Prabhu Lokanātha.

You are indeed a merciful Lord; please glance upon me just once and remove the darkness from Narottama’s heart.

IDX.first_line: jaya jaya sri srivasa thakura pandita
IDX.search: jaya jaya sri srivasa thakura pandita narada kheyati jara bhuvana vidita ki kahiba srivasera udara caritra tribhuvana haya jara smarane pavitra
IDX.author: śrīla vṛndāvana⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
jaya jaya śrī⋅śrīvāsa ṭhākura paṇḍita
nārada⋅kheyāti jā’ra bhuvana vidita

“ki kahiba śrīvāsera udāra caritra
tribhuvana haya jāra smaraṇe pavitra

satya sevilena caitanyere śrīnivāsa
jā̐ra ghare caitanyera sakala vilāsa

kṛṣṇa⋅avatāra jena vasudeva⋅ghare
jateka vihāra saba⋅nandera mandire

jagannātha⋅ghare haila gaura⋅avatāra
śrīvāsa⋅paṇḍita⋅gṛhe jateka vihāra

sarvavaiṣṇavera priya paṇḍita śrīvāsa
tāna bāḍī gele mātra sabāra ullāsa

ki kahiba śrīvāsera bhāgyera mahimā
jā̐ra dāsadāsīra bhāgyera nāhi sīmā

cāri vede jā̐re dekhibāre abhilāṣa
tāhā dekhe śrīvāsera jata dāsī⋅dāsa

putraśoka nā jānila je gaurāṅga⋅preme
bhāse āro śrī⋅kṛṣṇera saṅkīrtana⋅vāṇe

e saba saṁsāra⋅duḥkha tā̐hāra ki dāya
je tā̐hāre smare seha kabhu nāhi pāya

śrīvāsera caraṇe rahuka namaskāra
gaura⋅candra⋅nityānanda⋅nandana jā̐hāra’’
IDX.en_translation:
All glories, all glories to Śrī Śrīvāsa Ṭhākura Paṇḍita, whose fame is known throughout the world like that of Nārada.

What can I say about the magnanimous character of Śrīvāsa? By remembering him, the three worlds become pure.

Indeed, Śrīnivāsa served Caitanya. In his house were all the pastimes of Caitanya.

Just as the incarnation of Kṛṣṇa was in Vasudeva's house, but all His many pastimes were in Nanda's temple.

In Jagannātha's house was the incarnation of Gaura, but all His many pastimes were in Śrīvāsa Paṇḍita's home.

Śrīvāsa is dear to all Vaiṣṇavas. Just by going to his house, everyone becomes joyful.

What can I say about the glory of Śrīvāsa's fortune? There is no limit to the fortune of his servants and maidservants.

He whom the four Vedas desire to see, Him see all the servants and maidservants of Śrīvāsa.

He did not feel the sorrow of his son in that love for Gaurāṅga. He rather floated in the current of Śrī Kṛṣṇa's saṅkīrtana.

What concern are all these worldly miseries to him? Whoever remembers him also never experiences them.

Let my obeisances remain at the feet of Śrīvāsa, he whose sons are Gaura⋅candra and Nityānanda.

IDX.first_line: jaya jaya sriguru prema kalpataruadbhuta jako parakasahiya ageyana timiravara jnana sucandra kirane karu nasa
IDX.search: jaya jaya sriguru prema kalpataruadbhuta jako parakasahiya ageyana timiravara jnana sucandra kirane karu nasa iha locana ananda dhamaayacita mo hena patita heri jo pahujaci deyala harinama
IDX.author: vaiṣṇavadāsa
IDX.verses:
jaya jaya śrī-guru, prema⋅kalpataru,adbhuta jā̐ko parakāśahiyā ageyāna, timiravara jñāna⋅,sucandra kiraṇe karu nāśa

iha locana ānanda⋅dhāmaayācita mo⋅hena, patita heri jo pahu̐,jāci deyala harināma

duramati agati, satata asate mati,nāhi sukṛti laba leśaśrī-vṛndāvana, jugala⋅bhajana⋅dhana,tāhe karala upadeśa

niramala⋅gaura⋅, premarasa siñcane,pūrala jagajana āśaso caraṇāmbuje, rati nāhi hoyala,royata vaiṣṇava⋅dāsa
IDX.en_translation:
All glories to the spiritual master, the wish-fulfilling tree of divine love, whose manifestation is wondrous. The darkness of ignorance in the heart is destroyed by the rays of the beautiful moon of his knowledge.

He is the abode of joy for these eyes. Without being asked, and seeing a fallen soul like me, he mercifully gave the Holy Name.

I am of wicked mind and without any goal; my thoughts are always fixed on the mundane. I do not possess even a trace of pious merit. Yet he gave me the instruction to attain Vṛndāvana and the treasure of worshiping the Divine Couple.

By sprinkling the pure nectar of divine love, he fulfilled the hopes of the people of the world. In those lotus feet I have no attachment—so cries Vaiṣṇava dāsa.

IDX.first_line: jaya jaya sundara nanda kumara
IDX.search: jaya jaya sundara nanda kumara saurabha sankata vrndavana tata vihita vasanta vihara abhinava kutmala guccha samujjvala kuncita kuntala bhara pranayi janerita vandana sahakrta curnita vara ghanasara
IDX.author: śrīla rūpa gosvāmī
IDX.verses:
jaya jaya sundara nanda⋅kumāra
saurabha⋅saṅkaṭa⋅, vṛndāvana⋅taṭa⋅,
vihita⋅vasanta⋅vihāra

abhinava⋅kuṭmala⋅, guccha⋅samujjvala⋅,
kuñcita⋅kuntala⋅bhāra
praṇayi⋅janerita⋅, vandana⋅sahakṛta⋅,
cūrṇita⋅vara⋅ghanasāra

caṭula⋅digañcala⋅, racita⋅rasocchala⋅,
rādhā⋅madana⋅vikāra
bhuvana⋅vimohana⋅, mañjula⋅nartana,
gati⋅valgita⋅maṇihāra

adhara⋅virājita⋅, mandatara⋅smita⋅,
lobhita⋅nija⋅parivāra
nija⋅vallavajana⋅, suhṛt⋅sanātana⋅,
citta-viharadavatāra
IDX.en_translation:
Glory, glory to the beautiful Nanda-kumāra (son of Nanda)! On the banks of Vṛndāvana, filled with fragrance, He performs His spring pastimes (vihāra).

His curly hair is adorned with fresh buds and radiant bunches of flowers. He is worshipped by His beloved devotees and powdered with excellent camphor.

His playful glances, overflowing with sweet mellows (rasa), stir the Cupid (Madana) of Rādhā. His beautiful dance enchants the world, and His jewel necklace sways with His movements.

A gentle smile graces His lips, captivating His own family (parivāra). He is the delightful avatāra (descent) who resides in the hearts of His own cowherd people (vallavajana) and eternal friends (suhṛt sanātana).

IDX.first_line: jaya jaya vallavaraja kumara
IDX.search: jaya jaya vallavaraja kumara radha vaksasi hari manihara apaghana ghatita ghusrna ghanasara pincha khacita kuncita kacabhara
IDX.author: śrīla rūpa gosvāmī
IDX.verses:
jaya jaya vallavarāja⋅kumāra
rādhā⋅vakṣasi hari⋅maṇihāra

apaghana⋅ghaṭita⋅ghusṛṇa⋅ghanasāra
piñcha⋅khacita⋅kuñcita⋅kacabhāra

rādhā⋅dhṛtihara⋅muralī⋅tāra
nayanāñcalakṛta⋅madana⋅vikāra

rasa⋅rañjita⋅rādhā⋅parivāra
kalita⋅sanātana⋅cittavihāra
IDX.en_translation:
All glories, all glories to the Prince of the cowherd king! He is the captivating jewel necklace on Rādhā's chest.

His body is smeared with musk and camphor. His curly hair is adorned with a peacock feather.

The sound of His flute steals Rādhā's patience. The corners of His eyes create the god of love's emotional disturbances.

He enchants Rādhā's companions with His mellows. He sports in the heart of Sanātana.

IDX.first_line: jaya mora mukuta pitambaradhari
IDX.search: jaya mora mukuta pitambaradhari jaya muralidhara govardhanadhari sri radhavara kunjavihari muralidhara govardhanadhari
IDX.verses:
jaya mora mukuṭa pītāmbaradhārī
jaya muralīdhara govardhanadhārī

śrī⋅rādhāvara kuñjavihārī,
muralīdhara govardhanadhārī

jaya yaśodānandana kṛṣṇa murārī,
muralīdhara govardhanadhārī

jaya gopī⋅janavallabha vaṁśīvihārī,
muralīdhara govardhanadhārī
IDX.en_translation:
All glories to the wearer of the peacock crown and yellow garments! All glories to the Holder of the flute and the Lifter of Govardhana Hill!

He is the Beloved of Śrīmati Rādhā⋅rāṇī and sports in the groves. He is the Holder of the flute and the Lifter of Govardhana Hill.

All glories to the Son of Yaśodā, Lord Kṛṣṇa, the killer of the Mura demon! He is the Holder of the flute and the Lifter of Govardhana Hill.

All glories to the Beloved of the gopīs, who plays the flute in the forest! He is the Holder of the flute and the Lifter of Govardhana Hill.

IDX.first_line: jaya nandanandana gopi jana vallabharadhanayaka nagara syamaso saci nandana nadiya purandarasura munigana manomohana dhama
IDX.search: jaya nandanandana gopi jana vallabharadhanayaka nagara syamaso saci nandana nadiya purandarasura munigana manomohana dhama jaya nijakanta kanti kalevarajaya jaya preyasi bhava vinodajaya vraja sahacari locana mangalajaya nadiyavasi nayana amoda
IDX.author: govinda dāsa
IDX.verses:
jaya nanda-nandana, gopī⋅jana⋅vallabha,rādhānāyaka nāgara śyāmaso śacī⋅nandana, nadīyā⋅purandara,sura⋅munigaṇa⋅manomohana dhāma

jaya nija-kāntā⋅, kānti kalevara,jaya jaya preyasī⋅bhāva⋅vinodajaya vraja⋅sahacarī⋅, locana⋅maṅgala,jaya nadīyāvāsi⋅nayana⋅āmoda

jaya jaya śrīdāma, sudāma⋅subalārjuna,premavardhana navaghana rūpajaya rāmādi sundara, priya sahacara,jaya jaga-mohana gaura anupa

jaya atibala bala⋅, rāma⋅priyānuja,jaya jaya śrī⋅nityānanda⋅ānandajaya jaya sajjana⋅, gaṇa⋅bhaya⋅bhañjana,govindadāsa āśa anubandha
IDX.en_translation:
All glories to the son of Nanda, the beloved of the gopīs, the hero of Rādhā, the clever dark-complexioned Lord. He is the son of Śacī, the chief of Nadiyā, and the captivating abode for the minds of gods and sages.

All glories to Him whose body possesses the luster of His own beloved; all glories to the enjoyment of His beloved’s sentiments. All glories to the auspiciousness for the eyes of the companions of Vraja; all glories to the delight for the eyes of the residents of Nadiyā.

All glories to Śrīdāma, Sudāma, Subala, and Arjuna—His form like a fresh monsoon cloud increases divine love. All glories to beautiful Balarāma and the dear companions; all glories to the incomparable Gaura, who enchants the universe.

All glories to the one of immense strength, the beloved younger brother of Balarāma; all glories to the joy of Śrī Nityānanda. All glories to the one who removes the fears of the saintly devotees; this is the hope and attachment of Govinda dāsa.

IDX.first_line: jaya radhe jaya krsna jaya vrndavana
IDX.search: jaya radhe jaya krsna jaya vrndavana srigovinda gopinatha madanamohana syama kunda radha kunda giri govardhana kalindi jamuna jaya jaya mahavana
IDX.author: kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa
IDX.verses:
jaya rādhe, jaya kṛṣṇa, jaya vṛndāvana
śrī-govinda gopīnātha madanamohana

śyāma⋅kuṇḍa rādhā⋅kuṇḍa giri⋅govardhana
kālindī jamunā jaya, jaya mahāvana

keśīghāṭa vaṁśīvaṭa dvādaśa⋅kānana
jā̐hā saba līlā kaila śrī-nanda-nandana

śrī⋅nanda⋅yaśodā jaya, jaya gopagaṇa
śrīdāmādi jaya, jaya dhenu⋅vatsagaṇa

jaya vṛṣabhānu, jaya kīrtidā⋅sundarī
jaya paurṇamāsī, jaya ābhīranāgarī

jaya jaya gopīśvara vṛndāvana⋅mājha
jaya jaya kṛṣṇa-sakhā baṭu dvijarāja

jaya rāmaghāṭa, jaya rohiṇīnandana
jaya jaya vṛndāvana-vāsī jata jana

jaya dvija-patnī, jaya nāgakanyāgaṇa
bhaktite jā̐hārā pāila govinda-caraṇa

śrī⋅rāsamaṇḍala jaya, jaya rādhāśyāma
jaya jaya rāsa-līlā sarva-manorama

jaya jayojjvalarasa sarvarasa sāra
pārakīyabhāve jāhā vrajete pracāra

śrī⋅jāhnavā⋅pāda⋅padma kariyā smaraṇa
dīna kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa kahe nāma⋅saṅkīrtana
IDX.en_translation:
Glory to Rādhā, glory to Kṛṣṇa, glory to Vṛndāvana. Śrī Govinda, Gopīnātha, Madana-mohana.

Śyāma-kuṇḍa, Rādhā-kuṇḍa, Govardhana Hill. Glory to the Kālindī Yamunā, glory to Mahāvana.

Keśī-ghāṭa, Vaṁśīvaṭa, the twelve forests. Where the son of Nanda performed all His pastimes.

Glory to Śrī Nanda and Yaśodā, glory to the cowherd men. Glory to Śrīdāma and others, glory to the cows and calves.

Glory to Vṛṣabhānu, glory to the beautiful Kīrtidā. Glory to Paurṇamāsī, glory to the young cowherd women.

Glory, glory to Gopīśvara in the midst of Vṛndāvana. Glory, glory to Kṛṣṇa's friend, the brāhmaṇa boy (Madhu-maṅgala).

Glory to Rāma-ghāṭa, glory to the son of Rohiṇī. Glory, glory to all the residents of Vṛndāvana.

Glory to the wives of the brāhmaṇas, glory to the Nāga-kanyās. Who attained the feet of Govinda through devotion.

Glory to the Śrī Rāsa-maṇḍala, glory to Rādhā-Śyāma. Glory, glory to the rāsa-līlā, which enchants everyone.

Glory, glory to the ujjvala-rasa, the essence of all rasas. Which is preached in Vraja in the mood of paramour love.

Remembering the lotus feet of Śrī Jāhnavā, the lowly Kṛṣṇa Dāsa sings the saṅkīrtana of the Name.

IDX.first_line: jaya radhe jaya radhe radhe
IDX.search: jaya radhe jaya radhe radhe jaya radhe jaya sri radhe jaya krsna jaya krsna krsna jaya krsna jaya sri krsna syama gauri nityakisori pritamajori sri radhe rasika rasilo chailachabilo ganagaravilo sri krsna

jaya rādhe jaya rādhe rādhe,
jaya rādhe jaya śrī⋅rādhe
jaya kṛṣṇa jaya kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa,
jaya kṛṣṇa jaya śrī⋅kṛṣṇa

śyāmā gaurī nityakiśorī,
prītamajorī śrī⋅rādhe
rasika rasīlo chailachabīlo,
ganagaravīlo śrī⋅kṛṣṇa

rāsavihārini rasavistārini,
piyoura dhārini śrī⋅rādhe
nava⋅navaraṅgī navalatribhaṅgī,
śyāmasuaṅgī śrī⋅kṛṣṇa

atisukumārī, śrī⋅rādhe

sundaravaratara śrī⋅kṛṣṇa

śrī⋅bhagavantā, śrī⋅kṛṣṇa

candāvadanī kundāradanī,

atisukumārā śrī⋅kṛṣṇa

krīḍā kamanī śrī⋅rādhe
rūparasālā nayanaviśālā,
paramakṛpālā śrī⋅kṛṣṇa

kañcanavelī ratirasarelī,
ati alabelī śrī⋅rādhe
saba sukha sāgara saba gunoāgara,
rūpa ujāgara śrī⋅kṛṣṇa

ramaṇīramyā tarutaratamyā,
guṇa agamyā śrī⋅rādhe
dhāmanivāsī prabhāprakāśī,
sahaja suhāsī śrī⋅kṛṣṇa

śaktyāhlādini atipriyavādini,
urounmādini śrī⋅rādhe
aṅga⋅aṅga ṭonā sarasasalonā,
subhagasuṭhonā śrī⋅kṛṣṇa

rādhānāmini guṇoabhirāmini
śrī⋅haripriyā⋅svāminī śrī⋅rādhe
hare hare hari hare hare hari,
hare hare hari śrī⋅kṛṣṇa
IDX.en_translation:
All glories to Rādhā, all glories to Rādhā Rādhā, all glories to Rādhā, all glories to Śrī Rādhā. All glories to Kṛṣṇa, all glories to Kṛṣṇa Kṛṣṇa, all glories to Kṛṣṇa, all glories to Śrī Kṛṣṇa.

Śyāmā and Gaurī, eternally youthful, the beloved consort Śrī Rādhā. The playful and charming lover, who enchants the gopīs, Śrī Kṛṣṇa.

Performer of the Rāsa dance, expander of divine mellows, the holder of nectar, Śrī Rādhā. Ever-fresh and colorful, youthfully bent in three places, the beautiful dark-limbed one, Śrī Kṛṣṇa.

Dearer than life, illuminated by beauty, extremely delicate Śrī Rādhā. Charming to the eyes, greatly delightful, most supremely beautiful Śrī Kṛṣṇa.

Array of beauty, enchanting, with a voice like a cuckoo, Śrī Rādhā. Glorious, beloved of women, the supremely opulent Lord, Śrī Kṛṣṇa.

Moon-faced, with teeth like kunda flowers, abode of beauty Śrī Rādhā. Supremely generous, with unlimited effulgence, extremely delicate Śrī Kṛṣṇa.

With the gait of a swan, shining beautifully, delightful in pastimes Śrī Rādhā. Full of beauty and charm, with large eyes, supremely merciful Śrī Kṛṣṇa.

Golden creeper, flowing with amorous mellows, very charming and unique Śrī Rādhā. Ocean of all happiness, abode of all good qualities, illuminated by beauty Śrī Kṛṣṇa.

Charming among women, more tender than a fresh sprout, whose qualities are unfathomable Śrī Rādhā. Resident of the divine abode, manifesting divine effulgence, naturally sweetly smiling Śrī Kṛṣṇa.

The pleasure potency, speaking very sweetly, who maddens the heart, Śrī Rādhā. Enchanting in every limb, full of charming mellows, beautifully well-formed Śrī Kṛṣṇa.

Whose Name is Rādhā, delightful with good qualities, the queen beloved of Hari, Śrī Rādhā. Hare Hare Hari, Hare Hare Hari, Hare Hare Hari Śrī Kṛṣṇa.

IDX.first_line: jaya re jaya re jaya thakura sri narottama
IDX.search: jaya re jaya re jaya thakura sri narottama premabhakati maharaja jako mantri abhinna kalevara ramacandra kaviraja prema mukatamani bhusana bhavavali angahi anga viraja nrpa asana khetari mah baithata sangahi bhakata samaja
IDX.author: govinda dāsa
IDX.verses:
jaya re jaya re jaya, ṭhākura śrī⋅narottama,
premabhakati⋅mahārāja
jā̐ko mantrī, abhinna kalevara,
rāmacandra kavirāja

prema mukaṭamaṇi, bhūṣaṇa⋅bhāvāvalī,
aṅgahi̐ aṅga virāja
nṛpa⋅āsana, khetarī⋅māh baiṭhata,
saṅgahi̐ bhakata⋅samāja

sanātana⋅rūpa⋅kṛta, grantha bhāgavata,
anudina karata vicāra
rādhā⋅mādhava⋅, jugala⋅ujjvalarasa,
parānanda sukhasāra

śrī⋅saṅkīrtana, viṣayarasa unamata,
dharmādharma nāhi jāna
joga, jñāna, vrata, ādi bhaye bhāgavata,
royata karama geyāna

bhāgavata śāstragaṇa, jo dei bhakatidhana,
tāka gaura karu āpa
sāŋkhya, mīmāṁsaka, tarkādika jata,
kampita dekhi paratāpa

abhakata⋅caura, dūrahi bhāgi’ rahu,
niyaḍe nāhi parakāśa
dīna⋅hīna jane, deola bhakati⋅dhane,
vañcita govinda⋅dāsa
IDX.en_translation:
All glories, all glories, all glories to Ṭhākura Śrī Narottama, the great king of prema-bhakti! His minister, who is non-different from him in heart, is Rāmacandra Kavirāja.

The crown jewel of divine love, adorned with a succession of spiritual emotions, shines on every limb of his body. He sits upon a royal throne in Khetarī, accompanied by an assembly of devotees.

He constantly deliberates on the Bhāgavatam and the works of Sanātana and Rūpa. This brilliant mellow of the Divine Couple, Rādhā and Mādhava, is the essence of supreme bliss.

Through Śrī Saṅkīrtana and being intoxicated with the nectar of his subject, he transcends considerations of both pious and impious activities. Yoga, speculative knowledge, and various vows all weep before the presence of the Bhāgavatam.

The scriptures of the Bhāgavatam, which bestow the wealth of devotion, are themselves glorified by Gaura. All the schools of Sāṅkhya, Mīmāṁsā, and logic tremble seeing his spiritual prowess.

The thieves of non-devotion flee far away; they do not dare reveal themselves near him. To the poor and lowly people, the wealth of devotion is freely given. Only Govinda Dāsa feels deprived of this treasure.

IDX.first_line: jaya saci nandana jaya gaura harivisnupriya pranadhana nadiya vihari
IDX.search: jaya saci nandana jaya gaura harivisnupriya pranadhana nadiya vihari jaya saci nandana gaura gunakaraprema parasamani bhava rasa sagara
IDX.verses:
jaya śacī⋅nandana, jaya gaura⋅hariviṣṇupriyā⋅prāṇadhana, nadīyā⋅vihārī

jaya śacī⋅nandana, gaura guṇākaraprema⋅paraśamaṇi, bhāva⋅rasa⋅sāgara
IDX.en_translation:
All glories to the Son of Mother Śacī! All glories to Lord Gaura⋅hari! He is the treasure of Viṣṇupriyā’s life and He sports in the land of Nadīyā.

All glories to the Son of Śacī! He is Lord Gaura, the mine of good qualities. He is the touchstone of divine love and the ocean of emotional mellows.

IDX.first_line: jaya sankhacakragadadhara nila kalevara
IDX.search: jaya sankhacakragadadhara nila kalevara jeya candana carcita kundala mandita kaustubha lanchita dehi padam jaya pankaja locana bhuruta susobhana papavimocana dehi padam
IDX.verses:
(jaya) śaṅkhacakragadādhara, nīla kalevara,
jeya) candana carcita, kuṇḍala maṇḍita
kaustubha lāñchita dehi padam

(jaya) paṅkaja locana, bhuruta suśobhana,
pāpavimocana dehi padam

(jaya) veṇu ninādaka, rāsa⋅vihāraka,
baṅkima sundara dehi padam

(jaya) dhīra dhurandhara, adbhuta sundara,
deva sudurlabha dehi padam

(jaya) viśva vimohana, mānasa mohana,
saṁsthiti kāraṇa dehi padam

(jaya) satya sanātana, maṅgala kāraṇa,
antima bāndhava dehi padam

(jaya) durjana śāsana, keli parāyaṇa,
kālīya damana dehi padam

(jaya) bhaktajanāśraya, dīna dayāmaya,
cinmaya acyuta dehi padam

(jaya) parama pāvana, dharma parāyaṇa,
daitya nisūdana dehi padam

(jaya) veda vimohana, śrī⋅rādhā⋅ramaṇa
vṛndāvana dhana dehi padam

(jaya) nitya nirañjana, dūrgati bhañjana,
sajjana rañjana dehi padama
IDX.en_translation:
All glories to the dark-complexioned Holder of the conch, disc, and club! All glories to Him who is smeared with sandalwood, adorned with earrings, and marked with the Kaustubha gem! Please grant me shelter at Your lotus feet.

All glories to the lotus-eyed Lord with beautiful eyebrows! O deliverer from sin! Please grant me shelter at Your lotus feet.

All glories to the Flute-player and the enjoyer of the Rāsa dance! O beautiful and charming Lord! Please grant me shelter at Your lotus feet.

All glories to the grave and expert Leader, who is wonderfully beautiful! O Lord who is very rare to attain even for the demigods! Please grant me shelter at Your lotus feet.

All glories to the Enchanter of the universe and the Enchanter of the mind! O Cause of all existence! Please grant me shelter at Your lotus feet.

All glories to the eternal Truth and the cause of all auspiciousness! O my final friend! Please grant me shelter at Your lotus feet.

All glories to the Chastiser of the wicked, who is absorbed in His pastimes! O Subduer of the serpent Kāliya! Please grant me shelter at Your lotus feet.

All glories to the shelter of the devotees! O merciful Friend of the distressed! O transcendental and infallible Lord! Please grant me shelter at Your lotus feet.

All glories to the supreme Purifier, who is devoted to the path of righteousness! O Slayer of the demons! Please grant me shelter at Your lotus feet.

All glories to the Enchanter of the Vedas and the lover of Śrīmati Rādhā⋅rāṇī! O Treasure of Vṛndāvana! Please grant me shelter at Your lotus feet.

All glories to the eternal and spotless Lord, who is the destroyer of misfortune! O Delighter of the saintly! Please grant me shelter at Your lotus feet.
