jayati te ‘dhikaṁ janmanā vrajaḥ,śrayata indirā pyasya bhū⋅rajaḥtadapi hā’jahā nastvaṁ⋅atra bho!vayaṁ⋅imaḥ kathaṁ vānyatāṁ prabho!

parama⋅jātibhā⋅uttamaṁ svataḥśaradijañca jat⋅paṅkajaṁ tataḥdalakulā⋅vṛteḥ kānti⋅devatāṁharasi jaddṛśā śaśvadeva tām

kathaṁ⋅aho! vayaṁ svacchatāṁitābhavituṁ⋅īśmahe tad⋅vahiḥ⋅sthitāḥāpi kila tvayā jācñayā sitāḥsvayaṁ⋅amūrv⋅vareṇāpi sanditāḥ

iti gatāstava preṣyatāṁ punaḥkimu tayā dṛśā haṁsi tāstu naḥjadiha śastrataḥ kevalaṁ vadhaḥsphūrati nākṣitas⋅tanmataṁ tvadhaḥ

bahuvidhād⋅bhayād⋅yattva⋅yāvitaṁsakala⋅gokulaṁ kānanān⋅vitamtadapi manmahe svāvanaṁ jatas⋅tvadanu⋅rāgataḥ so’pi kiṁ hataḥ

kṛpaṇapālaka śrī⋅vrajeśvarītanuja⋅yogyadhīr⋅asti naśvarīiti tavātma⋅vallipti⋅riktatāparaga⋅pīḍayā bhāti tiktatā

tvamasi nānyavat ko’pi kopanaḥsphūrasi jat prabho! viśvagopanaḥtadiha padmaja⋅abhyarthitaḥsvabhūḥ paramasātvatād⋅anvayādabhūḥ

iti taveśituḥ suṣṭhu jaḥ karaḥprathita⋅saṁsṛter⋅bhītibhīkaraḥśirasi naḥ kuru śrī⋅karagrahaṁtamiha nānyathā bhāvayāgraham

vraja⋅gabhīhṛtau vīra! joṣitāṁśamayasi smite⋅naiva roṣitāmtadiha nānyathā daṇḍanaṁ kuruprakaṭayānana⋅śrīmahaḥ puru

jadapi te padaṁ śrī⋅niketanaṁvṛji⋅najinmahā⋅puṇyaketanamtadapi gogaṇasya⋅anuvartanaṁviṣabhṛtaḥ śiro⋅varti⋅nartanam

jadadhi tattadapyātta⋅duḥsthitiḥsvayamabhūttataḥ śāntatā⋅kṣitiḥahaha! tanmanāṅ nastta vakṣasisva⋅viraha⋅jvale kiṁ na rakṣasi

madhura⋅līlayā karṇa⋅ramyayāviśada⋅vākyayā vyakta⋅gamyayāsukha⋅sugandhitā⋅sātmyayācirāttṛṣāmitā vayaṁ hanta! te gira

tava kathāmṛtaṁ taptajīvanaṁkavibhir⋅īṛitaṁ kalmaṣārdhanampravitaranti je teṣu durjanāḥsapadi naḥ kṛte jñaptavarjanāḥ

prahasitaṁ tava preṅkhilocanamviharaṇakramād⋅bhāvarocanammuralikā⋅rutam sarvaśocanaṁvipinatas tu naḥ prāṇamocanam

calasi jarhi vā dhenu⋅cāraṇedalati dhīstu nakṣatra⋅kāraṇeśilā⋅tṛṇāṅkurair⋅yāvasannatākila bhavat⋅padoḥ sāsmakān gatā

sva⋅rativṛddhito’nanya⋅kāṅkṣiṇībhavati vaṁśikā jatra sākṣiṇītamadhara⋅dravaṁ śoka⋅nāśanaṁvitara vīra! bhoḥ! prāṇa⋅dāśanam

ahaha! jājvalaty antarāṇi naḥsmara nijān janān dīnamāninaḥcaraṇapaṅkajaṁ hṛdyamūdṛśāṁramaṇa! he tayā dhīyatāṁ bhṛśam

ahaha! jarhi jāsyahni kānanaṁkuṭila⋅kuntalaṁ śrīmad⋅ānanammuhur apaśyatāṁ tarhi sa kṣaṇaḥsphurati sarvadā kalpa⋅lakṣaṇaḥ

kimiha tacca vā vācyataṁ vrajen⋅nimirapīha jacchapyate vrajekathamaho! vayaṁ tāstu gopikāna hi bhavāma vā tatra kopikaḥ

vitata⋅gītikā⋅jāla⋅sanditāḥsvaka⋅janānatikramya cārditāḥkaramita⋅mahāraṇya⋅bhṛddiśicchalamayaḥ⋅striyaḥ⋅kastyajenniśi

prahasitaṁ priya! prema⋅vīkṣaṇaṁvihasitādikaṁ valgu⋅lakṣaṇambṛhad⋅uraḥ śriyo dhāma vīkṣyatenija⋅vimūḍhatā⋅smābhirīkṣyate

dinalaye’laka⋅dhvāntamekataḥprasṛtagorajaḥ sandhyamanyataḥmukhatayāmitaṁ jāminīśvaraṁdarśanan muhur⋅yacchasi smaram

sva⋅rativṛddhito’nanya⋅kāṅkṣiṇībhavati vaṁśikā jatra sākṣiṇītamadhara⋅dravaṁ śokanāśanaṁvitara vīra! bhoḥ! prāṇa⋅dāśanam

ititu jattvayā kāmakalpanaṁkila kalā⋅balāt⋅tacca kalpanamvayamanārataṁ sneha⋅bhāvanāḥkatham ayāmahe jātu kāmanāḥ

prakaṭatā tu te goṣṭhavāsināṁvṛjina⋅nāśinī prāṇa⋅vāsināmvitara naḥ sakṛt⋅tvat⋅spṛhā⋅yujāṁjadiha sūdanaṁ snigdha hṛdrujām

jat te sujāta⋅caraṇāmburuhaṁ staneṣubhītāḥ śanaiḥ priya dadhīmahi karkaśeṣutenāṭavīm aṭasi tad vyathate na kiṁ svitkūrpādibhir bhramati dhīr bhavad⋅āyuṣāṁ naḥ

iti gopyaḥ pragāyantyaḥpralapantyaś ca citradhāruruduḥ su⋅svaraṁ rājankṛṣṇa⋅darśana⋅lālasāḥ

tāsām āvirabhūc chauriḥsmayamāna⋅mukhāmbujaḥpītāmbara⋅dharaḥ sragvīsākṣān manmatha⋅manmathaḥ
IDX.en_translation:
All glories to Your birth in Vraja, where even the Goddess of Fortune seeks to reside upon its dust. Yet, alas, You do not abandon us here! How can we, Your devotees, be otherwise, O Lord?

Your beauty is superior to the finest autumn lotuses, from which You steal the very goddess of beauty with Your gaze.

Alas! How can we, who are outside Your grace, attain such purity? Even though You were bound by our pleas, and even those lotuses were blessed by You.

Having entered Your service again, why do You now slay us with that gaze? If death were to come by Your glance alone, it would be superior to death by a weapon.

From various fears, You have preserved all of Gokula along with its forests. We consider it Your protection, for how can that protection be lost due to Your love?

O protector of the humble! O Son of the Queen of Vraja! Our intelligence is perishable. Your apparent neglect of us appears bitter because of our suffering.

You are the universal protector and are not easily angered like others. Therefore, being entreated by Lord Brahmā, You appeared in the most pure lineage.

May that hand of Your Master, which is renowned and dispels the fear of the cycle of birth and death, place its auspicious touch upon our heads.

O hero! In the depths of Vraja, You pacify the angry gopīs with a smile alone. Therefore, do not punish us in any other way; simply manifest the glorious splendor of Your face.

Although Your feet are the abode of the Goddess of Fortune and are a great banner of merit for the distressed, You still follow the cows and dance on the heads of the venomous serpent.

Even though You were in great distress in that state, You became our tranquil support. Alas! Why do You not protect us, whose hearts are consumed by the fire of separation from You?

With Your sweet pastimes, delightful words, and joyful fragrance, we have been thirsty for a long time for Your nectarean speech.

Your nectarean stories are the life of the distressed and are praised by the poets for destroying all impurities. Those who do not propagate them are wicked and deprive us of knowledge.

Your smiling, dancing eyes and Your delightful moods dispels all sorrow, and Your presence in the forest is our very life.

When You go to graze the cows, our minds are broken by fate. The thought of the soft grass and sprouts touching Your feet becomes our only meditation.

Alas! Our inner hearts are ablaze! Remember Your own people, who consider themselves humble. May Your lotus feet be firmly placed in our hearts, O charmer!

Alas! When You go to the forest each day, we see Your beautiful face with curly locks and that moment feels like a vast age to us.

What more can be said about this in Vraja? Even for a moment, Your sight is desired. Alas, how can we gopīs not be anxious there?

Entangled in the net of Your songs and having left our own people, what woman would You abandon in the night in this wilderness?

O beloved! Your smiling, loving glances and Your broad chest—which is the abode of fortune—seeing all this, our own foolishness is revealed to us.

At the end of the day, with the darkness of Your hair and the spreading dust from the cows, You manifest the moon-like glory of Your face and cause us to remember You again and again.

Due to the increase of its own pleasure, the flute becomes a witness. Grant us, O hero, that nectar from Your lips which destroys all sorrow and is our life-giving food.

Even though You conceive of love in that way, it is in truth an artistic creation. How can we, who are constantly in the mood of affection, ever have other desires?

Your manifestation among the residents of Gokula is the destroyer of their sins. Grant us, who are filled with longing for You, the soothing cure for our heart’s disease.

Your supremely beautiful lotus feet, which we gently place upon our breasts, are pained when You wander in the forest. Our minds become bewildered by the thought of thorns and stones pricking them.

Thus, the gopīs, singing and lamenting in various ways, wept aloud, eager to see Lord Kṛṣṇa.

Then, the Lord, whose lotus face was smiling, appeared before them wearing yellow garments and a garland—He who is the direct bewilderer of even Cupid himself.

IDX.first_line: je anila premadhana karuna pracurahena prabhu kotha gela acarya thakura
IDX.search: je anila premadhana karuna pracurahena prabhu kotha gela acarya thakura kaha mora svarupa rupa kaha sanatanakaha dasa raghunatha patita pavana

kā̐hā mora svarūpa⋅rūpa, kā̐hā sanātanakā̐hā dāsa⋅raghunātha, patita⋅pāvana

kā̐hā mora bhaṭṭayuga, kā̐hā kavirājaekakāle kothā’ gelā gorā naṭa-rāja

pāṣāṇe kuṭiba māthā, anale paśibagaurāṅga guṇera nidhi kothā’ gele pāba

se⋅saba saṅgīra saṅge je kaila vilāsase⋅saṅga nā pāñā kānde narottama⋅dāsa
IDX.en_translation:
Where has that master gone, that revered teacher who brought the wealth of divine love and abundant mercy?

Where are Svarūpa Dāmodara and Rūpa Gosvāmī? Where is Sanātana Gosvāmī? Where are Raghunātha dāsa Gosvāmī and Raghunātha Bhaṭṭa Gosvāmī, the deliverers of the fallen?

Where are my two Bhaṭṭas? Where is Kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa Kavirāja? Where has the fair-complexioned king of dancers gone all at once?

I will smash my head against a stone; I will enter the fire. Where will I find Gaurāṅga, the treasure house of all qualities?

Not finding the association of those companions with whom He performed His divine pastimes, Narottama dāsa cries.

IDX.first_line: je rati janme jara sei mata rasa tara
IDX.search: je rati janme jara sei mata rasa tara rasa mukhya pancavidha haya gauna saptarasa punah haya ratira anuguna ratira sambandha bhavasraya panca mukhya madhye bhai madhurera guna gai sarvasrestha rasaraja bali guna anya rase jata madhurete ache tata ara bahu bale haya bali
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
je rati janme jā’ra, sei mata rasa tā̐’ra,
rasa mukhya pañcavidha haya
gauṇa saptarasa punaḥ, haya ratira anuguṇa,
ratira sambandha bhāvāśraya

pañca mukhya madhye bhāi, madhurera guṇa gāi,
sarvaśreṣṭha rasarāja bali
guṇa anya rase jata, madhurete āche tata,
āra bahu bale haya balī

gauṇa rasa āche jata, saba sañcārīra mata,
hañā śṛṅgārera puṣṭi kare
śrī-rūpera anugata, bhajane je haya rata,
sthiti tā’ra kevala madhure

madhura ujjvala rasa, sadā śṛṅgārera vaśa,
vraja⋅rāja⋅nandana⋅‘viṣaya’
aiśvarya su-gupta tā’te, mādhurya⋅prabhāve māte,
tāhāra ‘āśraya’⋅bhaktacaya
IDX.en_translation:
Whichever rati takes birth in someone, that kind of rasa is theirs. The principal rasas are of five kinds. Again, the seven secondary rasas are subservient qualities of rati; the relation of rati is the shelter of bhāva.

Among the five principal rasas, O brother, I sing the glories of madhura, calling it the supreme rasarāja. All the qualities that are in other rasas are present in madhura, and it becomes more powerful due to many more.

All the secondary rasas that exist are like sañcārī, and they nourish śṛṅgāra. One who is a follower of Śrī Rūpa and is absorbed in devotional service, their steadiness is only in madhura.

The madhura and ujjvala rasa is always under the sway of śṛṅgāra. The son of Vraja⋅rāja is the viṣaya. Aiśvarya is very hidden within it; it becomes intoxicated by the influence of mādhurya. Its āśraya are the devotees.

IDX.first_line: jhula jhule radhadamodara vrndavanamekaisi chai hariyali ali kunjaname
IDX.search: jhula jhule radhadamodara vrndavanamekaisi chai hariyali ali kunjaname ita nandako dularo uta bhanuki dularijori lage ati pyari basi nayananame
IDX.verses:
jhūlā jhūle rādhādāmodara vṛndāvaname̐kaisī chāī hariyālī ālī kuñjaname̐

ita nandako dulāro, uta bhānukī dulārījorī lāge ati pyārī basi nayananame̐

jamunāke kūla, pahira suraṅga dukūlataise khila rahe phūla ina kadamaname̐

gaura śyāma raṅga, ghana dāminīke saṅgabhoī akhiyā̐ apaṅga chavi bharī maname̐

rādhā mukha aura, naina śyāmake cakorasakhiyana prema ḍora lagī caraṇaname̐
IDX.en_translation:
Rādhā and Dāmodara are swinging on a swing in Vṛndāvana. How the greenery has spread in the groves, O friend!

Here is the darling of Nanda, there is the darling daughter of Bhānu. The Pair looks very dear, residing in the eyes.

On the bank of the Yamunā, wearing colorful fine cloth. Just as flowers are blooming on these Kadamba trees.

Fair and dark colors, like cloud and lightning together. My eyes became crippled (helplessly staring), the beauty filled the mind.

Rādhā’s face, and the eyes of Śyāma are the cakora. The rope of the love of the sakhīs is attached to the feet.

IDX.first_line: jhulana haite namila turite
IDX.search: jhulana haite namila turite rasavati rasaraja ratana asane basiya dujane ratana mandiba majha sucamara lei koi vijai sevaparayana sakhi suvasita jale vadana pakhale vasane mochaya akhi
IDX.author: vaiṣṇava⋅dāsa
IDX.verses: jhulanā haite, nāmila turite, rasavatī rasarāja ratana āsane, basiyā du’jane, ratana mandiba mājha sucāmara lei, koi vījai, sevāparāyaṇa sakhī suvāsita jale, vadana pākhāle, vasane mochāya ākhi thāri bhari koi, vividha miṭhāi, dhari muha samamukhe sakhīgaṇa sane, katai kaituke, bhojana karila sukhe tāmbula sājāiyā, kona sakhī laiyā, dohāra vadane dila e keśa kusume, āpāda vadane, nichiyā nichiyā nila kusuma talape, alape alape, vasilā rādhikā⋅śyāma alase iṣta, nayana mudita, heriya mohita kāma dekhi sakhīgaṇe, katahu jatane, śutāyala dohe tāya sakhīra iṅgite, caraṇe sevite, e dāsa vaiṣṇava dhāya
IDX.en_translation:
From the swing, the delightful Rādhā, full of nectar, and Kṛṣṇa, the King of nectar, quickly descended. The two of Them sat on jeweled seats within the jeweled temple.

Taking a fine yak-tail whisk, one devoted sakhī fans Them. With fragrant water, someone washes Their faces, and with a cloth, wipes Their eyes.

One fills a plate with various sweets, holding it before Their mouths. With the gopī friends, with much joy, They happily ate Their meal.

Preparing betel nuts, one sakhī took it and placed it in both Their mouths. They offered hair adorned with flowers, worshipping Them from head to foot, again and again.

On a bed of flowers, gently, Rādhikā and Śyāma sat. From weariness, with closed eyes, seeing this, even Cupid is enchanted.

Seeing this, the gopī friends, with much care, laid Them both down to rest. At the sakhī’s signal, to serve Their feet, this servant Vaiṣṇava Dāsa rushes.

IDX.first_line: jhulata syama gauri vama ananda range matiya
IDX.search: jhulata syama gauri vama ananda range matiya isata hasita rabhasa keli jhulayata kata sakhini meli gaota kata bhatiya hema mani jutavara hindora racita kusuma gandhe bhora padata bhramara patiya navina lataya jadita dala vrndavipina obhita bhala cada ujora ratiya
IDX.author: uddhava dāsa
IDX.verses: jhulata śyāma, gaurī vāma, ānanda raṅge mātiyā iṣata hasita rabhasa keli jhulāyata kata sakhinī meli gāota kata bhātiyā hema maṇi jutavara hiṇḍora, racita kusuma gandhe bhora paḍata bhramara pātiyā navīna latāya jaḍita dāla, vṛndāvipina obhita bhāla cā̐da ujora rātiyā nava ghana tanu dolye śyām, rāi saṅge jhulata vāma taḍita jaḍita kātiyā tārāmaṇi juta candra hāra, jhulite duliche gale dohāra hilana duhuka gātiyā dhidhi kaṭi dhiyā tāthaiyā bola, bāje mṛdaṅga mohana rola titinā titinā tātiyā bheda paḍala grāma pura, dhīra śabada jitasura varaṇa nāhika jātiyā maṇi ābharaṇa kiṅkinī baṅka, jhulane bājaye jhunura jhaṅka jhana jhana jhana jhātiyā rādhāmohana caraṇe āśa, kevala bharasā uddhava dāsa racita pūrita chātiyā
IDX.en_translation:
Lord Kṛṣṇa is swinging, with Rādhā on His left, reveling in joyful play. With slight smiles, many gopī friends gathered together are making Them swing in amorous play while singing various melodies.

The excellent golden swing, adorned with jewels, is filled with the intoxicating fragrance of flowers. Rows of bees are alighting upon it. The branches are entwined with fresh creepers, and the beautiful forest of Vṛndāvana shines splendidly. It is a bright moonlit night.

Lord Kṛṣṇa, whose body is like a fresh rain cloud, swings with Rādhā on His left. Their beauty is entwined like lightning and a cloud. Moon-like necklaces adorned with star-like jewels sway on Their necks as They swing. Their bodies are gracefully entwined.

To the rhythmic syllables of the drum, the mṛdaṅga plays an enchanting sound. The gentle sound, conquering all other sounds, spreads throughout the villages and towns—it is beyond description.

The jeweled ornaments and ankle bells make a jingling sound as They swing. Rādhāmohana expresses hope at Their feet, and Uddhava Dāsa has complete reliance upon Them. His heart is filled with this composition.

IDX.first_line: jiva jaga jiva jaga goracada balekata nidra jao maya pisacira kole
IDX.search: jiva jaga jiva jaga goracada balekata nidra jao maya pisacira kole bhajiba baliya ese samsara bhitarebhuliya rahile tumi avidyara bhare
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
jīva jāga, jīva jāga, gorācā̐da balekata nidrā jāo māyā⋅piśācīra kole

bhajiba baliyā ese’ saṁsāra⋅bhitarebhuliyā rahile tumi avidyāra bhare

tomāre loite āmi hoinu avatāraāmi vinā bandhu āra ke āche tomāra

enechi oṣadhi māyā nāśibāra lāgi’harināma⋅mahāmantra loo tumi māgi’

bhakativinoda prabhu⋅caraṇe paḍiyāsei harināma⋅mantra loila māgiyā
IDX.en_translation:
“Wake up, O soul, wake up!” says Gorāchānda. How long will you sleep in the lap of the witch Māyā?

Having come into this material world, thinking, “I will worship,” You remained deluded by the burden of ignorance.

To deliver you, I have descended as an Avatāra. Who else but Me is your friend?

I have brought the medicine to destroy Māyā. Take the Harināma-mahāmantra by humbly asking for it.

Bhaktivinoda, falling at the feet of the Lord, Humbly begged for and received that Harināma-mantra.

IDX.first_line: jivera bhagye avani vihare dona bhai
IDX.search: jivera bhagye avani vihare dona bhai bhuvanamohana goracada ara nitai kaliyuge jiva jata chila acetana harinamamrta diya karila cetana
IDX.author: śrīla locana⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
jīvera bhāgye avanī vihare dona bhāi
bhuvana-mohana gorācā̐da āra nitāi

kaliyuge jīva jata chila acetana
hari-nāmāmṛta diyā karilā cetana

hena avatāra bhāi, kabhu śuni nāi
pātakī uddhāra kailā ghare ghare jāi

hena avatāra bhāi nāhi kona juge
kon avatāre bala pāpīra pāpa māge

rudhira paḍila aṅge khāiyā prahāra
jāci prema diyā tā’re karilā uddhāra

nāma⋅prema⋅sudhāte bharila tribhuvana
ekelā vañcita bhela e dāsa locana
IDX.en_translation:
By the good fortune of all living entities, the two brothers have appeared on this earth: the moon-like Gaura and Nitāi, who enchant the entire universe.

In this Age of Kali, they have awakened all the unconscious souls by giving them the nectar of the Holy Name of Lord Hari.

O brother! I have never heard of such an incarnation! They delivered all the sinful persons by going from house to house.

Such an incarnation has never appeared in any other age. Tell me, which incarnation ever begged the sinners to give up their sins?

Although they were beaten until blood flowed from their bodies, they still gave divine love and delivered those who struck them.

The three worlds were filled with the nectar of the Holy Name and divine love. Only this servant Locana remains deprived.

IDX.first_line: jiyo syama lala jiyo syama lalapili teri pagadi ranga kala
IDX.search: jiyo syama lala jiyo syama lalapili teri pagadi ranga kala gokulame ayo nandalalagopiyo se pada gaya ava pala
IDX.verses:
jiyo śyāma lālā, jiyo śyāma lālāpīlī terī pagaḍī raṅga kālā

gokulame̐ āyo nandalālāgopiyo se paḍa gayā ava pālā

mata ro kānhā ū̐vā ū̐vāsamjhāve sunandā buā

khīra, jalebī, pūḍī laḍḍū puāyaśomatī ghara ānanda huā

caramara caramara kare palanāvrajavāsī gāye jiyo lalanā

nanda ke ānanda bhayojaya kanhaiyā lāla kīhāthi diyo ghoḍā diyo aura diyo pālakījaya kanhaiyā lāla kī!hāthi ghoḍā pālakī!
IDX.en_translation:
Long live Śyāma Lālā! Your turban is yellow, and Your complexion is dark.

Nandalālā has come to Gokula. Now it’s the turn of the cowherd girls.

“Don’t cry Kṛṣṇa!”, His aunt Sunandā explains.

Rice pudding, sweet pretzels, fried bread, and sweet cakes are made. Joy has come to Yaśodā’s home.

The cradle creaks as the residents of Vraja sing, “Long live the child!”

Nanda is overjoyed! Glory to Kanhaiyā Lāl! An elephant is given, a horse is given, and a palanquin is given. Glory to Kanhaiyā Lāl! Elephant, horse, palanquin!

IDX.first_line: jnani jnanayoge kariya jatane
IDX.search: jnani jnanayoge kariya jatane brahmera saksat kare brahma saksatkara aprarabdha karma sampurna jnanete hare tabu ta prarabdha nahi haya ksaya phalabhoga vina kabhu brahmabhuta jiva phalabhoga lagi janama marana labhu
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
jñānī jñānayoge, kariyā jatane,
brahmera sākṣāt kare
brahma⋅sākṣātkāra, aprārabdha karma,
sampūrṇa jñānete hare

tabu ta’ prārabdha, nāhi haya kṣaya,
phalabhoga vinā kabhu
brahmabhūta jīva, phalabhoga lāgi’,
janama⋅maraṇa labhu

kintu ohe nāma, tava sphūrti ha’le,
ekāntī janera āra
prārabdhāprārabdha, kichu nāhi thāke,
vede gāya bāra bāra

tomāra udaye, jīvera hṛdaya,
sampūrṇa śodhita haya
karmajñāna⋅bandha, saba dūre jāya,
anāyāse bhava⋅kṣaya

bhakativinoda, bāhu tule kaya,
nāmera niśāna dhara
nāmaḍaṅkā⋅dhvani, kariyā jāibe,
bheṭibe muralīdhara
IDX.en_translation:
A wise person, through the path of knowledge and with great effort, realizes the Absolute Truth. This realization completely removes the accumulated reactions to past actions through perfect knowledge.

Even then, the results of current actions are not destroyed; they must still be experienced. A soul absorbed in that Absolute Truth must take birth and death to experience those results.

But, O Name! When You manifest, for a one-pointed devotee, all karmic reactions cease to exist. The Vedas sing this again and again.

With Your appearance, the heart of the soul becomes completely purified. All the bondage of action and knowledge goes far away, and material existence is destroyed effortlessly.

Bhaktivinoda raises his arms and says: "Hold the banner of the Name! By making the sound of the Holy Name resound, you will meet the flute-player!"

IDX.first_line: joga jaga saba chara sraddha sakalera sara
IDX.search: joga jaga saba chara sraddha sakalera sara sei sraddha hrdaye jahara udiyache ekabindu krame bhaktirasa sindhu labhe tara haya adhikara jnana karma devadevi bahu jatanete sevi praptaphale haile tucchajnana sadhujana sangavese sri krsnakathaya sese visvasa ta haya balavan
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
joga jāga saba chāra, ‘śraddhā’ sakalera sāra,
sei śraddhā hṛdaye jāhāra
udiyāche ekabindu, krame bhaktirasa⋅sindhu⋅,
lābhe tā’ra haya adhikāra

jñāna, karma, devadevī, bahu jatanete sevi’,
prāptaphale haile tucchajñāna
sādhujana⋅saṅgāveśe, śrī⋅kṛṣṇakathāya śeṣe,
viśvāsa ta’ haya balavān

sei ta’ viśvāse bhāi, 'śraddhā' bali sadā gāi,
bhaktilatā⋅bīja bali tāre
karmī jñānī jane jāre, śraddhā bale bāre bāre,
sei vṛtti śraddhā hoite nāre

‘nāmera vivāda mātra’, śuniyā ta’ jvale gātra,
lauhe jadi balaha kāñcana
tabu lauha lauha raya, kāñcana ta’ kabhu naya,
maṇi⋅sparśa nahe jatakṣaṇa

kṛṣṇa-bhakti cintāmaṇi, tā̐ra sparśe lauha-khani,
karmajñānagata śraddhā⋅bhāva
hañā jāya hemabhāra, chāḍiyā ta’ ku-vikāra,
se kevala maṇira prabhāva
IDX.en_translation:
All yogas and sacrifices are insignificant; faith is the essence of all. One in whose heart a single drop of that faith has arisen, gradually becomes qualified to attain the ocean of the nectar of devotion.

Even after much endeavor in serving knowledge, fruitive activities, and demigods, when the obtained results are considered insignificant, then through the association of saintly persons and finally hearing topics of Śrī Kṛṣṇa, firm belief becomes strong.

O brother, we always sing of that firm belief as 'faith', and call it the seed of the creeper of devotion. What the fruitive worker and empiric philosopher repeatedly call faith, that tendency cannot be true faith.

Hearing that it is 'just a dispute of names,' one's body burns. If you call iron gold, still, iron remains iron, it is never truly gold, until it receives the touch of a divine gem.

Devotion to Kṛṣṇa is the wish-fulfilling gem. By its touch, an iron mine—the mood of faith related to karma and jñāna—becomes a heap of gold, leaving behind all impurities. That is solely the power of the gem.

IDX.first_line: jogapithopari sthita astasakhi suvestita
IDX.search: jogapithopari sthita astasakhi suvestita vrndaranye kadamba kanane radhasaha vamsidhari visvajana cittahari prana mora tahara carane sakhi ajnamata kari dohara sevana palyadasi sada bhavi dohara carana
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
joga-pīṭhopari sthita, aṣṭasakhī⋅suveṣṭita,
vṛndāraṇye kadamba⋅kānane
rādhāsaha vaṁśī-dhārī, viśvajana⋅cittahārī,
prāṇa mora tā̐hāra caraṇe

sakhī⋅ājñāmata kari dohāra sevana
pālya-dāsī sadā bhāvi dohāra caraṇa

kabhu kṛpā kari’, mama hasta dhari’,
madhura vacana bale
tāmbūla loiyā, khāya duijane,
mālā laya kutūhale

adarśana haya kakhana ki chale
nā dekhiyā dohe hiyā mora jvale

jekhāne sekhāne, thākuka du’jane,
āmi ta’ caraṇa⋅dāsī
milane ānanda, virahe jātanā,
sakala samāna vāsi

rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa prāṇa mora jīvane maraṇe
more rākhi’ māri’ sukhe thākuka du’jane

bhakativinoda, āna nāhi jāne,
paḍi’ nijasakhī⋅pāya
rādhikāra gaṇe, thākiyā satata,
jugala⋅caraṇa cāya
IDX.en_translation:
Situated on the altar of union, surrounded by the eight chief companions in the kadamba groves of Vṛndāvana, is the flute-bearer with Rādhā, the captivator of everyone's heart. My very life is at His feet.

According to the companions' command, I serve them both. As a nurtured maidservant, I always meditate on Their feet.

Sometimes, by Their grace, They take my hand and speak sweet words. Taking betel, They both eat, and they take garlands with eagerness.

When they disappear by some pretense, my heart burns at not seeing them.

Wherever They may be, let them remain. I am indeed a maidservant of Their feet. In union, there is joy; in separation, there is distress—I consider all these feelings to be equal.

Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa are my life in both life and death. Whether protecting me or killing me, let them remain happy.

Bhaktivinoda knows nothing else. Falling at the feet of his own companions and always remaining among Rādhā's associates, he desires the feet of the Divine Couple.

IDX.first_line: jugala carana prati parama ananda tati
IDX.search: jugala carana prati parama ananda tati ratiprema hou parabandhe krsna nama radhanama upasana rasadhama carane padiye paranande manera smarana prana madhura madhura nama vilasa jugala smrtisara sadhya sadhana ei ara nai iha boi ei tattva sarvatattva sara
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
jugala⋅caraṇa⋅prati, parama⋅ānanda⋅tati,
ratipremā hou parabandhe
kṛṣṇa⋅nāma⋅rādhānāma, upāsanā rasadhāma,
caraṇe paḍiye parānande

manera smaraṇa prāṇa, madhura madhura nāma,
vilāsa jugala smṛtisāra
sādhya sādhana ei, āra nāi ihā boi,
ei tattva sarvatattva⋅sāra

jalada sundara⋅kānti, madhura madhura bhāti,
vaidagadhi⋅avadhi suveśa
supīta vasana dhara, ābharaṇa maṇivara,
mayūra⋅candrakā karu keśa

mṛgamada sucandana, kuṅkumādi vilepana,
mugdhakārī mūrati tribhaṅga
navīna⋅kusumāvalī, śrīaṅge śobhaye bhāli,
madhulobhe phire matta bhṛṅga

īṣat madhurasmita, vaidagadhi⋅līlāmṛta,
lubadhala vrajavadhūvṛnda
caraṇa⋅kamala⋅para, maṇimaya sumañjīra,
nakhamaṇi jini’ bālacandra

nūpura⋅marāla⋅dhvani, kulavadhū⋅marālinī,
śuniyā rahite nāre ghare
hṛdaye bāḍhaye rati, jena mile pati satī,
kulera dharama jāya dūre

kṛṣṇamukha⋅dvijarāje, saralā vaṁśī virāje,
jāra dhvani bhuvana mātāya
śravaṇera patha diyā, hṛdaye praveśa hañā,
prāṇa ādi ākarṣi’ ānaya

govinda⋅sevana satya, tā̐hāra sevaka nitya,
vṛndāvana bhūmi tejomaya
tāhāre jamunājala, kare nitya jhalamala,
tāra tīre aṣṭa kuñja haya

śītala kiraṇa kara, kalpataru⋅guṇadhara,
tarulatā ṣaḍṛtu⋅sevā
pūrṇacandrasama jyoti, cidānandamaya mūrti,
mahānanda daraśana lobhā

govinda ānandamaya, nikaṭe vanitācaya,
vihare madhura ati śobhā
du̐hu preme ḍagamagi, du̐he dohā anurāgī,
du̐hu rūpe du̐hu manolobhā

vrajapura⋅vanitāra, caraṇa āśraya sāra,
kara mana ekānta kariyā
anya bola gaṇḍagola, nāhi śuna utarola,
rākha prema hṛdaye bhariyā

kṛṣṇa prabhu ekabāra, karibena aṅgīkāra,
jena’ mana e satya vacana
dhanya līlā vṛndāvana, rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa śrī⋅caraṇa,
dhanya sakhī mañjarīra gaṇa

pāpa⋅puṇyamaya deha, sakala anitya eha’,
dhana jana saba michā dhanda
marile jāibe kothā, tāhāte nā pāo vyathā,
tabu kārya kara sadā manda

rājāra je rājyapāṭa, jena nāṭuyāra nāṭa,
dekhite dekhite kichu naya
hena māyā kare jei, parama īśvara sei,
tā̐re mana sadā kara bhaya

pāpe nā kariha mana, adhama se pāpijana,
tāre mana dūre parihari’
puṇya se sukhera dhāma, tāra nā loio nāma,
‘puṇya’, ‘mukti’ dui tyāga kari’

premabhakti⋅sudhānidhi, tāhe ḍuba niravadhi,
āra jata kṣāranidhi prāya
nirantara sukha pābe, sakala santāpa jābe,
paratattva karile upāya

anyera paraśa jena, nāhi haya kadācana,
ihāte hoibe sāvadhāna
rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa nāma gāna, ei se parama dhyāna,
āra nā kariha paramāṇa

karmī, jñānī, michābhakta, nā ha’be tāya anurakta,
śuddhabhajanete kara mana
vraja⋅janera jei mata, tāhe habe anugata,
ei se parama tattvadhana

prārthanā kariba sadā, śuddhabhāve premakathā,
nāma⋅mantre kariyā abheda
āstika kariyā mana, bhaja rāṅgā śrī⋅caraṇa,
granthipāpa habe pariccheda

rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa⋅śrī⋅caraṇa, mātra paramārtha⋅dhana,
sayatane hṛdayete loo
du̐hu nāma śuni’ śuni’, bhaktamukhe puni puni,
parama ānanda sukha pāṅa

hemagauratanu rāi, ā̐khi daraśana cāi,
rodana karaye abhilāṣe

jaladhara ḍhara ḍhara, aṅga ati manohara,
rūpete bhuvana parakāśe

sakhīgaṇa cāripāśe, sevā kare abhilāṣe,
parama se śobhāsukha dhare
ei mane āśā mora, aiche rase hañā bhora,
narottama sadāi vihare
IDX.en_translation:
May my devotion and love be always bound to the feet of the Divine Couple, the highest happiness. The names of Kṛṣṇa and Rādhā are the abode of devotional mellows; I fall at Their feet in supreme bliss.

The remembrance of the mind is life's most precious possession; sweet are Their names—the essence of the Divine Couple's playful pastimes. This is the means and the goal; there is nothing beyond this truth, which is the essence of all truths.

With a beautiful cloud-like complexion and sweet radiance, Kṛṣṇa is adorned in beautiful garments with supreme artistry. He wears brilliant yellow clothes and the best of jewels, and His hair is decorated with peacock feathers.

Scented with musk, sandalwood paste, and vermilion, His enchanting form is bent in three places. Adorned with fresh flower garlands that beautify His divine body, intoxicated bees hover about Him, greedy for honey.

A slight, sweet smile—the nectar of His expert divine pastimes—enchants the damsels of Vraja. Upon His lotus feet are jeweled ankle bells, and His fingernails are brighter than the crescent moon.

Hearing the sound of His ankle bells, which is like the sound of swans, the chaste village women—like female swans themselves—cannot remain in their homes. Devotion grows in their hearts, as if husband and wife are meeting, and all worldly duties are cast aside.

On Kṛṣṇa's moon-like face, a simple flute shines, its sound intoxicating the universe. Entering through the path of the ears and into the heart, it attracts and brings forth the very life force.

Serving Govinda is the truth, and His servants are eternal. The land of Vṛndāvana is effulgent, and the waters of the Yamunā always sparkle there; on its banks are eight groves.

Cool rays of light shine there, and the trees and creepers possess the qualities of wish-fulfilling trees, offering service through the six seasons. His form is full of spiritual bliss and luminous like the full moon; I long to behold that great bliss.

Govinda is full of joy, surrounded by groups of women; His pastimes are sweet and exceedingly beautiful. Both are overflowing with love and devoted to each other, captivated by each other's beauty.

The shelter of the feet of the women of Vraja is the essence; make your mind exclusive to them. Do not listen to other clamors and disturbances; keep love filled in your heart.

Kṛṣṇa, the Master, will accept you; know this, O mind, for it is a true statement. Glorious are the pastimes of Vṛndāvana and the sacred feet of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa! Glorious are the companions and their attendants!

This body, full of sin and piety, everything is temporary; wealth and people are all false illusions. Where will you go after death? You feel no pain about it, yet you always do evil deeds.

The kingdom of a king is like an actor's play; while you watch it, it becomes nothing. He who creates such illusion is the Supreme Lord; fear Him always, O mind.

Do not set your mind on sin, for those who are sinful are degraded; cast them far away from your mind. Piety is the abode of happiness, but do not even utter its name; abandon both "piety" and "liberation."

Dive constantly into the nectar-ocean of loving devotion; all other oceans are like salty water. You will attain continuous happiness and all your distress will vanish if you make the Supreme Truth your means.

May there never be contact with those who are not devotees; be careful in this regard. Singing the names of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa is the supreme meditation; do not do anything else, even a tiny bit.

Do not be attached to fruitive workers, speculative philosophers, or false devotees; fix your mind on pure devotion. Follow the path of the people of Vraja; this is the supreme treasure of truth.

Always pray for pure loving talks, considering the Name and the mantra as non-different from God. With a faithful mind, worship the beautiful red feet, and the knot of sin will be severed.

The sacred feet of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa alone are the ultimate treasure; take them carefully into your heart. Hearing Their names again and again from the mouths of devotees, one attains supreme bliss and happiness.

I desire to see the golden-complexioned Rādhā; I cry with longing.

Like a fresh cloud, His form is most captivating, and His beauty illuminates the universe.

The companions around her serve her with longing, holding that supreme beauty and happiness. This is my heart's hope; immersed in such mellows, Narottama always sports.

IDX.first_line: ka kaliyuge sri krsna caitanya avatara
IDX.search: ka kaliyuge sri krsna caitanya avatara kha khelivara prabandhe kaila khola karatala ga gadagadi jana prabhu nija sankirtane gha ghare ghare harinama dena sarvajane
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
ka, kaliyuge śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅caitanya avatāra
kha, khelivāra prabandhe kaila khola⋅karatāla

ga, gaḍāgaḍi jāna prabhu nija saṅkīrtane
gha, ghare ghare harināma dena sarva-jane

ṅ, uccaiḥsvare kā̐de prabhu jīvera lāgiyā
ca, cetana karāna jīve kṛṣṇa-nāma diyā

cha, chala chala kare ā̐khi nayanera jale
ja, jagat pavitra kaila gaura⋅kalevare

jha, jhala jhala mukha jena pūrṇa śaśadhara
ña, emata ta’ dekhi nāi dayāra sāgara

ṭa, ṭalamala kare aṅga bhāvete vibhola
ṭha, ṭhamake ṭhamake cale bale hari-bola

ḍa, ḍorahi kaupīna kṣīṇa kaṭira upare
ḍha, ḍhaliyā ḍhaliyā paḍe gadādharera kroḍe

ṇa, āna parasaṅga gorā nā śune śravaṇe
ta, tāna māna gāna rase majāila mane

tha, thira nāhi haya prabhura nayanera jala
da, dīna hīna janere dhariyā deya kola

dha, dhoyāiyā pūrava pirīti parasaṅga
na, nā jāni kāhāra bhāve hoila tri-bhaṅga

pa, premarase bhāsāila akhila⋅saṁsāra
pha, phuṭala śrī-vṛndāvana suradhunī⋅dhāra

ba, brahmā, maheśvara jā̐’re kare anveṣaṇa
bha, bhāviyā nā pā’na jā̐’re sahasra-locana

ma, matta⋅mātaṅga⋅gati madhura mṛdu hāsa
ja, jaśomatī mātā jā̐’ra bhuvane prakāśa

ra, ratipati jini rūpa ati manorama
la, līlā lāvaṇya jā̐’ra ati anupama

ba, vasudeva⋅suta sei śrī⋅nanda⋅nandana
śa, śacīra nandana ebe bale sarva-jana

ṣa, ṣaḍbhuja rūpa hailā atyāścaryamaya
sa, sāvadhāna, prāṇanātha gorā⋅rasamaya

ha, hari hari bala bhāi, kara mahāyajña
kṣa, kṣititale janmi’ keha nā haiya avijña

e’ cautriśa padāvalī je kare kīrtana
dāsa narottama māge tā̐hāra caraṇa
IDX.en_translation:
Ka - In the Age of Kali, Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya is the incarnation. Kha - He took up the mṛdaṅga drum and karatālas to arrange His pastimes.

Ga - The Lord rolls on the ground in His congregational chanting. Gha - He gives the Holy Name to everyone in every home.

Ṅa - The Lord cries loudly for the sake of the living entities. Ca - He awakens the living entities by giving them the Holy Name of Kṛṣṇa.

Cha - His eyes are filled with tears. Ja - He purified the universe with His golden body.

Jha - His face shines like the full moon. Ña - I have never seen such an ocean of mercy.

Ṭa - His body trembles, overwhelmed with ecstatic emotion. Ṭha - He walks with a graceful gait and chants "Haribol!"

Ḍa - He wears a loincloth and a belt on His thin waist. Ḍha - Swaying to and fro, He falls into the lap of Gadādhara Paṇḍita.

Ṇa - Lord Gaura does not hear any other topics with His ears. Ta - His mind is absorbed in the nectar of melodies, beats, and songs.

Tha - The tears in the Lord's eyes are never still. Da - Embracing the poor and lowly people, He gives them His mercy.

Dha - He washes everything with the topics of previous divine love. Na - I do not know in whose mood He became bent in three places.

Pa - He flooded the entire world with the mellows of divine love. Pha - Śrī Vṛndāvana blossomed in the flow of the celestial Ganges.

Ba - He is the one whom Lord Brahmā and Lord Śiva search for. Bha - The thousand-eyed Indra cannot find Him even by meditation.

Ma - His gait is like a maddened elephant, and His smile is sweet and gentle. Ya - Mother Yaśodā is manifest in this world.

Ra - His form defeats Cupid and is very enchanting. La - His pastimes and beauty are very incomparable.

Va - He is that same Son of Vasudeva and Son of Nanda. Śa - Everyone now calls Him the Son of Śacī.

Ṣa - He manifested a six-armed form, which is very wonderful. Sa - Be careful, the Lord of my life, Gaura, is full of divine mellows.

Ha - Chant "Hari! Hari!" brother, and perform this great sacrifice. Kṣa - Being born on the surface of the earth, do not remain ignorant.

Whoever chants these thirty-four verses, Narottama Dāsa begs for his lotus feet.

IDX.first_line: kabe aha gauranga baliya
IDX.search: kabe aha gauranga baliya bhojana sayane dehera jatana chadiba virakta hana navadvipa dhame nagare nagare abhimana parihari dhamavasi ghare madhukari laba khaiba udara bhari
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
kabe āhā gaurāṅga baliyā
bhojana⋅śayane, dehera jatana,
chāḍiba virakta hañā

navadvīpa⋅dhāme, nagare nagare,
abhimāna parihari’
dhāmavāsī⋅ghare, mādhukarī la’ba,
khāiba udara bhari’

nadī-taṭe giyā, añjali añjali,
piba prabhu⋅padajala
taru-tale paḍi’, ālasya tyajiba,
pāiba śarīre bala

kākuti kariyā, ‘gaura⋅gadādhara’,
‘śrī⋅rādhā⋅mādhava’⋅nāma
kā̐diyā kā̐diyā, ḍāki’ uccarave,
bhramiba sakala dhāma

vaiṣṇava dekhiyā, paḍiba caraṇe,
hṛdayera bandhu jāni’
vaiṣṇava ṭhākura, ‘prabhura kīrtana’,
dekhāibe dāsa māni’
IDX.en_translation:
Alas, when, chanting 'Gaurāṅga', will I become detached and give up caring for my body in eating and sleeping?

In Navadvīpa-dhāma, from town to town, abandoning false pride, I will beg alms (madhukarī) at the homes of the residents of the dhāma and eat to fill my belly.

Going to the riverbank, palmful by palmful, I will drink the water of the Lord's feet. Lying under a tree, I will give up laziness and gain strength in my body.

Piteously chanting the names 'Gaura-Gadādhara', 'Śrī Rādhā-Mādhava', weeping and weeping, calling out loudly, I will wander throughout the dhāma.

Seeing a Vaiṣṇava, I will fall at his feet, knowing him to be a friend of my heart. The Vaiṣṇava Ṭhākura, accepting me as his servant, will show me the Lord's kīrtana.

IDX.first_line: kabe gaura vane suradhuni tateha radhe ha krsna balekadiya bedaba deha sukha chadinana lata tarutale
IDX.search: kabe gaura vane suradhuni tateha radhe ha krsna balekadiya bedaba deha sukha chadinana lata tarutale svapaca grhete magiya khaibapiba sarasvati jalapuline puline gadagadi dibakari krsna kolahala
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
kabe gaura⋅vane, suradhunī⋅taṭe,‘hā rādhe, hā kṛṣṇa’ balekā̐diyā beḍāba, deha⋅sukha chāḍi’,nānā latā⋅tarutale

śvapaca⋅gṛhete, māgiyā khāiba,piba sarasvatī⋅jalapuline puline, gaḍāgaḍi diba,kari’ kṛṣṇa⋅kolāhala

dhāmavāsi⋅jane, praṇati kariyā,māgiba kṛpāra leśavaiṣṇava⋅caraṇa, reṇu gāya mākhi’,dhari’ avadhūta⋅veśa

gauḍa⋅vraja⋅jane, bheda nā dekhiba,hoiba varajavāsīdhāmera svarūpa, sphuribe nayane,hoiba rādhāra dāsī
IDX.translation_intro: From The Harmonist, October 1930. Edited by Bhaktisiddhanta Sarasvati.
IDX.en_translation:
When in the wood-lands of Gaura By the bank of the celestial stream With the cry of “Oh Kṛṣṇa, Oh Rādhā!” Shall I roam weeping, Renouncing the pleasures of the body, Under all diverse creepers and Trees?

At the homes of the basest By begging I will feed, Sip the water of the Sarasvatī, From bank to bank I will roll on the ground With the tumult of cry of Kṛṣṇa?

To the denizens of the Abode of God Making obeisance I will beg for the trace of mercy: With the particles of dust from the feet of the Vaiṣṇavas Anointing my body, Assuming the garb of the wholly free?

The denizens of Braja and Gauḍa I will not deem as different, I will be a dweller of Braja-dhāma’s own beauty Will manifest to the eye, I will be the handmaid of Rādhā!

IDX.first_line: kabe habe bala se dina amara
IDX.search: kabe habe bala se dina amara amara aparadha ghuci suddha name ruci krpa bale habe hrdaye sancara trnadhika hina kabe nija mani sahisnuta guna hrdayete ani sakale manada apani amani haye asvadiva nama rasa sara
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
kabe ha’be bala se⋅dina āmāra
(āmāra) aparādha ghuci’, śuddha nāme ruci,
kṛpā⋅bale ha’be hṛdaye sañcāra

tṛṇādhika hīna, kabe nija māni’,
sahiṣṇutā⋅guṇa hṛdayete āni’
sakale mānada, āpani amānī,
ha’ye āsvādiva nāma⋅rasa⋅sāra

dhana jana āra, kavitā sundarī,
baliba nā cāhi deha sukhakarī
janme janme dāo, ohe gaura⋅hari,
ahaitukī bhakti caraṇe tomāra

(kabe) karite śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅, nāma uccāraṇa,
pulakita deha gadgada vacana
vaivarṇya⋅vepathu, ha’be saŋghaṭana,
nirantara netre ba’be aśrudhāra

kabe navadvīpe, suradhunī⋅taṭe,
gaura⋅nityānanda bali’ niṣkapaṭe
nāciyā gāhiyā, beḍāiba chuṭe,
bātulera prāya chāḍiyā vicāra

kabe nityānanda, more kari’ dayā,
chāḍāibe mora viṣayera māyā
diyā more nija⋅ caraṇera chāyā,
nāmera hāṭete dibe adhikāra

kiniba, luṭiba, hari⋅nāma⋅rasa,
nāmarase māti’ hoiba vivaśa
rasera rasika⋅ caraṇa paraśa,
kariyā majiba rase anivāra

kabe jīve dayā, hoibe udaya,
nijasukha bhuli’ sudīna⋅hṛdaya
bhakativinoda, kariyā vinaya,
śrī⋅ājñā ṭahala karibe pracāra
IDX.translation_intro: From The Harmonist, October 1932. Edited by Bhaktisiddhanta Sarasvati.
IDX.en_translation:
( Tune-Surat, Khambaj, Eltala ) Say, oh will that day be ever mine When all offence ceasing, The relish of the pure Name Will be infused into my heart by the pewer of mercy!

Owning myself humbler Than the blade of grass, Welcoming the quality of fortitude in my heart, Giving honour to all, Being myself free from all vanity, Oh, when shall I taste the essence of the mellow liquid of the Name!

Wealth, attendants, And the beautiful maid of poesy, I shall not covet for my happiness. In all my successive births, Be Thou pleased, O Gaur-Hari, To give me causeless devotion to Thy Feet!

To articulate the Name Of Sree Krishna Horripilation of the body, thickness of the voice, Pallor and shivering Will make their appearance; A constant stream of tears will flow from my eyes.

Oh, when shall I at Nabadwip, By the bank of the Celestial Stream, Call artlessly upon Gaur and Nityananda, And run about, Dancing and singing, Like one mad, giving up all consideration!

Oh, when will Nityananda, Taking mercy on me, Absolve me from the infatuation of the world; And giving me the shelter Of His Own Feet Allow me entry into the Mart of the Name!

And I shall buy and plunder  The mellow liquid of the Name of Hari, Intoxicated by the sweetness of the Name, lose all discretion; And by touching the feet Of those immersed in the mellow liquid Be ceaselessly mellowed by the liquid sweet!

Oh, when will pity for jiva Manifest its appearance, And with an humble mind, forgetting his own happiness, Bhaktivinode, By humble solicitation, Will propagate the tidings of Divine Command!

IDX.first_line: kabe habe hena dasa mora
IDX.search: kabe habe hena dasa mora tyaji jada asa vividha bandhana chadiba samsara ghora vrndavanabhede navadvipa dhame badhiba kutirakhani sacira nandana carana asraya kariba sambandha mani

tyaji’ jaḍa⋅āśā, vividha bandhana,

kā̐dite kā̐dite, ṣolakrośa⋅dhāma,
jāhnavī ubhaya⋅kūle
bhramite bhramite, kabhu bhāgya-phale,
dekhi kichu taru-mūle

hā hā manohara, ki dekhinu āmi,
baliyā mūrchita ha’ba
samvit pāiyā, kā̐diba gopane,
smari’ du̐hu kṛpā⋅laba
IDX.en_translation:
When will such a state be mine? Giving up material desires and diverse attachments, I will abandon the dense darkness of this material world.

In the abode of Navadvīpa, which is non-different from Vṛndāvana, I will build a small cottage. Taking the lotus feet of the son of Śacī as my shelter, I will establish that relationship.

On the banks of the Ganges, in a spiritual forest, sitting in a secluded spot, I will continuously drink the nectar of Kṛṣṇa's name, calling out "Gaurāṅga!"

O Gaura and Nitāi, You two brothers are the friends of the fallen! I am the lowest of the fallen, a wicked person; please become an ocean of mercy for me.

Weeping, I will wander on both banks of the Ganges through the sixteen-krośa abode. At some point, by good fortune, I will see something at the foot of a tree.

"Oh, oh, how beautiful! What have I seen?" Saying this, I will faint. Regaining consciousness, I will cry secretly, remembering a drop of Their mercy.

IDX.first_line: kabe habe hena dasa moratyaji jada asa vividha bandhanachadiba samsara ghora
IDX.search: kabe habe hena dasa moratyaji jada asa vividha bandhanachadiba samsara ghora vrndavanabhede navadvipa dhamebadhiba kutirakhanisacira nandana carana asrayakariba sambandha mani
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
kabe ha’be hena daśā moratyaji’ jaḍa⋅āśā, vividha bandhana,chāḍiba saṁsāra⋅ghora

vṛndāvanābhede, navadvīpa⋅dhāme,bā̐dhiba kuṭīrakhāniśacīra nandana⋅, caraṇa āśraya,kariba sambandha māni’

jāhnavī⋅puline, cinmaya⋅kānane,basiyā vijana⋅sthalekṛṣṇa-nāmāmṛta, nirantara piba,ḍākiba ‘gaurāṅga’ ba’le

hā⋅gaura⋅nitāi, torā du’ṭī bhāi,patitajanera bandhuadhama patita, āmi he durjana,hoo more kṛpā-sindhu

kā̐dite kā̐dite, ṣolakrośa⋅dhāma,jāhnavī ubhaya⋅kūlebhramite bhramite, kabhu bhāgya-phale,dekhi kichu taru-mūle

hā hā manohara, ki dekhinu āmi,baliyā mūrchita ha’basamvit pāiyā, kā̐diba gopane,smari’ du̐hu kṛpā⋅laba
IDX.en_translation:
When will such a state be mine? Giving up material desires and diverse attachments, I will abandon the dense darkness of this material world.

In the abode of Navadvīpa, which is non-different from Vṛndāvana, I will build a small cottage. Taking the lotus feet of the son of Śacī as my shelter, I will establish that relationship.

On the banks of the Ganges, in a spiritual forest, sitting in a secluded spot, I will continuously drink the nectar of Kṛṣṇa’s name, calling out “Gaurāṅga!”

O Gaura and Nitāi, You two brothers are the friends of the fallen! I am the lowest of the fallen, a wicked person; please become an ocean of mercy for me.

Weeping, I will wander on both banks of the Ganges through the sixteen-krośa abode. At some point, by good fortune, I will see something at the foot of a tree.

“Oh, oh, how beautiful! What have I seen?” Saying this, I will faint. Regaining consciousness, I will cry secretly, remembering a drop of Their mercy.

IDX.first_line: kabe krsnadhana paba hiyara majhare thoba
IDX.search: kabe krsnadhana paba hiyara majhare thoba judaiba tapita parana sajaiya diba hiya basaiba pranapriya nirakhiba se candrabayana he sajani kabe mora hoibe sudina se prananathera sange kabe va phiriba range sukhamaya jamunapulina
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
kabe kṛṣṇa-dhana pāba, hiyāra mājhāre thoba,
juḍāiba tāpita⋅parāṇa
sājāiyā diba hiyā, basāiba prāṇa-priyā,
nirakhiba se candra-bayāna

he sajani! kabe mora hoibe sudina
se prāṇa-nāthera saṅge, kabe vā phiriba raṅge,
sukhamaya jamunā-pulina

lalitā⋅viśākhā lañā, tā̐hāre bheṭiba giyā,
sājāiyā nānā upahāra
sadaya hoiyā vidhi, milāibe guṇa-nidhi,
hena bhāgya hoibe āmāra?

dāruṇa vidhira nāṭa, bhāṅgila premera hāṭa,
tilamātra nā rākhila tā’ra
kahe narottama⋅dāsa, ki mora jīvane āśa,
chāḍi’ gela vrajendra-kumāra
IDX.en_translation:
When will I obtain the treasure of Kṛṣṇa? I will place Him in the core of my heart, and soothe my distressed soul. I will decorate my heart, and seat the beloved of my life there; I will gaze upon His moon-like face.

Oh friend! When will that auspicious day be mine, when with that Lord of my life, I will playfully wander on the blissful banks of the Yamunā?

Taking Lalitā and Viśākhā, I will go and meet Him, having prepared various offerings. When will providence be merciful and unite me with that treasure-house of qualities? Will such fortune ever be mine?

The cruel play of fate has broken the marketplace of love, not even a sesame seed's worth remains of it. Naroṭṭamadasa says, what hope is there in my life, since the Prince of Vraja has left me?

IDX.first_line: kabe mora mudha mana chadi anya dhyana
IDX.search: kabe mora mudha mana chadi anya dhyana sri krsna carane pabe visramera sthana kabe ami janiba apane akincana amara apeksa ksudra nahi anya jana
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
kabe mora mūḍha mana chāḍi’ anya dhyāna
śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅caraṇe pā’be viśrāmera sthāna

kabe āmi jāniba āpane akiñcana
āmāra apekṣā kṣudra nāhi anya jana

kabe āmi ācaṇḍāle kariba praṇati
kṛṣṇa-bhakti māgi’ la’ba kariyā minati

sarvajīve dayā mora katadine ha’be
jīvera durgati dekhi’ lotaka paḍibe

kā̐dite kā̐dite āmi jā’ba vṛndāvana
vraja⋅dhāme vaiṣṇavera loiba śaraṇa

vrajavāsi⋅sannidhāne juḍi’ dui kara
jijñāsiba līlā⋅sthāna hoiyā kātara

ohe vrajavāsi! more anugraha kari’
dekhāo kothāya līlā karilena hari

tabe kona vraja⋅jana sakṛpa⋅antare
āmāre jā’bena la’ye vipina⋅bhitare

balibena, dekha ei kadamba⋅kānana
jathā rāsa-līlā kailā vrajendra-nandana

ai dekha nandagrāma nandera āvāsa
ai dekha baladeva jathā kailā rāsa

ai dekha jathā haila dukūla⋅haraṇa
ai sthāne bakāsura hoila nidhana

eirūpa vraja⋅janasaha vṛndāvane
dekhiba līlāra sthāna satṛṣṇa⋅nayane

kabhu vā jamunā⋅tīre śuni’ vaṁśīdhvani
avaśa hoiyā lābha kariba dharaṇī

kṛpāmaya vraja⋅jana ‘kṛṣṇa’ ‘kṛṣṇa’ bali’
pāna karāibe jala pūriyā añjali

harināma śune punaḥ pāiyā cetana
vraja⋅jana⋅saha āmi kariba bhramaṇa

kabe hena śubhadina hoibe āmāra
mādhukarī kari’ beḍāiba dvāra dvāra

jamunā⋅salila piba añjali bhariyā
devadvāre rātrikāle rahiba śuiyā

jakhana āsibe kāla e bhautika pura
jalajantu⋅mahotsava hoibe pracura

siddhadehe nija kuñje sakhīra caraṇe
nityakāla thākiyā seviba kṛṣṇa-dhane

ei se prārthanā kare e pāmara chāra
śrī-jāhnavā more dayā kara’ eibāra
IDX.en_translation:
When will my foolish mind abandon all other thoughts and find a place of rest at the lotus feet of Śrī Kṛṣṇa?

When will I truly know myself as insignificant, realizing that there is no one smaller than me?

When will I offer my obeisances even to the outcasts and beg for devotion to Kṛṣṇa with humble prayers?

When will compassion for all living beings awaken in me? Seeing the suffering of all souls, tears will fall from my eyes.

Weeping, I will go to Vṛndāvana and take shelter of the devotees in that sacred land.

Joining my hands before the residents of Vraja, I will anxiously inquire about the places of the Lord's pastimes.

"O residents of Vraja! Please be merciful to me and show me where the Lord performed His pastimes."

Then some compassionate resident of Vraja will take me into the forest.

He will say, "Look, this is the kadamba forest where the son of the King of Vraja performed the rāsa dance.

"Look, there is Nandagrāma, the home of Nanda; look, there Baladeva performed the rāsa dance.

"Look, there the stealing of the clothes took place; in that very spot, the demon Bakāsura was killed."

In this way, with the residents of Vraja, I will see the places of these pastimes with eager eyes.

Sometimes, on the bank of the Yamunā, hearing the sound of the flute, I will become overwhelmed and fall to the ground.

The merciful residents of Vraja, repeatedly chanting "Kṛṣṇa! Kṛṣṇa!", will offer me water from their cupped hands.

Hearing the Holy Name and regaining consciousness, I will travel with the residents of Vraja.

When will such an auspicious day be mine, that I will wander from door to door begging for alms?

I will drink the water of the Yamunā from my cupped hands, and at night I will lie down at the temple gate.

When my time comes to leave this material body, a great festival for aquatic creatures will take place.

With a perfected spiritual body, in my own grove at the feet of the companions, I will eternally remain and serve the treasure that is Kṛṣṇa.

This lowly wretch makes this prayer: O Śrī Jāhnavā, please bestow Your mercy upon me now.

IDX.first_line: kabe mui vaisnava ciniba hari harivaisnava carana kalyanera khanimatiba hrdaye dhari
IDX.search: kabe mui vaisnava ciniba hari harivaisnava carana kalyanera khanimatiba hrdaye dhari vaisnava thakura aprakrta sadanirdosa anandamayakrsnaname prita jade udasinajivete dayardra haya
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
kabe mui vaiṣṇava ciniba hari harivaiṣṇava⋅caraṇa, kalyāṇera khani,mātiba hṛdaye dhari’

vaiṣṇava⋅ṭhākura, aprākṛta sadā,nirdoṣa, ānandamayakṛṣṇa-nāme prīta, jaḍe udāsīna,jīvete dayārdra haya

abhimāna⋅hīna, bhajane pravīṇa,viṣayete anāsaktaantara⋅bāhire, niṣkapaṭa sadā,nityalīlā⋅anurakta

kaniṣṭha, madhyama, uttama prabhede,vaiṣṇava trividha gaṇikaniṣṭhe ādara, madhyame praṇati,uttame śuśrūṣā śuni

je jena vaiṣṇava, ciniyā loiyā,ādara kariba jabevaiṣṇavera kṛpā, jāhe sarva-siddhi,avaśya pāiba tabe

vaiṣṇava⋅caritra, sarvadā pavitra,jei ninde hiṁsā kari’bhakativinoda, nā sambhāṣe tā’re,thāke sadā mauna dhari
IDX.en_translation:
When will I recognize a vaiṣṇava, O Hari, Hari! The feet of a vaiṣṇava, is a mine of auspiciousness, I will hold them in my heart with ecstasy.

Vaiṣṇava-ṭhākura is always transcendental, Faultless, full of joy. Pleased by the name of Kṛṣṇa, indifferent to material things, Compassionate to all living beings.

Devoid of false ego, expert in devotional service, Unattached to worldly objects. Always sincere, both internally and externally, Attached to the eternal pastimes of the Lord.

Dividing vaiṣṇavas into neophyte, intermediate, and advanced, I consider vaiṣṇavas to be of three types. Affection for the neophyte, obeisances to the intermediate, And I hear of rendering service to the advanced.

When I recognize each vaiṣṇava according to their stage, And offer them respect, Then by the mercy of the vaiṣṇava, which is the source of all perfection, I will certainly attain it.

The character of a vaiṣṇava is always pure. Whoever slanders them out of envy, Bhaktivinoda does not converse with such a person, And always remains silent.

IDX.first_line: kabe sri caitanya more karibena daya
IDX.search: kabe sri caitanya more karibena daya kabe ami paiba vaisnavapada chaya kabe ami chadiba e visayabhimana kabe visnujane ami kariba sammana
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
kabe śrī⋅caitanya more karibena dayā
kabe āmi pāiba vaiṣṇavapada⋅chāyā

kabe āmi chāḍiba e viṣayābhimāna
kabe viṣṇu-jane āmi kariba sammāna

galavastra kṛtāñjali vaiṣṇava⋅nikaṭe
dante tṛṇa kari’ dā̐ḍāiba niṣkapaṭe

kā̐diyā kā̐diyā jānāiba duḥkhagrāma
saṁsāra⋅anala haite māgiba viśrāma

śuniyā āmāra duḥkha vaiṣṇava ṭhākura
āmā’ lāgi’ kṛṣṇe āvedibena pracura

vaiṣṇavera āvedane kṛṣṇa dayāmaya
e hena pāmara⋅prati ha’bena sadaya

vinodera nivedana vaiṣṇava⋅caraṇe
kṛpā kari’ saṅge laha ei akiñcane
IDX.en_translation:
When will Śrī Caitanya show mercy to me? When will I obtain the shade of the feet of the devotees?

When will I abandon this false ego of material enjoyment? When will I offer respect to the devotees of the Lord?

With a cloth around my neck and with folded hands, I will approach the devotees. Holding a blade of grass in my teeth, I will stand before them sincerely.

Weeping uncontrollably, I will reveal the multitude of my miseries. From the fire of material existence, I will beg for relief.

Having heard my miseries, the revered devotee will appeal greatly to Kṛṣṇa for me.

By the appeal of that devotee, the merciful Kṛṣṇa will become kind toward such a fallen soul.

This is the humble submission of Bhaktivinoda at the feet of the devotees: "Please be merciful and take this insignificant one along with you."

IDX.first_line: kadacit kalindi tata vipina sangita taralo
IDX.search: kadacit kalindi tata vipina sangita taralo mudabhiri nari vadana kamalasvada madhupah rama sambhu brahma marapati ganesarcitapado jagannathah svami nayanapatha gami bhavatu me bhuje savye venum sirasi sikhi piccham katitate dukulam netrante sahacari kataksam vidadhate sada srimad vrndavana vasati lila paricayo jagannathah svami nayanapatha gami bhavatu me
IDX.author: śrī⋅śaṅkarācārya
IDX.verses:
kadācit kālindī⋅taṭa⋅vipina⋅saṅgīta⋅taralo
mudābhīrī⋅nārī⋅vadana⋅kamalāsvāda⋅madhupaḥ
ramā⋅śambhu⋅brahma⋅marapati⋅gaṇeśārcitapado
jagannāthaḥ svāmī nayanapatha⋅gāmī bhavatu me

bhuje savye veṇuṁ śirasi śikhi⋅picchaṁ kaṭitaṭe
dukūlaṁ netrānte sahacari⋅kaṭākṣaṁ vidadhate
sadā śrīmad⋅vṛndāvana⋅vasati⋅līlā⋅paricayo
jagannāthaḥ svāmī nayanapatha⋅gāmī bhavatu me

mahāmbhodhes⋅tīre kanaka⋅rucire nīla⋅śikhare
vasan prāsādāntaḥ sahaja⋅balabhadreṇa balinā
subhadrā⋅madhyasthaḥ sakala⋅sura⋅sevāvasarado
jagannāthaḥ svāmī nayanapatha⋅gāmī bhavatu me

kṛpā⋅pārāvāraḥ sajala⋅jalada⋅śreṇi⋅ruciro
ramā⋅vāṇī⋅rāmaḥ sphuradamala⋅paṅkeruha⋅mukhaḥ
surendrairārādhyaḥ śrutigaṇaśikhā gītacarito
jagannāthaḥ svāmī nayanapatha⋅gāmī bhavatu me

rathārūḍho gacchan pathi milita⋅bhūdeva⋅paṭalaiḥ
stuti⋅prādurbhāvaṁ pratipadamupākarṇya sadayaḥ
dayāsindhurbandhuḥ sakala jagatāṁ sindhu⋅sutayā
jagannāthaḥ svāmī nayanapatha⋅gāmī bhavatu me

paraṁbrahmāpīḍaḥ kuvalaya⋅dalotphulla⋅nayano
nivāsī nīlādrau nihita⋅caraṇo‛nanta⋅śirasi
rasānandī rādhā⋅sarasa⋅vapurāliṅgana⋅sukho
jagannāthaḥ svāmī nayanapatha⋅gāmī bhavatu me

na vai jāce rājyaṁ na ca kanaka⋅māṇikya⋅vibhavaṁ
na jāce‛haṁ ramyāṁ sakala⋅jana⋅kāmyāṁ varavadhūm
sadā kāle kāle pramatha⋅patinā gīta⋅carito
jagannāthaḥ svāmī nayanapatha⋅gāmī bhavatu me

hara tvaṁ saṁsāraṁ drutatarama-sāraṁ sura⋅pate!
hara tvaṁ pāpānāṁ vitatima-parāṁ jādava⋅pate!
aho dīne‛nāthe nihita⋅caraṇo niścitamidaṁ
jagannāthaḥ svāmī nayanapatha⋅gāmī bhavatu me

jagannāthāṣṭakaṁ puṇyaṁ
jaḥ paṭhet prayataḥ śuciḥ
sarva⋅pāpa⋅viśuddhātmā
viṣṇulokaṁ sa gacchati
IDX.en_translation:
Sometimes He is vibrant with music in the forest groves on the banks of the Yamunā, a bee joyfully relishing the lotus-like faces of the cowherd women; whose feet are worshiped by Lakṣmī, Śiva, Brahmā, Indra, and Gaṇeśa—may that Jagannātha, the Lord of the Universe, be the object of my vision.

With a flute on His left arm, a peacock feather on His head, a fine garment around His waist, and the sidelong glances of His companions at the corners of His eyes; He is ever famous for His pastimes of residing in glorious Vṛndāvana—may that Jagannātha, the Lord of the Universe, be the object of my vision.

Residing within His temple on the golden, beautiful Blue Peak on the shore of the great ocean, with His strong elder brother Balabhadra; He stands with Subhadrā in the middle, granting the opportunity for service to all the gods—may that Jagannātha, the Lord of the Universe, be the object of my vision.

He is an ocean of mercy, charming like a fresh cloud; the beloved of Lakṣmī and Sarasvatī, with a glowing, pure lotus-like face; He is worshiped by the chief of the gods, and His glories are sung by the highest Vedic texts—may that Jagannātha, the Lord of the Universe, be the object of my vision.

Riding on His chariot and proceeding along the path, He is met by groups of priests; He graciously hears the manifestation of their praise at every step; He is an ocean of compassion, a friend to all the worlds, accompanied by Lakṣmī—may that Jagannātha, the Lord of the Universe, be the object of my vision.

He is the crown jewel of the Supreme Absolute, with eyes wide open like blooming blue lotus petals; He resides on the Blue Mountain, placing His feet on the head of the cosmic serpent; He experiences the joy of divine mellows, blissful from embracing the charming form of Rādhā—may that Jagannātha, the Lord of the Universe, be the object of my vision.

I do not pray for a kingdom, nor for the opulence of gold and jewels; I do not pray for a beautiful bride desired by all; His glories are always sung by Śiva in every age—may that Jagannātha, the Lord of the Universe, be the object of my vision.

O Lord of the gods! Quickly remove this worthless material existence! O Lord of the Yādavas! Remove the vast multitude of my sins! Oh, it is certain that His feet are placed on the helpless and destitute—may that Jagannātha, the Lord of the Universe, be the object of my vision.

One who recites these eight sacred verses on Jagannātha with devotion and purity, with a soul purified from all sins, goes to the abode of Viṣṇu.

IDX.first_line: kadamba tarura dala namiyache bhume bhala
IDX.search: kadamba tarura dala namiyache bhume bhala phutiyache phula sari sari parimale bharala sakala vrndavana keli kare bhramara bhramari rai kanu vilasoi range kiba rupa lavani vaidagdhakhani dhani manimaya abharana ange
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
kadamba tarura ḍāla, nāmiyāche bhūme bhāla,
phuṭiyāche phula sāri sāri
parimale bharala, sakala vṛndāvana,
keli kare bhramarā bhramarī

rāi⋅kānu vilāsoi raṅge
kibā rūpa lāvaṇi, vaidagdhakhani dhani,
maṇimaya ābhāraṇa āṅge

rādhāra dakṣiṇa kara, dhari’ priya giridhara,
madhura madhura cali’ jāya
āge pāche sakhī-gaṇa, kare phula variṣaṇa,
kona sakhī cāmara ḍhulāya

parāge dhūsara sthala, candra kare suśītala,
maṇimaya vedīra upare
rāi⋅kānu kara joḍi’, nṛtya kare phiri’ phiri’,
paraśe pulake tanu bhare

tāra madhye hemapīṭha, aṣṭadale veṣṭita,
aṣṭadale pradhānā nāyikā
tā’ra madhye ratnāsane, basi āchena duijane,
śyāma saṅge sundarī rādhikā

orūpa lāvaṇyarāśi, amiya paḍeche khasi’,
hāsya parihāsa⋅sambhāṣaṇe
narottama⋅dāsa kaya, nityalīlā sukhamaya,
sadāi sphuruka mora mane

mṛgamada⋅candana, kare kari’ sakhī-gaṇa,
barikhaye phula gandharāje
śramajala bindu bindu, śobhā kare mukha⋅indu,
adhare muralī kibā bāje

hāsya⋅vilāsa⋅rasa, sarala madhura bhāṣa,
narottama⋅manoratha bharu
duhu̐ka vicitra veśa, kusume racita keśa,
locana⋅mohana līlā karu
IDX.en_translation:
The branches of the kadamba tree have bent beautifully to the ground, and flowers have blossomed in rows upon rows. The entire Vṛndāvana is filled with fragrance, while bees playfully hum.

Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa enjoy playfully with great joy. Oh, what beauty and charm! A treasure-mine of cleverness, Their bodies are adorned with jeweled ornaments.

Holding Rādhā's right hand, her dear Giridhara walks very sweetly. Before and behind Them, the companions shower flowers, while some fan Them with whisks.

The ground is dusty with pollen, and the moon makes it very cool; upon a jeweled platform, Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa dance with joined hands, turning around and around, Their bodies filled with ecstatic thrills at each other's touch.

In its midst is a golden seat surrounded by eight petals; among them are the principal companions. Upon a jeweled throne, both of Them are seated—beautiful Rādhikā with her dark Lord.

From that abundance of beauty and charm, nectar overflows in Their laughter, jokes, and conversations. Narottama dāsa says: the eternal pastimes are full of joy; may they always manifest in my mind.

Holding musk and sandalwood paste in their hands, the companions shower Them with fragrant flowers. Drops of perspiration adorn Their moon-like faces. Oh, how beautifully the flute plays on His lips!

The mellow of playful laughter and simple sweet speech—may Narottama's heart's desires be fulfilled. In Their wonderful dresses, with Their hair adorned with flowers, They perform eye-enchanting pastimes.

IDX.first_line: kadaye ninduka saba kari haya haya
IDX.search: kadaye ninduka saba kari haya haya eka bara nadiyaya ele dhirava tara paya na jani mahima guna kahiyachi kata ei bara lagaila paile haba anugata
IDX.author: śrīla vṛndāvana⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses: kādaye ninduka saba kari hāya hāya eka⋅bāra nadīyāya ele dhirava tāra pāya nā jāni mahimā guṇa kahiyāchi kata ei⋅bāra lāgāila pāile haba anugata deśe deśe kata jīva tarāila śuni caraṇe dharile dayā karibe āpani nā budhiyā kahiyāchi kata kuvacana ei⋅vara pāile tāra laiba śaraṇe gaurangera sange jata pāriṣada⋅gaṇe tārā saba suniyāchi patita⋅pāvana ninduka pāṣanda jata pāila prakāśa kādiyā ākula bhela vṛndāvana⋅dāsa
IDX.en_translation:
All the critics cry out "Alas! Alas!" If one comes to Nadīyā just once, he takes shelter of Your feet.

Not knowing Your glorious qualities, I have spoken so much. This time, if I attain You, I will become submissive.

I hear that You have delivered so many souls in every land. If I hold onto Your feet, You Yourself will show mercy.

Not understanding, I have spoken so many abusive words. This time, if I attain You, I will take shelter of Him.

All the associates with Gaurāṅga, I have heard that they all are patita-pāvana.

All the critics and miscreants obtained enlightenment. Vṛndāvana Dāsa became overwhelmed, weeping.

IDX.en_translation:
A certain beautiful cowherd woman, separated by a quarrel, her body distressed by the heat of separation, anxiously spoke to her friend:

Alas, Gauri! Will He walk the path of my eyes? Śrī Kṛṣṇa, the ocean of mercy, Kṛṣṇa, the beloved of Gokula?

Govinda, the supreme bliss, the auspiciousness of Nanda's home, the ruby from the mine of Yaśodā, the moon of the ocean of the king of cowherds.

His splendor mocks the brilliance of a fresh raincloud. He is covered by a yellow garment (pītāmbara) that defeats the pride of gold.

The luster of His toenails steals the pride of Kandarpa's beauty. His feet shine with the marks of the flag, lotus, thunderbolt, barley, and goad.

His anklets chirp like wagtails in the cage of His feet. His knees are brilliant, holding the shape of a jewelry box or blue gems.

His thighs have the beauty of the trunk of an intoxicated elephant. His broad hips are surrounded by a girdle of jeweled bells.

The sweetness of His waist destroys the pride of the lion. His broad chest is like a great emerald mountain.

His conch-like neck is adorned with a king of jewels. The power of His arms challenges the jeweled pillars of Indra.

His face defeats the beauty of millions of full moons. He is an ocean of waves of charm, His lips comparable to vermillion.

His eyes are filled with the beauty of a blossoming lotus. Shark-shaped earrings dance, swinging at the edge of His cheeks.

His radiant form expands with the onset of fresh youth. The prowess of His sidelong glances surpasses millions of bows of Cupid.

His noble speech is laden with the sweetness of nectar. He is the fragrant elephant of the caves and bowers of the dense forest of Vṛndāvana.

He is the fresh cloud on the lap of the high peaks of blessed Govardhana. He is the auspicious swan playing in the Yamunā.

He is the delight of Nandīśvara, the dancer on the banks of Bhāṇḍīra. He is the killer of Śaṅkhacūḍa, and He made the lord of mountains His playing ball.

He is as deep as millions of oceans, as powerful as millions of lions. He delights the son of Rohiṇī and has intense friendship with Śrīdāmā.

He is dear to Subala due to love, the heart-throb of His friends. He has taken a great vow to ignite the bliss of the people of Nanda's Vraja.

His circle of flute music gathers the herds of cows. He is eager for the sport of fighting with tall bulls.

He is the personification of the erotic sentiment of the forest, the chosen deity of amorous love. His bright form is marked by a bunch of raised peacock feathers.

He is worshipped with a garland of five colors hovered over by moving bees. His belt is strung with golden jasmine flowers and red raṅgaṇa flowers.

His body is a wave of beauty painted with various mineral pigments. His ornaments are made of heaps of guñjā berries, and His bed is of tender leaves.

The fragrance of His body increases the intoxication of the young women. He inflames the desire of the lotus-eyed girls of Vṛndāvana.

He is muddy with the kunkuma from the breasts of the cowherd women who are agitated by Cupid. The stream of sweetness from His moon-like face blocks the eyes of chaste women.

He is the plunderer of the garments of the unmarried girls. He is expert in mature, playful joking. He is the ocean of nectar for Rādhā, anointed with abundant, charming cleverness.

He is the autumn season for the moonlight of Candrāvalī's intelligence. He possesses the fortune of being a steady and charming lover (dhīra-lalita), beautiful with the joy of Cupid.

He is the moon for the cakora bird of Candrāvalī. He is the honey for the mādhavī creeper of Rādhikā. He is charming in His pastimes with Lalitā, and the moon for the star Viśākhā.

He is a row of lotuses for the face of Padmā. His lotus feet are served by Śaibyā. He sleeps in the heart of Bhadrā, and yearns for the love of Śyāmalā.

He is the garden of extraordinary, astonishing pastimes. He is the magnet for the wealth of love, the beloved who has made a vow of iron-like attraction.

He is the thief of the flute, the snatcher of the bodices of the golden-limbed girls. He is the all-in-all of Rādhā's tryst, the guru of extensive amorous skills.

His body is bound by the creeper of Rādhā's playful words and desire to hear them. He is eager to be blocked by Rādhikā, who takes away His garland of kadamba flowers.

He delights in union with Rādhā, hidden in the lap of the bower. He is struck by the lotus-earring of the dignified Rādhā during their wild sports.

His armor is the crushed kunkuma from the battle of love. He has a form with three bends, and His lips are joined with the flute.

The elegance of His flute music spreads the essence of celestial melodies. He is the king of thousands of groups of gopīs, maddened by the intense mellows of the rāsa dance.

The corner of His eye agitates millions of Cupid's five arrows. He has begun a dance in the circle on the bank of the Yamunā.

He is the lake for the bee of Vṛṣabhānu's daughter, the wish-fulfilling cow. He creates a smile on Rādhikā's face with His hidden, meaningful jokes.

His soul is hidden in the dress of a woman, astounding the mind. His lotus mouth is mixed with betel containing camphor.

He is skilled in arranging reconciliation through the messenger of the proud Candrāvalī. He is struck by Rādhā's frown when blocked at the banks of the deceitful toll-station.

He is embarrassed by the reproaches disguised as laughter from Rādhā's expert friends. He is the embodied love of the cowherd women, the form of welfare and bliss.

He is eager for the tinkling sound of Bhadrā's bells as she happily goes to the tryst. His path is watched by Padmā who is preparing the bed.

He has become the object of the anxious Lalitā's conjectures. He increases the burden of lamentation of the cheated Viśākhā.

He is sought after by Śyāmā, who regrets her quarrel. He relishes the angry words of the fiercely intelligent, betrayed Śaibyā.

He is delighted by the message of Candrāvalī, who is distressed by separation. He is expert in decorating the blossoming Rādhā, who controls her Lover.

He is held by the hem of the garment by Rādhā, who won the wager of kissing the flute. His heart is bewildered by the whirlpool of Rādhā's love.

Thus, with a mind maddened by love, she described the Crusher of Kaṁsa. Seeing Him appearing before her, she attained a festival of mature bliss.

May the wise lovingly dive into this 'Premendu-sāgara' (Ocean of the Moon of Love), consisting of one hundred and eight names, which is a Mandara mountain for the tongue.

IDX.first_line: kalau jam vidvamsah sphutam abhiyajante dyuti bharad
IDX.search: kalau jam vidvamsah sphutam abhiyajante dyuti bharad akrsnam krsnangam makha vidhibhir utkirtanamayaih upasyam ca prahur jam akhila caturthasrama jusam sa devas caitanyakrtir atitaram nah krpayatu caritram tanvanah priyam aghavadahladana padam jayodghosaih samyag viracita saci soka haranah udancan martanda dyuti hara dukulancita katih sa devas caitanyakrtir atitaram nah krpayatu
IDX.author: śrīla rūpa gosvāmī
IDX.verses:
kalau jaṁ vidvāṁsaḥ sphuṭam abhiyajante dyuti⋅bharād
akṛṣṇaṁ kṛṣṇāṅgaṁ makha⋅vidhibhir utkīrtanamayaiḥ
upāsyaṁ ca prāhur jam akhila⋅caturthāśrama⋅juṣāṁ
sa devaś caitanyākṛtir atitarāṁ naḥ kṛpayatu

caritraṁ tanvānaḥ priyam aghavadāhlādana⋅padaṁ
jayodghoṣaiḥ samyag⋅viracita⋅śacī⋅śoka⋅haraṇaḥ
udañcan⋅mārtaṇḍa⋅dyuti⋅hara⋅dukūlāñcita⋅kaṭiḥ
sa devaś caitanyākṛtir atitarāṁ naḥ kṛpayatu

apāraṁ kasyāpi praṇayi⋅jana⋅vṛndasya kutukī
rasa⋅stomaṁ hṛtvā madhuram upabhoktuṁ kam api jaḥ
ruciṁ svām āvavre dyutim iha tadīyāṁ prakaṭayan
sa devaś caitanyākṛtir atitarāṁ naḥ kṛpayatu

anārādhyaḥ prītyā ciram asura⋅bhāva⋅praṇayināṁ
prapannānāṁ daivīṁ prakṛtim adhidaivaṁ trijagati
ajasraṁ jaḥ śrīmāñ jayati sahajānanda⋅madhuraḥ
sa devaś caitanyākṛtir atitarāṁ naḥ kṛpayatu

gatir jaḥ pauṇḍrāṇāṁ prakaṭita⋅navadvīpa⋅mahimā
bhavenālaŋkurvan bhuvana⋅mahitaṁ śrotriya⋅kulam
punāty aṅgīkārād bhuvi paramahaṁsāśrama⋅padaṁ
sa devaś caitanyākṛtir atitarāṁ naḥ kṛpayatu

mukhenāgre pītvā madhuram iha nāmāmṛta⋅rasaṁ
dṛśor dvārā jas taṁ vamati ghana⋅bāṣpāmbu⋅miṣataḥ
bhuvi premṇas tattvaṁ prakaṭayitum ullāsita⋅tanuḥ
sa devaś caitanyākṛtir atitarāṁ naḥ kṛpayatu

tanūm āviṣkurvan nava⋅puraṭa⋅bhāsaṁ kaṭi⋅lasat⋅
karaṅkālaṅkāras taruṇa⋅gaja⋅rājāñcita⋅gatiḥ
priyebhyo jaḥ śikṣāṁ diśati nija⋅nirmālya⋅rucibhiḥ
sa devaś caitanyākṛtir atitarāṁ naḥ kṛpayatu

smitālokaḥ śokaṁ harati jagatāṁ jasya parito
girāṁ tu prārambhaḥ kuśala⋅paṭalīṁ pallavayati
padālambaḥ kaṁ vā praṇayati nahi prema⋅nivahaṁ
sa devaś caitanyākṛtir atitarāṁ naḥ kṛpayatu

śacī⋅sūnoḥ kīrtiḥ stavaka⋅nava⋅saurabhya⋅niviḍaṁ
pumān jaḥ prītātmā paṭhati kila padyāṣṭakam idam
sa lakṣmīvān etaṁ nija⋅pada⋅saroje praṇayitāṁ
dadānaḥ kalyāṇīm anupadam abādhaṁ sukhayatu
IDX.en_translation:
In this age of Kali, learned persons worship Him—who is fair-complexioned but is Kṛṣṇa Himself—through sacrificial rites consisting of the loud chanting of His names. They declare Him to be the worshipable object for all who have accepted the renounced order of life. May that Lord, in the form of Caitanya, bestow upon us His abundant mercy.

He performs dear pastimes that are a source of joy even for the sinful, perfectly removing the sorrow of Mother Śacī with sounds of victory. His waist is adorned with silken cloth that surpasses the effulgence of the rising sun. May that Lord, in the form of Caitanya, bestow upon us His abundant mercy.

Eager to enjoy a certain boundless, sweet flood of mellows belonging to His loving devotees, He has stolen Their mood. He covered His own dark complexion and manifested Their golden effulgence here. May that Lord, in the form of Caitanya, bestow upon us His abundant mercy.

Though difficult to worship with love for a long time by those attached to a demoniac mentality, He bestows a divine nature upon the surrendered, being the supreme deity in the three worlds. He is glorious and naturally sweet with bliss and constantly triumphs. May that Lord, in the form of Caitanya, bestow upon us His abundant mercy.

He is the refuge of the people of Bengal and manifested the glories of Navadvīpa, adorning the community of learned priests glorified by the world. By accepting them, He purifies the status of the highest order of renunciants on earth. May that Lord, in the form of Caitanya, bestow upon us His abundant mercy.

Having first drunk the sweet nectar of the Holy Name through His mouth, He emits it through the doors of His eyes under the guise of profuse tears. His body is thrilled to manifest the essence of divine love on earth. May that Lord, in the form of Caitanya, bestow upon us His abundant mercy.

Manifesting a body with the effulgence of new gold, He is adorned at the waist with a water pot, and His gait is like that of a young elephant king. He gives instruction to His dear ones by the charm of His own remnants. May that Lord, in the form of Caitanya, bestow upon us His abundant mercy.

His smiling glance completely takes away the sorrow of the worlds, and the very beginning of His speech causes hosts of auspiciousness to sprout. The shelter of His feet bestows floods of divine love upon everyone. May that Lord, in the form of Caitanya, bestow upon us His abundant mercy.

The glories of the son of Śacī are as dense as the fresh fragrance of a cluster of flowers. May that fortunate person who reads these eight verses with a joyful heart be made happy at every step with unobstructed auspiciousness, as the Lord bestows devotion at His own lotus feet.

IDX.first_line: kalayati nayanam disi disi valitam
IDX.search: kalayati nayanam disi disi valitam pankajamiva mrdu maruta calitam keli vipinam pravisati radha pratipada samudita manasija badha
