IDX.verses:
kalayati nayanaṁ diśi diśi valitam
paṅkajamiva mṛdu⋅māruta⋅calitam

keli⋅vipinaṁ praviśati rādhā
pratipada⋅samudita manasija⋅bādhā

vinidadhatī mṛdu⋅manthara⋅pādam
racayati kuñjara⋅gatimanuvādam

janayati rudra⋅gajādhipa⋅muditam
rāmānanda⋅rāya⋅kavi⋅gaditam
IDX.en_translation:
Her eye turns in every direction, like a lotus flower moved by a gentle breeze.

(Rādhā) enters the pleasure grove; with every step, the obstacles of manasija (the god of love) arise.

Placing soft, slow steps, like the gait of an elephant.

Spoken by the poet Rāmānanda Rāya, this delights Rudra-gajādhipa (Gajapati Mahārāja Pratāparudra).

IDX.first_line: kali yuga pavana visvambhara
IDX.search: kali yuga pavana visvambhara gauda cita gagana sasadhara kirtana vidhata paraprema data saci suta purata sundara
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
kali⋅yuga⋅pāvana viśvambhara
gauḍa⋅cita⋅gagana⋅śaśadhara
 
kīrtana⋅vidhātā, paraprema⋅dātā,
śacī⋅suta puraṭa⋅sundara
IDX.en_translation:
All glories to Viśvambhara, the purifier of the Kali age! He is the moon in the sky of the hearts of the devotees of Bengal.

He is the ordainer of congregational chanting and the giver of supreme divine love—the son of Śacī, beautiful as pure gold.

IDX.first_line: kalighora timire garasala jagajana
IDX.search: kalighora timire garasala jagajana dharama karama rahu dura asadhane cintamani vidhi milaola ani gora bada dayara thakura bhaire bhai gora guna kahana na jaya kata sata anana kata caturanana varaniya ora nahi paya
IDX.author: nayanānanda dāsa
IDX.verses:
kali-ghora timire, garāsala jaga-jana,
dharama karama rahu dūra
asādhane cintāmaṇi, vidhi milāola āni’,
gorā baḍa dayāra ṭhākura

bhāire bhāi, gorā⋅guṇa kahana nā jāya
kata śata ānana, kata caturānana,
varaṇiyā ora nāhi pāya

cāriveda ṣaḍ⋅, daraśana paḍi’,
se jadi gaurāṅga nāhi bhaje
vṛthā tā’ra adhyayana, locanavihīnajana,
darapaṇe andhe kibā kāje

veda vidyā dui, kichui nā jānata,
se jadi gaurāṅga jāne sāra
nayanānanda bhaṇe, sei ta’ sakali jāne,
sarvasiddhi karatale tā’ra
IDX.en_translation:
In the dense darkness of the Age of Kali, the people of the world are swallowed up, and righteousness and pious activities remain far away. Without any effort on our part, destiny has brought us a wish-fulfilling gem. Lord Gorā is a very merciful Master.

O brothers! The qualities of Lord Gorā cannot be described. Even with hundreds of mouths, or even the four-faced Brahmā himself, one cannot reach the limit of His glories.

Having studied the four Vedas and the six systems of philosophy, if one does not worship Lord Gaurāṅga, his study is in vain. For a person without eyes, of what use is a mirror?

Even if one knows nothing of the Vedas or other knowledge, if he knows Lord Gaurāṅga as the essence, Nayanānanda says: "He indeed knows everything. All perfection is in the palm of his hand."

IDX.first_line: kalikukkura kadana jadi cao he
IDX.search: kalikukkura kadana jadi cao he kaliyuga pavana kalibhaya nasana srisaci nandana gao he gadadhara madana nitayera pranadhana advaitera prapujita gora nimani visvambhara srinivasa isvara bhaktasamuha cita cora
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
kalikukkura⋅kadana jadi cāo (he)
kaliyuga⋅pāvana, kalibhaya⋅nāśana,
śrī-śacī⋅nandana gāo (he)

gadādhara⋅mādana, nitā’yera prāṇa-dhana,
advaitera prapūjita gorā
nimāñi viśvambhara, śrīnivāsa⋅īśvara,
bhaktasamūha⋅cita⋅corā

nadīyā⋅śaśadhara, māyāpura⋅īśvara,
nāma⋅pravartana sura
gṛhi⋅jana⋅śikṣaka, nyāsikula⋅nāyaka,
mādhava rādhā-bhāva-pura

sārva-bhauma-śodhana, gajapati⋅tāraṇa,
rāmānanda⋅poṣaṇa vīra
rūpānanda⋅vardhana, sanātana⋅pālana,
haridāsa⋅modana dhīra

vrajarasa⋅bhāvana, duṣṭamata⋅śātana,
kapaṭi⋅vighātana kāma
śuddhabhakta⋅pālana, śuṣkajñāna⋅tāḍana,
chalabhakti⋅dūṣaṇa rāma
IDX.en_translation:
If you wish to conquer the dogs of the Kali age, sing the glories of the son of Śacī, who purifies the age and destroys its fears.

He is the enchanter of Gadādhara and the life-treasure of Nitāi; the fair-complexioned Lord worshiped by Advaita, Nimāi, and Viśvambhara—the Lord of Śrīnivāsa and the thief of the hearts of all devotees.

He is the moon of Nadiyā, the Lord of Māyāpur, and the hero who inaugurates the chanting of the Holy Name. He is the teacher of householders, the leader of the renunciants, and the Lord who is fully imbued with the emotions of Rādhā.

He purified Sārvabhauma and delivered King Pratāparudra; He is the heroic protector of Rāmānanda Rāya, the one who increases the bliss of Rūpa Gosvāmī, the maintainer of Sanātana Gosvāmī, and the sober Lord who enchants Haridāsa Ṭhākura.

He cultivates the mellows of Vraja and destroys wicked philosophies. He is the desire to defeat the deceitful and the protector of pure devotees—the Lord who chastises dry speculation and condemns hypocritical devotion.

IDX.first_line: kandarpa koti ramyaya sphurad indivara tvise
IDX.search: kandarpa koti ramyaya sphurad indivara tvise jagan mohana lilaya namo gopendra sunave krsnala krta haraya krsna lavanya saline krsnakula karindraya krsnaya karavai namah
IDX.author: śrīla rūpa gosvāmī
IDX.verses:
kandarpa⋅koṭi⋅ramyāya sphurad⋅indīvara⋅tviṣe
jagan⋅mohana⋅līlāya namo gopendra⋅sūnave

kṛṣṇalā⋅kṛta⋅hārāya kṛṣṇa⋅lāvaṇya⋅śāline
kṛṣṇākūla⋅karīndrāya kṛṣṇāya karavai namaḥ

sarvānanda⋅kadambāya kandamba⋅kusuma⋅sraje
namaḥ premāvalambāya pralambāri⋅kanīyase

kuṇḍala⋅sphurad⋅aṁsāya vaṁśāyatta⋅mukha⋅śriye
rādhā⋅mānasa⋅haṁsāya vrajottaṁsāya te namaḥ

namaḥ śikhaṇḍa⋅cūḍāya daṇḍa⋅maṇḍita⋅pāṇaye
kuṇḍalī⋅kṛta⋅puṣpāya puṇḍarīkekṣaṇāya te

rādhikā⋅prema⋅mādhvīka⋅mādhurī⋅muditāntaram
kandarpa⋅vṛnda⋅saundaryaṁ govindam abhivādaye

śṛṅgāra⋅rasa⋅śṛṅgāraṁ karṇikārātta⋅karṇikam
vande śriyā navābhrāṇāṁ vibhrāṇaṁ vibhramaṁ harim

sādhvī⋅vrata⋅maṇi⋅vrāta⋅paśyato⋅hara⋅veṇave
kahlāra⋅kṛta⋅cūḍāya śaṅkha⋅cūḍa⋅bhide namaḥ

rādhikādhara⋅bandhūka⋅makaranda⋅madhuvratam
daitya⋅sindhura⋅pārīndraṁ vande gopendra⋅nandanam

varhendrāyudha⋅ramyāya jagaj⋅jīvana⋅dāyine
rādhā⋅vidyud⋅vṛtāṅgāya kṛṣṇāmbhodāya te namaḥ

premāndha⋅vallavī⋅vṛnda⋅locanendīvarendave
kāśmīra⋅tilakāḍhyāya namaḥ pītāmbarāya te

gīrvāṇeśa⋅madoddāma dāva⋅nirvāṇa⋅nīradam
kandūkī⋅kṛta⋅śailendraṁ vande gokula⋅bāndhavam

dainyārṇave nimagno’smi mantu⋅grāva⋅bharārditaḥ
duṣṭe kāruṇya⋅pārīṇa mayi kṛṣṇa kṛpāṁ kuru

ādhāro’py aparādhānām aviveka⋅hato’py aham
tvat⋅kāruṇya⋅pratīkṣo’smi prasīda mayi mādhava
IDX.en_translation:
Salutations to the son of Nanda, who is charming like millions of Cupids, whose effulgence is like a blooming blue lotus, and whose pastimes enchant the world.

I offer my obeisances to Kṛṣṇa, who wears a garland of berries, who possesses dark beauty, and who is like a powerful, dark elephant.

Salutations to Him who is a cluster of all joys, who wears a garland of kadamba flowers, who is the support of divine love, and who is the younger brother of Balarāma.

Salutations to You, whose shoulders shine with earrings, whose face is beautiful with the flute, who is the swan in the mind of Rādhā, and who is the best jewel of Vraja.

Salutations to You, who wears a peacock feather on His crown, whose hand is adorned with a staff, who wears flowers as earrings, and whose eyes are like lotuses.

I bow down to Govinda, whose heart is delighted by the sweetness of the nectar-like love of Rādhikā, and whose beauty is like a multitude of Cupids.

I offer obeisances to Hari, who is the ornament of the mellow of conjugal love, who has yellow flowers in His ears, and who assumes the charm of fresh dark clouds with His splendor.

Salutations to Him whose flute steals the vows of chaste women just by its sight, who has a white lotus on His crown, and who is the slayer of Śaṅkha-cūḍa.

I offer obeisances to the son of Nanda, who is like a bee to the honey of the red flower of Rādhikā's lips and like a lion to the elephants of the demons.

Salutations to You, who are beautiful with a peacock feather like a rainbow, the giver of life to the universe, whose body is surrounded by Rādhā like lightning, and who are like a dark cloud.

Salutations to You who wear yellow garments, who are like the moon to the lotuses of the eyes of the love-blinded cowherd girls, and who are adorned with a mark of saffron.

I offer obeisances to the friend of Gokula, who is like a cloud extinguishing the fire of Indra's pride and who made Govardhana Hill into a play-ball.

O Kṛṣṇa, O ocean of mercy! I am immersed in the ocean of misery, afflicted by the heavy burden of my offenses. Please bestow Your mercy upon me, the wicked one.

O Mādhava! Even though I am a repository of offenses and slain by ignorance, I am awaiting Your mercy. Please be pleased with me.

IDX.first_line: kanta sane vihariya svakiya mandire giya
IDX.search: kanta sane vihariya svakiya mandire giya snana kari nija ramyavese krsnasukhalokanande rahe dhani paranande sakhigana saha krsnavese gocarana hoite kanta asibena haye sranta sei sranti karibare dura nanavidha bhaksyapeya prastuta karena teha upahara dibena pracura
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
kānta⋅sane vihariyā, svakīya mandire giyā,
snāna kari nija ramyaveśe
kṛṣṇasukhālokānande, rahe dhanī parānande,
sakhīgaṇa saha kṛṣṇāveśe

gocāraṇa hoite kānta, āsibena haye śrānta,
sei śrānti karibāre dūra
nānāvidha bhakṣyapeya, prastuta karena te̐ha ,
upahāra dibena pracura

śrī⋅rādhā⋅ramaṇa hari, sakhāgaṇa saṅge kari’,
dhenuvṛnda loiyā takhana
śrī⋅rādhā⋅darśana⋅sukha, labhi’ vraja⋅abhimukha,
calilena śrī⋅vaṁśīvadana

pitāmātā ādi sabe, āgubāḍi āsi’ tabe,
ānande dekhaye kṛṣṇamukha
śrī⋅gātra⋅mārjana kari’, jaśomati laye hari,
ādara kariyā pāya sukha
IDX.en_translation:
Having sported with Her Beloved and then returned to Her own home, Rādhā bathes and dresses in beautiful attire. In the joy of seeing Lord Kṛṣṇa's happiness, She remains in supreme bliss with Her gopī friends, completely absorbed in thoughts of Him.

Her Beloved will soon return tired from herding the cows, and to remove that tiredness, She prepares various kinds of food and drink and offers many gifts.

Lord Hari, the Beloved of Rādhā, taking His cowherd friends and the cows, then returns toward Vraja after having attained the happiness of seeing Śrīmati Rādhikā.

All His family members, including His father and mother, come forward to meet Him and happily gaze upon the Lord's face. After Mother Yaśodā wipes His beautiful body, she shows Him great affection and finds great happiness.

IDX.first_line: karanga kaupina lana cheda kantha gaye diya
IDX.search: karanga kaupina lana cheda kantha gaye diya teyagiba sakala visaya krsne anuraga habe vrajera nikunje kabe jaiya kariba nijalaya hari hari kabe mora hoibe sudina phalamula vrndavane khana diba avasane bhramiba hoiya udasina
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
karaṅga kaupīna lañā, che̐ḍā kānthā gāye diyā,
teyāgiba sakala viṣaya
kṛṣṇe anurāga ha’be, vrajera nikuñje kabe,
jāiyā kariba nijālaya

hari hari! kabe mora hoibe sudina
phalamūla vṛndāvane, khāñā dibā avasāne,
bhramiba hoiyā udāsīna

śītala jamunā⋅jale, snāna kari’ kutūhale,
premāveśe ānandita hañā
bāhura upara bāhu tuli’, vṛndāvane kuli⋅kuli,
kṛṣṇa bali’ beḍāba kāndiyā

dekhiba saṅketa⋅sthāna, juḍābe tāpita prāṇa,
premāveśe gaḍāgaḍi diba
kā̐hā rādhā prāṇeśvari, kā̐hā girivaradhāri,
kā̐hā nātha baliyā ḍākiba

mādhavī-kuñjera’pari, sukhe vasi’ śuka-śārī,
gāibeka rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa⋅rasa
tarumūle vasi’ tāhā, śuni’ juḍāiba hiyā,
kabe sukhe goṅāba divasa

śrī⋅govinda⋅gopīnātha, śrīmatī⋅rādhikā⋅sātha,
dekhiba ratana⋅siŋhāsane
dīna narottama⋅dāsa, karaye durlabha āśa,
ei mati haibe kata-dine
IDX.en_translation:
Taking a water pot and a loincloth and wearing a torn quilted cloth, I will give up all worldly attachments. When will my devotion to Kṛṣṇa arise, and when will I make my home in the groves of Vraja?

O Lord Hari! When will that auspicious day be mine? Eating fruits and roots in Vṛndāvana, I will wander with detachment at the end of the day.

Joyfully bathing in the cool waters of the Yamunā, absorbed in ecstatic love, I will become joyful. Raising my arms and calling out "Kṛṣṇa!" along the banks of Vṛndāvana, I will wander while weeping.

I will see the secret meeting places, and my distressed heart will be soothed; absorbed in ecstatic love, I will roll on the ground. "Where is Rādhā, my life's queen? Where is the lifter of Govardhana Hill? Where is my Lord?" I will call out.

In the bower of mādhavī flowers, parrots and mynah birds will sit happily and sing of the sweet mellows of the divine love of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa. Sitting at the foot of a tree and hearing that, my heart will be soothed. When will I pass my days in such happiness?

I will behold Śrī Govinda and Gopīnātha, along with Śrīmatī Rādhikā, on a jeweled throne. Humble Narottama dāsa cherishes this rare hope: when will such a state of mind come to pass?

vrajāṅganā⋅śiraḥśobhā, dharmilla⋅mallikā⋅prabhā,

ramāpati⋅śobhā jini’, kṛṣṇa tava rūpakhāni,
jagat mātāya vrajavane
ramā⋅jini, vrajāṅganā⋅, gaṇamadhye suśobhanā,

tavāṅga⋅saurabhakaṇa, vaṁśīgīta anukṣaṇa,

rādhe! aṅgagandha tava, tomāra suvīṇārava,

tomāra capalekṣaṇa, hare rādhā⋅dhairyadhana,

bā̐kā dṛṣṭi bhaṅgī tava, śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅hṛdayāsaba,

parihāse rādhikāra, kathā nāhi sare jāra,
tumi kṛṣṇa naṭakulaguru
kṛṣṇa⋅narma ukti śuni’, romāñcita tanukhāni,
tava rādhe rasakalpataru

aprākṛta⋅guṇamaṇi, vinirmita⋅giriśreṇī,
tumi kṛṣṇa sarva guṇamaya
umādi ramaṇījana⋅, vāñchanīya guṇagaṇa,

āmi ati mandamati, kari he kākuti nati,
niṣkapaṭe e prārthanā kari

tomādera kṛpā pāi, erūpa jogyatā nāi,

du̐he mahākṛpāmaya, jāni’ kainu padāśraya,

kevala ayogya nahi, aparādhī āmi hoi,

loke kṛpāviṣṭa jana, kṣame aparādhagaṇa,
tumi du̐he mahā⋅kṛpāvān

kṛpāhetu bhaktisāra, leśābhāsa nāhi tā’ra,
kṛpā⋅adhikārī nahi āmi
du̐he mahālīleśvara, hañā sei līlāpara,
kṛpā kara vraja⋅jana⋅svāmi

suduṣṭa abhakta jane, śivādi devatāgaṇe,
prasanna hoila kṛpā kari’
mahālīla sarveśvara, du̐hu mama prāṇeśvara,
dayā kara doṣa parihari’

adhame uttama māni, muḍha⋅vijña, abhimānī,
duṣṭa hañā śiṣṭa⋅abhimāna
ei doṣe doṣī hañā, gela ciradina vañā,
nā karinu bhajana⋅vidhāna

tathāpi e dīna⋅jane, jadi nāma⋅uccāraṇe,
nāmābhāsa karila jīvane
sarvadoṣa⋅nivāraṇa, du̐hu⋅nāma⋅saṁjalpana⋅
prasāde prasīda dui jana

bhakti⋅lavamātre kṣaya, sarva aparādha haya,
kṣamāśīla du̐hera kṛpāya
e āśā mane dhari’, caraṇe prārthanā kari,
śodha doṣa kṣamiyā āmāya

sādhana⋅sampattihīna, ohe ei jīva dīna,
atikaṣṭe dhṛṣṭatāra chāra
du̐hupāda⋅nipatita, prārthanā karaye hita,
prasannatā houka dohāra

dante tṛṇa dhari’ hāya, kā̐diteche ubharāya,
e pāpīra kampita śarīra
‘hā nātha! hā nātha’! bali’, ha’ye āji kṛtāñjali,
prasāda arpiyā kara sthira

e durbhāgā hā hā svare, prasāda prārthanā kare
anutāpe gaḍāgaḍi jāya
he rādhe! he kṛṣṇa⋅candra! śuna mama kākuvāda,
tu̐hu kṛpā vinā prāṇa jāya

phutkāra kariyā kā̐de, āhā āhā kākunāde,
bale hoo prasanna āmāya
ei ta’ ayogya jane, kṛpā kara nijaguṇe,
karuṇāsāgara rākha pāya

mukhete aṅguṣṭha diyā, uccaiḥsvare ārta hañā,
kā̐dite kīdite bale, nātha !
karuṇā⋅kaṇikādāne, rakṣā kara mora prāṇe,
kara ei dīne ātmasātha

ei tava mūḍha jana, dīnavākye satrundana,
prārthanā karaye dṛḍhamane
he karuṇā⋅sunidhāna! anugati kara dāna,
karuṇormicchaṭā vrajavane

bhāva cittasukhakara, jata āche sumadhura,
prakaṭāprakaṭa līlāsthale
rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa⋅premasāra, sakalera sārātsāra,
sei bhāva je̐i kṛpābale

jadi e dāsīra prati, prasanna karuṇāmati,
du̐hu⋅padasevā kara dāna
āra kichu nāhi cāi, jugala caraṇa pāi,
śītala houka mora prāṇa

anātha⋅vatsala tumi, adhama anātha āmi,
tvadīya sākṣāt⋅dāsya māgi
e prasāda kara dāna, rākha anāthera prāṇa,
chāḍi’ saba tava dāsya māgi

śirete añjali dhari’, opade vijñapti kari,
āmāra abhīṣṭa nivedana
ekabāra dāsya diyā, śītala kara he hiyā,
tabe māni sārthaka jīvana

kabe du̐he ei vane, vilokiba sammilane,
amūlyāṅga parimala⋅ghrāṇa
āmāra nāsikā⋅dvāre, praveśiyā cittapure,

du̐hāra nūpura⋅dhvani, haṁsa⋅kaṇṭhasvara jini’,
madhura madhura mama kāṇe
praveśiyā kona kṣaṇe, mama cita surañjane,
mātāibe sevārasa⋅pāne

dekhiyā e dāsī kabe, bhāsibe ānandotsave,

sakala⋅saundaryāspada, nīrājita du̐hupada,
he rādhe! he nandera nandana !
mamākṣi⋅gocare kabe, sarvādbhūta mahotsave,
karibe ānanda vitaraṇa

prācīnāśā, phalapūrti, tu̐hu padāmbuja⋅sphurti,
sei du̐hujana⋅daraśana
janme ki ha’be mama, e utkaṇṭhā suviṣama,

kariba sundara du̐hu jane
surata⋅līlāya rata, āmā hoite adūrata,

ghaṭanāvaśataḥ kabe, du̐huyoga asambhave,
paraspara sandeśa āniyā
bāḍāiba du̐hu sukha, jābe tabe manoduḥkha,
beḍāiba ānande mātiyā

kabe ei vṛndāvane, du̐hu du̐hā adarśane,
phire jāba du̐he anveṣiyā
sammilana karāiba, hāra⋅padakādi pāba,
parituṣṭa du̐hāre kariyā

du̐he hāra dhari’ paṇe, dyūtakrīḍā⋅samāpane,
‘āmi jayī’, ‘āmi jayī’ bali’
karibe kalaha tabe, hāra⋅saŋgrahete kabe,
āmi tāhā dekhiba sakali

āhā! kabe dui jane, kuñjamājhe suśayane,
kusuma⋅śayyāya virāmibe
se samaye du̐hupada⋅, samvāhana susampada,
e dāsīra saubhāgya milibe

kandarpa⋅kalahodgāre, chi̐ḍibe kaṇṭhera hāre,
latāgṛhe paḍibe khasiyā
se hāra gā̐thibe kabe, e dāsī niyukta ha’be

śikhipiñcha kare dhari’, kuntala⋅maṇḍita kari’,

kandarpa⋅krīḍāya jabe, du̐hu⋅srak srasta ha’be,
tabe āmi du̐hu⋅ājñā pāñā
ubhaya⋅lalāṭamājhe, kariba tilaka⋅sāje,

kṛṣṇa! tava vakṣe āmi, vanamālā diyā svāmi!
rādhe! tava nayane kajjala
kuñjamājhe kona dina, pā’ba sukha samīcīna,

śīrāśūnya karpūrādi juta
vīṭikā nirmāṇa kari’, du̐hu mukhe diba dhari’,

kothā e durāśā mora, kothā e duṣkarma ghora,
e prārthanā, jadi bala kena
he rādhe! he ghanaśyāma! du̐hujana⋅guṇagrāma⋅,
mādhurī balāya more hena

du̐hāra je kṛpāguṇe, pāinu dhāma⋅vṛndāvane,
se kṛpā abhīṣṭa pūraṇa
karuna āmāya nātha! pāñā tu̐hu⋅sakhī sātha,
kuñjasevā pāi anukṣaṇa

ohe rādhe! ohe kṛṣṇa! sei vrajarasatṛṣṇa,
kārpaṇya⋅pañjikā⋅kathā⋅chale
jalpanā karaye sadā, tā’ra vāñchā⋅pūrti tadā,
karuna du̐hu kṛpā⋅bale

śrī⋅rūpamañjarī⋅pada, śire dhari’ susampada,
kamala⋅mañjarī kare āśā
śrī⋅godruma⋅vrajavane, du̐hu⋅līlā⋅sandarśane,
pūrṇa hou rasera pipāsā

iti kārpaṇya⋅pañjikā samāpta
IDX.en_translation:
Kārpaṇya-pañjikā Or A Humble Petition I, with a very humble mind, residing in a grove of Vraja, today offer whatever is in my heart at the lotus feet of the Divine Couple, Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa, while weeping and abandoning all social shame.

You, Lord Kṛṣṇa, are a blue jewel, conquering the splendor of new clouds, and You bestow joy upon Vraja. You, Rādhā, are ever fair, stealing the pride of gorocana; in Vraja You captivate the mind of the moon-like Kṛṣṇa.

You, Lord Kṛṣṇa, in yellow garments, conquering the luster of gold, are always engaged in eternal pastimes in the forest of Vraja. You, Rādhā, in blue garments, stealing the pride of the palāśa flower, are always assisting Kṛṣṇa's pastimes.

You, Lord Kṛṣṇa, are a jewel among men and the crest-jewel of all youth; Śrīmati Rādhikā is the Mistress of Your life. You, Rādhā, are the ornament on the heads of the gopīs, and with the splendor of jasmine in Your hair, You are Kṛṣṇa's most beloved.

Conquering the beauty of Lord Viṣṇu, Your beautiful form, Lord Kṛṣṇa, intoxicates the entire world in Vraja. Conquering the Goddess of Fortune, You, Rādhā, are the most beautiful among the gopīs and reside in the courtyard of Kṛṣṇa's heart.

The particles of fragrance from Your body, O Kṛṣṇa, and the song of Your flute always captivate Rādhā's mind. O Rādhā! The fragrance of Your body and the sweet sound of Your vīṇā enchant Kṛṣṇa's heart.

Your restless glances steal Rādhā's treasure of patience; You, Lord Kṛṣṇa, are the crest-jewel of thieves. Your crooked glances and gestures are like wine for Śrī Kṛṣṇa's heart; You, Rādhā, are the wealthy possessor of all arts.

In jest, Rādhikā's words sometimes fail Her; You, Lord Kṛṣṇa, are the Master of all dancers. Hearing Kṛṣṇa's humorous words, Her body becomes thrilled; You, Rādhā, are the wish-fulfilling tree of divine mellows.

A jewel of transcendental qualities, You, Lord Kṛṣṇa, are full of all good qualities. Those qualities desired even by the goddesses are natural for You, O Rādhā.

I am very dull-minded, yet I make these humble entreaties and obeisances sincerely. O Master of Vṛndāvana! You, Lord Kṛṣṇa, are the Lord of my life. You, Rādhā, are the Empress of the forests of Vraja.

I have no qualification to receive Your mercy, although I am in Vṛndāvana. You both are supremely merciful; knowing this, I have taken shelter of Your feet. Please bestow mercy upon this lowly person.

Not only am I unqualified, I am also an offender; still, please grant me Your mercy. Merciful people in this world forgive offenses; You both are the most merciful.

Of that devotion which is the cause of mercy, there is not even a trace in me; I am not worthy of Your mercy. You both are the great Lords of pastimes; being devoted to those pastimes, please show mercy, O Masters of the residents of Vraja!

Even to very wicked and undevoted persons, and to Lord Śiva and the other demigods, You are pleased to show mercy. O great Masters of all pastimes! Both of You are the Lords of my life; please show kindness, overlooking my many faults.

Considering a lowly person like me to be great, being ignorant but thinking myself wise and proud, and being wicked but appearing well-behaved—being guilty of all these faults, my whole life has passed in delusion. I did not perform the prescribed devotional service.

Still, if this poor person has made even a faint appearance of the Holy Name in his life—that Name which removes all faults—then by the grace of Your Names, please be pleased with me.

Even by a trace of devotion, all offenses are destroyed by the mercy of You both, who are so forgiving. Holding this hope in my heart, I pray at Your feet; please purify my faults by forgiving me.

Devoid of the wealth of spiritual practice, this poor soul, fallen at Your feet, prays for his welfare. May Your kindness be upon me.

Alas, holding a blade of grass in my teeth and weeping profusely, my body trembles. Crying "O Master! O Master!", I offer my prostrated obeisances; please offer me Your mercy and stabilize my heart.

This unfortunate soul prays for Your mercy and rolls on the ground in repentance. O Rādhā! O moon-like Kṛṣṇa! Please hear my humble entreaty; without Your mercy, my life will depart.

Crying with piteous gasps, I beg You: "Please be pleased with me." To this unqualified person, please show mercy out of Your own compassion; O ocean of mercy, please keep me at Your feet.

Placing my thumb in my mouth in total submission and crying loudly in distress, I say: "O Master! By granting me a particle of mercy, please save my life. Take this poor person as Your own."

This foolish person of Yours, with humble words and weeping, prays with a firm mind. O treasure house of mercy! Please bestow upon me the privilege of following You. Let waves of mercy sprinkle upon the forest of Vraja.

I pray for that sentiment which brings joy to the heart, which is the most sweet essence of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa's love. May I attain that sentiment by Your mercy.

If You are pleased with this maidservant, please grant the service of both Your feet. I desire nothing else; to attain the lotus feet of the Divine Couple, may my heart become cool.

You are affectionate to the helpless, and I am a fallen and helpless person. I beg for Your direct service. Please bestow this mercy and save the life of this helpless one. Giving up everything else, I beg for Your service.

Offering my obeisances, I make this humble request at Your feet. This is my cherished desire. By once granting me Your service, please cool my heart. Only then will I consider my life successful.

When will I behold both of You in this forest in a meeting? The invaluable fragrance of Your bodies, entering the city of my heart through my nose, will render me unconscious in ecstasy.

The sound of Your ankle bells, sweeter than the voice of swans, is sweet to my ears. When will it enter my heart and intoxicate me with the mellow of Your service?

Auspicious signs like the disc are observed on both Your feet in the forest of Vṛndāvana. When will this maidservant, seeing them, float in a festival of joy, having secretly received the mercy of both of You?

O Rādhā! O Son of Nanda! When will You bestow joy upon me by appearing within my sight in an all-surpassing great festival?

Will the ancient hope of seeing Your lotus feet ever be fulfilled in my life? This intense anxiety disturbs my mind constantly.

When will I see the beautiful Divine Couple in Vṛndāvana, within the groves, engaged in Their intimate pastimes? I will be immersed in love upon seeing Them.

When, by chance, will I increase the happiness of both by bringing messages between You? Then all my mental distress will vanish, and I will wander about, intoxicated with joy.

When, in this Vṛndāvana, will I search for You and bring You together when You are separated? I will arrange Your meeting and receive garlands and medallions after satisfying both of You.

When You wager a garland at the conclusion of a game of dice, and both say, "I am victorious!", then You will quarrel. When You collect the garland, I will witness it all.

Alas! When will You both rest beautifully in the groves, lying on a bed of flowers? At that time, massaging Your feet will be my greatest fortune; when will this maidservant attain that luck?

In the outburst of Cupid's quarrel, the garlands around Your necks will break and fall off. When will this maidservant be engaged to re-string that garland, receiving Your merciful command?

When, in the waves of pastimes, Your hair becomes disheveled, I will receive a hint from You and, holding a peacock feather, I will adorn Your locks while remaining immersed in joy.

When in the sports of love Your garlands become disheveled, I will receive Your command and make tilaka decorations on both Your foreheads; I will become intoxicated seeing that beauty.

O Kṛṣṇa! When will I put a forest garland on Your chest? O Rādhā! When will I apply eyeliner to Your eyes? When will I find such perfect happiness in the groves? My heart will overflow with divine love.

When, taking a yellow betel leaf, free from veins, with camphor and other spices, will I prepare a betel quid and place it in Your mouths? My heart will be inundated with love.

Where is this desperate hope of mine, and where are my grave misdeeds? If you ask why I make this prayer—O Rādhā! O dark Lord Kṛṣṇa! The beauty of Your qualities and glories compels me to speak this way.

By Your mercy I have attained the abode of Vṛndāvana. May that mercy fulfill my desire, O Master! Attaining the companionship of Your sakhīs, may I constantly receive service in the groves.

O Rādhā! O Kṛṣṇa! This soul, thirsty for the mellows of Vraja, always speaks through this humble appeal. May both of You fulfill his desire by the power of Your mercy.

Holding the lotus feet of Śrī Rūpa Mañjarī upon her head, Kamala Mañjarī hopes that by seeing Your pastimes in Vraja, her thirst for transcendental mellows may be fulfilled.

Thus ends the Kārpaṇya-pañjikā.

IDX.first_line: kata paricarya kari juvadvaye tusta kari
IDX.search: kata paricarya kari juvadvaye tusta kari vrnda dohara sadhila milana sakhigana mili tabe sei prema mahotsave gana narma praheli natana sulapana rasalasya kari nanavidha dasya jugalavilasa dekhe kata prestha sabigana gheri badaya rati madhuri ujjvala sevaya thake rata gandhamalya sutambula vyajanadi himajala pada samvahana adi jata
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
kata paricaryā kari’, juvadvaye tuṣṭa kari’,
vṛndā dohāra sādhila milana
sakhīgaṇa mili tabe, sei prema⋅mahotsave,
gāna narma prahelī naṭana

sulapana rasalāsya, kari nānāvidha dāsya,
jugalavilāsa dekhe kata
preṣṭha sabīgaṇa gheri’, bāḍāya rati⋅mādhurī,
ujjvala⋅sevāya thāke rata
gandhamālya sutāmbūla, vyajanādi himajala,
pāda⋅samvāhanā ādi jata

jāra jei sevā kare, premānande ataḥpare,
rādhā⋅kṛṣṇe deya nānā sukha
viśrānti ālāpa⋅chale, nibhṛta nikuñjasthale,
nidrā āsi’ hoila sevonmukha

sukusuma⋅śayyopari, nidrā jāya rādhā⋅hari,
cāridike supta sakhīgaṇa
sukhanidrā⋅bhāva lakhi’, vaneśvarī vṛndāsakhī,
nibhṛta karila vṛndāvana

vṛndāra śāsana māni’, nistabdha sakala prāṇī,
vanavāsī paśupakṣīgaṇa
kuñje rādhā⋅dāmodara, sakhīsaṅge nidrāpara,
vṛndā ekā praharī takhana
IDX.en_translation:
Vṛndā-devī performed many services to satisfy the Divine Couple and arranged Their meeting. Then the gopīs gathered in that great festival of divine love, singing, joking, and dancing.

With graceful dancing and by performing various services, they watched many of the Divine Couple's pastimes. The dearest gopīs surrounded Them, increasing the sweetness of Their love while remaining engaged in brilliant service with fragrant garlands, betel nuts, and cool water.

Each gopī performed her specific service, and thus they all gave various kinds of happiness to Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa. Under the pretext of rest and conversation in the secluded grove, sleep came, ready to offer its service.

Upon a bed of beautiful flowers, Lord Hari and Śrīmati Rādhā⋅rāṇī sleep, while all around Them the gopīs also sleep. Observing Their peaceful slumber, Vṛndā-devī made the forest of Vṛndāvana completely secluded and silent.

Obeying Vṛndā's command, all the forest creatures, animals, and birds became silent. In the grove, Lord Dāmodara and Rādhā were asleep with Their companions, and Vṛndā-devī alone remained as the guard.

IDX.first_line: ke go tumi kangala vese desa videse ghure bedao
IDX.search: ke go tumi kangala vese desa videse ghure bedao ati bada vyathara vyathi tai nayana jala vaksa bhasao adhama patita acandale snehera kole loo go tule divya premera akhi khule bhava vanchita pada dekhaiya dao
IDX.verses:
ke go tumi kāṅgāla⋅veśe, deśa⋅videśe ghure beḍāo
ati baḍa vyathāra vyathi, (tāi) nayana⋅jala vaksa bhāsāo

adhama patita ācaṇḍāle, snehera kole loo go tule,
divya⋅premera ā̐khi khule, bhava⋅vāñchita⋅pada dekhāiyā dāo

emana dayāla ke go tumi, vilāle prema⋅cintāmaṇi,
dhara loo ba’le premera khani, ācaṇḍāle bilāiyā dāo

ācaṇḍāle prema vilāle, tri⋅tāpa⋅jvālā juḍāile,
(māyā⋅) mugdha⋅jīvera bhava⋅kṣudhā, ciratare miṭāiyā dāo

jamunāra kūle kadambera mūle, bājāte bā̐śī rādhā ba’le
sei nā tumi gaura haye, nade ese jīva tarāo
IDX.en_translation:
Who are You in the dress of a beggar, wandering in countries and foreign lands? You feel great pain for the suffering (so) You float Your chest in the water of Your eyes.

You take the lowly, fallen, down to the Caṇḍāla, into the lap of affection. Opening the eyes of divine love, You show the feet desired by the world.

Who are You, such a merciful person? You distributed the touchstone of love. Saying "Take, take," You distributed the mine of love down to the Caṇḍāla.

You distributed love down to the Caṇḍāla, You cooled the burning of the three-fold miseries. You satisfied the material hunger of the (māyā-)bewildered living beings forever.

On the bank of the Yamunā, at the root of the Kadamba, You used to play the flute calling "Rādhā". Are You not He, becoming Gaura, coming to Nadīyā to deliver the living beings?

IDX.first_line: kena ara kara dvesa videsi jana bhajane
IDX.search: kena ara kara dvesa videsi jana bhajane bhajanera linga nana nana dese nana jane keha mukta kacche bhaje keha hatu gadi puje keha va nayana mudi thake brahma aradhane
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
kena āra kara dveṣa, videśi⋅jana⋅bhajane
bhajanera liṅga nānā, nānā deśe nānā jane

keha mukta⋅kacche bhaje, keha hāṭu gāḍi’ pūje
keha vā nayana mudi’ thāke brahma⋅ārādhāne

keha jogāsane pūje, keha saṅkīrtane maje
sakalei bhajiche sei ekamātra kṛṣṇa-dhane

atoeva bhrātṛbhāve, thāka sabe susadbhāve
hari-bhakti sādha sadā, e jīvane vā maraṇe
IDX.en_translation:
Why do you still harbor hatred for the worship of foreign people? The forms of worship are varied, differing across lands and peoples.

Some worship with loose garments, some worship kneeling down, and some remain with eyes closed in worship of the Absolute.

Some worship in yogic postures, while some delight in congregational chanting. All of them are worshiping that one and only Kṛṣṇa, the sole treasure.

Therefore, all of you, remain in a brotherly spirit with good will. Always practice devotion to God, whether in this life or in death.

IDX.first_line: kena hare krsna nama hari bale
IDX.search: kena hare krsna nama hari bale mano prana kade na pakhi na jani kona aparadhe mukhe hare krsna nama bala na banera pakhi re dhare rakhalama hrdaya mandire madhu makha ei hari nama pakhi re sikhaile sikhe
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
kena hare kṛṣṇa nāma hari bale
mano prāṇa kā̐de nā
pākhi nā jāni kona aparādhe
mukhe hare kṛṣṇa nāma bala nā

banera pākhi re dhare rākhalāma hṛdaya mandire
madhu mākhā ei hari nāma pākhi re śikhāile śikhe

pākhi sakala nāma balate pāra
kena hare kṛṣṇa nāma bala nā
kena hare kṛṣṇa nāma hari bale mano prāṇa kā̐de nā

cala pākhi rūpera deśe jāi
je deśete manera mānuṣa āsā⋅yāoyā nāi

pākhi re tora maraṇa kālete caḍabi bā̐śera dolāte
ore cāra janete kā̐dhe kare laye jābe śmaśāna ghāṭete

ore o tora mukhe āguna jihve tule
ki karabi tāi bala nā
IDX.en_translation:
Why don't my mind and heart weep when chanting the Hare Kṛṣṇa and saying Hari? O bird, I don't know for what offense you do not chant the Hare Kṛṣṇa with your mouth.

O forest bird, I caught you and kept you in the temple of my heart. O bird, if taught, you will learn this Hari, which is sweet as honey.

O bird, you can say all names, why do you not chant the Hare Kṛṣṇa? Why don't my mind and heart weep when chanting the Hare Kṛṣṇa and saying Hari?

Come, O bird, let's go to the land of true beauty, where worldly-minded people cannot come and go.

O bird, at the time of your death, you will be placed on a bamboo stretcher. O, four people will carry you on their shoulders to the cremation ground.

O, when they place fire in your mouth and raise your tongue, what will you do then? Tell me.

IDX.first_line: ki kahaba re sakhi ajuka ananda ora
IDX.search: ki kahaba re sakhi ajuka ananda ora ciradina madhava mandire mora papa sudhakara jata sukha dela piyamukha darasane tata sukha bhela
IDX.author: śrī⋅vidyāpati
IDX.verses:
ki kahaba re sakhi ājuka ānanda ora
ciradina mādhava mandire mora

pāpa sudhākara jata sukha dela
piyāmukha⋅daraśane tata sukha bhela

ā̐cara bhariyā jadi mahānidhi pāi
tab hām piyā dūra deśe nā pāṭhāi

śītera oḍanī piyā, giriṣāra vā
variṣāra chatra piyā, dariyāra nā

bhaṇaye vidyāpati, śuna varanāri
sujanake duḥkha divasa dui cāri
IDX.en_translation:
O friend! What can I say of today's endless joy? My Lord Mādhava is in the temple forever.

As much happiness as the moonlight gave, that much more happiness arose by seeing my Beloved's face.

If I were to receive a great treasure, I would never send my Beloved to a distant land.

My Beloved is like a shawl in winter, shade in summer, an umbrella in the rainy season, and a boat upon the ocean.

Vidyāpati says: "Listen, beautiful woman—for good people, sorrow lasts only for a few days."

IDX.first_line: kirupe paiba seva mui duracarasri guru vaisnave rati na haila amara
IDX.search: kirupe paiba seva mui duracarasri guru vaisnave rati na haila amara asesa mayate mana magana hoilavaisnavete lesamatra rati na janmila
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
kirūpe pāiba sevā mui durācāraśrī⋅guru⋅vaiṣṇave rati nā haila āmāra

aśeṣa māyāte mana magana hoilavaiṣṇavete leśa-mātra rati nā janmila

viṣaye bhuliyā andha hainu divāniśigale phā̐sa dite phire māyā se piśācī

ihāre kariyā jaya, chāḍāna nā jāyasādhu-kṛpā vinā āra nāhika upāya

adoṣadaraśī prabho, patita uddhāra’eibāra narottame karaha nistāra
IDX.en_translation:
How shall I, a wicked soul, obtain service? My affection has not developed for my spiritual master and the devotees.

My mind became engrossed in endless illusion. Not even a trace of affection for the devotees was born.

Forgetting myself in material enjoyments, I became blind day and night. Illusion, like a witch, roams about to put a noose around my neck.

Once caught, there is no escape. Without the mercy of saintly persons, there is no other way.

O Lord, who overlooks the faults of others, please deliver the fallen! This time, please grant liberation to Narottama.

IDX.first_line: kothaya go premamayi radhe radhe
IDX.search: kothaya go premamayi radhe radhe radhe radhe go jaya radhe radhe dekha diye prana rakha radhe radhe tomara kangala tomaya dake radhe radhe
IDX.author: śrīla gaura⋅kiśoradāsa bābājī mahārāja
IDX.verses:
kothāya go premamayi rādhe rādhe
rādhe rādhe go, jaya rādhe rādhe

dekhā diye prāṇa rākha rādhe rādhe
tomāra kāṅgāla tomāya ḍāke rādhe rādhe

rādhe vṛndāvana⋅vilāsinī rādhe rādhe
rādhe kānumanomohinī rādhe rādhe

rādhe aṣṭasakhīra śiromaṇi rādhe rādhe
rādhe vṛṣabhānu⋅nandini rādhe rādhe

(gosāñī) niyama ka’re sadāi ḍāke, rādhe rādhe

(gosāñī) ekabāra ḍāke keśī-ghāṭe,
ābāra ḍāke vaṁśī-vaṭe, rādhe rādhe

(gosāñī) ekabāra ḍāke nidhu-vane,
ābāra ḍāke kuñjavane, rādhe rādhe

(gosāñī) ekabāra ḍāke rādhā-kuṇḍe,
ābāra ḍāke śyāma-kuṇḍe, rādhe rādhe

(gosāñī) ekabāra ḍāke kusuma-vane,
ābāra ḍāke govardhane, rādhe rādhe

(gosāñī) ekabāra ḍāke tāla-vane,
ābāra ḍāke tamāla-vane, rādhe rādhe

(gosāñī) malina vasana diye gāya,
vrajera dhūlāya gaḍāgaḍi jāya, rādhe rādhe

(gosāñī) mukhe rādhā rādhā bale,
bhāse nayanera jale, rādhe rādhe

(gosāñī) vṛndāvane kuli kuli,
ke̐de beḍāya rādhā bali’, rādhe rādhe

(gosāñī) chāppānna daṇḍa rātri dine
jāne nā rādhā⋅govinda vine, rādhe rādhe

tārapara cāri-daṇḍa śuti’ thāke
svapane rādhā⋅govinda dekhe, rādhe rādhe
IDX.en_translation:
Where are You, O Rādhā, the embodiment of love? All glories to You!

By appearing before me, please save my life. Your destitute servant calls You.

Rādhā, who enjoys pastimes in Vṛndāvana! Rādhā, who enchants Kṛṣṇa's mind!

Rādhā, the crest-jewel of the eight principal companions! Rādhā, the daughter of Vṛṣabhānu!

A great soul regularly and always calls out: "Rādhe! Rādhe!"

Sometimes he calls at Keśī-ghāṭa, and again he calls at Vaṁśīvaṭa.

Sometimes he calls in Nidhuvana, and again he calls in the forest groves.

Sometimes he calls at Rādhā-kuṇḍa, and again he calls at Śyāma-kuṇḍa.

Sometimes he calls in Kusumavana, and again he calls at Govardhana.

Sometimes he calls in Tālavana, and again he calls in Tamālavana.

With dirty clothes on his body, he rolls in the dust of Vraja.

With "Rādhā! Rādhā!" on his lips, he floats in the tears from his eyes.

From lane to lane in Vṛndāvana, he cries and wanders, calling Your name.

For fifty-six units of time, day and night, he knows nothing except Rādhā and Govinda.

After that, he sleeps for four units of time, and in his dreams he sees Rādhā and Govinda.

IDX.first_line: kothaya go premamayi radhe radheradhe radhe go jaya radhe radhe
IDX.search: kothaya go premamayi radhe radheradhe radhe go jaya radhe radhe dekha diye prana rakha radhe radhetomara kangala tomaya dake radhe radhe
IDX.author: śrīla gaura⋅kiśoradāsa bābājī mahārāja

(gosāñī) ekabāra ḍāke nidhu-vane,ābāra ḍāke kuñjavane, rādhe rādhe

(gosāñī) ekabāra ḍāke rādhā-kuṇḍe,ābāra ḍāke śyāma-kuṇḍe, rādhe rādhe

(gosāñī) ekabāra ḍāke kusuma-vane,ābāra ḍāke govardhane, rādhe rādhe

(gosāñī) ekabāra ḍāke tāla-vane,ābāra ḍāke tamāla-vane, rādhe rādhe

(gosāñī) mukhe rādhā rādhā bale,bhāse nayanera jale, rādhe rādhe

(gosāñī) vṛndāvane kuli kuli,ke̐de beḍāya rādhā bali’, rādhe rādhe

(gosāñī) chāppānna daṇḍa rātri dinejāne nā rādhā⋅govinda vine, rādhe rādhe

IDX.en_translation:
Where are You, O Rādhā, the embodiment of love? All glories to You! appearing before me, please save my life. Your destitute servant calls You.

Rādhā, who enjoys pastimes in Vṛndāvana! Rādhā, who enchants Kṛṣṇa’s mind!

Rādhā, the crest-jewel of the eight principal companions! Rādhā, the daughter of Vṛṣabhānu!

A great soul regularly and always calls out: “Rādhā! Rādhā!”

Sometimes he calls at Keśī-ghāṭa, and again he calls at Vaṁśīvaṭa.

Sometimes he calls in Nidhuvana, and again he calls in the forest groves.

Sometimes he calls at Rādhā-kuṇḍa, and again he calls at Śyāma-kuṇḍa.

Sometimes he calls in Kusumavana, and again he calls at Govardhana.

Sometimes he calls in Tālavana, and again he calls in Tamālavana.

With dirty clothes on his body, he rolls in the dust of Vraja.

With “Rādhā! Rādhā!” on his lips, he floats in the tears from his eyes.

From lane to lane in Vṛndāvana, he cries and wanders, calling Your name.

For fifty-six units of time, day and night, he knows nothing except Rādhā and Govinda.

IDX.first_line: krpa kara vaisnava thakura sambandha janiya bhajite bhajiteabhimana hou dura
IDX.search: krpa kara vaisnava thakura sambandha janiya bhajite bhajiteabhimana hou dura ami ta vaisnava e buddhi hoileamani na haba amipratisthasa asi hrdaya dusibehoiba nirayagami
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
kṛpā kara vaiṣṇava ṭhākura !sambandha jāniyā, bhajite bhajite,abhimāna hou dūra

‘āmi ta’ vaiṣṇava’, e buddhi hoile,amānī nā ha’ba āmipratiṣṭhāśā āsi’, hṛdaya dūṣibe,hoiba niraya-gāmī

tomāra kiṅkara, āpane jāniba,‘guru’⋅abhimāna tyaji’tomāra ucchiṣṭa, pada⋅jala⋅reṇu,sadā niṣkapaṭe bhaji

‘nije śreṣṭha’ jāni, ucchiṣṭādi⋅dāne,ha’be abhimāna⋅bhāratāi śiṣya tava, thākiyā sarvadā,nā loiba pūjā kāra

amānī mānada, hoile kīrtane,adhikāra dibe tumitomāra caraṇe, niṣkapaṭe āmi,kā̐diyā luṭiba bhūmi
IDX.en_translation:
Please bestow your mercy, O revered devotee! Knowing my relationship with the Lord, as I continuously worship, may all pride be cast far away.

If the thought “I am a devotee” arises, I will not be humble. The desire for prestige will come and pollute my heart, and I will be destined for hell.

I will consider myself your servant, giving up the pride of being a master. I will always sincerely worship your remnants and the dust from your feet.

Knowing myself to be superior while giving remnants to others, the burden of pride would increase. Therefore, remaining always as your disciple, I will not accept anyone's worship.

If I am humble and offer respect to others during the chanting of the Holy Name, you will grant me the qualification. At your feet, I will sincerely cry and roll on the ground.

IDX.first_line: krsna bhakti vina kabhu nahi phalodaya
IDX.search: krsna bhakti vina kabhu nahi phalodaya miche saba dharmadharma jivera upadhimaya joga yaga tapodhyana sannyasadi brahmajnana nana kandarupe jivera bandhana karana haya
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
kṛṣṇa⋅bhakti vinā kabhu nāhi phalodaya
miche saba dharmādharma jīvera upādhimaya

joga⋅yāga⋅tapodhyāna, sannyāsādi brahmajñāna
nānā kāṇḍa-rūpe jīvera bandhana⋅kāraṇa haya

vinodera vākya dhara, nānā kāṇḍa tyāga kara
nirupādhi kṛṣṇa-prema hṛdaye deha āśraya
IDX.en_translation:
Without devotion to Kṛṣṇa, there is never a favorable outcome. All religious and irreligious acts are useless when full of the material designations of the living entity.

Yoga, sacrifice, austerities, meditation, renunciation, and knowledge of the Absolute in the form of various activities only become the cause of the soul's bondage.

Accept the words of Bhaktivinoda and give up these various activities. Give shelter to pure love for Kṛṣṇa in your heart.

IDX.first_line: krsna caitanya advaita prabhu nityananda
IDX.search: krsna caitanya advaita prabhu nityananda gadadhara srinivasa murari mukunda svarupa rupa sanatana puri ramananda
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
kṛṣṇa⋅caitanya advaita prabhu nityānanda
gadādhara śrīnivāsa murāri mukunda
svarūpa⋅rūpa⋅sanātana⋅purī⋅rāmānanda
IDX.en_translation: Kṛṣṇa-Caitanya, Advaita Prabhu, Nityānanda, Gadādhara, Śrīnivāsa, Murāri, Mukunda, Svarūpa, Rūpa, Sanātana, Purī, Rāmānanda.

IDX.first_line: krsna govinda hare gopi vallabha saure
IDX.search: krsna govinda hare gopi vallabha saure srinivasa damodara sri rama murare
IDX.verses:
kṛṣṇa govinda hare, gopī⋅vallabha śaure

śrīnivāsa dāmodara śrī⋅rāma murāre

nanda⋅nandana mādhava nṛsiŋha kaṁsāre
IDX.en_translation:
O Kṛṣṇa, O Govinda, O Hare! O Beloved of the gopīs, O Scion of the Śūra dynasty!

O Abode of the Goddess of Fortune, O Dāmodara! O Lord Rāma, O Enemy of the Mura demon!

O Son of Nanda, O Mādhava! O Nṛsiṁha, O Enemy of Kaṁsa!

IDX.first_line: krsna haite caturmukha haya krsna sevonmukha
IDX.search: krsna haite caturmukha haya krsna sevonmukha brahma haite naradera mati narada hoite vyasa madhva kahe vyasadasa purnaprajna padmanabha gati nrhari madhava vamse aksobhya paramahamse sisya bali angikara kare aksobhyera sisya jaya tirtha name paricaya tara dasye jnanasindhu tare
IDX.author: śrīla bhaktisiddhānta sarasvatī ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
kṛṣṇa haite caturmukha, haya kṛṣṇa⋅sevonmukha,
brahmā haite nāradera mati
nārada hoite vyāsa, madhva kahe vyāsadāsa,
pūrṇaprajña padmanābha⋅gati

nṛhari⋅mādhava⋅vaṁśe, akṣobhya⋅paramahaṁse,
śiṣya bali’ aṅgīkāra kare
akṣobhyera śiṣya ‘jaya⋅ tīrtha’ nāme paricaya,
tā̐’ra dāsye jñānasindhu tare

tā̐hā haite dayānidhi, tā̐’ra dāsa vidyānidhi,
rājendra hoila tā̐hā ha’te
tā̐hāra kiṅkara ‘jaya⋅ dharma’ nāme paricaya,
paramparā jāna bhālamate

jayadharma⋅dāsye khyāti, śrī⋅puruṣottama⋅yati,
tā̐’ ha’te brahmaṇyatīrtha⋅sūri
vyāsatīrtha tā̐’ra dāsa, lakṣmīpati vyāsadāsa,
tā̐hā ha’te mādhavendrapurī

mādhavendrapurīvara⋅ śiṣyavara śrī⋅īśvara,
nityānanda, śrī⋅advaita vibhu
īśvarapurīke dhanya, karilena śrī⋅caitanya,
jagadguru gaura⋅mahāprabhu

mahāprabhu śrī⋅caitanya, rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa nahe anya,
rūpānuga⋅janera jīvana
viśvambhara⋅priyaṅkara, śrī⋅svarūpadāmodara,
śrī⋅gosvāmī rūpa⋅sanātana

rūpapriya mahājana, jīva, raghunātha hana,
tā̐’ra priya kavi kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa
kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa priyavara, narottama sevāpara,
jā̐’ra pada viśvanātha⋅āśa

viśvanātha bhaktasātha, baladeva, jagannātha,
tā̐’ra priya śrī⋅bhaktivinoda
mahābhāgavatavara, śrī⋅gaura⋅kiśora⋅vara,
hari⋅bhajanete jā̐’ra moda

‘śrī⋅vārṣabhānavī’⋅varā, sadā sevya⋅sevāparā,
tā̐hāra ‘dayitadāsa’ nāma
prabhupāda⋅antaraṅga, śrī⋅svarūpa⋅rūpānuga,
śrī⋅keśava bhakati⋅prajñāna

gauḍīya⋅vedāntavettā, māyāvāda⋅tamohantā,
gauravāṇī⋅pracārācāra⋅dhāma
pracārila gaurāvāṇī, bhakativedānta svāmī,
pūrāila prabhupādera kāma

keśavapriya⋅mahājana, vāmana nārāyaṇa hana
gaurāvāṇī tā̐dera prāṇadhana
ei saba harijana, gaurāṅgera nijajana,
tā̐’dera ucchiṣṭe mora kāma
IDX.en_translation:
From Kṛṣṇa came the four-faced Brahmā, who is inclined to the service of Kṛṣṇa. From Brahmā came the intelligence of Nārada. From Nārada came Vyāsa; Madhva calls himself the servant of Vyāsa. Pūrṇaprajña (Madhva) is the destination of Padmanābha.

In the lineage of Nṛhari and Mādhava, Akṣobhya accepted Paramahaṁsa as his disciple. Akṣobhya's disciple is known by the name 'Jaya Tīrtha'. His servant was Jñānasindhu.

From him came Dayānidhi, and his servant is Vidyānidhi. Rājendra came from him. His servant is known by the name 'Jaya Dharma'. Know this disciplic succession well.

Renowned in the service of Jayadharma was Śrī Puruṣottama Yati. From him came the learned Brahmaṇya Tīrtha. Vyāsa Tīrtha was his servant. Lakṣmīpati was the servant of Vyāsa. From him came Śrīla Mādhavendra Purī.

The best disciple of Śrīla Mādhavendra Purī was Śrī Īśvara Purī, and also Śrī Nityānanda Prabhu and the powerful Śrī Advaita Ācārya. Śrī Caitanya Mahāprabhu, the spiritual master of the universe, the great Golden Lord, made Śrī Īśvara Purī glorious.

Mahāprabhu Śrī Caitanya is none other than Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa, the life of the followers of Śrīla Rūpa Gosvāmī. The dearmost of Viśvambhara was Śrīla Svarūpa Dāmodara Gosvāmī, and the Gosvāmīs Śrīla Rūpa and Śrīla Sanātana.

Dear to Rūpa were the great souls Śrīla Jīva Gosvāmī and Śrīla Raghunātha Dāsa Gosvāmī. Their dear poet was Śrīla Kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa Kavirāja Gosvāmī. Kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa's dearmost was Śrīla Narottama Dāsa Ṭhākura, dedicated to service, whose feet were the hope of Śrīla Viśvanātha Cakravartī Ṭhākura.

Śrīla Viśvanātha Cakravartī Ṭhākura, along with the devotees Śrīla Baladeva Vidyābhūṣaṇa and Śrīla Jagannātha Dāsa Bābājī Mahārāja; their dear Śrīla Saccidānanda Bhaktivinoda Ṭhākura. The great mahā-bhāgavata, the excellent Śrīla Gaura-kiśora Dāsa Bābājī Mahārāja, whose delight was in the worship of Hari.

The excellent 'Śrī Vārṣabhānavī', always intent on the service of the Object of service—his name is 'Dayita-dāsa' (Śrīla Bhaktisiddhānta Sarasvatī Ṭhākura Prabhupāda). The intimate associate of Prabhupāda, the follower of Svarūpa and Rūpa, is Śrīla Bhaktiprajñāna Keśava Gosvāmī Mahārāja.

Knower of Gauḍīya Vedānta, destroyer of the darkness of Māyāvāda, the abode of the practice and preaching of Gaura's message. Śrīla Bhaktivedānta Svāmī Prabhupāda preached the message of Gaura, fulfilling the desire of Prabhupāda.

The great souls dear to Keśava are Śrīla Bhaktivedānta Vāmana Gosvāmī Mahārāja and Śrīla Bhaktivedānta Nārāyaṇa Gosvāmī Mahārāja; the message of Gaura is the treasure of their lives. All these devotees of Hari are Gaurāṅga's own; my desire is for their remnants.

IDX.first_line: krsna krsnabhakta gata guna nama sucarita
IDX.search: krsna krsnabhakta gata guna nama sucarita mandana sambandhi tatastha adi bhava jata aganana ai rasera uddipana hetu bali bale rasavedi manasa vacika punah kayikete tina guna nama krsna sri radha madhava nrtya vamsigana gati godohana go ahuti agoddhara gosthete tandava
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
kṛṣṇa⋅kṛṣṇabhakta⋅gata, ‘guṇa’ ‘nāma’ ‘sucarita’,
‘maṇḍana’ ‘sambandhi’ ‘taṭastha’ ādi
bhāva jata agaṇana, ai rasera uddīpana,
‘hetu’ bali’ bale rasavedī

mānasa vācika punaḥ, kāyikete tina ‘guṇa’,
‘nāma’⋅kṛṣṇa, śrī⋅rādhā⋅mādhava
nṛtya vaṁśī-gāna gati, go-dohana go⋅āhuti,
agoddhāra goṣṭhete tāṇḍava

mālyānulepana āra, vāsa bhūṣā ei cāra⋅,
prakāra ‘maṇḍana’ śobhākara
vaṁśī⋅śṛṅga⋅vīṇā⋅raba, gīta śilpa susaurabha,
padāṅka bhūṣaṇa⋅vādyasvara

śikhi-puccha gābhī jaṣṭi, veṇu śṛṅga preṣṭha-dṛṣṭi,
adridhātu nirmālya godhūli
vṛndāvana tadāśrita, govardhana ravi-sutā,
rāsa⋅ādi jata līlāsthalī

khaga bhṛṅga mṛga kuñja, tulasikā latā-puñja,
karṇikāra kadambādi taru
śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅‘sambandhi’ saba, vṛndāraṇya su-vaibhava,
uddīpana kare rasa cāru

jyotsnā ghana saudāminī, śarat pūrṇaniśāmaṇi,
gandhavaha āra khagacaya
‘taṭastha’ ākhya uddīpana, rasāsvāda⋅vibhāvana,
kare saba hoiyā sadaya
IDX.en_translation:
The qualities, names, and pastimes associated with Kṛṣṇa and His devotees; all the innumerable emotions like adornments, relationships, and neutral factors—these are what the experts call the stimulants that awaken the divine mellows.

The three qualities are mental, verbal, and bodily. The names include Kṛṣṇa and Śrī Rādhā-Mādhava. His dancing, flute playing, and movements; His milking of cows, cow worship, and His vigorous dance in the cowherd village for cow protection.

Garlands, anointments, garments, and ornaments—these four types are the adornments that enhance beauty. The sounds of the flute, horn, and vīṇā, sweet songs, art, and pleasant fragrances, His footprints, ornaments, and musical sounds.

The peacock feather, cows, shepherd's staff, flute, horn, and loving glances; minerals from the mountains, remnants, and cow-dust at twilight. Vṛndāvana and all its dependent places, Govardhana, the Yamunā River, and all the places of divine pastimes such as the Rāsa dance.

Birds, bees, deer, groves, Tulasi plants, clusters of creepers, Karnikara and Kadamba trees, and other trees. All these are related to Śrī Kṛṣṇa, the great opulence of the forest of Vṛndāvana, and they stimulate the charming mellow.

Moonlight, dense clouds, lightning, the autumn season, the full moon, the fragrant breeze, and flocks of birds. These are called neutral stimulants, all mercifully creating the experience of the divine mellow.

IDX.first_line: krsna prasadena samasta saila
IDX.search: krsna prasadena samasta saila samrajyam apnoti ca vairino pi sakrasya jah prapa balim sa saksad govardhano me disatam abhistam sva prestha hastambuja saukumarya sukhanubhuter ati bhumi vrtteh mahendra vajrahatim apy ajanan govardhano me disatam abhistam
IDX.author: śrīla viśvanātha cakrabartī ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
kṛṣṇa⋅prasādena samasta⋅śaila⋅
sāmrājyam āpnoti ca vairiṇo ‘pi
śakrasya jaḥ prāpa baliṁ sa sākṣād
govardhano me diśatām abhīṣṭam

sva⋅preṣṭha⋅hastāmbuja⋅saukumārya⋅
sukhānubhūter ati⋅bhūmi vṛtteḥ
mahendra⋅vajrāhatim apy ajānan
govardhano me diṣatām abhīṣṭam

jatraiva kṛṣṇo vṛṣabhānu⋅putryā
dānaṁ gṛhītuṁ kalahaṁ vitene
śruteḥ spṛhā jatra mahaty ataḥ śrī⋅
govardhano me diṣatām abhiṣṭam

snātvā saraḥ svaśu samīra⋅hastī
jatraiva nīpādi⋅parāga⋅dhūliḥ
ālolayan khelati cāru sa śrī⋅
govardhano me diṣatām abhīṣṭam

kastūrikābhiḥ śayitaṁ kim atrety
ūhaṁ prabhoḥ svasya muhur vitanvan
naisargika⋅svīya⋅śilā⋅sugandhair
govardhano me diṣatām abhīṣṭam

vaṁśa⋅pratidhvany⋅anusāra⋅vartma
didṛkṣavo jatra hariṁ hariṇyaḥ
jāntyo labhante na hi vismitāḥ sa
govardhano me diṣatām abhīṣṭam

jatraiva gaṅgām anu nāvi rādhām
ārohya madhye tu nimagna⋅naukaḥ
kṛṣṇo hi rādhānugalo babhau sa
govardhano me diṣatām abhīṣṭam

vinā bhavet kiṁ hari⋅dāsa⋅varya⋅
padāśrayaṁ bhaktir ataḥ śrayāmi
jam eva saprema nijeśayoḥ śrī⋅
govardhano me diṣatām abhīṣṭam

etat paṭhed jo hari⋅dāsa⋅varya⋅
mahānubhāvāṣṭakam ārdra⋅cetāḥ
śrī⋅rādhikā⋅mādhavayoḥ padābja⋅
dāsyaṁ sa vinded acireṇa sākṣāta
IDX.en_translation:
By Lord Kṛṣṇa's mercy, Govardhana Hill attained sovereignty over all mountains and received tribute even from His enemy Indra. May He grant the desire of my heart.

Govardhana Hill, who, due to the abundant joy experienced from the exquisite touch of Lord Kṛṣṇa's lotus-like hands, did not even feel the strike of Indra's thunderbolt—may He grant the desire of my heart.

May Śrī Govardhana, where Lord Kṛṣṇa engaged in a playful dispute with the daughter of Vṛṣabhānu to collect a toll, and where the desire to hear of these pastimes is great—may He grant the desire of my heart.

May that Śrī Govardhana, where the elephant-like wind, having bathed in the nearby lakes, beautifully plays by stirring the pollen of the Kadamba and other trees—may He grant the desire of my heart.

Govardhana Hill, which, by the natural fragrance of its own stones, repeatedly causes one to wonder: "Did my Lord sleep here with musk?"—may He grant the desire of my heart.

May that Govardhana Hill, where the deer, desiring to see Lord Hari, follow the path guided by the echo of His flute, grant the desire of my heart.

May that Govardhana Hill, where Lord Kṛṣṇa, having seated Rādhā in a boat along the Mānasa-gaṅgā, and the boat having playfully submerged in the middle, shone beautifully as He embraced Her—may He grant the desire of my heart.

Without the shelter of the feet of the best of Kṛṣṇa's servants, Govardhana Hill, what would devotion be? Therefore, I take shelter of Śrī Govardhana, who lovingly serves His own Lords, Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa. May He grant the desire of my heart.

Whoever reads these eight verses describing the great glories of the best of Kṛṣṇa's servants with a softened heart, he directly attains, without delay, servitude at the lotus feet of Śrīmati Rādhikā and Lord Mādhava.

IDX.first_line: krsna tava punya habe bhai
IDX.search: krsna tava punya habe bhai e punya karibe jabe radha rani khusi habe dhruva ati bali toma tai sri siddhanta sarasvati saci suta priya ati krsna sevaya jara tulya nai sei se mohanta guru jagatera madhye uru krsna bhakti deya thai thai
IDX.author: śrīmadbhaktibedānta svāmī prabhupāda
IDX.verses:
kṛṣṇa tava puṇya habe bhāi!
e puṇya karibe jabe, rādhā⋅rāṇī khuśi habe,
dhruva ati bali tomā tāi

śrī siddhānta sarasvatī, śacī⋅suta priya ati,
kṛṣṇa⋅sevāya jā̐ra tulya nāi
sei se mohānta⋅guru, jagatera madhye ūru,
kṛṣṇa⋅bhakti deya ṭhā̐i ṭhā̐i

tā̐ra icchā balavāna, pāścātyete thāna thāna,
haya jāte gaurāṅgera nāma
pṛthivīte nagarādi, āsamudra nada⋅nadī,
sakalei laya kṛṣṇa⋅nāma

tāhale ānanda haya, tabe haya digvijaya,
caitanyera kṛpā atiśaya
māyāduṣṭa jata duḥkhī, jagate sabāi sukhī,
vaiṣṇavera icchā pūrṇa haya

se kārya je karibāre, ājñā jadi dile more,
jogya nahi ati dīna hīna
tāi se tomāra kṛpā, māgitechi anurūpā,
āji tumi sabāra pravīṇa

tomāra se śakti pele, guru⋅sevā vastu mile,
jīvana sārthaka jadi haya
sei se sevā pele, tāhale sukhī hale,
tava saṅga bhāgyate milaya

evaṁ janaṁ nipatitaṁ prabhavāhikūpe
kāmābhikāmam anu jaḥ prapatana prasaṅgāta
kṛtvātmasāt surarṣiṇā bhagavan gṛhītaḥ
so‛haṁ kathaṁ nu visṛje tava bhṛtya⋅sevām

tumi mora cira⋅sāthī, bhuliyā māyāra lāthi,
khāiyāchi janma⋅janmāntare
āji punaḥ e suyoga, jadi haya jogāyoga,
tabe pāri tuhe milibāre

tomāra milane bhāi, ābāra se sukha pāi,
gocāraṇe ghuri dina bhora
kata vane chuṭāchuṭi, vane khāi luṭāpuṭi,
sei dina kabe habe mora

(āji se subidhā⋅ne tomāra smaraṇa bhela
baḍa āśā ḍākilāma tāi
āmi tomāra nitya⋅dāsa tāi kari eta āśa
tumi binā anya gati nāi)
IDX.en_translation:
Kṛṣṇa! You will accrue merit, brother! When you perform this pious act, Rādhā⋅rāṇī will be pleased; I tell you this as a certainty.

Śrī Siddhānta Sarasvatī is very dear to the Son of Śacī; there is no one equal to him in the service of Kṛṣṇa. That great Guru, the best in the world, distributes Kṛṣṇa-bhakti in place after place.

His desire is powerful—that the name of Gaurāṅga should spread in the West. In all the cities of the earth, from the ocean to the rivers, may everyone take the name of Kṛṣṇa.

Then there will be joy, then there will be victory in all directions, by the great mercy of Caitanya. All the miserable people polluted by Māyā in the world will be happy, and the desire of the Vaiṣṇava will be fulfilled.

To perform that task, if you give me the order—although I am unworthy, very low and fallen—I beg for your suitable mercy; today you are the authority for everyone.

If I get your strength, the object of Guru-sevā is obtained, and my life becomes successful. If I get that service, then I will be happy; by good fortune, your association is obtained.

"O Lord, I am a person fallen into the blind well of material existence, constantly engaged in lust. I was accepted by the celestial sage (Nārada) and made his own. How can I give up the service of Your servant?"

You are my eternal companion; forgetting You, I have suffered the kicks of Māyā birth after birth. Today, again, if this opportunity arises—if there is a connection—then I can meet You.

In meeting You, brother, I again find that happiness, wandering all day grazing the cows. Running in so many forests, rolling on the ground in the forest—when will that day be mine?

(Today, in that opportunity, I remembered You. With great hope, I called out. I am Your eternal servant, so I have so much hope. Without You, I have no other shelter.)

His desire is powerful—that the name of Gaurāṅga should spread in the West. In all the cities of the earth, from the ocean to the rivers, may everyone take the name of Kṛṣṇa.
