(Today, in that opportunity, I remembered You. With great hope, I called out. I am Your eternal servant, so I have so much hope. Without You, I have no other shelter.)

IDX.first_line: krsnavamsigita suni dekhi citrapatakhani
IDX.search: krsnavamsigita suni dekhi citrapatakhani lokamukhe guna sravaniya purvaragakranta cita unmada laksananvita sakhisange calila dhaiya nikunjakanane karila abhisara na manila nivarana grhakarya aganana dharmadharma na karila vicara
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
kṛṣṇavaṁśīgīta śuni’, dekhi’ citrapaṭakhāni,
lokamukhe guṇa śravaṇiyā
pūrvarāgākrānta cita, unmāda⋅lakṣaṇānvita,
sakhīsaṅge calilā dhāiyā

nikuñjakānane karila abhisāra
nā mānila nivāraṇa, gṛhakārya agaṇana,
dharmādharma nā karila vicāra

jamunāpuline giyā, sakhīgaṇe sambodhiyā,
jijñāsila priyera uddeśa
chāḍila prāṇera bhaya, vanete praveśa haya,
vaṁśīdhvani kariyā nirdeśa

nadī jathā sindhu⋅prati, dhāya ati vegavatī,
seirūpa rasavatī satī
ati vege kuñjavane, giyā kṛṣṇa⋅sannidhāne,
ātmanivedane kaila mati

kena mora durbalā lekhanī nāhi sare
abhisāra āramtiyā sakampa antare

milana, sambhoga, vipralambhādi⋅varṇana
prakāśa karite nāhi sare mama mana

durbhāgā nā bujhe rāsalīlā⋅tattvasāra
śūkara jemana nāhi cine muktāhāra

adhikārahīna⋅jana⋅maṅgala cintiyā
kīrtana karinu śeṣa, kāla vicāriyā
IDX.en_translation:
Hearing Kṛṣṇa's flute song, seeing His beautiful form in a painting, and hearing of His qualities, her heart was afflicted by initial attraction; showing symptoms of madness, she ran swiftly with her companions.

She made her tryst toward the forest bower. She heeded no prevention, nor her countless household duties, nor did she consider right or wrong.

Going to the bank of the Yamunā and addressing her companions, she inquired about the whereabouts of her beloved. Abandoning all fear for her life, she entered the forest, guided by the sound of the flute.

Just as a river swiftly rushes toward the ocean, that chaste woman, full of mellows, went with great speed to the forest grove near Kṛṣṇa; with a mood of self-surrender, she gave herself to Him.

Why does my feeble pen not move, as I begin to describe the tryst with a trembling heart?

My mind does not move to reveal the meeting, the enjoyment, the separation, and other such topics.

An unfortunate person cannot understand the essence of the divine rāsa dance, just as a hog cannot recognize a pearl necklace.

Thinking of the welfare of those who are not yet qualified, I conclude this narration here, considering the time.

IDX.first_line: krsnavarta vina ana asadvarta bali jana
IDX.search: krsnavarta vina ana asadvarta bali jana sei vesya ati bhayankari sri krsnavisaya mati jivera durlabha ati sei vesya mati laya hari suna mana bali he tomaya mukti name sardulini tara katha jadi suni sarvatmasampatti gili khaya
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
kṛṣṇavārtā vinā āna, ‘asadvārtā’ bali’ jāna,
sei veśyā ati bhayaṅkarī
śrī⋅kṛṣṇaviṣayā mati, jīvera durlabha ati,
sei veśyā mati laya hari’

śuna, mana, bali he tomāya
‘mukti’⋅nāme śārdūlinī, tā’ra kathā jadi śuni,
sarvātmasampatti gili’ khāya

tadubhaya tyāga kara, muktikathā parihara,
lakṣmīpati⋅rati rākha dūre
se⋅rati prabala ha’le, paravyome deya phele,
nāhi deya vāsa vrajapure

vraje rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa⋅rati, amūlya⋅dhanada ati,
tā’i tumi bhaja ciradina
rūpa⋅raghunātha⋅pāya, sei rati prārthanāya,
e bhaktivinoda dīna hīna
IDX.en_translation:
Without topics concerning Lord Kṛṣṇa, know all other talk to be "unreal talk." Such talk is like a very dreadful prostitute. Devotion concerning Lord Kṛṣṇa is extremely rare for the soul, and that prostitute steals away that devotion.

Listen, O my mind, I tell you: if one listens to the tigress named "liberation," she swallows all his spiritual wealth.

Abandon both sense gratification and liberation, and give up talk of impersonal liberation. Keep devotion for Lord Nārāyaṇa far away. If that devotion becomes strong, it throws one into the spiritual sky of Vaikuṇṭha, but does not grant residence in Vraja.

Devotion for Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa in Vraja is an extremely priceless bestower of wealth. Therefore, you should always worship that. At the feet of Śrīla Rūpa Gosvāmī and Śrīla Raghunātha Dāsa Gosvāmī, praying for that devotion, this Bhaktivinoda is poor and humble.

IDX.first_line: krsnotkirtana gana nartana parau premamrtambhonidhi
IDX.search: krsnotkirtana gana nartana parau premamrtambhonidhi dhiradhira jana priyau priya karau nirmatsarau pujitau sri caitanya krpa bharau bhuvi bhuvobharavahantarakau vande rupa sanatanau raghu yugau sri jiva gopalakau nanasastra vicaranaika nipunau saddharma samsthapakau lokanam hita karinau tribhuvane manyau saranyakarau radha krsna padaravinda bhajananandena mattalikau vande rupa sanatanau raghu yugau sri jiva gopalakau
IDX.author: śrīla śrīnivāsācārya
IDX.verses:
kṛṣṇotkīrtana⋅gāna⋅nartana⋅parau premāmṛtāmbhonidhī
dhīrādhīra⋅jana⋅priyau priya⋅karau nirmatsarau pūjitau
śrī⋅caitanya⋅kṛpā⋅bharau bhuvi bhuvobhārāvahantārakau
vande rūpa⋅sanātanau raghu⋅yugau śrī⋅jīva⋅gopālakau

nānāśāstra⋅vicāraṇaika⋅nipuṇau saddharma⋅saṁsthāpakau
lokānāṁ hita⋅kāriṇau tribhuvane mānyau śaraṇyākarau
rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa⋅padāravinda⋅bhajanānandena mattālikau
vande rūpa⋅sanātanau raghu⋅yugau śrī⋅jīva⋅gopālakau

śrī⋅gaurāṅga⋅guṇānuvarṇana⋅vidhau śraddhā⋅samṛddhyanvitau
pāpottāpa⋅nikṛntanau tanubhṛtāṁ govinda⋅gānāmṛtaiḥ
ānandāmbudhi⋅vardhanaika⋅nipuṇau kaivalya⋅nistārakau
vande rūpa⋅sanātanau raghu⋅yugau śrī⋅jīva⋅gopālakau

tyaktvā tūrṇamaśeṣa⋅maṇḍala⋅pati⋅śreṇīṁ sadā tucchavat
bhūtvā dīna⋅gaṇeśakau karuṇayā kaupīna⋅kanthāśritau
gopībhāva⋅rasāmṛtābdhi⋅laharī⋅kallola⋅magnau muhur⋅
vande rūpa⋅sanātanau raghu⋅yugau śrī⋅jīva⋅gopālakau

kūjat⋅kokila⋅haṁsa⋅sārasa⋅gaṇākīrṇe mayūrākule
nānāratna⋅nibaddha⋅mūla⋅viṭapa⋅śrīyukta⋅vṛndāvane
rādhā⋅kṛṣṇamaharniśaṁ prabhajatau jīvārthadau jau mudā
vande rūpa⋅sanātanau raghu⋅yugau śrī⋅jīva⋅gopālakau

saŋkhyāpūrvaka⋅nāma⋅gāna⋅natibhiḥ kālāvasānīkṛtau
nidrāhāra⋅vihārakādi⋅vijitau cātyantadīnau ca jau
rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa⋅guṇa⋅smṛtermadhurimānandena sammohitau
vande rūpa⋅sanātanau raghu⋅yugau śrī⋅jīva⋅gopālakau

rādhā⋅kuṇḍa⋅taṭe kalinda⋅tanayā⋅tīre ca vaṁśīvaṭe
premonmāda⋅vaśādaśeṣa⋅daśayā grastau pramattau sadā
gāyantau ca kadā harer⋅guṇa⋅varaṁ bhāvābhibhūtau mudā
vande rūpa⋅sanātanau raghu⋅yugau śrī⋅jīva⋅gopālakau

he rādhe! vraja⋅devike! ca lalite! he nandasūno! kutaḥ
śrī⋅govardhana⋅kalpa⋅pādapa⋅tale kālindī⋅vanye kutaḥ
ghoṣantāviti sarvato vraja⋅pure khedair⋅mahā⋅vihvalau
vande rūpa⋅sanātanau raghu⋅yugau śrī⋅jīva⋅gopālakau
IDX.en_translation:
Engaged in the congregational chanting of Kṛṣṇa's names, singing and dancing—they are oceans of the nectar of divine love. Dear to both the sober and the restless, they are doers of good, free from envy, and revered. Full of the mercy of Lord Caitanya and relieving the earth of its burden—I offer my obeisances to Rūpa, Sanātana, Raghunātha dāsa, Raghunātha Bhaṭṭa, Jīva, and Gopāla Bhaṭṭa.

Expert in deliberating on various scriptures and establishers of true religious principles, they are benefactors of humanity, respected in the three worlds, and abodes of refuge. Like intoxicated bees relishing the bliss of worshiping the lotus feet of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa—I offer my obeisances to Rūpa, Sanātana, Raghunātha dāsa, Raghunātha Bhaṭṭa, Jīva, and Gopāla Bhaṭṭa.

Endowed with abundant faith in the process of describing Lord Caitanya's qualities, they are the cutters of the miseries and sins of embodied beings through the nectar of the Lord's glories. Expert in increasing the ocean of bliss and delivering others from impersonal liberation—I offer my obeisances to Rūpa, Sanātana, Raghunātha dāsa, Raghunātha Bhaṭṭa, Jīva, and Gopāla Bhaṭṭa.

Having quickly given up all ranks of powerful leaders and always considering them insignificant, out of compassion they became the masters of the humble and poor. Taking shelter of simple loincloths and patched coverings, they are constantly immersed in the waves and surges of the nectar-ocean of the mood of the gopīs—I offer my obeisances to Rūpa, Sanātana, Raghunātha dāsa, Raghunātha Bhaṭṭa, Jīva, and Gopāla Bhaṭṭa.

In Vṛndāvana, which is filled with cooing cuckoos, swans, and cranes, crowded with peacocks, and adorned with trees whose roots are bound with jewels—they worship Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa day and night and joyfully bestow the ultimate benefit upon all souls. I offer my obeisances to Rūpa, Sanātana, Raghunātha dāsa, Raghunātha Bhaṭṭa, Jīva, and Gopāla Bhaṭṭa.

They utilized their time by chanting the Holy Names a fixed number of times and by offering obeisances. They conquered sleep, eating, and recreation, and were extremely humble. Enchanted by the sweet bliss of remembering the qualities of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa—I offer my obeisances to Rūpa, Sanātana, Raghunātha dāsa, Raghunātha Bhaṭṭa, Jīva, and Gopāla Bhaṭṭa.

On the bank of Rādhā-kuṇḍa, on the bank of the Yamunā, and at the Vaṁśīvaṭa tree, they were always overwhelmed by the madness of divine love, affected by various stages of ecstasy and always intoxicated. Sometimes they were joyfully overwhelmed by transcendental emotions while singing the excellent qualities of the Lord. I offer my obeisances to Rūpa, Sanātana, Raghunātha dāsa, Raghunātha Bhaṭṭa, Jīva, and Gopāla Bhaṭṭa.

"O Rādhā! O Goddess of Vraja! O Lalitā! O Son of Nanda! Where are You? Are You under the wish-fulfilling trees of Govardhana, or in the forest along the Yamunā?" Shouting in this way everywhere in Vraja and greatly agitated by the distress of separation—I offer my obeisances to Rūpa, Sanātana, Raghunātha dāsa, Raghunātha Bhaṭṭa, Jīva, and Gopāla Bhaṭṭa.

IDX.first_line: kunja kunda sarovara giri govardhana
IDX.search: kunja kunda sarovara giri govardhana vrksalata sri yamuna saba vrndavana cidananda maniratna vinirmita saba kichumatra nahi tatha mayara vaibhava
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
kuñja kuṇḍa sarovara giri⋅govardhana
vṛkṣalatā śrī⋅yamunā saba vṛndāvana

cidānanda maṇiratna⋅vinirmita saba
kichumātra nāhi tathā māyāra vaibhava

jei jīva siddha haya sakhīkṛpā bale
premamṛṣṭa cakṣe saba dekhe kutūhale

māṁsamayī dṛṣṭiyoge vahirmutha jana
māyikāvaraṇa mātra kare daraśana

māyāra je deśa kāla nāhika tathāya
cidāvṛtti deśakāla tathā śobhā pāya

parama dayālu kṛṣṇa jīve dayā kari
prakaṭa karila saba svayaṁ avatari’

māyābaddha jīva tathā jāya anukṣaṇa
kṛṣṇakṛpā vinā nāhi pāya daraśana

je jīvera karmakṣaye sādhusaṅga haya
seimātra vrajavana darśana karaya

bahirmukha jīva tathā rahite nā pāre
śīghra jaḍamāyā ākarṣiyā laya tāre

vraja⋅dhāme jei jīva jāya ekabāra
krame valavatī haya sukṛti tāhāra

sukṛti prabala hoile sādhusaṅga haya
sādhusaṅge vrajalābha sarvaśāstre kaya
IDX.en_translation:
The groves, the ponds, the lakes, Govardhana Hill, the trees and creepers, the River Yamunā—all of Vṛndāvana.

Everything there is made of spiritual jewels and gems; there is not even a trace of material illusion there.

The soul who becomes perfect by the grace of the gopīs sees everything with eyes cleansed by pure love and with great eagerness.

An externally oriented person, with material vision, sees only a covering of illusion.

There is no material place or time there; rather, the spiritual dimension of place and time is beautifully manifest.

The supremely merciful Lord Kṛṣṇa, showing mercy to the living entities, manifested everything Himself by His descent.

The soul bound by illusion goes there constantly, but without the Lord's mercy, he cannot see anything.

The soul whose past actions are exhausted obtains the association of the saintly. Only such a person can truly see the forest of Vraja.

An externally oriented soul cannot remain there; the material illusion quickly pulls them away.

That soul who goes to the holy land of Vraja even once gradually accumulates strong spiritual merit.

When such merit becomes strong, the association of the saintly occurs. All the scriptures declare that the attainment of Vraja comes through the association of the saintly.

IDX.first_line: kunkumakta kancanabja garva hari gaurabha
IDX.search: kunkumakta kancanabja garva hari gaurabha pitanancitabja gandha kirti nindi saurabha vallavesa sunu sarva vanchitartha sadhika mahyamastu pada padma dasyadastu radhika kauravinda kanti nindi citra patta satika krsna matta bhrnga keli phulla puspa vatika krsna nitya sangamartha padma bandhu radhika mahyamastu pada padma dasyadastu radhika
IDX.author: kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa kavirāja gosvāmī
IDX.verses:
kuṅkumākta⋅kāñcanābja⋅garva⋅hāri⋅gaurabhā
pītanāñcitābja⋅gandha⋅kīrti⋅nindi⋅saurabhā
vallaveśa⋅sūnu⋅sarva⋅vāñchitārtha⋅sādhikā
mahyamastu pāda⋅padma⋅dāsyadāstu rādhikā

kauravinda⋅kānti⋅nindi⋅citra⋅paṭṭa⋅śāṭikā
kṛṣṇa⋅matta⋅bhṛṅga⋅keli⋅phulla⋅puṣpa⋅vāṭikā
kṛṣṇa⋅nitya⋅saṅgamārtha⋅padma⋅bandhu⋅rādhikā
mahyamastu pāda⋅padma⋅dāsyadāstu rādhikā

saukumārya⋅sṛṣṭa⋅pallavāli⋅kīrti⋅nigrahā
candra⋅candanotpalendu⋅sevya⋅śīta⋅vigrahā
svābhimarṣa⋅vallavīśa⋅kāma⋅tāpādhikā
mahyamastu pāda⋅padma⋅dāsyadāstu rādhikā

viśvavandya⋅yauvatābhivanditāpi jā ramā
rūpa⋅navya⋅yauvanādi⋅sampadā na jatsamā
śīla⋅hārda⋅līlayā ca sā jato‛sti nādhikā
mahyamastu pāda⋅padma⋅dāsyadāstu rādhikā

rāsa⋅lāsya⋅gīta⋅narma⋅satkalāli⋅paṇḍitā
prema⋅ramya⋅rūpa⋅veśa⋅sadguṇāli⋅maṇḍitā
viśva⋅navya⋅gopa⋅yoṣidālito‛pi jādhikā
mahyamastu pāda⋅padma⋅dāsyadāstu rādhikā

nitya⋅nava⋅rūpa⋅keli⋅kṛṣṇa⋅bhāva⋅sampadā
kṛṣṇa⋅rāga⋅bandha⋅gopa⋅yauvateṣu kampadā
kṛṣṇa⋅rūpa⋅veśa⋅keli⋅lagna⋅satsamādhikā
mahyamastu pāda⋅padma⋅dāsyadāstu rādhikā

sveda⋅kampa⋅kaṇṭakāśru⋅gadgadādi⋅sañcitā⋅
marṣa⋅harṣa⋅vāmatādi⋅bhāva⋅bhūṣaṇāñcitā
kṛṣṇa⋅netra⋅toṣi⋅ratna⋅maṇḍanāli⋅dādhikā
mahyamastu pāda⋅padma⋅dāsyadāstu rādhikā

jā kṣaṇārdha⋅kṛṣṇa⋅viprayoga⋅santatoditā⋅
neka⋅dainya⋅cāpalādi⋅bhāva⋅vṛnda⋅moditā
jatnalabdha⋅kṛṣṇa⋅saṅga⋅nirgatākhilādhikā
mahyamastu pāda⋅padma⋅dāsyadāstu rādhikā

aṣṭakena jastvetena nauti kṛṣṇa⋅vallabhāṁ
darśane‛pi śailajādi⋅yoṣidāli⋅durlabhām
kṛṣṇa⋅saṅga⋅nanditātma⋅dāsya⋅sīdhu⋅bhājanaṁ
taṁ karoti nanditāli⋅sañcayāśu sā janam
IDX.en_translation:
Anointed with vermilion, She whose golden lotus-like beauty defeats all pride. She whose fragrance, surpassing the scent of a blossoming lotus, proclaims Her fame. She who fulfills all the desires of the son of the king of the cowherds. May that Rādhā, the beloved of Kṛṣṇa, grant me service to Her lotus feet.

She whose colorful silken garments defeat the luster of sapphire. She is a garden of blossoming flowers for Kṛṣṇa, the maddened bee, to sport in. Rādhā, who is like the sun for the eternal union with Kṛṣṇa. May She grant me service to Her lotus feet.

Her tenderness surpasses the beauty of young sprouts. She whose cool form is served by the moon, sandalwood, lotus, and camphor. She who relieves the fever of the Lord of the cowherds by Her touch. May She grant me service to Her lotus feet.

Even the goddess of fortune, who is worshiped by all the worlds, is not equal to Her in the wealth of beauty, youth, and other qualities. In character, affection, and pastimes, She is supreme. May She grant me service to Her lotus feet.

She is skilled in all the excellent arts of the rāsa dance, singing, and joking. Adorned with the charming beauty of love, attire, and all good qualities. She who is superior even to all the young gopīs of the world. May She grant me service to Her lotus feet.

Always endowed with the wealth of ever-new beauty, sports, and Kṛṣṇa's moods, She makes the young gopīs tremble in their bond of love for Him. She is perfectly absorbed in the form, attire, and pastimes of Kṛṣṇa. May She grant me service to Her lotus feet.

Filled with emotions like perspiration, trembling, thrills of ecstasy, and a choked voice, She is adorned with the ornaments of various moods like indignation, joy, and charm. She bestows a multitude of jeweled ornaments that please Kṛṣṇa's eyes. May She grant me service to Her lotus feet.

She who is pleased by the multitude of emotions like extreme humility and restlessness, which arise from even a half-moment's separation from Kṛṣṇa. She who becomes entirely perfect upon obtaining Kṛṣṇa's company. May She grant me service to Her lotus feet.

He who praises the beloved of Kṛṣṇa with these eight verses—She who is difficult for even the greatest goddesses to behold—is quickly made a recipient of the nectar of service to Her feet by Her, making all Her companions joyful.

IDX.first_line: kuvera pandita ati harasita
IDX.search: kuvera pandita ati harasita dekhiya putrera mukha kari jata karma achila dharma vadaye manera sukha sarva sulaksana varana kancana vadana kamala sobha ajanulambita bahu salalita jaga jana mano lobha
IDX.author: vaiṣṇava⋅dāsa
IDX.verses: kuvera paṇḍita, ati haraṣita, dekhiyā putrera mukha kari jāta⋅karma, āchila dharma, vāḍaye manera sukha sarva sulakṣaṇa varaṇa kāñcana, vadana⋅kamala śobhā ājānulambita, bāhu salalita, jaga⋅jana⋅mano⋅lobhā nābhi sagabhīra, parama sundara, nayana kamala jini aruṇa caraṇa, nakha⋅darapaṇa, jini kata vidhu maṇi mahāpuruṣera, citta manohara, dekhiyā vismaya sabe bujhi ihā haite, jagata taribe, ei kare anubhave jata pura⋅nārī, śiśumukha heri, ānanda sāyare bhāse nā dharaye hiyā, punaḥ punaḥ giyā, nirakhaye animeṣe tāhāra mātāre, kare parihāse, kahe hena suta jāra tāra bhāgya sīmā, ke diba upamā, bhuvane ke sama tāre e teka vacana, saba nārīgaṇe, kahe gadgad bhāṣa jagata tāraṇa, bujhala kāraṇa, dāsa vaiṣṇavera āśā
IDX.en_translation:
Kuvera Paṇḍita was greatly delighted to see the face of his son. He performed the birth rituals as prescribed by religious duty, and his happiness increased manifold.

The child was endowed with all auspicious markings and a golden complexion. He had the beauty of a lotus, and His graceful arms reached down to His knees, captivating the minds of all people.

He had a deep navel, was supremely beautiful, and His eyes surpassed the beauty of lotus flowers. His feet were reddish, and His toenails were like mirrors, conquering the luster of millions of moons and jewels.

Everyone was astonished to see these charming signs of a Great Personality, and they felt that through Him, the world would be delivered.

All the women of the town, beholding the child's face, floated in an ocean of bliss. Their hearts could not contain their joy, and they went again and again to gaze at Him without blinking.

They jokingly said to His mother, "Who can describe the limit of the fortune of one who has such a son? Who in the world is equal to her?"

All the women spoke with voices choked with emotion, understanding the reason for the world's deliverance. This is the hope of Vaiṣṇava Dāsa.

IDX.first_line: madana gopala sarana teri ayo
IDX.search: madana gopala sarana teri ayo carana kamala ki seva dijo cero kari rakho ghara jayo dhanya dhanya mata pita suta bandhu dhanya janani jina goda khilayo dhanya dhanya carana calata tirtha ko dhanya guru jina harinama sunayo
IDX.verses:
madana gopāla śaraṇa terī āyo
caraṇa kamala kī sevā dījo,
cero kari rākho ghara jāyo

dhanya dhanya māta pitā suta bandhū,
dhanya jananī jina goda khilāyo
dhanya dhanya caraṇa calata tīrtha ko,
dhanya guru jina harināma sunāyo

je nara vimukha bhaye govinda so,
janma aneka mahāduḥkha pāyo
‘śrī⋅bhaṭṭa’ ke prabhu diyo abhaya⋅pada,
yama ḍarapyo jaba dāsa kahāyo
IDX.en_translation:
O Madana Gopāla! I have come to Your shelter. Please grant me service to Your lotus feet and keep me as a servant in Your home.

Blessed are the mother, father, son, and relatives of Your devotee. Blessed is the mother who nursed him in her lap. Blessed are the feet that walk to the holy places, and blessed is the guru who made one hear the Holy Name.

Those persons who became averse to Lord Govinda have suffered greatly through many births. The Lord of the poet Śrī Bhaṭṭa gave the fearless state; even the God of Death became afraid when one was called His servant.

IDX.first_line: madhava bahuta minati kari toya
IDX.search: madhava bahuta minati kari toya dei tulasi tila deha samarpinu daya jani na chodabi moya ganoite dosa gunalesa na paobi jaba tuhu karabi vicara tuhu jagannatha jagate kahaosi jaga bahira nahi muni chara
IDX.author: śrī⋅vidyāpati
IDX.verses:
mādhava, bahuta minati kari toya
dei tulasī tila, deha samarpinu,
dayā jāni nā choḍabi moya

gaṇoite doṣa, guṇa-leśa nā pāobi,
jaba tuhu̐ karabi vicāra
tuhu̐ jagannātha, jagate kahāosi,
jaga⋅bāhira nahi muñi chāra

kiye mānuṣa paśu⋅, pākhī je janamiye,
athavā kīṭa⋅pataṅge
karama⋅vipāke, gatāgati punaḥ punaḥ,
mati rahu tuyā parasaṅge

bhaṇaye vidyāpati, atiśaya kātara,
taroite iha bhavasindhu
tuyā pada-pallava, kari avalamvana,
tila eka deha dīna-bandhu
IDX.en_translation:
O Mādhava, I have one great plea! Offering sacred Tulasi leaves and sesame seeds, I have surrendered my body. Knowing my surrender, please do not abandon me.

When You consider my faults, You will not find a trace of good quality. You are the Lord of the universe and are known as its Lord. I, though insignificant, am not outside this world.

Whether I am born as a human, animal, bird, insect, or worm, due to the consequences of my actions—coming and going again and again—may my mind always remain in Your association.

Vidyāpati says: I am extremely distressed to cross this ocean of material existence. Taking shelter of Your lotus feet, please grant me Your protection for a moment, O friend of the distressed.

IDX.first_line: madhava bahuta minati kari toyadei tulasi tila deha samarpinudaya jani na chodabi moya
IDX.search: madhava bahuta minati kari toyadei tulasi tila deha samarpinudaya jani na chodabi moya ganoite dosa gunalesa na paobijaba tuhu karabi vicaratuhu jagannatha jagate kahaosijaga bahira nahi muni chara
IDX.author: śrī⋅vidyāpati
IDX.verses:
mādhava, bahuta minati kari toyadei tulasī tila, deha samarpinu,dayā jāni nā choḍabi moya

gaṇoite doṣa, guṇa-leśa nā pāobi,jaba tuhu̐ karabi vicāratuhu̐ jagannātha, jagate kahāosi,jaga⋅bāhira nahi muñi chāra

kiye mānuṣa paśu⋅, pākhī je janamiye,athavā kīṭa⋅pataṅgekarama⋅vipāke, gatāgati punaḥ punaḥ,mati rahu tuyā parasaṅge

bhaṇaye vidyāpati, atiśaya kātara,taroite iha bhavasindhutuyā pada-pallava, kari avalamvana,tila eka deha dīna-bandhu
IDX.en_translation:
O Mādhava, I have one great plea! Offering sacred Tulasi leaves and sesame seeds, I have surrendered my body. Knowing my surrender, please do not abandon me.

When You consider my faults, You will not find a trace of good quality. You are the Lord of the universe and are known as its Lord. I, though insignificant, am not outside this world.

Whether I am born as a human, animal, bird, insect, or worm, due to the consequences of my actions—coming and going again and again—may my mind always remain in Your association.

Vidyāpati says: I am extremely distressed to cross this ocean of material existence. Taking shelter of Your lotus feet, please grant me Your protection for a moment, O friend of the distressed.

IDX.first_line: madhurera sthayi bhava labhe jate avirbhava
IDX.search: madhurera sthayi bhava labhe jate avirbhava bali taha suna ekamane abhiyoga o visaya sambandhabhimana dvaya tadiya visesa upamane svabhava asraya kari citte rati avatari srngara rasera kare pusti abhiyoga adi chaya anye ratihetu haya vrajadevira tahe nahi drsti
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
madhurera sthāyī bhāva, labhe jā’te āvirbhāva,
bali tāhā, śuna ekamane
‘abhiyoga’ o ‘viṣaya’, ‘sambandhābhimāna’⋅dvaya,
‘tadīya viśeṣa’ ‘upamāne’

‘svabhāva’ āśraya kari’, citte rati avatāri’,
śṛṅgāra rasera kare puṣṭi
abhiyoga⋅ādi chaya, anye ratihetu haya,
vrajadevīra tāhe nāhi dṛṣṭi

svataḥsiddha⋅rati tā̐’re, sambaddhādi⋅sahakāre,
samarthā kariyā rākhe sadā
kṛṣṇasevā vinā tā̐’ra, udyama nāhika āra,
svīya sukha⋅ceṣṭā nāhi kadā

ai rati prauḍhā haya, mahābhāva daśā pāya,
jā’ra tulya prāpti āra nāi
sarvādbhuta camatkāra, sambhogecchā e prakāra,
varṇibāre vākya nāhi pāi
IDX.en_translation:
The permanent sentiment of conjugal love finds its manifestation in what I will now describe; listen attentively: complaint, the object of desire, the two aspects of the sense of relationship, His specific qualities, and similes.

Taking refuge in one’s inherent nature, love descends into the heart, nurturing the conjugal sentiment. For others, these six factors become the cause of their love, but the cowherd girls of Vraja do not view it this way.

Their spontaneous love, along with Their sense of relationship, always keeps them capable of selfless service. For them, there is no other endeavor except the service of Kṛṣṇa; there is never any attempt for Their own happiness.

That love matures and attains the stage of the highest ecstatic love, to which there is no equal attainment. Such a desire for union, which is entirely wonderful and astonishing, I find no words to describe.

IDX.first_line: maha prasade govinde nama brahmani vaisnavesvalpa punya vatam rajan visvaso naiva jayate
IDX.search: maha prasade govinde nama brahmani vaisnavesvalpa punya vatam rajan visvaso naiva jayate bhairesarira avidya jala jadendriya tahe kalajive phele visaya sagaretara madhye jihva ati lobhamaya sudurmatitake jeta kathina samsare
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
(mahā⋅prasāde govinde nāma brahmaṇi vaiṣṇavesvalpa⋅puṇya⋅vatāṁ rājan viśvāso naiva jāyate)

bhāire!śarīra avidyā⋅jāla, jaḍendriya tāhe kāla,jīve phele viṣaya⋅sāgaretā’ra madhye jihvā ati, lobhamaya sudurmati,tā’ke jetā kaṭhina saṁsāre

kṛṣṇa baḍa dayāmaya, karibāre jihvā jaya,svaprasāda⋅anna dilā bhāisei annāmṛta pāo, rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa⋅guṇa gāo,preme ḍāka caitanya⋅nitāi
IDX.en_translation:
For those with little piety, faith in mahā-prasāda, Govinda, nāma-brahma, or a Vaiṣṇava never arises.

O brother! The body is a net of ignorance, and the material senses within it are like a deadly snake, throwing the soul into the ocean of material enjoyment. Among them, the tongue is most greedy and wicked-minded; conquering it is very difficult in this material world.

Kṛṣṇa is very merciful; to help us conquer the tongue, O brother, He has given us His own prasāda. Accept that nectar-like food, sing the glories of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa, and with love, call out Caitanya-Nitāi.

IDX.first_line: mahabhava cintamani udbhavita tanukhani
IDX.search: mahabhava cintamani udbhavita tanukhani sakhipati sajja prabhavati karunya tarunya ara lavanya amrtadhara tahe snata laksmijayi sati lajja pattavastra jara saundarya kunkuma sara kasturi citrita kalevara kampasru pulaka ranga stambha sveda svarabhanga jadyanmada navaratnadhara
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
mahābhāva⋅cintāmaṇi, udbhāvita tanukhāni,
sakhīpati⋅sajja⋅prabhāvatī
kāruṇya⋅tāruṇya āra, lāvaṇya⋅amṛtadhāra,
tāhe snātā lakṣmī-jayī satī

lajjā paṭṭa-vastra jā’ra, saundarya kuṅkuma⋅sāra,
kasturī⋅citrita kalevara
kampāśru⋅pulaka⋅raṅga, stambha⋅sveda⋅svarabhaṅga,
jāḍyānmāda⋅navaratnadhara

pañcaviṁśatika⋅guṇa⋅, phulamālā suśobhana,
dhīrādhīrā⋅bhāva⋅paṭṭavāsā
pihita⋅māna⋅dhammillā, saubhāgya⋅tilakojjvalā,
kṛṣṇa⋅nāma⋅yaśaḥ⋅karṇollāsā

rāgatāmbūlita⋅oṣṭha, kauṭilya⋅kajjala spaṣṭa,
smitakarpūrita⋅narmaśīlā
kīrtiyaśa⋅antaḥpure, garva⋅khaṭṭopari sphure,
dulita⋅premavaicittya⋅mālā

praṇayaroṣa⋅kañculī⋅, pihita⋅stanayugmakā,
candrājayī kacchapī⋅raviṇī
sakhīdvayaskandhe līlā⋅, karāmbujārpaṇaśīlā,
śyāmā śyāmāmṛta⋅vitaraṇī

e hena rādhikā⋅pada, tomādera susampada,
dante tṛṇa jāce tava pāya
e bhaktivinoda dīna, rādhā⋅dāsyāmṛta⋅kaṇa,
rūpa raghunātha! deha tāya
IDX.en_translation:
She is a jewel of the highest ecstatic love, whose transcendental form is manifested, glorious, and adorned as the chief of the companions. She is a stream of nectar of compassion, youth, and beauty, in which she—the chaste lady who conquers the goddess of fortune—is bathed.

Her silken garment is shyness, her beauty is the essence of saffron, and her body is adorned with musk. Trembling, tears, thrills of ecstasy, paralysis, perspiration, a faltering voice, and madness are her nine jewels.

Adorned with a beautiful flower garland of twenty-five qualities, her silken dress is a mood of both patience and impatience. Her braided hair conceals her pique, she shines with the mark of good fortune, and the glory of Kṛṣṇa’s names is the delight of her ears.

Her lips are red with the betel of attachment, her clear eyeliner is her charming crookedness, and she is playful, perfumed with the camphor of smiles. In the inner chambers of fame and glory, she shines upon the throne of pride, wearing the swaying garland of the uniqueness of love.

Her blouse of loving anger covers her breasts; she conquers the moon and is the melody of the lute. Playfully placing her lotus hands on the shoulders of two companions, she distributes the nectar of Kṛṣṇa.

Such feet of Rādhikā are your great wealth; with a blade of grass in my teeth, I beg at your feet. This humble Bhaktivinoda asks for a particle of the nectar of service to Rādhā. O Rūpa, O Raghunātha! Please give it to him.

IDX.first_line: mahabhava cintamani udbhavita tanukhanisakhipati sajja prabhavatikarunya tarunya ara lavanya amrtadharatahe snata laksmijayi sati
IDX.search: mahabhava cintamani udbhavita tanukhanisakhipati sajja prabhavatikarunya tarunya ara lavanya amrtadharatahe snata laksmijayi sati lajja pattavastra jara saundarya kunkuma sarakasturi citrita kalevarakampasru pulaka ranga stambha sveda svarabhangajadyanmada navaratnadhara
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
mahābhāva⋅cintāmaṇi, udbhāvita tanukhāni,sakhīpati⋅sajja⋅prabhāvatīkāruṇya⋅tāruṇya āra, lāvaṇya⋅amṛtadhāra,tāhe snātā lakṣmī-jayī satī

lajjā paṭṭa-vastra jā’ra, saundarya kuṅkuma⋅sāra,kasturī⋅citrita kalevarakampāśru⋅pulaka⋅raṅga, stambha⋅sveda⋅svarabhaṅga,jāḍyānmāda⋅navaratnadhara

pañcaviṁśatika⋅guṇa⋅, phulamālā suśobhana,dhīrādhīrā⋅bhāva⋅paṭṭavāsāpihita⋅māna⋅dhammillā, saubhāgya⋅tilakojjvalā,kṛṣṇa⋅nāma⋅yaśaḥ⋅karṇollāsā

rāgatāmbūlita⋅oṣṭha, kauṭilya⋅kajjala spaṣṭa,smitakarpūrita⋅narmaśīlākīrtiyaśa⋅antaḥpure, garva⋅khaṭṭopari sphure,dulita⋅premavaicittya⋅mālā

praṇayaroṣa⋅kañculī⋅, pihita⋅stanayugmakā,candrājayī kacchapī⋅raviṇīsakhīdvayaskandhe līlā⋅, karāmbujārpaṇaśīlā,śyāmā śyāmāmṛta⋅vitaraṇī

e hena rādhikā⋅pada, tomādera susampada,dante tṛṇa jāce tava pāyae bhaktivinoda dīna, rādhā⋅dāsyāmṛta⋅kaṇa,rūpa raghunātha! deha tāya
IDX.en_translation:
She is a jewel of the highest ecstatic love, whose transcendental form is manifested, glorious, and adorned as the chief of the companions. She is a stream of nectar of compassion, youth, and beauty, in which she—the chaste lady who conquers the goddess of fortune—is bathed.

Her silken garment is shyness, her beauty is the essence of saffron, and her body is adorned with musk. Trembling, tears, thrills of ecstasy, paralysis, perspiration, a faltering voice, and madness are her nine jewels.

Adorned with a beautiful flower garland of twenty-five qualities, her silken dress is a mood of both patience and impatience. Her braided hair conceals her pique, she shines with the mark of good fortune, and the glory of Kṛṣṇa’s names is the delight of her ears.

Her lips are red with the betel of attachment, her clear eyeliner is her charming crookedness, and she is playful, perfumed with the camphor of smiles. In the inner chambers of fame and glory, she shines upon the throne of pride, wearing the swaying garland of the uniqueness of love.

Her blouse of loving anger covers her breasts; she conquers the moon and is the melody of the lute. Playfully placing her lotus hands on the shoulders of two companions, she distributes the nectar of Kṛṣṇa.

Such feet of Rādhikā are your great wealth; with a blade of grass in my teeth, I beg at your feet. This humble Bhaktivinoda asks for a particle of the nectar of service to Rādhā. O Rūpa, O Raghunātha! Please give it to him.

IDX.first_line: mama mana mandire raha nisidina
IDX.search: mama mana mandire raha nisidina krsna murari sri krsna murari bhakti priti mala candana tumi nio he nio cittanandana
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
mama mana mandire raha niśidina
kṛṣṇa⋅murāri śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅murāri

bhakti⋅prīti⋅mālā⋅candana
tumi nio he nio cittānandana

jīvana maraṇa tava pūjā nivedana
sundara he manahārī

esa nandakumāra āra nandakumāra
habe premapradīpe ārati tomāra

nayane jamunā jhare anivāra
tomāra virahe giridhārī

vandana gāne tava bajuka jīvana
kṛṣṇa⋅murāri śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅murāri
IDX.en_translation:
In the temple of my mind, remain night and day, O Kṛṣṇa, the enemy of the demon Mura!

May You accept my garlands of devotion and love, and my sandalwood paste, O Lord who delights the heart.

My life and death are my worshipful offerings to You, O beautiful Lord who captivates the mind.

Come, O son of Nanda! Your ceremony will be performed with the lamp of love.

From my eyes, tears flow incessantly like the Yamunā in separation from You, O lifter of Govardhana Hill.

Let my life resound with songs of Your praise, O Lord who destroyed the demon Mura!

IDX.first_line: mana amara husara theko bhula naka
IDX.search: mana amara husara theko bhula naka suddha sahaja tattvadhane noile mayara vase avasese kadate habe ciradine suddhajive jada nai bhai thika bujha tai nije sukhi se vrndavane se jakhana krsna candre bhaje sukhete maje madhura rase anuksane
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
(mana āmāra) hu̐sā’ra theko, bhula’ nāka,
śuddha sahaja tattvadhane
noile māyāra vaśe, avaśeṣe,
kā̐date ha’be ciradine

śuddhajīve jaḍa nāi, bhāi, ṭhika bujha tāi,
nije sukhī (se) vṛndāvane
se jakhana kṛṣṇa⋅candre bhaje sukhete maje,
madhura⋅rase anukṣaṇe

jaḍadehe tāra sādhanabhakti, jñāna⋅virakti,
dehera jātrā dharmabhāve
se gṛhe thāke, vane vā thāke, majiye ḍāke,
‘kṛṣṇa’ bale’ ekamane

ekei ta’ bali sahaja⋅bhajana, śuddha mana,
kṛṣṇa pā’bāra eka upāya
ihā chāḍi’ je āropa kare, sei ta’ mare,
tāra ta’ nāhi bhajana haya

cā̐da⋅bāulera e viśvāsa, choṭa⋅haridāsa,
ekaṭu kevala vipathe cale
śacī⋅sutera kṛpāya, dūra ha’ye, hāya,
nā pāya āra gauracaraṇe
IDX.en_translation:
O my mind! Remain conscious and do not forget the wealth of pure and simple truth. Otherwise, under the sway of illusion, you will eventually have to weep eternally.

In a pure soul there is no materialism, brother; understand this clearly. The pure soul is naturally happy in Vṛndāvana. When he worships Kṛṣṇa⋅candra, he drowns in joy and relishes sweet mellows at every moment.

Within his material body he practices devotion and detachment from worldly knowledge; the journey of his body is conducted in a religious mood. Whether he lives at home or in the forest, he calls out "Kṛṣṇa!" with a single mind.

This indeed is called simple worship—with a pure mind, the one way to attain Kṛṣṇa. Whoever abandons this and imposes something artificial only perishes; he performs no true worship.

This is the belief of Cānd Bāula. Choṭa Haridāsa went but a little astray, yet by the decree of the Lord, he became distant and could no longer attain His feet.

IDX.first_line: mana re kahana gaura katha
IDX.search: mana re kahana gaura katha gaurera nama amiyara dhama piriti murati data sayane gaura svapane gaura gaura nayanera tara jivane gaura marane gaura gaura galara hara
IDX.author: narahari⋅dāsa
IDX.verses:
mana re! kahanā gaura⋅kathā
gaurera nāma, amiyāra dhāma,
pirīti mūrati dātā

śayane gaura, svapane gaura,
gaura nayanera tārā
jīvane gaura, maraṇe gaura,
gaura galāra hāra

hiyāra mājhāre, gaurāṅge rākhiye,
virale basiyā ra’ba
manera sādhete, se rūpacā̐dere,
nayane nayane thoba

gaura vihane, na bā̐ci parāṇe,
gaura ka’rechi sāra
gaura baliyā, jāuka jīvana,
kichu nā cāhiba āra

gaura gamana, gaura gaṭhana,
gaura mukhera hā̐si
gaura⋅pirīti, gaura mūrati,
hīyāya rahala paśi

gaura dharama, gaura karama,
gaura vedera sāra
gaura caraṇe, parāṇa sā̐pinu,
gaura karibena pāra

gaura śabada, gaura sampada,
jā̐hāra hiyāya jāge
naraharidāsa, tā̐ra dāsera dāsa,
caraṇe śaraṇa māge
IDX.en_translation:
O mind! Speak the topics of Gaura. Gaura's Name is the abode of nectar, the giver of the form of love.

Gaura in sleep, Gaura in dreams, Gaura is the star of the eye. Gaura in life, Gaura in death, Gaura is the necklace of the neck.

Keeping Gaurāṅga within my heart, I will remain sitting in solitude. With the desire of the mind, I will place that Moon of Beauty eye to eye.

Without Gaura, I do not survive in life; I have made Gaura the essence. Let life go saying "Gaura"; I will not want anything else.

Gaura's gait, Gaura's form, the smile on Gaura's face; Gaura's love, Gaura's form—remained entered in my heart.

Gaura is Dharma, Gaura is Karma, Gaura is the essence of the Vedas. At Gaura's feet I have offered my life; Gaura will take me across.

He in whose heart the Sound of Gaura and the Wealth of Gaura awaken—Narahari Dāsa, the servant of His servant's servant, begs for shelter at the feet.

IDX.first_line: manima sunima heu garivara daka
IDX.search: manima sunima heu garivara daka niraksa janaku raksa pankaja mukha mani krpasagara kahiki mo bedaku gola sukhi janili mo karmabaŋka nuhe sadakha atanga nasanabana na suna bhrtya bedana ebe hoiyiba maha kedeva daka
IDX.author: sadānanda dāsa
IDX.verses:
maṇimā śunimā heu garīvara ḍāka
nirakṣa janaku rakṣa paṅkaja mukha (maṇi)
kṛpāsāgara kā̐hiki, mo beḍaku gola śukhi
jānili mo karmabāŋka nuhe śaḍakha

ātaṅga nāśanabāṇā, na śuna bhṛtya bedanā
ebe hoiyiba mahā kedeva ḍāka

dāsa jebe nāśayiba, kṣitire akīrti heba
kīrati candare lagi jiba kalaṅka

kahe sadānanda chāra, nāhi āna pratikāra
e dīna avasthā mora netre na dekha
IDX.en_translation:
O precious Lord! Please hear the call of this poor one. Protect this ignorant person, O lotus-faced One! O ocean of mercy, why has my shelter dried up? I know that my actions are not pure.

O Destroyer of fear! Do You not hear Your servant's pain? Now there will be a great cry of sorrow.

If Your servant is destroyed, there will be dishonor on earth. A stain will befall the moon of Your glory.

Sadānanda Dāsa, the lowly one, says: "There is no other remedy. Do not let me witness this wretched condition with my own eyes."

IDX.first_line: manusa bhajana karcho o bhai
IDX.search: manusa bhajana karcho o bhai bhavera gana dhare gupta kare rakhcho bhala vyakta habe jamera ghare meye hijde purusa khoja tabe ta haya kartabhaja ei chale karcho maja manera prati cokha there
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
mānuṣa⋅bhajana karcho, o bhāi,
bhāvera gāna dha’re
gupta ka’re rākhcho bhāla,
vyakta ha’be jamera ghare

meye hijḍe, puruṣa khojā,
tabe ta’ haya kartābhajā,
ei chale karcho majā,
manera prati cokha ṭhere’

‘guru satya’ balcho mukhe,
ācha ta’ bhāi, jaḍera sukhe,
saṅga tomāra bahirmukhe,
śuddha ha’be kemana kare’

joṣitsaṅga⋅arthalobhe,
maje ta’ jīva citta-kṣobhe,
bāule ki se⋅saba śobhe,
āguna dekhe’ phaḍiṁ mare

cā̐da⋅bāula minati kari’
bale⋅o saba parihari’,
śuddha-bhāve bala ‘hari’,
jā’be bhavasāgara⋅pāre
IDX.en_translation:
O brother, you are engaging in mānuṣa-bhajana (worship of humans), singing songs of bhāva (ecstatic emotion). You keep the good hidden, but it will be revealed in the house of Yama (the lord of death).

If the female is hijṛe (lacking spiritual feminine qualities) and the male is khojā (lacking spiritual masculine qualities), then it becomes karttābhaṛā (worship of a worldly master). By this pretense, you are enjoying, deceiving your own mind.

You say "Guru (spiritual master) is truth" with your mouth, but, brother, you are immersed in material happiness. Your association is with outward things; how will you become pure?

The jīva (living entity) gets absorbed in yoṣitsanga (association with women) and arthalobha (greed for wealth), leading to mental agitation. Do such things suit a bāula (mendicant/devotee)? A moth dies seeing fire.

Cāṅda-Bāula humbly requests: giving up all these, purely chant 'Hari'; then you will cross the bhavasāgara (ocean of material existence).

IDX.first_line: miche maya vase samsara sagare
IDX.search: miche maya vase samsara sagare padiya chilama ami karuna kariya diya pada chaya amare tarile tumi suna suna vaisnava thakura tomara carane sapiyachi matha mora duhkha kara dura
IDX.author: kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa
IDX.verses:
miche māyā⋅vaśe, saṁsāra⋅sāgare,
paḍiyā chilāma āmi
karuṇā kariyā, diyā pada⋅chāyā,
āmāre tārile tumi

śuna śuna vaiṣṇava ṭhākura
tomāra caraṇe, sā̐piyāchi māthā,
mora duḥkha kara dūra

jātira gaurava, kevala raurava,
vidyā se avidyā⋅kalā
śadhiyā āmāya, nitāi⋅caraṇe,
sā̐pahe⋅jāuka jvālā

tomāra kṛpāya, āmāra jihvāya,
sphuruka jugala⋅nāma
kahe kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa, āmāra hṛdaye,
jāguka śrī⋅rādhāśyāma
IDX.en_translation:
Under the sway of illusion, I was fallen in the ocean of material existence. By showing mercy and giving me the shade of your feet, you saved me.

Listen, O Vaiṣṇava Master! At your feet I have surrendered my head; please remove all my sorrows.

Pride in one's caste or lineage is merely a hellish condition; such knowledge is only the art of ignorance. Purify me and offer me at the feet of Lord Nityānanda—let all my agony depart.

By your mercy, may the Names of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa manifest on my tongue. Kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa says: "May Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa awaken in my heart."

IDX.first_line: mora prabhu madanagopala govinda gopinathadaya kara muni adhameresamsara sagara majhe padiya baiyachi nathakrpadore bandhi laha more
IDX.search: mora prabhu madanagopala govinda gopinathadaya kara muni adhameresamsara sagara majhe padiya baiyachi nathakrpadore bandhi laha more adhama candala ami dayara thakura tumisuniyachi vaisnavera mukhee bada bharasa mane laiya phela vrndavanevamsivata jena dekhi sukhe
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
mora prabhu madana-gopāla, govinda gopīnātha,dayā kara muñi adhameresaṁsāra⋅sāgara⋅mājhe, paḍiyā baiyāchi nātha,kṛpāḍore bāndhi laha more

adhama caṇḍāla āmi, dayāra ṭhākura tumi,śuniyāchi vaiṣṇavera mukhee baḍa bharasā mane, laiyā phela vṛndāvane,vaṁśīvaṭa jena dekhi sukhe

kṛpā kara āguguri’, laha more keśe dhari’,śrī⋅yamunā deha’ padachāyāaneka dinera āśa, nahe jena nairāśa,dayā kara, nā karaha māyā

anitya e deha dhari’, āpana āpana kari’,pāche pāche śamanera bhayanarottama⋅dāsa bhaṇe, prāṇa kānde rātri-dine,pāche vrajaprāpti nāhi haya
IDX.en_translation:
My Lord Madana-gopāla, Govinda, Gopīnātha! Please show mercy to me, the most fallen soul. I have fallen into the midst of the ocean of material existence, O Lord! Please bind me with the rope of Your mercy.

I am a fallen outcaste, and You are the Lord of mercy; I have heard this from the devotees of the Lord. With this great hope in my heart, please take me and place me in Vṛndāvana, so that I may happily see the Vaṁśīvaṭa tree.

Please show mercy and, taking hold of me by my hair, lead me to the shade of Śrī Yamunā’s lotus feet. My long-cherished hope—let it not turn into despair. Please show mercy; do not use Your illusory energy.

Having attained this temporary body and considering it as “mine,” the fear of the God of Death follows behind me. Narottama Dāsa says: “My heart cries day and night, for fear that I may not attain Vraja”.

IDX.first_line: mrgamada candana kare kari sakhigana
IDX.search: mrgamada candana kare kari sakhigana sramajala bindu bindu sobha kare mukha indu adhare murali kiba baje hasya vilasa rasa sarala madhura bhasa narottama manoratha bharu duhuka vicitra vesa kusume racita kesa locana mohana lila karu
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
mṛgamada⋅candana, kare kari’ sakhīgaṇa,
śramajala bindu bindu, śobhā kare mukha⋅indu,
adhare muralī kibā bāje

hāsya⋅vilāsa⋅rasa, sarala madhura bhāṣa,
narottama⋅manoratha bharu
duhu̐ka vicitra veśa, kusume racita keśa,
locana⋅mohana līlā karu
IDX.en_translation:
Holding musk and sandalwood paste in their hands, the sakhīs serve the Divine Couple. Beads of perspiration adorn Their moon-like faces, As the flute plays so wondrously upon His lips.

With the nectar of playful laughter and simple, sweet speech, May the desires of Narottama’s heart be fulfilled. Both are dressed in exquisite raiment with hair entwined with flowers; May They perform Their eye-enchanting pastimes

IDX.first_line: mrta sisu laye tabe bhakatavatsala
IDX.search: mrta sisu laye tabe bhakatavatsala bhakata sangete gaya sri nama mangala gaite gaite gela jahnavira tire balake satkara kaila jahnavira nire
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
mṛta śiśu la’ye tabe bhakatavatsala
bhakata⋅saṅgete gāya śrī⋅nāma⋅maṅgala

gāite gāite gelā jāhnavīra tīre
bālake satkāra kaila jāhnavīra nīre

jāhnavī balena, mama saubhāgya apāra
saphala hoila vrata chila je āmāra

mṛta śiśu dena gorā jāhnavīra jale
uthali jāhnavī-devī śiśu laya kole

uthaliyā sparśe gorā⋅caraṇa⋅kamala
śiśu⋅kole preme devī haya ṭalamala

jāhnavīra bhāva dekhi’ jata bhaktagaṇa
śrī⋅nāma⋅maṅgaladhvani kare anukṣaṇa

svarga haite deve kare puṣpa⋅variṣaṇa
vimāna⋅saṅkula tabe chāila gagana

eirūpe nānā bhāve hoiyā magana
satkāra kariyā snāna kaila sarva-jana

parama⋅ānande sabe gela nija ghare
bhaktivinoda maje gorā⋅bhāvabhare
IDX.en_translation:
Then, taking the dead child, the Lord, who is affectionate to His devotees, sings the auspicious holy name with them.

While singing, he went to the bank of the Ganges and performed the funeral rites for the child in its waters.

The Jāhnavī says, "My good fortune is boundless; my vow has become successful."

Gorā gives the dead child into the waters of the Ganges. Swelling up, Jāhnavī-devī takes the child into her lap.

Swelling up, she touches Gorā's lotus feet. With the child in her lap, the goddess trembles with divine love.

Seeing the emotional state of the Jāhnavī, all the devotees continuously make the auspicious sound of the holy name.

From heaven, the demigods shower flowers. Then the sky becomes covered, filled with celestial airplanes.

In this way, becoming absorbed in various emotional states, all the people performed the funeral rites and then took a bath.

In supreme bliss, all went to their homes. Bhaktivinoda is immersed in the fullness of the mood of Gorā.

anupadam udayantyā rādhikāsaṅga⋅siddhyā
sthagita⋅pṛthur athāṅga⋅dvandva⋅rāgānubandhaḥ
madhurima⋅madhu⋅dhārā⋅dhoraṇīnām udanvān
vraja⋅nava⋅yuva⋅rājaḥ kāṅkṣitaṁ me kṛṣīṣṭa

alaghu⋅kuṭila⋅rādhā⋅dṛṣṭi⋅vārī⋅niruddha⋅
trijagad⋅aparatantroddāma⋅ceto⋅gajendraḥ
sukha⋅mukhara⋅viśākhā⋅narmaṇā smera⋅vaktro
vraja⋅nava⋅yuva⋅rājaḥ kāṅkṣitaṁ me kṛṣīṣṭa

tvayi rahasi milantyāṁ sambhrama⋅nyāsa⋅bhugnāpy
uṣasi sakhi tavālī⋅mekhalā paśya bhāti
iti vivṛta⋅rahasyair hrepayann eva rādhāṁ
vraja⋅nava⋅yuva⋅rājaḥ kāṅkṣitaṁ me kṛṣīṣṭa

vraja⋅nava⋅yuva⋅rājasyāṣṭakaṁ tuṣṭa⋅buddhiḥ
kalita⋅vara⋅vilāsaṁ jaḥ prayatnād adhīte
parijana⋅gaṇanāyāṁ nāma tasyānurajyan
vilikhati kila vṛndāraṇya⋅rājñī⋅rasajñaḥ
IDX.en_translation:
By the beauty of His limbs He crushes the pride of the god of love, and the splendor of His clothing surpasses the beauty of the lotus filament. He is the moon-like child whose immaturity is overshadowed by His rising youth; may the youthful prince of Vraja fulfill my desires.

He is like the churning rod that churns the very life of His father, and He is like the personified affection of His mother, Yaśodā. He is the nectar-like vision for the assembly of elders and cowherd girls; may the youthful prince of Vraja fulfill my desires.

He is the original teacher of captivating and skillful deeds, awakened in the entire world, and the resting place of supreme strength. He is the friend who delights everyone with His incomparable qualities; may the youthful prince of Vraja fulfill my desires.

By the twitching of His bow-like eyebrows, He creates waves of agitation in young women that are difficult even for Cupid with his countless arrows. He is the ocean for the fish-like lives of His dear companions; may the youthful prince of Vraja fulfill my desires.

He churns the deep lake of Rādhā's heart like a wild elephant, disturbed by the playful hooks of His eyes. He is the six-legged bee playing to taste the nectar of the mellow of divine love; may the youthful prince of Vraja fulfill my desires.

With the success of His union with Rādhikā, which arises at every step, His body is bound by Their mutual affection. He is an ocean of continuous streams of the honey of sweetness; may the youthful prince of Vraja fulfill my desires.

His great mind-elephant, which is unrestrained in the three worlds, is held captive by the abundant, crooked glances of Rādhā. With a smiling face due to the joyful jokes of Viśākhā, may the youthful prince of Vraja fulfill my desires.

"O friend, look! Even though it was crushed in the confusion of Your secret meeting, Your belt of friends shines in the morning!" Thus, by revealing these secrets, He makes Rādhā shy; may the youthful prince of Vraja fulfill my desires.

One who diligently studies these eight verses of the youthful prince of Vraja—which are full of excellent pastimes—will have his name recorded in the list of Her intimate associates by the Queen of Vṛndāvana, who is an expert in divine mellows.

IDX.first_line: na janiye goracadera kona bhava mane
IDX.search: na janiye goracadera kona bhava mane suradhuni tire gela sahacara sane priya gadadhara adi sangete kariya naukaya cadila gora premavesa hoiya
IDX.author: vasudeva ghoṣa
IDX.verses:
nā jāniye gorācā̐dera kona bhāva mane
suradhunī tīre gelā sahacara sane

priya gadādhara ādi saṅgete kariyā
naukāya caḍila gorā premāveśa hoiyā

āpani kāṇḍārī haye vāya naukākhāni
ḍubila ḍubila bali siñce sabe pāni

pāriṣadagaṇa sabe hari hari bale
pūrava soṅari kahe bhāse prema-jale

gadādhara mukha heri mṛdu mṛdu hāse
vāsudeva ghoṣa kahe manera ullāse
IDX.en_translation:
Without understanding what mood was in Gaurāṅgacāñda's mind, He went to the bank of the River Ganges with His associates.

Taking His dear Gadādhara and others with Him, Gaura ascended the boat, absorbed in ecstatic love.

He Himself became the helmsman and steered the boat. Everyone splashed water, saying, "It's sinking! It's sinking!"

All the associates chanted "Hari! Hari!" Remembering pastimes, they spoke, floating in the waters of divine love.

Seeing Gadādhara's face, He smiled gently. Vāsudeva Ghoṣa says with joy in his heart.

IDX.first_line: na joga siddhirna mamastu mokso
IDX.search: na joga siddhirna mamastu mokso vaikuntha lokepi na parsadatvam premapi na syaditi cettaram tu mamastu vrndavana eva vasah tarnam januryatra vidhiryayace sadbhakta cudamaniruddhavopi viksyaiva madhurya dhuram tadasmin mamastu vrndavana eva vasah
IDX.author: śrīla viśvanātha cakravartī ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
na joga⋅siddhirna mamāstu mokṣo
vaikuṇṭha⋅loke‛pi na pārṣadatvam
premāpi na syāditi cettarāṁ tu
mamāstu vṛndāvana eva vāsaḥ

tārṇaṁ januryatra vidhiryayāce
sadbhakta⋅cūḍāmaṇiruddhavo‛pi
vīkṣyaiva mādhurya⋅dhurāṁ tadasmin
mamāstu vṛndāvana eva vāsaḥ

kiṁ te kṛtaṁ hantatapaḥ kṣitīti
gopyo‛pi bhūme stuvate sma kīrtim
jenaiva kṛṣṇāṅghri⋅padāṅkite‛smin
mamāstu vṛndāvana eva vāsaḥ

gopāṅganā⋅lampaṭataiva jatra
jasyāṁ rasaḥ pūrṇatamatvamāpa
jato raso vai sa iti śrutista⋅
nmamāstu vṛndāvana eva vāsaḥ

bhāṇḍīra⋅govardhana⋅rāsa⋅pīṭhai⋅
strisīmake jojana⋅pañcakena
mite vibhutvādamite‛pi cāsmin
mamāstu vṛndāvana eva vāsaḥ

jatrādhipatyaṁ vṛṣabhānu⋅putryā
jenodayet prema⋅sukhaṁ janānām
jasminmamāśā balavatyato‛smin
mamāstu vṛndāvana eva vāsaḥ

jasmin mahā⋅rāsa⋅vilāsa⋅līlā
na prāpa jāṁ śrī⋅rapi sā tapobhiḥ
tatrollasanmañju⋅nikuñja⋅puñje
mamāstu vṛndāvana eva vāsaḥ

sadā rurunyaṅkumukhā viśaṅkaṁ
khelanti kūjanti pikāli⋅kīrāḥ
śikhaṇḍino jatra naṭanti tasmin
mamāstu vṛndāvana eva vāsaḥ

vṛndāvanasyāṣṭakametaduccaiḥ
paṭhanti je niścalabuddhayaste
vṛndāvaneśāṅghri⋅saroja⋅sevāṁ
sākṣāllabhante januṣo‛nta eva
IDX.en_translation:
I have no desire for yogic perfections or liberation. Nor do I desire to be an associate in the spiritual world. Even if I do not attain divine love, may my residence certainly be in Vṛndāvana.

Where Lord Brahmā prayed to be born even as a blade of grass, and Uddhava, the crest-jewel of pure devotees, also prayed, seeing its supreme sweetness—in that place, may my residence certainly be in Vṛndāvana.

"What austerities have you performed, O Earth?" Even the gopīs glorified the Earth, saying: "Because the marks of Lord Kṛṣṇa's lotus feet are imprinted upon you." In that place, may my residence certainly be in Vṛndāvana.

Where the attachment to the gopīs alone reaches its complete perfection, and because of which the Vedas declare "He is the personification of all mellows"—therefore, may my residence certainly be in Vṛndāvana.

Bounded by Bhaṇḍīravana, Govardhana, and the site of the Rāsa dance, marked by these three boundaries within a radius of forty miles—though measured, it is immeasurable in its glories. In that place, may my residence certainly be in Vṛndāvana.

Where the sovereignty belongs to the daughter of Vṛṣabhānu, by whom the joy of divine love arises in people, and in which my hope is very strong—therefore in that place, may my residence certainly be in Vṛndāvana.

Where the pastimes of the great Rāsa dance take place, which even the Goddess of Fortune could not attain through many austerities—in that place, filled with splendid and beautiful groves, may my residence certainly be in Vṛndāvana.

Where deer and antelopes always play fearlessly, cuckoos and parrots chirp sweetly, and peacocks dance—in that place, may my residence certainly be in Vṛndāvana.

Those who loudly recite these eight verses of Vṛndāvanāṣṭakam with steadfast intelligence will directly attain service to the lotus feet of the Mistress of Vṛndāvana at the end of their lives.

IDX.first_line: nace saci nandana bhakatajivana dhana
IDX.search: nace saci nandana bhakatajivana dhana sange nace priya nityananda advaita srinivasa ara nace haridasa vasughosa raya ramananda nityananda mukha heri bole pahu hari hari premaya dharani gadi jaya priya gadadhara asi prabhura bama pase basi ghana naraharimukha caya
IDX.verses:
nāce śacī⋅nandana, bhakatajīvana dhana,
saṅge nāce priya nityānanda
advaita śrīnivāsa, āra nāce haridāsa,
vāsughoṣa rāya rāmānanda

nityānanda mukha heri, bole pa̐hu hari hari,
premāya dharaṇī gaḍi jāya
priya gadādhara āsi, prabhura bāma pāśe basi,
ghana naraharimukha cāya

pa̐hu nāhi mele ā̐khi, kahe mora kāhā̐ sakhī,
kāhā̐ pāba rāi daraśana
kaha kaha narahari, āra sambarite nāri,
ihā bali bhela acetana

ekhani āchalu̐ tathā, ke more ānila ethā,
rāsa⋅rase nikuñja⋅bhavana
gela sukhasampada, ebe bhela vipada,
viṣādaye e dāsa locana
IDX.en_translation:
Śacī⋅nandana, the treasure and life of the devotees, dances. With Him dances dear Nityānanda. Advaita, Śrīnivāsa, and Haridāsa also dance. Vāsughoṣa and Rāya Rāmānanda likewise dance.

Seeing Nityānanda's face, the Lord chants "Hari! Hari!" and rolls on the earth in divine love. Dear Gadādhara comes and sits on the Lord's left side, frequently looking at Narahari's face.

The Lord does not open His eyes, saying, "Where is my sakhī? Where will I get the audience of Rādhā?" "Tell me, tell me, Narahari, I can no longer control myself," Saying this, He became unconscious.

"Just now I was there, who brought me here? In the forest grove with the mellows of the Rāsa dance." All happiness and opulence is gone, now there is only calamity— thus the servant Locana Dāsa laments.

IDX.first_line: nadiya nagare gora carita amrta
IDX.search: nadiya nagare gora carita amrta piya soka bhaya chada sthira kara cita anitya samsara bhai krsnamatra sara gora siksa mate krsna bhaja anivara
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
nadīyā⋅nagare gorā⋅carita⋅amṛta
piyā śoka⋅bhaya chāḍa, sthira kara cita

anitya saṁsāra, bhāi, kṛṣṇamātra sāra
gorā⋅śikṣā⋅mate kṛṣṇa bhaja anivāra

gorāra caraṇa dhari’ jei bhāgyavān
vraje rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa bhaje, sei mora prāṇa

‘rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa⋅gorācā̐da’, ‘nade⋅vṛndāvana’
eimātra kara sāra pā’be nityadhana

vidyā⋅buddhihīna dīna akiñcana chāra
karmajñāna⋅śūnya āmi śūnya⋅sadācāra

śrī⋅guru⋅vaiṣṇava more dilena upādhi
bhakti-hīne upādhi hoila ebe vyādhi

jatana kariyā sei vyādhi⋅nivāraṇe
śaraṇa loinu āmi vaiṣṇava⋅caraṇe

vaiṣṇavera padaraja mastake dhariyā
e ‘śoka⋅śātana’ gāya bhaktivinodiyā
IDX.en_translation:
In Nadiyā, drink the nectar of Gorā's pastimes. Drink it, give up sorrow and fear, and steady your mind.

This temporary world, O brother, only Kṛṣṇa is the essence. According to Gorā's teachings, worship Kṛṣṇa constantly.

Whoever is fortunate to grasp the lotus feet of Gorā and worships Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa in Vraja, that person is my life.

'Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa-Gorācānd' and 'Nadiyā-Vṛndāvana'- just make this the essence, and you will attain eternal wealth.

I am devoid of knowledge and intelligence, fallen, insignificant, and contemptible. I am without fruitive action or knowledge, and without good conduct.

My Guru and the Vaiṣṇavas gave me titles. But for one devoid of devotion, these titles have now become a disease.

Striving to remove that disease, I have taken shelter of the lotus feet of the Vaiṣṇavas.

Holding the dust of the lotus feet of the Vaiṣṇavas on my head, Bhaktivinoda sings this song, 'The Slayer of Sorrow'.

ānande karila gaṅgāsnāna
pāñā uparāga⋅chale, āpanāra manobale,

jagat ānandamaya, dekhi’ mane savismaya,
ṭhāre⋅ṭhore kahe haridāsa
‘tomāra aichana raṅga, mora mana parasanna,
dekhi⋅kichu kārye āche bhāsa’

ācārya⋅ratna, śrīvāsa, haila mane sukhollāsa,
jāi’ snāna kaila gaṅgājale
ānande vihvala mana, kare hari saŋkīrtana,
nānā dāna kaila manobale

ei mata bhakta⋅yati, jā̐’ra jei deśe sthiti,
tāhā̐ tāhā̐ pāñā manobale
nāce kare saṅkīrtana, ānande vihvala mana,
dāna kare grahaṇera chale

brāhmaṇa⋅sajjana⋅nārī, nānā dravye thāli bhari’,
āilā sabe jautuka loiyā
jena kā̐cāsonā⋅dyuti, dekhi’ bālakera mūrti,
āśīrvāda kare sukha pāñā

sāvitrī, gaurī, sarasvatī, śacī, rambhā, arundhatī,
āra jata deva⋅nārīgaṇa
nānā dravye pātra bhari’, brāhmaṇīra veśa dhari’,
āsi’ sabe karena daraśana

antarīkṣe devagaṇa, gandharva, siddha, cāraṇa,
stuti⋅nṛtya kare vādya⋅gīta
nartaka, vādaka, bhāṭa, navadvīpe jā’ra nāṭa,
sabe āsi’ nāce pāñā prīta

kebā āse kebā jāya, kebā nāce kebā gāya,
sambhālite nāre kāra bola
khaṇḍileka duḥkha-śoka, pramoda-pūrita loka,
miśra hailā ānande vihvala

ācāryaratna, śrīvāsa, jagannāthamiśra⋅pāśa,
āsi’ tā̐re kare sāvadhāna
karāila jāta-karma, je āchila vidhi⋅dharma,
tabe miśra kare nānā dāna

jautuka pāila jata, ghare vā āchila kata,
saba dhana vipre dila dāna
jata nartaka, gāyana, bhāṭa, akiñcana jana,
dhana diyā kailā sabāra māna

śrīvāsera brāhmaṇī, nāma tā̐’ra ‘mālinī’,
ācāryaratnera patnī⋅saṅge
sindura, haridrā, taila, khoi, kalā, nānā phala,
diyā pūje nārīgaṇa raṅge

advaita ācārya⋅bhāryā, jagat⋅pūjitā āryā,
nāma tā̐’ra ‘sītā ṭhākurāṇī’
ācāryera ājñā pāñā, gelā upahāra lañā,
dekhite bālaka⋅śiromaṇi

suvarṇera kaḍi⋅bouli, rajatamudrā⋅pāśuli,
suvarṇera aṅgada, kaṅkaṇa
du⋅bāhute divya śaṅkha, rajatera malabaṅka,
svarṇamudrāra nānā hāra-gaṇa

vyāghra-nakha hemajaḍi, kaṭi⋅paṭṭasūtra⋅ḍorī,
hasta⋅padera jata ābharaṇa
citravarṇa paṭṭaśāḍī, buni photo paṭṭapāḍī,
svarṇa⋅raupya⋅mudrā bahu-dhana

durvā, dhānya, gorocana, haridrā, kuṅkuma, candana,
maṅgala⋅dravya pātra bhariyā
vastra⋅gupta dolā caḍi’, saṅge lañā dāsī ceḍī,
vastrālaṅkāra peṭāri bhariyā

bhakṣya, bhojya, upahāra, saṅge loila bahu bhāra,
śacīgṛhe haila upanīta
dekhiyā bālaka⋅ṭhāma, sākṣāt gokula⋅kāna,
varṇamātra dekhi viparīta

sarva aṅga⋅sunirmāṇa, suvarṇa⋅pratimā⋅bhāna,
sarva aṅga⋅sulakṣaṇamaya
bālakera divya⋅jyoti, dekhi’ pāila bahuprīti,
vātsalyete dravila hṛdaya

durvā, dhānya dila śīrṣe, kaila bahu āśīṣe,
cirajīvi hoo dui bhāi
ḍākinī⋅śā̐khinī haite, śaṅkā upajila cite,
ḍare nāma thuila ‘nimāi’

putramātā⋅snāna⋅dine, dila vastra⋅vibhūṣaṇe,
putra⋅saha miśrere sammāni’
śacī⋅miśrera pūjā lañā, manete hariṣa hañā,
ghare āilā sītā-ṭhākurāṇī

aiche śacī⋅jagannātha, putra pāñā lakṣmī-nātha,
pūrṇa hoila sakala vāñchita
dhana⋅dhānye bhare ghara, lokamānya kalevara,
dine dine haya ānandita

miśra⋅vaiṣṇava, śānta, alampaṭa, śuddha, dānta,
dhanabhoge nāhi abhimāna
putrera prabhāve jata, dhana āsi’ mile tata,
viṣṇu-prīte dvije dena dāna

lagna gaṇi’ harṣamati, nīlāmbara cakravartī,
gupte kichu kahila miśrere
‘mahā-puruṣera cihna, lagne aṅge bhinna bhinna,
dekhi’,⋅ei tāribe saṁsāre’

aiche prabhu śacī-ghare, kṛpāya kaila avatāre,
jei ihā karaye śravaṇa
gaura-prabhu dayā-maya, tā̐’re hayena sadaya,
sei pāya tā̐hāra caraṇa

pāiyā mānuṣa⋅janma, je nā śune gaura-guṇa,
hena janma tā’ra vyartha haila
pāiyā amṛtadhunī, piye viṣagarta⋅pāni,
janmiyā se kene nāhi maila

śrī⋅caitanya⋅nityānanda, ācārya⋅advaitacandra,
svarūpa⋅rūpa⋅raghunāthadāsa
i̐hā sabāra śrī-caraṇa, śire vandi nijadhana,
janmalīlā gāila kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa
IDX.en_translation:
From the rising mountain of Nadīyā, the full moon of Lord Gaura⋅hari mercifully arose. The darkness of sin was destroyed, the three worlds rejoiced, and the whole world was filled with the sound of Lord Hari's Name.

At that time, Advaita Ācārya rose up in his own home and danced with a joyful heart. Taking Haridāsa Ṭhākura with him, he danced in the joy of congregational chanting, though no one knew why.

Seeing the eclipse, he smiled and quickly came to the bank of the Ganges to bathe. Taking the eclipse as a pretext, he gave various gifts to the brāhmaṇas with strong conviction.

Seeing the world filled with joy, Haridāsa Ṭhākura hinted: "Seeing your wonderful mood, my mind is very pleased. I see that some great event is about to manifest."

Śrīvāsa Paṇḍita was filled with great joy and went to bathe in the Ganges. His mind overwhelmed with joy, he performed congregational chanting and gave various donations.

In this way, devotees and renunciants wherever they resided received great strength. They danced and performed congregational chanting, their minds overwhelmed with joy, and gave donations on the pretext of the eclipse.

The women of the village, filling plates with various gifts, all came to see. Seeing the form of the child who glowed like raw gold, they gave their blessings, experiencing great happiness.

The celestial women, assuming the guise of village women, also came to see, filling their vessels with various articles.

In the sky, the demigods and celestial musicians danced and sang praises with their instruments. Dancers and musicians from all of Navadvīpa also came and danced with great joy.

Who was coming and who was going, who was dancing and who was singing—no one could keep track. Sorrow and lamentation were dispelled, the people were filled with delight, and Jagannātha Miśra became overwhelmed with joy.

Śrīvāsa Paṇḍita and other devotees came to Jagannātha Miśra and reminded him of his duties. He had the birth ceremony performed according to scriptural injunctions and then gave various donations.

All the gifts he received, and whatever he had in his house, he gave in charity to the priests. He honored all the dancers, singers, and poor people by giving them wealth.

Mālinī-devī, the wife of Śrīvāsa Paṇḍita, along with the wives of other devotees, joyfully offered worship with vermillion, turmeric, oil, and various fruits.

Sītā-Ṭhākurāṇī, the venerable wife of Advaita Ācārya, receiving her husband's order, went with gifts to see the crest-jewel of children.

She brought golden ornaments, silver coins, bangles, divine conch-shells, and various necklaces of gold coins.

She brought tiger-claw pendants set in gold, silk waist-threads, and all manner of ornaments for the hands and feet. She also brought multi-colored silk saris and much wealth in gold and silver coins.

Filling vessels with auspicious articles like grass, turmeric, saffron, and sandalwood, she rode in a covered palanquin with her attendants and baskets full of clothes and ornaments.

She arrived at the house of Mother Śacī with many gifts of food and clothing. Seeing the child's form, she saw directly the Lord of Gokula, only the complexion was different.

All His limbs were perfectly formed, appearing like a golden Deity and endowed with all auspicious marks. Seeing the child's divine effulgence, her heart melted with parental love.

She placed grass and paddy on His head and gave many blessings, saying: "May You two brothers live long." Fearing that witches might cast an evil eye, she named Him "Nimāi."

On the day of the mother and son's first bath, she gave them clothes and ornaments, honoring Jagannātha Miśra as well. Having accepted the hospitality of Mother Śacī and Jagannātha Miśra, Sītā-Ṭhākurāṇī returned home with a joyful heart.

Thus, Mother Śacī and Jagannātha Miśra, having received their Son, had all their desires fulfilled. Their house was filled with wealth and grain, and they became more joyful day by day.

Jagannātha Miśra was a peaceful and pure devotee; he had no pride in his wealth. By the influence of his Son, much wealth came to them, and he gave it all in charity to the priests for the pleasure of the Lord.

Calculating the astrological moment with joy, the grandfather of the Lord secretly told Jagannātha Miśra: "I see the marks of a great personality upon His limbs; He will surely deliver the entire world."

Thus the Lord mercifully made His advent in Mother Śacī's house. Whoever hears this narrative will receive the mercy of Lord Gaura and eventually attain His lotus feet.

Having attained a human birth, one who does not hear the glories of Lord Gaura makes such a birth useless. Having found a river of nectar, if one chooses to drink from a poisonous pit, why did they not simply die after taking birth?

I worship the lotus feet of Śrī Caitanya, Nityānanda, Advaita Ācārya, Svarūpa Dāmodara, Rūpa Gosvāmī, and Raghunātha Dāsa Gosvāmī. Kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa Kavirāja has thus sung the pastimes of the Lord's birth.

IDX.first_line: nadiyara ati punyavati pati
IDX.search: nadiyara ati punyavati pati vrataganera ki manera gati nija putre mana nahi anukhana bhane saci suta carita riti nisi sesa dekhi sayana upekhi tila adha nahi dhairaya badhe nana dravye thari bhari sari sari laiya cale dite nadiya cade
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
nadīyāra ati, puṇyavatī pati⋅
vratāgaṇera ki manera gati
nija⋅putre mana, nāhi anukhana,
bhaṇe śacī⋅suta⋅carita⋅rīti

niśi śeṣa dekhi’, śayana upekhi’,
tila ādha nāhi dhairaya bā̐dhe
nānā dravye thāri, bhari sāri sāri,
laiyā cale dite nadīyā⋅cā̐de

śacīra gṛhete, praveśite cite,
uthalaye kata kautukasindhu
dekhaye sakale, jananīra kole,
khele vasi’ gorā gokula⋅indu

juḍāya nayana, nārīgaṇa⋅prāṇa,
pā’yā kole kari pāsare dehā
kahe narahari, āhā mari mari,
kebā sirajila ehena lehā
IDX.en_translation:
What is the state of mind of the very virtuous and devoted wives of Nadīyā? Their minds are not with their own sons for even a moment, as they praise the character and pastimes of the Son of Śacī.

Seeing the end of the night, forsaking their beds, they cannot hold patience for even half a moment. Filling plates with various items, row after row, they set off to offer them to the Moon of Nadīyā.

As they enter Śacī-mātā's house, a sea of joy overflows in their hearts. Everyone sees, in the lap of His mother, Gorā, the Moon of Gokula, playing.

Their eyes are soothed, and holding Him in their laps, the women forget their own bodies. Narahari says, "Oh, wonderful! Oh, wonderful! Who has created such a loving sentiment?"

IDX.first_line: namah om visnu padaya
IDX.search: namah om visnu padaya acarya simha rupine sri srimad bhakti prajnana kesava iti namine sri sarasvatyabhipsitam sarvatha susthu paline sri sarasvatyabhinnaya patitoddhara karine
IDX.author: śrīmadbhaktivedānta trivikrama gosvāmī mahārāja
IDX.verses:
namaḥ oṁ viṣṇu⋅pādāya
ācārya⋅siṁha⋅rūpiṇe
śrī⋅śrīmad⋅bhakti⋅prajñāna⋅
keśava⋅iti⋅nāmine

śrī⋅sarasvatyabhīpsitaṁ
sarvathā suṣṭhu⋅pāline
śrī⋅sarasvatyabhinnāya
patitoddhāra⋅kāriṇe

vajrādapi kaṭhorāya
ca⋅apasiddhānta⋅nāśine
satyasyārthe nirbhīkāya
kusaṅga⋅parihāriṇe

atimartya⋅caritrāya
svāśritānāñca⋅pāline
jīva⋅duḥkhe sadārtāya
śrī⋅nāma⋅prema⋅dāyine

viṣṇu⋅pāda⋅prakāśāya
kṛṣṇa⋅kāmaika⋅cāriṇe
gaura⋅cintā⋅nimagnāya
śrī⋅guruṁ hṛdi⋅dhāriṇe

viśvaṁ viṣṇu⋅mayamiti
snigdha⋅darśana⋅śāline
namaste guru⋅devāya
kṛṣṇa⋅vaibhava⋅rūpiṇe

śrī⋅śrī⋅gauḍīya⋅vedānta⋅
samiteḥ sthāpakāya ca
śrī⋅śrī⋅māyāpura⋅dhāmnaḥ
sevā⋅samṛddhi⋅kāriṇe

navadvīpa⋅parikramā
jenaiva rakṣitā sadā
dīnaṁ prati dayālave
tasmai śrī⋅gurave namaḥ

dehi me tava śaktistu
dīneneyaṁ suyācitā
tava pāda⋅sarojebhyo
matirastu pradhāvitā
IDX.en_translation:
I offer my obeisances unto Viṣṇupāda Śrī Śrīmad Bhakti Prajñāna Keśava Gosvāmī, who is like a lion among the ācāryas.

He perfectly fulfills the desire of Śrī Sarasvatī in every way; he is non-different from Śrī Sarasvatī and is the deliverer of the fallen.

He is harder than a thunderbolt in destroying conclusions opposed to bhakti; he is fearless for the sake of truth and causes one to reject bad association.

His character is superhuman, and he protects those who take shelter of him; he is always distressed by the suffering of the jīvas and bestows love for the Holy Name.

He is the manifestation of Viṣṇupāda, and his only desire is to serve Kṛṣṇa; he is immersed in thoughts of Gaura and holds Śrī Guru in his heart.

He possesses affectionate vision, seeing the whole universe as pervaded by Viṣṇu; I offer obeisances to you, Gurudeva, who are the embodiment of Kṛṣṇa's opulence.

He is the founder of the Śrī Gauḍīya Vedānta Samiti and the one who brought about the prosperity of service in Śrī Māyāpura Dhāma.

He always protected the Navadvīpa Parikramā; I offer my obeisances to that Śrī Guru who is merciful to the fallen.

Please give me your strength; this is the earnest prayer of this fallen soul. May my mind run rapidly toward your lotus feet.

IDX.first_line: namamisvaram saccidananda rupam
IDX.search: namamisvaram saccidananda rupam lasat kundalam gokule bhrajamanam jasoda bhiyolukhaladdhavamanam paramrstamatyantatato drutya gopya rudantam muhurnetra yugmam mrjantam karambhoja yugmena satanka netram muhuhsvasa kampattri rekhanka kantha sthita graiva damodaram bhakti baddham
IDX.author: śrī satyavrata muni)
IDX.verses:
namāmīśvaraṁ saccidānanda⋅rūpaṁ
lasat⋅kuṇḍalaṁ gokule bhrājamānam
jaśodā⋅bhiyolūkhalāddhāvamānaṁ
parāmṛṣṭamatyantatato drutya gopyā

rudantaṁ muhurnetra⋅yugmaṁ mṛjantaṁ
karāmbhoja⋅yugmena sātaṅka⋅netram
muhuḥśvāsa⋅kampattri⋅rekhāṅka⋅kaṇṭha
sthita⋅graiva⋅dāmodaraṁ bhakti⋅baddham

itīdṛk sva⋅līlābhirānanda⋅kuṇḍe
sva⋅ghoṣaṁ nimajjantamākhyāpayantam
tadīyeśita-jñeṣu bhaktairjitattvaṁ
punaḥ prematastaṁ śatāvṛtti vande

varaṁ deva! mokṣaṁ na mokṣāvadhiṁ vā
na cānyaṁ vṛṇe‛haṁ vareśādapīha
idante vapurnātha! gopāla⋅bālaṁ
sadā me manasyāvirāstāṁ kima-nyaiḥ

idamte mukhāmbhojamavyakta⋅nīlai⋅
rvṛtaṁ kuntalaiḥ snigdha⋅raktaiśca gopyā
muhuścumbitaṁ bimba⋅raktādharaṁ me
manasyāvirāstāma-laṁ lakṣa⋅lābhaiḥ

namo deva! dāmodarānanta! viṣṇo!
prasīda prabho! duḥkha⋅jālāvdhi⋅magnam
kṛpādṛṣṭi⋅vṛṣṭyātidīnaṁ vatānu⋅
gṛhāṇeśa! māmajñamakṣyakṣi⋅dṛśyaḥ

kuverātmajau baddha⋅mūrtyaiva jadvat
tvayā mocitau bhakti⋅bhājau kṛtau ca
tathā prema⋅bhaktiṁ svakāṁ me prayaccha
na mokṣe graho me‛sti dāmodareha

namaste‛stu dāmne sphuraddīpti⋅dhāmne
tvadīyodarāyātha viśvasya dhāmne
namo rādhikāyai tvadīya⋅priyāyai
namo‛nanta⋅līlāya devāya tubhyam
IDX.en_translation:
I bow down to that Supreme Controller, whose form is eternal bliss and knowledge, whose ears are adorned with swinging earrings, and who is splendidly manifest in Gokula. He is fleeing from Mother Yaśodā due to fear, being pursued by her to the point of being caught.

He is crying again and again, rubbing His two eyes with His two lotus-like hands, His eyes filled with fear. His neck, marked with three lines, is trembling due to His frequent breaths, and the garland around His belly is tied by the devotion of Yaśodā.

To this Lord, who immerses His own people in pools of joy through such pastimes and who reveals that He is conquered by His devotees who possess knowledge of His supremacy, I offer my obeisances again and again with love.

O Lord! I do not pray for liberation, nor for its ultimate limit, nor any other boon from the bestowers of boons. O Lord! May Your form as a cowherd child always be manifest in my mind. What need is there for anything else?

O Lord! May Your lotus-like face—encircled by dark-blue, soft, curly hair and repeatedly kissed by Mother Yaśodā, with reddish bimba fruit-like lips—always be manifest in my mind. What is the use of millions of other gains?

O Lord! I offer my obeisances to You, O Dāmodara! O Ananta! O Viṣṇu! Be pleased, O Master! I am submerged in a network of miseries. O Lord! Please bestow Your merciful glance upon me, who am extremely poor and ignorant. Become visible before my eyes!

Just as the two sons of Kuvera, though bound in material forms, were liberated by You and made into devotees, similarly, please bestow upon me Your own loving devotion. I have no desire for liberation in this world, O Dāmodara!

My obeisances to the rope which is a brilliant abode! And to Your belly, which is the abode of the entire universe! My obeisances to Rādhikā, Your beloved! My obeisances to You, O Lord, whose pastimes are endless!

IDX.first_line: namas tri kala satyaya

jagannātha⋅sutāya ca
sa⋅bhṛtyāya sa⋅putrāya
sa⋅kalatrāya te namaḥ

vande śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅caitanyaṁ
bhaktānugraha⋅kātaram
jena kenāpi santuṣṭaṁ
bhakta⋅dattena śraddhayā

vande śrī kṛṣṇa⋅caitanyaṁ
bhagavantaṁ kṛpārṇavam
prema⋅bhakti⋅vitānārthaṁ
gauḍeṣv⋅avatatāra jaḥ

viśvambharāya gaurāya

śacī⋅putrāya mitrāya

śrīman navadvīpa⋅kiśora⋅candra
hā nātha viśvambhara nāgarendra
hā śrī śacī⋅nandana citta⋅caura
prasīda he viṣṇupriyeśa gaura

ānanda⋅līlā⋅maya⋅vigrahāya
hemābha⋅divya⋅cchavi⋅sundarāya
tasmai mahā⋅prema⋅rasa⋅pradāya
caitanya⋅candrāya namo namas te

vairāgya⋅vidyā⋅nija⋅bhakti⋅yoga⋅
śikṣārtham ekaḥ puruṣaḥ purāṇaḥ
śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅caitanya⋅śarīra⋅dhārī
kṛpāmbudhir jas tam ahaṁ prapadye

jayati jayati devaḥ śrī⋅śacī⋅garbha⋅janma
jayati jayati bhakti⋅prema⋅dānaika⋅dharma
jayati jayati meruspardhi⋅gaurāṅga⋅dhāmā
jayati jayati dhanyaḥ kṛṣṇa⋅caitanya⋅nāmā

saṁsāra⋅duḥkha⋅jaladhau patitasya
kāma⋅krodhādi⋅nakra⋅makaraiḥ kavalī⋅kṛtasya
durvāsanā⋅nigaḍitasya nirāśrayasya
caitanya⋅candra mama dehi padāvalambam

anarpita⋅carīṁ cirāt karuṇayāvatīrṇaḥ kalau
samarpayitum unnatojjvala⋅rasāṁ sva⋅bhakti⋅śriyam
hariḥ puraṭa⋅sundara⋅dyuti⋅kadamba⋅sandīpitaḥ
sadā hṛdaya⋅kandare sphuratu vaḥ śacī⋅nandanaḥ

rādhā kṛṣṇa⋅praṇaya⋅vikṛtir hlādinī śaktir asmāt
ekātmānāv api bhuvi purā deha⋅bhedaṁ gatau tau
caitanyākhyaṁ prakaṭam adhunā tad⋅dvayaṁ caikyam āptaṁ
rādhā⋅bhāva⋅dyuti⋅suvalitaṁ naumi kṛṣṇa⋅svarūpam

śrī⋅rādhāyāḥ praṇaya⋅mahimā kīdṛśo vānayaivā⋅
svādyo jenādbhuta⋅madhurimā kīdṛśo vā madīyaḥ
saukhyaṁ cāsyā mad⋅anubhavataḥ kīdṛśaṁ veti lobhāt
tad⋅bhāvāḍhyaḥ samajani śacī⋅garbha⋅sindhau harīnduḥ
IDX.en_translation:
I offer my obeisances unto You, the son of Jagannātha Miśra, who are the Absolute Truth in the past, present, and future. I offer my respectful obeisances unto You, along with Your servants, Your followers, and Your associates.

I worship Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya, who is very eager to bestow mercy upon His devotees. He is satisfied by anything whatsoever offered by a devotee with faith.

I worship Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, an ocean of mercy, who descended in Bengal for the purpose of bestowing loving devotion.

Repeated obeisances to the maintainer of the universe, the golden Lord, the Supreme Soul, the son of Śacī, and the husband of the goddess of fortune.

O beautiful young moon of Navadvīpa! O Lord Viśvambhara, best among men! O son of Śacī, thief of my thoughts! O Lord of Viṣṇupriyā, please be pleased!

Repeated obeisances to You, whose form is full of joyful pastimes, who are beautiful with a divine golden luster, and who bestow the great mellow of divine love.

I surrender to the one ancient Supreme Person, an ocean of mercy, who—for the purpose of teaching renunciation and His own devotional service—assumed the form of Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya.

All glories to the Lord who was born from the womb of Śacī! All glories to Him whose only principle is to give devotional love! All glories to His golden form that rivals Mount Meru! Blessed is the name of Kṛṣṇa Caitanya!

O Lord Caitanya! I have fallen into the ocean of worldly miseries, swallowed up by the crocodiles and sharks of lust, anger, and greed. I am chained by bad desires and am without shelter. Please give me the support of Your feet.

May the son of Śacī, Lord Hari—resplendent with a cluster of beautiful golden effulgence—mercifully descend in this age to bestow the opulence of His own devotion in the most elevated and brilliant mellow, which was never given before. May He always manifest in the cave of your heart.

Rādhā is the transformation of Kṛṣṇa's love, His internal pleasure potency. Although They are one in nature, They formerly appeared on earth in two separate bodies. Now those two have attained unity as Caitanya. I offer obeisances to that form of Kṛṣṇa, who is covered by the mood and luster of Rādhā.

Out of a desire to know the glory of Rādhā's love, the wonderful sweetness She experiences through Me, and the happiness She derives from My experience, the moon-like Lord Hari was born from the womb of Śacī.

IDX.first_line: namaste narasiŋhaya
IDX.search: namaste narasiŋhaya prahladahlada dayine hiranyakasipor vaksah sila tanka nakhalaye ito nrsiŋhah parato nrsiŋho jato jato jami tato nrsiŋhah bahir nrsiŋho hrdaye nrsiŋho nrsiŋhamadim saranam prapadye
