IDX.verses:
namaste narasiŋhāya
prahlādāhlāda⋅dāyine
hiraṇyakaśipor⋅vakṣaḥ
śilā⋅ṭaṅka⋅nakhālaye

ito nṛsiŋhaḥ parato nṛsiŋho
jato jato jāmi tato nṛsiŋhaḥ
bahir⋅nṛsiŋho hṛdaye nṛsiŋho
nṛsiŋhamādiṁ śaraṇaṁ prapadye

tava kara⋅kamala⋅vare nakhamadbhuta⋅śṛṅgaṁ
dalita⋅hiraṇyakaśipu⋅tanu⋅bhṛṅgam
keśava! dhṛta⋅narahari⋅rūpa! jaya jagadīśa! hare

śrī⋅nṛsiŋha, jaya nṛsiŋha, jaya jaya nṛsiŋha
prahlādeśa jaya padmā⋅mukha⋅padma⋅bhṛṅga

vāgīśā jasya vadane
lakṣmir jasya ca vakṣasi
jasyāste hṛdaye samvit
taṁ nṛsiŋhamahaṁ bhaje

prahlāda⋅hṛdayāhlādaṁ
bhaktāvidyā⋅vidāraṇam
śaradindu⋅ruciṁ vande
pārīndra⋅vadanaṁ harim

ugro‛pyanugra evāyaṁ
svabhaktānāṁ nṛkeśarī
keśarīva svapotānām
anyeṣāṁ ugra⋅vikramaḥ
IDX.en_translation:
I offer my obeisances to Lord Narasimha, who gives joy to Prahlada Maharaja and whose nails are like chisels on the stonelike chest of the demon Hiranyakasipu.

Lord Nrsimhadeva is here, and He is also there on the opposite side. Wherever I go, there I see Lord Nrsimhadeva. He is outside and within my heart. Therefore I take shelter of Lord Nrsimhadeva, the original Supreme Personality of Godhead.

O Kesava! O Lord of the universe! O Lord Hari, who have assumed the form of half-man, half-lion! All glories to You! Just as one can easily crush a wasp between one's fingernails, so in the same way the body of the wasp-like demon Hiranyakasipu has been ripped apart by the wonderful pointed nails on Your beautiful lotus hands.

All glories to Nrsimhadeva! All glories to Nrsimhadeva! All glories to Nrsimhadeva! All glories to the Lord of Prahlada, the bee who sits on the lotus face of the Goddess of Fortune.

I worship Lord Nrsimha, within whose mouth resides the Goddess of Learning, on whose chest resides the Goddess of Fortune, and within whose heart resides the Divine Will.

I offer my obeisances to Lord Hari, who has the face of a lion, whose effulgence is like that of the autumn moon, who destroys the ignorance of His devotees, and who gives pleasure to the heart of Prahlada.

Although very ferocious, the lioness is very kind to her cubs. Similarly, although very ferocious to non-devotees like Hiranyakasipu, Lord Nrsimhadeva is very soft and kind to devotees like Prahlada Maharaja.

IDX.first_line: namo namo tulasi maharani
IDX.search: namo namo tulasi maharani vrnde maharani namo namah namore namore maiya namo narayani jako darase parase aghanasoi mahima veda purane bakhani
IDX.verses:
namo namo tulasī mahārāṇī
vṛnde mahārāṇī namo namaḥ
namore namore mā̐iyā namo nārāyaṇī

jāko daraśe paraśe aghanāśoi
mahimā veda⋅purāṇe bākhāni

jāko patra, mañjarī komala
śrīpati⋅caraṇa kamale lapaṭāni

dhanya tulasī, pūraṇa tapa kiye
śrī⋅śālagrāma⋅mahāpāṭarāṇī

dhūpa dīpa naivedya ārati
phulana kiye varaṣā varaṣāṇi

chāppānna bhoga chatriśa vyañjana
vinā tulasī prabhu eka nāhi māni

śiva śuka nārada āura brahmādika
cu̐ḍata phirata mahāmuni jñānī

candraśekhara mā̐iyā terā jaśa gāoye
bhakati dāna dijīye mahārāṇī
IDX.en_translation:
Obeisances, obeisances to Tulasī Mahārāṇī! O Queen of Vṛndāvana, obeisances, obeisances. O Mother! O Nārāyaṇī! Obeisances unto you.

By whose sight and touch all sins are destroyed, and whose glories are described in the Vedas and Purāṇas.

Whose leaves and tender flower buds embrace the lotus feet of the Lord of Fortune.

Blessed Tulasī, you have performed perfect penance and are the great chief queen of Lord Śālagrāma.

Incense, lamps, food offerings, and ārati are performed, and flowers are showered upon you.

Without Tulasī, the Lord does not accept any of the many types of food offerings and preparations.

Lord Śiva, Śukadeva Gosvāmī, Nārada Muni, Lord Brahmā, and all the great sages and wise persons are searching for you.

The poet Candraśekhara sings your glories, O Mother. O Queen, please bestow upon me the gift of devotion.

IDX.first_line: nanda nandana candra candana gandha nindiha anga
IDX.search: nanda nandana candra candana gandha nindiha anga jalada sundara kambu kandhara nindi sindhura anga prema akulagopa kula kulaja kamini kanta kusuma ranjana manju vanjula kunja mandire santa
IDX.author: govinda dāsa
IDX.verses: nanda⋅nandana candra⋅candana gandha nindiha aṅga jalada sundara⋅kambu⋅kandhara nindi sindhura aṅga prema ākulagopa kula kulajā kāminī kānta kusuma rañjana⋅mañju⋅vañjula kuñja⋅mandire santa gaṇḍa⋅maṇḍala balita kuṇḍala uḍa cūḍa śikhaṇḍa keli⋅tāṇḍabatāla⋅paṇḍita bāhu⋅daṇḍita⋅daṇḍa kañja⋅locana kaluṣa⋅mocana śravaṇa⋅rocana bhāṣa amala komalacaraṇa⋅kiśalaya nilaya govinda⋅dāsa
IDX.en_translation:
The body of the Son of Nanda surpasses the fragrance of moon-like sandalwood paste. His beautiful neck is like a conch-shell, and His body excels the beauty of a fresh cloud or the color of a blue jewel.

He is the Beloved of the gopīs of Vraja, who are restless with divine love. He is peaceful in the beautiful forest grove, which is delightful with flowers and lovely cane thickets.

His cheeks are adorned with swaying earrings, and a peacock feather flies from His topknot. His graceful arms are skilled in the rhythm of His playful dancing.

He has lotus-like eyes and He removes all sins; His speech is pleasing to the ears. Govinda Dāsa takes refuge at His pure, tender, and sprout-like lotus feet.

IDX.first_line: nandadulala ghanasyama nandadulala ghanasyama
IDX.search: nandadulala ghanasyama nandadulala ghanasyama jaya giridhari mukunda murari venudhara sundara syama
IDX.verses:
nandadulāla ghanaśyāma, nandadulāla ghanaśyāma

jaya giridhārī, mukunda⋅murārī,
veṇudhara sundara śyāma

navanīta taskara, gopa⋅veśadhara,
nava naṭavara ghanaśyāma

abhinava jaladhara, rūpa⋅manohara,
śrī⋅rādhā⋅vallabha śyāma
IDX.en_translation:
Nandadulāla, Ghanaśyāma! Nandadulāla, Ghanaśyāma!

All glories to Giridhārī, Mukunda, Murāri, the beautiful Śyāma who holds a flute.

The butter thief, dressed as a cowherd, the youthful, supreme dancer, Ghanaśyāma.

Like a fresh new cloud, with an enchanting form, Śyāma, the beloved of Śrī Rādhā.

IDX.first_line: narada muni bajaya vina
IDX.search: narada muni bajaya vina radhikaramana name nama amani udita haya bhakata gita same amiya dhara varise ghana sravana yugale giya bhakata jana saghane nace bhariya apana hiya
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
nārada muni, bājāya vīṇā,
rādhikāramaṇa⋅nāme
nāma amani, udita haya,
bhakata⋅gīta sāme

amiya⋅dhārā, variṣe ghana,
śravaṇa⋅yugale giyā
bhakata⋅jana, saghane nāce,
bhariyā āpana hiyā

mādhurī⋅pura, āsava paśi’,
mātāya jagata⋅jane
keha vā kā̐de, keha vā nāce,
keha māte mane mane

pañcavadana, nārade dhari’,
premera saghana rola
kamalāsana, nāciyā bale,
‘bola bola haribola’

sahasrānana, paramasukhe,
‘hari hari’ bali’ gāya
nāma⋅prabhāve, mātila viśva,
nāma⋅rasa sabe pāya

śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅nāma, rasane sphuri’,
pūrā’la āmāra āśa
śrī⋅rūpa⋅pade, jācaye ihā,
bhakativinoda⋅dāsa
IDX.en_translation:
Nārada Muni plays his vīṇā, chanting the name of the delight of Rādhikā. The Holy Name immediately arises in the melody of the devotees' songs.

A thick stream of nectar rains down, entering the ears. The devotees dance intensely, their hearts filled with joy.

The abundance of sweetness enters like wine, intoxicating the people of the world. Some cry, some dance, and some become intoxicated within their minds.

Lord Śiva, following Nārada, roars loudly with divine love. Lord Brahmā, dancing, says, "Chant, chant Haribol!"

Lord Ananta, in supreme bliss, sings "Hari Hari!" By the influence of the Holy Name, the universe becomes intoxicated, and everyone receives the taste of that Name.

May the name of Śrī Kṛṣṇa appear on my tongue and fulfill my hope. Bhaktivinoda Dāsa prays for this at the feet of Śrī Rūpa.

IDX.first_line: nare narottama dhanya granthakara agraganya
IDX.search: nare narottama dhanya granthakara agraganya aganya punyera ekadhara sadhane sadhaka sestha dayate ati garistha ista prati bhakti camatkara candrika pancama sara tina mani saratsara gurusisya samvada patala tribhuvane anupama prarthana granthera nama hata pattana madhura kevala
IDX.verses:
nare narottama dhanya, granthākāra agragaṇya,
agaṇya puṇyera ekādhāra
sādhane sādhaka⋅śeṣṭha, dayāte ati gariṣṭha
iṣṭa prati bhakti camatkāra

candrikā pañcama sāra, tina maṇi sārātsāra,
guruśiṣya saṁvāda⋅paṭala
tribhuvane anupama, ‘prārthanā’ granthera nāma,
‘hāṭa⋅pattana’ madhura kevala

racilā asaŋkhya pada, haiyā bhāve gadagada,
kavitvera sampada se saba
je śune jebā paḍe, jebā tāhā gāna kare,
sei jāne padera gaurava

sadā sādhu⋅mukhe śuni’, śrī⋅cetanya āsi puni,
narottama⋅rūpe janamilā
narottama guṇādhāra, vallabhe karahe pāra,
jalete bhāsāo punaḥ śilā
IDX.en_translation:
Among men, Śrīla Narottama Dāsa Ṭhākura is glorious. He is the foremost author and a unique reservoir of countless virtues. In spiritual practice, he is the best of all practitioners, and in compassion, he is supreme. His devotion to his chosen Deity is wonderful.

His book 'Prema-bhakti-candrikā' is the fifth essence, and his 'Prārthanā' is the essence of essences of the three jewels—a conversation between Guru and disciple. In the three worlds, his 'Prārthanā' is incomparable, and 'Hāṭa-pattana' is exceedingly sweet.

He composed countless verses while overwhelmed with ecstatic emotion; all of them are the wealth of poetry. Whoever hears, reads, or sings them knows the true glory of these verses.

I always hear from the mouths of the saintly that Lord Caitanya came again and was born in the form of Narottama. O Narottama, abode of all good qualities! O Master, please deliver me and make the stone float on water once again.

IDX.first_line: nastikata apadharma judila samsara
IDX.search: nastikata apadharma judila samsara krsnapuja krsnabhakti nahi kotha ara dekhiya advaita prabhu visadita haila kemane taribe jiva bhavite lagila
IDX.author: śrīla locana⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
nāstikatā apadharma juḍila saṁsāra
kṛṣṇa-pūjā kṛṣṇa-bhakti nāhi kothā āra

dekhiyā advaita⋅prabhu viṣādita hailā
kemane taribe jīva bhāvite lāgilā

netra buji’ tulasī pradāni’ viṣṇupade
huṅkāri’ dilena lampha ācārya āhlāde

jitilu̐ jitilu̐, mukhe bale bāra bāra
jīva⋅nistārite habe gaura⋅avatāra

e kathā śuniyā nāce sādhu haridāsa
locana bale khasila jīvera moha⋅pāśa
IDX.en_translation:
Atheism and false religion pervaded the entire world. The worship of Lord Kṛṣṇa and devotion to Him were nowhere to be found.

Seeing this, Lord Advaita became deeply distressed and began to contemplate how all living entities could be delivered.

Closing his eyes and offering Tulasī leaves at the lotus feet of Lord Viṣṇu, He jumped in joy with a great roar.

"I have won! I have won!" he said repeatedly. "To deliver the living entities, Lord Gaura must now incarnate."

Hearing these words, the saintly Haridāsa Ṭhākura danced in joy. Locana Dāsa says: "The noose of illusion around the souls was thus cut."

IDX.first_line: nava gaura varam nava puspa saram
IDX.search: nava gaura varam nava puspa saram nava bhava dharam nava lasya param nava hasya karam nava hema varam pranamami saci suta gaura varam nava prema yutam nava nita sucam nava vesa krtam nava prema rasam navadha vilasat subha prema mayam pranamami saci suta gaura varam
IDX.author: śrīla sārvabhauma bhaṭṭācārya
IDX.verses:
nava⋅gaura⋅varaṁ nava⋅puṣpa⋅śaraṁ
nava⋅bhāva⋅dharaṁ nava⋅lāsya⋅param
nava⋅hāsya⋅karaṁ nava⋅hema⋅varaṁ
praṇamāmi śacī⋅suta⋅gaura⋅varam 

nava⋅prema⋅yutaṁ nava⋅nīta⋅śucaṁ
nava⋅veśa⋅kṛtaṁ nava⋅prema⋅rasam
navadhā vilasat śubha⋅prema⋅mayaṁ
praṇamāmi śacī⋅suta⋅gaura⋅varam 

hari⋅bhakti⋅paraṁ hari⋅nāma⋅dharaṁ
kara⋅japya⋅karaṁ hari⋅nāma⋅param
nayane satataṁ praṇayāśru⋅dharaṁ
praṇamāmi śacī⋅suta⋅gaura⋅varam 

satataṁ janatā⋅bhava⋅tāpa⋅haraṁ
paramārtha⋅parāyaṇa⋅loka⋅gatim
nava⋅leha⋅karaṁ jagat⋅tāpa⋅haraṁ
praṇamāmi śacī⋅suta⋅gaura⋅varam 

nija⋅bhakti⋅karaṁ priya⋅cārutaraṁ
naṭa⋅nartana⋅nāgara⋅rāja⋅kulam
kula⋅kāmini⋅mānasa⋅lāsya⋅karaṁ
praṇamāmi śacī⋅suta⋅gaura⋅varam 

karatāla⋅valaṁ kala⋅kaṇṭha⋅ravaṁ
mṛdu⋅vādya⋅suvīṇikayā madhuram
nija⋅bhakti⋅guṇāvṛta⋅nāṭya⋅karaṁ
praṇamāmi śacī⋅suta⋅gaura⋅varam 

juga⋅dharma⋅yutaṁ punar nanda⋅sutaṁ
dharaṇī⋅sucitraṁ bhava⋅bhāvocitam
tanu⋅dhyāna⋅citaṁ nija⋅vāsa⋅yutam
praṇamāmi śacī⋅suta⋅gaura⋅varam 

aruṇaṁ nayanaṁ caraṇaṁ vasanaṁ
vadane skhalitaṁ svaka⋅nāma⋅dharam
kurute su⋅rasaṁ jagataḥ jīvanaṁ
praṇamāmi śacī⋅suta⋅gaura⋅varam
IDX.en_translation:
I offer my obeisances to the excellent Golden Lord, the son of Śacī, who is eternally fresh and golden, whose arrows are fresh flowers, who embodies new emotions, and who is engrossed in new playful dancing, bringing forth new laughter.

I offer my obeisances to the son of Śacī, who is filled with fresh love, whose purity is like fresh butter, and who is adorned with new attire—the very essence of love, shining in nine ways with auspicious, loving emotions.

I offer my obeisances to the son of Śacī, who is devoted to the Lord and bears His name, whose hands are engaged in chanting on beads, and whose eyes always stream with tears of love.

I offer my obeisances to the son of Śacī, who constantly removes the miseries of material existence for the people, who is the refuge for those devoted to the ultimate spiritual goal, and who bestows new attachments and removes the world's afflictions.

I offer my obeisances to the son of Śacī, who bestows His own devotion, who is supremely charming, and who is the king among expert performers, captivating the minds of chaste women with His playful dancing.

I offer my obeisances to the son of Śacī, whose hands hold cymbals, whose voice is sweet like a cuckoo, and who sweetly plays the vīṇā, performing dances imbued with the qualities of His own devotion.

I offer my obeisances to the son of Śacī, who embodies the religious principles for this age, who is the son of Nanda Himself, whose beautiful form on earth is appropriate for the emotions of love, and whose mind is absorbed in meditation.

I offer my obeisances to the son of Śacī, whose eyes are reddish, whose feet are adorned, and whose garments are beautiful—the Lord whose own name is uttered falteringly from His mouth, bestowing supreme nectar to the world.

IDX.first_line: nava ghana kanana sobhana kunja
IDX.search: nava ghana kanana sobhana kunja vikasita kusuma madhukara gunja nava nava pallave sobhita dala sari suka pika gaoye rasala
IDX.author: govinda dāsa
IDX.verses:
nava ghana kānana śobhana kuñja
vikaśita kusuma madhukara guñja

nava nava pallave śobhita ḍāla
sāri śuka pika gāoye rasāla

tahi̐ vani aparūpa ratana hiṇḍora
tā para vaiṭhaka jugala kiśora

vraja ramaṇīgaṇa deota jhakora
girata jāni dhanī karatahi̐ kora

kata kata upajila rasa parasaṅga
govinda dāsa tahi dekhata raṅga
IDX.en_translation:
The dense and beautiful groves are new. The flowers are blossoming and the humming of bees is heard.

The branches are adorned with fresh new sprouts. Parrots and cuckoos sing melodiously.

There, an incomparable jeweled swing is found. Upon it are seated the youthful Divine Couple.

The young women of Vraja give the swing a push. Thinking She might fall, the beautiful Rādhā embraces Her Lord.

Many kinds of divine mellows arise from Their association. Govinda Dāsa witnesses that joyful play.

IDX.first_line: navadvipe suni siŋhanada
IDX.search: navadvipe suni siŋhanada sajala vaisnavagana kari hari saŋkirtana mudhamati ganila pramada gaura candra maharathi nityananda senapati advaita juddhera aguyana premadora phasa kari vandhila aneka airi nirantara garje harinama
IDX.author: kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa
IDX.verses:
navadvīpe śuni siŋhanāda
sājala vaiṣṇavagaṇa, kari hari saŋkīrtana,
muḍhamati gaṇila pramāda

gaura⋅candra mahārathī, nityānanda senāpati,
advaita juddhera āguyāna
premaḍora phā̐sa kari, vāndhila aneka airi,
nirantara garje harināma

śrī⋅caitanya kare raṇa, kaligaje ārohaṇa,
pāṣaṇḍadalana vīravānā
kalijīva tarāite, āila prabhu avanīte,
caudige cāpiyā dila thānā

uttama adhama jana, sabe pāila prema⋅dhana,
nitāi⋅caitanya kṛpāleśe
samukhe śamana dekhi, kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa baḍa dukhī,
nā pāiyā premera uddeśe
IDX.en_translation:
In Navadvīpa, a lion's roar is heard! The devotees are arrayed, performing the congregational chanting of Lord Hari's Names. The foolish-minded consider this to be a calamity.

Lord Gaura⋅candra is the great charioteer, and Lord Nityānanda is the commander-in-chief. Advaita Ācārya is the leader of the battle. With the rope of divine love, they bound many enemies, and the Holy Name of Lord Hari roars continuously.

Lord Caitanya performs battle, riding the elephant of the Age of Kali. He is the hero who crushes the atheists. To deliver the souls of this age, the Lord came to this world and established His outposts in all directions.

Everyone, whether high or low, received the wealth of divine love by a drop of mercy from Lord Nitāi and Lord Caitanya. Seeing the God of Death before him, Kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa is very unhappy as he has not yet attained the goal of divine love.

IDX.first_line: navanirada nindita kantidharam
IDX.search: navanirada nindita kantidharam rasanagara nagarabhupa varam subha vankima caru sikhandasikham bhaja krsnanidhim vrajarajasutam bhru visankita vankima sakradhanum mukhacandra vinindita koti vidhum mrdumanda suhasya subhasya yutam bhaja krsnanidhim vrajarajasutam
IDX.verses:
navanīrada⋅nindita⋅kāntidharaṁ
rasanāgara⋅nāgarabhūpa⋅varam
śubha⋅vaṅkima⋅cāru⋅śikhaṇḍaśikhaṁ
bhaja kṛṣṇa-nidhiṁ vraja-rāja-sutam

bhru⋅viśaṅkita⋅vaṅkima⋅śakradhanuṁ
mukhacandra⋅vinindita⋅koṭi⋅vidhum
mṛdumanda⋅suhāsya⋅subhāṣya⋅yutaṁ

vrajavāsi⋅manohara⋅veśakaram

śruti⋅dolita⋅mākara⋅kuṇḍalakam
kaṭi⋅veṣṭita⋅pītapaṭaṁ sudhaṭaṁ
bhaja kṛṣṇa-nidhiṁ vraja-rāja-sutam

kala⋅nūpura⋅rājita⋅cāru⋅padaṁ
maṇi⋅rañjita⋅gañjita⋅bhṛṅgamadam
dhvaja⋅vajra⋅jhaṣāṅkita⋅pādayugaṁ
bhaja kṛṣṇa-nidhiṁ vraja-rāja-sutam

bhṛśa⋅candana⋅carcita⋅cāru⋅tanuṁ
maṇi⋅kaustubha⋅garhita⋅bhānutanum
vraja⋅vāla⋅śiromaṇi⋅rūpa⋅dhṛtaṁ
bhaja kṛṣṇa-nidhiṁ vraja-rāja-sutam

suravṛnda⋅suvandya⋅mukunda⋅hariṁ
suranātha⋅śiromaṇi⋅sarvagurum
giridhāri⋅murāri⋅purāri⋅paraṁ
bhaja kṛṣṇa-nidhiṁ vraja-rāja-sutam

vṛṣabhānusutā⋅vara⋅keli⋅paraṁ
rasarāja⋅śiromaṇi⋅veśadharam
jagadīśvaramīśvaramīḍya-varaṁ
bhaja kṛṣṇa-nidhiṁ vraja-rāja-sutam
IDX.en_translation:
Worship Kṛṣṇa, the treasure and son of Vraja⋅rāja, whose bodily luster shames a fresh cloud, who is the best of clever kings of divine mellows, and whose auspicious, graceful, and beautiful peacock feather crest shines.

Worship Kṛṣṇa, the treasure and son of Vraja⋅rāja, whose arched eyebrows mock the rainbow, whose moon-like face condemns millions of moons, and who is endowed with a gentle, sweet smile and pleasing speech.

Worship Kṛṣṇa, the treasure and son of Vraja⋅rāja, whose beautiful limbs make Cupid himself tremble, who captivates the minds of the residents of Vraja with His attire, and whose eyes intensely shame the blue lotuses.

Worship Kṛṣṇa, the treasure and son of Vraja⋅rāja, whose forehead is adorned with locks of hair, whose shark-shaped earrings swing by His ears, and whose waist is encircled by a well-fitting yellow garment.

Worship Kṛṣṇa, the treasure and son of Vraja⋅rāja, whose beautiful feet are graced with tinkling ankle bells, whose jewel-decorated feet mock the pride of bees, and whose feet bear the marks of a flag, a thunderbolt, and a fish.

Worship Kṛṣṇa, the treasure and son of Vraja⋅rāja, whose beautiful body is smeared with sandalwood, whose Kaustubha gem shames the radiance of the sun, and who has assumed the form of the crest-jewel of the youth of Vraja.

Worship Kṛṣṇa, the treasure and son of Vraja⋅rāja, who is worshiped by hosts of demigods, and who is the crest-jewel of their lords and the guru of all—the supreme Giridhārī, Murāri, and Purāri.

Worship Kṛṣṇa, the treasure and son of Vraja⋅rāja, who is absorbed in excellent pastimes with the daughter of Vṛṣabhānu, who assumes the form of the crest-jewel of the King of Mellows, the Lord of the universe, the supreme controller, and the most worshipable one.

IDX.first_line: nayikara siromani vraje radha thakurani
IDX.search: nayikara siromani vraje radha thakurani pancavidha sakhigana tara sakhinityasakhi ara pranasakhi atahpara priyasakhiei haila cara pancama paramaprestha sakhigana madhye srestha bali saba suna vivarana kusumika vindhavati dhanisthadi vrajasati sakhigana madhyete ganana
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
nāyikāra śiromaṇi, vraje rādhā ṭhākurāṇī,
pañcavidha sakhī-gaṇa tā̐ra
‘sakhī’,’nityasakhī’ āra, ‘prāṇa-sakhī’ ataḥpara,
‘priyasakhī’,ei haila cāra

pañcama ‘parama-preṣṭha’, sakhī-gaṇa madhye śreṣṭha,
bali saba, śuna vivaraṇa
kusumikā, vindhāvatī, dhaniṣṭhādi vraja-satī,
‘sakhī’gaṇa⋅madhyete gaṇana

śrī-rūpa, rati, kasturī, śrī-guṇa, maṇi-mañjarī,
prabhṛti rādhikā⋅‘nityasakhī’
‘prāṇa-sakhī’ bahu tā̐ra, vāsantī nāyikā āra,
pradhānā tā̐hāra śaśimukhī

kuraṅgākṣī, mañjukeśī, sumadhyā, madanālasī,
kamalā, mādhurī, kāmalatā
kandarpa-sundarī āra, mādhavī, mālatī āra,
śaśikalā rādhā⋅sevāratā

lalitā, viśākhā, citrā, tuṅgavidyā, campakalatā,
indulekhā, raṅgadevī satī
sudevīti aṣṭajana, ‘paramapreṣṭha⋅sakhī’gaṇa,
rādhā⋅kṛṣṇe seve ekamati
IDX.en_translation:
The crest-jewel of heroines in Vraja is Rādhā. She has five types of companions: the Sakhī, the Nityasakhī, the Prāṇasakhī, and the Priyasakhī.

The fifth type is the most beloved companion, who is the best among them all. I will tell all; listen to the details. Kusumikā, Vindhāvatī, Dhaniṣṭhā, and other chaste women of Vraja are counted among the group of companions.

Śrī Rūpa, Rati, Kasturī, Śrī Guṇa, Maṇi Mañjarī, and others are Rādhā's eternal companions. She has many dear friends, and their chief is moon-faced Śaśimukhī.

Kuraṅgākṣī, Mañjukeśī, Sumadhyā, Madanalasī, Kamalā, Mādhurī, Kāmalatā, Kandarpasundarī, Mādhavī, Mālatī, and Śaśikalā are all engaged in the service of Rādhā.

Lalitā, Viśākhā, Citrā, Tuṅgavidyā, Campakalatā, Indulekhā, Raṅgadevī, and Sudevī—these eight are the most beloved companions, who serve Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa with single-minded devotion.

IDX.first_line: nija pati bhuja danda cchatrabhavam prapadya

nija⋅nikaṭa⋅nivāsaṁ dehi govardhana! tvam

priyasakhamiha kaṇṭhe narmaṇāliṅgya guptaḥ

pratipadamanukālaṁ hanta samvardhayan gāḥ
tri-jagati nija-gotraṁ sārthakaṁ khyāpayan me
nija⋅nikaṭa⋅nivāsaṁ dehi govardhana! tvam

surapati⋅kṛta⋅dīrgha⋅drohato goṣṭha⋅rakṣāṁ
tava navagṛha⋅rūpasyāntare kurvataiva
agha⋅baka⋅ripuṇoccairdattamāna! drutaṁ me
nija⋅nikaṭa⋅nivāsaṁ dehi govardhana! tvam

giri-nṛpa! hari⋅dāsa⋅śreṇi⋅varyati nāmā⋅
mṛtamidamuditaṁ śrī⋅rādhikā⋅vaktracandrāt
vrajanava⋅tilakatve kḷpta vedaiḥ sphuṭaṁ me
nija⋅nikaṭa⋅nivāsaṁ dehi govardhana! tvam

nijajana⋅yuta⋅rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa⋅maitrī⋅rasākta⋅
vrajanara⋅paśu⋅pakṣi⋅vrāta⋅saukhyaikadātaḥ
agaṇita⋅karuṇatvānmāmurīkṛtya tāntaṁ
nija⋅nikaṭa⋅nivāsaṁ dehi govardhana! tvam

nirupadhi⋅karuṇena śrī⋅śacī⋅nandanena
tvayi kapaṭi⋅śaṭho‛pi tvatpriyeṇārpito‛smi
iti khalu mama jogyāyogyatāṁ tām agṛhṇan
nija⋅nikaṭa⋅nivāsaṁ dehi govardhana! tvam

rasada⋅daśakamasya śrīla⋅govardhanasya
kṣitidhara⋅kulabharturyaḥ prayatnādadhīte
sa sapadi sukhade‛smin vāsamāsādya sākṣā⋅
cchubhada⋅yugalasevāratnamāpnoti tūrṇam
IDX.en_translation:
O Govardhana! You assumed the form of an umbrella held by the arm of my Lord, and thus crushed the arrogant pride of the fierce king of the gods. O peerless, vast King of Mountains! Please grant me residence near you.

O Govardhana! In your every cave, the youthful Divine Couple joyfully perform Their passionate loving pastimes. Knowing this and aspiring to serve Them, please grant me residence near you.

O Govardhana! With great delight, You allow my Beloved to play with Balarāma and His friends amidst Your incomparable jeweled altars, gem-studded thrones, trees, waterfalls, caves, peaks, and valleys. Please grant me residence near you.

O Govardhana! You make manifest the beautiful and fragrant dark altar, which is a witness to the tax-collecting pastime of the ocean of divine mellows—the youthful Divine Couple—thus bringing joy to the most discerning devotees. Please grant me residence near you.

O Govardhana! You secretly embrace Rādhā-kuṇḍa—the unique and most beloved friend of Hari—and thus confidentially witness the pastimes of the divine youthful couple there. Please grant me residence near you.

O Govardhana! You constantly nourish the cows with your fresh grass, pure water, and the shade of your trees at every step, thus making your name meaningful throughout the three worlds. Please grant me residence near you.

O Govardhana! By providing refuge in your form as a new home, you protected the cowherd village from the prolonged wrath of Indra, and thus received great honor from Kṛṣṇa, the enemy of Agha and Baka. Please grant me residence near you.

O King of Mountains! The nectar of your name—"the best among the servants of Hari"—emanated from the moon-like face of Śrī Rādhā, and the Vedas clearly declare you to be the ornament of Vraja. Please grant me residence near you.

O Govardhana! You are the sole bestower of happiness upon the cowherd men, animals, and birds of Vraja, who are saturated with the mellow of friendship with Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa and Their associates. Due to your boundless compassion, please accept me and grant me residence near you.

O Govardhana! By the causelessly merciful son of Śacī, who is most dear to you, I—who am deceitful and wicked—have been offered to you. Therefore, disregarding my fitness or unfitness, please grant me residence near you.

One who diligently recites these ten verses—which bestow divine mellows in praise of Śrīla Govardhana, the chief of all mountains—will quickly attain residence in this blissful place and directly achieve the jewel of service to the auspicious Divine Couple.

IDX.first_line: nikhila sruti mauli ratna mala
IDX.search: nikhila sruti mauli ratna mala dyuti nirajita pada pankajanta ayi mukta kulair upasyamanam paritas tvam hari nama samsrayami jaya namadheya muni vrnda geya he jana ranjanaya param aksarakrte tvam anadarad api manag udiritam nikhilogra tapa patalim vilumpasi
IDX.author: śrīla rūpa gosvāmī
IDX.verses:
nikhila⋅śruti⋅mauli⋅ratna⋅mālā⋅
dyuti⋅nīrājita⋅pāda⋅paṅkajānta
ayi mukta⋅kulair upāsyamānaṁ
paritas tvāṁ hari⋅nāma saṁśrayāmi

jaya nāmadheya muni⋅vṛnda⋅geya
he jana⋅rañjanāya param akṣarākṛte
tvam anādarād api manāg udīritaṁ
nikhilogra⋅tāpa⋅paṭalīṁ vilumpasi

jad⋅ābhāso 'py udyan kavalita⋅bhava⋅dhvānta⋅vibhavo
dṛśiṁ tattvāndhānām api diśati bhakti⋅praṇayinīm
janas tasyodāttaṁ jagati bhagavan⋅nāma⋅taraṇe
kṛtī te nirvaktuṁ ka iha mahimānaṁ prabhavati

jad⋅brahma⋅sākṣāt⋅kṛti⋅niṣṭhayāpi
vināśam āyāti vinā na bhogaiḥ
apaiti nāma sphuraṇena tat te
prārabdha⋅karmeti virauti vedaḥ

aghadamana⋅yaśodā⋅nandanau nanda⋅sūno
kamalanayana⋅gopīcandra⋅vṛndāvanendrāḥ
praṇata⋅karuṇa⋅kṛṣṇāv ity aneka⋅svarūpe
tvayi mama ratir uccair vardhatāṁ nāmadheya

vācyaṁ vācakam ity udeti bhavato nāma svarūpa⋅dvayaṁ
pūrvasmāt param eva hanta karuṇaṁ tatrāpi jānīmahe
jas tasmin vihitāparādha⋅nivahaḥ prāṇī samantād bhaved
āsyenedam upāsya so 'pi hi sadānandāmbudhau majjati

sūditāśrita⋅janārti⋅rāśaye
ramya⋅cida⋅ghana⋅sukha⋅svarūpiṇe
nāma gokula⋅mahotsavāya te
kṛṣṇa pūrṇa⋅vapuṣe namo namaḥ

nārada⋅vīṇojjīvana
sudhormi⋅niryāsa⋅mādhurī⋅pūra
tvaṁ kṛṣṇa⋅nāma kāmaṁ
sphura me rasane rasena sadā
IDX.translation_intro: From The Harmonist, November 1934. Edited by Bhaktisiddhanta Sarasvati.
IDX.en_translation:
O Hari Nama! the Tips of Thy Lotus Feet have eternally been adored by the glowing radiance diffused from the gemmed chaplets of the Upanishads, the crest jewels of the Vedas. Thou art eternally worshipped by the liberated souls. O Hari Nama! clearing myself of all offences, I entirely take shelter in Thee.

O Holy Name! all glory to Thee who art eternally chanted by the saints. To confer eternal bliss upon the people Thou hast appeared before them as the Transcendental Word. Thou killest all sorts of terrible sufferings of him who happens to chant even Thy dimly reflected Name known as Namabhasa.

O Sun of Blessed Name! even the slightest reflection of Thy Radiant Self is suficient to dispel the gloom of ignorance from the minds of those who are steeped in the foul sink of worldliness, and confer spiritual vision of pure devotion on those who are blind of the inner sight. Who is there so expert in the world that can sing the glories of Thy Name to finish?

O Holy Name! the seed of deeds, which necessitates rebirth for their fruition, is not totally destroyed despite veritable at-one-ment with Brahman by dint of constant meditation. But O Name! no sooner dost Thou gain a free scope on the tongue of Thy chanter than all the seeds of deeds of his prenatal existence are destroyed. Such the Vedas loudly declare.

O Killer of the demon Agha! O Son of Yashoda! O Son of Nanda! O Lotus-Eyed! O Moon of the Gopees! O Lord of Brindaban! O Merciful to the bowed! O Kṛṣṇa! Thou hast manifested Thy self in such various Forms out of Thy infinite mercy. Let my ardour of love go on increasing, in a great measure, for Thee.

O Holy Name! Thou hast a double Form, viz., Vachya (Signified) i.e., the Transcendental Personality, and Vachaka (Significant) i. e., the Transcendental Word or Name- such Kṛṣṇa, Govinda, etc., -identical with the Vachya. To us the latter Vachaka is more compassionate than the former Vachya against whom we fallen souls as have sinned. Because as soon as we chant the Vachaka, i.e., Thy Holy Name, keeping clear of offences against the Name, we are at once immersed the blissful ocean of Divine Love.

O Holy Name! O Kṛṣṇa! Thou killest the offences of Thy devotees who have taken shelter in Thy Lotus Feet. Thou art veritable Living Entity of the most Beautiful self conscious concentrated Bliss. Thou art, O Name! the Entity of the great Rejoicings of Gokula. O Kṛṣṇa! I offer my humble greetings, time and again, to Thee- the Divine Form of all Exquisiteness in the fullest degree.

O Holy Name of Kṛṣṇa! Thou art the very life of Narada's guitar and the Elixir of the ambrosial ripples of the ocean surcharged with Divine mellowness. I beseech Thee, O Holy Name! do Thou ever find a full scope in my tongue with all the ardour of Divine Love.

IDX.first_line: nirakara nirakara kariya citkara
IDX.search: nirakara nirakara kariya citkara kena sadhakera santi bhanga bhai bara bara tumi ja bujhecha bhala tai laye kata kala bhakti vina phalodaya tarke nahi jana sara
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
‘nirākāra nirākāra’ kariyā cītkāra
kena sādhakera śānti bhāṅga, bhāi, bāra bāra

tumi jā’ bujhecha bhāla, tāi la’ye kāṭa kāla
bhakti vinā phalodaya, tarke nāhi jāna sāra

sāmānya tarkera bale, bhakti nāhi āsvādile
janama hoila vṛthā, nā karile suvicāra

rūpāśraye kṛṣṇa bhaji’, jadi haripreme maji
tā’ ha’le alabhya, bhāi, ki rahibe bala āra
IDX.en_translation:
Shouting "Formless! Formless!", why do you repeatedly break the peace of the practitioner, O brother?

Whatever you have understood as good, spend your time with that. Without devotion, there is no fruit; you cannot know the essence through argument.

By the strength of ordinary arguments, you did not taste devotion. Your life became useless because you did not properly consider this.

Worshiping Kṛṣṇa by taking shelter of His form, if I become absorbed in love for God—then, O brother, tell me, what will remain unattainable?

IDX.first_line: nirjana kutire sri radha carana
IDX.search: nirjana kutire sri radha carana smarane thakiba rata sri rupamanjari dhire dhire asi kahibe amaya kata balibe o sukha ki kara vasiya dekhaha bahire asi jugala milana sobha nirupama hoibe carana dasi
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
nirjana kuṭīre, śrī⋅rādhā⋅caraṇa⋅
smaraṇe thākiba rata
śrī⋅rūpamañjarī, dhīre dhīre āsi,
kahibe āmāya kata

balibe o sukha! ki kara vasiyā,
dekhaha bāhire āsi’
jugala milana⋅, śobhā nirupama,
hoibe caraṇa dāsī

svārasikī siddhi, vrajagopī dhana,
parama cañcalā satī
jogīra dheyāna,. nirviśeṣa jñāna,
nā pāya ekhāne sthiti

sākṣāt darśana, madhyāhna līlāya,
rādhāpada⋅sevārthinī
jakhana je sevā, karaha jatane,
śrī⋅rādhā⋅caraṇe dhani
IDX.en_translation:
In a secluded hut, I will remain absorbed in remembering Śrī Rādhā's feet. Śrī Rūpa Mañjarī, slowly coming, will tell me much.

She will say, "Oh, dear soul! What are you doing sitting? Come outside and see! Behold the unparalleled beauty of the divine couple's union, and become a servant at their feet."

The perfection of spontaneous devotion, the treasure of the cowherd maidens of Vraja, is an extremely dynamic and pure devotion. The meditation of yogis, the knowledge of the impersonal Brahman, does not find a place here.

I long for direct vision in the midday divine play, desiring service at Rādhā's feet. Whatever service, whenever, perform with care, O opulent one at Śrī Rādhā's feet.

IDX.first_line: nirveda visada mada dainya glani sramonmada
IDX.search: nirveda visada mada dainya glani sramonmada garva trasa sanka apasmrti avega alasya vyadhi moha mrtyu jadatadi vrida avahittha ara smrti vitarka capalya mati cintautsukya harsa dhrti augrasuya nidramarsa supti bodha ei bhavacaya trayastrimsat sabe haya vyabhicari name labhe jnapti
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
nirveda viṣāda mada, dainya glāni śramonmāda,
garva trāsa śaṅkā apasmṛti
āvega ālasya vyādhi, moha mṛtyu jaḍatādi,
vrīḍā avahitthā āra smṛti

vitarka cāpalya mati, cintautsukya harṣa dhṛti,
augrāsūyā nidrāmarṣa supti
bodha⋅ei bhāvacaya, trayastriṁśat sabe haya,
‘vyabhicārī’ nāme labhe jñapti

atulya madhura rase, augrālasya nā paraśe,
āra saba bhāva jathāyatha
udi’ bhāvāveśa sukhe, sthāyibhāvera abhimukhe,
viśeṣa āgrahe haya rata

bāgaṅga⋅sattva⋅āśraye, rasayoga sañcāraye,
jena sthāyī sāgarera ḍheu
nijakārya sādhi’ tūrṇa, sāgara kariyā pūrṇa,
nibhe āra nāhi dekhe keu
IDX.en_translation:
Detachment, dejection, pride, humility, weariness, and the madness of exertion; arrogance, fear, apprehension, and forgetfulness; agitation, laziness, and sickness; delusion, death, and inertia; bashfulness, dissimulation, and memory.

Deliberation, mental fickleness, anxiety, joy, and steadfastness; fierceness, envy, sleepiness, and deep slumber—know all these groups of emotions, thirty-three in total, are recognized as transitory states.

In the unparalleled sweet mellow, fierceness and laziness do not appear; all other emotions remain as they should. Arising in the bliss of emotional absorption, they are especially eager to move toward the permanent sentiment.

Through the support of speech, body, and goodness, they convey the union of the mellow, like the enduring waves of an ocean. Quickly fulfilling their own purpose and making the ocean full, they then subside and are seen no more.

IDX.first_line: nisanta hayeche dekhi tryasta haye vrnda sakhi
IDX.search: nisanta hayeche dekhi tryasta haye vrnda sakhi jagaite vrajayuvadvandve upaya karaya kata raba karaya nanamata sukasari gaya nana chande priyapriya rabe hari sukhasayya parihari pyari saha uthila takhana tatkalika rati sobha ratajanera manolobha sakhigana kare darasana
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
niśānta hayeche dekhi’, tryasta haye vṛndā⋅sakhī,
jāgāite vrajayuvadvandve
upāya karaya kata, raba karāya nānāmata,
śukasārī gāya nānā chande

priyāpriya rabe hari, sukhaśayyā parihari,
pyārī saha uṭhila takhana
tātkālika rati⋅śobhā, ratajanera manolobhā,
sakhīgaṇa kare daraśana

kakkhaṭīra svara śuni’, camaki uṭhiyā dhanī,
priya saha karaya prayāṇa
āgāmī saŋketa bali’, nija nija gṛhe cali’,
svaśayyāya karaya śayana

śayyotthāna kāle tathā, haya kata līlākathā,
du̐hera mandire manoharā
mukha⋅prakṣālana snāna, vāsa⋅bhūṣā⋅paridhāna,
svīya svīya kāye haya tvarā
IDX.en_translation:
Seeing that the night has ended, Vṛndā-sakhī becomes anxious and devises many plans to awaken the Divine Youthful Couple. She makes various sounds, and the parrots and mynahs sing in diverse meters.

Hearing those beloved and pleasing sounds, Lord Hari rises from His comfortable bed along with Rādhā. The gopīs witness the immediate beauty of Their union, which captivates the hearts of all who see it.

Hearing the sound of the monkey Kakkhaṭī, Rādhā startles awake and departs with Her Beloved. Giving a signal for Their next meeting, They go to Their respective homes and lie down in Their own beds.

At the time of rising from Their beds, many divine pastimes occur in Their enchanting abodes. After washing Their faces and bathing, They dress in beautiful clothes and ornaments and quickly attend to Their own tasks.

IDX.first_line: nitai amara parama dayala
IDX.search: nitai amara parama dayala aniya premera vanya jagata karila dhanya bharila premete nadi khala lagiya premera dheu baki na rahila keu papi tapi calila bhasiya sakala bhakata meli se premete kare keli keha keha jaya satariya
IDX.author: vasudeva ghoṣa
IDX.verses:
nitāi āmāra parama dayāla
āniyā premera vanyā, jagata karila dhanyā,
bharila premete nadī khāla

lāgiyā premera ḍheu, bāki nā rahila keu,
pāpī⋅tāpī calila bhāsiyā
sakala bhakata meli, se premete kare keli,
keha keha jāya sā̐tāriyā

ḍubila nadīyāpura, ḍube preme śāntipura,
dohe mili bāicha khelāya
tā dekhi nitāi hāse, sakalei preme bhāse,
vāsu ghoṣa hābuḍubu khāya
IDX.en_translation:
Lord Nitāi is supremely merciful to me. Bringing a flood of divine love, He made the world blessed. He filled the rivers and canals with love.

As the waves of love surged, no one remained untouched. The sinful and distressed floated away in that current. All the devotees joined together and played in that love, and some even swam across.

The towns of Nadiyā and Śāntipur were submerged in divine love. Together, everyone joined in the joyous play. Seeing that, Nitāi smiles as everyone floats in love, and Vāsudeva Ghoṣa bobs up and down.

IDX.first_line: nitai caitanya dohe bada avatara
IDX.search: nitai caitanya dohe bada avatara emana dayala data na hoibe ara mleccha candala ninduka pasandadi jata karunamaya uddhara karila sata sata
IDX.author: kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa
IDX.verses:
nitāi caitanya dohe baḍa avatāra
emana dayāla dātā nā hoibe āra

mleccha⋅caṇḍāla⋅ninduka⋅pāṣaṇḍādi jata
karuṇā-maya uddhāra karilā śata śata

hena avatāre mora kichui nā haila
hāyare dāruṇa prāṇa ki sukhe rahila

jata jata avatāra hoila bhuvane
hena avatāra bhāi nā haya kakhane

hena prabhura pāda⋅padma nā kari bhajana
hāte tuli’ mukhe viṣa karinu bhakṣaṇa

gaura⋅kīrtana⋅rase jagata ḍubila
hāyare abhāgāra bindu paraśa nahila

kā̐de kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa keśa chi̐ḍi’ nija kare
dhik dhik abhāgiyā kena nāhi mare
IDX.en_translation:
Lord Nityānanda and Lord Caitanya are both great incarnations; such merciful and generous givers will never appear again.

They mercifully delivered hundreds upon hundreds of outcasted, blasphemous, and atheistic persons.

But even during such incarnations, nothing happened for me. Alas! How can my hard heart still remain happy?

However many incarnations there have been in the world, such merciful ones as these, O brother, have never appeared before.

Not having worshipped the lotus feet of such Masters, I have effectively taken poison into my mouth with my own hands.

The entire world is submerged in the nectar of congregational chanting for Lord Gaura, yet this unfortunate soul has not felt even a drop of it.

Kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa weeps, tearing his hair with his own hands. Shame, shame! Why does this unfortunate one not simply die?

IDX.first_line: nitai caitanya dohe bada avataraemana dayala data na hoibe ara
IDX.search: nitai caitanya dohe bada avataraemana dayala data na hoibe ara mleccha candala ninduka pasandadi jatakarunamaya uddhara karila sata sata
IDX.author: kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa
IDX.verses:
nitāi caitanya dohe baḍa avatāraemana dayāla dātā nā hoibe āra

mleccha⋅caṇḍāla⋅ninduka⋅pāṣaṇḍādi jatakaruṇā-maya uddhāra karilā śata śata

hena avatāre mora kichui nā hailahāyare dāruṇa prāṇa ki sukhe rahila

jata jata avatāra hoila bhuvanehena avatāra bhāi nā haya kakhane

hena prabhura pāda⋅padma nā kari bhajanahāte tuli’ mukhe viṣa karinu bhakṣaṇa

gaura⋅kīrtana⋅rase jagata ḍubilahāyare abhāgāra bindu paraśa nahila

kā̐de kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa keśa chi̐ḍi’ nija karedhik dhik abhāgiyā kena nāhi mare
IDX.en_translation:
Lord Nityānanda and Lord Caitanya are both great incarnations; such merciful and generous givers will never appear again.

They mercifully delivered hundreds upon hundreds of outcasted, blasphemous, and atheistic persons.

But even during such incarnations, nothing happened for me. Alas! How can my hard heart still remain happy?

However many incarnations there have been in the world, such merciful ones as these, O brother, have never appeared before.

Not having worshipped the lotus feet of such Masters, I have effectively taken poison into my mouth with my own hands.

The entire world is submerged in the nectar of congregational chanting for Lord Gaura, yet this unfortunate soul has not felt even a drop of it.

Kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa weeps, tearing his hair with his own hands. Shame, shame! Why does this unfortunate one not simply die?

IDX.first_line: nitai gaura nama anandera dhama
IDX.search: nitai gaura nama anandera dhama jei jana nahi laya tare jamaraya dhare laye jaya narake dubaya taya tulasira hara na pare je chara jamalaye vasa tara tilaka dharana na kare je jana vrthaya janama tara
IDX.author: śrīla locana⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
nitāi⋅gaura⋅nāma, ānandera dhāma,
jei jana nāhi laya
tā’re jamarāya, dha’re la’ye jāya,
narake ḍubāya tāya

tulasīra hāra, nā pare je chāra,
jamālaye vāsa tā’ra
tilaka dhāraṇa, nā kare je⋅jana,
vṛthāya janama tā’ra

nā laya hari-nāma, vidhi tā’re bāma,
pāmara pāṣaṇḍamati
vaiṣṇava⋅sevana, nā kare je⋅jana,
ki ha’be tāhāra gati

gurumantra sāra, kara eibāra,
vrajete hoibe vāsa
tamo-guṇa jā’be, sattva⋅guṇa pā’be,
hoibe kṛṣṇera dāsa

e dāsa locana, bale anukṣaṇa,
(nitāi) gauraguṇa gāo sukhe
ei rase jā’ra, rati nā hoila,
cūṇa⋅kāli tā’ra mukhe
IDX.en_translation:
The names of Nitāi and Gaura are the abode of bliss. Whoever does not chant these names will be seized by the lord of death and drowned in hell.

That wretched person who does not wear a Tulasi garland will dwell in the abode of death. Whoever does not wear a sacred tilaka, his birth is in vain.

Whoever does not chant the Holy Name is sinful and stone-hearted, and fate is against him. Whoever does not render service to the devotees, what will be his destination?

Take the essence of the guru's mantra now, and you will then reside in Vraja. The mode of ignorance will depart, you will attain the mode of goodness, and you will become a servant of Kṛṣṇa.

This servant Locana constantly says: "Happily sing the glories of Gaura." Whoever does not develop affection for this nectar—disgrace be upon his face!

IDX.first_line: nitai gaura nama anandera dhamajei jana nahi layatare jamaraya dhare laye jayanarake dubaya taya
IDX.search: nitai gaura nama anandera dhamajei jana nahi layatare jamaraya dhare laye jayanarake dubaya taya tulasira hara na pare je charajamalaye vasa taratilaka dharana na kare je janavrthaya janama tara
IDX.author: śrīla locana⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
nitāi⋅gaura⋅nāma, ānandera dhāma,jei jana nāhi layatā’re jamarāya, dha’re la’ye jāya,narake ḍubāya tāya

tulasīra hāra, nā pare je chāra,jamālaye vāsa tā’ratilaka dhāraṇa, nā kare je⋅jana,vṛthāya janama tā’ra

nā laya hari-nāma, vidhi tā’re bāma,pāmara pāṣaṇḍamativaiṣṇava⋅sevana, nā kare je⋅jana,ki ha’be tāhāra gati

gurumantra sāra, kara eibāra,vrajete hoibe vāsatamo-guṇa jā’be, sattva⋅guṇa pā’be,hoibe kṛṣṇera dāsa

e dāsa locana, bale anukṣaṇa,(nitāi) gauraguṇa gāo sukheei rase jā’ra, rati nā hoila,cūṇa⋅kāli tā’ra mukhe
IDX.en_translation:
The names of Nitāi and Gaura are the abode of bliss. Whoever does not chant these names will be seized by the lord of death and drowned in hell.

That wretched person who does not wear a Tulasi garland will dwell in the abode of death. Whoever does not wear a sacred tilaka, his birth is in vain.

Whoever does not chant the Holy Name is sinful and stone-hearted, and fate is against him. Whoever does not render service to the devotees, what will be his destination?

Take the essence of the guru’s mantra now, and you will then reside in Vraja. The mode of ignorance will depart, you will attain the mode of goodness, and you will become a servant of Kṛṣṇa.

This servant Locana constantly says: “Happily sing the glories of Gaura”. Whoever does not develop affection for this nectar—disgrace be upon his face!

IDX.first_line: nitai gunamani amara nitai gunamani
IDX.search: nitai gunamani amara nitai gunamani aniya premera vanya bhasaila avani premera vanya loiya nitai aila gaudadese dubila bhakatagana dina hina bhase
IDX.author: śrīla locana⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
nitāi guṇamaṇi āmāra nitāi guṇamaṇi
āniyā premera vanyā bhāsāila avanī

premera vanyā loiyā nitāi āila gauḍadeśe
ḍubila bhakatagaṇa dīna hīna bhāse

dīna hīna patita pāmara nāhi bāche
brahmāra durlabha prema sabākāre jāce

ābaddha karuṇā⋅sindhu kāṭiyā muhāna
ghare ghare bule prema⋅amiyāra vāna

locana bale mora nitāi jebā nā bhajila
jāniyā śuniyā sei ātmaghātī haila
IDX.en_translation:
Nitāi is my jewel of virtues! Bringing the flood of divine love, He inundated the earth.

Nitāi came to Bengal with this flood of divine love. The devotees drowned in it, while the fallen and lowly floated on its surface.

He does not reject the fallen, the degraded, or the sinful. He begs everyone to accept this love which is difficult even for Brahmā to attain.

Breaking open the dam of the ocean of His boundless mercy, the current of nectar-like love flows from house to house.

Locana says: whoever did not worship my Nitāi, knowing and hearing of Him, has committed spiritual suicide.

IDX.first_line: nitai mora jivana dhana nitai mora jati
IDX.search: nitai mora jivana dhana nitai mora jati nitai vihane mora ara nahi gati samsara sukhera mukhe tule diba chai nagare magiya khaba gaiya nitai
IDX.author: śrīla locana⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
nitāi mora jīvana⋅dhana, nitāi mora jāti
nitāi vihane mora āra nāhi gati

saṁsāra⋅sukhera mukhe tule diba chāi
nagare māgiyā khā’ba gāiyā nitāi

je⋅deśe nitāi nāi, se⋅deśe nā jā’ba
nitāi⋅vimukha⋅janāra mukha nā heriba

gaṅgā jā̐’ra padajala, hara śire dhare
hena nitāi nā bhajiyā duḥkha peye mare

locana bale mora nitāi jebā nāhi māne
anala bhejāi tā’ra mājha⋅mukhakhāne
IDX.en_translation:
Nitāi is the treasure of my life, Nitāi is my identity. Without Nitāi, I have no other destination.

I will throw ashes in the face of worldly happiness. I will beg in the town, singing of Nitāi.

I will not go anywhere where Nitāi is absent. I will not look at the face of a person averse to Nitāi.

Hara (Śiva) holds on his head the Ganges, which is the water of His (Nitāi's) feet. Not worshipping such a Nitāi, one dies in misery.

Locana says: Whoever does not accept my Nitāi, I will set fire to the middle of his face.

IDX.first_line: nitai nama hate o ke jabire bhai aya chute
IDX.search: nitai nama hate o ke jabire bhai aya chute ese pasanda jagai madhai du jana sakala hatera mala nile lute hatera amsi mahajana sri advaita sanatana bhandari sri gadadhara pandita vicaksana
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
nitāi nāma hāṭe, o ke jābire bhāi, āya chuṭe
ese pāṣaṇḍa jagāi mādhāi du⋅jana sakala hāṭera māla nile luṭe

hāṭera aṁśī mahājana, śrī advaita, sanātana
bhāṇḍārī śrī⋅gadādhara paṇḍita vicakṣaṇa

āchena caukidāra haridāsera ādi halena śrī⋅sañjaya
śrī⋅śrīdhara māte dālāla keśava bhāratī, śrī⋅vidyā vācaspati

paricāraka āchena kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa prabhṛti
hana koṣādhyakṣa śrīvāsa⋅paṇḍita, jhāḍudāra kedāra juṭe

hāṭera mūlya nirūpaṇa, naya⋅bhakti prakāśana
prema hena mudrā sarva⋅sāra, saṁyamana nāi kami beśī samāna

o jana re, saba eto mane bojhāya uṭhe ei premera uddeśya,
eka sādhu upadeśa sudhā⋅maya hari⋅nāma rūpa su⋅sandeśa
ete baḍa nāi re dveṣādveṣa khāya eka pāte kānā⋅khu̐ḍe
IDX.en_translation:
O brother, who will go to the marketplace of the Holy Name? Come quickly! Those two wicked persons, Jagāi and Mādhāi, have already come and looted all the goods of the marketplace.

The respected proprietors of this marketplace are Advaita and Sanātana. The expert treasurer is Gadādhara Paṇḍita.

The watchmen are Haridāsa and others, including Śrī Sañjaya. Śrīdhara serves as a broker, along with Keśava Bhāratī and Vidyā Vācaspati.

Kṛṣṇa-dāsa and others are the attendants. Śrīvāsa Paṇḍita is the treasurer, and Kedāra has joined as the sweeper.

The value of this marketplace is determined by the manifestation of the nine kinds of devotional service. Divine love is the essence of all currency; there is no restriction of more or less, for it is available equally to all.

O people, all this is understood in the mind: the very purpose of this divine love. A single saintly instruction, full of nectar, is the good message in the form of the Holy Name. In this there is no partiality; all eat from the same plate, even the most fallen.

IDX.first_line: nitai pada kamala koticandra susitala
IDX.search: nitai pada kamala koticandra susitala je chayaya jagat judaya hena nitai vine bhai radha krsna paite nai drdha kari dhara nitaira paya se sambandha nahi jara vrtha janma gela tara sei pasu bada duracara nitai na balila mukhe majila samsara sukhe vidyakule ki karibe tara
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
nitāi⋅pada⋅kamala, koṭicandra⋅suśītala,
je chāyāya jagat juḍāya
hena nitāi vine bhāi, rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa pāite nāi,
dṛḍha kari’ dhara nitāira pāya

se⋅sambandha nāhi jā’ra, vṛthā janma gela tā’ra,
sei paśu baḍa durācāra
nitāi nā balila mukhe, majila saṁsāra⋅sukhe,
vidyākule ki karibe tā’ra

ahaṅkāre matta haiñā, nitāi⋅pada pāsariyā,
asatyare satya kari’ māni
nitāiyera karuṇā ha’be, vraje rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa pā’be,
dhara nitāiyera caraṇa du’khāni

nitāiyera caraṇa satya, tā̐hāra sevaka nitya,
nitāi⋅pada sadā kara āśa
narottama baḍa duḥkhī, nitāi more kara sukhī,
rākha rāṅgā caraṇera pāśa
IDX.en_translation:
Nitāi’s lotus feet are as cool and soothing as millions of moons; in their shade, the whole world finds peace. O brother, without such a Nitāi, one cannot attain Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa. Therefore, firmly hold on to Nitāi’s feet.

One who has no connection with Nitāi has wasted their life; that (two legged) animal is most wicked. If one does not utter Nitāi’s name and is absorbed in worldly pleasures, what good will their knowledge or family lineage do for them?

(That person) Being intoxicated with false ego, forgets Nitāi’s feet, and acceps untruth as truth. By Nitāi’s mercy, one will attain Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa in Vraja. Therefore, hold on to Nitāi’s two feet.

Nitāi’s feet are the ultimate truth, and His servants are eternal. Always yearn for Nitāi’s feet. Narottama is very unhappy; O Nitāi, please make me happy, and keep me by your reddish lotus feet.

IDX.first_line: nrsiŋha kavacam vaksye
IDX.search: nrsiŋha kavacam vaksye prahladenoditam pura sarva raksa karam punyam sarvo padrava nasanam sarva sampatkaram caiva svarga moksa pradayakam dhyatva nrsiŋham devesam hema siŋhasana sthitam
IDX.author: brahmāṇḍa purāṇa
IDX.verses:
nṛsiŋha⋅kavacaṁ vakṣye
prahlādenoditaṁ purā
sarva⋅rakṣa⋅karaṁ puṇyaṁ
sarvo⋅padrava⋅nāśanam

sarva⋅sampatkaraṁ caiva
svarga⋅mokṣa⋅pradāyakam
dhyātvā nṛsiŋhaṁ deveśaṁ
hema⋅siŋhāsana⋅sthitam

vivṛtāsyaṁ trinayanaṁ
śaradindu⋅sama⋅prabham
lakṣmyāliṅgita⋅vāmāṅgam
vibhūtibhir upāśritam

caturbhujaṁ komalāṅgaṁ
svarṇa⋅kuṇḍala⋅śobhitam
saroja⋅śobhitoraskaṁ
ratna⋅keyūra⋅mudritam

tapta⋅kāñcana⋅saṅkāśaṁ
pīta⋅nirmala⋅vāsasam
indrādi⋅sura⋅mauliṣṭhaḥ
sphuranmāṇikya⋅dīptibhiḥ

virājita⋅pada⋅dvandvaṁ
śaṅkha⋅cakrādi⋅hetibhiḥ
garutmatā ca vinayāt
stūyamānaṁ mudānvitam

svahṛt⋅kamala⋅samvāsaṁ
kṛtvā tu kavacaṁ pathet
nṛsiŋho me śiraḥ pātu
loka⋅rakṣārtha⋅sambhavaḥ

sarvago‛pi stambha⋅vāsaḥ
phalaṁ me rakṣatu dhvanim
nṛsiŋho me dṛśau pātu
soma⋅sūryagni⋅locanaḥ


smṛtaṁ me pātu nṛhariḥ
muni⋅vārya⋅stuti⋅priyaḥ
nāsaṁ me siŋha⋅nāśastu
mukhaṁ lakṣmī⋅mukha⋅priyaḥ

sarva⋅vidyādhipaḥ pātu
nṛsiŋho rasanaṁ mama
vaktraṁ pātvindu⋅vadanaṁ
sadā prahlāda⋅vanditaḥ

nṛsiŋhaḥ pātu me kaṇṭhaṁ
skandhau bhūbhṛdananta⋅kṛt
divyāstra⋅śobhita⋅bhujaḥ
nṛsiŋhaḥ pātu me bhujau

karau me deva⋅varado
nṛsiŋhaḥ pātu sarvataḥ
hṛdayaṁ jogi⋅sādhyaśca
nivāsaṁ pātu me hariḥ

madhyaṁ pātu hiraṇyākṣa⋅
vakṣaḥ⋅kukṣi⋅vidāraṇaḥ
nābhiṁ me pātu nṛhariḥ
svanābhi⋅brahma⋅saṁstutaḥ

brahmāṇḍa⋅koṭayaḥ kaṭyāṁ
jasyāsau pātu me kaṭim
guhyaṁ me pātu guhyānāṁ
mantrāṇāṁ guhya⋅rūpa⋅dṛk

ūrū manobhavaḥ pātu
jānunī nara⋅rūpa⋅dṛk
jaṅghe pātu dharā⋅bhara⋅
hartā jo‛sau nṛ⋅keśarī

sura⋅rājya⋅pradaḥ pātu
pādau me nṛharīśvaraḥ
sahasra⋅śīrṣā⋅puruṣaḥ
pātu me sarvaśastanum

manograḥ pūrvataḥ pātu
mahā⋅vīrāgrajo‛gnitaḥ
mahāviṣṇurdakṣiṇe tu
mahājvalastu nairṛtaḥ

paścime pātu sarveśo
diśi me sarvatomukhaḥ
nṛsiŋhaḥ pātu vāyavyāṁ
saumyāṁ bhūṣaṇa⋅vigrahaḥ

īśānyāṁ pātu bhadro me
sarva⋅maṅgala⋅dāyakaḥ
saṁsāra⋅bhayataḥ pātu
mṛtyormṛtyurnṛkeśarī

idaṁ nṛsiŋha⋅kavacaṁ
prahlāda⋅mukha⋅maṇḍitam
bhaktimān jaḥ paṭhennityaṁ
sarva⋅pāpaiḥ pramucyate

putravān dhanavān loke
dīrghāyurupajāyate
jaṁ jaṁ kāmayate kāmaṁ
taṁ taṁ prāpnotyasaṁśayam

sarvatra jayamāpnoti
sarvatra vijayī bhavet
bhūmyantarīkṣa⋅divyānāṁ
grahāṇāṁ vinivāraṇam

vṛścikoraga⋅sambhūta⋅
viṣāpaharaṇaṁ param
brahma⋅rākṣasa⋅yakṣāṇāṁ
dūrotsāraṇa⋅kāraṇam

bhuje vā tala⋅pātre vā
kavacaṁ likhitaṁ śubham
kara⋅mūle dhṛtaṁ jena
sidhyeyuḥ karma⋅siddhayaḥ

devāsura⋅manuṣyeṣu
svaṁ svameva jayaṁ labhet
ekasandhyaṁ trisandhyaṁ vā
jaḥ paṭhenniyato naraḥ

sarva⋅maṅgala⋅maṅgalyaṁ
bhuktiṁ muktiṁ ca vindati
dvātriṁśati⋅sahasrāṇi
pathet śuddhātmanāṁ nṛṇām

kavacasyāsya mantrasya
mantra⋅siddhiḥ prajāyate
anena mantra⋅rājena
kṛtvā bhasmābhirmantrānām

tilakaṁ vinyasedyastu
tasya graha⋅bhayaṁ haret
trivāraṁ japamānastu
dattaṁ vāryābhimantrya ca

prāśayedyo naro mantraṁ
nṛsiŋha⋅dhyānamācaret
tasya rogaḥ praṇaśyanti
je ca syuḥ kukṣi⋅sambhavāḥ

garjantaṁ garjayantaṁ
nija⋅bhuja⋅patalaṁ sphoṭayantaṁ hatantaṁ
rūpyantaṁ tāpayantaṁ
divi bhuvi ditijaṁ kṣepayantam kṣipantam
krandantaṁ roṣayantaṁ
diśi diśi satataṁ saŋharantaṁ bharantaṁ
vīkṣantaṁ pūrṇayantaṁ
kara⋅nikara⋅śatairdivya⋅siŋhaṁ namāmi

iti śrī⋅brahmāṇḍapurāṇe prahlādoktaṁ śrī⋅nṛsiŋha⋅kavacaṁ sampūrṇam
IDX.en_translation:
I shall now speak the Nṛsiṁha-kavaca, which was spoken by Prahlāda in ancient times. It gives all protection, is pious, and destroys all disturbances.

It bestows all opulence and grants heaven and liberation. Having meditated on Nṛsiṁha, the Lord of gods, seated on a golden throne.

With open mouth, three eyes, and luster like the autumn moon; embraced by Lakṣmī on His left side, and surrounded by opulences.

Four-armed, with soft limbs, adorned with golden earrings; with a chest beautified by a lotus, and marked with jeweled armlets.

Resembling molten gold, wearing yellow, spotless garments; the crest-jewel of Indra and the gods, with the brilliance of shining rubies.

His two feet shine with weapons like the conch and disc; He is praised by Garuḍa with humility and joy.

Having placed Him in the lotus of one's own heart, one should recite the Kavaca. May Nṛsiṁha, who appeared for the protection of the world, protect my head.

May He who is all-pervading and resides in the pillar protect the fruit of my words. May Nṛsiṁha, whose eyes are the moon, sun, and fire, protect my eyes.

May Nṛhari, who is dear to the prayers of the best of sages, protect my memory. May Siṁha-nāsa protect my nose, and He who is dear to the face of Lakṣmī protect my face.

May the Lord of all knowledge, Nṛsiṁha, protect my tongue. May He whose face is like the moon and who is always worshiped by Prahlāda protect my mouth.

May Nṛsiṁha protect my neck, and the Sustainer of the earth and Maker of Ananta protect my shoulders. May Nṛsiṁha, whose arms are adorned with divine weapons, protect my arms.

May Nṛsiṁha, the bestower of boons to the gods, protect my hands from all sides. May Hari, who is attainable by yogīs, protect my heart, His residence.

May He who tore open the chest and abdomen of Hiraṇyākṣa protect my middle. May Nṛhari protect my navel, He who is praised by Brahmā who sits on His own navel.

May He in whose hips are millions of universes protect my hips. May He who sees the secret form of the secret mantras protect my private parts.

May Manobhava protect my thighs, and He who appears in a human form protect my knees. May the Man-Lion, who removes the burden of the earth, protect my calves.

May the Lord Nṛhari, the giver of the kingdom of the gods, protect my feet. May the Thousand-headed Person protect my body entirely.

May the Ferocious One protect me from the East, and the Elder Brother of the Great Hero (Maha-vīra-agraja) from the Southeast (Agni). May Mahā-viṣṇu protect me from the South, and the Greatly Blazing One from the Southwest.

May the Lord of all protect me from the West, He whose faces are in all directions. May Nṛsiṁha protect me from the Northwest, and the Ornamented Form from the North.

May the Auspicious One, the giver of all auspiciousness, protect me from the Northeast. May the Man-Lion, the Death of death, protect me from the fear of material existence.

This Nṛsiṁha-kavaca is adorned by the mouth of Prahlāda. One who reads it daily with devotion is freed from all sins.

He becomes wealthy and has sons in this world, and lives a long life. Whatever desire he wishes for, he undoubtedly obtains it.

He attains victory everywhere; he becomes victorious everywhere. It is the preventer of the influence of earthly, aerial, and celestial planets.

It is the supreme remover of poison born of scorpions and snakes. It is the cause of driving away Brahma-rākṣasas and Yakṣas.

Whether written on the arm or on a palm leaf, this auspicious Kavaca—if one wears it on the wrist, all his activities are perfected.

One attains victory over one's own self among gods, demons, and humans. A man who recites it with regulation at one sandhyā or three sandhyās (junctions of the day).

He finds all-auspiciousness, enjoyment, and liberation. Reading thirty-two thousand times for pure souls.

The perfection of the mantra of this Kavaca is born. By this King of Mantras, having made ashes (bhasma) with the mantras.

One who applies the tilaka removes the fear of planets. Chanting three times and enchanting water given.

The man who drinks the mantra and practices meditation on Nṛsiṁha—his diseases are destroyed, even those born in the abdomen.

Roaring and making others roar, cracking the surface of the earth with His own arms, killing, ripping, and heating. Throwing and casting down the sons of Diti in heaven and on earth. Crying and causing anger, constantly destroying and filling in every direction. Seeing and filling—I bow to the Divine Lion with hundreds of multitudes of hands.

Thus ends the Śrī Nṛsiṁha-kavaca spoken by Prahlāda in the Śrī Brahmāṇḍa Purāṇa.

IDX.first_line: o mora karunamaya srithakura mahasaya
IDX.search: o mora karunamaya srithakura mahasaya narottama premera murati kiba se komala tanu sirisa kusuma janu jiniya kanaka deha jyoti alapa vayasa taya kona sukha nahi bhaya gora guna suni sada jhure rajyabhoga teyagiya ati lalayita haiya gamana karila vrajapure

o mora karuṇā-maya, śrī-ṭhākura mahāśaya,

śrīnivāsācārya⋅sane, je marma tā’ kebā jāne,
prāṇa eka, bhinna-mātra deha

śrī-rādhā-vinoda dekhi’, sadāi juḍāya ā̐khi,
prabhu⋅lokanātha sevārata
bhaktiśāstra⋅adhyayane, mahānanda bāḍaye mane,

prabhu⋅anumati mate, śrī-vraja-maṇḍala haite,
śrī⋅gauḍamaṇḍale praveśilā
prabhu⋅anugrahabale, navadvīpa⋅nīlācale,
bhakta-gṛhe bhramaṇa karilā

kibā se madhura rīti, khetari⋅grāmete sthiti,
seve ‘gaura’, ‘śrī⋅rādhā⋅ramaṇe’
‘śrī⋅vallabhīkānta’⋅nāma, ‘rādhā-kānta’ rasa-dhāma,
‘rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa’, ‘śrī-vraja-mohane’

ei chaya vigraha* jena, sākṣāt vihare hena,
śobhā dekhi’ kebā nāhi bhule
priya⋅rāmacandra⋅saṅge, narottama mahāraṅge,
bhāse prema-rasera hillole

*gaurāṅga! vallabhikānta! śrī⋅kṛṣṇa! vrajamohana!
rādhā⋅ramaṇa! he rādhe! rādhakānta! namo‛stute

narottama guṇa jata, ke tāhā kahibe kata,
premavṛṣṭi jā̐’ra saṅkīrtane
śrī⋅advaita, nityānanda, gaṇasaha gaura⋅candra,
nācaye⋅dekhila bhāgyavāne

gauragaṇa⋅priya ati, narottama mahāmati,
vaiṣṇava⋅sevane jā̐’ra dhvani
ki adbhuta dayāvān, kāre vā nā kare dāna,
nirmala⋅bhakati cintāmaṇi

pāṣaṇḍī asuragaṇe, mātāilā gorā⋅guṇe,
vihvala hoiyā premāveśe
alaukika kriyā jā’ra, hena ki hoibe āra,
se nā jaśa ghoṣe deśe deśe

kahe narahari dīna, ha’be ki emana dina,
narottama⋅pade vikāiba ?
saghane du’bāhu tuli’, ‘prabhu⋅narottama’ bali’,
kā̐di’ ki dhūlāya loṭāiba
IDX.en_translation:
O my most merciful Śrīla Narottama Dāsa Ṭhākura! He is the very personification of divine love. How tender is his form, like a śiriṣa blossom, surpassing even the golden luster of a physical body.

In his youth, no worldly pleasure appealed to him. Hearing the qualities of Lord Gaura, he always wept. Abandoning kingly enjoyments and with great longing, he journeyed to Vraja.

Entering Vṛndāvana with a joyful mind, he surrendered himself to Śrīla Lokanātha Dāsa Gosvāmī. Lokanātha Dāsa, being merciful, accepted him as his own and initiated him with the Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa mantra.

Seeing Narottama's endeavor, everyone in Vṛndāvana was happy and showered him with affection as if he were their very life. He and Śrīnivāsa Ācārya had such a deep connection—their lives were one, and only their bodies were distinct.

Seeing the Deity of Śrī Rādhā-vinoda, his eyes were always soothed, as Lord Lokanātha was engaged in His service. Through the study of devotional scriptures, great joy increased in his mind, and all his desires were fulfilled.

By his Guru's permission, he left Vraja and entered the land of Gauḍa. By the grace of his Master, he traveled to the homes of devotees in Navadvīpa and Jagannātha Purī.

What a sweet tradition he established in the village of Khetari! He served Lord Gaura, Rādhā-ramaṇa, Śrī Vallabhīkānta, Rādhākānta, Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa, and Śrī Vrajamohana.

These six Deities seemed to be directly performing Their pastimes. Who would not be enchanted by such beauty? With his dear companion Rāmacandra, Narottama floats in the waves of the mellows of divine love.

O Lord Gaurāṅga! O Vallabhīkānta! O Lord Kṛṣṇa! O Vrajamohana! O Rādhā-ramaṇa! O Rādhā! O Rādhākānta! I offer my obeisances unto You.

Who can describe all the qualities of Narottama? His congregational chanting is like a shower of divine love. Advaita Ācārya, Lord Nityānanda, and Lord Gaura⋅candra with Their associates all dance in his kīrtana—the fortunate ones witnessed this.

Narottama is supremely dear to the associates of Lord Gaura and is of great wisdom. His fame resounds in the service of the devotees. What an astonishingly merciful soul! To whom does he not give the spotless jewel of pure devotion?

Even the atheistic and demoniac persons he intoxicated with the qualities of Lord Gaura, overwhelmed with ecstatic love. His supernatural activities are unique; his fame is proclaimed in every land.

The lowly Narahari says: "Will such a day ever come when I will be completely surrendered at the feet of Narottama? Raising both arms repeatedly and crying out 'O Master Narottama!', will I weep and roll in the dust?"

IDX.first_line: ohe
IDX.search: ohe vaisnava thakura doyara sagara e dase koruna kori diya padachaya sodho he amaya tomara carana dhori chaya vega domi chaya dosa sodhi chaya guna deho dase chaya satsanga deho he amare bosechi sangera ase
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
ohe!
vaiṣṇava ṭhākura, doyāra sāgara,
e dāse korunā kori’
diyā pada-chāyā, śodho he āmāya,
tomāra caraṇa dhori

chaya vega domi’, chaya doṣa śodhi’,
chaya guna deho’ dāse
chaya sat-saṅga, deho’ he āmāre,
bosechi saṅgera āśe

ekakī āmāra, nāhi pāya bala,
hari-nāma-saṅkīrtane
tumi kṛpā kori’, śraddhā-bindu diyā,
deho’ kṛṣṇa-nāma-dhane

kṛṣṇa se tomāra, kṛṣṇa dite pāro,
tomāra śakati āche
āmi to’ kāṅgāla, “kṛṣṇa” “kṛṣṇa” boli’,
dhāi tava pāche pāche
IDX.en_translation:
O revered Vaiṣnava Ṭhākura! O ocean of mercy! Please be merciful to me, your humble servant. Purify me by giving the shade of your lotus feet. Your feet I clasp submissively.

Please help me to control the six urges <footnote>(speech, mind, anger, tongue, belly, and genitals)</footnote>, purify me of the six faults <footnote>(overeating or collecting more funds than required, over-endeavoring for material objectives, unnecessarily talking of mundane affairs, being too attached or too neglectful of scriptural rules and regulations, associating with worldly minded people, and lusting after mundane achievements)</footnote>, and please instill in your servant the six good qualities <footnote>(enthusiasm, patience, confidence, determination to perform activities favorable to devotional service, the resolution to abandon the association of non-devotees, and the discipline required to always follow in the footsteps of predecessor ācāryas)</footnote>. Oh, bestow upon me the six kinds of holy association! <footnote>(giving and receiving gifts, accepting and offering spiritual food, revealing one’s inner thoughts, and posing confidential questions regarding devotional service)</footnote> I am seated here in the hope of having your company.

On my own, I find I have no strength to chant the holy name of Lord Hari. Please be merciful by awarding me with a particle of faith and giving me the great treasure of the holy name of Kṛṣṇa.

Kṛṣṇa is yours, therefore you are able to give Him to others. This is certainly within your power. I am indeed wretched and fallen, simply running after you crying “Kṛṣṇa! Kṛṣṇa!”

IDX.first_line: ohe harinama tava mahima apara
IDX.search: ohe harinama tava mahima apara tava pade nati ami kari barabara gokulera mahotsava anandasagara tomara carane padi hoiya katara
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
ohe harināma, tava mahimā apāra
tava pade nati āmi kari bārabāra

gokulera mahotsava ānandasāgara !
tomāra caraṇe paḍi hoiyā kātara

tumi kṛṣṇa pūrṇavapu rasera nidāna
tava pade paḍi’ tava guṇa kari gāna

je kare tomāra pade ekānta āśraya
tā’ra ārtirāśi nāśa karaha niścaya

sarva aparādha tumi nāśa kara tā’ra
nāma⋅aparādhāvadhi nāśaha tāhāra

sarvadoṣa dhauta kari’ tāhāra hṛdaya⋅
siŋhāsane baisa tumi parama āśraya

atiramya cidghana⋅ānanda⋅mūrtimān
‘raso vai saḥ’ bali’ veda kare tuyā gāna

bhaktivinoda rūpa⋅gosvāmi⋅caraṇe
māgaye sarvadā nāma⋅sphūrti sarvakṣaṇe
IDX.en_translation:
O Holy Name, Your glories are boundless. I repeatedly offer my obeisances at Your feet.

You are the great festival and the ocean of bliss for Gokula. I fall at Your feet, full of longing.

You are Kṛṣṇa in Your complete form, the source of all spiritual mellows. Falling at Your feet, I sing Your qualities.

Whoever takes exclusive shelter at Your feet, You certainly destroy their many miseries.

You destroy all their offenses, and You even eradicate offenses to the Holy Name.

Washing away all faults from their heart, You sit on the throne of their heart, O supreme refuge.

You are supremely enchanting, the embodied form of concentrated spiritual bliss. The Vedas sing Your praises, proclaiming You to be the very essence of rasa.

Bhaktivinoda, at the feet of Rūpa Gosvāmī, always prays for the clear manifestation of the Holy Name at every moment.

IDX.first_line: ohe premera thakura gora
IDX.search: ohe premera thakura gora pranera jatana kiba kaba natha hayechi apana hara ki ara baliba je kajera tare enechile natha jagate amare etadina pare kahite se katha khede duhkhe hoi sara
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
ohe premera ṭhākura gorā
prāṇera jātanā kibā kaba, nātha!
hayechi āpana⋅hārā

ki āra baliba, je⋅kājera tare,
enechile nātha! jagate āmāre,
etadina pare kahite se⋅kathā
khede duḥkhe hoi sārā

tomāra bhajane nā janmila rati,
jaḍamohe matta sadā duramati⋅
viṣayīra kāche theke theke āmi
hoinu viṣayīpārā

ke āmi, kena je esechi ekhāne,
se⋅kathā kakhano nāhi bhāvi mane,
kakhano bhogera, kakhano tyāgera
chalanāya mana nāce

ki gati hoibe, kakhano bhāvi nā,
hari⋅bhakatera kācheo jāi nā,
hari⋅vimukhera kulakṣaṇa jata
āmātei saba āche

śrī⋅guru⋅kṛpāya bheṅgeche svapana,
bujhechi ekhana tumii āpana,
tava nija⋅jana parama bāndhava
saṁsāra⋅kārāgāre

āna nā bhajiba bhakta⋅pada vinu,
rātula caraṇe śaraṇa loinu,
uddhāraha nātha! māyā-jāla ha’te
e dāsera keśe dha’re

pātakīre tumi kṛpā kara nāki?
jagāi⋅mādhāi chila je pātakī,
tāhāte jenechi,⋅premera ṭhākura!
pātakīreo tāra tumi

āmi bhakti-hīna, dīna, akiñcana⋅
aparādhī⋅śire dāo du’caraṇa,
tomāra abhaya śrī-caraṇe cira⋅
śaraṇa loinu āmi
IDX.en_translation:
O Lord of love! O Lord Gaura⋅candra! How shall I express the anguish of my soul, O Lord? I have lost my very self.

What more can I say? I have forgotten the very purpose for which You brought me into this world. To speak of it now, after so long, fills me with regret and sorrow.

No attachment for Your worship has arisen in me; my evil mind is always intoxicated by material illusion. By staying near materialistic people repeatedly, I have become like them.

Who am I? Why have I come here? I never think about these things. Sometimes enjoying and sometimes renouncing, my mind only dances in deception.

What will be my fate? I never consider it, nor do I seek the company of Your devotees. All the inauspicious signs of one who is averse to the Lord are present within me.

By the mercy of my spiritual master, the dream is broken. Now I understand that only You are my own; Your devotees are my only true friends in this prison house of material existence.

I shall not worship anyone but Your devotees; I take refuge in Your lotus feet. Deliver me, O Lord, from the net of illusion by grabbing this servant by the hair!

Do You not show mercy even to sinners? Jagāi and Mādhāi were great sinners, yet I have learned that You, the Lord of love, deliver even such as them.

I am devoid of devotion, fallen and insignificant. Please place Your two feet on the head of this offender. At Your fearless feet I take refuge forever.

IDX.first_line: omityuccarato mohanidra duram palayate
IDX.search: omityuccarato mohanidra duram palayate taya grastam jagannatha trahi mam madhusudana na gatirvidyate natha tvameva saranam mama papa panke nimagnosmi trahi mam madhusudana
IDX.author: śrīla śukadeva gosvāmī
IDX.verses:
omityuccārato mohanidrā dūraṁ palāyate
tayā grastaṁ jagannātha! trāhi māṁ madhusūdana

na gatirvidyate nātha! tvameva śaraṇaṁ mama
pāpa⋅paṅke nimagno‛smi trāhi māṁ madhusūdana

mohito‛jñāna⋅tamasā putra⋅dāra gṛhādiṣu
tṛṣṇayā pīḍyamāno‛smi trāhi māṁ madhusūdana!

bhaktihīna dīnañca duḥkha⋅śokāturaṁ prabho!
anāśrayamanāthañca trāhi māṁ madhusūdana!

gatāgatena śrānto‛smi dīrgha⋅saṁsāra⋅vartmasu
purnanāgantumicchāmi trāhi māṁ madhusūdana!

vahavo hi mayā dṛṣṭvā joni dvāraḥ pṛthak pṛthak
garbhavāsa⋅mahāduḥkhāt trāhi māṁ madhusūdana!

tena deva! prapanno‛smi trāṇārthastvat⋅parāyaṇaḥ
duḥkhārṇava nimagno‛haṁ trāhi māṁ madhusūdana!

vācā jacca pratijñātaṁ karmaṇā nopapāditaṁ
tatpāpāvdhinimagno‛smi trāhi māṁ madhusūdana!

sukṛtaṁ na kṛtaṁ kiñcidduṣkṛtañca kṛtaṁ mayā
saṁsārārṇava⋅magno‛smi trāhi māṁ madhusūdana!

dehāntara⋅sahaśreṣu prāpitaṁ bhramatā mayā
tiryaktva mānuṣatvañca trāhi māṁ madhusūdana!

vācayāmi jathonmattaḥ pralapāmi tavāgrataḥ
jarāmaraṇa⋅bhīto‛smi trāhi māṁ madhusūdana!

jatra jatra ca jāto‛smi strīṣu vā puruṣeṣu vā
dehi tatrācalāṁ bhakti trāhi māṁ madhusūdana!

gatvā gatvā nivartante candra⋅sūryādayo grahāḥ
kadāpi na nivartante dvādaśākṣara⋅cinantakāḥ

santi stotrāṇi vahavo vāñchitārtha pradāni vai
dvādaśārṇāt paraṁ nāsti vāsudevena bhāṣitaṁ

dvādaśārṇat mahāstotraṁ sarva⋅kāma⋅phala⋅pradaṁ
garbhavāsa⋅nirāsāya śukena paribhāṣitaṁ

dvādaśārṇaṁ nirāhāro jaḥ paṭhet harivāsare
sa gacched vaiṣṇavaṁ dhāma jatra jogeśvara hariḥ

iti śrīla⋅śukadeva⋅gosvāmi⋅viracitaṁ
śrīmadhusūdana⋅stotraṁ sampūrṇaṁ
IDX.en_translation:
By the utterance of "Om", the sleep of illusion flees far away. O Lord of the Universe! I am seized by that. Save me, O Madhusūdana!

There is no other goal, O Lord! You alone are my shelter. I am drowned in the mud of sin. Save me, O Madhusūdana!

Bewildered by the darkness of ignorance, absorbed in son, wife, home, etc., I am tormented by thirst. Save me, O Madhusūdana!

Devoid of devotion and destitute, afflicted with misery and grief, O Lord! Shelterless and helpless, save me, O Madhusūdana!

I am exhausted by coming and going on the long paths of material existence. I do not wish to come again. Save me, O Madhusūdana!

Many doors of the womb have been seen by me, one by one. From the great misery of residence in the womb, save me, O Madhusūdana!

Therefore, O God! I am surrendered, devoted to You for the sake of deliverance. I am drowned in the ocean of misery. Save me, O Madhusūdana!

Whatever was promised by speech was not accomplished by action. I am drowned in that ocean of sin. Save me, O Madhusūdana!

No pious deed was done by me, but sin was done. I am drowned in the ocean of material existence. Save me, O Madhusūdana!

Obtained by me wandering in thousands of other bodies—animal-ness and human-ness—save me, O Madhusūdana!

I speak like a madman, I prattle before You. I am afraid of old age and death. Save me, O Madhusūdana!

Wherever I have been born, among women or among men, give me unalloyed devotion there. Save me, O Madhusūdana!

Having gone, having gone, the moon, sun, and other planets return. Those who think of the Twelve Syllables never return.

There are many Stotras granting desired objects indeed. There is nothing superior to the Twelve Syllables spoken by Vāsudeva.

The great Stotra of the Twelve Syllables, bestowing the fruit of all desires, was spoken by Śuka for the removal of residence in the womb.

He who recites the Twelve Syllables without food on the day of Hari goes to the abode of Viṣṇu where Hari, the Lord of Yoga, is.

Thus ends the Śrī Madhusūdana Stotra composed by Śrīla Śukadeva Gosvāmī.

IDX.first_line: ore bhai krsna se e tina loka bandhu
IDX.search: ore bhai krsna se e tina loka bandhu jiva nija karme bandha mayate padiya andha uddharite karunara sindhu nija sakti gunagana saba name samarpana nyunadhika nahika vicara nama nami bheda nai namera gune nami pai nama kare helaya uddhara
IDX.author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
ore bhāi! kṛṣṇa se e tina⋅loka⋅bandhu
jīva nija⋅karme bandha, māyāte paḍiyā andha,
uddhārite karuṇāra sindhu

nija⋅śakti⋅guṇagaṇa, saba ‘nāme’ samarpaṇa,
nyūnādhika nāhika vicāra
nāma⋅nāmī bheda nāi, nāmera guṇe nāmī pāi,
nāma kare helāya uddhāra

nāhi kālākāla tā’ra, śuci ki aśuci āra,
nāma laite niṣedha nā ithe
ki mora durdaiva hāya, hena se dayālu⋅pāya,
anurāga nā janmila tā’te

ore mana! pāye paḍi, asat prayāsa chāḍi’,
‘kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa’ kaha anukṣaṇa
e baḍa sulabha ati, nāme jadi kara prīti,
tabe premānandera nandana
IDX.en_translation:
O brothers! Lord Kṛṣṇa is the only friend of these three worlds. The individual soul is bound by its own past actions and is fallen into illusion and blinded. He is an ocean of mercy to deliver us.

The Lord has surrendered all His own potencies and qualities to His Name; there is no consideration of more or less. There is no difference between the Name and the Lord Himself; by the qualities of the Name we obtain the Lord, for the Name easily delivers us.

There is no consideration of time or place for it, nor of purity or impurity; there is no prohibition in chanting the Name. Alas! What is my misfortune! At the feet of such a merciful Lord, no attachment has arisen in me.

O my mind! I fall at your feet—abandoning all useless endeavors, chant "Kṛṣṇa! Kṛṣṇa!" constantly. This is very easily obtained; if you develop love for the Name, then you will experience true divine love and bliss.

IDX.first_line: ore mana ara ki hoibe hena janma
IDX.search: ore mana ara ki hoibe hena janma na jani ki punya phale manusa uttama kule hele jara na bujhile marma dekha ayu saŋkhya jata nidrate ardheka gata cauthi roga soka apakatha cauthi vidya dhane mane kama krodha durvasane hasya kautuke gela vrtha
IDX.author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
ore mana! āra ki hoibe hena janma
nā jāni ki puṇya⋅phale, mānuṣa⋅uttama⋅kule,
hele jā’ra nā bujhile marma

dekha āyu⋅saŋkhyā jata, nidrāte ardheka gata,
cauṭhi roga⋅śoka⋅apakathā
cauṭhi vidyā⋅dhane⋅māne, kāma⋅krodha⋅durvāsane,
hāsya⋅kautuke gela vṛthā

satya⋅tretā⋅dvāparete, bahu āyu chila tāte,
vinā saŋkhyā⋅pūrṇa mṛtyu nāi
kata kari’ pariśrama, ācarita juga⋅dharma,
dhyāna jajñārcana bhari āi

ebe kali alpa āi, śateka vatsara bhāi,
seha dṛḍha nahe nirūpaṇa
tā goṅāli michā kāje, ki balibi kon lāje,
jabe tore sudhābe śamana

emana sulabha kali, jāte ‘hare kṛṣṇa’ bali,
hena nāme nā karili rati
premānanda kahe puni, e caurāśīlakṣa joni,
bhramāibe kateka durgati
IDX.en_translation:
O my mind! Will such a birth ever happen again? I do not know by what great piety you obtained this excellent human birth, but you have negligently failed to understand its true purpose.

Observe that half of your life is spent in sleep, and a fourth is lost to disease, sorrow, and useless talk. Another fourth is consumed by the pursuit of learning, wealth, and honor, or lost to lust, anger, and evil desires. Thus, it passes in vain.

In previous ages, life was very long and death did not come prematurely. Much effort was made then to perform the duties of the age through meditation, sacrifice, and worship.

But in the Age of Kali, life is short—perhaps a hundred years, and even that is not certain. Still, you waste it in useless activities. What will you say, and with what shame, when the God of Death questions you?

This Age of Kali is so beneficial because one can simply chant "Hare Kṛṣṇa!" yet you have not developed love for such a Name. Premānanda says: "The cycle of eight million four hundred thousand species will make you wander through endless misfortune."

IDX.first_line: ore mana bhaviya na bujha apanake
IDX.search: ore mana bhaviya na bujha apanake jara lagi duhkha kara svadese videse phira se jana ki sukha dibe toke javat samarthya ache tavat tomara kache javat aniya deha artha jakhana se gandha nai dakile na sune bhai na puche dekhile asamartha
IDX.author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
ore mana! bhāviyā nā bujha āpanāke
jā’ra lāgi’ duḥkha kara, svadeśe videśe phira,
se⋅jana ki sukha dibe toke

jāvat sāmarthya āche, tāvat tomāra kāche,
jāvat āniyā deha artha
jakhana se gandha nāi, ḍākile nā śune bhāi,
nā puche, dekhile asamartha

avasthā dekhiyā hāse, bhāla kathā manda vāse,
bā̐kā⋅mukhe o nāka⋅tolāi
kṣudhāya nā deya bhāta, tā’te āra kaṭu vāta,
kahe eki hoila bālāi

dine dine khāṭa rati, kise āra pitā pati,
parijane nā kara baḍāi
jebā āge joḍa⋅hāte, tā’rā śunāya nirghāte,
e samaye bandhu ke re bhāi

parake āpana kari, bhebe mali janma bhari,
ke tumi, tomāra āche kebā ?
premānanda kahe mati, ‘kṛṣṇa’ vinā nāhi gati,
kaha ‘kṛṣṇa’⋅e duḥkha taribā
IDX.en_translation:
O my mind! Reflect deeply and understand your situation. The ones for whom you grieve, wandering everywhere—how will those people ever give you true happiness?

As long as you have strength and provide wealth, they are with you. But when that usefulness is gone, if you call, they won't even listen, brother; they won't even inquire about you when they see you are helpless.

Seeing your plight, they will laugh, and your good words will seem bitter to them. With a crooked face and an upturned nose, they won't even give you food when you are hungry, and they will add harsh words, saying: "What a nuisance this person has become!"

Day by day, their affection for you will wane. What good are family members then? Do not rely on them. Those who once stood with folded hands will now speak harshly, saying: "Who is your friend at this time, O brother?"

Treating others as your own, you have wasted your entire life. Who are you, and who truly belongs to you? Premānanda says: "Without Lord Kṛṣṇa, there is no refuge. Say 'Kṛṣṇa!' and you will overcome this sorrow."

IDX.first_line: ore mana dhana jana jivana yauvana
IDX.search: ore mana dhana jana jivana yauvana ei ache ei nai cakse kina dekha tai tumi kise balicha apana nisira svapana jena e dhana sampada tena tileke sakali bhai miche dekhiya na dekha kene suniya na suna kane ki lagi chadite nara icche
IDX.author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
