ore mana! dhana⋅jana⋅jīvana⋅yauvana
ei āche ei nāi, cakṣe kinā dekha tāi,
tumi kise balicha āpana

niśira svapana jena, e dhana⋅sampada tena,
tileke sakali bhāi miche
dekhiyā nā dekha kene, śuniyā nā śuna kāṇe,
ki lāgi chāḍite nāra icche

kanyā putra jata ithi, marile se jāya kathi,
ki jāni kothāya tumi jāo
mithyā ‘mora mora’ kara, rātridina bhāvi’ mara,
para lāgi’ āpanā hārāo

kibā āra anya para, āpanā e kalevara,
se nāhi tomāra saṅge jāya
pāchu nāhi dekha evā, tora lāgi kānde kebā,
kāra lāgi kara hāya hāya

jebā hoiyāche āyu, se mātra nāsāra vāyu,
sariyā paḍile āra nāi
kibā vṛddha kibā vāla, nāhi tāra kālākāla,
kothā thāke jauvana⋅baḍāi

e⋅sakala jā̐ra māyā, tā̐re kena bhula bhāyā,
jā̐ra nāme tribhuvana tare
premānanda kahe jadi, ‘kṛṣṇa’ kaha niravadhi,
tabe ki e jana āra mare
IDX.en_translation:
O my mind! Wealth, family, life, and youth—they are here one moment and gone the next. Do you not see this with your own eyes? How then can you call them your own?

These wealth and possessions are just like a dream in the night. In an instant, O brother, it is all revealed to be false. Why do you see yet remain blind? Why do you hear yet remain deaf? For what reason can you not abandon your worldly desires?

All your children—where will they go when you die? And who knows where you yourself will go? Falsely you say "mine, mine," and you worry and die in such thoughts. For the sake of others, you lose yourself.

What to speak of others? Even this very body of yours does not go with you. Do you not see what remains behind? Who will truly cry for you? For whom do you lament so much?

Whatever life you have is merely the breath in your nostrils. Once it leaves, it is no more. Whether you are old or young, there is no fixed time for death. Where then remains the pride of youth?

All of this is the illusion of Lord Kṛṣṇa. Why do you forget Him, O brother? By His Name, the three worlds are delivered. Premānanda says: "If you chant the Name of Kṛṣṇa constantly, then how can you ever really die?"

IDX.first_line: ore mana dhikre tomaya
IDX.search: ore mana dhikre tomaya paiya manusya janma na cintile krsna karma vrtha janma gelare khelaya kateka sukrti phale manusa uttama kule tahate bharatavarse janma dhanya kaliyuga tate sri krsnacaitanya jate prakasila nama matra dharma
IDX.author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
ore mana dhikre tomāya
pāiyā manuṣya janma, nā cintile kṛṣṇa karma,
vṛthā janma gelare khelāya

kateka sukṛti⋅phale, mānuṣa uttama kule,
tāhāte bhāratavarṣe janma
dhanya kaliyuga tā’te, śrī⋅kṛṣṇacaitanya jā’te,
prakāśilā nāma mātra dharma

pāye dhari chāḍa bhrama, kichu nāhi pariśrama,
kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa kaha avirāma
kaha lakṣa kathā āna, tāhe nā āsila jñāna,
ki bhāra ki bojhā kṛṣṇa⋅nāma

e jadi nā śuna bhāi, tabe āra gati nāi,
hena janma nā hoibe āra
kahe premānanda ebe, nā bhaja śrī⋅kṛṣṇa tabe,
koṭi kalpe nāhika nistāra
IDX.en_translation:
O my mind! Shame on you! Having received a human birth, you did not think of the service of Lord Kṛṣṇa; your life passed in vain in mere play.

Due to the results of many pious deeds, you were born as a human in a noble family, and furthermore, you were born in the sacred land of Bhārata. Blessed is this Age of Kali, where Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya revealed the religious practice of only the Holy Name.

I beg you, give up your illusion—there is no great effort involved. Just constantly chant "Kṛṣṇa! Kṛṣṇa!" You speak a million other things, but no knowledge comes from them. What is the burden, what is the weight of Kṛṣṇa's Name?

If you do not listen to this, O brother, then there is no other path. Such a birth will not come again. Śrīla Premānanda Dāsa says: "If you do not worship Lord Kṛṣṇa now, then even in millions of cosmic ages there will be no liberation."

IDX.first_line: ore mana ebara bujhiba bharibhuri
IDX.search: ore mana ebara bujhiba bharibhuri kupiyache surya suta bandhibe tahara duta jena phira asatai kari jadi mora bola dhara tabe moke raksa kara jadi jaya karibe samana krsnanama gada kari sadhugana sura bhari tara majhe raha anuksana
IDX.author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
ore mana! ebāra bujhiba bhāribhuri
kupiyāche sūrya⋅suta, bāndhibe tāhāra dūta,
jena phira asatāi kari

jadi mora bola dhara, tabe moke rakṣā kara,
jadi jaya karibe śamana
kṛṣṇa-nāma gaḍa kari’, sādhugaṇa⋅śūra bhari’,
tāra mājhe raha anukṣaṇa

tribhuvane jei ālā, tilaka, tulasī⋅mālā,
dṛḍha kari’ dhara āguyāna
dekhi he̐ṭa kari’ māthā, sasainye se jamabhrātā,
bhaṅga diyā karibe prasthāne

śrī⋅guru⋅karuṇā⋅chāyā, candrātapa ṭāṅgāiyā,
vasi’ thāka ānanda⋅hṛdaya
kṛṣṇa⋅nityadāsa bali, sarvatra phirāo ḍhuli,
premānanda kahe kāre bhaya
IDX.en_translation:
O my mind! Now you will understand the gravity of your situation. The God of Death is angered, and his messengers will bind you to correct your unrighteousness.

If you heed my words, then save yourself if you wish to conquer death. Make the Name of Lord Kṛṣṇa your fortress, fill yourself with the valor of the saintly, and remain there constantly.

Hold firmly to the light of the three worlds—the tilaka markings and the necklace of Tulasī beads. Seeing these, the messengers of death will bow their heads and retreat.

Remain seated with a joyful heart under the canopy of your Spiritual Master's merciful shade. Proclaiming everywhere that you are the eternal servant of Lord Kṛṣṇa, beat the drum of victory. Premānanda says: "Who then is there to fear?"

IDX.first_line: ore mana ebe tora e kemana rita
IDX.search: ore mana ebe tora e kemana rita je karme aili hetha se saba rahila kotha ebe je dekhiye viparita krsna karma lagi kara tahe kena barbvara se kare parera vitta hara se avasa nahe kene ki susara bahu dane tahe ara kara va na kara
IDX.author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
ore mana! ebe tora e kemana rīta
je karme āili hethā, se⋅saba rahila kothā,
ebe je dekhiye viparīta

kṛṣṇa⋅karma lāgi’ kara, tāhe kena barbvara,
se⋅kare parera vitta hara
se avaśa nahe kene, ki susāra bahu dāne,
tāhe āra kara vā nā kara

mukhe ka’be ‘hṛṣīkeśa’, tāhe jadi sādhu⋅dveṣa,
tabe bakramukha kene noo
agni diyā hena mukha, poḍā’le nā ghuce dukha,
tāhe ‘kṛṣṇa’ koo vā nā koo

bhramite kṛṣṇera tīrtha, padera nā ehi kṛtya,
tāhe jadi para⋅dāre cala
ki kāja padera ei, paṅgu kene nahe sei,
tabe tīrthe gela vā nā gela

kṛṣṇa⋅līlā⋅guṇa⋅kathā, karṇete śunibe jathā,
tāhe jadi ku⋅kathāya bhora
jadi āra sādhu⋅nindā, śuniyā bāḍaye śraddhā,
se kāṇa badhira hou tora

guru⋅kṛṣṇa⋅vaiṣṇava⋅mūrti, dekhibe kariyā ārti,
se jadi dekhaye para⋅dāre
asantoṣa sādhu dekhi’, kena vidhi hena ā̐khi,
āśu andha nā kare tāhāre

tumi kṛṣṇa⋅smṛti⋅kāje, janmilā saṁsāra⋅mājhe,
tāhā chāḍi’ dhane⋅jane āśa
tabe jīye kibā kāja, paḍuka tora muṇḍe bāja,
kene āra nahe sarvanāśa

premānanda kahe mana, ‘kṛṣṇa’ kaha anukṣaṇa,
kene bhula āpanāra prabhu
mukhe ‘hari hari’ bala, sadāi ānande dola,
tina⋅loke duḥkha nahe kabhu
IDX.en_translation:
O my mind! What kind of conduct is this of yours now? The spiritual deeds for which you came here—where have they all gone? Now you show only the opposite.

You are meant to engage in Lord Kṛṣṇa's service—why then are you so barbaric? You spend your time stealing the wealth of others. Why are you not subdued? What good is giving much in charity if your heart is not right?

With your mouth you say "Hṛṣīkeśa," but if there is hatred for the saintly in your heart, then why is your mouth not crooked? Even if such a mouth is burned with fire, the sorrow does not end, whether you say "Kṛṣṇa" or not.

To wander to the holy places of Lord Kṛṣṇa is the duty of your feet—but if with them you go to the wife of another, what is the use of those feet? Why are they not lame? Whether they went to holy places or not would then make no difference.

The pastimes and qualities of Lord Kṛṣṇa should be heard with the ears—but if instead you are engrossed in evil talk, or if by hearing blasphemy of the saintly your faith increases, then may those ears of yours be deaf.

You should look upon the forms of the Guru, Kṛṣṇa, and the devotees with earnest longing—but if instead your eyes look with lust at the wife of another, or if you look upon a saintly person with dissatisfaction, why does destiny not quickly make you blind?

You were born into this material world for the purpose of remembering Lord Kṛṣṇa, but leaving that aside, you only desire wealth and people. Then what is the use of living? May thunder strike your head; why is there not total destruction?

Premānanda says: "O my mind! Chant 'Kṛṣṇa!' always. Why do you forget your own Lord? Say 'Hari! Hari!' with your mouth and always sway in bliss—then there will never be any sorrow in the three worlds."

IDX.first_line: ore mana kena hena bujha viparita
IDX.search: ore mana kena hena bujha viparita dande pale ayuksaya tate tora bodha naya aise dina ithe harasita dine mase avde badha aiche janiyacha dadha ghate ke ta bujhite na para naye cadi caha kule dekha jena prthvi cale tumi je calicha ta na hera
IDX.author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
ore mana! kena hena bujha viparīta
daṇḍe pale āyukṣaya, tāte tora bodha naya,
āise dina ithe haraṣita

dine māse avde bāḍha, aiche jāniyācha daḍha,
ghāṭe ke, tā bujhite nā pāra
nāye caḍi’ cāha kule, dekha jena pṛthvī cale,
tumi je calicha tā nā hera

dhana jana āpanāra, se nā bhāviyācha sāra,
se ki tora, jāna nā se kāra
tileke kāḍiyā laya, jāre icchā tāre deya,
nahe tumi marileo tāra

vṛthā ahaṅkāre mara, vicāriyā pūrvāpara,
sādhujana⋅pathete dā̐ḍāo
manuṣya durlabha janma, kena kara apakarma,
kare ratna pāiyā phelāo

jāvata sāmarthya āche, jarā nā āsiche kāche,
‘kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa’ kaha avirāma
jarāya bhāṅgibe tanu, sarvendriya habe kṣīṇu,
tabe ki sphuribe kṛṣṇa-nāma

nahe vā kakhane jāi, kichu nirūpaṇa nāi,
tile eka nāhika viśvāsa
premānanda kahe bhāi, kaha ‘kṛṣṇa’, byāja nāi,
e jīvana kevala niśvāsa
IDX.en_translation:
O my mind! Why do you understand things so incorrectly? Moment by moment, your life is declining, but you do not perceive it; instead, you are happy as the days pass by.

You firmly believe that the days, months, and years are increasing for you, but you do not understand who is truly diminishing. Sitting in a boat, you look at the shore and think the earth is moving, but you do not see that it is actually you who are moving.

You consider your wealth and family to be your own and essential to you. But are they truly yours? You do not even know to whom they really belong. In a moment they can be taken away and given to someone else; even after you die, they will not belong to you.

You are dying in vain pride—consider your past and future and stand on the path of the saintly. This human birth is rare; why do you commit sinful acts? It is like throwing away a jewel after finally finding it in your hand.

As long as you have strength and old age has not yet approached, chant "Kṛṣṇa! Kṛṣṇa!" incessantly. In old age, your body will break and all your senses will weaken; will the Name of Kṛṣṇa then appear in your mind?

No one knows when death will come; there is no certainty at all. There is not a single moment of assurance. Premānanda says: "O brother! Chant 'Kṛṣṇa!' without any delay, for this life is merely a single breath."

IDX.first_line: ore mana ki bhaya samane kari ara
IDX.search: ore mana ki bhaya samane kari ara jadi krsnapade rati ki karibe pitr pati iha kene na kara vicara je pada bharasa kari brahma srsti adhikari je pada vanchaye pancanana je pade gangara janma laksmi jane jara marma aharnisi smare anuksana
IDX.author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
ore mana! ki bhaya śamane kari āra
jadi kṛṣṇa-pade rati, ki karibe pitṛ⋅pati,
ihā kene nā kara vicāra

je pada bharasā kari’, brahmā sṛṣṭi⋅adhikārī,
je⋅pada vāñchaye pañcānana
je pade gaṅgāra janma, lakṣmī jāne jā’ra marma,
aharniśi smare anukṣaṇa

dhruva⋅ādi je prasāde, jogīndra dharaye hṛde,
munigaṇa je⋅pada dheyāya
draupadī prahlāda kari, je⋅pada hṛdaye smari,
dekha kata saṅkaṭa eḍāya

jadi kara nija kāja, mitra habe dharmarāja,
vṛthā cinta asāra⋅saṁsāra
kahe dīna premānanda, cinta kṛṣṇa⋅padadvandva,
tribhuvane śatru nahe āra
IDX.en_translation:
O my mind! What further fear do you have of the God of Death? If you have deep attachment for the lotus feet of Lord Kṛṣṇa, what can the Lord of the Dead do to you? Why do you not consider this?

Relying on those lotus feet, Lord Brahmā became the master of creation. Those same feet are desired by the five-faced Lord Śiva. From those feet the River Ganges was born, and the Goddess of Fortune knows their true essence and remembers them day and night.

By the grace of those feet, Dhruva Mahārāja and the great kings among the yogīs hold them in their hearts, and all the sages meditate upon them. Consider how many dangers were averted by Draupadī and Prahlāda Mahārāja just by remembering those lotus feet.

If you perform your true duty of devotion, the King of Death will become your friend. You are worrying uselessly about this worthless material world. The humble Premānanda says: "Meditate upon the twin lotus feet of Lord Kṛṣṇa, and you will have no more enemies in the three worlds."

IDX.first_line: ore mana ki rase haiya raili bhora
IDX.search: ore mana ki rase haiya raili bhora ki baliya eli setha ki kaja va kara hetha tileka cetana nahi tora putra dara sampada jivana yauvana mada je kara sakali asara jalabimba kataksana temati janiha mana tribhuvane krsna matra sara
IDX.author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
ore mana! ki rase haiyā raili bhora
ki baliyā eli sethā, ki kāja vā kara hethā,
tileka cetana nāhi tora

putra dārā⋅sampada, jīvana⋅yauvana⋅mada,
je kara, sakali asāra
jalabimba katakṣaṇa, temati jāniha mana,
tribhuvane ‘kṛṣṇa’ mātra sāra

je dina je gela jāya, jā āche sāmālo tāya,
kāladūta dā̐ḍāiyā pathe
chāḍiyā anyathā kāma, bala ‘rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa’ nāma,
kabhu dekhā nā habe tā⋅sāthe

ājñākārī brahmā⋅hara, śamana⋅kiṅkara āra,
sura⋅muni je pada dheyāya
hena kṛṣṇa-pada chāḍi’, gale diyā māyādaḍi,
duḥkha deha kena re āmāya

premānanda kahe bhāi, ‘kṛṣṇa’ vinā gati nāi,
bhaja kṛṣṇa⋅caraṇāravinde
saṁsāra⋅sāgare paḍi’, kena kara kāḍubāḍi,
kaha ‘kṛṣṇa’ taribe ānande
IDX.en_translation:
O my mind! In what pleasure have you remained immersed? For what reason did you come to this world, and what work are you doing here? You do not have even a moment of awareness.

Children, family, wealth, life, and the pride of youth—whatever you pursue, all of it is worthless. How long does a bubble on the water last? Know that everything is like that. In the three worlds, only Lord Kṛṣṇa is the true essence.

Whatever days have passed are gone; take care of what remains. The messenger of death is standing on the path. Abandoning all other desires, chant the Names of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa, and then you will never have to meet that messenger.

Lord Brahmā and Lord Śiva are obedient to Him, as are the servants of death. The demigods and sages meditate upon His feet. Abandoning such lotus feet of Lord Kṛṣṇa and putting the rope of illusion around your neck, why do you give me so much sorrow?

Premānanda says: "O brother! Without Lord Kṛṣṇa there is no refuge. Worship the lotus feet of Lord Kṛṣṇa. Having fallen into the ocean of material existence, why do you make such a fuss? Chant 'Kṛṣṇa!' and you will cross over with joy."

IDX.first_line: ore mana kiba tumi vicari na cao
IDX.search: ore mana kiba tumi vicari na cao krsna bhuli ei papa teha tora tina tapa nana joni bhramiya bedao tumi krsna nityadasa kotha gela se abhyasa dhana jana made haiya andhe vinamulye matha pati dasa haye khao lathi sraddhate vasana diya kandhe
IDX.author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
ore mana! kibā tumi vicāri nā cāo
‘kṛṣṇa’ bhuli ei pāpa, te̐ha tora tina tāpa,
nānā joni bhramiyā beḍāo

tumi kṛṣṇa⋅nityadāsa, kothā gela se abhyāsa,
dhana⋅jana⋅made haiyā āndhe
vināmūlye māthā pāti, dāsa haye khāo lāthi,
śraddhāte vasana diyā kāndhe

ei mora sadā dhanda, kaha lakṣa kathā manda,
‘kṛṣṇa⋅nāma’ loite ālisa
thākite rasanā tuṇḍa, jāo kena naraka⋅kuṇḍa,
ihā haite ke āra bāliśa

vṛthā tabe nara⋅tanu, śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅bhajana vinu,
kemane pāmara jīte cāya
kṛṣṇa vinā koṭiyuga, jīyei vā kon sukha,
se jīvana pātharera prāya

ebāra mānuṣa⋅deha, āra ki hoibe eha,
bhaja kṛṣṇa, chāḍa anācāra
dekha saba nāśā⋅phā̐dā, kevala anartha⋅dhā̐dhā,
asamaye haya kebā kā’ra

premānanda kahe mana, ‘kṛṣṇa’ kaha anukṣaṇa,
āpanāra tattve hoo daḍha
saṁsāra⋅vāsanā⋅garta, kīṭa⋅krimimaya kata,
dekhiyā śuniyā kena paḍa
IDX.en_translation:
O my mind! Why do you not consider this? Forgetting Lord Kṛṣṇa is the root of all sin; it brings you the three-fold miseries and makes you wander through various species.

You are an eternal servant of Lord Kṛṣṇa—where has that understanding gone? Becoming blind with the intoxication of wealth and family, you bow your head to illusion without any gain, becoming its slave and receiving only kicks.

This is my constant confusion: you speak a million useless words, but you are lazy to utter the Name of Kṛṣṇa. While having a tongue in your mouth, why do you choose to go to the pit of hell? Who could be more foolish than this?

This human body is useless without the worship of Lord Kṛṣṇa. How can a wretched soul even desire to live like this? Without Lord Kṛṣṇa, what happiness is there in living for millions of ages? Such a life is like a stone.

You have this human body now—will you ever get it again? Worship Lord Kṛṣṇa and give up all unrighteousness. See all these destructive snares and useless puzzles of this world. In the end, who will truly belong to whom?

Premānanda says: "O my mind! Chant the Name of Kṛṣṇa constantly and become firm in your own spiritual identity. Why do you fall into the pit of material desires, full of worms and insects, even after seeing and hearing the truth?"

IDX.first_line: ore mana kise kara dehera gumana
IDX.search: ore mana kise kara dehera gumana maile dehera je avastha naha ki tahara jnata dekhiye suniye nahe jnana bhusane bhusita jei paciye padibe sei podaye karibe nahe chai kukkura sakuni siva bediye khaibe kiba kimva krmi iha ki edai
IDX.author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
ore mana! kise kara dehera gumāna
maile dehera je⋅avasthā, naha ki tāhāra jñātā,
dekhiye śuniye nahe jñāna

bhūṣaṇe bhūṣita jei, paciye paḍibe sei,
poḍā’ye karibe nahe chāi
kukkura⋅śakuni⋅śivā, beḍiye khāibe kibā,
kimvā kṛmi, ihā ki eḍāi

satye lakṣya⋅varṣa jā’rā, keha nāhi āche tā’rā,
ebe kali ki āyu tomāra
carācara dekha jata, sakali hoibe hata,
dhana⋅jana⋅sampada āra

‘kṛṣṇa’ haite janma tora, māyāte bhuliyā bhora,
curi⋅dārī pravañca⋅vacane
āpana⋅uddhāra⋅pathe, tile dṛṣṭi nāhi tā’te,
narakera hetu rātri⋅dine

cāri juge tribhuvane, bhūta⋅bhaviṣya⋅vartamāne,
satya satya ‘harināma’ sāra
smṛti chāḍi’ hari-pade, ḍubile saṁsāra⋅made,
e sukha luṭibe jama⋅dvāra

kahe premānanda⋅dāsa, dante tṛṇa, gale vāsa,
‘hari hari’ kaha ore bhāi
jadi ‘hari’ bala vaktre, phukāra karaye śāstre,
tribhuvane tāra sama nāi
IDX.en_translation:
O my mind! Why are you so proud of this body? Do you not know what its state will be when you die? Have you gained no wisdom even after seeing and hearing the truth?

This body, though now adorned with ornaments, will eventually decay or be burnt to ashes. Dogs, vultures, and jackals will devour it, or it will be consumed by worms—this cannot be avoided.

Those who lived for a hundred thousand years in the first age are all gone now. What then is your lifespan in this Age of Kali? All that you see—moving or non-moving—will be destroyed, along with all wealth, people, and possessions.

Your origin is from Lord Kṛṣṇa, but bewildered by illusion, you spend your time in theft, adultery, and deceitful words. You have no concern for the path of your own deliverance, and your actions only lead to hell day and night.

In all four ages and in all three worlds, the only truth is the Holy Name of Lord Hari. Forgetting His lotus feet and drowning in the intoxication of material existence, your current happiness will be plundered at the door of death.

Premānanda Dāsa says: "With a straw in my teeth and a cloth around my neck in surrender, I beg you, brother, chant 'Hari! Hari!' If you utter 'Hari' with your mouth, the scriptures proclaim that there is no one equal to you in the three worlds."

IDX.first_line: ore mana krsna nama sama nahi ana
IDX.search: ore mana krsna nama sama nahi ana dharma karma tapa tyaga dhyana jnana vrata yaga kichu nahe namera samana je nama loite hara preme matta digambara valmiki hoila tapodhana ajamila papi chila namabhase tare gela putrake dakiya narayana
IDX.author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
ore mana! kṛṣṇa⋅nāma⋅sama nāhi āna
dharma⋅karma⋅tapa⋅tyāga, dhyāna⋅jñāna⋅vrata⋅yāga,
kichu nahe nāmera samāna

je nāma loite hara, preme matta digambara,
vālmīki hoila tapodhana
ajāmila pāpī chila, nāmābhāse tare gela,
putrake ḍākiyā nārāyaṇa

je⋅nāmera svāda peye, tambure phiraye geye,
devoṛṣi nārada⋅gosāi
satya-bhāmā vrata⋅chale, kṛṣṇa⋅saṅge kari’ tūle,
dekhāilā nāmera baḍāi

ananta sahasra mukhe, je nāma gāyena sukhe,
tabu to karite nāre sīmā
lakṣya kari’ arjunake, prabhu āpanāra mukhe,
kahechena nāmera mahimā

premānanda kahe mana, ‘kṛṣṇa’ bala anukṣaṇa,
durvāsanā chāḍiyā hṛdaya
preme ucca nāma kari’, avaśya pāibe hari,
nāma āra nāmī bhinna naya
IDX.en_translation:
O my mind! There is nothing equal to the Name of Lord Kṛṣṇa. Religious duties, pious activities, penance, renunciation, meditation, knowledge, vows, and sacrifices—none of these are equal to the Holy Name.

By taking that Name, Lord Śiva became intoxicated with love, and Vālmīki became a great sage. Ajāmila was a great sinner, but he was delivered even by a shadow of the Name when he called for his son, Nārāyaṇa.

Having tasted the sweetness of that Name, the divine sage Nārada Muni wanders about, singing while playing his vīṇā. Under the guise of a vow, Satyabhāmā weighed Lord Kṛṣṇa against the Holy Name and demonstrated its supreme greatness.

Lord Ananta Śeṣa, with His thousands of mouths, happily sings that Name, yet He can never reach its end. Addressing Arjuna, the Lord Himself spoke of the glory of the Name with His own mouth.

Premānanda says: "O my mind! Chant 'Kṛṣṇa!' constantly, abandoning all evil desires from your heart. By loudly chanting the Name with love, you will surely attain Lord Hari, for the Name and the Lord are not different."

IDX.first_line: ore mana nivedana sunaha amara
IDX.search: ore mana nivedana sunaha amara janmile marana ache kaladuta ache piche bhunjaibe karma anusare javata achaye ai krsna krsna kaha bhai kahi krsna sara apanake krsnanama je vadane se jitila tribhuvane ki bhaya samane ache take
IDX.author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
ore mana! nivedana śunaha āmāra
janmile maraṇa āche, kāla-dūta āche piche,
bhuñjāibe karma⋅anusāre

jāvata āchaye āi, ‘kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa’ kaha bhāi,
kahi ‘kṛṣṇa’ sāra’ āpanāke
kṛṣṇa-nāma je vadane, se jitila tribhuvane,
ki bhaya śamane āche tāke

jadi cinta nija⋅hita, sādhu⋅saṅge kara prīta,
asatsaṅga nā kariha kṣaṇe
kukkura⋅bhavane gele, asthi⋅carma khuba mile,
gaja⋅danta⋅muktā siŋha⋅sthāne

kṛṣṇa⋅nāma⋅līlā⋅guṇa⋅, śravaṇa⋅kīrtana, mana,
aśru⋅kampa⋅pulaka ānande
sādhu-saṅge sadā vasi’, vilasaha divāniśi,
tabe vāñchā pure premānande
IDX.en_translation:
O my mind! Please listen to my humble appeal. If you are born, you must die, and the messengers of death are always behind you. They will make you experience the results according to your past actions.

As long as you still have life, chant "Kṛṣṇa! Kṛṣṇa!" my brother. By chanting the Name, protect your soul. One on whose lips the Name of Kṛṣṇa resides has already conquered the three worlds; what fear can he have of the God of Death?

If you desire your own welfare, then develop affection for the company of the saintly. Do not associate with the unholy for even a single moment. If you go to a dog's house, you will find only bones and skin, but in a lion's place, you will find elephant tusks and pearls.

O my mind! With tears, trembling, and ecstatic joy, always hear and chant the Name, pastimes, and qualities of Lord Kṛṣṇa. Always sitting in the association of the saintly, enjoy these pastimes day and night; then your desires will be fulfilled in divine bliss.

IDX.first_line: ore mana ruci nahe kena krsna nama
IDX.search: ore mana ruci nahe kena krsna nama tabe jani purva janme ache kata papa karme se lagi vidhata tore bama jadi anya katha pao atiya satiya koo krsna nama loite alisa jadi suna krsna katha vajra jena pade matha ghume jhume tallasa balisa
IDX.author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
ore mana! ruci nahe kena kṛṣṇa⋅nāma
tabe jāni pūrva⋅janme, āche kata pāpa⋅karme,
se lāgi’ vidhātā tore bāma

jadi anya kathā pāo, ā̐ṭiyā sā̐ṭiyā koo,
‘kṛṣṇa⋅nāma’ loite ālisa
jadi śuna ‘kṛṣṇa⋅kathā’, vajra jena paḍe māthā,
ghume jhume tallāsa’ bāliśa

jadi haya asat⋅kathā, ghumete ciyāo tathā,
śunite bāḍaye kata rati
nīca⋅saṅge sadā vāsa, sādhu-jana dekhi’ hāsa,
kulaṭā vandiyā ninda satī

śrāddha⋅deva⋅adhikārī, bhāṅgibe e bhāribhuri,
āsi’ dūta loibe bāndhiyā
ki gumāna kara deha, paci’ gali’ jā’be eha,
dhana⋅jana rahibe paḍiyā

je⋅sukhe ha’yecha matta, bujhi’ dekha tā’ra tattva,
ihā tora rahibe kothāya?
āji mara, mara kāli, maraṇa e nahe gāli,
‘kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa’ kaha, dina jāya

je⋅kaile se⋅kaile mana, ebe hoo sacetana,
phire baisa ke tore hārāya
kahe premānanda sukhe, ‘rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa’ bala mukhe,
śamana jiniyā uṭha nāya
IDX.en_translation:
O my mind! Why do you have no taste for the Name of Lord Kṛṣṇa? I know that due to many sinful actions in your previous births, your destiny is unfavorable toward you.

If you find other topics to discuss, you speak with great effort, but you are lazy to utter the Name of Kṛṣṇa. If you hear topics about Lord Kṛṣṇa, it feels as if a thunderbolt has struck your head, and you immediately search for a pillow to sleep.

If there is unrighteous talk, you are wide awake and listen with great attachment. You always reside with the wicked and laugh at the saintly. You praise the unchaste and criticize those who are faithful.

The master of funeral rites will eventually break this illusion when his messengers come to bind you. What pride do you have in this body? It will only rot and decompose, while your wealth and family will remain behind.

The happiness you are currently intoxicated with—understand its true nature. Where will it be for you in the end? You may die today or tomorrow; death is not just an insult, it is a reality. Chant "Kṛṣṇa! Kṛṣṇa!" as the days pass by.

Whatever you have done in the past, O mind, now become conscious. Return to the right path—who then can defeat you? Premānanda says: "Chant 'Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa!' with your mouth, conquer death, and rise up!"

IDX.first_line: ore mana sadhu sange karaha vasati
IDX.search: ore mana sadhu sange karaha vasati jadi karmapasa bandhe magana karaye andhe jadi kula vihina utpati jadi pasu paksi krmi janmiya janmiya bhrami satata karaya gatagati jemana temana sthane grhe va parvate vane kaha kene na haya vasati
IDX.author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
ore mana! sādhu saṅge karaha vasati
jadi karmapāśa⋅bandhe, magana karaye andhe,
jadi kula⋅vihīna utpati

jadi paśu⋅pakṣī⋅kṛmi, janmiyā janmiyā bhrami,
satata karāya gatāgati
jemana temana sthāne, gṛhe vā parvate vane,
kā̐hā kene nā haya vasati

thāke jena ei sūtra, dṛḍha⋅cita ei mātra,
śrī⋅haricaraṇe rati⋅mati
ghucibe sakala dukha, pāibe aśeṣa sukha,
bujhi kara śrī⋅hari⋅bhakati

dharma⋅karma⋅jñāna⋅yoga, svarga⋅mokṣa⋅bhukti⋅bhoga,
‘kṛṣṇa⋅sevānanda’⋅ihā vine
jadi ithe kona kṣaṇa, bāndha tāya āmāya mana,
tabe jena haya ta’ maraṇe

‘rādhā’ ‘kṛṣṇa’ du’ṭī nāma, jihvā jena avirāma,
du̐hu⋅guṇa⋅līlāte śravaṇa
kahe premānanda dīne, du̐hu cintā anukṣaṇe,
rūpe jena thākaye nayana
IDX.en_translation:
O my mind! Live always in the company of the saintly. Even if you are blindly bound by the results of your past actions, and even if you are born into a family without any prestige.

Even if you are born repeatedly as an animal, a bird, or an insect, wandering constantly through the cycle of birth and death—wherever you may be, whether in a home, on a mountain, or in a forest, always seek the company of the saintly.

Let this be your only firm principle: to maintain love and devotion for the lotus feet of Lord Hari. Then all your miseries will cease, and you will attain endless happiness. Understanding this, perform devotional service to Lord Hari.

Religious duties, fruitive actions, speculative knowledge, mystic yoga, heaven, liberation, and material enjoyment—if my mind is bound to any of these for even a single moment instead of the bliss of serving Lord Kṛṣṇa, then let that moment be my death.

May the two Names "Rādhā" and "Kṛṣṇa" be upon my tongue without ceasing, and may my ears always hear of Their qualities and pastimes. Premānanda humbly says: "May I constantly meditate upon Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa, and may my eyes always be fixed upon Their divine forms."

IDX.first_line: ore mana suna suna tu ati barbara
IDX.search: ore mana suna suna tu ati barbara sata sandhi jarajara peye ei kalevara kiba garva karicha antara trayatmika vyadhi jata bediya achaye kata ki jani kakhana keba nase e ami amara bali nija prabhu pasarili samanakinkara dekhi hase
IDX.author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
ore mana! śuna śuna tu ati barbara
śata⋅sandhi⋅jarajara, peye ei kalevara,
kibā garva karicha antara

trayātmikā vyādhi jata, beḍiyā āchaye kata,
ki jāni kakhana kebā nāśe
‘e āmi āmāra’ bali, nija⋅prabhu pāsarili,
śamana-kiṅkara dekhi’ hāse

je deha āpana⋅jñāne, jatna kara’ rātri-dine,
vasana bhūṣaṇa kata veśa
paramātmā bhagavān, jabe habe antardhāna,
bhasma kīṭa kṛmi avaśeṣa

nidrāte paḍile mana, kothā ghara⋅dvāra⋅dhana,
strī⋅putra⋅bāndhava thāke kathi
ihāte nā lāge dhanda, tabu kārya kara manda,
nā cintile āpanāra gati

niti niti jīya mara, ite nā vicāra kara,
emati jāibe ekabāra
kahe dīna premānanda, bhaja kṛṣṇa⋅padadvandva,
māyā⋅pāśa ghucibe galāra
IDX.en_translation:
O my mind! Listen, you are very ignorant. Having attained this body which is subject to so many weaknesses, what pride do you harbor within?

Three-fold miseries surround you—who knows when one will destroy you? Saying "this is I" and "this is mine," you have forgotten your own Lord. The servants of death only laugh at your condition.

This body, which you consider your own and care for day and night with clothes and ornaments—when the Supreme Lord disappears from it, only ashes, worms, and insects will remain.

When the mind falls asleep, where then are your house, wealth, family, and friends? You are not confused by this reality, yet you continue to do evil deeds without thinking of your ultimate destination.

Daily you experience a kind of life and death, yet you do not consider that you will surely depart one day. The lowly Premānanda says: "Worship the twin lotus feet of Lord Kṛṣṇa, and the noose of illusion will be removed from your neck."

IDX.first_line: ore mana suna suna tu badi gonara
IDX.search: ore mana suna suna tu badi gonara chadiya satera sanga asat sange sada ranga parinama na kara vicara kamadira vasa haye sada phira matta haye jana toma aksaya amara danda karta ache jei dande dande likhe sei tileke bhangibe garva tora
IDX.author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
ore mana! śuna śuna tu baḍi goṅāra
chāḍiyā satera saṅga, asat⋅saṅge sadā raṅga,
pariṇāma nā kara vicāra

kāmādira vaśa ha’ye, sadā phira matta ha’ye,
jāna’ tomā akṣaya amara
daṇḍa kartā āche jei, daṇḍe daṇḍe likhe sei,
tileke bhāṅgibe garva tora

khara⋅prāya baha bhāra, jebā kanyā⋅putra⋅dāra,
pāla’ jā’re āpanā jāniyā
jabe kāla bāndhi’ labe, e deha paḍiyā ra’be,
dekhi’ mukha rahibe phiriyā

kariyā bāhira⋅bāṭī, gṛhe dibe chaḍā⋅jhāṭī,
snāna kare pavitra lāgiyā
kaha dekhi kebā chila, kāhāra ādara kaila,
ebe kene phele poḍāiyā

kahe premānanda cita, jadi cāha nija hita,
‘kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa’ kaha śvāsa śvāsa
kṛṣṇa jagatera kartā, kṛṣṇa tina⋅lokatrātā,
bhaji’ kṛṣṇa kāṭa karmaphā̐sa
IDX.en_translation:
O my mind! Listen, you are very stubborn. Leaving the company of the saintly and always enjoying bad company, you do not consider the consequences.

Becoming subject to lust and other desires, always wandering about intoxicated, you think yourself to be immortal. But the Supreme Judge records your every moment, and in an instant, your pride will be shattered.

Like a donkey, you carry heavy burdens—your family and children—whom you nurture while thinking they are your own. When Time eventually binds you, this body will remain fallen, and everyone's face will turn away from it.

They will take your body outside and sweep the house, then bathe to become purified. Tell me, who was that body really? Who was it that they once cherished? Why do they now throw it away to be burnt?

Premānanda says: "If you desire your own welfare, chant 'Kṛṣṇa! Kṛṣṇa!' with every breath. Lord Kṛṣṇa is the Master of the universe and the deliverer of the three worlds. By worshipping Him, cut the noose of your past actions."

IDX.first_line: ore mana svarga naraka bujha kotha
IDX.search: ore mana svarga naraka bujha kotha je jemana karma kare temani bhunjaya tare bhaviya dekhile saba hetha keha ghodaya dolaya phere keha skandhe vahe kare chatra dhari keha cale pathe keha karma anusare janma bhari karagare karo vistha keha vahe mathe
IDX.author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
ore mana! svarga naraka bujha kothā
je jemana karma kare, temani bhuñjāya tāre,
bhāviyā dekhile saba hethā

keha ghoḍāya dolāya phere, keha skandhe vahe kāre,
chatra dhari keha cale pathe
keha karma⋅anusāre, janma bhari kārāgāre,
kāro viṣṭhā keha vahe māthe

śata sahasrāyuta lakṣa, keha pāle diyā bhakṣya,
udara bharite keha nāre
ekhāne dekhicha jebā, pare jā’ tā’ jāne kebā,
vidhātāra mane se vicāre

devatā⋅gandharva⋅yakṣa, preta⋅piśāca⋅daitya⋅rakṣa,
svabhāve sakala paracāra
jāhāra jemana mata, sei karme anurata,
sei mata bhakṣya se ācāra

kṛṣṇa⋅pāriṣada bhakta, kṛṣṇa karme sadā rata,
kabhu lipta nahe e saṁsāre
se rahe māyāra pāra, tāte kāra adhikāra,
nitya⋅saṅga nitya⋅parivāre

kṛṣṇa⋅līlā⋅guṇa⋅nāma, rātridine avirāma,
śravaṇa⋅kīrtana sadānanda
premānanda kahe mati, haye tā̐’ra anugati,
‘kṛṣṇa’ kahi chi̐ḍa karma-bandha
IDX.en_translation:
O my mind! Where do you seek to understand heaven and hell? As one performs their actions, so they experience the results. If you reflect deeply, you will see everything right here.

Some ride on horses and some carry others on their shoulders. Some walk on the path holding an umbrella. Some, according to their past actions, are imprisoned for life, and some carry the waste of others on their heads.

Some maintain millions of people by giving them food, while others cannot even fill their own stomachs. Whatever you see here, who knows what comes next? It is all decided in the mind of the Creator.

Demigods, ghosts, and demons are all revealed according to their natures. As one's disposition is, so one is devoted to that activity, and accordingly are their food and customs.

Lord Kṛṣṇa's eternal associates and devotees are always engaged in His service. They are never entangled in this material world. They remain beyond illusion—who has power over them? They enjoy eternal companionship with the Lord's eternal family.

Lord Kṛṣṇa's pastimes, qualities, and Names are heard and chanted day and night, bringing eternal bliss. Premānanda says: "Becoming His follower, chant 'Kṛṣṇa!' and break the bonds of your past actions."

IDX.first_line: pahu mora gauranga gosani
IDX.search: pahu mora gauranga gosani ei krpa kara jena tomara guna gai je se kule janma hauka je se deha pana tomara bhaktasange phiri tomara guna gaiya
IDX.author: vaiṣṇavadāsa
IDX.verses:
pahu̐ mora gaurāṅga gosāñī
ei kṛpā kara jena tomāra guṇa gāi

je se kule janma hauka, je se deha pāñā
tomāra bhakta-saṅge phiri tomāra guṇa gāiyā

cirakāla āśā prabhu āchaye hiyāya
tomāra nigūḍha līlā sphuraye āmāya

tomāra nāme sadā ruci hauka mora
tomāra guṇa-gāne jena sadāi hoi bhora

tomāra guṇa gāite śunite bhakta⋅saṅge
sāttvika vikāra ki hoibe mora aṅge

aśru⋅kampa⋅pulake pūribe saba tanu
bhūmite paḍibe preme ageyāna janu

je se kara prabhu, tumi ekamātra gati
kahaye vaiṣṇavadāsa tomāya rahuka mati
IDX.en_translation:
My Lord, Gaurāṅga Gosāñī! Please grant me this mercy, so that I may sing Your glories.

Whatever family I may be born into, whatever body I may attain, may I always wander in the company of Your devotees, singing Your glories.

O Lord, this hope remains forever in my heart, that Your confidential pastimes may manifest within me.

May I always have a taste for Your Holy Name, and may I always be absorbed in singing Your glories.

While singing and hearing Your glories in the company of devotees, what ecstatic transformations will manifest in my body!

My whole body will be filled with tears, trembling, and ecstatic bliss. I will fall to the ground, unconscious due to divine love.

Whatever You do, O Lord, You are my only refuge. Vaiṣṇava Dāsa says: "May my mind remain fixed on You."

IDX.first_line: palyadasi kari lalita sundari
IDX.search: palyadasi kari lalita sundari amare loiya kabe sri radhika pade kale milaibe ajna seva samarpibe sri rupamanjari sange jaba kabe rasa seva siksa tare tadanuga haye radha kunda tate rahiba harsitantare
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
pālya-dāsī kari’, lalitā sundarī,
āmāre loiyā kabe
śrī⋅rādhikā pade, kāle milāibe,
ājñā⋅sevā samarpibe

śrī⋅rūpamañjarī, saṅge jāba kabe,
rasa⋅sevā⋅śikṣā tare
tadanugā ha’ye, rādhā⋅kuṇḍa taṭe,
rahiba harṣitāntare

śrī-viśākhā-pade, saṅgīta śikhiba,
kṛṣṇalīlā rasamaya
śrī⋅rati⋅mañjarī, śrī⋅rasa mañjarī,
hoibe sabe sadaya

parama ānande, sakale miliyā,
rādhikā caraṇe raba
ei parākāṣṭa, siddhi kabe ha’be,
pā’ba rādhā⋅padāsava
IDX.en_translation:
When will beautiful Lalitā make me her maidservant, take me, and in time introduce me to the lotus feet of Śrī Rādhikā and entrust me with serving Her commands?

When will I go with Śrī Rūpa Mañjarī to learn the service of divine love? Becoming her follower, I will joyfully remain on the banks of Rādhā-kuṇḍa.

At the feet of Śrī Viśākhā, I will learn music, which is full of the divine pastimes of Kṛṣṇa. Śrī Rati Mañjarī and Śrī Rasa Mañjarī will all be merciful.

With supreme bliss, meeting with all of them, I will remain at the lotus feet of Rādhikā. When will this ultimate perfection be achieved, and I will attain the nectar of Rādhā's feet?

IDX.first_line: pancasakhi madhye cara nityasiddha radhikara
IDX.search: pancasakhi madhye cara nityasiddha radhikara se sakale sadhana na kaila sakhi bali ukta jeha sadhana prabhave teha vraja raja pure vasa paila sei sakhi dviprakara sadhanete siddha ara sadhanapara baliya ganana siddha bali akhya tara gopi deha hoila jara kari rage jugala bhajana
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
pañcasakhī⋅madhye cāra, nityasiddha rādhikāra,
se sakale sādhana nā kaila
‘sakhī’ bali’ ukta je̐ha, sādhana⋅prabhāve te̐ha,
vraja⋅rāja⋅pure vāsa pāila

sei sakhī dvi-prakāra, sādhanete siddha āra,
sādhanaparā baliyā gaṇana
‘siddhā’ bali ākhyā tā̐ra, gopī⋅deha hoila jā̐ra,
kari’ rāge jugala⋅bhajana

kṛṣṇākṛṣṭa muni-jana, tathā upaniṣadgaṇa,
je nā laila gopīra svarūpa
sādhana⋅āveśe bhaje, siddhi tabu nā upaje,
vrajabhāva⋅prāpti aparūpa

je je śruti munigaṇa, gopī hañā subhajana,
karila sakhīra pada dhari’
nityasakhī⋅kṛpābale, tatsālokya lābha⋅phale,
sevā kare śrī⋅rādhā⋅śrī⋅hari

devī-gaṇa sei bhāve, sakhīra sālokya⋅lābhe,
kṛṣṇasevā kare sakhī haye
vrajera vidhāna eha, gopī vinā āra keha,
nā pāibe vrajayuvadvaye
IDX.en_translation:
Among the Pañcasakhī, four are eternally perfect associates of Rādhikā. They did not perform any spiritual practice. Those who are called ‘sakhī’, through the influence of spiritual practice, attained residence in Vraja⋅rāja-pura.

These sakhīs are of two kinds: those perfected through practice and those engaged in practice. They are called ‘perfected’ when they attain the body of a gopī by performing the worship of the Divine Couple with intense affection.

Sages attracted to Lord Kṛṣṇa and the Upaniṣads did not assume the form of a gopī. Even if they worship with the intensity of spiritual practice, perfection does not arise; attaining the mood of Vraja is unique.

Those Vedas and sages who performed excellent worship by becoming gopīs and taking the shelter of the sakhīs, by the power of the mercy of the eternally perfect sakhīs, serve Śrī Rādhā and Lord Kṛṣṇa as a result of attaining the same abode.

In the same way, the goddesses, by attaining the same abode as a sakhī, serve Kṛṣṇa as companions. This is the rule of Vraja; no one else except a gopī can attain the Divine Couple of Vraja.

IDX.first_line: parama dayala he radhesyama
IDX.search: parama dayala he radhesyama jasodajivana gopipranadhana vrndavanadhana gopala syama devaki nandana murali vadana kaliya damana he ghanasyama
IDX.verses:
parama dayāla he rādheśyāma!
jaśodājīvana, gopīprāṇadhana,
vṛndāvanadhana gopāla śyāma

devakī nandana, muralī vadana,
kālīya damana he ghanaśyāma

kaṁsake damana, jadukula pālana,
bhaktake jīvana dayāla śyāma
IDX.en_translation:
O supremely merciful Rādhā and Śyāma! You are the life of Mother Yaśodā and the wealth of the gopīs' lives. You are the treasure of Vṛndāvana, O Gopāla Śyāma!

O Son of Devakī! O You whose face holds the flute! O Subduer of the serpent Kāliya! O dark Lord Kṛṣṇa!

O Subduer of Kaṁsa and Protector of the Yadu dynasty! You are the life of Your devotees, O merciful Lord Śyāma!

IDX.first_line: parama karuna pahu duijana
IDX.search: parama karuna pahu duijana nitai gaura candra saba avatara sara siromani kevala ananda kanda bhaja bhaja bhai caitanya nitai sudrdha visvasa kari visaya chadiya se rase majiya mukhe bala hari hari
IDX.author: śrīla locana⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
parama karuṇa, pa̐hu duijana,
nitāi gaura⋅candra
saba avatāra⋅, sāra śiromaṇi,
kevala ānanda⋅kanda

bhaja bhaja bhāi, caitanya⋅nitāi,
sudṛḍha viśvāsa kari’
viṣaya chāḍiyā, se⋅rase majiyā,
mukhe bala hari hari

dekha ore bhāi, tri-bhuvane nāi,
emana dayāla dātā
paśu pākhī jhure, pāṣāṇa vidare,
śuni’ jā̐’ra guṇa-gāthā

saṁsāre majiyā, rahile paḍiyā,
se⋅pade nahila āśa
āpana karama, bhuñjāya śamana,
kahaye locana⋅dāsa
IDX.en_translation:
Supremely merciful are the two Lords, Nitāi and Gaura⋅candra. They are the crest-jewel and the essence of all incarnations—the only source of joy.

Worship them, O brother! Worship Caitanya and Nitāi with firm faith. Giving up material attachments and absorbed in that divine mellow, continuously chant "Hari! Hari!"

See, O brother! In the three worlds there are no givers so merciful. Animals and birds weep, and even stones melt upon hearing their glorious qualities.

Remaining fallen and drowning in the material world, no desire arose in me for their lotus feet. One's own karma makes one experience the consequences of death—so says Locana dāsa.

IDX.first_line: parama karuna pahu duijananitai gaura candrasaba avatara sara siromanikevala ananda kanda
IDX.search: parama karuna pahu duijananitai gaura candrasaba avatara sara siromanikevala ananda kanda bhaja bhaja bhai caitanya nitaisudrdha visvasa karivisaya chadiya se rase majiyamukhe bala hari hari
IDX.author: śrīla locana⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
parama karuṇa, pa̐hu duijana,nitāi gaura⋅candrasaba avatāra⋅, sāra śiromaṇi,kevala ānanda⋅kanda

bhaja bhaja bhāi, caitanya⋅nitāi,sudṛḍha viśvāsa kari’viṣaya chāḍiyā, se⋅rase majiyā,mukhe bala hari hari

dekha ore bhāi, tri-bhuvane nāi,emana dayāla dātāpaśu pākhī jhure, pāṣāṇa vidare,śuni’ jā̐’ra guṇa-gāthā

saṁsāre majiyā, rahile paḍiyā,se⋅pade nahila āśaāpana karama, bhuñjāya śamana,kahaye locana⋅dāsa
IDX.en_translation:
Supremely merciful are the two Lords, Nitāi and Gaura⋅candra. They are the crest-jewel and the essence of all incarnations—the only source of joy.

Worship them, O brother! Worship Caitanya and Nitāi with firm faith. Giving up material attachments and absorbed in that divine mellow, continuously chant “Hari! Hari!”

See, O brother! In the three worlds there are no givers so merciful. Animals and birds weep, and even stones melt upon hearing their glorious qualities.

Remaining fallen and drowning in the material world, no desire arose in me for their lotus feet. One’s own karma makes one experience the consequences of death—so says Locana dāsa.

IDX.first_line: paramacaitanya hari tara sakti vanesvari
IDX.search: paramacaitanya hari tara sakti vanesvari parasakti bali vede gaya saktimane sevibare sakti kayavyuha kare nana sakti tahe bahiraya adhara saktite dhama ahvaya saktite nama sandhini saktite vastu jata samvit saktite jnana tatasthe jivavidhana haladinite kaila sakhi vrata
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
paramacaitanya hari, tā̐’ra śakti vaneśvarī,
parāśakti bali’ vede gāya
śaktimāne sevibāre, śakti kāyavyūha kare,
nānā śakti tāhe bāhirāya

ādhāra⋅śaktite dhāma, āhvaya⋅śaktite nāma,
sandhinī śaktite vastu jāta
samvit⋅śaktite jñāna, taṭasthe jīva-vidhāna,
halādinīte kaila sakhī⋅vrāta

nitya-siddha sakhī saba, hlādinīra su-vaibhava,
hlādinīsvarūpa mūla rādhā
candrāvalī⋅ādi jata, śrī⋅rādhāra anugata,
keha nahe rādhā⋅premera bādhā

premera vicitra gati, pratidvandvī ha’ye satī,
candrā kare rādhā⋅prema puṣṭa
saba sakhīra eka mana, nānākāre nānā jana,
vraja-yuva-dvandve kare tuṣṭa
IDX.en_translation:
Hari is the Supreme Consciousness; His energy is Vaneśvarī (Rādhā). The Vedas sing of Her as the Parā-śakti (Supreme Energy). To serve the Energetic, the Energy expands Herself; many energies emerge from that.

By the Ādhāra-śakti (supporting energy) is the Dhāma; by the Āhvaya-śakti (calling energy) is the Name; by the Sandhinī-śakti, all things are born. By the Samvit-śakti is knowledge; the jīva is the arrangement of the Taṭasthā-śakti; and by the Hlādinī-śakti, the group of sakhīs is created.

All the eternally perfect sakhīs are the beautiful opulence of the Hlādinī. The root of the form of Hlādinī is Rādhā. All those headed by Candrāvalī are followers of Śrī Rādhā; no one is an obstacle to Rādhā's love.

The movement of love is wonderful; becoming a rival, the chaste Candrā nourishes Rādhā's love. All the sakhīs have one mind; as various persons in various forms, they please the Youthful Couple of Vraja.

IDX.first_line: paritosita gopa vadhu patalam
IDX.search: paritosita gopa vadhu patalam patalanghita kancana sara cayam racayantam udara vilasa kalam sakalancita padam agadha balam dhavalam nava kirti kulair amitam
IDX.author: śrīla rūpa gosvāmī
IDX.verses:
paritoṣita⋅gopa⋅vadhū⋅paṭalaṁ
paṭalaṅghita⋅kāñcana⋅sāra⋅cayam
racayantam udāra⋅vilāsa⋅kalāṁ
sakalāñcita⋅pādam agādha⋅balam
dhavalaṁ nava⋅kīrti⋅kulair amitam
IDX.en_translation: He fully satisfies the groups of cowherd women. He surpasses heaps of pure gold in brightness. He performs wonderful, charming pastimes. His feet are worshiped by everyone, and He possesses immeasurable strength. He is pure and endless in His ever-new glories.

IDX.first_line: pasya saci sutamanupama rupam
IDX.search: pasya saci sutamanupama rupam kalitamrta rasa nirupama kupam krsna raga krta manasa tapam lila prakatita rudra pratapam
IDX.author: rādhāmohana dāsa
IDX.verses:
paśya śacī⋅sutamanupama⋅rūpam
kalitāmṛta⋅rasa⋅nirupama⋅kūpam

kṛṣṇa⋅rāga⋅kṛta⋅mānasa⋅tāpam
līlā⋅prakaṭita⋅rudra⋅pratāpam

prakalita⋅puruṣottama⋅suviṣādam
kamalā⋅kara⋅kamalāñcita⋅pādam

rohita⋅vadana⋅tirohita⋅bhāṣam
rādhā⋅mohana⋅kṛta⋅caraṇāśam
IDX.en_translation:
Behold the son of Mother Śacī, whose form is incomparable! He is a matchless reservoir filled with the nectar of divine mellows.

His mind is burning with the heat of deep attachment to Kṛṣṇa. In His pastimes, He manifests a formidable and intense power.

He is deeply immersed in the great sorrow of separation from the Supreme Person, Śrī Kṛṣṇa. His feet are worshipped by the lotus-like hands of Lakṣmī-devī.

His face is flushed reddish, and His speech is choked up. Rādhāmohana Dāsa has taken shelter at His lotus feet.

IDX.first_line: patita pavana vani au kete bedaku
IDX.search: patita pavana vani au kete bedaku bhasigali bhavajale navadio kudaku ropivara vrksa goti katuyachu mudaku tini pure apakrti heba adi mudaku
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
patita pāvana vāṇī, āu kete beḍaku
bhāsigali, bhavajale, nāvadio kuḍaku

ropivāra vṛkṣa goṭi, kāṭuyachu muḍaku
tini pure apakṛti heba ādi muḍaku

dūre thāi ḍāka dele, ḍerī thāo karṇaku
nikaṭīra nā śunivā, mo karma aveḍaku

lakṣe jojanāra gaja, ḍāka dela tumbhaku
nakra nāśi vāku cakra, dela pejī jaḍaku

nakṣatra dipuḍi jaḍe, sandhyā dhūpa beḍaku
lamviche dayanā māḍe, pāda⋅padma taḍaku

jogījana māne hṛde, dheyāuchanti jāhāku
bhanile vīra kiśora, āśā pāda taḍaku
IDX.en_translation:
O Purifier of the fallen! How much longer will I await Your words? I am drowning in the ocean of material existence; please give me a boat.

I am myself cutting the head of the tree I have planted. This primal misdeed will be the origin of trouble in the three worlds.

When I call out from afar, it takes a long time to reach Your ear. Even when I am near, You do not hear; my past actions are the obstruction.

The elephant-king Gajendra called out to You from a vast distance. Destroying the crocodile, Your disc quickly granted him his life.

The stars sparkle and the evening incense spreads. Extending myself upon the bed of mercy, my only yearning is toward Your lotus feet.

The yogīs meditate upon You in their hearts. The poet says: "My only hope is at Your lotus feet."

IDX.first_line: piriti saccidanande rupavati nari
IDX.search: piriti saccidanande rupavati nari dayadharma adi guna alankara saba tahari jnana tara pattasati joga gandha paripati e sabe sobhita sati kare krsnamana curi
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
pīriti saccidānande rūpavatī nārī
dayādharma⋅ādi guṇa alaṅkāra saba tāhāri

jñāna⋅tā’ra paṭṭaśāṭī, joga⋅gandha⋅paripāṭī
e sabe śobhitā satī kare kṛṣṇa-mana curi

rūpa vinā alaṅkāre, kibā śobhā e⋅saṁsāre
pīriti⋅vihīna guṇe, kṛṣṇa nā tuṣite pāri

bānarīra alaṅkāra, śobhā nāhi haya tā’ra
kṛṣṇa-prema vinā tathā guṇe nā ādara kari
IDX.en_translation:
Divine love is a beautiful woman in the state of eternal existence, knowledge, and bliss. Qualities like compassion and righteousness are all her ornaments.

Knowledge is her silk sari, and yoga is her fragrant perfume. Adorned with all these, the chaste woman steals the heart of Kṛṣṇa.

What beauty is there in this world for ornaments without form? Without divine love, Kṛṣṇa cannot be pleased by mere qualities.

A monkey's ornaments do not make her beautiful. Similarly, without love for Kṛṣṇa, qualities are not appreciated.

IDX.first_line: pitavarana kali pavana gora
IDX.search: pitavarana kali pavana gora gaoyoi aichana bhava vibhora cittadarpana parimarjanakari krsnakirtana jaya cittavihari
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
pītavaraṇa kali⋅pāvana gorā
gāoyoi aichana bhāva⋅vibhorā

cittadarpaṇa⋅parimārjanakārī
kṛṣṇa-kīrtana jaya cittavihārī

helā⋅bhavadāva⋅nirvāpaṇa⋅vṛtti
kṛṣṇa-kīrtana jaya kleśanivṛtti

śreyaḥ⋅kumudavidhu⋅jyotsnā⋅prakāśa
kṛṣṇa-kīrtana jaya bhaktivilāsa

viśuddha⋅vidyāvadhū⋅jīvanarūpa
kṛṣṇa-kīrtana jaya siddhasvarūpa

ānanda⋅payonidhi⋅vardhana⋅kīrti
kṛṣṇa-kīrtana jaya plāvana⋅mūrti

pade pade pīyūṣa⋅svāda⋅pradātā
kṛṣṇa-kīrtana jaya premavidhātā

bhaktivinoda svātmasnapana vidhāna
kṛṣṇa-kīrtana jaya prema⋅nidāna
IDX.en_translation:
Golden-colored Gaura⋅candra, the purifier of the Kali age, sings while absorbed in divine ecstasy.

All glories to the congregational chanting of Kṛṣṇa's names, which cleanses the mirror of the heart and delights the mind!

All glories to the chanting of Kṛṣṇa's names, which easily extinguishes the forest fire of material existence and ends all miseries!

All glories to the chanting of Kṛṣṇa's names, the enjoyment of devotion, which illuminates the night-lotus of ultimate good with moonlight!

All glories to the chanting of Kṛṣṇa's names, the perfected form and the very life of the pure bride of knowledge!

All glories to the chanting of Kṛṣṇa's names, the inundating form that increases the ocean of bliss!

All glories to the chanting of Kṛṣṇa's names, the bestower of divine love, which gives the taste of nectar at every step!

Bhaktivinoda proclaims this as the process to bathe one's own self; all glories to the congregational chanting of Kṛṣṇa's names, the treasure house of divine love!

IDX.first_line: prabhu he
IDX.search: prabhu he suna mora duhkhera kahini visaya halahala sudhabhane piyalu ab avasana dinamani khelarase saisava padhoite kaisora goyaolu na bhela viveka bhogavase jauvane ghara pati basilu suta mita badala aneka
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
(prabhu he!)
 śuna mora duḥkhera kāhinī
viṣaya⋅halāhala, sudhābhāṇe piyalu̐,
āb avasāna dina-maṇi

khelārase śaiśava, paḍhoite kaiśora,
goyāolu̐, nā bhela viveka
bhoga-vaśe jauvane, ghara pāti’ basilu̐,
suta⋅mita bāḍala aneka

vṛddha-kāla āola, saba sukha bhāgala,
pīḍāvaśe hoinu kātara
sarvendriya durbala, kṣīṇa kalevara,
bhogā-bhāve duḥkhita antara

jñāna⋅laba⋅hīna, bhakti-rase vañcita,
āra mora ki ha’be upāya
patita bandhu tuhu̐, patitādhama hāma,
kṛpāya uṭhāo tava pā⋅ya

vicārite āvahi, guṇa nāhi pāobi,
kṛpā kara, choḍata vicāra
tava pada-paṅkaja⋅, sīdhu pibāota,
bhakativinode kara’ pāra
IDX.translation_intro: From The Harmonist, October 1927. Edited by Bhaktisiddhanta Sarasvati.
IDX.en_translation:
Hear, O Lord, the tale of my sorrows-- The poison of worldly enjoyment I have sipped, pretending it was the nectar; But now the sun is setting.

In the diversions of play my childhood, My boyhood in studies, I wasted; There arose no consciousness of good in me. For enjoying my youth, I devised a home and settled down in it; Children and friends did fast multiply.

Old age soon arrived, All joys departed; With disease I was smitten down. The senses lost their vigour, The body grew feeble, The heart was sad lacking enjoyment.

Devoid of a particle of true knowledge, Missing the elixir of devotion, What help is there for me? Thou art the Friend of the fallen; I am the worst of sinners; In mercy lift me to Thy feet.

Judging me Thou wilt find no good; Have pity, judge me not! The nectar of Thy lotus feet Make me taste; Do Thou deliver Bhaktivinode.

IDX.first_line: prabhu he eibara karaha karuna
IDX.search: prabhu he eibara karaha karuna jugalacarana dekhi saphala kariba akhi ei mora manera kamana nija padaseva diba nahi more upekhiba duhu pahu karunasagara duhu vinu nahi jano ei bada bhagya mano muni bada patita pamara
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
prabhu he, eibāra karaha karuṇā
jugala-caraṇa dekhi’, saphala kariba ā̐khi,
ei mora manera kāmanā

nija⋅padasevā dibā, nāhi more upekhibā,
du̐hu pa̐hu karuṇāsāgara
du̐hu vinu nāhi jāno, ei baḍa bhāgya māno,
muñi baḍa patita pāmara

lalitā⋅ādeśa pāñā, caraṇa seviba jāñā,
priyasakhī⋅saṅge haya mane
du̐hu dātā śiromaṇi, ati dīna more jāni’,
nikaṭe caraṇa dibe dāne

pāba rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa⋅pā, ghucibe manera ghā,
dūre jābe esaba vikala
narottama⋅dāse kaya, ei vāñchā siddhi haya,
deha⋅prāṇa⋅sakala saphala
IDX.en_translation:
O Lord, be merciful this time. Seeing the feet of the Couple, I will make my eyes successful; this is the desire of my mind.

Give me service to Your own feet, do not neglect me; Both Lords are oceans of mercy. I do not know anything without Both of You; I consider this a great fortune. I am very fallen and sinful.

Receiving the order of Lalitā, going to serve the feet—this is in my mind, in the company of the dear sakhīs. Both are the crest-jewels of givers; knowing me to be very lowly, They will give Their feet nearby in charity.

I will get the feet of Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa, the wound of the mind will be healed; all this distress will go away. Narottama Dāsa says: This desire is fulfilled; body, life—everything is successful.

IDX.first_line: prabhu heemana durmati samsara bhitarepadiya achinu amitava nija jana kona mahajanepathaiya dile tumi
IDX.search: prabhu heemana durmati samsara bhitarepadiya achinu amitava nija jana kona mahajanepathaiya dile tumi daya kari more patita dekhiyakahila amare giyaohe dinajana suna bhala kathaullasita habe hiya
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
(prabhu he!)emana durmati, saṁsāra⋅bhitare,paḍiyā āchinu āmitava nija⋅jana, kona mahājane,pāṭhāiyā dile tumi

dayā kari’ more, patita dekhiyā,kahila āmāre giyā‘ohe dīnajana, śuna bhāla kathā,ullasita habe hiyā

tomāre tārite, śrī-kṛṣṇa-caitanya,navadvīpe avatāratomā’ hena kata, dīna hīna jane,karilena bhava-pāra

vedera pratijñā, rākhibāra tare,rukmavarṇa viprasutamahāprabhu nāme, nadīyā mātāya,saṅge bhāi avadhūta

nanda-suta jini, caitanya⋅gosāñī,nija⋅nāma kari’ dānatārila jagat, tumio jāiyā,laha nija paritrāṇa’

se⋅kathā śuniyā, āsiyāchi, nātha!tomāra caraṇatalebhakativinoda, kā̐diyā kā̐diyā,āpana kāhinī bale
IDX.en_translation:
From The Harmonist, November 1927. Edited by Bhakti Siddhānta Sarasvatī.

With a mind so evil, In the midst of this world, Forsaken as I lay, Thy Own servant, A superior person, Thou didst send.

Pitying me, Fallen as I was, He came to me and said,-- “Thou seem’st humble, Listen to good words That will cheer thy heart.

“To deliver Thee Sri Krishna Chaitanya Has appeared in Navadwipa; Many another like thyself, As humble and as base, He has conducted safely across the sea of the world.

“The promise of the Vedas To fulfill, The Son of Brahman, of golden colour, Bearing the Name of Mahaprabhu, Fills with ecstasy Nadia In company of the super-ascetic, His brother.

“The Supreme Lord Chaitanya, Who is no other than the Son of Nanda, By the free gift of His Own Name, Has wrought the deliverance of the world, Go thou also And receive thy salvation.

“Hearing those words I have come, O Lord, For the shelter of Thy feet,” Bhakti-vinode, Crying out in grief, Narrates the story of himself.

IDX.first_line: prabhu tava padayuge mora nivedana
IDX.search: prabhu tava padayuge mora nivedana nahi magi deha sukha vidya dhana jana nahi magi svarga ara moksa nahi magi na kari prarthana kona vibhutira lagi
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
prabhu! tava padayuge mora nivedana
nāhi māgi deha⋅sukha, vidyā, dhana, jana

nāhi māgi svarga, āra mokṣa nāhi māgi
nā kari prārthanā kona vibhūtira lāgi

nija⋅karma⋅guṇa⋅doṣe je je⋅janma pāi
janme janme jena tava nāma⋅guṇa gāi

eimātra āśā mama, tomāra caraṇe
ahaitukī bhakti hṛde jāge anukṣaṇe

viṣaye je prīti ebe āchaye āmāra
sei mata prīti houka, caraṇe tomāra

vipade sampade tāhā thākuka samabhāve
dine dine vṛddhi houka nāmera prabhāve

paśupakṣī haye thāki svarge vā niraye
tava bhakti rahu bhaktivinoda⋅hṛdaye
IDX.en_translation:
O Lord! This is my humble submission at Your lotus feet: I do not ask for bodily happiness, knowledge, wealth, or followers.

I do not ask for heaven, nor do I ask for liberation. I do not pray for any opulence.

By the merits and demerits of my own karma, whatever births I may receive, may I, birth after birth, sing Your names and glories.

This is my only hope at Your feet: that causeless devotion awakens in my heart at every moment.

Whatever affection I currently have for sense objects, may that same affection be for Your feet.

May that affection remain equal in distress and prosperity, and may it increase day by day by the influence of Your Holy Name.

Whether I become an animal or a bird, whether I am in heaven or in hell, may devotion to You remain in Bhaktivinoda's heart.

IDX.first_line: prabhura vacana suniya takhana
IDX.search: prabhura vacana suniya takhana srivasa lotana bhumi bale suna natha tava rasabhanga sahite na pari ami ekati tanaya mariyache natha tahe mora kiba duhkha jadi saba mare tomare heriya tabu ta paiba sukha
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
prabhura vacana, śuniyā takhana,
śrīvāsa loṭāñā bhūmi
bale, śuna nātha! tava rasa-bhaṅga,
sahite nā pāri āmi

ekaṭī tanaya, mariyāche nātha,
tāhe mora kibā duḥkha
jadi saba mare, tomāre heriyā,
tabu ta’ pāiba sukha

tava nṛtya-bhaṅga, hoile āmāra,
maraṇa hoita, hari!
tāi kusaṁvāda, nā dila tomāre,
vipad āśaṅkā kari’

ebe ājñā deha, mṛta suta la’ye,
satkāra karuna sabe
eteka śuniyā, gorā dvijamaṇi,
kā̐dite lāgila tabe

kemane e sabe, chāḍiyā jāiba,
parāṇa vikala haya
se⋅kathā śuniyā, bhakativinoda,
manete pāila bhaya
IDX.en_translation:
Hearing the Lord's words, Śrīvāsa then prostrated himself on the ground and said, "Listen, my Lord! I cannot bear to see Your spiritual absorption broken.

One son, my Lord, has died; what sorrow is that to me? Even if everyone were to die, if I can only behold You, I will still find happiness.

If Your dancing were interrupted, O Lord, that would be my true death! Therefore, fearing misfortune, no bad news was given to You.

Now give Your permission; taking the dead son, let everyone perform the funeral rites." Hearing this, Lord Caitanya, the jewel of the twice-born, then began to cry.

"How can I leave such devotees?" His heart became distressed. Hearing those words, Bhaktivinoda felt fear in his heart.

IDX.first_line: pradosa aila dekhi harasita jata sakhi
IDX.search: pradosa aila dekhi harasita jata sakhi abhisare karaya jatana vrnda upadesa mata jamuna pulina gata kalpavrksa kunje kaila mana sitasita nisa dhari tara jogya vesa kari radhake karaya abhisara hetha krsna gunamani gopasabha siromani kare kata gunera vicara
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
pradoṣa āila dekhi’, haraṣita jata sakhī,
abhisāre karaya jatana
vṛndā upadeśa⋅mata, jamunā⋅pulina gata,
kalpavṛkṣa kuñje kaila mana

sitāsita niśā dhari’, tāra jogya veśa kari’,
rādhāke karāya abhisāra
hethā kṛṣṇa guṇamaṇi, gopasabhā⋅śiromaṇi,
kare kata guṇera vicāra

‘śrānta haye bāchā mora, karu nidrā akātara’,
bali’ jaśomatī kṛṣṇe lañā
śayyāya śāyita kare, ekākī nibhṛta care,
nidrā heri’ anyagṛhe jāñā

āpane ekaka pāya, tabe śayyā chāḍi jāya,
kuñje giyā hoila udaya
praphullita vṛndāvana, dekhi’ kṛṣṇa⋅āgamana,
sakhīsaba ānandita haya
IDX.en_translation:
Seeing the evening arrive, all the gopīs become joyful and prepare for the secret meeting. Following Vṛndā's instructions, Rādhā goes to the banks of the Yamunā, Her heart set on the grove of wish-fulfilling trees.

Adorned in appropriate attire for the dark night, Rādhā sets out for the meeting. Meanwhile, Lord Kṛṣṇa, the crest-jewel of the cowherd assembly, contemplates many excellent qualities.

"My dear child is tired, let him sleep soundly," saying this, Mother Yaśodā takes Lord Kṛṣṇa and lays Him upon His bed. The Lord then secretly and alone departs, and after observing His mother to be asleep, He goes to another dwelling.

Finding Himself alone, He leaves the bed and appears in the grove. The entire Vṛndāvana forest blossoms with joy upon seeing the Lord's arrival, and all the gopīs become delighted.

IDX.first_line: pradosa samaye srivasa angane
IDX.search: pradosa samaye srivasa angane sangopane goramani sri harikirtane nace nana range uthila mangaladhvani mrdanga madala baje karatala majhe majhe jayatura prabhura natana dekhi sakalera hoila santapa dura
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
pradoṣa⋅samaye, śrīvāsa⋅aṅgane,
saṅgopane gorāmaṇi
śrī⋅harikīrtane, nāce nānā raṅge,
uṭhila maṅgaladhvani

mṛdaṅga⋅mādala, bāje karatāla,
mājhe mājhe jayatura
prabhura naṭana, dekhi’ sakalera,
hoila santāpa dūra

akhaṇḍa premete, mātala takhana,
sakala bhakatagaṇa
āpanā pāsari’, gorācā̐de gheri’,
nāce gāya anukṣaṇa

emata samaye, daiva⋅vyādhiyoge,
śrīvāsera antaḥpure
tanaya⋅viyoge, nārīgaṇa śoke,
prakāśala uccaiḥsvare

krandana uṭhile, ha’be rasabhaṅga,
bhakativinoda ḍare
śrīvāsa amani, bujhila kāraṇa,
paśila āpana ghare
IDX.en_translation:
At the time of twilight, in the courtyard of Śrīvāsa, Gaura⋅candra secretly dances with various ecstasies during the chanting of the Lord's names, and auspicious sounds arise.

Drums and cymbals sound, interspersed with calls of victory. Seeing the dancing of the Lord, everyone's distress was dispelled.

With unbroken love, all the devotees became intoxicated; forgetting themselves, they surrounded Gaura⋅candra, dancing and singing constantly.

At such a time, due to a divine illness, in the inner chambers of Śrīvāsa, the women cried out loudly in grief over the separation from a son.

Bhaktivinoda fears that if weeping arises, the spiritual mellow will be broken. Śrīvāsa immediately understood the reason and entered his own home.

IDX.first_line: pralaya payodhi jale dhrtavanasi vedam
IDX.search: pralaya payodhi jale dhrtavanasi vedam vihita vahitra caritram akhedam kesava dhrta mina sarira jaya jagadisa hare ksitiriha vipulatare tisthati tava prsthe dharani dharana kina cakra garisthe kesava dhrta kurma sarira jaya jagadisa hare
IDX.author: śrī⋅jayadeva gosvāmī
IDX.verses:
pralaya⋅payodhi⋅jale dhṛtavānasi vedaṁ
vihita⋅vahitra⋅caritram⋅akhedam
keśava⋅dhṛta⋅mīna⋅śarīra jaya jagadīśa hare

kṣitiriha vipulatare tiṣṭhati tava pṛṣṭhe
dharaṇī⋅dharaṇa⋅kiṇa⋅cakra⋅gariṣṭhe
keśava⋅dhṛta⋅kūrma⋅śarīra jaya jagadīśa hare

vasati daśana⋅śikhare dharaṇī tava lagnā
śaśini kalaṅkakaleva nimagnā
keśava⋅dhṛta⋅śūkara⋅rūpa jaya jagadīśa hare

tava kara⋅kamala⋅vare nakham⋅adbhuta⋅śṛṅgaṁ
dalita⋅hiraṇyakaśipu⋅tanu⋅bhṛṅgam
keśava⋅dhṛta⋅narahari⋅rūpa jaya jagadīśa hare

chalayasi vikramaṇe balim⋅adbhuta⋅vāmana
pada⋅nakha⋅nīra⋅janita⋅jana⋅pāvana
keśava⋅dhṛta⋅vāmana⋅rūpa jaya jagadīśa hare

kṣatriya⋅rudhiramaye jagad⋅apagata⋅pāpaṁ
snapayasi payasi śamita bhavatāpam
keśava⋅dhṛta⋅bhṛgupati⋅rūpa jaya jagadīśa hare

vitarasi dikṣu raṇe dikpati⋅kamanīyaṁ
daśamukha⋅mauli⋅valiṁ ramaṇīyam
keśava⋅dhṛta⋅rāma⋅śarīra jaya jagadīśa hare

vahasi vapuṣi viśade vasanaṁ jaladābhaṁ
halahati⋅bhīti⋅milita⋅yamunābham
keśava⋅dhṛta⋅haladhara⋅rūpa jaya jagadīśa hare

nindasi jajña⋅vidherahaha śruti-jātaṁ
sadaya⋅hṛdaya⋅darśita⋅paśu⋅ghātam
keśava⋅dhṛta⋅buddha⋅śarīra jaya jagadīśa hare

mleccha⋅nivaha⋅nidhane kalayasi karavālaṁ
dhūmaketumiva kimapi karālam
keśava⋅dhṛta⋅kalki⋅śarīra jaya jagadīśa hare

śrī⋅jayadeva⋅kaveridam⋅uditam⋅udāraṁ
śṛṇu śubhadaṁ sukhadaṁ bhavasāram
keśava⋅dhṛta⋅daśavidha⋅rūpa jaya jagadīśa hare

vedān⋅uddharate jaganti vahate bhūgolam⋅udvibhrate
daityān dārayate valiṁ chalayate kṣatrakṣayaṁ kurvate
paulastyaṁ jayate halaṁ kalayate kāruṇyam⋅ātanvate
mlecchān mūrchayate daśākṛtikṛte kṛṣṇāya tubhyaṁ namaḥ
IDX.en_translation:
From the waters of the deluge, You rescued the Vedas; acting like a boat, You saved them without effort. O Keśava, assuming the form of a fish—all glory to You, Lord of the universe, O Hari!

The Earth rests on Your immense back, which is adorned with the marks of carrying the world. O Keśava, assuming the form of a tortoise—all glory to You, Lord of the universe, O Hari!

The Earth rests on the tip of Your tusk, appearing like a dark mark on the moon. O Keśava, assuming the form of a boar—all glory to You, Lord of the universe, O Hari!

On Your beautiful lotus-like hand, Your nails are like wondrous sharp weapons that tore apart the body of the demon king Hiraṇyakaśipu. O Keśava, assuming the form of a man-lion—all glory to You, Lord of the universe, O Hari!

You tricked the demon king Bali with Your strides; in Your wondrous dwarf form, the waters from Your toenail purify all beings. O Keśava, assuming the form of a dwarf—all glory to You, Lord of the universe, O Hari!

You cleanse the world of its sins with the blood of warriors, bathing it in waters that extinguish the fires of material existence. O Keśava, assuming the form of Paraśurāma—all glory to You, Lord of the universe, O Hari!

You distribute the lovely heads of the ten-headed demon in all directions during battle—heads desired even by the guardians of the directions. O Keśava, assuming the form of Rāma—all glory to You, Lord of the universe, O Hari!

You wear garments the color of a cloud on Your pure body, just like the Yamunā appears when frightened by Your plow. O Keśava, assuming the form of the plow-bearer Balarāma—all glory to You, Lord of the universe, O Hari!

You condemn the animal slaughter in Vedic rituals, showing a compassionate heart for the creatures therein. O Keśava, assuming the form of Buddha—all glory to You, Lord of the universe, O Hari!

You wield a sword for the destruction of the impious, appearing terrible like a comet. O Keśava, assuming the form of Kalki—all glory to You, Lord of the universe, O Hari!

Śrī Jayadeva has sung this exalted, auspicious, blissful, and essential song. O Keśava, assuming ten different forms—all glory to You, Lord of the universe, O Hari!

Obeisances to You, Kṛṣṇa, who delivered the Vedas, who upholds the worlds, who holds up the earth, who tore apart the demons, who deceived Bali, who destroyed the arrogant warriors, who conquered Rāvaṇa, who wields the plow, who expands compassion, and who stupefied the impious. O Lord who took on these ten forms, our obeisances!

IDX.first_line: pranesvara nivedana eijana karegovinda gokulacandra parama anandakandagopikula priya dekha more
IDX.search: pranesvara nivedana eijana karegovinda gokulacandra parama anandakandagopikula priya dekha more tuya pada padma seva ei dhana more dibatumi natha karunara nidhiparamamangala yasa sravane parama rasakara kiba karya nahe siddhi
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
prāṇeśvara! nivedana eijana karegovinda gokula-candra, parama ānandakanda,gopīkula⋅priya dekha more

tuyā pāda⋅padma⋅sevā, ei dhana more dibā,tumi nātha karuṇāra nidhiparamamaṅgala⋅yaśa, śravaṇe parama rasa,kā’ra kibā kārya nahe siddhi

dāruṇa saṁsāra⋅gati, viṣama viṣaya⋅mati,tuyā vismaraṇa⋅śela bukejara jara tanu mana, acetana anukṣaṇa,jīyante maraṇa bhela duḥkhe

mo hena adhama jane, kara kṛpā nirīkṣaṇe,dāsa kari’ rākha vṛndāvaneśrī⋅kṛṣṇacaitanya⋅nāma, prabhu mora gaura-dhāma,narottama loila śaraṇe
IDX.en_translation:
O Lord of my life! This person offers a petition: O Govinda, Moon of Gokula, the ultimate source of bliss, please consider me dear to the cowherd girls.

Grant me the service of Your lotus feet; this is my true wealth. You, my Lord, are a treasure house of mercy. Your supremely auspicious glories—hearing them is the highest nectar. What purpose cannot be accomplished through them?

The terrible current of material existence, a difficult material mentality, and the dart of forgetting You pierce my heart. My body and mind are worn out and unconscious every moment. While living, I am as good as dead from sorrow.

Upon such a fallen person like me, please cast Your merciful glance and keep me as a servant in Vṛndāvana. The name of Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya is my Lord, my golden abode. Narottama takes refuge in Him.

IDX.first_line: pranesvari eibara karuna kara more
IDX.search: pranesvari eibara karuna kara more dasanete trna dhari anjali mastake kari eijana nivedana kare priya sahacari sange sevana kariba range ange vesa karibeka sadhe rakha ei seva kaje nija pada pankaje priya sahacarigana majhe
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
prāṇeśvari! eibāra karuṇā kara more
daśanete tṛṇa dhari’, añjali mastake kari,
eijana nivedana kare

priya⋅sahacarī⋅saṅge, sevana kariba raṅge,
aṅge veśa karibeka sādhe
rākha ei sevā⋅kāje, nija pada⋅paṅkaje,
priya sahacarīgaṇa⋅mājhe

sugandhi candana, maṇimaya ābharaṇa,
kauṣika vasana nānā raṅge
ei saba sevā jā̐ra, dāsī jena haṅa tā̐ra,
anukṣaṇa thāki tā̐ra saṅge

jala suvāsita kari, ratana bhṛṅgāre bhari,
karpūravāsita guyā pāna
esaba sājāiyā ḍālā, lavaṅga, mālatī⋅mālā,
bhakṣyadravya nānā anupama

sakhīra iṅgita habe, esaba āniyā kabe,
jogāiba lalitāra kāche
narottama⋅dāsa kaya, ei jena mora haya,
dā̐ḍāiyā rahu sakhīra pāche
IDX.en_translation:
Mistress of my life! This time, please show mercy to me. Holding a blade of grass in my teeth and with folded hands upon my head, I submit this petition.

With Your beloved companions, I will joyfully perform service and eagerly dress Your body with ornaments. Engage me in this service at Your lotus feet, among Your dear companions.

Fragrant sandalwood paste, jewel-studded ornaments, and silk garments of various colors—may I become the maidservant of Her for whom all these services are performed, and always remain with Her.

Making the water fragrant and filling a jeweled pitcher, preparing betel nut flavored with camphor—arranging all these in a basket, along with cloves, a garland of jasmine flowers, and various incomparable foodstuffs.

When the companion gives a signal, when will I bring all these and offer them to Lalitā? Narottama dāsa says: may this be my destiny, to stand behind the companions.

IDX.first_line: pranesvari kabe more habe krpa dithi
IDX.search: pranesvari kabe more habe krpa dithi ajnaya aniya kabe vividha phula vara sunaba racana duhu mithi aruna kamala dale sesa bichaiba basaiba kisora kisori alakabhrta mukha pankaja manohara marakata syama hema gauri
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses: prāṇeśvari kabe more habe kṛpa diṭhi ājñāya āniyā kabe vividha phula⋅vara śunaba racana duhu miṭhi arūṇa⋅kamala⋅dale śeṣa bichāiba basāiba kiśora⋅kiśorī alakābhṛta⋅mukha⋅paṅkaja manohara marakata⋅śyāma hema⋅gaurī mṛgamada tilaka sa⋅sindūra banāyaba lepaba candana⋅gandhe gā̐thi mālatī⋅phula hāra parāiba dhāoyāiba madhukara⋅vṛnde lalitā kabe more vījana deoyaba vījana māruta mande śrama⋅jala sakala miṭaba duhu⋅kalevara heraba parama ānande narottama⋅dāsa āśa pada⋅paṅkaja sevana mādhurī⋅pāne ho⋅oba hena dina nā dekhiye kona cihna duhu⋅jana heraba nayane
IDX.en_translation:
O Mistress of my life! When will You cast Your merciful glance upon me? When will I bring various excellent flowers by Your order and listen to the sweet conversation of the Divine Couple?

I will make a bed of red lotus petals and seat the Youthful Couple upon it. Their lotus faces are enchanting, surrounded by curling hair. One is dark like an emerald and the other has a golden complexion.

I will make tilaka markings with musk and vermilion and anoint Them with the scent of sandalwood. I will string a garland of jasmine flowers and place it on Them, while swarms of bumblebees hover nearby.

When will Lalitā-sakhī give me a fan and allow me to fan Them with a gentle breeze? All the perspiration of fatigue will vanish from Their bodies, and I will gaze upon Them with supreme bliss.

Narottama Dāsa hopes to drink the sweetness of serving Their lotus feet. When will such a day come? I do not see any sign of it now, but I long to see the Divine Couple with my own eyes.

IDX.first_line: pravesiya antahpure narigane santa kare
IDX.search: pravesiya antahpure narigane santa kare srivasa amiya upadese suna pagalinigana soka kara akarana kiba duhkha thake krsnavese krsna nitya suta jara soka kabhu nahi tara anitye asakti sarvanasa asiyacha e samsare krsna bhajibara tare nitya tattve karaha vilasa
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
praveśiyā antaḥpure, nārīgaṇe śānta kare,
śrīvāsa amiya⋅upadeśe
śuna pāgalinī-gaṇa, śoka kara akāraṇa,
kibā duḥkha thāke kṛṣṇāveśe

kṛṣṇa nitya suta jā’ra, śoka kabhu nāhi tā’ra,
anitye āsakti sarvanāśa
āsiyācha e saṁsāre, kṛṣṇa bhajibāra tare,
nitya⋅tattve karaha vilāsa

e dehe jāvat sthiti, kara kṛṣṇa-candre rati,
kṛṣṇe jāna, dhana⋅jana⋅prāṇa
e deha⋅anuga jata, bhāi⋅bandhu⋅pati⋅suta,
anitya sambandha bali’ māna

kebā kā’ra pati⋅suta, anitya⋅sambandha⋅kṛta,
cāhile rākhite nāre tā’re
karama⋅vipāka⋅phale, suta ha’ye baise kole,
karmakṣaye āra raite nāre

ite sukha⋅duḥkha māni’, adhogati labhe prāṇī,
kṛṣṇapada haite paḍe dūre
śoka sambariyā ebe, nāmānande maja’ sabe,
bhakativinoda⋅vāñchā pūre
IDX.en_translation:
Entering the inner chambers, Śrīvāsa pacified the women with nectarean instructions: "Listen, O mad women, you lament without cause; what sorrow can remain when one is absorbed in Kṛṣṇa?

One whose eternal son is Kṛṣṇa never has sorrow; attachment to the temporary is utter ruin. You have come to this world for the purpose of worshiping Kṛṣṇa; therefore, delight in the eternal truth.

As long as there is existence in this body, develop attachment for Kṛṣṇa⋅candra; know Kṛṣṇa as your wealth, people, and life. Consider all those connected to this body—brothers, friends, husband, son—as temporary relationships.

Who is whose husband or son? Even if you wish, you cannot keep these temporary relationships. As a result of past actions, a son sits on your lap, but when the effect of karma is finished, he cannot remain.

Considering these temporary things as happiness and sorrow, the living entity attains a lower destination, falling far from Kṛṣṇa's feet. Therefore, suppressing your grief now, everyone immerse yourselves in the bliss of the Holy Name; thus fulfill the desire of Bhaktivinoda."

IDX.first_line: preme matta nityananda sahaje ananda kanda
IDX.search: preme matta nityananda sahaje ananda kanda dhuliya dhuliya cali jaya bhaiyara bhavete matta janena sakala tattva hari bali avani lotaya nitaira gorapreme gada tanukhani gadadhara mukha here loliya loliya pade dhara vahe sincita dharani
IDX.author: ananta dāsa
IDX.verses:
preme matta nityānanda, sahaje ānanda kanda,
ḍhuliyā ḍhuliyā cali’ jāya
bhāiyāra bhāvete matta, jānena sakala tattva,
hari bali’ avanī loṭāya

nitāira gorāpreme gaḍā tanukhāni
gadādhara mukha here, loliyā loliyā paḍe,
dhārā vahe siñcita dharaṇī

advaita ānanda kanda, heri’ nitāira mukha-candra,
huṅkāra pulaka śobhā pāya
hari hari bola bale, punaḥ ‘gaura’ ‘gaura’ bale,
priya pāriṣada-gaṇa dhāya

golokera prema-vanyā, jagat karila dhanyā,
atula apāra rasasindhu
mātila jagat bhari’, nitāi caitanya kari’,
anantadāsa māge eka bindu
IDX.en_translation:
Lord Nityānanda is intoxicated with divine love. He is naturally the root of all joy. He sways and moves along, absorbed in the mood of a brother to Lord Caitanya. He knows all truths. Crying "Hari!", He rolls on the ground.

Lord Nitāi's body is made of the divine love of Lord Gaurāṅga. Seeing the face of Gadādhara Paṇḍita, He becomes overwhelmed and falls down, and a stream of tears flows, sprinkling the earth.

Advaita Ācārya, the root of joy, sees the moon-like face of Lord Nitāi and roars with ecstatic joy. He chants "Hari! Hari! Bol!" and again says "Gaura! Gaura!". The beloved associates run toward Them.

The flood of divine love from Goloka has made the world blessed. It is an incomparable, boundless ocean of divine mellows. The whole world became mad with love through Nitāi and Caitanya. Ananta Dāsa begs for a single drop.

IDX.first_line: purave govardhana dharala anuja jara
IDX.search: purave govardhana dharala anuja jara jagajane kahe balarama ebe se caitanya sange aila kirtana range dhari pahu nityananda nama parama udara karunamaya vigraha bhuvanamangala guna dhama gaura piriti rase katira vasana khase avatara ati anupama
IDX.author: jñānadāsa
IDX.verses:
pūrave govardhana, dharala anuja jā̐’ra,
jagajane kahe balarāma
ebe se caitanya⋅saṅge, āilā kīrtana⋅raṅge,
dhari’ pa̐hu nityānanda nāma

parama udāra, karuṇāmaya vigraha,
bhuvanamaṅgala guṇa⋅dhāma
gaura⋅pīriti⋅rase, kaṭira vasana khase,
avatāra ati anupama

nācata gāota, hari hari bolata,
avirata gaura⋅gopāla
hāsa⋅prakāśa, milita madhurādhare,
bolata parama rasāla

rāmadāsera pa̐hu, sundara vigraha,
gaurīdāsera dhana⋅prāṇe
akhila jīva jata, iha rase unamata,
jñānadāsa nitāi guṇagāne
IDX.en_translation:
In the past, His younger brother held Govardhana Hill. The people of the world call Him Balarāma. Now, He has come with Lord Caitanya in the joy of congregational chanting, assuming the name of Lord Nityānanda.

He is supremely magnanimous, the embodiment of mercy, and the abode of auspicious qualities for the whole universe. In the nectar of love for Lord Gaura, His waist-cloth loosens. He is a most incomparable incarnation.

He dances and sings, chanting "Hari! Hari!" incessantly for Lord Gaura-Gopāla. A radiant smile adorns His sweet lips, and He speaks words full of supreme nectar.
