He is the Master of Rāmadāsa, a beautiful form, and the wealth and life of Gaurīdāsa Paṇḍita. All the living entities in the universe become intoxicated in this mellow. Jñānadāsa sings the glories of Lord Nitāi.

IDX.first_line: purnacidananda tumi tomara citkana ami
IDX.search: purnacidananda tumi tomara citkana ami svabhavatah ami tuya dasa parama svatantra tumi tuya paratantra ami tuya pada chadi sarvanasa svatantra haye jakhana maya prati kainu mana sva svabhava chadila amaya prapance mayara bandhe padinu karmera dhande karmacakre amare phelaya
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
“pūrṇa-cidānanda tumi, tomāra citkaṇa āmi,
svabhāvataḥ āmi tuyā dāsa
parama svatantra tumi, tuyā paratantra āmi,
tuyā pada chāḍi’ sarvanāśa

svatantra ha’ye jakhana, māyā⋅prati kainu mana,
sva⋅svabhāva chāḍila āmāya
prapañce māyāra bandhe, paḍinu karmera dhande,
karma-cakre āmāre phelāya

māyā tava icchāmate, bā̐dhe mora e jagate,
adṛṣṭa⋅nirbandha⋅lauha⋅kare
sei ta’ nirbandha more, āne śrīvāsera ghare,
putrarūpe mālinī⋅jaṭhare

se⋅nirbandha punarāya, more ebe la’ye jāya,
āmi ta’ thākite nāri āra
tava icchā suprabala, mora icchā sudurbala,
āmi jīva akiñcana chāra

jathāya pāṭhāo tumi, avaśya jāiba āmi,
kā’ra kebā putra⋅pati⋅pitā
jaḍera sambandha saba, tāhā nāhi satya saba,
tumi jīvera nitya pālayitā

saṁyoga⋅viyoge jini, sukha⋅duḥkha mane gaṇi’,
tava pade chāḍena āśraya
māyāra gardabha ha’ye, majena saṁsāra la’ye”,
bhaktivinodera sei bhaya
IDX.en_translation:
You are full of eternal bliss and knowledge, and I am a tiny particle of Your consciousness. Naturally, I am Your servant. You are supremely independent, and I am dependent on You. To leave Your feet is complete ruin.

When I desired to be independent and turned my mind toward illusion, my true nature left me. In this material world, bound by illusion, I fell into the confusion of fruitive action. The cycle of karma throws me about.

By Your will, illusion binds me in this world with the iron shackles of destiny. That very destiny brought me to the house of Śrīvāsa as a son in the womb of Mālinī.

That destiny now takes me away again. I cannot stay any longer. Your will is supremely powerful, and my will is very weak. I am a lowly, insignificant soul.

Wherever You send me, I will certainly go. Who is anyone's son, husband, or father? All these material relationships are not ultimately real. You are the eternal maintainer of the soul.

One who, in union or separation, considers happiness and distress in the mind and forsakes shelter at Your feet becomes a beast of burden for illusion and is absorbed in the material world." That is the fear of Bhaktivinoda.

IDX.first_line: radha bhajane jadi mati nahi bhela
IDX.search: radha bhajane jadi mati nahi bhela krsnabhajana tava akarane gela atapa rahita suraya nahi jani radha virahita madhava nahi mani
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
rādhā⋅bhajane jadi mati nāhi bhelā
kṛṣṇa-bhajana tava akāraṇe gelā

ātapa⋅rahita sūraya nāhi jāni
rādhā⋅virahita mādhava nāhi māni

kevala mādhava pūjaye, so ajñānī
rādhā⋅anādara karoi abhimānī

kabahi̐ nāhi karabi tā̐kara saṅga
citte icchasi jadi vrajarasa⋅raṅga

rādhikā⋅dāsī jadi hoya abhimāna
śīghroi miloi tava gokula⋅kāna

brahmā, śiva, nārada, śruti, nārāyaṇī
rādhikā⋅padaraja pūjaye māni

umā, ramā, satyā, śacī, candrā, rukmiṇī
rādhā⋅avatāra sabe,⋅āmnāya⋅vāṇī

hena rādhā⋅paricaryā jā̐kara dhana
bhakativinoda tā̐’ra māgaye caraṇa
IDX.en_translation:
If your mind does not turn toward the worship of Rādhā, Your Kṛṣṇa-bhajana goes in vain.

I know of no sun without its heat, I recognize no Mādhava without Rādhā.

One who worships only Mādhava is ignorant; One who neglects Rādhā is filled with false pride.

Never keep the company of such people, If your heart desires the joys of Vraja-rasa.

If you possess the pride of being a maidservant of Rādhikā, You will very quickly meet the Kāna of Gokula.

Brahmā, Śiva, Nārada, the Śruti, and Nārāyaṇī, All honor and worship the foot-dust of Rādhikā.

Pārvatī, Lakṣmī, Satyā, Śacī, Candrā, Rukmiṇī, All are incarnations of Rādhā-this is the verdict of the scriptures.

Bhaktivinoda begs for the feet of those Whose only wealth is the service of such a Rādhā.

IDX.first_line: radha bhajane jadi mati nahi bhelakrsnabhajana tava akarane gela
IDX.search: radha bhajane jadi mati nahi bhelakrsnabhajana tava akarane gela atapa rahita suraya nahi janiradha virahita madhava nahi mani
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
rādhā⋅bhajane jadi mati nāhi bhelākṛṣṇa-bhajana tava akāraṇe gelā

ātapa⋅rahita sūraya nāhi jānirādhā⋅virahita mādhava nāhi māni

kevala mādhava pūjaye, so ajñānīrādhā⋅anādara karoi abhimānī

kabahi̐ nāhi karabi tā̐kara saṅgacitte icchasi jadi vrajarasa⋅raṅga

rādhikā⋅dāsī jadi hoya abhimānaśīghroi miloi tava gokula⋅kāna

brahmā, śiva, nārada, śruti, nārāyaṇīrādhikā⋅padaraja pūjaye māni

umā, ramā, satyā, śacī, candrā, rukmiṇīrādhā⋅avatāra sabe,⋅āmnāya⋅vāṇī

hena rādhā⋅paricaryā jā̐kara dhanabhakativinoda tā̐’ra māgaye caraṇa
IDX.en_translation:
If your mind does not turn toward the worship of Rādhā, Your Kṛṣṇa-bhajana goes in vain.

I know of no sun without its heat, I recognize no Mādhava without Rādhā.

One who worships only Mādhava is ignorant; One who neglects Rādhā is filled with false pride.

Never keep the company of such people, If your heart desires the joys of Vraja-rasa.

If you possess the pride of being a maidservant of Rādhikā, You will very quickly meet the Kāna of Gokula.

Brahmā, Śiva, Nārada, the Śruti, and Nārāyaṇī, All honor and worship the foot-dust of Rādhikā.

Pārvatī, Lakṣmī, Satyā, Śacī, Candrā, Rukmiṇī, All are incarnations of Rādhā-this is the verdict of the scriptures.

Bhaktivinoda begs for the feet of those Whose only wealth is the service of such a Rādhā.

IDX.first_line: radha cinta nivesena
IDX.search: radha cinta nivesena jasya kantir vilopita sri krsna caranam vande radhalingita vigraham sevya sevaka sambhoge dvayor bhedah kuto bhavet vipralambhe tu sarvasya bhedah sada vivardhate
IDX.author: śrīmadbhaktiprajñāna keśava gosvāmī mahārāja
IDX.verses:
rādhā⋅cintā⋅niveśena
jasya kāntir⋅vilopitā
śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅caraṇaṁ vande
rādhāliṅgita⋅vigraham

sevya⋅sevaka⋅sambhoge
dvayor⋅bhedaḥ kuto bhavet
vipralambhe tu sarvasya
bhedaḥ sadā vivardhate

cillīlā⋅mithunaṁ tattvaṁ
bhedābhedamacintyakam
śakti⋅śaktimatoraikyaṁ
jugapadvartate sadā

tattvamekaṁ paraṁ vidyāl
līlayā taddvidhāsthitam
gauraḥ kṛṣṇaḥ svayaṁ hyetad
ubhāvubhayamāpnutaḥ

sarve varṇā jatrāviṣṭā
gaura⋅kāntir⋅vikāśate
sarva⋅varṇena hīnastu
kṛṣṇa⋅varṇaḥ prakāśate

saguṇaṁ nirguṇaṁ tattvam
ekamevādvitīyakam
sarva⋅nitya⋅guṇair⋅gauraḥ
kṛṣṇa⋅rasastu nirguṇaiḥ

śrī⋅kṛṣṇaṁ mithunaṁ brahma
tyaktvā tu nirguṇaṁ hi tat
upāsate mṛṣā vijñāḥ
jathā tuṣāvaghātinaḥ

śrī⋅vinoda⋅vihārī jo
rādhayā milito jadā
tadāhaṁ vandanaṁ kuryāṁ
sarasvatī⋅prasādataḥ

iti tattvāṣṭakaṁ nityaṁ
jaḥ paṭhet śraddhayānvitaḥ
kṛṣṇa⋅tattvamabhijñāya
gaura⋅pade bhavenmatiḥ
IDX.en_translation:
Due to absorption in thoughts of Rādhā, His own luster is hidden; I bow to the lotus feet of Śrī Kṛṣṇa, whose form is embraced by Rādhā.

In the enjoyment of the served and the servant, how can there be a difference between them? Yet in separation, the difference between them always increases.

The principle of the divine playful couple is inconceivable as both different and non-different. The oneness of the energy and its possessor always exists simultaneously.

Know the Supreme Reality to be one; through divine sport, it exists in two ways. Gaura and Kṛṣṇa are verily the same; both obtain each other.

Where all colors are absorbed, the golden luster manifests. But when devoid of all colors, the blackish color shines forth.

The Absolute with attributes and without attributes is one and without a second. Gaura is endowed with all eternal qualities, while Kṛṣṇa's mellow is beyond them.

The so-called wise falsely worship the impersonal Absolute, abandoning Śrī Kṛṣṇa, the Divine Couple; they are just like those who beat empty husks.

When Śrī Vinoda-bihārī is united with Rādhā, I offer my obeisances by the grace of Śrīla Bhaktisiddhānta Sarasvatī Prabhupāda.

Thus, if one daily reads these eight verses with faith, knowing the principle of Kṛṣṇa, his mind becomes fixed at the feet of Lord Caitanya.

IDX.first_line: radha krsna gunagana mithasakti samvardhana
IDX.search: radha krsna gunagana mithasakti samvardhana ubhayabhisara sampadana krsne sakhi samarpana narmavakya asvadana ubhayera suvesa racana cittabhava udghatana mithacchidra saŋgopana pratipajanera suvancana kusala siksana ara sammilana dujanara vyajanadi vividha sevana
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa⋅guṇagāna, mithāsakti⋅samvardhana,
ubhayābhisāra⋅sampādana
kṛṣṇe sakhī⋅samarpaṇa, narmavākya⋅āsvādana,
ubhayera suveśa⋅racana

cittabhāva⋅udghāṭana, mithacchidra⋅saŋgopana,
pratīpajanera suvañcana
kuśala⋅śikṣaṇa āra, sammilana du’janāra,
vyajanādi vividha sevana

nāyikāra daśākāle, prāṇarakṣā sukauśale,
sakhī⋅sādhāraṇakārya jāna

jebā je sakhīra kārya, viśeṣa baliyā dhārya,
pradarśita ha’be jathāsthāne
rūpānuga bhaje jebā, je sakhīra jei sevā,
tadanuga sei sevā māne
IDX.en_translation:
Singing the glories of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa, increasing their mutual affection, and arranging for their meeting; presenting the female friend to Kṛṣṇa, relishing witty conversations, and beautifully adorning the Divine Couple.

Revealing the sentiments of the heart, concealing their mutual faults, and skillfully deceiving rivals; teaching skillful acts, arranging the meeting of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa, and performing various services like fanning.

Skillfully saving the life of the heroine during her various emotional states—know these as the general duties of a female friend.

Whatever specific duty belongs to which friend will be explained in due course. Whoever worships following Rūpa Gosvāmī accepts that service according to whichever friend's service he follows.

IDX.first_line: radha krsna karo dhyana svapane na bala ana
IDX.search: radha krsna karo dhyana svapane na bala ana prema vinu ara nahi cana jugalakisora prema jini laksavana hema arati piriti rase dhana jala vinu jena mina duhkha paya ayuhina prema vinu eimata bhakta cataka jalada gati emati ekanta rati jei jane sei anurakta
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa karo dhyāna, svapane nā bala āna,
prema vinu āra nāhi cāṅa
jugalakiśora prema, jini’ lakṣavāna hema,
ārati⋅pirīti⋅rase dhāṅa

jala vinu jena mīna, duḥkha pāya āyuhīna,
prema vinu eimata bhakta
cātaka jalada gati, emati ekānta⋅rati,
jei jāne, sei anurakta

saroja⋅bhramara jena, cakora⋅candrikā tena,
pativratā strīlokera pati
anyatra nā cale mana, jena daridrera dhana,
eimata premabhakti rīti

viṣaya garalamaya, nāhe māna’ sukhacara,
se nā sukha, duḥkha kari’ māna’
govinda viṣaya rasa, saṅga kara tā̐ra dāsa,
premabhakti satya kari’ jāna’

madhye madhye āche duṣṭa, dṛṣṭi kari’ haya ruṣṭa,
guṇahi viguṇa kari’ māne
govinda vimukhajane, sphūrti nahe hena dhane,
laukika kariyā saba jāne

ajñāna abhāgā jata, nāhi laya sata⋅mata,
ahaṅkāra nā jāne āpanā
abhimānī bhaktihīna, jagamājhe sei dīna,
vṛthā tāra aśeṣa bhāvanā

āra saba parihari’, parama īśvara hari,
seva mana prema kari’ āśā
eka vraja⋅rājapura, govinda rasikavara,
karaha sadāi abhilāṣā

narottama⋅dāsa kahe, sadā mora prāṇa dahe,
hena bhakta⋅saṅga nā pāiyā
abhāgyera nāhi ora, michāmohe hainu bhora,
duḥkha rahe antare jāgiyā
IDX.en_translation:
I meditate on Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa; in my dreams I speak of nothing else. Except for divine love, I desire nothing else. The love of the youthful Divine Couple, surpassing the beauty of millions of gold coins—I chase after it with ardent affection.

Just as a fish suffers endlessly without water, its life ending, so too is a devotee without divine love. Like the chakora bird's desire for rain clouds—such is one-pointed devotion; whoever knows this is truly devoted.

Like a bee to a lotus, or a chakora bird to the moonlight, or a chaste wife to her husband—my mind does not wander elsewhere. Like a poor man's treasure, this is the principle of loving devotion.

Material enjoyments are like poison; do not consider them happiness. Regard that not as happiness, but as sorrow. The sweet mellows of Govinda—associate with His servants and know that loving devotion is the truth.

Among them are wicked people who become angry when they see truth and consider good qualities to be faults. To those averse to Govinda, such wealth does not manifest; they consider everything to be mundane.

All the ignorant and unfortunate ones do not accept the true path; due to ego, they do not know themselves. The arrogant and devotionless are the most wretched in this world; their endless thoughts are in vain.

Giving up everything else, serve the Supreme Lord Hari with your mind, desiring divine love. The abode of Vraja, where Govinda is the best of those who relish mellows—always yearn for Him.

Narottama dāsa says: "My heart always burns, not having found the association of such devotees. My misfortune has no end; I am absorbed in false illusion, and sorrow remains awake within my heart."

IDX.first_line: radha krsna nivedana ei jana karedohe ati rasamaya sakaruna hrdayaavadhana kara natha more
IDX.search: radha krsna nivedana ei jana karedohe ati rasamaya sakaruna hrdayaavadhana kara natha more he krsna gokulacandra gopi jana vallabhahe krsnapreyasi siromanihemagauri syama gaya sravane parasa payaguna suni judaya parani
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa! nivedana ei jana karedohe ati rasamaya, sakaruṇa⋅hṛdaya,avadhāna kara nātha more

he kṛṣṇa gokula-candra, gopī⋅jana⋅vallabha,he kṛṣṇapreyasī⋅śiromaṇihema-gaurī śyāma⋅gāya, śravaṇe paraśa pāya,guṇa śuni’ juḍāya parāṇī

adhama durgata jane, kevala karuṇā mane,tribhuvane e jaśaḥkheyātiśuniyā sādhura mukhe, śaraṇa loinu sukhe,upekhile nāhi mora gati

jaya rādhe jaya kṛṣṇa, jaya jaya rādhe kṛṣṇa,kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa jaya jaya rādheañjali mastake kari’, narottama bhūme paḍi’,kahe dohe purāo manaḥ-sādhe
IDX.en_translation:
O Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa! This soul makes this humble submission. Both of You are full of divine mellows and have compassionate hearts; please listen to me, my Lords.

O Kṛṣṇa, Moon of Gokula, beloved of the cowherd girls! O best among Kṛṣṇa’s beloveds! You are golden-complexioned, and He is dark-complexioned. Just hearing about You brings solace; hearing Your qualities pacifies my soul.

For the fallen and distressed, You have only compassion in Your minds. Your fame and renown are known throughout the three worlds. Hearing from the mouths of the saintly, I joyfully took refuge. If You neglect me, I have no other recourse.

All glories to Rādhā! All glories to Kṛṣṇa! All glories, all glories to Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa! With folded hands above his head, Narottama falls to the ground and says: “Both of You, please fulfill my heart’s desire”.

IDX.first_line: radha krsna prana mora jugala kisora
IDX.search: radha krsna prana mora jugala kisora jivane marane gati ara nahi mora kalindira kule keli kadambera vana ratana vedira upara basaba dujana
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa prāṇa mora jugala⋅kiśora
jīvane maraṇe gati āra nāhi mora

kālindīra kūle keli⋅kadambera vana
ratana vedīra upara basāba du’jana

śyāmagaurī aṅge diba (cuyā) candanera gandha
cāmara ḍhulāba kabe heriba mukha-candra

gā̐thiyā mālatīra mālā diba dohāra gale
adhare tuliyā diba karpūra tāmbūle

lalitā viśākhā ādi jata sakhīvṛnda
ājñāya kariba sevā caraṇāravinda

śrī⋅kṛṣṇacaitanya prabhura dāsera anudāsa
sevā abhilāṣa kare narottama⋅dāsa
IDX.en_translation:
Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa are my life, the youthful Couple. In life or death, I have no other refuge.

On the bank of the Kālindī, in the forest of playful kadamba trees, I will seat the Two on a jeweled throne.

I will smear sandalwood paste scented with 'cuyā' on the dark and fair limbs of Śyāma and Gaurī. Fanning Them with a yak-tail whisk, when will I gaze upon Their moon-like faces?

Stringing a garland of mālatī flowers, I will place it around Both of Their necks. I will lift camphor-laced betel nuts to Their lips.

Lalitā, Viśākhā, and all the other sakhīs—by their order, I will serve the lotus feet.

The servant of the servant of Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya Prabhu—Narottama Dāsa desires this service.

IDX.search: radha krsna prana mora jugala kisorajivane marane gati ara nahi mora kalindira kule keli kadambera vanaratana vedira upara basaba dujana
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa prāṇa mora jugala⋅kiśorajīvane maraṇe gati āra nāhi mora

kālindīra kūle keli⋅kadambera vanaratana vedīra upara basāba du’jana

śyāmagaurī aṅge diba (cuyā) candanera gandhacāmara ḍhulāba kabe heriba mukha-candra

gā̐thiyā mālatīra mālā diba dohāra galeadhare tuliyā diba karpūra tāmbūle

lalitā viśākhā ādi jata sakhīvṛndaājñāya kariba sevā caraṇāravinda

śrī⋅kṛṣṇacaitanya prabhura dāsera anudāsasevā abhilāṣa kare narottama⋅dāsa
IDX.en_translation:
Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa are my life, the youthful Couple. In life or death, I have no other refuge.

On the bank of the Kālindī, in the forest of playful kadamba trees, I will seat the Two on a jeweled throne.

I will smear sandalwood paste scented with “cuyā” on the dark and fair limbs of Śyāma and Gaurī. Fanning Them with a yak-tail whisk, when will I gaze upon Their moon-like faces?

Stringing a garland of mālatī flowers, I will place it around Both of Their necks. I will lift camphor-laced betel nuts to Their lips.

Lalitā, Viśākhā, and all the other sakhīs—by their order, I will serve the lotus feet.

The servant of the servant of Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya Prabhu—Narottama Dāsa desires this service.

IDX.first_line: radha kundatata kunjakutira
IDX.search: radha kundatata kunjakutira govardhana parvata jamunatira kusumasarovara manasaganga kalinda nandini vipulataranga
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
rādhā⋅kuṇḍataṭa⋅kuñjakuṭīra
govardhana⋅parvata, jāmunatīra

kusumasarovara, mānasagaṅgā
kalinda⋅nandinī vipulataraṅgā

vaṁśīvaṭa, gokula, dhīrasamīra
vṛndāvana⋅taru⋅latikā⋅vānīra

khaga⋅mṛgakula, malaya⋅vātāsa
mayūra, bhramara, muralī, vilāsa

veṇu, śṛṅga, padacihna, meghamālā
vasanta, śaśāṅka, śaṅkha, karatālā

jugalavilāse anukūla jāni
līlā⋅vilāsa⋅uddīpaka māni

e saba choḍata ka̐hi nāhi jā̐u
e saba choḍata parāṇa hārā̐u

bhakativinoda kahe, śuna kāna !
tuyā uddīpaka hāmārā parāṇa
IDX.translation_intro: From The Harmonist, February 1929. Edited by Bhaktisiddhanta Sarasvati.
IDX.en_translation:
The groved cottage by the side of the pool of Radha, The Gobardhan Mount, the Bank of the Yamuna

The Kusuma Tank. the Manasa Ganga, The'Darling Daughter of Kalinda of mighty current,

Bansibata, Gokula, Dhir-Samira, The water thut serves the trees and creepers of Brindaban,

All different species of Birds and Deer, the Malaya Breeze, Peacocks, Black Bees, the Diversions of the Flute,

Cane, Horn, Font.prints, Wreaths of Clouds, Spring, Moon, Conch, karatala,-

Knowing these to accord well with the blissful sports of the Divine pair I cherish as excitants of the resurgence of the Divine pastimes

I go nowhere leaving these, I lose my life if T give them up

‘Listen, O Kan, says Bhaktivinode, ‘My life is an excitant of Thee’.

IDX.first_line: radha madhava kunjaviharigopi janavallabha girivaradharijasoda nandana vraja janaranjanajamunatira vanacari
IDX.search: radha madhava kunjaviharigopi janavallabha girivaradharijasoda nandana vraja janaranjanajamunatira vanacari
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses: rādhā⋅mādhava kuñjavihārīgopī⋅janavallabha girivaradhārījaśodā⋅nandana, vraja⋅janarañjana,jāmunatīra⋅vanacārī
IDX.en_translation: Rādhā-Mādhava is the enjoyer of pastimes in the groves. He is the beloved of the gopīs and the lifter of Govardhana Hill. He is the son of Mother Yaśodā, the delighter of the inhabitants of Vraja, and the wanderer in the forests along the banks of the Yamunā.

IDX.first_line: radha rani ki jaya maharani ki jaya
IDX.search: radha rani ki jaya maharani ki jaya bolo barsanevali ki jaya jaya jaya thakurani ki jaya hari pyari ki jaya vrsabhanu dulali ki jaya jaya jaya
IDX.verses:
rādhā⋅rāṇī kī jaya! mahārāṇī kī jaya!
bolo barsāṇevālī kī jaya jaya jaya!

ṭhākurāṇī kī jaya! hari⋅pyārī kī jaya!
vṛṣabhānu⋅dulālī kī jaya jaya jaya!

gaurāṅgī kī jaya! hemāṅgī kī jaya!
vraja⋅rāja⋅kumārī kī jaya jaya jaya!

vrajarāṇī kī jaya! vraja⋅devī kī jaya!
gahavara⋅vanavālī kī jaya jaya jaya!

giridhārī kī jaya! vana⋅varī kī jaya!
bolo kuñja⋅vihari jiu kī jaya jaya jaya!

bolo vinoda vihari jiu kī jaya jaya jaya!
bolo ramaṇa vihari jiu kī jaya jaya jaya!
IDX.en_translation:
Glory to Queen Rādhā! Glory to the Great Queen! Chant "Glory, Glory, Glory to Her who resides in Barṣāṇā!"

Glory to the Mistress! Glory to the Beloved of Hari! Chant "Glory, Glory, Glory to the Daughter of Vṛṣabhānu!"

Glory to the Golden-limbed One! Glory to the Golden-bodied One! Chant "Glory, Glory, Glory to the Princess of the King of Vraja!"

Glory to the Queen of Vraja! Glory to the Goddess of Vraja! Chant "Glory, Glory, Glory to Her who lives in the Gahavara forest!"

Glory to the Holder of the Hill! Glory to the Lady of the Forest! Chant "Glory, Glory, Glory to the Enjoyer of the Groves!"

Chant "Glory, Glory, Glory to the Playful Enjoyer!" Chant "Glory, Glory, Glory to the Lover and Enjoyer!"

IDX.first_line: radhadesa nama ekacakra grama
IDX.search: radhadesa nama ekacakra grama hadai pandita ghara subha magha masi sukla trayodasi janamila haladhara hadai pandita ati harasita putra mahotsava kare dharani mandala kare talamala ananda nahika dhare
IDX.author: kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa
IDX.verses:
rāḍhadeśa nāma, ekacakrā⋅grāma,
hāḍāi paṇḍita ghara
śubha māgha⋅māsi, śuklā⋅trayodaśī,
janamilā haladhara

hāḍāi paṇḍita, ati haraṣita,
putra⋅mahotsava kare
dharaṇī⋅maṇḍala, kare ṭalamala,
ānanda nāhika dhare

śāntipura⋅nātha, mane haraṣita,
kari’ kichu anumāna
antare jānilā, bujhi janamilā,
kṛṣṇera agraja rāma

vaiṣṇavera mana, haila parasanna,
ānanda⋅sāgare bhāse
e dīna pāmara, hoibe uddhāra,
kahe duḥkhī kṛṣṇa⋅dāse
IDX.en_translation:
In the land named Rāṛha, in the village of Ekacakrā, in the home of Haṛāi Paṇḍita—on the auspicious thirteenth day of the bright fortnight of the month of Māgha—Lord Balarāma was born.

Haṛāi Paṇḍita, exceedingly joyous, celebrated a grand festival for his son. The entire world trembled with delight, unable to contain its joy.

Advaita Ācārya, the Lord of Śāntipura, became joyful in his heart and made an inference. He understood within that Lord Balarāma, the elder brother of Kṛṣṇa, had taken birth.

The minds of all the devotees became very pleased and they floated in an ocean of joy. The sorrowful Kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa says: "This lowly and fallen soul will surely be delivered."

IDX.first_line: radhamukunda padasambhava gharmabindu
IDX.search: radhamukunda padasambhava gharmabindu nirmanchanopakaranikrta dehalaksam uttunga sauhrda visesa vasat pragalbham devim gunaih sulalitam lalitam namami rakasudha kirana mandala kanti dandi vaktrasriyam cakita caru camuru netram radha prasadhana vidhanakala prasiddham devim gunaih sulalitam lalitam namami
IDX.author: śrīla rūpa gosvāmī
IDX.verses:
rādhāmukunda⋅padasambhava⋅gharmabindu⋅
nirmañchanopakaraṇīkṛta⋅dehalakṣām
uttuṅga⋅sauhṛda⋅viśeṣa⋅vaśāt pragalbhāṁ
devīṁ guṇaiḥ sulalitāṁ lalitāṁ namāmi

rākāsudhā⋅kiraṇa⋅maṇḍala⋅kānti⋅daṇḍi⋅
vaktraśriyaṁ cakita⋅cāru⋅camūru⋅netrām
rādhā⋅prasādhana⋅vidhānakalā⋅prasiddhāṁ
devīṁ guṇaiḥ sulalitāṁ lalitāṁ namāmi

lāsyollasad⋅bhujagaśatru⋅patatracitra⋅
paṭṭāṁśukābharaṇa⋅kañculikāñcitāṅgīm
gorocanā⋅ruci⋅vigarhaṇa⋅gaurimāṇaṁ
devīṁ guṇaiḥ sulalitāṁ lalitāṁ namāmi

dhūrte vrajendra⋅tanaye tanu suṣṭhu vāmyaṁ
mā dakṣiṇā bhava kalaṅkini! lāghavāya
rādhe! giraṁ śṛṇu hitāmiti śikṣayantīṁ
devīṁ guṇaiḥ sulalitāṁ lalitāṁ namāmi

rādhāmabhi vrajapateḥ kṛtamātmajena
kūṭaṁ manāgapi vilokya vilohitākṣīm
vāgbhaṅgibhistamacireṇa vilajjayantīṁ
devīṁ guṇaiḥ sulalitāṁ lalitāṁ namāmi

vātsalyavṛnda⋅vasatiṁ paśupāla⋅rājñyāḥ
sakhyānuśikṣaṇa⋅kalāsu guruṁ sakhīnām
rādhā⋅valāvaraja⋅jīvita⋅nirviśeṣāṁ
devīṁ guṇaiḥ sulalitāṁ lalitāṁ namāmi

jāṁ kāmapi vrajakule vṛṣabhānujāyāḥ
prekṣya svapakṣa⋅padavīmanu ruddhamānām
sadyastadiṣṭa⋅ghaṭanena kṛtārthayantīṁ
devīṁ guṇaiḥ sulalitāṁ lalitāṁ namāmi

rādhā⋅vrajendrasuta⋅saṅgama⋅raṅgacaryāṁ
varyāṁ viniścitavatīmakhilotsavebhyaḥ
tāṁ gokula⋅priyasakhī⋅nikuramva⋅mukhyāṁ
devīṁ guṇaiḥ sulalitāṁ lalitāṁ namāmi

nandannamūni lalitā⋅guṇa⋅lālitāni
padyāni jaḥ paṭhati nirmala⋅dṛṣṭiraṣṭau
prītyā vikarṣati janaṁ nija-vṛnda-madhye
taṁ kīrtidāpati⋅kulojjvala⋅kalpavallī
IDX.en_translation:
I offer my obeisances to the goddess Lalitā, who is very charming with all her qualities. Her body is used as an instrument to wipe away the drops of sweat originating from the lotus feet of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa, and she is very bold due to her exceedingly elevated and special friendship.

I offer my obeisances to the goddess Lalitā, who is very charming with all her qualities. Her beautiful face surpasses the luster of the rays of the full moon, her beautiful eyes are restless like those of a startled deer, and she is famous for her art of decorating Rādhā.

I offer my obeisances to the goddess Lalitā, who is very charming with all her qualities. Her body is adorned with silken garments, ornaments, and a bodice, beautifully colored like the feathers of Garuḍa dancing joyfully, and her golden complexion shames the luster of yellow pigment.

I offer my obeisances to the goddess Lalitā, who is very charming with all her qualities. She instructs Rādhā by saying, "O my friend, be very contrary toward the cunning son of Nanda! Do not be submissive, for that will only lead to your degradation! Rādhā, listen to my beneficial words!"

I offer my obeisances to the goddess Lalitā, who is very charming with all her qualities. Her eyes become slightly red with anger upon seeing even a little trick played by Kṛṣṇa upon Rādhā, and she quickly shames Him with her clever words.

I offer my obeisances to the goddess Lalitā, who is very charming with all her qualities. She is the abode of all motherly affection for the queen of the cowherds, she is the teacher of the gopīs in the art of friendship, and she is non-different from the very life of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa.

I offer my obeisances to the goddess Lalitā, who is very charming with all her qualities. She immediately fulfills the desires and makes successful anyone in Vraja whom the daughter of Vṛṣabhānu sees to be following her path.

I offer my obeisances to the goddess Lalitā, who is very charming with all her qualities. She considers the joyful union of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa as the most excellent of all festivals, and she is the chief among the beloved companions of Gokula.

One who delights in these eight verses, cherished by the qualities of Lalitā, and reads them with pure vision—that person is lovingly attracted by Rādhā, the effulgent wish-fulfilling creeper of Vṛṣabhānu's family, into Her own group.

IDX.first_line: radhe jaya jaya madhava dayite
IDX.search: radhe jaya jaya madhava dayite gokula tarunimandala mahite damodara rativardhana vese hari niskuta vrndavipinese
IDX.author: śrīla rūpa gosvāmī
IDX.verses:
rādhe jaya jaya mādhava⋅dayite
gokula⋅taruṇīmaṇḍala⋅mahite

dāmodara⋅rativardhana⋅veśe
hari⋅niṣkuṭa⋅vṛndāvipineśe

vṛṣabhānudadhi⋅navaśaśilekhe
lalitā-sakhi guṇaramita⋅viśākhe

karuṇāṁ kuru mayi karuṇā⋅bharite
sanaka⋅sanātana⋅varṇita⋅carite
IDX.en_translation:
Rādhe, all glories to You, beloved of Mādhava, O worshiped by the circle of young women of Gokula.

Whose attire increases the love of Dāmodara, O queen of Hari's pleasure-groves in Vṛndāvana.

O new crescent moon in the ocean of Vṛṣabhānu's family, O friend of Lalitā, whose qualities delight Viśākhā.

O full of mercy, please bestow mercy upon me, Whose character is described by Sanaka and Sanātana.

IDX.first_line: radhika caranarenu bhusana kariya tanu
IDX.search: radhika caranarenu bhusana kariya tanu anayase pabe giridhari radhika caranasraya je kare se mahasaya tare muni jao balihari jaya jaya radha nama vrndavana jara dhama krsnasukha vilasera nidhi hena radha gunagana na sunila mora kana vancita karila more vidhi
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
rādhikā⋅caraṇareṇu, bhūṣaṇa kariyā tanu,
anāyāse pābe giridhārī
rādhikā⋅caraṇāśraya, je kare se mahāśaya,
tā̐’re muñi jā̐o balihārī

jaya jaya ‘rādhā’ nāma, vṛndāvana jā̐’ra dhāma,
kṛṣṇasukha⋅vilāsera nidhi
hena rādhā⋅guṇagāna, nā śunila mora kāṇa,
vañcita karila more vidhi

tā̐’ra bhakta⋅saṅge sadā, rasa-līlā prema-kathā,
je kare se pāya ghanaśyāma
ihāte vimukha jei, tā’ra kabhu siddhi nāi,
nāhi jena śuni tā’ra nāma

kṛṣṇa⋅nāma⋅gāne bhāi, rādhikā⋅caraṇa pāi,
rādhānāma⋅gāne kṛṣṇa⋅candra
saŋkṣepe kahinu kathā, ghucāo manera vyathā,
duḥkha-maya anya kathā⋅dvandva
IDX.en_translation:
By making the dust of Rādhā’s feet your ornament, you will easily attain the Lord who lifted Govardhana Hill. Whoever takes shelter of Rādhā’s feet—that great soul—I dedicate myself to them.

All glories to the name "Rādhā," whose abode is Vṛndāvana, the treasure house of Kṛṣṇa’s blissful pastimes. My ears did not hear such glorification of Rādhā; fate has deprived me.

Whoever is always in the company of Her devotees, speaking of Their divine pastimes and loving talks, attains the Lord whose complexion is like a dark cloud. Whoever is averse to this will never achieve perfection; I do not even want to hear their name.

O brother! By singing the name of Kṛṣṇa one attains Rādhā’s feet, and by singing Rādhā’s name one attains Kṛṣṇa. I have told you this truth in brief—now end your mind's distress; all other talk is full of sorrow and conflict.

tā̐’ra bhakta⋅saṅge sadā, rasa-līlā prema-kathā,je kare se pāya ghanaśyāmaihāte vimukha jei, tā’ra kabhu siddhi nāi,nāhi jena śuni tā’ra nāma

By making the dust of Rādhā’s feet your ornament, you will easily attain the Lord who lifted Govardhana Hill. Whoever takes shelter of Rādhā’s feet—that great soul—I dedicate myself to them.

All glories to the name “Rādhā,” whose abode is Vṛndāvana, the treasure house of Kṛṣṇa’s blissful pastimes. My ears did not hear such glorification of Rādhā; fate has deprived me.

Whoever is always in the company of Her devotees, speaking of Their divine pastimes and loving talks, attains the Lord whose complexion is like a dark cloud. Whoever is averse to this will never achieve perfection; I do not even want to hear their name.

O brother! By singing the name of Kṛṣṇa one attains Rādhā’s feet, and by singing Rādhā’s name one attains Kṛṣṇa. I have told you this truth in brief—now end your mind’s distress; all other talk is full of sorrow and conflict.

IDX.first_line: radhika hrdayonmadi vamsi kvana madhu cchatah
IDX.search: radhika hrdayonmadi vamsi kvana madhu cchatah radha parimalodgara garimaksipta manasah kamra radha mano mina vadisikrta vibhramah prema garvbandha gandharva kilakincita ranjitah
IDX.author: śrīla rūpa gosvāmī
IDX.verses:
rādhikā⋅hṛdayonmādi⋅vaṁśī⋅kvāṇa⋅madhu⋅cchaṭaḥ
rādhā⋅parimalodgāra⋅garimākṣipta⋅mānasaḥ

kamra⋅rādhā⋅mano⋅mīna⋅vaḍiśīkṛta⋅vibhramaḥ
prema⋅garvbāndha⋅gāndharvā⋅kilakiñcita⋅rañjitaḥ

lalitā⋅vaśya⋅dhīrā⋅dhāmānābhāsa⋅vaśīkṛtaḥ
rādhā⋅vakrokti⋅pīyūṣa⋅mādhurya⋅bhara⋅lampaṭaḥ

mukhendu⋅candrikodghūrṇa⋅rādhikā⋅rāga⋅sāgaraḥ
vṛṣabhānu⋅sutā⋅kaṇṭha⋅hāri⋅hāra⋅harin⋅maṇiḥ

phulla⋅rādhā⋅kamalinī⋅mukhāmbuja⋅madhuvrataḥ
rādhikā⋅kuca⋅kastūrī⋅patra⋅sphurad⋅uraḥ⋅sthalaḥ

iti gokula⋅bhūpāla⋅sūnu⋅līlā⋅manoharam
jaḥ paṭhen nāma⋅daśakaṁ so’sya vallabhatāṁ vrajet
IDX.en_translation:
The shower of the honey of the sound of the flute, which maddens Rādhikā's heart. He whose mind is distracted by the heaviness of the emission of Rādhā's fragrance.

He who has made His pastime a fish-hook for the fish of the mind of lovely Rādhā. He is colored by the kilakiñcita of Gāndharvā, who is blinded by the pride of love.

He is controlled by the mere semblance of the anger of the patient (heroine) controlled by Lalitā. A libertine for the abundance of the sweetness of the nectar of Rādhā's crooked words.

The ocean of Rādhikā's passion, agitated by the moonlight of the moon of Her face. The emerald of the enchanting necklace on the neck of the Daughter of Vṛṣabhānu.

The bee of the lotus-face of the blooming Rādhā-lotus. He whose chest shines with the musk-leaf on Rādhikā's breasts.

He who reads this Name-Decad, charming with the pastimes of the Son of the King of Gokula—he becomes dear to Him.

IDX.search: radhika sarada indu nindi mukha mandali kuntale vicitra veni campaka puspa sobhani nilapatta ange sobhe tahe adha odani vandiye sripadapadma vrsabhanu nandini taruna aruna jini sindurera mandali jaichi ali matta bhare malayaja gandhini bhurura bhangima koti koti kama ganjini vandiye sripadapadma vrsabhanu nandini

rādhikā śarada⋅indu⋅nindi mukha maṇḍalī
kuntale vicitra veṇī campaka⋅puṣpa⋅śobhanī
nīlapaṭṭa aṅge śobhe tāhe ādha oḍanī
vandiye śrī-pāda-padma vṛṣabhānu⋅nandinī

taruṇa aruṇa jini sindurera maṇḍalī
jaichi ali matta bhare malayaja⋅gandhinī
bhurūra bhaṅgima koṭī koṭī kāma⋅gañjinī
vandiye śrī-pāda-padma vṛṣabhānu⋅nandinī

khañjana⋅gañjana diṭhi vaṅgima⋅sucāhanī
añjana rañjita tāhe kāmaśara⋅sandhinī
tila⋅puṣpa jini nāsā vesara⋅sudolanī
vandiye śrī-pāda-padma vṛṣabhānu⋅nandinī

pakva bimbaphala jini adhara suraṅgiṇī
daśana dāḍimva⋅bīja jini ati śobhanī
vasanta kokila jini sumadhura⋅volanī
vandiye śrī-pāda-padma vṛṣabhānu⋅nandinī

kanaka⋅mukura jini gaṇḍayuga⋅śobhanī
ratana⋅mañjīra pāye vaṅkarāja⋅dolanī
keśara mukatā hāra ura’para jholanī
vandiye śrī-pāda-padma vṛṣabhānu⋅nandinī

kanaka kalasa jini kucayuga⋅śobhanī
karivara⋅kara jini vāhuyuga⋅dolanī
sulalita aṅgulite mudrikāra sājanī
vandiye śrī-pāda-padma vṛṣabhānu⋅nandinī

gaja⋅ari jini mājā guruyā nitambinī
tā’para śobhita bhāla kanakera kiṅkinī
kanaka⋅ulaṭa⋅rambhā jānuyuga⋅śobhanī
vandiye śrī-pāda-padma vṛṣabhānu⋅nandinī

haṁsarāja⋅gati jini sumanthara⋅calanī
rātula caraṇe rāje kanayā supañjinī
jugala caraṇe śobhe jāvaka surañjinī
vandiye śrī-pāda-padma vṛṣabhānu⋅nandinī
IDX.en_translation:
The face of Śrīmati Rādhā⋅rāṇī shames the autumn moon. Her hair is a wonderful braid adorned with jasmine flowers. A blue cloth and a fine veil adorn Her body. I worship the lotus feet of the daughter of Vṛṣabhānu.

The mark of vermilion on Her forehead surpasses the beauty of the rising sun. She is like a honeybee intoxicated by the fragrance of sandalwood. The curves of Her eyebrows defeat millions of Cupids. I worship the lotus feet of the daughter of Vṛṣabhānu.

Her sidelong glances defeat the graceful movements of the wagtail bird. Her eyes, adorned with kohl, are like the arrows of Cupid. Her nose surpasses the beauty of a sesame flower and is adorned with a nose ornament. I worship the lotus feet of the daughter of Vṛṣabhānu.

Her lips are red like ripe bimba fruit, and Her teeth surpass the beauty of pomegranate seeds. Her sweet voice outshines the cuckoo of spring. I worship the lotus feet of the daughter of Vṛṣabhānu.

Her cheeks are beautiful like golden mirrors. Jeweled anklets jingle upon Her feet. A pearl necklace hangs upon Her chest. I worship the lotus feet of the daughter of Vṛṣabhānu.

Her breasts are beautiful like golden pitchers, and Her graceful arms are like the trunk of a magnificent elephant. Adorned rings are upon Her delicate fingers. I worship the lotus feet of the daughter of Vṛṣabhānu.

Her waist is slender like that of a lion, and Her hips are beautifully heavy. A golden belt is adorned upon them. Her knees are as beautiful as inverted golden banana trees. I worship the lotus feet of the daughter of Vṛṣabhānu.

Her graceful walk surpasses the gait of a royal swan. On Her reddish feet shine golden anklets. Her feet are beautifully colored with red lac dye. I worship the lotus feet of the daughter of Vṛṣabhānu.

IDX.first_line: radhikacarana padma sakala sreyera sadma
IDX.search: radhikacarana padma sakala sreyera sadma jatane je nahi aradhila radha padankita dhama vrndavana jara nama taha je na asraya karila radhikabhava gambhira citta jeba mahadhira gana sanga na kaila jivane kemane se syamananda rasasindhu snanananda labhibe bujhaha ekamane
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
rādhikācaraṇa⋅padma, sakala śreyera sadma,
jatane je nāhi ārādhila
rādhā⋅padāṅkita⋅dhāma, vṛndāvana jā’ra nāma,
tāhā je nā āśraya karila

rādhikābhāva⋅gambhīra⋅, citta jebā mahādhīra,
gaṇa⋅saṅga nā kaila jīvane
kemane se śyāmānanda, rasasindhu⋅snānānanda,
labhibe bujhaha ekamane

rādhikā ujjvala⋅rasera ācārya
rādhā⋅mādhava⋅śuddhaprema vicārya

je dharila rādhāpada parama jatane
se pāila kṛṣṇa-pada amūlyaratane

rādhāpada vinā kabhu kṛṣṇa nāhi mile
rādhāra dāsīra kṛṣṇa, sarva-vede bale

choḍata dhana⋅jana, kalatra⋅suta⋅mita,
choḍata karama⋅geyāna
rādhā pada-paṅkaja⋅, madhurata⋅sevana,
bhakativinoda paramāṇa
IDX.en_translation:
Whoever has not carefully worshipped the lotus feet of Rādhikā, the abode of all auspiciousness, And has not taken shelter in that abode marked by Rādhā's footprints, named Vṛndāvana.

How will they attain the joy of Kṛṣṇa, the bliss of bathing in the ocean of divine mellows, Whoever has not associated in life with those great, sober persons whose minds are deeply absorbed in the mood of Rādhikā? Understand this with full attention.

Rādhikā is the preceptor of the brightest, most exalted divine mellow. The pure love of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa is to be contemplated.

Whoever takes hold of Rādhā's feet with utmost care, They attain Kṛṣṇa's feet as an invaluable jewel.

Without Rādhā's feet, Kṛṣṇa is never attained. Kṛṣṇa belongs to the maidservant of Rādhā; all the sacred texts declare this.

Give up wealth and people, give up wife, son, and friends, give up fruitive activities and speculative knowledge. Bhakati Vinoda makes the sweet service of Rādhā's lotus feet his ultimate conclusion.

IDX.first_line: radhikacarana padma sakala sreyera sadmajatane je nahi aradhilaradha padankita dhama vrndavana jara namataha je na asraya karila
IDX.search: radhikacarana padma sakala sreyera sadmajatane je nahi aradhilaradha padankita dhama vrndavana jara namataha je na asraya karila radhikabhava gambhira citta jeba mahadhiragana sanga na kaila jivanekemane se syamananda rasasindhu snananandalabhibe bujhaha ekamane

rādhikācaraṇa⋅padma, sakala śreyera sadma,jatane je nāhi ārādhilarādhā⋅padāṅkita⋅dhāma, vṛndāvana jā’ra nāma,tāhā je nā āśraya karila

rādhikābhāva⋅gambhīra⋅, citta jebā mahādhīra,gaṇa⋅saṅga nā kaila jīvanekemane se śyāmānanda, rasasindhu⋅snānānanda,labhibe bujhaha ekamane

rādhikā ujjvala⋅rasera ācāryarādhā⋅mādhava⋅śuddhaprema vicārya

je dharila rādhāpada parama jatanese pāila kṛṣṇa-pada amūlyaratane

IDX.en_translation:
Whoever has not carefully worshipped the lotus feet of Rādhikā, the abode of all auspiciousness, And has not taken shelter in that abode marked by Rādhā’s footprints, named Vṛndāvana.

How will they attain the joy of Kṛṣṇa, the bliss of bathing in the ocean of divine mellows, Whoever has not associated in life with those great, sober persons whose minds are deeply absorbed in the mood of Rādhikā? Understand this with full attention.

Rādhikā is the preceptor of the brightest, most exalted divine mellow. The pure love of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa is to be contemplated.

Whoever takes hold of Rādhā’s feet with utmost care, They attain Kṛṣṇa’s feet as an invaluable jewel.

Without Rādhā’s feet, Kṛṣṇa is never attained. Kṛṣṇa belongs to the maidservant of Rādhā; all the sacred texts declare this.

Give up wealth and people, give up wife, son, and friends, give up fruitive activities and speculative knowledge. Bhakati Vinoda makes the sweet service of Rādhā’s lotus feet his ultimate conclusion.

IDX.first_line: ragavese vraja dhama vase jadi tivra kama
IDX.search: ragavese vraja dhama vase jadi tivra kama thake tava hrdaya bhitare radha krsna lilarasa paricarya sulalasa hoo jadi nitanta antare bali tabe suna mama mana bhajana caturavara sri svarupadamodara prabhuseva jahara jivana
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
rāgāveśe vraja⋅dhāma⋅, vāse jadi tīvra kāma,
thāke tava hṛdaya⋅bhitare
rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa⋅līlārasa, paricaryā⋅sulālasa,
hoo jadi nitānta antare

bali tabe, śuna, mama mana
bhajana caturavara, śrī⋅svarūpadāmodara,
prabhusevā jā̐hāra jīvana

sagaṇa śrī⋅rūpa⋅yini, rasatattva⋅jñānamaṇi,
līlātattva je kaila prakāśa
tā̐hāra agraja bhāi, jā̐hāra samāna nāi,
varṇila je jugalavilāsa

sei saba mahājane, spaṣṭaprema⋅vijñāpane,
smara, mana tumi nirantara
bhaktivinodera nati, mahājanagaṇa⋅prati,
vijñāpita karaha satvara
IDX.en_translation:
If there is an intense desire within your heart for residing in the holy land of Vraja with great attachment, and if you deeply desire the sweet nectar of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa's pastimes and Their service,

Then I say, listen, my mind, to the best of those expert in worship, Śrī Svarūpa Dāmodara, whose very life is the service of the Lord.

Together with his associates, Śrīla Rūpa Gosvāmī—who is a jewel of knowledge of transcendental mellows and who revealed the truth of the Lord's pastimes—and his elder brother, who has no equal, have described the pastimes of the Divine Couple.

Remember those great personalities with clear declarations of love continuously, O my mind. Make Bhaktivinoda's obeisances quickly known to those great personalities.

IDX.first_line: ragera bhajana patha kahi ebe abhimata
IDX.search: ragera bhajana patha kahi ebe abhimata loka veda sara ei vani sakhira anuga hana vraje siddhadeha pana ei bhave judabe parani sri radhikara sakhi jata taha na kahiba kata mukhya sakhi kariya ganana lalita visakha tatha sucitra campakalata rangadevi sudevi kathana
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
rāgera bhajana⋅patha, kahi ebe abhimata,
loka veda⋅sāra ei vāṇī
sakhīra anugā hañā, vraje siddhadeha pāñā,
ei bhāve juḍābe parāṇī

śrī⋅rādhikāra sakhī jata, tāhā nā kahiba kata,
mukhya sakhī kariyā gaṇana
lalitā, viśākhā tathā, sucitrā, campakalatā,
raṅgadevī, sudevī kathana

tuṅgavidyā, indurekhā, ei aṣṭasakhī lekhā,
ebe kahi narma⋅sakhīgaṇa
iho sevā⋅sahacarī, ‘priya⋅preṣṭha’ nāma dhari’
prema⋅sevā kare anukṣaṇa

(samasnehā viṣama snehā, nā kariha dui lehā,
kahi mātra adhikasnehāgaṇa
nirantara thāke saṅge, kṛṣṇakathā līlāraṅge,
narmasakhī ei saba jana )
śrī⋅rasamañjarī āra, śrī⋅ratimañjarī sāra,
lavaṅgamañjarī, mañjunālī
lavaṅgamañjarī⋅saṅge, kastūrikā⋅ādi raṅge,
premasevā kare kutūhalī

e⋅sabāra anugā hañā, premasevā niba cāñā,
iṅgite bujhiba saba kāje
rūpe guṇe ḍagamagī, sadā haba anurāgī,
vasati kariba sakhāmājhe

vṛndāvane dui jana, cāridike sakhīgaṇa,
samayera sevā⋅rasa⋅sukhe
sakhīra iṅgita habe, cāmara ḍhulāba tabe,
tāmbūla jogāba cā̐da mukhe

jugala caraṇa sevi, nirantara ei bhāvi,
anurāge thākiba sadāya
sādhane bhāviba jāhā, siddha dehe pāba tāhā,
rāgapathera ei se upāya

sādhane je dhana cāi, siddhadehe tāhā pāi,
pakvāpakva mātra se vicāra
pākile je premabhakti, apakve ‘sādhana’ khyāti,
bhakati⋅lakṣaṇa⋅anusāra

narottama⋅dāsa kahe, ei jena mora haye,
vrajapure anurāge vāsa
sakhīgaṇa⋅gaṇanāte, āmāre gaṇibe tāte,
tavahu̐ pūriba abhilāṣa

tathāhi⋅
sakhīnāṁ saṅginīrūpāmātmānaṁ vāsanāmayīm
ājñāsevāparāṁ tattat kṛpālaṅkārabhūṣitām

kṛṣṇaṁ smaran janañcāsya preṣṭhaṁ nijasamīhitam
tattatkathārataścāsau kuryādvāsaṁ vraje sadā
IDX.en_translation:
I will now speak my opinion on the path of worship in rāga (spontaneous devotion); this is the essence of the words of the people and the Vedas. Becoming a follower of the sakhīs, attaining a siddha-deha in Vraja—in this way, the life will be soothed.

How many sakhīs of Śrī Rādhikā are there? I will not say how many. Counting the principal sakhīs: Lalitā, Viśākhā, and Sucitrā, Campakalatā, Raṅgadevī, Sudevī are mentioned.

Tuṅgavidyā, Indurekhā—these eight sakhīs are written. Now I speak of the narma-sakhīs. They are assistants in service, bearing the name 'Priya-preṣṭha'; they perform loving service at every moment.

(Sama-snehā, Viṣama-snehā—do not make affection for these two; I speak only of the Adhika-snehā group. They remain constantly in company, in Kṛṣṇa-kathā and the joy of pastimes; all these people are narma-sakhīs.)

Śrī Rūpa-mañjarī, and Śrī Rati-mañjarī—the essence; Lavaṅga-mañjarī, Mañjulālī. Along with Lavaṅga-mañjarī, with musk and so on, they enthusiastically perform loving service in joy.

Becoming a follower of all of them, I will ask for and take loving service; I will understand all tasks by hints. Overwhelmed by beauty and qualities, I will always be affectionate; I will reside in the midst of the sakhīs.

The Two Persons in Vṛndāvana, sakhīs on four sides, in the happiness of the rasa of timely service. There will be a hint from the sakhī, then I will wave the whisk; I will supply betel nuts to the moon-faces.

Serving the lotus feet of the Couple, constantly thinking this, I will always remain in affection. What I think of in sādhana, I will get that in the siddha-deha; this is the method of the path of rāga.

The wealth I want in sādhana, I get that in the siddha-deha; the consideration is only ripe and unripe. When ripe, it is prema-bhakti; in the unripe state, it is known as 'sādhana', according to the characteristic of bhakti.

Narottama Dāsa says: May this be mine—residence in Vrajapura in affection. In the counting of the sakhīs, they will count me in that; then my desire will be fulfilled.

Thus it is said: "One should meditate upon oneself in a form that is a female companion (sakhī) to the Divine Couple—a form born of spiritual desire, dedicated to following their commands and serving them, and adorned with the ornaments of their mercy. While remembering Kṛṣṇa and His beloved associates who are dear to one’s own heart, one should remain constantly absorbed in narrations about them and reside eternally in Vraja."

IDX.first_line: rahu kavale indu parakasa nama sindhu
IDX.search: rahu kavale indu parakasa nama sindhu kalimardana baje bana pahu bhela parakasa bhuvana caturdasa jaya jaya padila ghosana dekhite gaurangacandra nadiyara loka soka saba nasala dine dine badala ananda engrefraineng
IDX.author: śrīla vṛndāvana⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
rāhu⋅kavale indu, parakāśa nāma⋅sindhu,
kalimardana bāje bāṇā
pahu̐ bhela parakāśa, bhuvana caturdaśa,
jaya jaya paḍila ghoṣaṇā

dekhite gaurāṅgacandra
nadīyāra loka⋅, śoka saba nāśala,
dine dine bāḍala ānanda (<eng>refrain</eng>)

dundubhi bāje, śata śaṅkha gāje,
bāje veṇu⋅viṣāṇa
śrī⋅caitanya⋅ṭhākura, nityānanda⋅prabhu,
vṛndāvana⋅dāsa gāna
IDX.en_translation:
Just as the moon is swallowed by Rāhu, the ocean of the Name has appeared. The destroyer of Kali sounds His powerful proclamation. The Lord has manifested throughout the fourteen worlds, and a proclamation of "All Glories!" was heard.

Upon seeing Gaurāṅgacandra, the people of Nadīyā had all their sorrow destroyed, and their joy increased day by day.

The dundubhi sounds, hundreds of conch shells resound, and the flute and horn play. Vṛndāvana Dāsa sings of Śrī Caitanya Ṭhākura and Nityānanda Prabhu.

IDX.first_line: rai kanu jamunara majhe
IDX.search: rai kanu jamunara majhe phiraye tarani jalera ghurani dura gela kula laje kumbhira makara mina uthata saghane vadana tuli harise samuna uthale dviguna rai kanu rupe bhuli
IDX.verses:
rāi⋅kānu jamunāra mājhe
phiraye taranī, jalera ghūraṇī,
dūra gela kula lāje

kumbhīra makara, mīna uṭhata,
saghane vadana tuli
hariṣe ṣamunā, uthale dviguṇa,
rāi kānu rūpe bhuli

kahaye lalitā, haye sacakitā,
śuna lo mukharā buḍī
tomāri kathāya, caḍi bhāṅgā nāya,
parāṇa sahita mari

mukharā kahaye, jā māge kāṇḍārī,
tāhāi karaha dāna
e bhāṅgā taraṇī, pāra hare ekhani,
kena vā jāibe prāṇa

e saba vacana, śuniyā kāṇḍārī,
kahaye lalitā pāśe
tomāra sakhīra, paraśa māgiye,
vamśī śuniyā hāse
IDX.en_translation:
Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa are in the middle of the Yamunā. The boat turns while the water swirls, and they have no shame regarding family honor.

Crocodiles, alligators, and fish rise up, frequently lifting their faces to see. The Yamunā overflows twofold in joy, enchanted by the beauty of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa.

Lalitā says, becoming alarmed: "Listen, O talkative old woman! Because of your words, we have boarded this broken boat, and I fear we will lose our lives."

The old woman replies: "Whatever the boatman asks for, just give it to him. This broken boat will cross now; why should your lives be lost?"

Hearing these words, the boatman says to Lalitā: "I am only asking for the touch of your friend." He laughs upon hearing the sound of His own flute.

IDX.first_line: ramani siromani vrsabhanu nandini
IDX.search: ramani siromani vrsabhanu nandini nilavasana paridhana chinna purata jini varna vikasini baddhakavari hariprana abharana mandita harirasa pandita tilaka susobhita bhala kanculikacchadita stanamani mandita kajjala nayani rasala
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
ramaṇī⋅śiromaṇi, vṛṣabhānu⋅nandinī,
nīlavasana⋅paridhānā
chinna⋅puraṭa jini’, varṇa⋅vikāśinī,
baddha-kavarī hari-prāṇā

ābharaṇa⋅maṇḍitā, harirasa⋅paṇḍitā,
tilaka⋅suśobhita⋅bhālā
kañculikācchāditā, stanamaṇi⋅maṇḍitā,
kajjala⋅nayanī rasālā

sakala tyajiyā se⋅rādhā⋅caraṇe
dāsī ha’ye bhaja parama⋅yatane

saundarya⋅kiraṇa dekhiyā jā̐hāra
rati⋅gaurī⋅līlā⋅garva⋅parihāra

śacī⋅lakṣmī⋅satyā saubhāgya⋅balane
parājita haya jā̐hāra caraṇe

kṛṣṇa⋅vaśīkāre candrāvalī⋅ādi
parājaya māne hoiyā vivādī

haridayita⋅rādhā⋅caraṇa⋅prayāsī
bhakativinoda śrī-godruma-vāsī
IDX.en_translation:
The crest-jewel of women, the daughter of Vṛṣabhānu, is dressed in blue garments. Conquering the beauty of pure gold, her complexion blossoms forth; with braided hair, she is the very life of Hari.

Adorned with ornaments and expert in the mellows of devotion, her forehead is beautifully decorated with a tilaka. Covered by a bodice, her jewel-like breasts adorned, her eyes are beautified with eyeliner and are full of sweetness.

Abandoning everything, at those feet of Rādhā, become a maidservant and worship with utmost care.

Seeing the rays of her beauty, the goddess of love and the goddess Pārvatī abandon their pride in their own pastimes.

Śacī, Lakṣmī, and Satyabhāmā are defeated at her feet by the power of her good fortune.

In captivating Kṛṣṇa, even Candrāvalī and other rivals accept defeat.

Bhaktivinoda, a resident of Godruma, aspires for the feet of Rādhā, who is very dear to Hari.

saundarya⋅kiraṇa dekhiyā jā̐hārarati⋅gaurī⋅līlā⋅garva⋅parihāra

śacī⋅lakṣmī⋅satyā saubhāgya⋅balaneparājita haya jā̐hāra caraṇe

kṛṣṇa⋅vaśīkāre candrāvalī⋅ādiparājaya māne hoiyā vivādī

haridayita⋅rādhā⋅caraṇa⋅prayāsībhakativinoda śrī-godruma-vāsī
IDX.en_translation:
The crest-jewel of women, the daughter of Vṛṣabhānu, is dressed in blue garments. Conquering the beauty of pure gold, her complexion blossoms forth; with braided hair, she is the very life of Hari.

Adorned with ornaments and expert in the mellows of devotion, her forehead is beautifully decorated with a tilaka. Covered by a bodice, her jewel-like breasts adorned, her eyes are beautified with eyeliner and are full of sweetness.

Abandoning everything, at those feet of Rādhā, become a maidservant and worship with utmost care.

Seeing the rays of her beauty, the goddess of love and the goddess Pārvatī abandon their pride in their own pastimes.

Śacī, Lakṣmī, and Satyabhāmā are defeated at her feet by the power of her good fortune.

In captivating Kṛṣṇa, even Candrāvalī and other rivals accept defeat.

Bhaktivinoda, a resident of Godruma, aspires for the feet of Rādhā, who is very dear to Hari.

IDX.first_line: rasa valita mrgaksi mauli manikya laksmih
IDX.search: rasa valita mrgaksi mauli manikya laksmih pramudita mura vairi prema vapi marali vraja vara vrsabhanoh punya girvana valli snapayati nija dasye radhika mam kada nu sphurad aruna dukula dyotitodyan nitamba sthalam abhi vara kanci lasyam ullasayanti kuca kalasa vilasa sphita mukta sara srih snapayati nija dasye radhika mam kada nu
IDX.author: śrīla raghunāthadāsa gosvāmī
IDX.verses:
rasa⋅valita⋅mṛgākṣī⋅mauli⋅māṇikya⋅lakṣmīḥ
pramudita⋅mura⋅vairi⋅prema⋅vāpī⋅marālī
vraja⋅vara⋅vṛṣabhānoḥ puṇya⋅gīrvāṇa⋅vallī
snapayati nija⋅dāsye rādhikā māṁ kadā nu

sphurad⋅aruṇa⋅dukūla⋅dyotitodyan⋅nitamba⋅
sthalam abhi⋅vara⋅kāñci⋅lāsyam ullāsayantī
kuca⋅kalasa⋅vilāsa⋅sphīta⋅muktā⋅sara⋅śrīḥ
snapayati nija⋅dāsye rādhikā māṁ kadā nu

sarasija⋅vara⋅garbhākharva⋅kāntiḥ samudyat⋅
taruṇima⋅ghanasārāśliṣṭa⋅kaiśora⋅sīdhuḥ
dara⋅vikasita⋅hāsya⋅syandi⋅bimbādharāgrā
snapayati nija⋅dāsye rādhikā māṁ kadā nu

ati⋅caṭulataraṁ taṁ kānanāntar milantaṁ
vraja⋅nṛpati⋅kumāraṁ vīkṣya śaṅkā⋅kulākṣī
madhura⋅mṛdu⋅vacobhiḥ saṁstutā netra⋅bhaṅgyā
snapayati nija⋅dāsye rādhikā māṁ kadā nu

vraja⋅kula⋅mahilānāṁ prāṇa⋅bhūtākhilānāṁ
paśupa⋅pati⋅gṛhiṇyāḥ kṛṣṇa⋅vat prema⋅pātram
sulalita⋅lalitāntaḥ sneha⋅phullāntarātmā
snapayati nija⋅dāsye rādhikā māṁ kadā nu

niravadhi sa⋅viśākhā śākhi⋅yūtha⋅prasūnaiḥ
srajam iha racayantī vaijayantīṁ vanānte
agha⋅vijaya⋅varoraḥ preyasī śreyasī sā
snapayati nija⋅dāsye rādhikā māṁ kadā nu

prakaṭita⋅nija⋅vāsaṁ snigdha⋅veṇu⋅praṇādair
druta⋅gati⋅harim ārāt prāpya kuñje smitākṣī
śravaṇa⋅kuhara⋅kaṇḍūṁ tanvatī namra⋅vaktrā
snapayati nija⋅dāsye rādhikā māṁ kadā nu

amala⋅kamala⋅rāji⋅sparśi⋅vāta⋅praśīte
nija⋅sarasi nidāghe sāyam ullāsinīyam
parijana⋅gaṇa⋅yuktā krīḍayantī vakāriṁ
snapayati nija⋅dāsye rādhikā māṁ kadā nu

paṭhati vimala⋅cetā mṛṣṭa⋅rādhāṣṭakaṁ jaḥ
parihṛta⋅nikhilāśā⋅santatiḥ kātaraḥ san
paśupa⋅pati⋅kumāraḥ kāmam āmoditas taṁ
nija⋅jana⋅gaṇa⋅madhye rādhikāyās tanoti
IDX.en_translation:
Rādhikā is the jewel among all beautiful gopīs, the swan upon the lake of Lord Kṛṣṇa's joyful love, and the sacred vine of the excellent Vṛṣabhānu. When will She immerse me in Her divine service?

When will Rādhikā, whose hips are illuminated by a glittering red silk garment and gracefully adorned with a beautiful belt, and whose beauty is enhanced by a lustrous pearl necklace, immerse me in Her service?

When will Rādhikā, whose beauty surpasses the finest lotus and who possesses the essence of blossoming youth, whose adolescent charm is like nectar and whose enchanting smile emanates from Her reddish lips, immerse me in Her service?

When will Rādhikā, with eyes filled with apprehension upon seeing the restless Prince of Vraja meeting Her in the forest, and who is praised with sweet and gentle words and glances, immerse me in Her service?

When will Rādhikā, who is the life of all the women of Vraja, who is as dear to Mother Yaśodā as Lord Kṛṣṇa himself, and whose heart blossoms with affection for the charming Lalitā, immerse me in Her service?

When will the excellent and beloved consort of Lord Kṛṣṇa—who along with Viśākhā is incessantly making a beautiful garland in the forest from the flowers of the trees—immerse me in Her service?

When will Rādhikā, whose eyes smile as She quickly approaches Lord Hari in a grove, drawn by the sweet sound of His flute, and whose lowered face expresses Her longing for the sound to fill Her ears, immerse me in Her service?

When will Rādhikā, who delights in the evening in Her own cool pond, refreshed by the breeze touching the pure lotus flowers, and who plays with Lord Kṛṣṇa accompanied by Her companions, immerse me in Her service?

The pure-hearted person who recites these sweet eight verses in praise of Rādhā, having given up all other desires—the Prince of Nanda Mahārāja, being greatly pleased, will establish him among Rādhikā's own associates.

IDX.first_line: rasatattva nitya jaiche vrajatattva nitya taiche
IDX.search: rasatattva nitya jaiche vrajatattva nitya taiche lilarasa eka kari jana krsna je saksat rasa sakaloi krsnera vasa veda bhagavate kare gana sri krsna parama tattva tara lila suddhasattva maya jara durasthita dasi jiva prati krpa kari lila prakasila hari jivera mangala abhilasi
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
rasa-tattva nitya jaiche, vraja-tattva nitya taiche,
līlārasa eka kari’ jāna
kṛṣṇa je sākṣāt rasa, sakaloi kṛṣṇera vaśa,
veda⋅bhāgavate kare gāna

śrī⋅kṛṣṇa parama⋅tattva, tā̐’ra līlā śuddhasattva,
māyā jā̐’ra dūrasthitā dāsī
jīva⋅prati kṛpā kari’, līlā prakāśila hari,
jīvera maṅgala⋅abhilāṣī

brahmā, śeṣa, śiva jā̐ra, anveṣiyā bāra bāra,
tattva nāhi bujhibāre pāre
brahmera āśraya jini, paramātmāra aṁśī tini,
‘svayaṁ bhagavān’ bali jā̐re

sei kṛṣṇa dayāmaya, mūla-tattva sarvāśraya,
ananta līlāra eka khani
nirviśeṣa⋅līlābhare, brahmatā prakāśa kare,
svīya aṅgakānti guṇamaṇi

aṁśe paramātmā haye, baddhajīvagaṇe la’ye,
karmacakre līlā kare kata
deva-loke deva-saha, upendrādi haye te̐ha,
deva-līlā kare śata śata

paravyome nārāyaṇa, haye pāle dāsa-jana,
deva-deva rāja⋅rājeśvara
sei kṛṣṇa sarvāśraya, vraje nara⋅paricaya,
nara-līlā karila vistāra
IDX.en_translation:
Just as the principle of divine mellows is eternal, the principle of Vraja is similarly eternal. Know the mellows of His pastimes to be one. Kṛṣṇa is the direct embodiment of rasa, and everything is under His control, as sung in the Vedas and scriptures.

Śrī Kṛṣṇa is the Supreme Truth, and His pastimes are of pure goodness. Illusion is His distant maidservant. Showing mercy to all living entities, Kṛṣṇa revealed His pastimes, desiring their welfare.

Brahmā, Śeṣa, and Śiva—searching repeatedly—cannot understand His truth. He is the shelter of the impersonal Absolute and the complete manifestation of the Supersoul, whom we call the Supreme Lord Himself.

That merciful Kṛṣṇa is the original truth and the shelter of all—a mine of endless pastimes. By the abundance of His impersonal manifestations, He reveals His nature as the Absolute through the effulgence of His own body, which is a jewel of all qualities.

As a partial manifestation, becoming the Supersoul, He engages the conditioned souls in the cycle of karma through many pastimes. In the celestial planets He performs hundreds of divine pastimes with the demigods.

In the spiritual sky He maintains His servants as the God of gods and the King of kings. That same Kṛṣṇa, the shelter of all, assumed a human identity in Vraja and expanded His human-like pastimes.

IDX.first_line: rasera adhara jini tara citta rasakhani
IDX.search: rasera adhara jini tara citta rasakhani sei cittera avastha visese sraddha nistha rucyasakti krame haya bhava vyakti rati name tahara nirdese viruddhaviruddha bhava sarvopari sva prabhava prakasiya laya nijavase sakalera adhipati hana sobha paya ati sthayi bhava nama paya rase
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
rasera ādhāra jini, tā̐’ra citta rasa-khani,
sei cittera avasthā⋅viśeṣe
śraddhā⋅niṣṭhā⋅rucyāsakti, krame haya bhāva⋅vyakti,
‘rati’⋅nāme tā̐hāra nirdeśe

viruddhāviruddha bhāva, sarvopari sva⋅prabhāva,
prakāśiyā laya nijavaśe
sakalera adhipati, hañā śobhā pāya ati,
‘sthāyī bhāva’⋅nāma pāya rase

mukhya⋅gauṇa⋅bhede tā̐’ra, paricaya dvi-prakāra,
mukhya pañca, gauṇa saptavidha
śānta, dāsya, sakhya āra, vātsalya, madhura sāra,
ei pañca rati mukhyābhidha

hāsyādbhuta , vīra āra, karuṇa o raudrākāra,
bhayānaka⋅vībhatsa⋅vibhede
rati sapta gaunī haya, saba kṛṣṇa⋅bhaktimaya,
śobhā pāya rasera prabhede
IDX.en_translation:
He who is the abode of rasa, His heart is a mine of rasa. Within that heart, in specific states, faith, steadfastness, taste, and attachment gradually manifest as spiritual emotion, designated as love by His command.

Both opposing and non-opposing emotions, above all His own influence, manifest and take control. Becoming the Lord of all, they shine most beautifully and obtain the name 'permanent sentiment' in the experience of rasa.

Its identity is of two types, distinguished as primary and secondary. There are five primary and seven secondary types. Neutrality, servitude, friendship, parental affection, and conjugal love are the essence; these five types of love are known as primary.

Laughter, wonder, heroism, compassion, and anger are the forms, along with fear and disgust. These seven types of love become secondary; all are full of devotion to Kṛṣṇa, shining in the various distinctions of rasa.

IDX.first_line: rasika nagari gana siromani
IDX.search: rasika nagari gana siromani krsnaprema sarahamsi vrsabhanuraja suddhakalpavalli sarvalaksmigana amsi rakta pattavastra nitamba upari ksudra ghanti dule taya kucayugopari duli muktamala cittahari sobha paya
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
rasika⋅nāgarī⋅, gaṇa⋅śiromaṇi,
kṛṣṇaprema⋅sarahaṁsī
vṛṣabhānurāja⋅, śuddhakalpavallī,
sarvalakṣmīgaṇa⋅aṁśī

rakta paṭṭavastra, nitamba⋅upari,
kṣudra ghaṇṭi dule tā’ya
kucayugopari, duli’ muktāmālā,
cittahārī śobhā pāya

sarasijavara⋅, karṇikā⋅samāna,
atiśaya kāntimatī
kaiśora⋅amṛta⋅, tāruṇya⋅karpūra⋅,
miśrasmitādharā satī

vanānte āgata, vrajapati⋅suta,
parama cañcalavare
heri’ śaṅkākula, nayana⋅bhaṅgite,
ādarete stava kare

vrajera mahilā⋅, gaṇera parāṇa,
jaśomatī⋅priyapātrī
lalita⋅lalitā⋅, snehete praphulla⋅,
śarīrā lalitagātrī

viśākhāra sane, vanaphula tuli’,
gā̐the vaijayantī mālā
sakala⋅śreyasī, kṛṣṇa⋅vakṣaḥsthitā,
parama preyasī bālā

snigdha veṇurave, drutagati jāi’,
kuñje pe’ye naṭavare
hasita⋅nayanī, namramukhī satī,
karṇa kaṇḍūyana kare

sparśiyā kamala, vāyu suśītala,
kare jabe kuṇḍanīra
nidāghe tathāya, nijagaṇa⋅saha,
tuṣaya gokula⋅vīra

bhakativinoda, rūpa⋅raghunāthe,
kahaye caraṇa dhari’
hena rādhā⋅dāsya, sudhīra⋅sampad,
kabe dibe kṛpā kari’
IDX.en_translation:
The expert lover and crest-jewel of all, She is a swan in the lake of Kṛṣṇa's love. She is a pure desire-creeper from King Vṛṣabhānu and a part of all the goddesses of fortune.

Upon her hips are red silk garments, and tiny bells swing there. Upon her breasts, a swinging pearl necklace creates a heart-stealing beauty.

She is exceedingly radiant, like the beautiful heart of a lotus flower—a chaste lady with a sweet, gentle smile, infused with the nectar of youth.

When the son of the King of Vraja, the supremely restless lover, arrives at the forest edge, She sees Him with anxious glances and affectionately offers praise.

She is the very life of the women of Vraja and dear to Yaśodā. She is delicate and charming, her graceful body blooming with affection.

With Viśākhā, she picks forest flowers and weaves a Vaijayantī garland. She is the most auspicious of all, residing on Kṛṣṇa’s chest—the supremely beloved young girl.

Hearing the soft sound of the flute, she goes quickly to the grove and finds the best of dancers; with smiling eyes, the chaste woman with a humble face scratches her ear.

When the cool breeze touching the lotuses makes ripples in the lake during the summer, there she pleases the hero of Gokula along with her companions.

Bhaktivinoda says, holding the feet of Rūpa and Raghunātha: “When will you mercifully bestow upon me such service to Rādhā, that peaceful treasure?”

IDX.first_line: rati prema sneha mana pranaya o ragakhyana
IDX.search: rati prema sneha mana pranaya o ragakhyana anuraga bhava ei sata rati jata gadha haya krame sapta nama laya sthayi bhava sada avadata snehadi je bhava chaya prema name paricaya sadharana janera nikate je bhava krsnete jara sei bhave krsna tara e rahasya rase nitya bate
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
rati⋅prema, sneha, māna, praṇaya o rāgākhyāna,
anurāga, bhāva⋅ei sāta
rati jata gāḍha haya, krame sapta nāma laya,
sthāyī bhāva sadā avadāta

snehādi je bhāva chaya, ‘prema’ nāme paricaya,
sādhāraṇa janera nikaṭe
je bhāva kṛṣṇete jā̐ra, sei bhāve kṛṣṇa tā̐ra,
e rahasya rase nitya baṭe

bhaktacitta siŋhāsana, tāte upaviṣṭa hana,
sthāyī bhāva sarvabhāva⋅rāja
hlādinī je parā śakti, tā̐ra sāra śuddhabhakti,
bhāva-rūpe tā̐hāra virāja

vibhāvādi bhāva-gaṇe, nijāyatte ānayane,
karena je rasera prakāśa
rasa nityānanda⋅tattva, nitya-siddha sāra-sattva,
jīva-citte tāhāra vikāśa
IDX.en_translation:
Rati, prema, sneha, māna, praṇaya, rāga, anurāga, and bhāva—these seven. As rati deepens, it gradually takes these seven names. The sthāyī-bhāva (permanent emotion) is always pure.

The six emotions beginning with sneha are known as 'prema' to the common people. Whatever emotion one has for Kṛṣṇa, in that way Kṛṣṇa belongs to him. This mystery is indeed eternal in rasa.

The sthāyī-bhāva, the king of all emotions, is seated on the throne of the devotee's heart. The Hlādinī (pleasure-giving potency) is the supreme energy; its essence is pure devotion. It reigns in the form of bhāva.

Bringing the vibhāvas and other components of emotion under its control, it manifests rasa. Rasa is the truth of eternal bliss, the essence of the eternally perfect reality. It develops in the heart of the jīva.

IDX.first_line: ratrisese prata ara purvahna madhyahna para
IDX.search: ratrisese prata ara purvahna madhyahna para sayaŋkala pradosa o nakta ei astakala haya radha krsna lilamaya smare jaha anuksana bhakta nisi avasane hari bhogakunja parihari sviya grhe karaya gamana prate lila godohana sukhadya dravya bhojana purvahne karena gocarana
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
rātriśeṣe prāta (āra), pūrvāhṇa madhyāhna para,
sāyaŋkāla pradoṣa o nakta
ei aṣṭakāla haya, rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa līlāmaya,
smare jāhā anukṣaṇa bhakta

niśi avasāne hari, bhogakuñja parihari,
svīya gṛhe karaya gamana
prāte līlā godohana, sukhādya dravya bhojana,
pūrvāhṇe karena gocāraṇa

madhyāhne vipinadeśe, rādhāsaṅga⋅sukhāveśe,
nitya nitya karena vilāsa
aparāhṇe goṣṭhāpana, sāyaŋkāle godohana,
pradoṣe suhṛtsaha vāsa

rātre punaḥ kuñjaprāpti, rādhāsaṅga⋅sukhāvāpti,
eirūpa līlā aṣṭakāla
nityalīlā ei haya, virāma ta kabhu naya,
cidānanda parama rasāla
IDX.en_translation:
Morning, forenoon, midday, afternoon, evening, dusk, and night—these are the eight periods of the day full of the divine pastimes of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa, which the devotees constantly remember.

At the end of the night, Lord Hari leaves the pleasure groves and returns to His own home. In the morning, His pastimes include milking the cows and eating delicious food, and in the forenoon, He goes out to tend the cows.

At midday, in the forest, He constantly performs pastimes while immersed in the joy of Rādhā's company. In the afternoon, He brings the cows home, in the evening He milks them again, and at dusk He resides with His friends.

At night, He again reaches the groves and attains the joy of Rādhā's company. Such are the eight periods of these divine pastimes. This is the eternal pastime that never ceases; it is full of spiritual bliss and supreme nectar.

IDX.first_line: ratyasvada hetu jata vibhava namete khyata
IDX.search: ratyasvada hetu jata vibhava namete khyata alambana uddipana haya visaya asraya gata alambana dui mata krsna krsnabhakta se ubhaya nayakera siromani svayam krsna gunamani nitya gunadhama paratpara tara bhave anurakta gunadhya jateka bhakta siddha eka sadhaka apara
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
ratyāsvāda⋅hetu jata, ‘vibhāva’⋅nāmete khyāta,
ālambana, uddīpana haya
viṣaya⋅āśraya⋅gata, ālambana dui mata,
‘kṛṣṇa’, ‘kṛṣṇa-bhakta’ se ubhaya

nāyakera śiromaṇi, svayaṁ kṛṣṇa guṇa-maṇi,
nitya guṇa-dhāma parātpara
tā̐’ra bhāve anurakta, guṇāḍhya jateka bhakta,
siddha eka sādhaka apara

bhāva uddīpana kare, ‘uddīpana’ nāma dhare,
kṛṣṇera sambandha vastu saba
smitāsya, saurabha, śṛṅga, vaṁśī, kambu, kṣetra, bhṛṅga,
padāṅka, nūpura, kalarava

tulasī⋅bhajana⋅cina, bhaktajana⋅daraśana,
eirūpa nānā uddīpana
bhaktirasa⋅āsvādane, ei saba hetu-gaṇe,
nirdeśilā rūpa⋅sanātana
IDX.en_translation:
All causes for relishing love are known as 'vibhāva', which consist of supporters and excitants. In relation to the object and the shelter, the supporters are of two kinds: Kṛṣṇa and the devotees of Kṛṣṇa.

Kṛṣṇa Himself is the crown-jewel of lovers, the jewel of all qualities, the eternal abode of virtues, and the Supreme Absolute. Those many devotees who are attached to His mood and are rich in qualities are either perfected or practitioners.

Things that excite these feelings are called 'uddīpana'; these are all objects related to Kṛṣṇa: a smiling face, fragrance, a horn, a flute, a conch, a holy place, a bumblebee, footprints, ankle bells, and sweet sounds.

The Tulasi plant, a sign of worship, and the sight of devotees are various excitants. For relishing the mellow of devotion, all these causes were described by Rūpa and Sanātana.

IDX.first_line: rupanuga tattvasara bujhite akanksa jara
IDX.search: rupanuga tattvasara bujhite akanksa jara rasajnana tara prayojana cinmaya anandarasa sarvatattva jara vasa akhanda paramatattvadhana jara bhane jnani jana brahmalaya anvesana kare nahi bujhi veda marma jara chayamatra vare jogi jana joga kare jara chale karmi kare karma
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
rūpānuga⋅tattvasāra, bujhite ākāṅkṣā jā̐’ra,
rasajñāna tā̐’ra prayojana
cinmaya⋅ānandarasa, sarvatattva jā̐'ra vaśa,
akhaṇḍa paramatattvadhana

jā̐’ra bhāṇe jñānī jana, brahmalaya anveṣaṇa,
kare, nāhi bujhi’ veda⋅marma
jā̐’ra chāyāmātra⋅vare, jogī jana joga kare,
jā̐’ra chale karmī kare karma

vibhāvānubhāva āra, sāttvika, sañcārī⋅cāra,
‘sthāyī bhāve’ milana sundara
sthāyī bhāve ‘rasa’ haya, nitya cidānandamaya,
parama āsvādya nirantara

je rasa prapañca⋅gata, jaḍa kāvye prakāśita,
parama rasera asanmūrti
asanmūrti nitya naya, ādarśera chāyā haya,
jena marīcikā⋅jalasphūrti
IDX.en_translation:
One who desires to understand the essence of the truth according to Rūpa Gosvāmī needs knowledge of the transcendental mellows. The spiritual mellow of bliss, to which all truths are subservient, is the indivisible supreme wealth of truth.

By its mere reflection, the wise people search for merging into the Absolute without understanding the essence of the Vedas. By its mere shadow, the yogis practice discipline; by its mere pretense, the fruitive workers perform their actions.

The causes of emotion, external manifestations, involuntary ecstatic symptoms, and transitory emotions—these four, in beautiful union with the permanent sentiment, become rasa, which is eternally full of spiritual bliss and constantly supremely relishable.

That mellow which is material, manifest in worldly poetry, is a distorted reflection of the supreme mellow. A distorted reflection is not eternal; it is a shadow of the ideal, like the appearance of water in a mirage.

IDX.first_line: rupanuganam pravaram sudantam
IDX.search: rupanuganam pravaram sudantam sri gaura candra priya bhakta rajam sri radhika madhava cittaramam vande jagannatha vibhum varenyam sri surya kundasrayinah krpalo rvidvadvaram srimadhusudanasya prestha svarupena virajamanam vande jagannatha vibhum varenyam
IDX.author: śrīla bhaktirakṣaka śrīdhara gosvāmī mahārāja
IDX.verses:
rūpānugānāṁ pravaraṁ sudāntaṁ
śrī⋅gaura⋅candra⋅priya⋅bhakta⋅rājam
śrī⋅rādhikā⋅mādhava⋅cittarāmaṁ
vande jagannātha⋅vibhuṁ vareṇyam

śrī⋅sūrya⋅kuṇḍāśrayiṇaḥ kṛpālo⋅
rvidvadvaraṁ śrīmadhusūdanasya
preṣṭha⋅svarūpeṇa virājamānaṁ
vande jagannātha⋅vibhuṁ vareṇyam

śrīdhāma⋅vṛndāvana⋅vāsi⋅bhakta⋅
nakṣatrarājisthita⋅somatulyam
ekānta⋅nāmāśrita⋅saṅghapālaṁ
vande jagannātha⋅vibhuṁ vareṇyam

vairāgya⋅vidyā⋅hari⋅bhakti⋅dīptaṁ
daurjanya⋅kāpaṭya⋅vibhedavajram
śraddhāyuteṣvādara⋅vṛttimantaṁ
vande jagannātha⋅vibhuṁ vareṇyam

saṁprerito gaura⋅sudhāṁśunā ja⋅
ścakre hi tajjanma⋅gṛha⋅prakāśam
devairnutaṁ vaiṣṇava⋅sārvabhaumaṁ
vande jagannātha⋅vibhuṁ vareṇyam

sañcārya sarvaṁ nija⋅śakti⋅rāśiṁ
jo bhaktipūrve ca vinoda⋅deve
tene jagatyāṁ hari⋅nāma⋅vanyām
vande jagannātha⋅vibhuṁ vareṇyam

śrī⋅nāma⋅dhāmnoḥ pravala⋅pracāre
īhāparaṁ prema⋅rasābdhimagnam
śrī⋅yogapīṭhe kṛta⋅nṛtya⋅bhaṅgaṁ
vande jagannātha⋅vibhuṁ vareṇyam

māyāpure dhāmani sakta⋅cittaṁ
gaura⋅prakāśena ca modayuktam
śrī⋅nāma⋅gānairgaladaśru⋅netraṁ
vande jagannātha⋅vibhuṁ vareṇyam

he deva! he vaiṣṇava⋅sārvabhauma!
bhaktyā parābhūta⋅mahendradhiṣṇya!
tvadgotra⋅vistāravatīṁ supuṇyāṁ
vande muhurbhakti⋅vinoda⋅dhārām
IDX.en_translation:
I worship the great and powerful Śrīla Jagannātha dāsa Bābājī Mahārāja, the best of the followers of Śrīla Rūpa Gosvāmī. He is perfectly self-controlled, the beloved devotee of Śrī Gaura⋅candra, and the delight of Śrī Śrī Rādhā-Mādhava.

I worship the great and powerful Śrīla Jagannātha dāsa Bābājī Mahārāja. He is the best of scholars, compassionate, and resides at Sūrya-kuṇḍa. He shines as the dearest associate of Śrīla Madhusūdana dāsa Bābājī.

I worship the great and powerful Śrīla Jagannātha dāsa Bābājī Mahārāja. He is the moon amongst the stars of the devotees of Vṛndāvana, and he is the guardian of those who have taken exclusive shelter of the Holy Name.

I worship the great and powerful Śrīla Jagannātha dāsa Bābājī Mahārāja. He radiates renunciation, wisdom, and devotion to Lord Hari. He is a thunderbolt smashing wickedness and deceit, yet he is full of respect for the faithful.

I worship the great and powerful Śrīla Jagannātha dāsa Bābājī Mahārāja. Inspired by the moon-like Lord Gaura, he revealed the Lord's birthplace. Praised by the demigods, he is the emperor of the Vaiṣṇavas.

I worship the great and powerful Śrīla Jagannātha dāsa Bābājī Mahārāja. By infusing his own power into Śrīla Bhaktivinoda Ṭhākura, he caused a flood of the Holy Name to drown the entire world.

I worship the great and powerful Śrīla Jagannātha dāsa Bābājī Mahārāja. Eager to preach the glories of the Name and the Abode, and submerged in the ocean of divine love, he dances in ecstasy at the Yoga-pīṭha.

I worship the great and powerful Śrīla Jagannātha dāsa Bābājī Mahārāja. His heart is attached to Māyāpura Dhāma, and he is filled with joy by the revelation of Lord Gaura. Tears pour from his eyes as he sings the Holy Names.

O Master! O Emperor of Vaiṣṇavas! Your devotion conquers the abode of Indra. I bow again and again to the holy line of Śrīla Bhaktivinoda Ṭhākura, which has expanded your divine family.

IDX.first_line: sabu meli balaka bhaga vicari
IDX.search: sabu meli balaka bhaga vicari chodabi moha soka cittavikari caudda bhuvana pati nandakumara saci nandana bhela nadiya avatara
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
sabu meli’ bālaka⋅bhāga vicāri’
choḍabi moha⋅śoka citta-vikārī

caudda⋅bhuvana⋅pati nandakumārā
śacī⋅nandana bhela nadīyā⋅avatārā

sohi gokulacā̐da aṅgane mora
nācoi bhakta⋅saha ānanda⋅vibhora

śunata nāmagāna bālaka mora
choḍala deha hari⋅prīti⋅vibhora

aichana bhāga jaba bhoi hāmārā
tavahu̐ hou bhava⋅sāgara⋅pārā

tuhu̐ sabu vichari’ ehi vicārā
kā̐he karabi śoka cittavikārā

sthira nāhi hoobi jadi upadeśe
vañcita hoobi rase avaśeṣe

paśibu̐ hāma sura⋅taṭinī māhe
bhaktivinoda pramāda dekhe tāhe
IDX.en_translation:
Considering the pure fortune of a child, You should abandon the delusion and grief that disturb the mind.

Kṛṣṇa, the Lord of the fourteen worlds, Appeared as Caitanya Mahāprabhu in Nadīyā.

That very Kṛṣṇa dances in my courtyard, Overwhelmed with bliss, along with His devotees.

Hearing the chanting of holy names, my child Left his body, utterly absorbed in love for Kṛṣṇa.

When such fortune comes to me, Then I too will cross over the ocean of material existence.

Forgetting all this consideration, Why do you cause grief and mental agitation?

If you are not steady in these instructions, You will eventually be deprived of the divine nectar.

I will enter the celestial Ganges, Bhaktivinoda sees the danger in that.

IDX.first_line: sacira anginaya nace visvambhara raya
IDX.search: sacira anginaya nace visvambhara raya hasi hasi phiri phiri mayere lukaya vayane vasana diya bale lukainu saci bale visvambhara ami na dekhinu
IDX.author: vasudeva ghoṣa
IDX.verses:
śacīra āṅgināya nāce viśvambhara rāya
hāsi hāsi phiri phiri māyere lukāya

vayane vasana diyā bale lukāinu
śacī bale viśvambhara āmi nā dekhinu

māyera añcala dhari cañcala caraṇe
nāciyā nāciyā jāya khañjanagamane

vāsudeva ghoṣa kaya aparūpa śobhā
śiśurūpa dekhi haya jagamana lobhā
IDX.en_translation:
Lord Viśvambhara dances in Mother Śacī's courtyard. Smiling and spinning around, He hides from His mother.

Covering His face with a cloth, He says, "I have hidden!" Mother Śacī says, "O Viśvambhara! I cannot see You!"

Holding His mother's cloth with His restless hands, He dances and moves like a wagtail bird.

Vāsudeva Ghoṣa says: "What wondrous beauty! Seeing His child-form, the minds of everyone in the world are captivated."

IDX.first_line: sacira nandana gora e cada bayane
IDX.search: sacira nandana gora e cada bayane dhavali sanli vali dake ghane ghane bujhiya bhavera gati nityananda raya singara savada kari vadane vajaya
IDX.author: vaṁśīvadane
IDX.verses: śacīra nandana gorā e cā̐da bayāne dhavalī śāṅlī vali ḍāke ghane ghane bujhiyā bhāvera gati nityānanda rāya śiṅgāra śavada kari vadane vājāya nitāi cādera mukje śṛaṅgāra niśāna śuniyā bhakatagaṇe preme ageyāna dhāila paṇḍita gaurīdāsa jāra nāma bhāiyāre bhāiyāre vali dhāya abhirāma dekhiyā gaurāṅga rūpa premera āveśa śire cuḍā śikhi⋅pākhā naṭavara veśa caraṇe nūpura bāje sarvāṅge candana vaṁśīvadane kahe cala govardhana
IDX.en_translation:
Lord Gorā, the son of Mother Śacī, with His moon-like face, calls out again and again with delightful sounds to the cows.

Understanding the flow of His mood, Lord Nityānanda makes a loving sound with a horn.

From the moon-like face of Lord Nitāi comes a signal of divine love; hearing this, the devotees are overwhelmed with ecstatic love.

Paṇḍita Gaurīdāsa ran forward, and Abhirāma Ṭhākura followed him, crying out: "O brothers! O brothers!"

Upon seeing the form of Lord Gaurāṅga, he entered a trance of divine love. The Lord wore an enchanting crown with a peacock feather and was dressed like the best of dancers.

Ankle bells jingle on His feet, and sandalwood paste covers His entire body. Vaṁśīvadana says: "Let us go to Govardhana Hill."
