IDX.en_translation: sādhāraṇa-vandanā (General Obeisance) I offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of the Spiritual Master and the assembly of devotees. I offer my respectful obeisances to Śrī Caitanya Mahāprabhu, who has risen along with Śrī Nityānanda Prabhu. I offer my respectful obeisances to Śrī Nanda⋅nandana and the lotus feet of Śrīmati Rādhikā, accompanied by the gopīs, who make Vṛndāvana so enchanting. I offer my respectful obeisances to the Spiritual Master, to Lord Gaura⋅candra, to Śrīmati Rādhikā and Her abode, to Lord Kṛṣṇa, to His devotees, and to the devotees of His devotees. śrī-guru-vandanā (Obeisance to Śrī Guru) I bow down to that Śrī Guru, by whose abundant mercy I have attained the greatest name, the mantra, Śrī Śacī⋅nandana here, Śrī Svarūpa Dāmodara, Śrīla Rūpa Gosvāmī and his elder brother Śrīla Sanātana Gosvāmī, the great city of Mathurā, the cowherd village, Śrī Rādhā-kuṇḍa, Govardhana Hill, and the hope of attaining Śrī Śrī Rādhā-Mādhava. Obeisance to you, O Gurudeva, the bestower of all perfection, the embodiment of all auspiciousness, and the source of all bliss. O Śrī Guru, giver of divine knowledge, friend of the destitute, bestower of your own bliss, ocean of mercy, residing in Vṛndāvana! O incarnation of welfare! Please be merciful to me, O preacher of Rādhā's love. Save me, O Master of the Universe, O Guru! I am burnt by the fire of material existence and bitten by the serpent of time. I have taken shelter of you. śrī-guru-rūpa-sakhī-vandanā (Obeisance to Guru as a Sakhī) I constantly offer obeisances to her, who is the abode of shelter for Mādhava, the cohesive energy facing Rādhā, residing in the company of the sakhīs. You are a gopī near the daughter of Vṛṣabhānu. O Guru, having the authority of service! Having given me the service of your own lotus feet, please make me happy in the ocean of bliss, in the mellow of serving the feet of Śrī Rādhā in the forest of Vraja. śrī-vaiṣṇava-vandanā (Obeisance to Vaiṣṇavas) I offer obeisances to those Vaiṣṇavas who are fully submerged in the waves of the excellent nectar of Vṛndāvana, which is the pure ocean of the nectar of Śrī Caitanyacandra's character, and who purify the world by their own qualities and are devoted to the Holy Name of Hari. caturyugīya vaiṣṇava-vandanā (Obeisance to Vaiṣṇavas of the Four Ages) Svayambhū, Nārada, Śambhu, the Kumāras, Kapila, Manu, Prahlāda, Janaka, Bhīṣma, Bali, Vaiyāsaki, and us. Mārkaṇḍeya, Ambarīṣa, Vasu, Vyāsa, Vibhīṣaṇa, Puṇḍarīka, Bali, Śambhu, Prahlāda, Vidura, and Dhruva. Dālbhya, Parāśara, Bhīṣma, Nārada, and others are to be served by the Vaiṣṇavas. I serve Hari; otherwise, I would commit a great offense. śrīman-madhvācārya-vandanā (Obeisance to Śrī Madhvācārya) May the renunciate named Ānanda Tīrtha be victorious! He is an abode of happiness, and the wise praise him as the boat for crossing the ocean of material existence. śrīla-mādhavendra-purī-vandanā (Obeisance to Śrīla Mādhavendra Purī) I offer obeisances to Śrīla Mādhavendra Purī, the best of sannyāsīs, along with his disciples. By him, the wish-fulfilling tree of Kṛṣṇa-bhakti was made to sprout in this world. śrīla-sanātana-gosvāmī-vandanā (Obeisance to Śrīla Sanātana Gosvāmī) I take shelter of that master, Śrīla Sanātana Gosvāmī, the ocean of mercy, who is distressed by the suffering of others. With great effort, he made me drink the nectar of devotion mixed with renunciation, even though I was unwilling and blind. śrīla-rūpa-gosvāmī-vandanā (Obeisance to Śrīla Rūpa Gosvāmī) Holding a straw between my teeth, I beg this again and again: may I become the dust of the lotus feet of Śrīla Rūpa Gosvāmī birth after birth. When will Śrīla Rūpa Gosvāmī himself give me the shelter of his own feet? He who established on this earth the innermost heart's desire of Śrī Caitanya Mahāprabhu. śrīla-rūpa-raghunātha gosvāmī-vandanā (Obeisance to Śrīla Rūpa and Raghunātha Dāsa Gosvāmīs) O tongue! Always chant the name "Rūpa". O mind! Remember Śrīla Rūpa Gosvāmī, the embodiment of mercy. Bow your head to Śrīla Rūpa, whose glance is full of compassion, and to his second beautiful form, Śrīla Raghunātha Dāsa Gosvāmī. śrīla-gopāla-bhaṭṭa-jīva-gosvāmī-vandanā (Obeisance to Śrīla Gopāla Bhaṭṭa and Jīva Gosvāmīs) I bow to Śrīla Gopāla Bhaṭṭa Gosvāmī, who fulfills the desires of the devotees, whose heart is flooded with eternal love, who is distinguished by the friendship of Śrīla Rūpa Gosvāmī, and whose only life is Śrī Rādhā-Ramaṇa. I constantly offer obeisances to Śrīla Jīva Gosvāmī, who is attached to the lotus feet of Śrīla Rūpa Gosvāmī, who is a resident of Vraja, and who gives bliss to the ignorant. śrī-ṣaḍ-gosvāmī-vandanā (Obeisance to the Six Gosvāmīs) I offer obeisances to Śrīla Rūpa Gosvāmī along with his elder brother, to the merciful Raghunātha Dāsa, to Śrīla Jīva Gosvāmī, and to the two Bhaṭṭas, who bestow happiness upon the saintly. I hold on my head the dust of the feet of these Gosvāmīs, who are naturally affectionate, again and again, for the destruction of all obstacles. śrī-pañca-tattva-vandanā (Obeisance to the Pañca-tattva) I bow to Lord Kṛṣṇa, who appears as the Pañca-tattva: in the form of a devotee, the manifestation of a devotee, the incarnation of a devotee, the pure devotee, and the devotional energy. I offer obeisances to the Gurus, the devotees of the Lord, the Lord Himself, His incarnations, His manifestations, and His energies. I bow to the Lord known as Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya. O Viśvambhara! O abode of the great nectar of mellows! O ocean of love! O son of mother Śacī! O You whose heart melts with compassion! You are the best friend of the fallen! O Lord of Sītā! O Master of the moving and non-moving! O You who are capable of bringing down the Gaura avatar! O object of affection for Śrīvāsa and Gadādhara! You are my shelter, You are my goal. śrī-śrīvāsa-vandanā (Obeisance to Śrī Śrīvāsa Paṇḍita) I praise Śrīvāsa Paṇḍita, the dear associate of Lord Gaurāṅga. By even a drop of his mercy, attachment to Lord Gaurāṅga is born. O Śrīvāsa! O bliss of kīrtana! O beloved of the assembly of devotees! I bow to you, O great mystic! You are Nārada Muni in the form of a devotee. I bow with joy to that Śrīvāsa, the foremost Paṇḍita, the recipient of Śrī Gaurāṅga's mercy, whose mind finds bliss in kīrtana. śrī-gadādhara-paṇḍita-vandanā (Obeisance to Śrī Gadādhara Paṇḍita) I offer obeisances to Śrī Gadādhara Paṇḍita, the delight of Śrī Madhvācārya, the embodiment of Mahābhāva, whose form is non-different from Śrī Caitanya. Obeisances forever to Śrī Gadādhara, who is the form of Śrīmati Rādhā⋅rāṇī, the wealth of Lord Gaurāṅga's love. O merciful one! O Gadādhara! Obeisances to you, the embodiment of the Hlādinī-śakti, the companion of Lord Gaurāṅga, the bestower of devotional energy. śrī-śrī-advaita-prabhu-vandanā (Obeisance to Śrī Advaita Prabhu) Mahā-Viṣṇu, the creator of the universe, who creates this world through Māyā; this Advaita Ācārya is indeed His avatar, the Supreme Controller. I take shelter of that Advaita Ācārya, who is the Supreme Lord, the incarnation of a devotee. He is called 'Advaita' because He is non-different from Hari, and 'Ācārya' because He teaches devotion. O Śrī Advaita! Obeisances to you, the ocean of mercy for the people of Kali-yuga. Obeisances to the Lord of Śrī Sītā Ṭhākurāṇī, the bestower of love for Gaura. I offer obeisances to Śrīla Advaita Prabhu, the giver of loving devotion, who is dear to Śrīman Mādhavendra Purī and belongs to the Śrī Mādhava Sampradāya. Obeisances to Advaita, the Great Lord, the Great Soul, by whose mercy attachment to the lotus feet of Śrī Caitanya is born. śrī-śrī-nityānanda-prabhu-vandanā (Obeisance to Śrī Nityānanda Prabhu) O Nityānanda! Obeisances to You, the giver of the bliss of divine love. Obeisances to the Lord of Jāhnavā Devī, for the destruction of the impurities of Kali-yuga. O son of Hāḍāi Paṇḍita! O ocean of mercy! O son of Padmāvatī! Your lotus feet are the shelter of all holy places! You are the wish-fulfilling tree of love, the avatar who removes all distress! Please protect me, a sinner, an orphan who has no other friend. I constantly offer obeisances to that Nityānanda, the Spiritual Master of the Universe, by whom this entire joyless world was made the abode of the bliss of love. I offer obeisances to Śrī Nityānanda Prabhu, who has a pearl shining in His ear, and who purified the earth by appearing as the elder brother of Śrī Caitanya. I constantly bow to Śrī Nityānanda Prabhu, the Lord who increases the ocean of love. In generosity, He surpasses the wish-fulfilling cows, celestial trees, moons, and gems. In beauty, He defeats the Cupids. He is the reservoir of the happiness of Brahman. In motherly affection, He challenges the herds of cows. I offer obeisances to Śrīman Nityānanda Candra, the embodiment of mercy, whose body is non-different from Śrī Caitanya, and who is dear to everyone. śrī-śrī-gaurāṅga-mahāprabhu-vandanā (Obeisance to Śrī Gaurāṅga Mahāprabhu) I offer obeisances to Your lotus feet, O Great Personality! You are always to be meditated upon. You destroy the insult of material life and fulfill all desires. You are the abode of all holy places, praised by Śiva and Brahmā. You are the only shelter, the reliever of the distress of Your servants, the protector of the surrendered, and the boat for crossing the ocean of material existence. I offer obeisances to Your lotus feet, O Great Personality! Abandoning the opulence of royalty desired by the gods, which is hard to renounce, You, the most righteous one, went to the forest in honor of the word of a brāhmaṇa, and ran after the illusionary deer desired by Your beloved. Obeisances, obeisances to You, O Śrī Caitanyacandra! Your form is full of blissful pastimes. You are beautiful with a brilliant golden luster, and You are the bestower of the great nectar of love. All glories to the power of the new lamp of Navadvīpa, the lion who defeats the elephants of atheism! Śrī Caitanyacandra, the Supreme Lord Murāri, holds the chanting beads of His own Holy Name. He is victorious! His valor is pure, He shines like gold, His eyes are large like lotus petals, His arms extend to His excellent knees, and He dances, manifesting the nectar of devotion in many ways. O Śrī Caitanyacandra! Please give me the support of Your lotus feet. I have fallen into the ocean of the misery of material existence, swallowed by the crocodiles and sharks of lust, anger, and greed, fettered by wicked desires, and I am without shelter. O Śrī Caitanyacandra! Make the lily of my heart bloom and delight it with the play of the bumblebee of Your own remembrance. Having shaken off the dense darkness of offenses, O merciful one, please make me, this unfortunate soul, drink the nectar of Your lotus feet. May that son of Mother Śacī always shine in the cave of your heart. He is Śrī Hari Himself, glowing with a multitude of beautiful golden rays. He descended in the Age of Kali out of mercy to bestow the wealth of His own devotion, the elevated brilliant mellow of Vraja, which had not been offered for a long time. I praise the form of Kṛṣṇa, now appearing as Śrī Caitanya, with the luster of the emotions of Śrī Rādhā. These two—the Hlādinī-śakti and the Śaktimān—are eternally one identity, yet previously on earth, They attained separate bodies. Now, They have again obtained unity in the form of Śrī Caitanya. śrī-śrī-gaura-nityānanda-vandanā (Obeisance to Śrī Gaura and Nityānanda) I offer obeisances to the two compassionate avatars, Śrī Gaura and Śrī Nityānanda, who make the world happy. They are the protectors of the religion of the age. Their arms extend to Their knees, Their complexion is golden, They are the fathers of the Saṅkīrtana movement, Their eyes are broad like lotuses. They are the sustainers of the universe and the best of the brāhmaṇas. I offer obeisances to Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya and Śrī Nityānanda, who have risen together on the horizon of Gauḍa like the sun and the moon. They are wonderful, bestowers of all auspiciousness, and dispellers of the darkness of ignorance. I worship the two brothers, Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya and Śrī Nityānanda, who have descended to this world. They are the embodiments of compassion, accompanied by Their associates, and are the Masters of the saintly. śrī-navadvīpa-dhāma-vandanā (Obeisance to Śrī Navadvīpa Dhāma) I offer obeisances to that Navadvīpa, which gives supreme happiness. It is the spiritually risen forest of Vraja. The Chāndogya Upaniṣad calls it the city of the Supreme Brahman, the Smṛti scriptures call it the abode of Viṣṇu named Vaikuṇṭha, others call it Śvetadvīpa, and a rare Rasika calls it the forest of Vraja. I offer obeisances to Śrī Navadvīpa, which maddens everyone with new mercy and possesses a new taste. It is the abode of the Master of the new golden form, Lord Gaura, and the new devotion. It is surrounded by rows of new forests and refreshed by breezes from the new celestial Gaṅgā. It is the place for the performance of the new loud chanting, full of the nectar of Śrī Śrī Rādhā and Hari.

IDX.first_line: sadharani samanjasa sthayi labhe bhava dasa
IDX.search: sadharani samanjasa sthayi labhe bhava dasa kubja ara mahisi pramana eka vrajadevigane mahabhava saŋghatane rudha adhirudha suvidhana nimesasahata taya hrnmanthane khinna praya kalpaksana saukhye sankakula atmavadhi vismarana ksanakalpa vivecana joge va viyoge samatula
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
sādhāraṇī, samañjasā, sthāyī labhe bhāva daśā,
kubjā āra mahiṣī pramāṇa
ekā vrajadevīgaṇe, mahābhāva⋅saŋghaṭane,
‘rūḍha’ ‘adhirūḍha’ su-vidhāna

nimeṣāsahatā tāya, hṛnmanthane khinna prāya,
kalpakṣaṇa, saukhye śaṅkākula
ātmāvadhi vismaraṇa, kṣaṇakalpa vivecana,
joge vā viyoge samatula

adhirūḍha bhāve punaḥ, dvi-prakāra bheda śuna,
‘modana’ ‘mādana’ nāme khyāta
viśleṣa⋅daśāte punaḥ, modana haya ‘mohana’,
divyonmāda tāhe haya jāta

divyonmāda dvi-prakāra, citrajalpodghūrṇā āra,
citrajalpa bahubidha tāya
modanete śrī⋅rādhāra, mādanākhya⋅daśā sāra,
nityalīlāmayī bhāva pāya

sādhāraṇī dhūmāyitā, samañjasā sadā dīptā,
rūḍhe tathoddīptā samarthāya
sūddīptā śrī-rādhā-prema, jena ujjvalita hema,
modanādi bhāve sadā bhāya
IDX.en_translation:
The ordinary and balanced heroines attain a permanent emotional state, with Kubjā and the queens as examples. Only among the cowherd damsels of Vraja, in the manifestation of mahābhāva, are the advanced stages of 'rūḍha' and 'adhirūḍha' found.

In that state, there is intolerance of even a blink of an eye, and one is almost distressed by the churning of the heart; a moment feels like an eon, and even in happiness, one is filled with apprehension. Forgetting oneself and considering a moment as an eon are the same in both union and separation.

Again, listen to the two distinctions in the adhirūḍha stage, known as 'modana' and 'mādana'. In the state of separation, modana becomes 'mohana', from which divine madness is born.

Divine madness is of two types: citrajalpa and udghūrṇā. Citrajalpa itself has many kinds. In modana, Śrī Rādhā's state is the essence of mādana. She attains an emotional state full of eternal pastimes.

Ordinary love is like smoldering, and balanced love is always glowing. In rūḍha, it is intensely shining. The brilliantly shining love of Śrī Rādhā is like blazing gold; it always appears in emotional states like modana.

IDX.first_line: sadhusanga na hoila haya
IDX.search: sadhusanga na hoila haya gela dina akarana kari artha uparjana paramartha rahila kothaya suvarna kariya tyaga tuccha lostre anuraga durbhagara ei ta laksana krsnetara sanga kari sadhujane parihari madagarve katanu jivana
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
sādhu-saṅga nā hoila hāya!
gela dina akāraṇa, kari’ artha upārjana,
paramārtha rahila kothāya

suvarṇa kariyā tyāga, tuccha loṣṭre anurāga,
durbhāgāra ei ta’ lakṣaṇa
kṛṣṇetara saṅga kari’, sādhu-jane parihari’,
madagarve kāṭā’nu jīvana

bhaktimudrā⋅daraśane, hāsya karitāma mane,
bātulatā baliyā tāhāya
je sabhyatā śreṣṭha gaṇi’, hārāinu cintāmaṇi,
śeṣe tāhā rahila kothāya

jñānera garimā bale, bhaktirūpa susambale,
upekṣinu svārtha pāśariyā
duṣṭa jaḍāśrita jñāna, ebe ha’la antardhāna,
karma-bhoge āmāke rākhiyā

ebe jadi sādhu-jane, kṛpā kari’ e durjane,
dena bhakti⋅samudrera bindu
tā’ hoile anāyāse, mukta ha’ye bhavapāśe,
pāra hoi e saṁsāra⋅sindhu
IDX.en_translation:
Alas, association with devotees did not happen! Days passed needlessly in earning money; where did spiritual welfare remain?

Abandoning gold and having an attachment to a trivial clod of earth—this is the sign of the unfortunate. Associating with those not devoted to God and avoiding saintly persons, I spent my life in intoxicated pride.

Upon seeing the signs of devotion, I would laugh in my mind, calling it madness. That civilization which I considered supreme—by it I lost the wish-fulfilling gem; where did it ultimately remain?

Due to the glory of knowledge, I neglected the excellent treasure of devotion, forgetting my own true interest. This wicked knowledge based on matter has now disappeared, leaving me in the experience of my actions.

Now, if saintly persons, out of kindness to this wretched soul, give a drop from the ocean of devotion, then effortlessly, being freed from the noose of material existence, I can cross this ocean of material life.

IDX.first_line: sadopasyah sriman dhrta manuja kayaih pranayitam
IDX.search: sadopasyah sriman dhrta manuja kayaih pranayitam vahadbhir girvanair girisa paramesthi prabhrtibhih sva bhaktebhyah suddham nija bhajana mudram upadisan sa caitanyah kim me punar api drsor jasyati padam suresanam durgam gatir atisayenopanisadam muninam sarva svam pranata patalinam madhurima viniryasah premno nikhila pasu palambuja drsam sa caitanyah kim me punar api drsor jasyati padam
IDX.author: śrīla rūpa gosvāmī
IDX.verses:
sadopāsyaḥ śrīmān dhṛta⋅manuja⋅kāyaiḥ praṇayitāṁ
vahadbhir gīrvāṇair giriśa⋅parameṣṭhi⋅prabhṛtibhiḥ
sva⋅bhaktebhyaḥ śuddhāṁ nija⋅bhajana⋅mudrām upadiśan
sa caitanyaḥ kiṁ me punar api dṛśor jāsyati padam

sureśānāṁ durgaṁ gatir atiśayenopaniṣadāṁ
munīnāṁ sarva⋅svaṁ praṇata⋅paṭalīnāṁ madhurimā
viniryāsaḥ premṇo nikhila⋅paśu⋅pālāmbuja⋅dṛśāṁ
sa caitanyaḥ kiṁ me punar api dṛśor jāsyati padam

svarūpaṁ vibhrāṇo jagad⋅atulam advaita⋅dayitaḥ
prapanna⋅śrīvāso janita⋅paramānanda⋅garimā
harir dīnoddhārī gaja⋅pati⋅kṛpotseka⋅taralaḥ
sa caitanyaḥ kiṁ me punar api dṛśor jāsyati padam

rasoddāmā kāmārbuda⋅madhura⋅dhāmojjvala⋅tanur
jatīnām uttaṁsas taraṇi⋅kara⋅vidyoti⋅vasanaḥ
hiraṇyānāṁ lakṣmī⋅bharam abhibhavann āṅgika⋅rucā
sa caitanyaḥ kiṁ me punar api dṛśor jāsyati padam

hare kṛṣṇety uccaiḥ sphurita⋅rasano nāma⋅gaṇanā⋅
kṛta⋅granthi⋅śreṇī⋅subhaga⋅kaṭi⋅sūtrojjvala⋅karaḥ
viśālākṣo dīrghārgala⋅yugala⋅khelāñcita⋅bhujaḥ
sa caitanyaḥ kiṁ me punar api dṛśor jāsyati padam

payorāśes tīre sphurad⋅upavanālī⋅kalanayā
muhur vṛndāraṇya⋅smaraṇa⋅janita⋅prema⋅vivaśaḥ
kvacit kṛṣṇāvṛtti⋅pracala⋅rasano⋅bhakti⋅rasikaḥ
sa caitanyaḥ kiṁ me punar api dṛśor jāsyati padam

rathārūḍhasyārād adhipadavi nīlācala⋅pater
adabhra⋅premormi⋅sphurita⋅naṭanollāsa⋅vivaśaḥ
sa⋅harṣaṁ gāyadbhiḥ parivṛta⋅tanur vaiṣṇava⋅janaiḥ
sa caitanyaḥ kiṁ me punar api dṛśor jāsyati padam

bhuvaṁ siñcann aśru⋅śrutibhir abhitaḥ sāndra⋅pulakaiḥ
parītāṅgo nīpa⋅stabaka⋅nava⋅kiñjalka⋅jayibhiḥ
ghana⋅sveda⋅stoma⋅stimita⋅tanur utkīrtana⋅sukhī
sa caitanyaḥ kiṁ me punar api dṛśor jāsyati padam

adhīte gaurāṅga⋅smaraṇa⋅padavī⋅maṅgalataraṁ
kṛtī jo viśrambha⋅sphurad⋅amala⋅dhīr aṣṭakam idam
parānande sadyas tad⋅amala⋅padāmbhoja⋅yugale
parisphārā tasya sphuratu nitarāṁ prema⋅laharī
IDX.en_translation:
Will that glorious Śrī Caitanya, who is always worshiped by demigods like Giriśa (Lord Śiva) and Parameṣṭhi (Lord Brahmā) and others, who assume human bodies out of affection, and who instructs His devotees in the pure method of His own worship, again become visible to my eyes?

Will that Śrī Caitanya, who is an inaccessible destination for the kings of the demigods, the special goal of the Upaniṣads, the very life of the sages, the sweetness for the multitude of surrendered souls, and the essence of love for all the lotus-eyed gopīs, again become visible to my eyes?

Will that Śrī Caitanya, who manifests a unique form in the world, the beloved of Advaita Ācārya, the protector of Prapanna Śrīvāsa, the source of supreme bliss and greatness, Hari, the deliverer of the fallen, and who is moved by the outpouring of grace to Gajapati (King Pratāparudra), again become visible to my eyes?

Will that Śrī Caitanya, who is full of ecstatic rasa, whose brilliant form is as sweet as millions of Cupids, the crest-jewel of ascetics, whose garments shine like sunbeams, and who overcomes the opulence of gold and Lakṣmī with the splendor of His bodily luster, again become visible to my eyes?

Will that Śrī Caitanya, whose tongue vibrates loudly chanting "Hare Kṛṣṇa," whose charming hand shines with a beautiful thread (mālā) for counting names, with a series of knots, who has broad eyes, and whose playfully moving arms resemble long bolts, again become visible to my eyes?

Will that Śrī Caitanya, who, on the shore of the ocean, seeing the shining rows of gardens, becomes repeatedly overwhelmed by love arising from the remembrance of Vṛndāvana, and who sometimes, with a restless tongue repeating Kṛṣṇa's names, is a connoisseur of devotional rasa, again become visible to my eyes?

Will that Śrī Caitanya, who, near the Ratha (chariot)-riding Lord of Nīlācala (Jagannātha Purī), becomes overwhelmed by boundless waves of love, manifesting ecstatic dancing, and whose body is surrounded by Vaiṣṇavas joyfully singing, again become visible to my eyes?

Will that Śrī Caitanya, who sprinkles the earth with streams of tears, whose body is covered all around with dense goosebumps surpassing the fresh pollen of a nīpa flower cluster, whose body is moistened by profuse streams of sweat, and who finds joy in loud chanting, again become visible to my eyes?

May the waves of love of that fortunate person, whose pure intelligence shines with faith, and who studies this aṣṭakam (eight verse prayer), the supremely auspicious path of remembering Gaurāṅga, spread abundantly and immediately in supreme bliss at His immaculate lotus feet.

IDX.first_line: sajala jalada anga manohara chataye cahila nahe
IDX.search: sajala jalada anga manohara chataye cahila nahe isata hasiya manera akuti aruna nayane kahe aju ki pekhalu vinoda nagara keli kadambera tale rupa nirakhite ankhira laja bhasila ananda jale
IDX.author: govinda dāsa
IDX.verses: sajala jalada aṅga manohara chaṭāye cāhila nahe īṣata hāsiyā⋅manera ākuti aruṇa nayane kahe āju ki pekhalu⋅vinoda nāgara keli⋅kadambera tale rūpa nirakhite⋅āṅkhira lāja bhāsila ānanda⋅jale baula⋅māla diyā kuntala ṭāniyā ma⋅ura⋅pucchera chānde raṅginī locana⋅khañjana vandhite pātila viṣama phānde makara⋅kuṇḍala⋅saṅge dolaye gaṇḍa darapaṇa⋅bhāne bhāle se madana tāhe bimbita govinda⋅dāsa anumāne
IDX.en_translation:
With a body like a fresh raincloud, His enchanting splendor makes it impossible to look away. Gently smiling, His reddish eyes express the deep yearning of His heart.

What did I see today? The playful Lover beneath the Kadamba tree of pastimes. Gazing at His beauty, the shyness of my eyes melted into tears of joy.

With a flower garland pulling His hair, He is adorned with a peacock-feather crown. To captivate the restless eyes of the gopīs, He has set an intricate trap.

His shark-shaped earrings sway, reflecting upon His mirror-like cheeks. On His forehead, Cupid himself is reflected—so Govinda Dāsa surmises.

IDX.first_line: sajani pekhalu varaja kisora
IDX.search: sajani pekhalu varaja kisora piba ite vadana sudhakara madhuri matala nayana cakora mattama ura sikhandika mandita cudaye malati mala parimale mati panti mata madhukara gunjare tahi rasala
IDX.author: govinda dāsa
IDX.verses: sajani pekhalu varaja⋅kiśora piba⋅ite vadana⋅sudhākara⋅mādhurī mātala nayana⋅cakora mattama⋅ura⋅śikhaṇḍika⋅maṇḍita cūḍāye mālatī māla parimale māti pānti mata madhukara guñjare tāhi rasāla nīla jalada tanu bhāṅu madana dhanu nayana⋅kamala pañca bāṇa jara jara antara kulavatī⋅gaurava saṁśaya rahala parāṇe madana makara janu maṇimaya kuṇḍala ṭalamala dolata kāne hera⋅ite jaga⋅mana⋅mīna garāsaye govinda⋅dāsa paramāṇe
IDX.en_translation:
O friend! I saw the youthful Lord of Vraja! Drinking the sweetness of His moon-like face, the cakora birds of my eyes became intoxicated.

He is adorned with a beautiful peacock feather, and a jasmine garland rests upon His topknot. Intoxicated by its fragrance, rows of bees hum sweetly there.

His body is like a dark cloud, and His eyebrows break the bow of Cupid. His lotus eyes are like the five arrows of Cupid. My heart is agitated, and my very life is at stake.

Like the shark-shaped emblem of Cupid, jeweled earrings sway upon His ears. Seeing this, the fish of the world's mind is swallowed by His beauty; Govinda Dāsa testifies to this.

IDX.first_line: sakala vaisnava gosani daya kara moredante trna dhari kahe e dina pamare
IDX.search: sakala vaisnava gosani daya kara moredante trna dhari kahe e dina pamare sri guru carana ara sri krsnacaitanyapadapadma paoyaiya more kara dhanya
IDX.author: rādhāmohana dāsa
IDX.verses:
sakala vaiṣṇava gosāñi dayā kara moredante tṛṇa dhari’ kahe e dīna pāmare

śrī⋅guru⋅caraṇa āra śrī⋅kṛṣṇacaitanyapāda-padma pāoyāiyā more kara dhanya

tomā’ sabāra karuṇā vine ihā prāpti nayaviśeṣe ayogya muñi kahila niścaya

vāñchā⋅kalpataru hoo karuṇā⋅sāgaraei ta’ bharasā muñi dhariye antara

guṇa⋅leśa nāhi mora aparādhera sīmāāmā’ uddhāriyā loke dekhāo mahimā

nāma⋅saṅkīrtana⋅ruci āra prema⋅dhanae rādhāmohane deha’ haiyā sakaruṇa
IDX.en_translation:
All the spiritual masters and devotees, please be merciful to me! This poor, fallen soul says this while holding a straw between my teeth.

helping me attain the lotus feet of my spiritual master and Lord Caitanya, please make me blessed.

Without the mercy of all of you, this cannot be attained. I declare with certainty that I am especially unworthy.

Be like wish-fulfilling trees and an ocean of mercy. This is the hope I hold within my heart.

I have no trace of good qualities, and my offenses are limitless. By delivering me, please show your glory to the people.

Being merciful, please bestow upon me the taste for congregational chanting and the wealth of divine love.

IDX.first_line: sakha gana sanga chadi jadunandana
IDX.search: sakha gana sanga chadi jadunandana calatahi nagara raje bhavini manoratha calala vipina pathe sadhite manoratha kaje catura siromani kana heri jamuna jala manamatha uthalala purala murali nisana
IDX.verses: sakhā⋅gaṇa saṅga, chāḍi jadunandana calatahi nāgara rāje bhāvinī manoratha, calala vipina pathe sādhite manoratha kāje catura śiromaṇi kāna heri jamunā jala, manamatha uthalala purala mūralī niśāna sirajilā tarīkhāni, prvāla mukutā āni mājhe mājhe hīrāra gāthani śikhipuccha guñjāchaḍā, rajata kāñcane moḍā keroyāle rajata kiṅkiṇī tapana⋅tanayā⋅tīre, taruṇi looyā phire vidagara rāja govinda dāsa bhaṇe, ki ānanda haila mane runu jhunu nūpūra bāja
IDX.en_translation:
Leaving the company of His friends, Lord Kṛṣṇa, the King of Lovers, walks along. Contemplating His desires, He walks on the forest path to fulfill them.

Lord Kṛṣṇa, the crest-jewel of the clever, seeing the waters of the Yamunā, feels the desires of love overflow, and the sound of His flute fills the air.

He fashioned a boat, bringing coral and pearls, with diamonds strung between them. It was adorned with peacock feathers, guñjā berries, silver, and gold, and the oars were decorated with silver bells.

On the banks of the Yamunā, the clever King takes the young gopīs around. Govinda Dāsa says: "What bliss arose in His mind as the anklets tinkled runu-jhunu."

IDX.first_line: sakhagana dhenugana laye krsna jana vana
IDX.search: sakhagana dhenugana laye krsna jana vana gosthaloka sange sange jaya mata pita adi jata sange jaya sata sata sri krsnadarsana lalasaya nanachala upadese vidaiya savasese sri radhaya saŋketa kariya dura vane jaya hari radhara saŋketa smari radha kunde govardhana hana
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
sakhāgaṇa dhenugaṇa, laye kṛṣṇa jāna vana,
goṣṭhaloka saṅge saṅge jāya
mātā pitā ādi jata, saṅge jāya śata śata,
śrī⋅kṛṣṇadarśana lālasāya

nānāchala upadeśe, vidāiyā savaśeṣe,
śrī⋅rādhāya saŋketa kariyā
dūra vane jāya hari, rādhāra saŋketa smari,
rādhā⋅kuṇḍe govardhana hañā

hethā kundalatā sakhī, laye rādhā guṇavatī,
jaṭilāre kare samarpaṇa
jaṭilā santuṣṭa haye, ādeśe⋅śrī⋅rādhā laye,
karibāre sūryera arcana

chala peye sakhīgaṇa, kare nānā saŋghaṭana,
rādhā⋅kuṇḍe abhisāra tare
‘tulasī’ mālikā laye, kṛṣṇe deya ‘dūtī’ haye,
kṛṣṇavārtā āne rādhāghare
IDX.en_translation:
Taking His friends and the cows, Lord Kṛṣṇa goes to the forest, followed by the residents of the cowherd village. Hundreds of mothers and fathers go along with Him out of their longing to see the Lord.

With various pretexts and instructions He finally dismisses everyone, after giving a signal to Śrīmati Rādhā⋅rāṇī. Lord Hari goes to a distant forest, and remembering Rādhā's signal, He arrives at Rādhā-kuṇḍa near Govardhana Hill.

Meanwhile, the gopī friend Kundalatā takes the virtuous Rādhā and entrusts Her to Jaṭilā. Jaṭilā, becoming pleased, instructs them to take Śrī Rādhā to perform worship of the Sun-god.

Taking this opportunity, the gopīs make various arrangements for the meeting at Rādhā-kuṇḍa. Taking a Tulasī garland, one gopī becomes a messenger and gives it to Lord Kṛṣṇa, then brings His message back to Rādhā's house.

IDX.first_line: sakhe kalaya gauramudaram
IDX.search: sakhe kalaya gauramudaram nindita hataka kanti kalevara garvitamarakamaram madhukara ranjita malati mandita jitaghana kuncita kesam tilaka vinindita sasadhara rupaka bhuvana manohara vesam
IDX.author: rādhāmohana dāsa
IDX.verses:
sakhe, kalaya gauramudāram
nindita⋅hāṭaka⋅kānti⋅kalevara⋅
garvita-māraka-māram

madhukara⋅rañjita⋅mālati⋅maṇḍita⋅
jitaghana⋅kuñcita⋅keśam
tilaka⋅vinindita⋅śaśadhara⋅rūpaka⋅
bhuvana⋅manohara⋅veśam

madhu⋅madhurasmita⋅lobhita⋅tanubhṛtam
anupama⋅bhāva⋅vilāsam
nidhuvana⋅nāgarī⋅mohita⋅mānasa⋅
vikathita⋅gadgada⋅bhāṣam

paramākiñcana⋅kiñcana⋅naragaṇa⋅
karuṇā⋅vitaraṇaśīlam
kṣobhita⋅durmati⋅rādhāmohana⋅
nāmaka⋅nirupama⋅līlam
IDX.en_translation:
Friend, behold the munificent Gaura. His body, with its golden luster, reproaches all; He vanquishes the pride of Cupid.

His dense, curly hair, adorned with jasmine flowers that attract bees, surpasses the dark clouds. His sacred mark surpasses the beauty of the moon, and His attire enchants the world.

His sweet, honey-like smile entices all living beings. He exhibits incomparable divine emotional pastimes. His mind is enchanted by the young cowherd girls of Nidhuvan, and His speech is choked with ecstasy.

He mercifully bestows compassion upon the truly humble and even upon ordinary people. His unequaled pastimes agitate the mind of Rādhāmohana.

IDX.first_line: samsara davanala lidha loka
IDX.search: samsara davanala lidha loka tranaya karunya ghanaghanatvam praptasya kalyana gunarnavasya vande guroh sri caranaravindam maha prabhoh kirtana nrtya gita vaditra madyan manaso rasena romanca kampasru taranga bhajo vande guroh sri caranaravindam
IDX.author: śrīla viśvanātha cakrabartī ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
saṁsāra⋅dāvānala⋅līḍha⋅loka⋅
trāṇāya kāruṇya⋅ghanāghanatvam
prāptasya kalyāṇa⋅guṇārṇavasya
vande guroḥ śrī⋅caraṇāravindam

mahā⋅prabhoḥ kīrtana⋅nṛtya⋅gīta⋅
vāditra⋅mādyan⋅manaso rasena
romāñca⋅kampāśru⋅taraṅga⋅bhājo
vande guroḥ śrī⋅caraṇāravindam

śrī⋅vigrahārādhana⋅nitya⋅nānā⋅
śṛṅgāra⋅tanmandira⋅mārjanādau
juktasya⋅bhaktāṁś⋅ca⋅niyuñjato’pi
vande guroḥ śrī⋅caraṇāravindam

catur⋅vidha⋅śrī⋅bhagavat⋅prasāda⋅
svādv⋅anna⋅tṛptān hari⋅bhakta⋅saṅghān
kṛtvaiva tṛptiṁ bhajataḥ sadaiva
vande guroḥ śrī⋅caraṇāravindam

śrī⋅rādhikā⋅mādhavayor⋅apāra⋅
mādhurya⋅līlā⋅guṇa⋅rūpa⋅nāmnām
prati⋅kṣaṇāsvādana⋅lolupasya
vande guroḥ śrī⋅caraṇāravindam

nikuñja⋅yūnoḥ⋅rati⋅keli⋅siddhyai
jā jālibhir⋅yuktir⋅apekṣaṇīyā
tatrāti⋅dākṣād⋅ati⋅vallabhasya
vande guroḥ śrī⋅caraṇāravindam

sākṣād⋅dharitvena samasta śāstrair⋅
uktas tathā bhāvyata eva sadbhiḥ
kintu prabhor⋅yaḥ priya eva tasya
vande guroḥ śrī⋅caraṇāravindam

jasya prasādād bhagavat⋅prasādo
jasyā⋅prasādān⋅na gatiḥ kuto’pi
dhyāyan stuvaṁsa tasya jaśas⋅tri⋅sandhyaṁ
vande guroḥ śrī⋅caraṇāravindam

śrīmad⋅guroraṣṭakam⋅etad⋅uccair
brāhme muhūrte paṭhati prayatnāt
jastena vṛndāvana⋅nātha⋅sākṣāt
sevaiva labhyā januṣo’nta eva
IDX.en_translation:
I offer obeisances to the lotus feet of the spiritual master, who is like a dense cloud of mercy appearing to save those who are scorched by the blazing forest fire of material existence. He is an ocean of auspicious qualities.

I offer obeisances to the lotus feet of the spiritual master, whose mind is maddened by the nectar of the congregational chanting, dancing, singing, and musical instruments of Lord Caitanya. He is always experiencing waves of ecstatic thrills, trembling, and tears.

I offer obeisances to the lotus feet of the spiritual master, who is engaged in the daily worship of the Deity of the Lord, adorning Them with various ornaments and cleaning Their temple. He also engages the devotees in these services.

I offer obeisances to the lotus feet of the spiritual master, who is always satisfied by seeing the assemblies of devotees contentedly partaking of the four kinds of delicious food offered to the Supreme Lord.

I offer obeisances to the lotus feet of the spiritual master, who is ever greedy to relish, moment after moment, the unlimited sweetness of the pastimes, qualities, forms, and names of Śrī Rādhā and Mādhava.

I offer obeisances to the lotus feet of the spiritual master, who is very expert and dear to the Divine Couple in bringing about the success of Their loving pastimes through whatever arrangements are required by the companions.

I offer obeisances to the lotus feet of the spiritual master, who is directly the Lord Himself according to all the scriptures and is also considered so by the saintly. However, he is the dearmost beloved of the Lord.

I offer obeisances to the lotus feet of the spiritual master. By his mercy, one achieves the mercy of the Lord. Without his mercy, there is no progress toward the destination. Therefore, one should meditate upon and glorify his fame three times a day.

Whoever carefully recites these eight stanzas glorifying the spiritual master loudly during the auspicious hour before dawn will surely attain the direct service of the Lord of Vṛndāvana at the end of this very life.

IDX.first_line: sandarsana samsparsana jalpa vartmanirodhana
IDX.search: sandarsana samsparsana jalpa vartmanirodhana rasa vrndavana lila bhuri jalakeli jamunaya naukakhela cauryataya ghatta lila kunje lukocuri madhupana vadhuvesa kapata nidra avesa dyutakrida vastra tanatani cumbaslesa nakharpana bimbadhara sudhapana samprayoga adi lila mani
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
sandarśana, saṁsparśana, jalpa, vartma-nirodhana,
rāsa, vṛndāvana⋅līlā bhūri
jala-keli jamunāya, naukā-khelā, cauryatāya,
ghaṭṭa⋅līlā, kuñje lukocuri

madhupāna, vadhūveṣa, kapaṭa⋅nidrā⋅āveśa,
dyūtakrīḍā, vastra⋅ṭānāṭāni
cumbāśleṣa, nakhārpaṇa, bimbādhara⋅sudhāpāna,
samprayoga ādi līlā māni

sambhogaprakāra saba, sambhogera mahotsava,
līlā haya sadā supeśala
sei līlā aparūpa, ujjvala rasera kūpa,
tāhe jāra haya kautūhala

cidvilāsa⋅rasabhare, rati bhāva⋅daśā dhare,
mahābhāva paryanta bāḍaya
je jīva saubhāgyavān, līlā-yoge su-sandhāna,
vraje vasi’ satata karaya
IDX.en_translation:
Sandarśana, saṁsparśana, jalpa, vartmanirodhana, the rāsa, and the abundant pastimes of Vṛndāvana—water sports in the Yamunā, boat playing, stealing, pastimes on the river bank, and hide-and-seek in the groves.

Madhupāna, vadhūveṣa, feigned sleep, gambling, pulling at clothes, kissing and embracing, nail marks, and drinking the nectar of bimba-like lips—I consider these and other intimate pastimes.

All types of union and the great festival of enjoyment, the pastimes are always delightful. Those unique pastimes are a well of brilliant rasa; for one who has a keen interest in them,

filled with the mellow of spiritual enjoyment, rati attains the stage of bhāva and increases up to mahābhāva. That fortunate soul, through connection with these pastimes, constantly resides in Vraja and performs them.

IDX.first_line: saptadvipa dipta kari sobhe navadvipa puri
IDX.search: saptadvipa dipta kari sobhe navadvipa puri jahe visvambhara devaraja tahe tara bhakta jata tahate srivasa khyata sri krsna kirtana jara kaja jaya jaya thakura pandita jara krpalesa matra haiya gaura premapatra anupama sakala carita
IDX.author: premadāsa
IDX.verses:
saptadvīpa dīpta kari’, śobhe navadvīpa⋅purī,
jāhe viśvambhara devarāja
tāhe tā̐’ra bhakta jata, tāhāte śrīvāsa khyāta,
śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅kīrtana jā̐’ra kāja

jaya jaya ṭhākura paṇḍita
jā̐’ra kṛpā-leśa mātra, haiyā gaura⋅premapātra,
anupama sakala carita

gaurāṅgera sevā vine, deva⋅devī nāhi jāne,
cāri bhāi dāsa⋅dāsī laiyā
satata kīrtana⋅raṅge, gaura⋅gaurabhakta⋅saṅge,
aharniśi prema-matta haiyā

jā̐’ra bhāryā śrī-mālinī, pativratā⋅śiromaṇi,
jā̐’re prabhu kahaye jananī
nityānanda rahe ghare, putra⋅sama sneha kare,
stana jhare, netre vahe pānī

kabhu vā īśvara⋅jñāne, nati kare śrī-caraṇe,
kabhu kole karaye lālana
prabhura nṛtya-bhaṅga lāgi, mṛta putraśoka⋅tyāgī,
śuni’ prabhu karaye rodana

bhrātṛsutā nārāyaṇī, vaiṣṇava-maṇḍale dhvani,
jā̐’ra putra vṛndāvana⋅dāsa
varṇiyā caitanya-līlā, tribhuvana uddhārilā,
premadāsa kare jā̐’ra āśa
IDX.en_translation:
Navadvīpa-purī is resplendent, making the seven islands of the world shine. There, Viśvambhara reigns as the King of the Gods. Among all His devotees, Śrīvāsa Paṇḍita is famous, and his only work is the congregational chanting of the Holy Names of Lord Kṛṣṇa.

All glories, all glories to Śrīla Śrīvāsa Paṇḍita! By even a drop of his mercy, one becomes a recipient of Lord Gaura's love. All his deeds are incomparable.

Except for the service of Lord Gaurāṅga, he knows no other gods or goddesses. Together with his four brothers and his servants, he is constantly immersed in the joy of kīrtana. In the association of Lord Gaura and His devotees, he remains intoxicated with divine love day and night.

His wife is Śrīmati Mālinī-devī, the crest jewel of chaste wives, whom the Lord calls "Mother." Lord Nityānanda stays in their house and she shows Him affection just like a son. Milk flows from her breasts and tears flow from her eyes out of motherly love.

Sometimes, knowing Him to be the Supreme Lord, she bows at His lotus feet. At other times, she nurtures Him on her lap. For the sake of the Lord's ecstatic dancing, she even abandoned the grief for her dead son. Hearing of this, the Lord wept.

His brother's daughter is Nārāyaṇī, who is renowned throughout the Vaiṣṇava community. Her son is Śrīla Vṛndāvana Dāsa Ṭhākura. By describing the pastimes of Lord Caitanya, he delivered the three worlds. Premadāsa places his hope in such a personality.

IDX.first_line: saraccandra bhrantim sphuradamala kantim gajagatim
IDX.search: saraccandra bhrantim sphuradamala kantim gajagatim hari premonmattam dhrta parama sattvam smitamukham sada ghurnannetram kara kalita vetram kali bhidam bhaje nityanandam bhajana taru kandam niravadhi rasanamagaram svajanagana sarvasvamatulam tadiyaika pranapratima vasudha jahnava patim sada premonmadam parama viditam manda manasam bhaje nityanandam bhajana taru kandam niravadhi
IDX.author: śrīla vṛndāvana⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
śaraccandra⋅bhrāntiṁ sphuradamala⋅kāntiṁ gajagatiṁ
hari⋅premonmattaṁ dhṛta⋅parama⋅sattvaṁ smitamukham
sadā ghūrṇannetraṁ kara⋅kalita⋅vetraṁ kali⋅bhidaṁ
bhaje nityānandaṁ bhajana⋅taru⋅kandaṁ niravadhi

rasānāmāgāraṁ svajanagaṇa⋅sarvasvamatulaṁ
tadīyaika⋅prāṇapratima⋅vasudhā⋅jāhnavā⋅patim
sadā premonmādaṁ parama⋅viditaṁ manda⋅manasāṁ
bhaje nityānandaṁ bhajana⋅taru⋅kandaṁ niravadhi

śacī⋅sūnu⋅preṣṭhaṁ nikhila⋅jagadiṣṭaṁ sukhamayaṁ
kalau⋅majjaj⋅jīvoddharaṇa⋅karaṇoddāma⋅karuṇam
harerākhyānādvā bhava⋅jaladhi⋅garvonnati⋅haraṁ
bhaje nityānandaṁ bhajana⋅taru⋅kandaṁ niravadhi

aye bhrātarnṛṇāṁ kali⋅kaluṣiṇāṁ kiṁ nu bhavitā
tathā prāyaścittaṁ racaya jadanāyāsata ime
vrajanti tvāmitthaṁ saha bhagavatā mantrayati jo
bhaje nityānandaṁ bhajana⋅taru⋅kandaṁ niravadhi

jatheṣṭaṁ re bhrātaḥ! kuru hari⋅hari⋅dhvānamaniśaṁ
tato vaḥ saṁsārāmbudhi⋅taraṇa⋅dāyo mayi laget
idaṁ bāhu⋅sphoṭairaṭati raṭayan jaḥ pratigṛhaṁ
bhaje nityānandaṁ bhajana⋅taru⋅kandaṁ niravadhi

balāt saṁsārāmbhonidhi⋅haraṇa⋅kumbhodbhava⋅maho
satāṁ śreyaḥ⋅sindhūnnati⋅kumuda⋅bandhuṁ samuditam
khalaśreṇī⋅sphūrjat⋅timira⋅hara⋅sūrya⋅prabham⋅aham
bhaje nityānandaṁ bhajana⋅taru⋅kandaṁ niravadhi

naṭantaṁ gāyantaṁ harimanuvadantaṁ pathi pathi
vrajantaṁ paśyantaṁ svamapi nadayantaṁ janagaṇam
prakurvantaṁ santaṁ sakaruṇa⋅dṛgantaṁ prakalanād
bhaje nityānandaṁ bhajana⋅taru⋅kandaṁ niravadhi

suvibhrāṇaṁ bhrātuḥ kara⋅sarasijaṁ komalataraṁ
mitho vaktrālokocchalita⋅paramānanda⋅hṛdayam
bhramantaṁ mādhuryairahaha! madayantaṁ purajanān
bhaje nityānandaṁ bhajana⋅taru⋅kandaṁ niravadhi

rasānāmādhānaṁ rasika⋅vara⋅sadvaiṣṇavadhanaṁ
rasāgāraṁ sāraṁ patita⋅tati⋅tāraṁ smaraṇataḥ
paraṁ nityānandāṣṭakamidamapūrvaṁ paṭhati ja⋅
stadaṅghri⋅dvandvābjaṁ sphuratu nitarāṁ tasya hṛdaye
IDX.en_translation:
I constantly worship Nityānanda, the root of the tree of devotion. He has the shining, spotless luster of the autumn moon; His gait is like that of an elephant. He is maddened with love for Hari; He is the form of the supreme truth. His face is smiling. His eyes are always rolling. He holds a staff in His hand. He destroys the Kali age.

I constantly worship Nityānanda, the root of the tree of devotion. He is the reservoir of rasas. He is the incomparable all-in-all of His own people. He is the Lord of Vasudhā and Jāhnavā, who are like His one life-breath. He is always maddened by love; He is supremely known to the dull-minded.

I constantly worship Nityānanda, the root of the tree of devotion. He is most dear to the son of Śacī. He is desired by the whole universe. He is full of happiness. He has great mercy for delivering the jīvas drowning in the age of Kali. By the narration of Hari, He removes the swelling pride of the ocean of material existence.

I constantly worship Nityānanda, the root of the tree of devotion. He says, "O brother! What will happen to men polluted by Kali? Make such an atonement by which they can go to You effortlessly." Thus He counsels the Lord.

I constantly worship Nityānanda, the root of the tree of devotion. He goes to every house, shouting with raised arms: "O brother! Constantly make the sound of 'Hari, Hari' as much as you like! Then the responsibility for your crossing the ocean of material existence will fall upon Me."

I constantly worship Nityānanda, the root of the tree of devotion. He is the Agastya Muni who forcibly removes the ocean of material existence. He is the risen Moon, the friend of the lily of the increase of the ocean of welfare of the saintly. He is the sun-glare that destroys the dense darkness of the multitude of the wicked.

I constantly worship Nityānanda, the root of the tree of devotion. He dances, sings, and calls out "Hari!" on every path. He wanders, He looks; He makes the people roar. He makes them saintly; He casts merciful glances.

I constantly worship Nityānanda, the root of the tree of devotion. He holds the very soft lotus hand of His brother. His heart is surged with supreme bliss by looking at each other's faces. He wanders; ah! He intoxicates the townspeople with sweetness.

He who reads this unprecedented Nityānandāṣṭakam, which is the reservoir of rasas, the wealth of the best Rasikas and true Vaiṣṇavas, the storehouse of rasa, the essence, the deliverer of the multitude of the fallen—by remembrance (of Nityānanda), may the lotus of His two feet manifest constantly in his heart.

IDX.first_line: saranagatera prarthana
IDX.search: saranagatera prarthana tava dasyasukhaikasanginam bhavanesvastvapi kitajanma me itaravasathesu masmabhudapi janma caturmukhatmana yamunacarya kamadinam kati na katidha palita durnidesa stesam jata mayi na karuna na trapa nopasantih utsrjyaitanatha jadupate sampratam labdha buddhi stvatpadabjam saranamagamam sarva lokaika natham
IDX.verses:
śaraṇāgatera prārthanā
“tava dāsyasukhaikasaṅgināṁ
bhavaneṣvastvapi kīṭajanma me
itarāvasatheṣu māsmabhūdapi
janma caturmukhātmanā”
(yāmunācārya)

“kāmādīnāṁ kati na katidhā pālitā durnideśā⋅
steṣāṁ jātā mayi na karuṇā, na trapā nopaśāntiḥ
utsṛjyaitānatha jadupate sāmprataṁ labdha⋅buddhi⋅
stvatpādābjaṁ śaraṇamagamaṁ sarva⋅lokaika⋅nāthaṁ

naitanmanastava kathāsu vikuṇṭha⋅nātha
samprīyate duritaduṣṭamasādhu tīvram
kāmāturaṁ harṣaśokabhayaiṣaṇārtaḥ
tasmin kathaṁ tava gatiṁ vimṛṣāmi dīnaḥ’
(śrī.bhā. 7.9.39)

durita dūṣita mama asādhu mānasa
kāma⋅harṣa⋅śoka⋅bhaya⋅eṣaṇāra vaśa

tava kathā⋅rati kise hoive āmāra?
kise kṛṣṇa tava līlā kariva vicāra?

“jihvakato‛cyuta vikarṣati māvitṛptā
śiśno‛nyatastvagudaraṁ śravaṇaṁ kutaścit
ghrāṇo‛nyataścapaladṛk kva ca karmaśakti⋅
rvahvāḥ sapatnya iva gehapatiṁ lunanti”
(śrī.bhā. 7.9.40)

jihvā ṭāne rasa prati, upastha kadarthe
udara bhojane ṭāne, viṣama anarthe

carma ṭāne śayyādite, śravaṇa⋅kathāya
ghrāṇa ṭāne surabhite, cakṣu dṛśye jāya

karmendriya karme ṭāne, vahu patnī jathā
gṛhapati ākarṣaya, mora mana tathā

emata avasthā mora śrī⋅nanda⋅nandana
kirūpe tomāra līlā kariva smaraṇa?

“tadastu me nātha sa bhūribhāgo
bhave‛tra vānyatra tu vā tiraścām
jenāhamekoihapi bhavajjanānāṁ
bhūtvā niṣeve tava pāda⋅pallavam”
(śrī.bhā. 10.14.30)

ei brahma⋅janmei vā anya kona bhave
paśupakṣī ha’ye janmi tomāra vibhave

eimātra āśā, tava bhaktagaṇa⋅saṅge
thāki’ tava padasevā kari nānā raṅge

“ko nvīśa te pādasarojabhājāṁ
sudurlabho‛rtheṣu caturaṣvapīha
tathāpi nāhaṁ pravṛṇomi bhūman
bhavatpadāmbhojaniyevaṇotsukaḥ”
(śrī.bhā. 3.4.15)

kṛṣṇa! tava pāda⋅padme bhakti āche jā̐ra
caturvarga⋅madhye kivā aprāpya tā̐hāra

tathāpi tomāra padasevā mātra cāi
anya kona arthe mora prayojana nāi

“na kāmaye nātha tadapyahaṁ kvacinna
jatra juṣmaccaraṇāmvujāsavaḥ
mahattamāntarhṛdayānmukhacyuto
vidhatsva karṇāyutameṣa me varaḥ”
(śrī.bhā. 4.20.24)

jāhāte tomāra padasevā⋅sukha nāi
sei vara āmi nātha kabhu nāhi cāi

bhaktera hṛdaya haite tava guṇa⋅gāna
śunite ayuta karṇa karaha vidhāna

“na nākapṛṣṭhaṁ na ca pārameṣṭhyaṁ
na sārvabhaumaṁ na rasādhipatyam
na jogasiddhīrapunarbhavaṁ vā
samañjasa tvā virahayya kāṅkṣe”
(śrī.bhā. 6.11.25)

svarga, parameṣṭhī⋅sthāna, sārvabhauma pada
rasātala⋅ādhipatya, jogera sampad

nirvāṇa ityādi jata chāḍi’ sevā tava
nāhi māgi, e mora pratijñā akaitava

“ahaṁ hare tava pādaika⋅mūla⋅
dāsānudāso bhāvitāsmi bhūyaḥ
manaḥ smaretāsupatergaṇāṁste
gṛṇīta vāk karma karotu kāyaḥ”
(śrī.bhā. 6.11.24)

chinu tava nityadāsa, gale vā̐dhi’ māyā⋅pāśa,
saṁsāre pāinu nānā⋅kleśa

ebe punaḥ kari āśa, hañā tava dāsera dāsa,
bhaji’ pāi tava bhaktileśa

prāṇeśvara! tava guṇa, smaruka mana punaḥ punaḥ,
tava nāma jihvā karuka gāna
karadvaya tava karma, kariyā labhuka śarma,
tava pade sa̐pinu parāṇa
IDX.en_translation:
Prayer of the Surrendered Soul "May I be born even as a worm in the house of those who are attached only to the happiness of serving You, but let me not be born even as a Lord Brahmā in a house where You are not worshipped."

"I have obeyed the wicked commands of lust and other enemies in so many ways and for so long. They have no mercy, no shame, and give me no peace. O Lord of the Yadus! Abandoning them, and now having regained my intelligence, I have come to You for fearless shelter. Please engage me in Your personal service."

"O Lord of Vaikuṇṭha! My mind does not take pleasure in topics about You because it is polluted by sin, wicked, and very intense. It is afflicted by lust and tormented by happiness, distress, fear, and hankering. How can I, who am so wretched, think of Your path?"

My wicked mind is polluted by sins and controlled by lust, happiness, grief, fear, and hankering.

How will I develop attachment to topics about You? How, O Kṛṣṇa, will I contemplate Your pastimes?

"O Infallible One! My unsatisfied tongue drags me in one direction, my genitals in another, my stomach, ears, nose, and restless eyes in others, and my working senses elsewhere. These many co-wives tear the householder apart."

The tongue pulls towards tastes, the genitals towards bad purposes. The belly pulls towards eating, a terrible calamity.

The skin pulls towards soft beds, the ears towards talks. The nose pulls towards sweet scents, the eyes go towards sights.

The working senses pull towards work; just as many wives attract the master of the house, my mind is pulled like that.

This is my condition, O Son of Nanda. How shall I remember Your pastimes?

"O Lord, let me have that great fortune, whether in this birth or another, or even as an animal, that I may become one of Your devotees and serve Your tender feet."

Whether in this birth as Brahmā or in any other existence, even if I am born as a beast or bird in Your creation...

My only hope is this: that staying in the company of Your devotees, I may serve Your feet in various ways.

"O Controller, for those who worship Your lotus feet, what is difficult to obtain among the four goals of life? Still, O Great One, I do not desire them. I am eager only to serve Your lotus feet."

O Kṛṣṇa! For one who has devotion to Your lotus feet, what is unattainable among the four goals of life?

Still, I only want service to Your feet. I have no need for any other goal.

"O Lord, I do not desire that liberation where the nectar of Your lotus feet is not present. Let the words flowing from the mouths of great souls about Your glories enter my heart. Give me ten thousand ears for this; this is my boon."

O Lord, I never want that benediction in which there is no happiness of serving Your feet.

To hear the songs of Your qualities coming from the hearts of devotees, please arrange for me to have ten thousand ears.

"I do not desire heaven, the position of Brahmā, sovereignty over the earth, rulership of the lower planets, yogic perfections, or liberation, if it means being separated from You, O Source of all opportunities."

Heaven, the place of Brahmā, the position of emperor, lordship over the lower worlds, the opulence of yoga...

...liberation and so on—abandoning Your service, I do not beg for any of these. This is my sincere vow.

"O Hari, I will become the servant of the servant of Your lotus feet. May my mind remember the qualities of the Lord of my life, may my words chant Your glories, and may my body work for You."

I was Your eternal servant, but being bound by the noose of illusion around my neck, I suffered various miseries in this material world.

Now I hope again that becoming the servant of Your servant, and worshipping You, I may obtain a trace of Your devotion.

O Lord of my life! Let my mind remember Your qualities again and again. Let my tongue sing Your name. Let my two hands find happiness doing Your work. I have offered my soul at Your feet.

IDX.first_line: sata sajjana vandita pada yugam
IDX.search: sata sajjana vandita pada yugam juga dharma pracaraka dhurya janam janata su subhasana sakti dharam pranamami ca madhava deva padam atidirgha manohara gaura tanum mrdu manda suhasya yutasya dharam uru lambita hasta surupa yutam pranamami ca madhava deva padam
IDX.verses:
śata⋅sajjana⋅vandita pāda⋅yugaṁ,
juga⋅dharma pracāraka⋅dhurya⋅janaṁ
janatā⋅su⋅subhāṣaṇa⋅śakti⋅dharaṁ,
praṇamāmi ca mādhava⋅deva⋅padam

atidīrgha manohara gaura⋅tanuṁ,
mṛdu⋅manda⋅suhāsya⋅yutāsya⋅dharaṁ
uru⋅lambita⋅hasta⋅surūpa⋅yutaṁ,
praṇamāmi ca mādhava⋅deva⋅padam

śiśu⋅kāla⋅supāṭhya⋅suyatna⋅paraṁ,
jananī⋅savidhe⋅śruta⋅śāstra⋅mataṁ
paramārtha⋅kṛte parihīna⋅gṛhaṁ,
praṇamāmi ca mādhava⋅deva⋅padam

prabhupāda⋅pade‛rpita deha⋅matiṁ,
guru⋅kārya⋅kṛte jati⋅veśa⋅dharaṁ
praṇateṣu sadāhita⋅kāri⋅varaṁ,
praṇamāmi ca mādhava⋅deva⋅padam

prabhupāda⋅manomata kārya⋅rataṁ,
susamādṛta⋅bhaktivinoda⋅padaṁ
raghu⋅rūpa⋅sanātana⋅labdha pathaṁ,
praṇamāmi ca mādhava⋅deva⋅padam

taru⋅dhikkṛta⋅mārjana⋅śakti⋅dharaṁ,
laghu⋅sevana⋅mātraka⋅hṛṣṭa⋅hṛdaṁ
hari⋅kīrtana⋅santata⋅datta⋅matiṁ,
praṇamāmi ca mādhava⋅deva⋅padam

maṭha⋅mandira⋅nirmati kīrti⋅dharaṁ,
guru⋅gaura⋅kathāsu ca nitya⋅rataṁ
svayamācaraṇe para⋅dhairya⋅paraṁ,
praṇamāmi ca mādhava⋅deva⋅padam

karuṇārdrahṛdāhṛta viṣṇu⋅janaṁ,
jana⋅nandita vandita kṛtya⋅kulaṁ
nija⋅deśa⋅videśa suvandya⋅padaṁ,
praṇamāmi ca mādhava⋅deva⋅padam

guru⋅paŋkti⋅surakṣaṇa⋅yatna⋅paraṁ,
guru⋅sodara⋅gaurava⋅dāna⋅rataṁ
anurakta⋅susevaka⋅vākya⋅dharaṁ,
praṇamāmi ca mādhava⋅deva⋅padam

bhagavad⋅bhajanehyanurāga⋅paraṁ,
vrata⋅pālana⋅karma⋅sudārḍhya⋅yutaṁ
prabhupāda⋅padoddhṛta⋅kāri⋅janaṁ,
praṇamāmi ca mādhava⋅deva⋅padam

kṛpayā kṣamatāmaparādhi⋅janaṁ,
kaluṣāyuta⋅sakta⋅sudīna⋅naraṁ
supathe paricālaya sarva⋅dinaṁ,
praṇamāmi ca mādhava⋅deva⋅padam
IDX.en_translation:
I offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Śrīla Bhakti Dayita Mādhava Gosvāmī Mahārāja. His lotus feet are worshipped by hundreds of saintly persons. He is the foremost preacher of the religious practice for this age, and he possesses the power of eloquent speech.

I offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Śrīla Mādhava Mahārāja. His body is very tall, enchanting, and has a golden complexion. His face always holds a gentle and sweet smile, and his beautiful hands extend to his knees.

I offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Śrīla Mādhava Mahārāja. He was very diligent in his studies during childhood and heard the essence of the scriptures from his mother. He renounced his home for the sake of the highest spiritual truth.

I offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Śrīla Mādhava Mahārāja. His body and mind are dedicated to the lotus feet of Śrīla Prabhupāda. He assumed the dress of a sannyāsī for his Guru's mission and is the best benefactor to those who surrender.

I offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Śrīla Mādhava Mahārāja. He is engaged in the activities dear to Śrīla Prabhupāda's heart. He greatly respects the path of Śrīla Bhaktivinoda Ṭhākura and follows the path shown by the Gosvāmīs—Rūpa, Sanātana, and Raghunātha.

I offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Śrīla Mādhava Mahārāja. He has the power to cleanse the heart more than any other means. His heart is gladdened by even a small service, and his mind is constantly engaged in the congregational chanting of Lord Hari's names.

I offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Śrīla Mādhava Mahārāja. He established great fame by building temples and was always absorbed in topics concerning the Guru and Lord Gaura. He is supremely patient in his own conduct.

I offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Śrīla Mādhava Mahārāja. His compassionate heart attracts all the devotees. All his activities are worshipped by the people, and his feet are venerable in his own country and abroad.

I offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Śrīla Mādhava Mahārāja. He is diligent in protecting the lineage of spiritual masters and is always engaged in bestowing honor upon the Guru's associates. He values the words of his devoted servants.

I offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Śrīla Mādhava Mahārāja. He is intensely attached to the devotional service of the Supreme Lord and is firm in following his vows. He delivers many souls at the lotus feet of Śrīla Prabhupāda.

I offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Śrīla Mādhava Mahārāja. By his mercy, he forgives all offenders. He always guides the most fallen and sinful persons onto the right path.

IDX.first_line: satakoti gopi madhava mana
IDX.search: satakoti gopi madhava mana rakhite narila kari jatana venugite dake radhika nama esa esa radhe dakaye syama
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
śatakoṭī gopī mādhava⋅mana
rākhite nārila kari’ jatana

veṇugīte ḍāke rādhikā⋅nāma
‘esa esa rādhe!’ ḍākaye śyāma

bhāṅgiyā śrī⋅rāsa⋅ maṇḍala tabe
rādhā⋅anveṣaṇe calaye jabe

‘dekhā diyā rādhe! rākhaha prāṇa!’
baliyā kā̐daye kānane kāna

nirjana⋅kānane rādhāre dhari’
miliyā parāṇa juḍāya hari

bale, ‘tu̐hu vinā kāhāra rāsa ?
tu̐hu lāgi’ mora varaja vāsa’

ehena rādhikā⋅ caraṇa⋅tale
bhakativinoda kā̐diyā bale

“tuyā gaṇa⋅mājhe āmāre gaṇi’
kiṅkarī kariyā rākha āpani”
IDX.en_translation:
Hundreds of millions of Gopī, despite their efforts, could not hold Mādhava's heart.

With the song of the flute, Śyāma calls Rādhikā's name, "Come, come, Rādhe!"

Breaking the glorious rāsa dance circle, when He goes in search of Rādhā,

Saying, "Show Yourself, Rādhe! Save My life!", Kṛṣṇa cries in the forest.

In the secluded forest, finding Rādhā, Hari's heart becomes peaceful upon their union.

He says, "Without You, whose rāsa dance is it? For Your sake is My residence in Vraja."

At the feet of such a Rādhikā, Bhakativinoda says, crying:

"Please consider me among Your associates and keep me as Your maidservant."

IDX.first_line: satakoti gopi madhava manarakhite narila kari jatana
IDX.search: satakoti gopi madhava manarakhite narila kari jatana venugite dake radhika namaesa esa radhe dakaye syama
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
śatakoṭī gopī mādhava⋅manarākhite nārila kari’ jatana

veṇugīte ḍāke rādhikā⋅nāma‘esa esa rādhe!’ ḍākaye śyāma

bhāṅgiyā śrī⋅rāsa⋅ maṇḍala taberādhā⋅anveṣaṇe calaye jabe

‘dekhā diyā rādhe! rākhaha prāṇa!’baliyā kā̐daye kānane kāna

nirjana⋅kānane rādhāre dhari’miliyā parāṇa juḍāya hari

bale, ‘tu̐hu vinā kāhāra rāsa ?tu̐hu lāgi’ mora varaja vāsa’

ehena rādhikā⋅ caraṇa⋅talebhakativinoda kā̐diyā bale

“tuyā gaṇa⋅mājhe āmāre gaṇi’kiṅkarī kariyā rākha āpani”
IDX.en_translation:
Hundreds of millions of Gopī, despite their efforts, could not hold Mādhava’s heart.

With the song of the flute, Śyāma calls Rādhikā’s name, “Come, come, Rādhe!”

Breaking the glorious rāsa dance circle, when He goes in search of Rādhā,

Saying, “Show Yourself, Rādhe! Save My life!”, Kṛṣṇa cries in the forest.

In the secluded forest, finding Rādhā, Hari’s heart becomes peaceful upon their union.

He says, “Without You, whose rāsa dance is it? For Your sake is My residence in Vraja.”

At the feet of such a Rādhikā, Bhakativinoda says, crying:

“Please consider me among Your associates and keep me as Your maidservant”.

IDX.first_line: sattvagune adhisthita punyavan karmi
IDX.search: sattvagune adhisthita punyavan karmi haripriya jana bali gaya saba dharmi karmi hoite jnani hari priyatara jana sukhabhoga buddhi jnani na kare ganana
IDX.author: śrīla bhaktisiddhānta sarasvatī ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
sattvaguṇe adhiṣṭhita puṇyavān karmī
haripriya⋅jana bali’ gāya saba⋅dharmī

karmī hoite jñānī hari⋅priyatara jana
sukhabhoga⋅buddhi jñānī nā kare gaṇana

jñānamiśra bhāva chāḍi’ mukta jñānī jana
para⋅bhakti samāśraye haripriya ha’na

bhaktimān jana haite premaniṣṭha śreṣṭha
premaniṣṭha haite gopī śrī⋅harira preṣṭha

gopī haite śrī⋅rādhikā kṛṣṇa⋅priyatamā
se rādhā⋅sarasī priya haya tā̐’ra samā

se kuṇḍa⋅āśraya chāḍi’ kon mūḍha jana
anyatra basiyā cāya harira sevana
IDX.en_translation:
Virtuous workers situated in the mode of goodness are sung about by all as being dear to Lord Hari.

More dear to Lord Hari than such workers are the wise. The wise person does not value the intelligence required for enjoying material pleasure.

Abandoning the attitude mixed with speculative knowledge, the liberated wise person becomes dear to Lord Hari by taking shelter of pure devotion.

Superior even to such a devotee is one who is steadfast in divine love. More dear to Lord Hari than those steadfast in love are the gopīs.

More dear to Lord Kṛṣṇa than even the gopīs is Śrīmati Rādhā⋅rāṇī. Her lake, Rādhā-kuṇḍa, is just as dear to the Lord as She is.

Abandoning the shelter of that lake, what foolish person would desire service to Lord Hari while sitting elsewhere?

IDX.first_line: sayana mandire gaurange sundara
IDX.search: sayana mandire gaurange sundara uthila rajani sese mane drdha asa kariba sannyasa ghucaba e saba vese aichana bhaviya mandira tejiya aila suradhuni tire dui kara judi namaskara kari parasa karila nire
IDX.author: śrīla locana⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
śayana⋅mandire, gaurāṅge sundara,
uṭhilā rajanī⋅śeṣe
mane dṛḍha āśa, kariba sannyāsa,
ghucāba e saba veśe

aichana bhāviyā, mandira tejīyā,
āilā suradhunī⋅tīre
dui kara juḍi, namaskāra kari,
paraśa karilā nīre

gaṅgā parihari, navadvīpa chāḍi,
kāñcana nagara⋅pathe
karilā gamana, śuni sarvajana,
vajara paḍila māthe

pāṣāṇa samāna, hṛdaya kaṭhina,
seho śuni gali jāya
paśu⋅pākhī jhure, galaye pāthare,
e dāsa locana gāya
IDX.en_translation:
In His sleeping chamber, the beautiful Lord Gaurāṅga rose at the end of the night. In His mind, He had a firm desire: "I will accept the renounced order of life and give up all these worldly garments."

Thinking thus, He left His home and came to the bank of the River Ganges. Joining His two hands in prayer, He offered His obeisances and touched the water.

Leaving the Ganges and departing from His birthplace, Navadvīpa, He proceeded on the path to the town of Kāñcana-nagara. When the people heard this, it was as if a thunderbolt had fallen upon their heads.

Even hearts as hard as stone would melt upon hearing this news. Animals and birds weep and even stones melt—so sings this servant, Locana Dāsa.

IDX.first_line: sayaŋkale vanesvari diya nija sahacari
IDX.search: sayaŋkale vanesvari diya nija sahacari krsnarthe pathaya bhojya kata krsnabhukta avasesa sakhi ane jathadesa peye radha haya pulakita hetha vana haite hari vrajamukhe aise phiri dhenu ara sakhagana sane jatanete jasomati dasa dasi laye sati vyasta krsna sarira marjane
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
sāyaŋkāle vaneśvarī, diyā nija sahacarī,
kṛṣṇārthe pāṭhāya bhojya kata
kṛṣṇabhukta avaśeṣa, sakhī āne jathādeśa,
peye rādhā haya pulakita

hethā vana haite hari, vrajamukhe āise phiri,
dhenu āra sakhāgaṇa sane
jatanete jaśomati, dāsa⋅dāsī laye satī,
vyasta kṛṣṇa⋅śarīra⋅mārjane

ramyaveśa parāiyā , śrānti pūra karāiyā,
katamate karena lālana
kṛta⋅godohana hari, gṛhete praveśa kari’,
nānādravya karena bhojana

sakhāgaṇe saṅge kari’, bhojana kariyā hari,
pitāmātāya karena santoṣa
divasera kathā jata, ālocite nānāmata,
upasthita hayata pradoṣa
IDX.en_translation:
In the evening, the Mistress of the forest sends Her companion to bring many kinds of food for Lord Kṛṣṇa. The companion also brings back the remnants of the Lord's meal, and upon receiving them, Śrīmati Rādhā⋅rāṇī becomes thrilled.

Meanwhile, Lord Hari returns from the forest toward Vraja with the cows and His cowherd friends. With great care, Mother Yaśodā, along with her maidservants, becomes busy cleansing the Lord's body.

Dressing Him in beautiful clothes and relieving His fatigue, she cares for Him in various ways. Lord Kṛṣṇa, after milking the cows, enters the house and partakes of various foods.

Keeping His cowherd friends with Him, Lord Kṛṣṇa pleases His father and mother after eating. As they discuss all the day's events in various ways, the evening twilight arrives.

IDX.first_line: sidati sakhi mama hrdayamadhiram
IDX.search: sidati sakhi mama hrdayamadhiram jadabhajamiha na hi gokula viram nakarnamatisuhrdupadesam madhava catu patalamapi lesam
IDX.author: śrīla rūpa gosvāmī
IDX.verses:
sīdati sakhi mama hṛdayamadhīram
jadabhajamiha na hi gokula⋅vīram

nākarṇamatisuhṛdupadeśam
mādhava⋅cāṭu⋅paṭalamapi leśam

nālokayamarpitamuruhāram
praṇamantañca dayitamanuvāram

hanta sanātana⋅guṇamabhiyāntam
kimadhārayamahamurasi na kāntam
IDX.en_translation:
My unsteady heart languishes, O friend, because I did not worship the hero of Gokula here.

I did not listen to the advice of very dear friends, nor even a bit of the flattering words of Mādhava.

I did not behold the offered great garland, nor my beloved bowing down again and again.

Alas! Why did I not hold my beloved to my chest, who embodies eternal qualities?

nija⋅sevaka⋅vatsa⋅sudhenu⋅nibhaṁ
supathaḥ pari⋅rakṣaka⋅saṅgha⋅śiraṁ
nija⋅lekha⋅prapīḍita⋅dhūrta⋅janaṁ
bhaja keśava⋅komala⋅kānta⋅padaṁ

prabhupāda⋅dhanaṁ prabhupāda⋅janaṁ
prabhupāda⋅manoratha⋅pūrṇakaraṁ
prabhupāda⋅gatiṁ mama manda⋅manaḥ
bhaja keśava⋅komala⋅kānta⋅padaṁ
IDX.en_translation:
I worship the soft and beautiful lotus feet of Śrīla Keśava Gosvāmī Mahārāja. He is a Master deeply attached to the lotus feet of the Divine Couple, Śrī Śrī Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa. He is a Guru bathed in the nectarean mercy of Śrīla Raghunātha Dāsa Gosvāmī and Śrīla Rūpa Gosvāmī. His feet are served by millions of saintly persons.

I worship the soft and beautiful lotus feet of Śrīla Keśava Gosvāmī Mahārāja. His tender feet put the lotus petals to shame. His two lotus feet are worshipped by the poor and distressed. The splendor of His toenails destroys the sufferings of this material world.

I worship the soft and beautiful lotus feet of Śrīla Keśava Gosvāmī Mahārāja. He has long, graceful arms and is very gentle. His two eyes are filled with compassion. The beauty of His lovely body outshines the moon, the king of the night.

I worship the soft and beautiful lotus feet of Śrīla Keśava Gosvāmī Mahārāja. He is solely devoted to the service of His Guru. He is a confidential Guru who dedicated His entire life to His Master. He is known as the beloved associate of the daughter of Śrī Vṛṣabhānu.

I worship the soft and beautiful lotus feet of Śrīla Keśava Gosvāmī Mahārāja. He is the most expert vessel of the conclusions of Vedānta. His words explain the science of devotion to Lord Hari. He is non-different from the great teacher Śrīla Baladeva Vidyābhūṣaṇa.

I worship the soft and beautiful lotus feet of Śrīla Keśava Gosvāmī Mahārāja. He holds the sword of scriptural evidence that refutes the false conception of the world as being separate from the Supreme Truth. He is the knower of the nectarean essence of all the Vedas.

I worship the soft and beautiful lotus feet of Śrīla Keśava Gosvāmī Mahārāja. He is the best disciple who propagated the practice of circumambulating the holy places (dhāma-parikrama) established by Śrīla Prabhupāda. He is dear to Lord Kṛṣṇa and is embraced by the Queen of Rasikas, Śrīmati Rādhā⋅rāṇī.

I worship the soft and beautiful lotus feet of Śrīla Keśava Gosvāmī Mahārāja. He wears saffron garments and is devoted to the Holy Name. His heart is always immersed in the mood of Vraja. He is like a powerful lion against the mad elephant of false pride.

I worship the soft and beautiful lotus feet of Śrīla Keśava Gosvāmī Mahārāja. He is like a mother cow to His servants, who are like His calves. He is the leader of the community that protects the path of truth. He torments the deceitful with His powerful writings.

I worship the soft and beautiful lotus feet of Śrīla Keśava Gosvāmī Mahārāja. He is the wealth and the very life of Śrīla Prabhupāda. He fulfills all the desires of His Master. He is the ultimate destination of Śrīla Prabhupāda. O my foolish mind, please worship His lotus feet!

IDX.search: smaratu mano mama niravadhi radham madhu pati rupa guna sravanodita sahaja manobhava vadham varasimanta rasamrta sarani dhrta sindura surekham sri vrsabhanu kulambudhi sambhava subhaga sudhakara lekham
IDX.author: śrīla prabodhānanda sarasvatīpāda
IDX.verses:
smaratu mano mama niravadhi rādhām
madhu⋅pati⋅rūpa⋅guṇa⋅śravaṇodita⋅
sahaja⋅manobhava⋅vādhām

varasīmanta⋅rasāmṛta⋅saraṇī⋅
dhṛta⋅sindūra⋅surekhām
śrī⋅vṛṣabhānu⋅kulāmbudhi⋅sambhava⋅
subhaga⋅sudhākara⋅lekhām

surucira⋅kavarī⋅virājita⋅komala⋅
parimala⋅malli⋅sumālām

mada⋅kavirāja⋅virājadanuttama⋅
malina⋅lalitagati⋅bhaṅgīm
atisukumāra⋅kanaka⋅navacampaka⋅
gaura⋅madhura⋅madhurāṅgīm

maṇi⋅keyūra⋅lalita⋅valayāvali⋅
maṇḍita⋅mṛdubhujavallīm
pratipadama adbhuta⋅rūpa⋅camatkṛti⋅
mohana⋅yuvati⋅matallīm

mṛdu⋅mṛduhāsa⋅lalita⋅mukha⋅maṇḍala⋅
kṛtaśaśi⋅bimba⋅biḍambām
kiṅkiṇi⋅jāla⋅khacita⋅pṛthusundara⋅
nava⋅rasa⋅rāśi⋅nitambām

citrita⋅kañculikā⋅sthagitodbhaṭa⋅

sakanaka⋅ratna⋅khacita⋅pṛthumauktika⋅

ujjvala⋅rāga⋅rasāmṛta⋅sāgara⋅
sāratanuṁ⋅sukharūpām
nipatati⋅mādhava⋅mugdhamano⋅mṛga⋅
nābhi⋅sudhārasa⋅kūpām

nūpura⋅hāra⋅manohara⋅kuṇḍala⋅
