pathi pathi madana⋅madākula⋅gokula⋅
candrakalita⋅padamūlām

rasika⋅sarasvatī⋅gīti⋅mahādbhuta⋅
rādhā⋅rūpa⋅rahasyam
vṛndāvana⋅rasa⋅lālasa⋅manasā⋅
midamupageyamavaśyam
IDX.en_translation:
Let my mind constantly remember Rādhā. Her beauty and qualities, heard of by the Lord of Madhu (Kṛṣṇa), are a natural obstacle to Cupid.

She wears a beautiful line of vermillion on the nectar-path of the rasa of Her excellent parting. She is a streak of the beautiful moon born from the ocean of the family of Śrī Vṛṣabhānu.

She is adorned with a soft, fragrant garland of jasmine flowers on Her beautiful braid. She defeats the play of the intoxicated, moving wagtail birds with Her large lotus eyes.

She shames the excellent, graceful gait of the king of elephants intoxicated with rut. She has a very delicate, golden, fresh Campaka-like, fair, sweet, sweet body.

Her soft creeper-like arms are adorned with jeweled armlets and beautiful rows of bracelets. At every step, She is a wonder of wonderful beauty, the best of enchanting young women.

She mocks the orb of the moon with Her soft, gentle smile and beautiful face. Her large, beautiful hips are inlaid with a net of small bells and are a heap of new rasa.

The beauty of Her golden pot-like breasts is concealed by a colorful bodice. She creates greed in the mind of Hari with the nectar-wine of Her quivering red lips.

She is adorned with a beautiful chin, enchanting with the beauty of a black dot (musk). Her nose is beautiful and radiant, inlaid with gold, gems, and a large pearl.

She is the ocean of the nectar of blazing passion (love), the essence-body, the form of happiness. She is the well of the nectar of musk for the deer of the bewildered mind of Mādhava, who falls into it.

She has made the red silk cloth beautiful with anklets, necklaces, and enchanting earrings. Her lotus feet are worshipped by the moon-like people of Gokula, who are agitated by Cupid on every path.

This song, the great secret of the form of Rādhā, is very wonderful, by Rasika Sarasvatī. It must be sung by one whose mind is eager for the rasa of Vṛndāvana.

IDX.first_line: snata vibhusita sati grhe rai rasavati
IDX.search: snata vibhusita sati grhe rai rasavati sakhisaha kare alapana jasoda prerita bala kundalata sukomala jatilare janaya takhana ogo jatile suna jasodara vijnapana sri radhaya tathaya loiba karibena pakakarya ekatha hoila dharya pakante vadhuke ani diba
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
snātā vibhūṣitā satī, gṛhe rāi rasavatī,
sakhīsaha kare ālāpana
jaśodā⋅preritā bālā, kundalatā sukomalā,
jaṭilāre jānāya takhana

‘ogo jaṭile, śuna, jaśodāra vijñāpana,
śrī⋅rādhāya tathāya loiba
karibena pākakārya, ekathā hoila dhārya,
pākānte vadhūke āni diba’

jadio kṛṣṇere bhaya, tathāpi sammata haya,
śuniyā jaśodā⋅vijñāpana
sakhīsaha rādhā jāya, praṇamiyā jaśodāya,
pākakārya kare sampādana

kṛṣṇa nidrā parihari’, goṣṭhe godohana kari’,
sakhāsaha snānānte bhojana
kṛṣṇabhukta śeṣa jāhā, sakhīgaṇa saṅge tāhā,
rādhikā karena āsvādana
IDX.en_translation:
Bathed and adorned, the charming Rāi converses with her gopī friends in her house. The gentle girl Kundalatā, sent by Yaśodā, then informs Jaṭilā.

"O Jaṭilā! Listen to Yaśodā's request. I will take Śrī Rādhā there; it has been decided that She will cook. After She finishes, I will bring your daughter-in-law back."

Although she fears Lord Kṛṣṇa, Jaṭilā agrees after hearing Yaśodā's request. Rādhā, with her gopī friends, goes and bows down to Mother Yaśodā, then performs the cooking.

Lord Kṛṣṇa gives up His sleep and milks the cows in the pasture. After bathing, He eats with His cowherd friends. Whatever remnants the Lord leaves, Śrīmati Rādhikā and Her friends relish with great joy.

IDX.first_line: sraddhadevi nama jara duiti svabhava tara
IDX.search: sraddhadevi nama jara duiti svabhava tara vidhimula rucimula bhede sastrera sasane jabe sraddhara udaya habe vaidhi sraddha tare bale vede vrajavasi seve krsne sei suddhaseva drste jabe haya sraddhara udaya lobhamayi sraddha sati raganuga suddha mati bahu bhagye sadhaka labhaya
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
‘śraddhādevī’ nāma jā’ra, duiṭi svabhāva tā’ra,
vidhimūla⋅rucimūla⋅bhede
śāstrera śāsane jabe, śraddhāra udaya ha’be,
vaidhī śraddhā tā’re bale vede

vrajavāsī seve kṛṣṇe, sei śuddhasevā⋅dṛṣṭe,
jabe haya śraddhāra udaya
lobhamayī śraddhā satī, rāgānugā śuddha mati,
bahu bhāgye sādhaka labhaya

śraddhābheda⋅bhaktibheda, gāiteche caturveda,
‘vaidhī’ ‘rāgānugā’ bhaktidvaya
sādhana⋅samaye jaiche, siddhi-kāle prāpti taiche,
eirūpa bhakti-śāstre kaya

vaidhī bhakti dhīragati, rāgānugā tīvra ati,
atiśīghra rasāvasthā pāya
rāgavartma⋅susādhane, ruci haya jā’ra mane,
rūpānuga haite sei dhāya
IDX.en_translation:
Goddess Śraddhā has two natures, differing by being regulated or having spontaneous attraction. When faith arises under the direction of the scriptures, the Vedas call that regulated faith.

When, by observing the pure service of the residents of Vraja to Kṛṣṇa, faith awakens, that chaste, greedy faith is pure devotion following spontaneous attachment. A practitioner obtains this by great fortune.

The four Vedas sing about the differences in faith and devotion - the two types of devotion being regulated and spontaneous. What is practiced during the time of spiritual discipline is attained in the state of perfection; thus say the scriptures of devotion.

Regulated devotion is slow-moving, while spontaneous devotion is very swift and quickly attains the state of spiritual ecstasy. One in whose mind a taste for the excellent practice on the path of spontaneous attachment arises, rushes to become a follower of Rūpa.

IDX.first_line: sri gaudadese suradirghikaya
IDX.search: sri gaudadese suradirghikaya stireti ramye pura punya mayyah lasantamananda bharena nityam tam sri navadvipamaham smarami jasmai paravyoma vadanti kecit kecic ca goloka itirayanti vadanti vrndavanameva taj jnas tam sri navadvipamaham smarami
IDX.author: śrīla rūpa gosvāmī
IDX.verses:
śrī⋅gauḍadeśe suradīrghikāyā⋅
stīre‛ti⋅ramye pura⋅puṇya⋅mayyāḥ
lasantamānanda⋅bhareṇa nityaṁ
taṁ śrī⋅navadvīpamahaṁ smarāmi

jasmai paravyoma vadanti kecit
kecic ca goloka itīrayanti
vadanti vṛndāvanameva taj⋅jñās
taṁ śrī⋅navadvīpamahaṁ smarāmi

jaḥ sarva-dikṣu sphuritaiḥ suśītai⋅
rnānā⋅drumaiḥ sūpavanaiḥ parītaḥ
śrī⋅gauramadhyāhnavihāra pātrai⋅
staṁ śrī⋅navadvīpamahaṁ smarāmi

śrī⋅svarṇadī jatra vihāra⋅bhūmiḥ
suvarṇa⋅sopāna⋅nibaddha⋅tīrā
vyāptormibhirgaura⋅vagāha⋅rūpai⋅
staṁ śrī⋅navadvīpamahaṁ smarāmi

mahāntyanantāṇi gṛhāṇi jatra
sphuranti haimāni manoharāṇi
pratyālayaṁ jaṁ śrayate sadā śrī⋅
staṁ śrī⋅navadvīpamahaṁ smarāmi

vidyā⋅dayā⋅kṣānti⋅makhaiḥ samastaiḥ
sadbhirguṇairyatra janāḥ prapannāḥ
saṁstūyamānā ṛṣi⋅deva⋅siddhai⋅
staṁ śrī⋅navadvīpamahaṁ smarāmi

jasyāntare miśra⋅purandarasya
svānanda⋅gamyaika⋅padaṁ nivāsaḥ
śrī⋅gaurajanmādika⋅līlayāḍhya⋅
staṁ śrī⋅navadvīpamahaṁ smarāmi

gauro bhramanyatra hariḥ svabhaktaiḥ
saṅkīrtana⋅prema⋅bhareṇa sarvam
nimajjayatyullasadunmadāvdhau
taṁ śrī⋅navadvīpamahaṁ smarāmi

etannavadvīpa⋅vicintyanāḍhyaṁ
padyāṣṭakaṁ prīta⋅manāḥ paṭhed jaḥ
śrīmacchacīnandana⋅pāda⋅padme
sudurlabhaṁ prema samāpnuyātsaḥ
IDX.en_translation:
I remember that Śrī Navadvīpa, which is always shining with great bliss on the very beautiful bank of the celestial river Ganges in the sacred land of Gauḍa.

I remember that Śrī Navadvīpa, which some call the supreme spiritual sky, some say is Goloka, and the knowers of truth declare to be Vṛndāvana itself.

I remember that Śrī Navadvīpa, which is surrounded in all directions by cool, beautiful groves of various trees, suitable for Śrī Gaura’s midday pastimes.

I remember that Śrī Navadvīpa, where the sacred celestial river is the place of His pastimes, its banks adorned with golden steps, and its waves filled with forms of Gaura immersing Himself.

I remember that Śrī Navadvīpa, where countless grand and enchanting golden houses shine, and where the Goddess of Fortune always resides in every home.

I remember that Śrī Navadvīpa, where people are endowed with all good qualities like knowledge, compassion, and forgiveness, and are praised by sages, gods, and perfected beings.

I remember that Śrī Navadvīpa, within which is the residence of Miśra Purandara, a unique place attainable by one's own bliss, and which is enriched by the pastimes of Śrī Gaura, beginning with His birth.

I remember that Śrī Navadvīpa, where Gaura, Hari, wandering with His devotees, immerses everyone in an ocean of joyful madness with the full force of love for congregational chanting.

Whoever, with a pleased mind, recites these eight verses, which are enriched with meditation on Śrī Navadvīpa, will attain extremely rare divine love for the lotus feet of Śrīmān Śacī⋅nandana.

IDX.first_line: sri gaura dhamasrita suddhabhaktam
IDX.search: sri gaura dhamasrita suddhabhaktam rupanugadyam niravadyarupam vairagya dharmojjvala vigraham tam vande prabhum gaurakisorasamjnam asat prasangam parihaya nityam gauranga seva vrata magnacittam gauda vrajabheda visista prajnam vande prabhum gaura kisora samjnam
IDX.author: śrīmadbhaktideśika ācārya mahārāja
IDX.verses:
śrī⋅gaura⋅dhāmāśrita⋅śuddhabhaktaṁ
rūpānugādyaṁ niravadyarūpam
vairāgya⋅dharmojjvala⋅vigrahaṁ taṁ
vande prabhuṁ gaura-kiśora-saṁjñam

asat⋅prasaṅgaṁ parihāya nityaṁ
gaurāṅga⋅sevā⋅vrata⋅magnacittam
gauḍa⋅vrajābheda⋅viśiṣṭa⋅prajñaṁ
vande prabhuṁ gaura⋅kiśora⋅saṁjñam

śrīdhāma⋅māyāpura⋅divya⋅gūḍha⋅
māhātmya⋅gītonmukharaṁ vareṇyam
dhanyaṁ mahā-bhāgavatāgragaṇyaṁ
vande prabhuṁ gaura⋅kiśora⋅saṁjñam

pūtāvadhūta⋅vraja⋅śīrṣaratnaṁ
śrī⋅rādhikā⋅kṛṣṇa⋅nigūḍha⋅bhaktam
sadā vrajāveśa⋅sarāga⋅ceṣṭaṁ
vande prabhuṁ gaura⋅kiśora⋅saṁjñam

śokāspadātīta⋅prabhāva⋅ramyaṁ
mūḍhairavedyaṁ praṇatābhigamyam
nityānubhūtācyuta⋅sadvilāsaṁ
vande prabhuṁ gaura⋅kiśora⋅saṁjñam

kāpaṭya⋅dharmānvita⋅caṇḍa⋅daṇḍa⋅
vidhāyakaṁ sajjana⋅saṅga⋅raṅgam
śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅caitanya⋅padābja⋅bhṛṅgaṁ
vande prabhuṁ gaura⋅kiśora⋅saṁjñam

dāmodarotthāna⋅dine pradhāne
kṣetre pavitre kuliyābhidhāne
prapañca⋅līlā⋅parihāravantaṁ
vande prabhuṁ gaura⋅kiśora⋅saṁjñam

tava hi ‘dayita⋅dāse’ satya⋅sūrya⋅prakāśe
jagati durita⋅nāśe prodyate cidvilāse
vayama-nugata-bhṛtyāḥ pāda-padmaṁ prapannā
anudinamanukampāṁ prārthayāmo nagaṇyāḥ
IDX.en_translation:
I worship Śrīla Gaurakiśora dāsa Bābājī Mahārāja, the pure devotee sheltered in Śrī Gauradhāma. He is the foremost follower of Śrīla Rūpa Gosvāmī, his form is faultless, and he shines with the brilliance of intense renunciation.

I worship Śrīla Gaurakiśora dāsa Bābājī Mahārāja, who always rejects bad association and whose mind is immersed in the vow of serving Lord Gaurāṅga. He possesses the unique wisdom of seeing the non-difference between Gauḍa and Vraja.

I worship Śrīla Gaurakiśora dāsa Bābājī Mahārāja. He eloquently sings the divine, hidden glories of Śrīdhāma Māyāpura. He is the worshipable, blessed, and foremost among the great Mahā-bhāgavatas.

I worship Śrīla Gaurakiśora dāsa Bābājī Mahārāja, the crest jewel of the pure avadhūtas. He is a confidential devotee of Śrī Śrī Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa, always absorbed in the mood of Vraja with deep attachment.

I worship Śrīla Gaurakiśora dāsa Bābājī Mahārāja. He is charming, residing in a realm beyond lamentation. Unknowable to fools but approachable by the humble, he eternally experiences the divine pastimes of the Infallible Lord.

I worship Śrīla Gaurakiśora dāsa Bābājī Mahārāja. He wields a fierce staff to punish deceitful dharma, yet he delights in the association of the saintly. He is a honeybee at the lotus feet of Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya.

I worship Śrīla Gaurakiśora dāsa Bābājī Mahārāja, who concluded his earthly pastimes on the holy day of Utthāna Ekādaśī in the sacred place of Kuliyā.

O Dayita Dāsa! You are the shining sun of truth, destroying sins in this world and revealing spiritual pastimes. We, your insignificant servants, have surrendered to your lotus feet and pray for your causeless mercy day after day.

IDX.first_line: sri guru carana padma kevala bhakati sadma
IDX.search: sri guru carana padma kevala bhakati sadma vando muni savadhana mate jahara prasade bhai e bhava tariya jai krsna prapti haya jaha hate guru mukhapadma vakya cittete kariya aikya ara na kariha mane asa sri guru carane rati ei sei uttama gati je prasade pure sarva asa
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
śrī⋅guru⋅caraṇa⋅padma, kevala bhakati⋅sadma,
vando muñi sāvadhāna⋅mate
jā̐hāra prasāde bhāi, e bhava tariyā jāi,
kṛṣṇa⋅prāpti haya jā̐hā ha’te

guru⋅mukhapadma⋅vākya, cittete kariyā aikya,
āra nā kariha mane āśā
śrī⋅guru⋅caraṇe rati, ei sei uttamā gati,
je⋅prasāde pūre sarva āśā

cakṣu⋅dāna dilā jei, janme janme prabhu sei,
divyajñāna hṛde prakāśita
prema⋅bhakti jā̐hā haite, avidyā vināśa jāte,
vede gāya jā̐hāra carita

śrī⋅guru karuṇā⋅sindhu, adhama janāra bandhu,
‘lokanātha’ lokera jīvana
hā hā prabho! kara dayā, deha’ more pada⋅chāyā,
tuyā pade loinu śaraṇa
IDX.en_translation:
The lotus feet of the spiritual master are the sole abode of devotion; I worship them with great care. By his mercy, O brother, we cross over this material existence, and the attainment of Kṛṣṇa occurs through him.

Making the words from the lotus mouth of the spiritual master one with my heart, I have no other desire. Love for the feet of the spiritual master is the supreme destination; by his mercy, all desires are fulfilled.

He who has given the gift of spiritual vision is the master in every birth; divine knowledge is revealed in the heart. From him loving devotion comes, by which ignorance is destroyed; the Vedas sing his glories.

The spiritual master is an ocean of mercy, a friend of the fallen souls; he is the master and the life of all people. O Lord! Be merciful, give me the shelter of your feet; I take refuge at your feet.

IDX.first_line: sri guru sri gaura candra vrndavane juvadvandva
IDX.search: sri guru sri gaura candra vrndavane juvadvandva vrajavasijana sri carana vandiya praphullamane e bhaktivinoda bhane rupanuga bhajana darpana
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
śrī⋅guru⋅śrī⋅gaura⋅candra, vṛndāvane juvadvandva,
vrajavāsijana⋅śrī⋅caraṇa
vandiyā praphullamane, e bhaktivinoda bhaṇe,
rūpānuga⋅bhajana⋅darpaṇa
IDX.en_translation: Śrī Guru, Śrī Gaura⋅candra, the divine youthful couple in Vṛndāvana, and the holy feet of the residents of Vraja- Having offered obeisances with a joyful heart, this Bhaktivinoda says: This is a mirror of the devotional service of the followers of Rūpa Gosvāmī.

IDX.first_line: sri guru vaisnava krpa kata dine habe
IDX.search: sri guru vaisnava krpa kata dine habe upadhi rahita rati citte upajibe kabe siddhadeha mora hoibe prakasa sakhi dekhaibe more jugala vilasa
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
śrī⋅guru⋅vaiṣṇava⋅kṛpā kata dine ha’be
upādhi⋅rahita⋅rati citte upajibe

kabe siddhadeha mora hoibe prakāśa
sakhī dekhāibe more jugala⋅vilāsa

dekhite dekhite rūpa, hoiba vātula
kadamba⋅kānane jā’ra tyaji’ jāti⋅kula

‘sveda’ ‘kampa’ ‘pulakāśru’ ‘vaivarṇya’ ‘pralaya’
‘stambha’ ‘svarabheda’ kabe hoibe udaya

bhāvamaya vṛndāvana heriba nayane
sakhīra kiṅkarī ha’ye seviba du’jane

kabe narottama⋅saha sākṣāt hoibe
kabe vā ‘prārthanā’⋅rasa citte praveśibe

caitanyadāsera dāsa chāḍi’ anya rati
karayuḍi’ māge āja śrī⋅caitanya mati
IDX.en_translation:
When will the mercy of my spiritual master and the devotees come? When will unalloyed love arise in my heart?

When will my perfected spiritual body manifest? When will a companion show me the divine pastimes of the Divine Couple?

Gazing upon their forms, I will become mad with love; in the kadamba forest, I will abandon all social conventions and family ties.

When will perspiration, trembling, thrills of ecstasy, tears, and fainting—along with stupefaction and a choking voice—manifest in me?

With my own eyes, I will behold the spiritual Vṛndāvana; becoming a maidservant of a companion, I will serve the Divine Couple.

When will I meet Narottama dāsa Ṭhākura? And when will the essence of his prayers enter my heart?

Abandoning all other attachments and becoming a servant of the servant of Caitanya, today, with folded hands, I beg for the spirit of Śrī Caitanya.

IDX.first_line: sri guru vaisnava tomara carana
IDX.search: sri guru vaisnava tomara carana smarana na kainu ami visaya visama visa bhala mani khaichu hoiya kami sei vise more jariya marila badoi visama haila janame janame emana katoi atmaghati papa kaila
IDX.author: rādhāmohana dāsa
IDX.verses:
śrī⋅guru⋅vaiṣṇava, tomāra caraṇa,
smaraṇa nā kainu āmi
viṣaya viṣama, viṣa bhāla māni,
khāichu hoiyā kāmī

sei viṣe more, jāriyā mārila,
baḍoi viṣama haila
janame janame, emana katoi,
ātmaghātī pāpa kaila

sei aparādhe, e bhava⋅saṁsāre,
bā̐dhile e māyājāle
tomā’ nā bhajiyā, āpanā khāiyā,
āpani ḍubechi hele

āra kata kāla, e duḥkha bhuñjiba,
bhoga⋅deha nāhi jāya
sahite nāriyā, kātara hoiyā,
nivedichi tuyā pāya

o rāṅgā caraṇa, paraśa kevala,
vicāriyā ei dāya
uddhāra kariyā, laha dīna-bandhu,
āpana caraṇa⋅nāya

tomāra sevana⋅, amṛta bhojana,
karāiyā more rākha
e rādhāmohana, khate vikāila,
dāma gagane lekha
IDX.en_translation:
O Śrī Guru and Vaiṣṇava, I did not remember your feet. I considered the dangerous poison of sense objects as good, and being lusty, I consumed it.

That poison burned me and killed me; it became very severe. In birth after birth, how much such self-killing sin have I committed!

For that offense, in this material world, I was bound in this net of illusion. Not worshipping you, devouring myself, I have drowned myself with ease.

How much longer shall I suffer this misery? The body for enjoyment does not go away. Unable to tolerate, being distressed, I am submitting at your feet.

Only the touch of those reddish feet—considering this crisis, delivering me, take me, O Friend of the Poor, on the boat of your feet.

Your service is eating nectar; make me do it and keep me. This Rādhāmohana is sold by a written contract; write the price in the sky.

IDX.first_line: sri jagannatha niladri siromukuta ratna he
IDX.search: sri jagannatha niladri siromukuta ratna he darubrahman ghanasyama prasida purusottama praphulla pundarikaksa lavanavdhi tatamrta gutikodara mam pahi nanabhoga purandara
IDX.author: śrīla sanātana gosvāmī
IDX.verses:
śrī⋅jagannātha nīlādri śiromukuṭa⋅ratna he
dārubrahman ghanaśyāma prasīda puruṣottama

praphulla⋅puṇḍarīkākṣa lavaṇāvdhi taṭāmṛta
guṭikodara māṁ pāhi nānābhoga⋅purandara

nijādhara⋅sudhādāyinnindradyumna⋅prasādita
subhadrā⋅pālana⋅vyagra rāmānuja namo‛stu te

guṇḍicā⋅rathayātrādi⋅mahotsava⋅vivardhana
bhaktavatsala vande tvāṁ guṇḍicārathamaṇḍanam

dīnahīna⋅mahānīca⋅dayārdrīkṛta⋅mānasa
nitya⋅nūtana⋅māhātmyadarśin caitanyavallabha
IDX.en_translation:
O Śrī Jagannātha, jewel on the crown of the Blue Mountain! O wooden Brahman, dark-blue like a cloud, O Supreme Person, please be kind to me.

O You whose eyes are like fully bloomed lotus flowers, O nectar on the seashore! O pot-bellied One, please protect me, O provider of various enjoyments.

O Giver of the nectar of Your own lips, You who were pleased by King Indradyumna! O You who are eager to protect Subhadrā, O younger brother of Balarāma, salutations to You.

O Enhancer of great festivals like the Guṇḍicā chariot festival! O You who are affectionate to Your devotees, I bow to You, the adornment of the Guṇḍicā chariot.

O You whose mind is softened with compassion for the poor and the most fallen! O Revealer of ever-new glories, O beloved of Lord Caitanya!

IDX.first_line: sri krsna brahma devarsi vadarayana samjnakan
IDX.search: sri krsna brahma devarsi vadarayana samjnakan sri madhva sri padmanabha sriman nrhari madhavan aksobhya jayatirtha sri jnana sindhu dayanidhin sri vidyanidhi rajendra jayadharman kramad vayam
IDX.author: śrīla⋅kabikarṇapūrānumoditā, śrīla⋅gopāla⋅bhaṭṭa⋅gosvāminā, śrīla⋅baladeva⋅vidyā⋅bhūṣaṇena coddhṛtā
IDX.verses:
śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅brahma⋅devarṣi⋅vādarāyaṇa⋅saṁjñakān
śrī⋅madhva⋅śrī⋅padmanābha⋅śrīman⋅nṛhari⋅mādhavān

akṣobhya⋅jayatīrtha⋅śrī⋅jñāna⋅sindhu⋅dayānidhīn
śrī⋅vidyānidhi⋅rājendra⋅jayadharmān⋅kramād⋅vayam

puruṣottama⋅brahmaṇya⋅vyāsatīrthāṁś⋅ca saṁstumaḥ
tato lakṣmī⋅patiṁ⋅śrīman⋅mādhavendrañca bhaktitaḥ

tacchinyān śrī⋅śvarādvaita⋅nityānandān jagad⋅gurūn
devamīśvara⋅śiṣyaṁ śrī⋅caitanyañca bhajāmahe

śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅prema⋅dānena jena nistāritaṁ jagat
kali⋅kaluṣa⋅santaptaṁ karuṇā⋅sindhunā svayam

mahā⋅prabhu⋅svarūpa⋅śrīdāmodaraḥ priyaṅkaraḥ,<sup>1</sup>
rūpa⋅sanātanau dvau ca gosvāmi⋅pravarau prabhū
<sup>1</sup><sub><eng>hereafter, jagad⋅guru⋅śrīla⋅bhakti⋅siddhānta⋅sarasvatī⋅ṭhakura is the author</eng></sub>

śrī⋅jīvo raghunāthaśca rūpa⋅priyo mahāmatiḥ
tat⋅priyaḥ kavirājaḥ śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa⋅prabhurmataḥ

tasya priyottamaḥ śrīla sevā⋅paro narottamaḥ
tadanugata⋅bhaktaḥ śrī⋅viśvanāthaḥ saduttamaḥ

tadāsaktaśca gauḍīya⋅vedāntācārya⋅bhūṣaṇam
vidyā⋅bhūṣaṇa⋅pāda⋅śrī⋅baladevaḥ sadāśrayaḥ

vaiṣṇava⋅sārvabhaumaḥ śrī⋅jagannātha⋅prabhustathā
śrī⋅māyāpura⋅dhāmnastu nirdeṣṭā sajjana⋅priyaḥ

śuddha⋅bhakti⋅pracārasya mūlībhūta ihottamaḥ
śrī⋅bhaktivinodo devastat⋅priyatvena viśrutaḥ

tadabhinna⋅suhṛd⋅varyo mahā⋅bhāgavatottamaḥ
śrī⋅gaura⋅kiśoraḥ sākṣād vairāgyaṁ vigrahāśritam

māyāvādi⋅ku⋅siddhānta⋅dhvānta⋅rāśi⋅nirāsakaḥ<sup>2</sup>
viśuddha⋅bhakti⋅siddhāntaiḥ svānta⋅padma⋅vikāśakaḥ
<sup>2</sup><sub><eng>śuddha⋅gauḍīya⋅vaiṣṇavas appended the remaining verses</eng></sub>

devo‛sau paramahaṁso mataḥ śrī⋅gaura⋅kīrtane
pracārācāra⋅kārye nirantaraṁ mahotsukaḥ

hari⋅priya⋅janairgamya oṁ viṣṇupāda⋅pūrvakaḥ
śrī⋅pādo bhakti⋅siddhānta⋅sarasvatī⋅mahodayaḥ

tadantaraṅga⋅varyaḥ śrī⋅bhakti⋅prajñāna⋅keśavaḥ
gaura⋅vāṇī⋅vinode jaḥ kṛtiratneti saṁjñakaḥ

tadantaraṅga⋅varyau dvau matau śrī⋅gaura⋅kīrtane
vāmana⋅nārāyaṇau ca gosvāmi⋅pravarau prabhū

sarve te gaura⋅vaṁśyāśca paramahaṁsa⋅vigrahāḥ
vayañca praṇatā dāsāstaducchiṣṭa⋅grahāgrahāḥ
IDX.en_translation:
We offer respects to Śrī Kṛṣṇa, Brahmā, Devarṣi Nārada, and Bādarāyaṇa. We offer respects to Śrī Madhva, Śrī Padmanābha, Śrīmān Nṛhari, and Mādhava.

We offer respects to Akṣobhya, Jayatīrtha, Śrī Jñānasindhu, Dayānidhi, Śrī Vidyānidhi, Rājendra, and Jayadharma in chronological order.

We praise Puruṣottama, Brahmaṇya, and Vyāsatīrtha. Then, with devotion, we praise Lakṣmīpati and Śrīmān Mādhavendra Purī.

We worship their disciples, the Jagad-gurūs Śrī Īśvara Purī, Advaita Ācārya, and Nityānanda Prabhu. We worship the Lord, the disciple of Īśvara Purī, Śrī Caitanya Mahāprabhu.

He, who is the ocean of mercy Himself, delivered the universe which was burning in the filth of Kali by the gift of Śrī Kṛṣṇa-prema.

Mahāprabhu's Svarūpa Dāmodara is His associate who gives pleasure. Then the two best of the Gosvāmīs, Rūpa and Sanātana.

And Śrī Jīva and Raghunātha, the great-minded one who is dear to Rūpa. His beloved is the great poet Kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa Kavirāja.

His dearest friend is the one dedicated to service, Narottama Dāsa Ṭhākura. The devotee following him is the always excellent Śrī Viśvanātha Cakravartī Ṭhākura.

Attached to him is the ornament of the Gauḍīya Vedānta teachers, Śrī Baladeva Vidyābhūṣaṇa, who is our eternal shelter.

Then the Vaiṣṇava-sārvabhauma, Śrī Jagannātha Dāsa Bābājī, who indicated the abode of Śrī Māyāpura and is dear to the saintly people.

The best here, the very root of the preaching of śuddha-bhakti, is the divine Śrī Bhaktivinoda Ṭhākura, renowned as dear to him.

His non-different best friend, the topmost mahā-bhāgavata, is Śrī Gaura Kiśora Dāsa Bābājī, who is vairāgya personified.

The dispeller of the mass of darkness of the false conclusions of the Māyāvādīs, he causes the lotus of the heart to bloom with the conclusions of viśuddha-bhakti.

He is the Paramahaṁsa, celebrated in the kīrtana of Śrī Gaura, always very eager in the activities of preaching and proper conduct.

Understandable by the people dear to Hari, known as Oṁ Viṣṇupāda Śrī Śrīmad Bhaktisiddhānta Sarasvatī Ṭhākura Prabhupāda.

His best intimate associate is Śrī Bhakti Prajñāna Keśava, who is known as Kṛtiratna in the pleasure of Gaura-vāṇī.

His two best intimate associates, celebrated in the kīrtana of Śrī Gaura, are Vāmana and Nārāyaṇa, the best of Gosvāmīs and masters.

All of them belong to the lineage of Gaura and are the personifications of Paramahaṁsas. And we are their surrendered servants, eager to take their remnants.

IDX.first_line: sri krsna gopala hare mukunda
IDX.search: sri krsna gopala hare mukunda govinda he nanda kisora krsna ha sri yasoda tanaya prasida sri vallavi jivana radhikesa
IDX.verses:
śrī⋅kṛṣṇa gopāla hare mukunda,
govinda he nanda kiśora⋅kṛṣṇa

hā śrī⋅yaśodā⋅tanaya prasīda,
śrī⋅vallavī⋅jīvana rādhikeśa
IDX.en_translation:
Śrī Kṛṣṇa, Gopāla, Hare, Mukunda, Govinda, O Nanda-kiśora Kṛṣṇa!

O son of Mother Yaśodā, please be merciful! O Life of the gopīs, O Master of Rādhikā!

IDX.first_line: sri krsna seviba bali grha chadi kunje cali
IDX.search: sri krsna seviba bali grha chadi kunje cali jaite haya abhisari sakhi kunjasajja kare jabe vasakasajja hana tabe utkanthita krsnapatha lakhi kala ullanghiya hari bhogacihna dehe dhari aile hana khandita takhana sanketa paiya baise tabu kanta na aise vipralabdha nayika ta hana
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
śrī⋅kṛṣṇa seviba bali’, gṛha chāḍi’ kuñje cali’,
jāite haya ‘abhisārī’ sakhi
kuñjasajjā kare jabe, ‘vāsakasajjā’ hana tabe,
‘utkaṇṭhitā’ kṛṣṇa-patha lakhi’

kāla ullaṅghiyā hari, bhoga-cihna dehe dhari’,
āile hana ‘khaṇḍitā’ takhana
saṅketa pāiyā baise, tabu kānta nā āise,
‘vipralabdhā’ nāyikā ta’ ha’na

manera kalahe hari, jāna cali’ duḥkha kari’,
‘kalahāntaritā’ santāpinī
mathurāte kānta gela, bahu-dina nā āila,
‘proṣita⋅bhartṛkā’ kāṅgālinī

nijāyatte kānte peye, krīḍā kare kānta laye,
‘svādhīna⋅bhartṛkā’ se ramaṇī
nāyikāmātrera haya, ei aṣṭadaśodaya,
vipralambha⋅sambhoga⋅bodhinī
IDX.en_translation:
Thinking, "I will serve Śrī Kṛṣṇa," leaving home and going to the grove, she becomes an 'abhisārī'. When she decorates the grove, she then becomes a 'vāsakasajjā', and when watching for Kṛṣṇa's path, she is an 'utkaṇṭhitā'.

When Hari arrives late, bearing the marks of enjoyment on His body, she then becomes a 'khaṇḍitā'. When she waits after receiving a signal but her beloved does not come, she becomes a 'vipralabdhā'.

In a quarrel of the mind, Hari goes away sorrowfully; she is a 'kalahāntaritā', full of distress. When her beloved goes to Mathurā and does not return for a long time, she becomes a 'proṣita-bhartṛkā', a pauper in love.

Having her beloved under her control, she plays with him; that woman is a 'svādhīna-bhartṛkā'. These eighteen states arise for every heroine, illuminating both separation and union.

IDX.first_line: sri krsna visno madhukaitabhare
IDX.search: sri krsna visno madhukaitabhare bhaktanukampita bhagavan murare trayasva mam kesava lokanatha govinda damodara madhaveti gopala vamsidhara rupasindho lokesa narayana dinabandho uccasvaraistvam vada sarvadaiva govinda damodara madhaveti
IDX.author: śrī⋅bilvamaṅgala ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
śrī⋅kṛṣṇa viṣṇo madhukaiṭabhāre
bhaktānukampita bhagavan murāre
trāyasva māṁ keśava lokanātha
govinda dāmodara mādhaveti

gopāla vaṁśīdhara rūpasindho
lokeśa nārāyaṇa dīnabandho
uccasvaraistvaṁ vada sarvadaiva
govinda dāmodara mādhaveti

jihve rasajñe madhurapriyā tvaṁ
satyaṁ hitaṁ tvāṁ paramaṁ vadāmi
āvarṇayethā madhurākṣarāṇi
govinda dāmodara mādhaveti

govinda govinda hare murāre
govinda govinda mukunda kṛṣṇa
govinda govinda rathāṅgapāṇe
govinda dāmodara mādhaveti

śrī⋅kṛṣṇa rādhāvara gokuleśa
gopāla govardhana⋅nātha viṣṇo
jihve pivasvāmṛtametadeva
govinda dāmodara mādhaveti

śrīnātha viśveśvara viśva⋅mūrte
śrī⋅devakī⋅nandana daityaśatro
jihve pivasvāmṛtametadeva
govinda dāmodara mādhaveti

gopī⋅pate kaṁsaripo mukunda
lakṣmī⋅pate keśava vāsudeva
jihve pivasvāmṛtametadeva
govinda dāmodara mādhaveti

gopī⋅janāhlādakara vrajeśa
gocāraṇāraṇya⋅kṛtapraveśa
jihve pivasvāmṛtametadeva
govinda dāmodara mādhaveti

prāṇeśa viśvambhara kaiṭabhāre
vaikuṇṭha nārāyaṇa cakrapāṇe
jihve pivasvāmṛtametadeva
govinda dāmodara mādhaveti

hare murāre madhusūdanādya
śrī⋅rāma sītāvara rāvaṇāre
jihve pivasvāmṛtametadeva
govinda dāmodara mādhaveti

śrī⋅yādavendrādri⋅dharāmvujākṣa
go⋅gopa⋅gopī⋅sukhadānadakṣa
jihve pivasvāmṛtametadeva
govinda dāmodara mādhaveti

dharābharottāraṇa gopaveṣa
vihāralīlā⋅kṛtabandhu⋅śeṣa
jihve pivasvāmṛtametadeva
govinda dāmodara mādhaveti

vakī⋅vakāghāsura⋅dhenukāre
keśī⋅tṛṇavārta⋅vighātadakṣa
jihve pivasvāmṛtametadeva
govinda dāmodara mādhaveti

śrī⋅jānakī⋅jīvana rāma⋅candra
niśācarāre bharatāgrajeśa
jihve pivasvāmṛtametadeva
govinda dāmodara mādhaveti

nārāyaṇānanta hare nṛsiŋha
prahlāda⋅vādhāhara he kṛpālo
jihve pivasvāmṛtam etad eva
govinda dāmodara mādhaveti

līlā⋅manuṣyākṛti⋅rāma⋅rūpa
pratāpa⋅dāsī⋅kṛta⋅sarva⋅bhūpa
jihve pivasvāmṛtam etad eva
govinda dāmodara mādhaveti

śrī⋅kṛṣṇa govinda hare murāre
he nātha nārāyaṇa vāsudeva
jihve pivasvāmṛtam etad eva
govinda dāmodara mādhaveti

jihve sadaivaṁ bhaja sundararāṇi
nāmāni kṛṣṇasya manoharāṇi
samasta bhaktārti⋅vināśanāni
govinda dāmodara mādhaveti

tvameva jāce mama dehi jihve
samāgate daṇḍadhare kṛtānte
vaktavyamevaṁ madhuraṁ subhaktyā
govinda dāmodara mādhaveti

sukhāvasāne tvidameva sāraṁ
duḥkhāvasāne dvidameva geyam
dehāvasāne tvidameva jāpyaṁ
govinda dāmodara mādhaveti
IDX.en_translation:
Śrī Kṛṣṇa, Viṣṇu, enemy of Madhu and Kaiṭabha, O Supreme Lord Murāri, who is compassionate to the devotees, protect me, O Keśava, O Lord of the worlds, O Govinda, Dāmodara, Mādhava!

O Gopāla, O holder of the flute, O ocean of beauty, O Lord of the universe, O Nārāyaṇa, O friend of the poor, always chant with a loud voice: Govinda, Dāmodara, Mādhava!

O tongue, knower of taste, you are fond of sweetness, I tell you the supreme truth for your benefit, please chant these sweet syllables: Govinda, Dāmodara, Mādhava!

Govinda, Govinda, Hare, Murāri! Govinda, Govinda, Mukunda, Kṛṣṇa! Govinda, Govinda, holder of the chariot wheel! Govinda, Dāmodara, Mādhava!

Śrī Kṛṣṇa, beloved of Rādhā, Lord of Gokula, O Gopāla, O Lord of Govardhana Hill, O Viṣṇu, O tongue, drink this very nectar: Govinda, Dāmodara, Mādhava!

O Master of the Goddess of Fortune, O Lord of the universe, O form of the universe, O Son of Devakī, O enemy of the demons, O tongue, drink this very nectar: Govinda, Dāmodara, Mādhava!

O Lord of the gopīs, O enemy of Kaṁsa, O Mukunda, O Lord of Lakṣmī, O Keśava, O Vāsudeva, O tongue, drink this very nectar: Govinda, Dāmodara, Mādhava!

O delighter of the gopīs, O King of Vraja, You who entered the forest to graze the cows, O tongue, drink this very nectar: Govinda, Dāmodara, Mādhava!

O Lord of my life, O maintainer of the universe, O enemy of Kaiṭabha, O Vaikuṇṭha, O Nārāyaṇa, O holder of the discus, O tongue, drink this very nectar: Govinda, Dāmodara, Mādhava!

Hare, Murāri, Madhusūdana, the Primeval One, Śrī Rāma, beloved of Sītā, enemy of Rāvaṇa, O tongue, drink this very nectar: Govinda, Dāmodara, Mādhava!

O King of the Yādavas, O lifter of the hill, O lotus-eyed one, expert in giving happiness to the cows, gopas, and gopīs, O tongue, drink this very nectar: Govinda, Dāmodara, Mādhava!

O reliever of the earth's burden, clad in the dress of a cowherd, who performs pastimes with friends and Śeṣa, O tongue, drink this very nectar: Govinda, Dāmodara, Mādhava!

O enemy of Pūtanā, Baka, Aghāsura, and Dhenuka, expert in destroying Keśī and Tṛṇāvarta, O tongue, drink this very nectar: Govinda, Dāmodara, Mādhava!

O life of Jānakī, Rāmacandra, enemy of the demons, O elder brother of Bharata, O tongue, drink this very nectar: Govinda, Dāmodara, Mādhava!

O Nārāyaṇa, O Ananta, O Hare, O Nṛsiṁha, remover of Prahlāda's pain, O merciful one, O tongue, drink this very nectar: Govinda, Dāmodara, Mādhava!

O Lord who accepts a human-like form for pastimes as Rāma, who made all kings Your servants by Your power, O tongue, drink this very nectar: Govinda, Dāmodara, Mādhava!

Śrī Kṛṣṇa, Govinda, Hare, Murāri! O Master, Nārāyaṇa, Vāsudeva! O tongue, drink this very nectar: Govinda, Dāmodara, Mādhava!

O tongue, always worship the beautiful and captivating names of Kṛṣṇa, which destroy the suffering of all devotees: Govinda, Dāmodara, Mādhava!

I beg you alone, O tongue, please grant me this: when the God of Death arrives, this sweet name must be spoken with great devotion: Govinda, Dāmodara, Mādhava!

At the end of happiness, this alone is the essence, at the end of sorrow, this alone is to be sung, at the end of life, this alone is to be chanted: Govinda, Dāmodara, Mādhava!

IDX.first_line: sri krsnacaitanya nityananda dui prabhu
IDX.search: sri krsnacaitanya nityananda dui prabhu ei krpa kara jena na pasara kabhu hoila papistha janma na hoila takhane vancita hoinu sei sukha darasane
IDX.author: śrīla vṛndāvana⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
śrī⋅kṛṣṇacaitanya nityānanda dui prabhu
ei kṛpā kara jena nā pāsara kabhu

hoila pāpiṣṭha⋅janma nā hoila takhane
vañcita hoinu sei sukha⋅daraśane

tathāpiha ei kṛpā kara mahāśaya
e saba vihāra mora rahuka hṛdaya

jaya jaya śrī⋅caitanya, nityānanda rāya
tomāra caraṇa⋅dhana rahuka hiyāya

sapārṣade tumi nityānanda jathā jathā
kṛpā kara muñi jena bhṛtya hoi tathā

saṁsārera pāra hañā bhaktira sāgare
je ḍubibe, se bhajuka nitāicā̐dere

hena dina hoibe caitanya⋅nityānanda
dekhiba veṣṭita ki sakala bhakta-vṛnda

śrī⋅caitanya⋅nityānandacā̐da pa̐hu jāna
vṛndāvana⋅dāsa tachu padayuge gāna
IDX.en_translation:
Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya and Nityānanda are my two Lords. Please grant this mercy, so that I never forget them.

I was born a sinner; I was not present then. I was deprived of the sight of that happiness.

Still, please grant this mercy, O great souls. May all these pastimes remain in my heart.

All glories, all glories to Śrī Caitanya, Nityānanda Rāya. May the treasure of Your lotus feet remain in my heart.

Wherever You are with Your associates, O Nityānanda, Please grant mercy so that I may be Your servant there.

Crossing beyond this material world into the ocean of devotion, Whoever will dive, let them worship Nitāicāñda.

Will such a day come when Caitanya and Nityānanda I will see surrounded by all devotees?

Know that Śrī Caitanya-Nityānandacāñda are Lords. Vṛndāvana Dāsa sings at their lotus feet.

IDX.first_line: sri krsnacaitanya prabhu daya kara moretoma vina ke dayalu jagat samsare
IDX.search: sri krsnacaitanya prabhu daya kara moretoma vina ke dayalu jagat samsare patita pavana hetu tava avataramo sama patita prabhu na paibe ara
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
śrī⋅kṛṣṇacaitanya prabhu dayā kara moretomā’ vinā ke dayālu jagat⋅saṁsāre

patita⋅pāvana⋅hetu tava avatāramo sama patita prabhu nā pāibe āra

hā hā prabhu nityānanda premānandasukhīkṛpāvalokana kara āmi baḍa duḥkhī

dayā kara sītāpati advaita gosāñitava kṛpābale pāi caitanya⋅nitāi

hā hā svarūpa, sanātana, rūpa, raghunāthabhaṭṭa-yuga, śrī-jīva, hā prabhu lokanātha

dayā kara śrī⋅ācārya prabhu śrīnivāsarāmacandra⋅saṅga māge narottama⋅dāsa

[dayā kara prabhupāda śrī⋅dayitadāsavaiṣṇavera kṛpā māge e adhama dāsa

dayā kara gurudeva patita⋅pāvanaśrī⋅caraṇe sevā māge e patitajana]
IDX.en_translation:
O Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya, please be merciful to me. Who else but You is merciful in this world?

Your incarnation is for the purpose of delivering the fallen. O Lord, You will not find anyone as fallen as me.

Alas, alas, Lord Nityānanda, who is joyful in divine love! Please cast Your merciful glance upon me; I am very distressed.

Please be merciful, Advaita Gosāi, the Lord of Sītā. By the strength of Your mercy, I attain Caitanya and Nitāi.

Alas, alas, Svarūpa, Sanātana, Rūpa, and Raghunātha! O two Bhaṭṭas, O Śrī Jīva, O Lord Lokanātha!

Please be merciful, Śrīnivāsa Ācārya. Narottama dāsa begs for the association of Rāmacandra Kavirāja.

Please be merciful, Prabhupāda Śrīla Bhaktisiddhānta Sarasvatī! This wretched servant begs for the mercy of the devotees.

Please be merciful, Gurudeva, deliverer of the fallen! This fallen person begs for service at your divine feet.

IDX.first_line: sri krsnacaitanya prabhu jive daya kari
IDX.search: sri krsnacaitanya prabhu jive daya kari svaparsada sviya dhama saha avatari atyanta durlabha prema karibare dana sikhaya saranagati bhakatera prana
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
śrī⋅kṛṣṇacaitanya prabhu jīve dayā kari’
svapārṣada svīya dhāma saha avatari’

atyanta durlabha prema karibāre dāna
śikhāya śaraṇāgati bhakatera prāṇa

dainya, ātmanivedana, goptṛtve varaṇa
‘avaśya rakṣibe kṛṣṇa’⋅viśvāsa⋅pālana

bhakti⋅anukūlamātra kāryera svīkāra
bhakti⋅pratikūla⋅bhāva varjanāṅgīkāra

ṣaḍaṅga śaraṇāgati hoibe jā̐hāra
tā̐hāra prārthanā śune śrī⋅nandakumāra

rūpa⋅sanātana⋅pade dante tṛṇa kari’
bhakativinoda paḍe duhu̐ pada dhari’

kā̐diyā kā̐diyā bale⋅‘āmi ta’ adhama
śikhāye śaraṇāgati kara he uttama”
IDX.translation_intro: From The Harmonist, July 1927. Edited by Bhaktisiddhanta Sarasvati.
IDX.en_translation:
Sree Krishna Chaitanya, the Supreme Lord, out of divine pity for jiva, With His own associates and His own abode descending

The love of God that can only be obtained with the utmost difficulty to give freely away, Teaches to take refuge in Him: resignation, that is the very life of the devotee.

Lowliness of spirit, dedication of self, loving acceptance of Him as protector, Cherishing faith that Krishna will surely protect.

Assenting only to acts that are conducive to devotion, Promising to renounce conduct that is adverse to it--

In these sixfold ways, whoever takes refuge in Him, His prayer reaches the ears of the gracious Child of Nanda.

At the feet of Rupa and Sanatana holding the straw between the teeth Falls Bhaktivinode clasping both the feet with his hands

Crying all the while as he says: 'I am the vilest, I do realise, Teach thou me resignation and make me the worthiest.'

IDX.first_line: sri krsnah paramanando govindo nanda nandanah
IDX.search: sri krsnah paramanando govindo nanda nandanah tamala syamala rucih sikhanda krta sekharah pita kauseya vasano madhura smita sobhitah kandarpa koti lavanyo vrndaranya mahotsavah
IDX.author: śrīla rūpa gosvāmī
IDX.verses:
śrī⋅kṛṣṇaḥ paramānando govindo nanda⋅nandanaḥ
tamāla⋅śyāmala⋅ruciḥ śikhaṇḍa⋅kṛta⋅śekharaḥ

pīta⋅kauśeya⋅vasano madhura⋅smita⋅śobhitaḥ
kandarpa⋅koṭi⋅lāvaṇyo vṛndāraṇya⋅mahotsavaḥ

vaijayantī⋅sphurad⋅vakṣāḥ kakṣātta⋅laguḍottamaḥ
kuñjārpitaratir guñjā⋅puñja⋅mañjula⋅kaṇṭhakaḥ

karṇikārāḍhya⋅karṇa⋅śrī⋅r⋅dhṛta⋅svarṇābha⋅varṇakaḥ
muralī⋅vādana⋅paṭur vallavī⋅kula⋅vallabhaḥ

gāndharvāpti⋅mahā⋅parvā rādhārādhana⋅peśalaḥ
iti śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅candrasya nāma viṁśati⋅saṁjñakam

ānandākhyaṁ mahā⋅stotraṁ jaḥ paṭhec chṛṇuyāc ca jaḥ
sa paraṁ saukhyam āsādya kṛṣṇa⋅prema⋅samanvitaḥ

sarva⋅loka⋅priyo bhūtvā sad⋅guṇāvali⋅bhūṣitaḥ
vraja⋅rāja⋅kumārasya sannikarṣam avāpnuyāt
IDX.en_translation:
Śrī Kṛṣṇa, the Supreme Bliss, Govinda, the Son of Nanda. His luster is dark like the Tamāla; He has made a crown of a peacock feather.

He wears yellow silk cloth; He is adorned with a sweet smile. His beauty is that of millions of Cupids; He is the great festival of the forest of Vṛndā.

His chest shines with the Vaijayantī garland; He holds an excellent staff under His arm. He is attached to the groves; His throat is charming with a cluster of Guñjā.

His ears are wealthy with the Karṇikāra; He wears golden-colored pigments. He is expert in playing the flute; He is the beloved of the community of cowherd women.

He is the great festival of the attainment of Gāndharvā; He is expert in the worship of Rādhā. Thus, these are the twenty names of Śrī Kṛṣṇa⋅candra.

He who reads and he who hears this great Stotra named 'Ānanda' (Bliss), attaining supreme happiness, endowed with love for Kṛṣṇa.

Becoming dear to all people, adorned with a multitude of good qualities, he attains the vicinity of the Prince of Vraja.

IDX.first_line: sri krsnakirtane jadi manasa tohara
IDX.search: sri krsnakirtane jadi manasa tohara parama jatane tahi labha adhikara trnadhika hina dina akincana chara apane manabi sada chadi ahankara
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
śrī⋅kṛṣṇakīrtane jadi mānasa tohāra
parama jatane ta̐hi labha adhikāra

tṛṇādhika hīna dīna akiñcana chāra
āpane mānabi sadā chāḍi’ ahaṅkāra

vṛkṣasama kṣamā guṇa karabi sādhana
pratihiṁsā tyaji’ anye karabi pālana

jīvana⋅nirvāhe āne udvega nā dibe
para⋅upakāre nija⋅sukha pāsaribe

hoileo sarvaguṇe guṇī mahāśaya
pratiṣṭhāśā chāḍi’ kara amānī hṛdaya

kṛṣṇa⋅adhiṣṭhāna sarva jīve jāni’ sadā
karabi sammāna sabe ādare sarvadā

dainya, dayā, anye māna, pratiṣṭhā varjana
cāriguṇe guṇī hoi’, karaha kīrtana

bhakativinoda kā̐di’ bale prabhu pāya
hena adhikāra kabe dibe he āmāya
IDX.en_translation:
If your mind is set on the glorification of Śrī Kṛṣṇa, then with great care, attain the qualification for it.

Always consider yourself more humble than a blade of grass, poor and insignificant, and give up all false ego.

Practice the quality of forgiveness like a tree; giving up all vengeance, maintain others.

Do not cause anxiety to others for the sake of your own livelihood; for the welfare of others, forget your own happiness.

Even if you are a great soul endowed with all good qualities, give up the desire for prestige and keep your heart free from pride.

Always knowing that Kṛṣṇa resides in all living entities, you should always respectfully honor everyone with affection.

Humility, compassion, respecting others, and rejecting prestige—being qualified with these four virtues, perform congregational chanting.

Bhaktivinoda cries and says at the feet of the Lord: "When will You grant me such qualification?"

śrī⋅padma⋅purāṇa gaṅgādi⋅tīrtha⋅pariṣevita⋅pāda⋅padmāṁ go⋅loka⋅saukhya⋅rasa⋅pūramahiṁ mahimnā āplāvitākhila⋅sudhā⋅sujalāṁ sukhābdhau rādhā⋅mukunda⋅muditāṁ jamunāṁ namāmi śrī⋅gaṅgā⋅vandanā sadyaḥ pātaka⋅saŋhantrī sadeyā duḥkha⋅vināśinī sukhadā mokṣadā gaṅgā gaṅgaiva paramā gatiḥ
IDX.en_translation:
I offer obeisances to Lalitā-devī, who is beautifully adorned with all good qualities. Seeing Rādhā on the path of the party of Kṛṣṇa, she immediately makes Her successful by fulfilling Her desire.

Lalitā, Viśākhā, Citrā, Campakalatā, Raṅgadevī, Sudevī, Tuṅgavidyā, and Indurekhā. I offer my obeisances again and again to these eight Sakhīs. Still, Lalitā is my all-in-all, worshipped by everyone.

O wish-fulfilling creeper of mercy, Lalitā! Obeisances to you. O Viśākhā, possessing cleverness and qualities equal to Rādhā! I praise you, O Campakalatā, who steals the mind of Acyuta! I bow to you, O friend Citralekhā, of wonderful character!

O Śrī Raṅgadevī! O beloved! O stage for the limbs of love! Let there be obeisances to you, O giver of happiness, O beloved Sudevī! O Tuṅgavidyā, even in the abode of knowledge and pleasure! O Indulekhā, whose nails are like pieces of the full moon and who has a beautiful face!

I take shelter of the maidservants headed by Rūpa Mañjarī in the play-grounds. They please the Great Queen of Vṛndāvana (Rādhā) with affection by offering betel, massaging Her feet, giving water, and arranging trysts—tasks which are unhesitatingly performed even by the group of Sakhīs who are dearer to Her than life.

You are the form of Mahābhāva, the best of the beloveds of Kṛṣṇa. O Devi Rādhikā, bestower of loving devotion, I bow to You.

O Rādhe, Queen of Vṛndāvana, River of the nectar of compassion! Please mercifully give me the service of Your own lotus feet.

I worship Rādhā who has lotus eyes; I remember Rādhā who has a sweet smile on Her face; I speak of Rādhā who is melted by the burden of compassion. Therefore, I have no other goal.

Alas, O Devi! Today, with a voice choked with piteous words, falling on the ground like a stick, I beg with intense distress: bestowing mercy on this ignorant person, O Gāndharvikā, please count me among Your own people.

O Devi! I indeed never beg for anything else at any time except for the excellent service of Your lotus feet. Obeisances to Your friendship; obeisances always. May my taste be for Your service; may it be true.

I bow to the Goddess Śrī Hari-priyā, Rādhā, the Queen of the Rāsa dance, beautiful, the supreme enchantress of Govinda, the daughter of Vṛṣabhānu.

O You who enjoy the festival of the Rāsa dance! Obeisances to You, O Supreme Goddess! O Rādhā, dearer than the life of Kṛṣṇa! O Form of supreme bliss!

I take shelter of Rādhikā, the beloved of the son of the King of Vraja. She has the luster of a spotless lotus, wears blue garments, has beautiful hair, a face like the moon, and eyes like a wagtail bird. She is charming, and shines with a pearl necklace on Her chest. She is an adolescent.

When will I be the sweeper of the courtyard of the play-grove of the daughter of Vṛṣabhānu, the ocean of rasa? The Supreme Person with a peacock-feather crown (Kṛṣṇa) always speaks many plaintive words to Her maidservants.

I remember with my mind the child Mukunda, who is placing His lotus foot with His lotus hand into His lotus mouth, sitting on the lap of the Queen of Vraja and smiling.

I praise Kṛṣṇa in the form of Gopāla, the son of Yaśodā, dark like a fresh raincloud, with eyes like blue lotuses.

I worship the child dark like a tamāla leaf, with restless eyes due to the blowing breath of the Queen of the Cowherds (Yaśodā), crawling on the earth with both knees, His lips like nectar with a slight fresh smile.

We meditate on the root of Brahman, the protector of the dynasty of the people of Vraja, the athlete Śrīla Gopāla. His very sweet pastimes, His habit of playing the flute, holding the hill in His palm; His dark limbs put to shame the blue raincloud.

Having abandoned sleep at dawn due to the sound of churning yogurt, entering the solitary room of the cowherd women, quickly extinguishing the lamps with the wind from His lotus mouth, having eaten the fresh butter—may the child Kṛṣṇa protect me.

Holding the string of small bells with His left hand to silence them, bending down, smiling gently, moving on tiptoes; forbidding the smiling girls in front of Him with a glance of His eyes, Hari stole the fresh butter from behind His mother.

Obeisances to the Lord of the brāhmaṇas, and to the benefactor of the cows and brāhmaṇas; to the benefactor of the world, to Kṛṣṇa, to Govinda, obeisances, obeisances.

To remove the fear of material existence, the author of the scriptures gathered the essence of knowledge and realization, the essence of the Vedas, like a bee; and made the servants drink the nectar from the ocean. I bow to the First Person, the Best, named Kṛṣṇa.

I worship Govinda, who is dedicated to playing the sweet flute, who is adorned with divine ornaments. His luster is like a blooming blue lotus, His face is like the moon, He is fond of the peacock feather ornament. He bears the Śrīvatsa mark, wears the large Kaustubha jewel, is dressed in yellow garments, and is beautiful. His body is worshiped by the lotus eyes of the gopīs, and He is surrounded by the herds of cows and cowherds.

Musk tilaka on the forehead, Kaustubha on the chest, a fine pearl on the tip of the nose, a flute in the palm of the hand, a bracelet on the wrist. Sandalwood paste on the whole body, a pearl necklace on the neck—surrounded by the cowherd women, the Crest-jewel of Cowherds is victorious.

Delightful with a peacock feather crown, with a musk tilaka, with cheeks touched by earrings. Lotus-eyed, conch-necked, with a face beautiful with a smile, with a flute placed on His lips. Dark, peaceful, three-fold bending, with garments like the sun's rays, adorned with a Vaijayantī garland. I bow to Him standing in Vṛndāvana, surrounded by hundreds of young women, in the dress of a brāhmaṇa-cowherd.

I praise the son of Nanda, the beloved of the gopīs, Śrī Govinda. Who is dark like a cloud, wearing supreme yellow garments.

Obeisances to Kṛṣṇa, to Kṛṣṇa⋅candra, sporting in Vṛndāvana. Obeisances to You, the Lord of the cowherd women; obeisances to the Husband of Rādhikā.

Obeisances to the son of the King of Cowherds, pleasing to millions of Cupids, with the luster of a blooming blue lotus, whose pastimes enchant the world.

I praise the son of Śrī Nanda, whose body is sweet rasa, who is full of the highest sweetness. Who has placed the flute on His moon-face, endowed with a smile, incomparable, wearing excellent yellow garments. Lotus-eyed, expert in everything, like a fresh cloud, with a peacock-feather crown, the Shelter. With a body curved in three places, accompanied by the young women of Vraja, the Hero who destroyed Keśī and others.

Obeisances to the lotus-eyed one, who delights in playing the flute. Who is accustomed to drinking the nectar of Rādhā's lips, who wears a forest garland.

Obeisances to Kṛṣṇa, Vāsudeva, Hari, the Supreme Soul. To the destroyer of the distress of the surrendered, to Govinda, obeisances, obeisances.

Meditate on Rādhikā and Kṛṣṇa⋅candra in the solitary grove of Cupid. Their bodies are like gold and a raincloud; Their eyes are like blue and red lotuses. Their foreheads are excellent with musk; They wear garlands of mālatī and kunda flowers. Their dress is trembling and youthful; They are the Lords of blue and yellow garments.

We meditate on Dāmodara, whose arm, bright with luster, is placed on Rādhā's shoulder. Who dims the blue lotus on all sides with the rays of the blackness of His body. Who defeats the luster of saffron with the beauty of His silk garment. Who resides in the forest of Vṛndā; who is fragrant with garlands shining on His chest.

All glories, all glories to this forest of Vṛndā of Murāri! Dearer than Vaikuṇṭha because of the presence of the very saintly ones. Where He always gives pleasure to the cows, protecting the gopīs; increasing love in the Rāsa dance with His sounding sweet flute.

I bow to that Vṛndāvana, the form of sweetness. Which is filled with the multitudes of bliss of Indirā (Rādhā). Where the son of Nanda is delighted by the multitudes of bliss. Which is delighted by the beautiful bride of Govinda.

Obeisances to You, O Holder of the Plow! Obeisances, O Wielder of the Club! Obeisances, O Husband of Revatī! Obeisances, O Affectionate to the Devotees!

Obeisances, O Best of the Strong! Obeisances, O Holder of the Earth! O Enemy of Pralamba, obeisances to You! Protect me, O Elder Brother of Kṛṣṇa!

Alas! This Hill, O friend, is the best of the servants of Hari! Because, being jubilant by the touch of the feet of Rāma and Kṛṣṇa, it offers respect to Them along with the cows and cowherd boys, with drinking water, good grass, caves, roots, and bulbs.

Victory to Govardhana, the King of the mountains! Who was declared by the gopīs to be the best servant of Hari. Who was worshipped by Kṛṣṇa to stop the sacrifice of Indra. Who resided on the palm of His lotus hand for seven days.

I bow my head to Govardhana Hill. Which was served by Hari with His own groups for seven days. Which became like a bee on the lotus hand of Acyuta; with fruits, roots, and caves.

We praise the divine lake (Rādhā-kuṇḍa) dear to the Mistress of That One (Kṛṣṇa) maddened in battle (of love). Which is always a mine of astonishment of great rasa by the sweet pastimes of the Endless Hari and Rādhikā. Which is greatly effulgent and most fragrant in Vṛndāvana.

O Śrī Sarovara (Lake)! My Mistress always plays here with Her Beloved with amorous sports. If You are thus dearer than the dear to Them, alas! show me Her, my life, today by mercy.

Just as Rādhā is dear to Viṣṇu, so is Her Kuṇḍa dear. Among all the gopīs, She alone is the most beloved of Viṣṇu.

I worship forever that dear lake (Śyāma-kuṇḍa). Which appeared from the lotus foot of Kṛṣṇa Himself at the killing of the wicked Ariṣṭa. Which is expanded like the spreading of honey; which is desired (iṣṭa) and named Ariṣṭa. Which is surrounded by steps made by Śrī Rādhā with affection. Which embraces the Lake of the Beloved (Rādhā-kuṇḍa) with love.

May the daughter of the Sun (Yamunā) purify us. She is the vessel of supreme love for the son of Sadānanda (Nanda), the sun of consciousness and bliss. Her body is liquid Brahman. She is the destroyer of sins, the giver of welfare to the world.

I bow to the Yamunā, delighted by Rādhā and Mukunda in the ocean of happiness. Whose lotus feet are served by the Ganges and other holy places. Who is great with the glory of the flood of the rasa of happiness of Goloka. Who has flooded everything with sweet water like nectar.

The Gaṅgā destroys sins immediately; She is always to be worshiped, the destroyer of misery. Giver of happiness, giver of liberation, the Gaṅgā is indeed the supreme destination.

IDX.first_line: sri nanda nandana dhana tadiya vallabhagana
IDX.search: sri nanda nandana dhana tadiya vallabhagana madhura rasera alambana gopigata rati jaha gopicittasraya taha krsnamatra visaya takhana jaha rati krsnagata ratyasraya krsnacitta gopi taha ratira visaya visaya asraya dhare sthayibhava rati care naile rati udgata na haya
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
śrī⋅nanda⋅nandana⋅dhana, tadīya vallabhāgaṇa,
madhura⋅rasera ‘ālambana’
gopīgata⋅rati jāhā̐, gopīcittāśraya tāhā̐,
kṛṣṇa-mātra ‘viṣaya’ takhana

jāhā̐ rati kṛṣṇa-gata, ratyāśraya kṛṣṇa-citta,
gopī tāhā̐ ratira ‘viṣaya’
viṣaya⋅āśraya dhare’, sthāyibhāva⋅rati care,
naile rati udgata nā haya

vibhāvete ‘ālambana’, rase nitya prayojana,
vraje tāi kṛṣṇa⋅gopīnātha
madanamohana⋅dhana⋅ vrajāṅganā, gopī⋅jana⋅,
vallabha⋅rasika rādhānātha

svīyā, parakīyā bhede, rasa⋅rasāntarāsvāde,
nityānande virāje mādhava
baḍa bhāgyavān jei, nije ālambana hoi’,
āsvādaye se rasa⋅āsava
IDX.en_translation:
The treasure Śrī Nanda⋅nandana and His beloved consorts are the 'ālambana' of madhura-rasa. Where rati is directed toward the gopīs and is sheltered in their hearts, Kṛṣṇa alone is then the 'viṣaya'.

Where rati is directed toward Kṛṣṇa, and the 'āśraya' of that rati is Kṛṣṇa's heart, the gopī is then the 'viṣaya'. Holding both the 'viṣaya' and 'āśraya', the permanent sentiment of rati unfolds; otherwise, rati does not manifest.

The 'ālambana' in the causes of emotion is eternally necessary for rasa; therefore, in Vraja, Kṛṣṇa is Gopīnātha. The treasure of Madana-mohana are the women of Vraja, the gopīs, and the beloved rasika, Rādhānātha.

In the distinction of svīyā and parakīyā, in tasting various rasas, Mādhava resides in eternal bliss. He who is very fortunate, becoming the ālambana himself, tastes that intoxicating rasa.

IDX.first_line: sri narada uvaca
IDX.search: sri narada uvaca navina nirada syamam nilendivara locanam vallavi nandanam vande krsnam gopala rupinam sphurad varhi dalodvaddha nila kuncita murdhajam kadamba kusumodvaddha vanamala vibhusitam
IDX.verses:
śrī⋅nārada uvāca⋅
navīna⋅nīrada⋅śyāmaṁ nīlendīvara⋅locanam
vallavī⋅nandanaṁ vande kṛṣṇaṁ gopāla⋅rūpiṇam

sphurad⋅varhi⋅dalodvaddha⋅nīla⋅kuñcita⋅mūrdhajam
kadamba⋅kusumodvaddha⋅vanamālā⋅vibhūṣitam

gaṇḍa⋅maṇḍalasaṁsargi⋅calat⋅kuñcita⋅kuntalam
sthūlaṁ muktāphalodāra⋅hārodyotita⋅vakṣasam

hemāṅga⋅tulākoṭi⋅kirīṭojjvala⋅vigraham
manda⋅mārūta⋅saṅkṣobha⋅calitāmvara⋅sañcayam

rūcirauṣṭha⋅puṭa⋅nyasta⋅vaṁśī⋅madhura⋅niḥsvanaiḥ
lasad⋅gopālikā⋅ceto mohayantaṁ punaḥ punaḥ

vallavī⋅vadanāmbhoja ⋅ madhu⋅pāna⋅madhu⋅vratam
kṣobhayantaṁ manas tāsāṁ sasmerāpāṅga⋅vīkṣaṇaiḥ

jauvanod bhinna dehābhiḥ saṁsaktābhiḥ parasparam
vicitrāmvara⋅bhūṣābhir gopa⋅nārībhir āvṛtam

prabhinnāñjana⋅kālindī⋅jala⋅keli⋅kalotsukam
jodhayantaṁ kvacid gopān vyāharantaṁ gavāṁ gaṇam

kālindī⋅jala⋅saṁsargi⋅śītalānila⋅sevite
kadamba⋅pādapacchāye sthitaṁ vṛndāvane kvacit

ratna⋅bhūdhara⋅saŋlagna⋅ratnāsana⋅parigraham
kalpa⋅pādapa⋅madhyastha⋅hema⋅maṇḍapikā⋅gatam

vasanta⋅kusumāmoda⋅surabhī⋅kṛta⋅diṅ⋅mukhe
govardhana⋅girau ramye sthitaṁ rāsa⋅rasotsukam

savya⋅hasta⋅tala⋅nyasta⋅giri⋅varyāta⋅patrakam
khaṇḍitākhaṇḍalon⋅mukta⋅muktāsāra⋅ghanāghanam

veṇu⋅vādya⋅mahollāsa⋅kṛta⋅huṅkāra⋅niḥsvanaiḥ
sa⋅vatsair unmukhaiḥ śaśvad gokulair abhivīkṣitam

kṛṣṇam eva tu gāyadbhis tac⋅ceṣṭā⋅vaśa⋅vartibhiḥ
daṇḍa⋅pāśodyata⋅karair gopālai rūpa⋅śobhitamnāradādyair muni⋅śreṣṭhair veda⋅vedāṅga⋅pāragaiḥ
prīti⋅susnigdhayā vācā stūyamānaṁ parāt param

ja evaṁ cintayed devaṁ bhaktyā saṁstauti mānavaḥ
trisandhyaṁ tasya tuṣṭo’sau dadāti varam īpsitam

rāja⋅vallabhatām eti bhavet sarva⋅jana⋅priyaḥ
acalāṁ śriyam āpnoti sa vāgmī jāyate dhruvam

iti śrī⋅nārada⋅pañcarātre
jñānāmṛta⋅sāre caturtha⋅rātre gopāla⋅stotraṁ nāma
ṣaṣṭho’dhyāyaḥ
IDX.en_translation:
Śrī Nārada said: I offer my respectful obeisances to the son of the cowherd woman, Kṛṣṇa, whose form is that of a cowherd boy. His complexion is dark like a fresh raincloud, and His eyes resemble blue lotus flowers.

His dark, curly hair is tied with a shining peacock feather, and He is adorned with a garland of forest flowers strung with Kadamba blossoms.

His moving, curly locks of hair touch His round cheeks, and His broad chest is illuminated by a necklace of large pearls.

His form shines with golden armlets, waistbands, and a crown. His garments flutter in the agitation of the gentle breeze.

With the sweet sounds of the flute placed upon His beautiful lips, He again and again enchants the minds of the shining cowherd girls.

He is the bee drinking the honey of the lotus faces of the cowherd women. He agitates their minds with His smiling sidelong glances.

He is surrounded by the wives of the cowherds, who are decorated with wonderful clothes and ornaments, whose bodies are blossoming with youth, and who are attached to one another.

He is eager for the artful sports in the waters of the Yamunā, which are dark like crushed collyrium. Sometimes He fights with the cowherd boys and sometimes He calls out to the herd of cows.

Sometimes He stays in Vṛndāvana, in the shade of a Kadamba tree, served by the cooling breeze touching the waters of the Yamunā.

He accepts a jeweled throne located on a jeweled mountain. He has entered a golden pavilion situated in the midst of desire trees.

He stays on the beautiful Govardhana Hill, where the directions are perfumed by the fragrance of spring flowers, eager for the mellows of the Rāsa dance.

He holds the best of mountains like an umbrella in the palm of His left hand, shielding Vraja from the dense clouds releasing torrents of rain sent by the angry Indra.

He is constantly gazed upon by the herds of cows with their calves, who look up with faces lifted, making loud lowing sounds in great jubilation at the music of His flute.

He is beautified by the cowherd boys, who sing only of Kṛṣṇa, who are under the control of His activities, and who hold sticks and ropes in their uplifted hands. He is the Supreme beyond the Supreme, praised with words full of affection by the best of sages led by Nārada, who are learned in the Vedas and Vedāṅgas.

A human being who thinks of that Lord in this way and praises Him with devotion three times a day pleases Him, and He grants the desired boon.

He becomes dear to the king and beloved by all people. He attains steady opulence and certainly becomes an orator.

Thus ends the Gopāla-stotra in the sixth chapter of the fourth night of the Jñānāmṛta-sāra of the Śrī Nārada-pañcarātra.

IDX.first_line: sri radha krsna padakamale mana
IDX.search: sri radha krsna padakamale mana kemane labhibe carama sarana ciradina kariya o carana asa ache he basiya e adhama dasa
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
śrī⋅rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa⋅padakamale mana
kemane labhibe carama śaraṇa

ciradina kariyā o⋅caraṇa⋅āśa
āche he basiyā e adhama dāsa

he rādhe, he kṛṣṇa⋅candra bhaktaprāṇa
pāmare jugala⋅bhakti kara’ dāna

bhaktihīna bali’ nā kara’ upekṣā
mūrkha-jane deha’ jñāna⋅suśikṣā

viṣaya⋅pipāsā⋅prapīḍita dāse
deha’ adhikāra jugala⋅vilāse

cañcala jīvana⋅, srota pravāhiyā,
kālera sāgare dhāya
gela je divasa, nā āsibe āra,
ebe kṛṣṇa ki upāya

tumi patitajanera bandhu
jāni he tomāre nātha, tumi ta’ karuṇā⋅jalasindhu

āmi bhāgya-hīna, ati arvācīna,
nā jāni bhakati⋅leśa
nija⋅guṇe nātha, kara’ ātmasāt,
ghucāiyā bhava⋅kleśa

siddha⋅deha diyā, vṛndāvana⋅mājhe,
sevāmṛta kara’ dāna
piyāiyā prema, matta kari’ more,
śuna nija guṇagāna

jugala⋅sevāya, śrī-rāsa-maṇḍale,
niyukta kara’ āmāya
lalitā sakhīra, ayogyā kiṅkarī,
vinoda dhariche pāya
IDX.en_translation:
O mind, how will you attain ultimate refuge at the lotus feet of Śrī Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa?

Always hoping for those feet, this fallen servant is waiting.

O Rādhe! O Kṛṣṇa⋅candra, life of the devotees! Please bestow devotion to the Divine Couple upon this sinner.

Do not neglect me, calling me devoid of devotion. Please give good instruction in knowledge to this foolish person.

To this servant afflicted by the thirst for material enjoyment, please grant the qualification for the pastimes of the Divine Couple.

This restless life, flowing like a current, rushes into the ocean of time. The days that have passed will not come again. Now, O Kṛṣṇa, what is the way?

You are the friend of the fallen. I know You, O Lord, indeed You are an ocean of the water of mercy.

I am unfortunate, very insignificant, and do not know a trace of devotion. By Your own quality, O Lord, please accept me, removing the miseries of material existence.

Granting me a spiritual body in Vṛndāvana, bestow the nectar of service. Making me drink love and intoxicating me, please listen to my singing of Your glories.

Engage me in the service of the Divine Couple in the Śrī Rāsa-maṇḍala. Vinoda, an unworthy maidservant of Lalitā, holds onto Your feet.

IDX.en_translation:
He who has stolen the waves of the beauty and qualities of Śrīmatī Rādhikā, whose complexion is golden like molten gold, and who is the essence of the Vedānta, Vedāṅgas, and Purāṇas—may that Lord Gaura, the incarnation of mercy, be victorious.

He whose lotus feet are praised by Brahmā, Indra, and Śiva, who is the abode of the ocean of magnanimity and sweetness, and who bears the burden of ecstasy manifested as horripilation, trembling, and tears—may that Lord Gaura, the incarnation of mercy, be victorious.

He who is the life and soul of Svarūpa Dāmodara and Rūpa Gosvāmī, who is the Lord of Gopāla, Govinda, and Mukunda, and who tears apart the misery, low birth, and sins of the poor—may that Lord Gaura, the incarnation of mercy, be victorious.

He who removes the darkness of the illusory philosophy of Māyā, who delivered the group of sannyāsīs in Vārāṇasī, and who spreads pure, transcendental devotion—may that Lord Gaura, the incarnation of mercy, be victorious.

He who removed the pride of the world-conquering brāhmaṇa, who bestowed great mercy upon Sārvabhauma Bhaṭṭācārya, and who enjoyed pastimes in Jagannātha Purī for eighteen years—may that Lord Gaura, the incarnation of mercy, be victorious.

He who bestows the mellows of brilliant love, who establishes the supreme devotion to the Holy Name, and whose eternal pastimes are in Goloka Vṛndāvana—may that Lord Gaura, the incarnation of mercy, be victorious.

He who is always intoxicated by the sweet singing of the Hare Kṛṣṇa mantra, who is the object of meditation for the best of yogīs and sages, and who gives the sweet essence of the love of Vraja—may that Lord Gaura, the incarnation of mercy, be victorious.

He whose chest is broad like a door and whose eyes are like fresh lotus flowers, whose body is the condensed form of eternity, knowledge, and bliss, and whose bodily luster defeats millions of Cupids—may that Lord Gaura, the incarnation of mercy, be victorious.

He who is like a cakora bird for the nectar of the moon of Nīlācala, who is agitated by the nectar of singing in front of the Ratha-yātrā chariot, and whose body shines amidst the assembly of Vaiṣṇavas—may that Lord Gaura, the incarnation of mercy, be victorious.

He who is the royal swan in the minds of the devotees, who is the crown jewel of the sannyāsīs and brāhmaṇas, and who is the son of Jagannātha Miśra and Mother Śacī—may that Lord Gaura, the incarnation of mercy, be victorious.

He who sings this praise of Lord Gaura with devotion obtains the nectar of pure love. Freed from the misery of the forest fire of the three-fold sufferings, the devotee of the lotus feet of Lord Kṛṣṇa rejoices.

IDX.search: sri rama candra krpalu bhaju mana harana bhava bhaya darunam nava kanja locana kanja mukha kara kanja pada kanjarunam kandarpa aganita amita chabi nava nila nirada sundaram pata pita manahu tadita ruci suci naumi janaka suta varam
IDX.author: tulasī⋅dāsa
IDX.verses: śrī⋅rāma⋅candra kṛpālu bhaju mana haraṇa bhava⋅bhaya dāruṇam nava⋅kañja⋅locana, kañja⋅mukha kara⋅ kañja pada⋅kañjāruṇam kandarpa agaṇita amita chabi, nava⋅nīla⋅nīrada⋅sundaram paṭa⋅pīta mānahu taḍita ruci⋅śuci naumi janaka sutā⋅varam bhaju dīna⋅bandhu dineśa dānava⋅ daitya⋅vaṁśa⋅nikandanam raghu⋅nanda ānanda⋅kanda kausala⋅ canda daśaratha⋅nandanam śira⋅mukuṭa kuṇḍala tilaka cāru udāra aṅga vibhūṣaṇam ājānubhuja śara⋅cāpa⋅dhara saŋgrāma⋅ jita⋅khara⋅dūṣaṇam iti vadati tulasī⋅dāsa śaŋkara⋅ śeṣa⋅muni⋅mana⋅rañjanam mama hṛdaya⋅kañja nivāsa kuru kāmādi⋅ khala⋅dala⋅gañjanam
IDX.en_translation:
O mind! Worship the merciful Śrī Rāmacandra, who removes the terrible fear of material existence. His eyes are like new lotuses, His face is like a lotus, His hands are like lotuses, and His feet are like reddish lotuses.

He has the limitless beauty of countless Cupids, beautiful like a fresh blue cloud. His yellow garment shines like lightning, I bow to the husband of the daughter of Janaka.

Worship the friend of the poor, the sun (of the solar dynasty), the destroyer of the demons and Diti's lineage. The delight of the Raghu dynasty, the root of bliss, the moon of Kauśalyā, the son of Daśaratha.

Beautiful with a crown on His head, earrings, and tilaka; His generous limbs are adorned. With long arms reaching His knees, holding a bow and arrow, He conquered Khara and Dūṣaṇa in battle.

Thus says Tulasī Dāsa: O Lord who delights the minds of Śaṅkara, Śeṣa, and the sages! Please reside in the lotus of my heart, destroying the group of wicked enemies headed by lust.
