IDX.en_translation:
Śrī Rudra said: O Best of the Self-Realized! Victory to You! O Self of all, let there be auspiciousness for me. I offer my obeisances to You, who are served by You.

Obeisances to the Lotus-naveled One, the Soul of the subtle elements and senses. To Vāsudeva, the peaceful, the unchanging, the self-effulgent.

To Saṅkarṣaṇa, the subtle, the endless, and the destroyer. Obeisances to the awakener of the universe, Pradyumna, the inner soul.

Obeisances, obeisances to Aniruddha, the Master of the senses, the Soul of the senses. Obeisances to the Paramahaṁsa, the Full, the concealed Soul.

Obeisances to Him who is the door to heaven and liberation, who always dwells in the pure heart. Obeisances to Hiraṇya-vīrya, the expander of the Cātur-hotra sacrifice.

Obeisances to Ūrja (strength), Iṣa (food), the Lord of the three Vedas, the source of sacrifice. To the giver of satisfaction to the jīvas, obeisances to the Soul of all rasas.

To Him whose body is all-existence, the Specific, the Grossest. Obeisances to the Protector of the three worlds, along with Ojas (vitality) and Bala (strength).

To the Meaning-gendered, the Sky, obeisances to the Inner and Outer Soul. Obeisances to the Pious World, to that One of great splendor.

To the active, the renounced, the Worshipable Deity of the forefathers, to Karma. Obeisances to the result of irreligion, to Death, and to the giver of misery.

Obeisances to You, the Lord of benedictions, to Manu, to the Causal Soul. Obeisances to Dharma, the Great, to Kṛṣṇa of stunted intelligence. To the Person, the Ancient, the Lord of Sāṅkhya and Yoga.

To Him endowed with the three energies, to the Showerer, to the Ego-soul. To the form of Consciousness, obeisances to the manifestation of Speech.

Give us, who are eager to see, the vision worshipped by the devotees. The form most dear to Your own, the collyrium for all the senses and qualities.

Dark like a glistening raincloud, the reservoir of all beauty. With four beautiful long arms, and a well-formed, charming face. Eyes like lotus petals, beautiful eyebrows, beautiful nose. Beautiful teeth, beautiful cheeks and face, adorned with equal earrings.

Beautified by sidelong glances laughing with love and by locks of hair. With a garment like the filaments of a shining lotus, and polished earrings. With shining crown, bracelets, necklaces, anklets, and girdle. Endowed with the excellent opulence of conch, disc, club, lotus, garland, and jewels.

Bearing the Kaustubha on a beautiful neck, with the shoulders of a lion. Shining on the chest, defeating the touchstone with the never-departing Śrī (Lakṣmī).

With an abdomen beautiful with folds agitated by breathing in and out. Deep with a navel-whirlpool that seems to draw the universe back in.

With a golden belt on a shining cloth over dark hips. With symmetrical, beautiful feet, shins, thighs, and knees—beautiful to see.

By Your feet, which have the luster of autumn lotus petals, and by the light of Your nails which destroys our inner sin. Show us Your own feet, which remove fear, O Guru! You are the Guru of the path for those in darkness.

This form should be meditated upon by those desiring self-purification. Whose bhakti-yoga grants fearlessness to those performing their own duty.

You are attainable by one who has devotion, but rare for all embodied beings. Even for the King of Heaven, the goal of the knowers of the Self is desired in solitude.

Having worshipped Him who is difficult to worship, with unalloyed devotion rare even for the saintly. Who would desire anything outside, without the root of Your feet?

Where death does not think to enter, absorbed. Destroying the universe with eyebrows flashing with heroism and valor.

I do not weigh heaven or liberation against even half a moment of the association of those attached to the Lord. What to speak of the benedictions of mortals?

Therefore, let us have the association of those whose sins have been washed away by bathing internally and externally in the holy rivers of Your glories and Your sinless feet. Who are endowed with compassion and good character towards living beings. This is Your mercy upon us.

Whose mind, bewildered by external objects, does not enter the cave of darkness, being purified. By whose bhakti-yoga, graced easily, the sage sees Your path there.

Where this universe is manifested, which shines in the universe. You are that Brahman, the Supreme Light, expanded like the sky.

Who created this with the Maya of many forms, maintains it again, and destroys it, being unchanging. By whom the intelligence is different, as if existing, due to the bad state of the self. We understand You, the Lord, to be independent.

With these activities, the yogis endowed with faith worship this very thing perfectly for perfection. Characterized by the elements, senses, and inner instrument, in the Veda and Tantra, they alone are the knowers.

You are the One, the Original Person, whose energy is asleep. By that, passion, goodness, and ignorance are divided. From whom came this: the Mahat, the Ego, the Sky, Air, Fire, Water, Earth, the Suras, Sages, and hosts of beings.

Created by Your own energy, having entered this fourfold city with a portion of Yourself. Then they know that Person to be existing within, who enjoys the honey made by bees with the senses.

He, this one, of very fierce speed, You pull the worlds, driving Time. Beings by beings, the Truth inferred, like the wind the clouds, unbearable.

Thinking loudly "This is to be done," greedy, lusty for objects. You, always alert, suddenly seize the madman, like a hungry snake a mouse licking its lips.

What learned person abandons Your lotus feet, when You remove the banner of disrespect? Out of fear, our Guru worshiped, without argument, the fourteen Manus.

Now You are ours, O Brahman, O Supreme Soul of the wise! The universe is destroyed by the fear of Rudra; You are the destination of the fearless.

Chant this, may it be well with you, O pure sons of the King! Performing your own duty, with minds offered to the Lord.

Worship, praise, and meditate on that very Self situated in the self, situated in all beings—Hari.

Accepting the order of yoga, observing the vow of silence, holding. With concentrated minds, all of you practice this with respect.

This the Lord, the Master of the creators of the universe, spoke to us in the past. Desiring to create, to the sons like Bhṛgu who were desiring to create.

We, all the Prajāpatis, urged by that in the creation of progeny. By this, our darkness destroyed, we desire to create various progeny.

Now, a person constantly engaged in chanting this with attention. He quickly attains the ultimate good, devoted to Vāsudeva.

Of all benedictions here, knowledge is the supreme beatitude. He crosses the ocean of misery, difficult to cross, on the boat of knowledge, happily.

He who studies this hymn to the Lord sung by me with faith. He worships Hari, who is difficult to worship.

A person obtains from Him whatever he desires quickly. From Him, the only beloved of benedictions, who is well-pleased by the song sung by me.

A mortal who, rising at dawn, with folded hands and endowed with faith. Hears or causes to hear this, is released from the bondages of karma.

This hymn of the Supreme Person, the Supreme Soul, sung by me, O sons of the King! Chanting with one-pointed minds, perform great austerity; at the end, you will obtain the desired from Him.

IDX.first_line: sri rupa manjari kabe madhura vacane
IDX.search: sri rupa manjari kabe madhura vacane radhakunda mahima varnibe saŋgopane e caudda bhuvanopari vaikuntha nilaya tadapeksa mathura parama srestha haya
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
śrī⋅rūpa mañjarī kabe madhura vacane
rādhā-kuṇḍa⋅mahimā varṇibe saŋgopane

e caudda bhuvanopari vaikuṇṭha nilaya
tada-pekṣā mathurā parama śreṣṭha haya

māthuramaṇḍale rāsalīlā sthāna jathā
vṛndāvana śreṣṭha ati śuna mama kathā

kṛṣṇalīlā⋅sthala govardhana śreṣṭhatara
rādhā-kuṇḍa śreṣṭhatama sarva-śakti-dhara

rādhā-kuṇḍa⋅mahimā ta’ kariyā śravaṇa
lālāyita ha’ye āmi paḍiba takhana

sakhīra caraṇe kabe kariba ākuti
sakhī kṛpā kari’ dibe svārasikī sthiti
IDX.en_translation:
When will Śrī Rūpa Mañjarī, with sweet words, describe the glories of Rādhā-kuṇḍa to me in a secret place?

Above the fourteen planetary systems lies the spiritual abode of Vaikuṇṭha. Yet, Mathurā is far superior to that.

Within the district of Mathurā, Vṛndāvana is the most excellent place, where the Rāsa dance takes place. Please hear my words.

Govardhana Hill, the site of Kṛṣṇa's pastimes, is even more excellent. But Rādhā-kuṇḍa is the most excellent of all, possessing all spiritual potencies.

Hearing the glories of Rādhā-kuṇḍa, I will fall down, overcome with great longing.

When will I plead at the lotus feet of the sakhīs? By their mercy, the sakhīs will grant me my eternal position in the flow of divine love.

IDX.first_line: sri rupa pascate ami rahiba bhita hana
IDX.search: sri rupa pascate ami rahiba bhita hana dohe punah kahibena ama pane cana sadaya hrdaye dohe kahibena hasi kothaya paile rupa ei nava dasi
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
śrī⋅rūpa⋅paścāte āmi rahiba bhīta hañā
dohe punaḥ kahibena āmā pāne cāñā

sadaya⋅hṛdaye dohe kahibena hāsi
kothāya pāile rūpa! ei nava dāsī

śrī-rūpa-mañjarī tabe dohavākya śuni
mañjunālī dila more ei dāsī āni’

ati namracitta āmi ihāre jānila
sevākārya niyā tabe hethāya rākhila

hena tattva dohākāra sākṣāte kahiyā
narottame sevāya dibe niyukta kariyā
IDX.en_translation:
I will remain behind Śrī Rūpa, being afraid. Both will again say, looking at me.

Smiling with kind hearts, Both will say: "Where did you get this new maidservant, Rūpa?"

Then Śrī Rūpa Mañjarī, hearing the words of Both: "Mañjunālī brought this maidservant and gave her to me.

I knew her to be very humble in heart; therefore I kept her here engaged in service."

Speaking such truths in the presence of Both, she will engage Narottama in service.

IDX.first_line: sri rupa vadane sri sacikumara
IDX.search: sri rupa vadane sri sacikumara svanama mahima karala pracara jo nama so hari kichu nahi bheda so nama satyamiti gayati veda
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
śrī⋅rūpa⋅vadane, śrī⋅śacīkumāra
svanāma⋅mahimā karala pracāra

jo nāma so hari, kichu nāhi bheda
so nāma satyamiti gāyati veda

sabu upaniṣad, ratnamālā⋅dyuti,
jhakamaki’ caraṇa⋅samīpe
maṅgala⋅ārati, karaba anukṣaṇa,
dviguṇita⋅pañcapradīpe

cauddabhuvana māha, deva⋅nara⋅dānava,
bhāga jākara balabān
nāmarasa⋅pīyuṣa, piyoi anukṣaṇa,
choḍata karama⋅geyāna

nityamukta punaḥ, nāma⋅upāsanā,
satata karoi sāmagāne
goloke baiṭhata, gāoye nirantara,
nāma⋅viraha nāhi jāne

sabu rasa⋅ākara, ‘hari’ itidvyakṣara,
sabubhāve karala āśraya
nāmacaraṇe pa’ḍe, bhakativinoda kahe,
tuyā pade māgahu nilaya
IDX.en_translation:
Through the mouth of Śrī Rūpa, the son of Śacī proclaimed the glories of His own Holy Name.

That Name is Hari; there is no difference. The Vedas sing that the Name is the Truth.

All the Upaniṣads, like the radiance of a garland of jewels, sparkle at His lotus feet. I will constantly perform auspicious worship with twice five lamps.

In the fourteen worlds, among demigods, men, and demons, those whose fortune is strong constantly drink the nectar of the Holy Name, giving up fruitive activities and speculative knowledge.

Eternally liberated souls worship the Holy Name, always singing in Vedic melodies. Seated in Goloka, they sing ceaselessly; they know no separation from the Holy Name.

The two syllables 'Hari', the source of all mellows, are the shelter in all circumstances. Falling at the feet of the Holy Name, Bhaktivinoda says: "At Your feet, I beg for a dwelling."

IDX.first_line: sri sri guru gauranga gandharvika giridhari
IDX.search: sri sri guru gauranga gandharvika giridhari sri sri radha vinoda vihari jiu ki jaya engnow your diksa gurus name should be glorifiedeng
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
śrī⋅śrī⋅guru⋅gaurāṅga⋅gāndharvikā⋅giridhārī
śrī⋅śrī⋅rādhā⋅vinoda⋅vihārī⋅jīu kī jaya,

[ <eng>now your dīkṣā⋅guru’s name should be glorified</eng> ]

jaya nitya⋅līlā⋅praviṣṭa oṁ
viṣṇu⋅pāda aṣṭottara⋅śata⋅
śrī⋅śrīmad⋅bhakti⋅vedānta
nārāyaṇa gosvāmī mahārāja kī jaya,

jaya nitya⋅līlā⋅praviṣṭa oṁ
viṣṇu⋅pāda aṣṭottara⋅śata⋅
śrī⋅śrīmad⋅bhakti⋅vedānta
vāmana gosvāmī mahārāja kī jaya,

jaya nitya⋅līlā⋅praviṣṭa oṁ
viṣṇu⋅pāda aṣṭottara⋅śata⋅
śrī⋅śrīmad⋅bhakti⋅prajñāna
keśava gosvāmī mahārāja kī jaya,

jaya nitya⋅līlā⋅praviṣṭa oṁ
viṣṇu⋅pāda aṣṭottara⋅śata⋅
śrī⋅śrīla bhakti⋅siddhānta
sarasvatī gosvāmī prabhupāda kī jaya,

jaya nitya⋅līlā⋅praviṣṭa paramahaṁsa bābājī
śrī⋅śrīla gaura⋅kiśora⋅dāsa gosvāmī mahārāja kī jaya,

jaya nitya⋅līlā⋅praviṣṭa śrī⋅śrīla saccidānanda
bhakti⋅vinoda ṭhākura kī jaya,

jaya nitya⋅līlā⋅praviṣṭa vaiṣṇava⋅sārvabhauma
śrīla jagannātha⋅dāsa bābājī mahārāja kī jaya,

jaya samasta⋅rūpānugā⋅gauḍīya⋅guru⋅varga kī jaya,
jaya nitya⋅līlā⋅praviṣṭa oṁ
viṣṇu⋅pāda aṣṭottara⋅śata⋅
śrī⋅śrīmad⋅bhakti⋅vedānta svāmī mahārāja kī jaya,

jaya nitya⋅līlā⋅praviṣṭa oṁ
viṣṇu⋅pāda aṣṭottara⋅śata⋅
śrī⋅śrīmad⋅bhakti⋅vedānta
trivikrama gosvāmī mahārāja kī jaya,

jaya gauḍīya vedāntācārya śrīla
baladeva vidyā⋅bhūṣaṇa prabhu kī jaya,
jaya śrīla viśvanātha cakravartī ṭhākura kī jaya,

jaya śrīla narottama⋅śrīnivāsa⋅
śyāmānanda⋅prabhu⋅traya kī jaya,
jaya śrīla kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa kavi⋅rāja gosvāmī prabhu kī jaya,
jaya śrīla vṛndāvana⋅dāsa ṭhākura kī jaya,

jaya śrī⋅rūpa, sanātana, bhaṭṭa⋅raghunātha,
śrī⋅jīva, gopāla⋅bhaṭṭa, dāsa⋅raghunātha
ṣaḍ⋅gosvāmī prabhu kī jaya,

jaya śrī⋅svarūpa⋅dāmodara⋅
rāya⋅rāmānandādi gaura⋅pārṣada⋅vṛnda kī jaya,
jaya nāmācārya śrīla hari⋅dāsa ṭhākura kī jaya,

premase kaho śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅caitanya⋅
prabhu⋅nityānanda⋅śrī⋅advaita⋅gadādhara⋅
śrīvāsādi⋅śrī⋅gaura⋅bhakta⋅vṛnda kī jaya,

śrī⋅antardvīpa māyāpura, sīmantadvīpa,
godrumadvīpa, madhyadvīpa, koladvīpa, ṛtudvīpa,
jahnudvīpa, modadrumadvīpa, rudradvīpātmaka
śrī⋅navadvīpa⋅dhāma kī jaya,

śrī⋅śrī⋅rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa⋅gopa⋅gopī⋅go⋅
govardhana dvādaśa⋅vanātmaka
śrī⋅vraja⋅maṇḍala⋅dhāma kī jaya,

śrī⋅rādhā⋅kuṇḍa⋅śyāma⋅kuṇḍa⋅gaṅgā⋅
jamunā⋅tulasī⋅bhakti⋅devī kī jaya,
śrī⋅jagannātha⋅baladeva⋅subhadrā⋅sudarśanaju kī jaya,

śrī⋅gopīśvara⋅mahādeva kī jaya,
śrī joga⋅māyā paurṇamāsī devī kī jaya,

śrīmatī vṛndā⋅devī kī jaya
śrī⋅nṛsiŋha⋅varāha⋅deva kī jaya,
bhakta⋅pravara śrī⋅prahlāda mahārāja kī jaya,

cāri⋅dhāma, sampradāya, ācārya⋅vṛnda kī jaya,
vraja⋅patana⋅śrī⋅caitanya⋅maṭha kī jaya,

śrī⋅keśavajī gauḍīya maṭha o tat⋅śākhā⋅maṭha⋅samūha kī jaya,
śrī⋅harināma⋅saṅkīrtana kī jaya,

ananta⋅koṭī vaiṣṇava⋅vṛnda kī jaya,
śrī⋅gaura⋅premānande hari hari bola
IDX.en_translation:
All glories to Śrī Śrī Guru, Gaurāṅga, Gāndharvikā, Giridhārī, and Śrī Śrī Rādhā-Vinoda-bihārī-jīu!

Now your dīkṣā-guru's name should be glorified.

All glories to nitya-līlā-praviṣṭa Oṁ Viṣṇupāda Aṣṭottara-śata Śrī Śrīmad Bhaktivedānta Nārāyaṇa Gosvāmī Mahārāja!

All glories to nitya-līlā-praviṣṭa Oṁ Viṣṇupāda Aṣṭottara-śata Śrī Śrīmad Bhaktivedānta Vāmana Gosvāmī Mahārāja!

All glories to nitya-līlā-praviṣṭa Oṁ Viṣṇupāda Aṣṭottara-śata Śrī Śrīmad Bhakti Prajñāna Keśava Gosvāmī Mahārāja!

All glories to nitya-līlā-praviṣṭa Oṁ Viṣṇupāda Aṣṭottara-śata Śrī Śrīla Bhaktisiddhānta Sarasvatī Gosvāmī Prabhupāda!

All glories to nitya-līlā-praviṣṭa Paramahaṁsa Bābājī Śrī Śrīla Gaura Kiśora Dāsa Gosvāmī Mahārāja!

All glories to nitya-līlā-praviṣṭa Śrī Śrīla Saccidānanda Bhaktivinoda Ṭhākura!

All glories to nitya-līlā-praviṣṭa Vaiṣṇava-sārvabhauma Śrīla Jagannātha Dāsa Bābājī Mahārāja!

All glories to the entire Rūpānuga Gauḍīya Guru-varga! All glories to nitya-līlā-praviṣṭa Oṁ Viṣṇupāda Aṣṭottara-śata Śrī Śrīmad Bhaktivedānta Svāmī Mahārāja!

All glories to nitya-līlā-praviṣṭa Oṁ Viṣṇupāda Aṣṭottara-śata Śrī Śrīmad Bhaktivedānta Trivikrama Gosvāmī Mahārāja!

All glories to Gauḍīya Vedāntācārya Śrīla Baladeva Vidyābhūṣaṇa Prabhu! All glories to Śrīla Viśvanātha Cakravartī Ṭhākura!

All glories to the three Lords: Śrīla Narottama, Śrīnivāsa, and Śyāmānanda! All glories to Śrīla Kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa Kavirāja Gosvāmī Prabhu! All glories to Śrīla Vṛndāvana Dāsa Ṭhākura!

All glories to the Six Gosvāmīs: Śrī Rūpa, Sanātana, Bhaṭṭa Raghunātha, Śrī Jīva, Gopāla Bhaṭṭa, and Dāsa Raghunātha!

All glories to the associates of Gaura, including Śrī Svarūpa Dāmodara and Rāya Rāmānanda! All glories to Nāmācārya Śrīla Haridāsa Ṭhākura!

Say with love: All glories to Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya, Prabhu Nityānanda, Śrī Advaita, Gadādhara, Śrīvāsa, and all the devotees of Gaura!

All glories to Śrī Navadvīpa-dhāma, which consists of Antardvīpa (Māyāpura), Sīmantadvīpa, Godrumadvīpa, Madhyadvīpa, Koladvīpa, Ṛtudvīpa, Jahnudvīpa, Modadrumadvīpa, and Rudradvīpa!

All glories to Śrī Vraja-maṇḍala-dhāma, consisting of the twelve forests, Śrī Śrī Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa, the cowherd boys and girls, the cows, and Govardhana Hill!

All glories to Śrī Rādhā-kuṇḍa, Śyāma-kuṇḍa, Gaṅgā, Yamunā, Tulasī, and Bhakti-devī! All glories to Śrī Jagannātha, Baladeva, Subhadrā, and Sudarśana-jīu!

All glories to Śrī Gopīśvara Mahādeva! All glories to Śrī Yogamāyā Paurṇamāsī Devī!

All glories to Śrīmatī Vṛndā Devī! All glories to Śrī Nṛsiṁha and Varāhadeva! All glories to the best of devotees, Śrī Prahlāda Mahārāja!

All glories to the four Dhāmas, the four Sampradāyas, and the Ācāryas! All glories to Vrajapaṭṭana Śrī Caitanya Maṭha!

All glories to Śrī Keśavajī Gauḍīya Maṭha and all its branch Maṭhas! All glories to Śrī Harināma-saṅkīrtana!

All glories to the unlimited millions of Vaiṣṇavas! Chant "Hari! Hari!" in the bliss of Gaura-prema!

IDX.first_line: sri ujjvala rasasara svabhavatah dviprakara
IDX.search: sri ujjvala rasasara svabhavatah dviprakara vipralambha sambhoga akhyana vina vipralambhasraya sambhogera pusti naya tai vipralambhera vidhana purvaraga tatha mana pravasa vaicittyajnana vipralambha cari ta prakara sangamera purvariti labhe purvaraga khyati darsane sravane janma tara
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
śrī⋅ujjvala rasasāra, svabhāvataḥ dviprakāra,
‘vipralambha’ ‘sambhoga’ ākhyāna
vinā vipralambhāśraya, sambhogera puṣṭi naya,
tāi vipralambhera vidhāna

‘pūrva-rāga’ tathā ‘māna’, ‘pravāsa’, ‘vaicittya’jñāna,
vipralambha cāri ta’ prakāra
saṅgamera pūrvarīti, labhe ‘pūrva-rāga’⋅khyāti,
darśane śravaṇe janma tā’ra

anurakta dampatira, abhīṣṭa āśleṣa, sthira⋅,
darśana⋅virodhī bhāva ‘māna’
‘sa-hetu’ ‘nirhetu’ māna, praṇayera pariṇāma,
praṇayera vilāsa pramāṇa

sāma, bhedakriyā, dāne, natyupekṣā⋅suvidhāne,
sahetu⋅mānera upaśama
deśa, kāla, veṇu-rave, nirhetuka mānotsave,
kare atiśīghra uparama

viccheda⋅āśaṅkā haite, premera ‘vaicittya’ citte,
premera svabhāve upajaya
deśa, grāma, vanāntare, priya je pravāsa kare,
‘pravāsa’ ākhya vipralambha haya
IDX.en_translation:
The essence of the brilliant transcendental mellow is naturally of two types: separation and union. Without the foundation of separation, union cannot be fully nourished; therefore, separation is a necessary arrangement.

Prior attraction, transcendental pique, separation due to dwelling elsewhere, and the feeling of novelty despite constant union—these are the four types of separation. The preliminary stage before union attains the renown of "prior attraction"; it is born from seeing and hearing.

For loving couples, the desired embrace, though steady, is opposed by the sentiment of pique. Pique with a cause and pique without a cause are both outcomes of pure affection; they are evidence of love's delightful pastimes.

By means of conciliation, creating a division of interest, giving gifts, and feigned neglect—through these well-arranged methods, pique with a cause is pacified. By considering the place, the time, and the sound of the flute, the festival of causeless pique very quickly comes to an end.

From the apprehension of separation, a feeling of novelty naturally arises from the very nature of love. When the beloved departs for another country, village, or forest, that is the separation known as "absence."

IDX.first_line: sri yamuna snana vandana
IDX.search: sri yamuna snana vandana kalinda tanaye devi paramananda vardhini snami te salile sarvaparadhanmam vimocaya sri radha kunda snana vandana radhika sama saubhagyam sarvatirtha pravanditam prasida radhika kunda snami te salile subhe
