snāmi te salile śubhe śrī⋅śyāma⋅kuṇḍa⋅snāna⋅vandanā udbhutaṁ kṛṣṇa⋅pādābjā⋅dariṣṭa⋅vadhataś⋅chalāt pāhi māṁ pāmaraṁ snāmi śyāma⋅kuṇḍa! jale tava śrī⋅paurṇamāsī⋅vandanā rādheśa⋅keli⋅prabhutā⋅vinoda⋅ vinyāśa⋅vijñāṁ vraja⋅vanditāṅghrim kṛpālutādyakhila⋅viśvandyāṁ śrī⋅paurṇamāsīṁ śirasā namāmi śrī⋅vṛndā⋅devī⋅vandanā vṛndāvana⋅sthira⋅carān paripālayitri! vṛnde! tayorasikayoritisaubhagena āḍhyāsi tat kuru kṛpāṁ gaṇanā jathaiva śrī⋅rādhikā⋅parijaneṣu mamāpi siddhyet śrī⋅saṅkalpa⋅kalpa⋅drumaḥ bhaktyā vihīnā aparādha⋅lakṣaiḥ kṣiptās ca kāmādi⋅taraṅga⋅madhye kṛpāmayi! tvāṁ śaraṇaṁ prapannā vṛnde numaste caraṇāravindam śrī⋅vṛndādevyaṣṭakam śrī⋅vraja⋅vāsi⋅vṛnda⋅vandanā mudā jatra brahmā tṛṇa⋅nikara⋅gulmādiṣu paraṁ sadā kāṅkṣan janmārpita⋅vividha⋅karmāpyanudinam kramād je tatraiva vraja⋅bhuvi vasanti priya⋅janā mayā te te vandyāḥ parama⋅vinayāḥ puṇya⋅khacitāḥ śrī⋅vraja⋅vilāsa⋅stavaḥ śrī⋅gopīśvara⋅śiva⋅vandanā vṛndāvanāvani⋅pate jaya soma soma⋅maule! sanaka⋅sanandana⋅sanātana⋅nāradeḍya gopīśvara vraja⋅vilāsi jugāṅghri⋅padme prema prayaccha nirupādhi namo namaste śrī⋅saṅkalpa⋅kalpa⋅drumaḥ śrī⋅tulasī⋅vandanā mahā⋅prasāda⋅jananī sarva⋅saubhāgya⋅vardhinī ādhi⋅vyādhi⋅harī nityaṁ tulasi! tvaṁ namo’stu te śrī⋅gaṅgā⋅vandanā prabhu⋅krīḍāpātrīmamṛta⋅rasa⋅gātrīm ṛṣi⋅ghaṭā⋅ śiva⋅brahmendrādīḍita⋅mahita⋅māhātmya⋅mukharām lasat⋅kiñjalkāmbhojani⋅madhupa⋅garbhoru⋅karuṇā⋅ mahaṁ vande gaṅgāmagha⋅nikara⋅bhaṅgājala⋅kaṇām śrī⋅vedavyāsa⋅deva⋅vandanā pitā⋅parāśaro jasya śukadevasya jaḥ pitā taṁ vyāsaṁ vadarī⋅vāsaṁ kṛṣṇa⋅dvaipāyanaṁ bhaje śrī⋅siddhānta⋅darpaṇam śrī⋅śukadeva⋅vandanā jaṁ pravajantam⋅anupetam⋅apeta⋅kṛtyaṁ dvaipāyano viraha⋅kātara ājuhāva putreti tan⋅maya⋅tayā taravo’bhinedus⋅ taṁ sarva⋅bhūta⋅hṛdayaṁ munim⋅ānato’smi jaḥ svānubhāvam⋅akhila⋅śruti⋅sāram⋅ekam⋅ adhyātma⋅dīpam⋅atititīrṣatāṁ tamo’ndham saṁsāriṇāṁ karuṇayāha purāṇa⋅guhyaṁ taṁ vyāsa⋅sūnum⋅upayāmi guruṁ munīnām śrī.bhā. 1.2.2-3 śrīmad⋅bhāgavata⋅vandanā tamādi⋅devaṁ karuṇā⋅nidhānaṁ tamāla⋅varṇaṁ su⋅hitāvatāram apāra⋅saṁsāra⋅samudra⋅setuṁ bhajāmahe bhāgavata⋅svarūpam śrī⋅padma⋅purāṇa śrī⋅śrī⋅nāma⋅vandanā jayati jayati nāmānanda⋅rūpaṁ murārer viramita⋅nija⋅dharma⋅dhyāna⋅pūjādi⋅yatnam katham⋅api sakṛd āttaṁ mukti⋅daṁ prāṇināṁ jat paramam⋅amṛtam⋅ekaṁ jīvanaṁ bhūṣaṇaṁ me śrī⋅bṛhad⋅bhāgavatāmṛtama madhura⋅madhuram etan maṅgalāṁ maṅgalānāṁ sakala⋅nigama⋅vallī⋅sat⋅phalaṁ cit⋅svarūpam sakṛd api parigītaṁ śraddhayā helayā vā bhṛgu⋅vara! nara⋅mātraṁ tārayet kṛṣṇa⋅nāma śrī⋅hari⋅bhakti⋅vilāsa śrī⋅śrī⋅jagannātha⋅deva⋅vandanā mahāmbhodhes tīre kanaka⋅rucire nīla⋅śikhare vasan prasādāntaḥ sahaja⋅bala⋅bhadreṇa balinā subhadrā⋅madhyasthaḥ sakala⋅sura⋅sevā⋅vasarado⋅ jagannāthaḥ svāmī nayana⋅pathagāmī bhavatu me śrī⋅śrī⋅jagannāthāṣṭakama nīlācala⋅nivāsāya nityāya paramātmane balabhadra⋅subhadrābhyāṁ jagannāthāya te namaḥ śrī⋅śrī⋅nṛsiŋha⋅deva⋅vandanā namaste narasiŋhāya prahlādāhlāda⋅dāyine hiraṇyakaśipor⋅vakṣaḥ śilā⋅ṭaṅka⋅nakhālaye śrī⋅nṛsiŋha⋅purāṇa ito nṛsiŋhaḥ parato nṛsiŋho jato jato jāmi tato nṛsiŋhaḥ bahir⋅nṛsiŋho hṛdaye nṛsiŋho nṛsiŋham⋅ādiṁ śaraṇaṁ prapadye śrī⋅nṛsiŋha⋅purāṇa śrī⋅nṛsiŋha, jaya nṛsiŋha, jaya jaya nṛsiŋha prahlādeśa jaya padmā⋅mukha⋅padma⋅bhṛṅga vāgīśā jasya vadane lakṣmīr⋅yasya ca vakṣasi jasyāste hṛdaye saṁvit taṁ nṛsiŋham⋅ahaṁ bhaje prahlāda⋅hṛdayā⋅hlādaṁ bhaktāvidyā⋅vidāraṇam śarad⋅indu⋅ruciṁ vande pārīndra⋅vadanaṁ harim śrī⋅bhāvārtha⋅dīpikā śrī⋅śrī⋅rāmacandra⋅vandanā nīlāmbuja⋅śyāmala⋅komalāṅgaṁ sītā⋅samāropita⋅vāma⋅bhāgam pāṇau mahāsāyaka⋅cārucāpaṁ namāmi rāmaṁ raghu⋅vaṁśa⋅nātham śrī⋅rāma⋅carita⋅mānasa dakṣiṇe lakṣmaṇo dhanvī vāmato jānakī śubhā purato mārutīr jasya taṁ namāmi raghūtamam śrī⋅śrī⋅daśāvatāra⋅vandanā vedān uddharate jaganti vahate bhū⋅golam udvibrate daityān dārayate baliṁ chalayate kṣatra⋅kṣayaṁ kurvate paulastyaṁ jayate halaṁ kalayate kāruṇyam⋅ātanvate mlecchān⋅mūrchayate daśākṛti⋅kṛte kṛṣṇāya tubhyaṁ namaḥ śrī⋅gīta⋅govindama
IDX.en_translation: śrī-yamunā-snāna-vandanā O Kalinda-tanayā! O Devī! O increaser of supreme bliss! I bathe in your waters; please liberate me from all offenses. śrī-rādhā-kuṇḍa-snāna-vandanā Possessing good fortune equal to Rādhikā, worshipped by all tīrthas. Be pleased, O Rādhikā-kuṇḍa! I bathe in your auspicious waters. śrī-śyāma-kuṇḍa-snāna-vandanā Manifested from the lotus foot of Kṛṣṇa on the pretext of killing Ariṣṭa. Protect me, a fallen soul; I bathe in your waters, O Śyāma-kuṇḍa! śrī-paurṇamāsī-vandanā Expert in arranging the various playful pastimes of the master of Rādhā, whose lotus feet are worshipped by Vraja. Worshipped by the entire universe for her mercy and other qualities. I bow my head to Śrī Paurṇamāsī. śrī-vṛndā-devī-vandanā O protectress of the moving and non-moving living entities of Vṛndāvana! O Vṛndā! By the good fortune of those two Rasikas, you are wealthy. Please grant mercy so that I may be counted among the attendants of Śrī Rādhikā. Devoid of devotion, tossed by hundreds of thousands of offenses into the waves of lust and other vices. O merciful one! I have taken shelter of you. O Vṛndā, I bow to your lotus feet. śrī-vraja-vāsi-vṛnda-vandanā Where Brahmā joyfully desires birth among the grass and bushes, always daily offering various activities. Those dear people who reside there in the land of Vraja are worshipable by me with supreme humility; they are inlaid with piety. śrī-gopīśvara-śiva-vandanā O master of the land of Vṛndāvana, glories to you, O Soma, O Soma-maule! Worshipped by Sanaka, Sanandana, Sanātana, and Nārada. O Gopīśvara, O enjoyer of Vraja, please bestow unconditional prema at your two lotus feet. Obeisances, obeisances unto you. śrī-tulasī-vandanā Mother of Mahā-prasāda, increaser of all good fortune. Remover of mental and physical diseases eternally, O Tulasī! Obeisances unto you. śrī-gaṅgā-vandanā The vessel of the Lord's pastimes, whose body is nectarean rasa, whose greatness is loudly proclaimed by the assembly of sages, Śiva, Brahmā, and Indra. Filled with great mercy, I worship Gaṅgā, whose drops of water destroy the multitude of sins. śrī-vedavyāsa-deva-vandanā Whose father is Parāśara and who is the father of Śukadeva. I worship that Vyāsa, who resides in Badarī, Kṛṣṇa Dvaipāyana. śrī-śukadeva-vandanā Whom, leaving home without undergoing ceremonies, having no duties, Dvaipāyana called out "O Son!", agitated by separation, and absorbed in him, the trees responded. I bow to that Muni who is the heart of all beings. Who spoke this essence of all scriptures, the unique lamp of knowledge for those desiring to cross the blinding darkness. Who spoke the confidential Purāṇa out of compassion for materialistic people. I surrender to that son of Vyāsa, the guru of munis. śrīmad-bhāgavata-vandanā That primeval Lord, the abode of mercy, the color of a Tamāla tree, the most beneficial incarnation. The bridge over the boundless ocean of material existence. We worship the Śrīmad Bhāgavatam. śrī-śrī-nāma-vandanā Glories, glories to the blissful form of the name of Murāri, which causes one to give up efforts in prescribed duties, meditation, and pūjā. Which, if taken even once somehow or other, bestows liberation to living entities. My supreme nectar, my only life and ornament. Sweeter than sweet, the most auspicious of auspicious things, the excellent fruit of the creeper of all Vedas, conscious by nature. Chanted even once, with faith or with negligence, O best of the Bhṛgus! The name of Kṛṣṇa delivers any human being. śrī-śrī-jagannātha-deva-vandanā On the shore of the great ocean, on the blue summit shining like gold. Residing inside the palace with his brother Balabhadra. Between them is Subhadrā; giving opportunity for service to all suras. May that Jagannātha Svāmī be visible to my eyes. To the resident of Nīlācala, the eternal Paramātmā. To Balabhadra and Subhadrā, and to you Jagannātha, obeisances. śrī-śrī-nṛsiṁha-deva-vandanā Obeisances to Narasiṁha, who gives joy to Prahlāda. Whose nails are like chisels on the stone-like chest of Hiraṇyakaśipu. Narasiṁha is here, Narasiṁha is there. Wherever I go, there is Narasiṁha. Narasiṁha is outside, Narasiṁha is in the heart. I take shelter of Narasiṁha, the origin. Śrī Narasiṁha, glories to Narasiṁha, glories glories to Narasiṁha. Lord of Prahlāda, glory to the bee of the lotus face of Padmā. On whose face is Vāgīśā, on whose chest is Lakṣmī. In whose heart is knowledge, I worship that Narasiṁha. The joy of Prahlāda's heart, the destroyer of the devotees' ignorance. Resplendent like the autumn moon, I bow to the lion-faced Hari. śrī-śrī-rāmacandra-vandanā Whose limbs are soft and dark like a blue lotus, with Sītā seated on his left side. Holding a great arrow and beautiful bow in his hands, I bow to Rāma, the lord of the Raghu dynasty. On the right is Lakṣmaṇa holding a bow, on the left is the auspicious Jānakī. In front is Mārutī, I bow to that best of the Raghus. śrī-śrī-daśāvatāra-vandanā You deliver the Vedas, support the worlds, lift the earth, tear apart the demon, deceive Bali, destroy the warrior class, conquer Paulastya, hold the plow, extend mercy, and bewilder the mlecchas. I offer obeisances to You, Kṛṣṇa, who assume these ten forms.

IDX.first_line: sridama bale ogo rani vidaya de tora nilamani
IDX.search: sridama bale ogo rani vidaya de tora nilamani laye jaba gostha vihare godhana carana kari ami diba tomara hari nivedana kari joda kare rani bale ki balili na pathava vanamali tomara sabao jao vane bada hale lalane laye jeo kanane pathaiba toma saba sane
IDX.author: jñānadāsa
IDX.verses: śrīdāma bale ogo rāṇī, vidāya de tora nīlamaṇi laye jāba goṣṭha vihāre godhana cāraṇa kari, āmi diba tomāra hari nivedana kari joḍa kare rāṇī bale ki balili, nā pāṭhāva vanamālī tomārā sabāo jāo vane baḍa ha’le lālane, la’ye jeo kānane pāṭhāiba tomā sabā sane śunare śrīdāma bhāi, āmāra jāoyā ha’la nāi mā vidāya nāhi dila more jñāna⋅dāsa kahe śuna, jaśodāra jīvana jāni ki nā jāni vidāya kare
IDX.en_translation:
Śrīdāma says: "O Queen! Bid farewell to your Blue Jewel. I will take Him for pastimes in the pasture. Grazing the cows, I will give (back) your Hari. I make this request with folded hands."

The Queen says: "What did you say? I will not send Vanamālī. You all go to the forest too. When He grows up, take Him to the forest with affection. I will send Him with all of you."

"Listen, O brother Śrīdāma! My going has not happened. Mother did not give me farewell." Jñāna Dāsa says: "Listen, I know Yaśodā's life; I do not know if she will bid farewell or not."

IDX.first_line: srikrsnacaitanya balarama nityananda
IDX.search: srikrsnacaitanya balarama nityananda parisada sange avatara golokera premadhana sabare jacina dila na loinu muni duracara are pamara mana marame rahala bada sela sankirtana prema badale saba hiya dubala mohe vidhi vancita kaila
IDX.author: govinda dāsa
IDX.verses:
śrī-kṛṣṇa-caitanya, balarāma nityānanda,
pāriṣada⋅saṅge avatāra
golokera prema-dhana, sabāre jāciñā dila,
nā loinu muñi durācāra

āre pāmara mana, marame rahala baḍa śela
saṅkīrtana⋅prema⋅bādale, saba hiyā ḍubala,
mohe vidhi vañcita kaila

śrī⋅guru⋅vaiṣṇavapada, kalpataru⋅chāyā pāñā,
saba jīva tāpa pāsarila
muñi abhāgiyā viṣa⋅, viṣaye mātiyā roinu,
hena juge nistāra nā haila

āgune puḍiyā maro, jale paraveśa karo,
viṣa khāñā maro mo pāpiyā
ei mata kari’ jadi, maraṇa nā kare vidhi,
prāṇa rahe ki sukha lāgiyā

e hena gaurāṅga⋅guṇa, nā karinu śravaṇa,
hāya hāya, kari hā hutāśa
‘hare kṛṣṇa’⋅mahāmantra, mukha bhari’ nā loilāma,
jīvanmṛta govindadāsa
IDX.en_translation:
Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya and Nityānanda, along with Their associates, have descended to this world. They distributed the treasure of divine love from Goloka to everyone, but I, a wicked soul, did not accept it.

O my wretched mind! A great spear remains in my heart. In the shower of saṅkīrtana and divine love, all hearts were immersed, but destiny has cheated me through illusion.

Having attained the shade of the wish-fulfilling tree of the feet of the Guru and the Vaiṣṇavas, all living entities forgot their miseries. I, an unfortunate soul, remained absorbed in poisonous sense objects, and thus I did not attain deliverance in this age.

I would rather die by fire, drown in water, or die by eating poison. If destiny does not grant me death in this way, for what happiness does my life still remain?

I did not hear the glorious qualities of such a Lord Gaurāṅga. Alas, I cry out in lamentation! I did not chant the Hare Kṛṣṇa Mahā-mantra to my heart's content. I am but the living dead, says Govinda Dāsa.

IDX.first_line: srikrsnavirahe radhikara dasa
IDX.search: srikrsnavirahe radhikara dasa ami ta sahite nari jugala milana sukhera karana jivana chadite pari radhika carana tyajiya amara ksaneke pralaya haya radhikara tare satabara mari se duhkha amara saya
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
śrī-kṛṣṇa-virahe, rādhikāra daśā,
āmi ta’ sahite nāri
jugala⋅milana, sukhera kāraṇa,
jīvana chāḍite pāri

rādhikā⋅caraṇa, tyajiyā āmāra,
kṣaṇeke pralaya haya
rādhikāra tare, śatabāra mari,
se⋅duḥkha āmāra saya

e hena rādhāra, caraṇa⋅yugale,
paricaryā pā’ba kabe
hā hā vraja⋅jana, more dayā kari’,
kabe vraja-vane la’be

vilāsa-mañjarī, anaṅgamañjarī,
śrī⋅rūpamañjarī āra
āmāke tuliyā, laha nija-pade,
deha mora siddhi⋅sāra
IDX.en_translation:
The condition of Rādhikā in separation from Śrī Kṛṣṇa - I cannot tolerate it. For the sake of the happiness of the divine couple's union, I can give up my life.

Abandoning Rādhikā's feet even for a moment, is a great distress for me. For Rādhikā's sake, I can die a hundred times; I will endure that agony.

When will I attain the service At the lotus feet of such a Rādhā? Alas, alas, O people of Vraja, showing mercy to me, When will you take me to the forest of Vraja?

O Vilāsa Mañjarī, Anaṅga Mañjarī, And Śrī Rūpa Mañjarī, Please lift me up and take me to your feet, Grant me the essence of ultimate perfection.

IDX.first_line: srikrsnavirahe radhikara dasaami ta sahite narijugala milana sukhera karanajivana chadite pari
IDX.search: srikrsnavirahe radhikara dasaami ta sahite narijugala milana sukhera karanajivana chadite pari radhika carana tyajiya amaraksaneke pralaya hayaradhikara tare satabara marise duhkha amara saya
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
śrī-kṛṣṇa-virahe, rādhikāra daśā,āmi ta’ sahite nārijugala⋅milana, sukhera kāraṇa,jīvana chāḍite pāri

rādhikā⋅caraṇa, tyajiyā āmāra,kṣaṇeke pralaya hayarādhikāra tare, śatabāra mari,se⋅duḥkha āmāra saya

e hena rādhāra, caraṇa⋅yugale,paricaryā pā’ba kabehā hā vraja⋅jana, more dayā kari’,kabe vraja-vane la’be

vilāsa-mañjarī, anaṅgamañjarī,śrī⋅rūpamañjarī āraāmāke tuliyā, laha nija-pade,deha mora siddhi⋅sāra
IDX.en_translation:
The condition of Rādhikā in separation from Śrī Kṛṣṇa - I cannot tolerate it. For the sake of the happiness of the divine couple’s union, I can give up my life.

Abandoning Rādhikā’s feet even for a moment, is a great distress for me. For Rādhikā’s sake, I can die a hundred times; I will endure that agony.

When will I attain the service At the lotus feet of such a Rādhā? Alas, alas, O people of Vraja, showing mercy to me, When will you take me to the forest of Vraja?

O Vilāsa Mañjarī, Anaṅga Mañjarī, And Śrī Rūpa Mañjarī, Please lift me up and take me to your feet, Grant me the essence of ultimate perfection.

IDX.first_line: srimati radhika krsnakanta siromani
IDX.search: srimati radhika krsnakanta siromani krsnapriya madhye tara sama nahi dhani munigana sastre radha kundera varnane gandharvika tulya kunda karaye ganane
IDX.author: śrīla bhaktisiddhānta sarasvatī ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
śrīmatī rādhikā⋅kṛṣṇakāntā⋅śiromaṇi
kṛṣṇapriya⋅madhye tā̐’ra sama nāhi dhanī

munigaṇa śāstre rādhā⋅kuṇḍera varṇane
gāndharvikā⋅tulya kuṇḍa karaye gaṇane

nāradādi⋅priyavarge je prema durlabha
anya sādhakete tāhā kabhu nā sulabha

kintu, rādhā⋅kuṇḍe snāna jei jana kare
madhura rasete tā̐’ra snāne siddhi dhare

aprākṛta⋅bhāve sadā jugala⋅sevana
rādhā⋅pāda⋅padma labhe sei harijana

śrī⋅vārṣabhānavī kabe dayitadāsere
kuṇḍa⋅tīre sthāna dibe nija⋅jana ka’re

‘upadeśāmṛta’ dhari’ rūpānuga⋅bhāve
jīvana jāpile kṛṣṇa⋅kṛpā sei pā’be

satya, tretā, dvāparera je⋅sakala bhakta
kṛṣṇakṛpā labhiyāche gṛhastha⋅virakta

bhāvī⋅kāle, vartamāne bhaktera samāja
sakalera padarajaḥ jāce dīna āja

bhakativinoda⋅prabhu anuga je⋅jana
dayita⋅dāsera tā’ra pade nivedana

dayā kari’, doṣa hari’, bala ‘hari! hari!’
‘upadeśāmṛta’⋅vāri śiropari dhari’
IDX.en_translation:
Śrīmatī Rādhikā is the crest-jewel of Kṛṣṇa's beloveds. Among Kṛṣṇa's beloveds, there is no one as rich in love as Her.

In the scriptures describing Rādhā-kuṇḍa, the sages count the Kuṇḍa as equal to Gāndharvikā (Rādhā).

The love that is rare even for the dear devotees like Nārada is never easily attainable by other practitioners.

But one who bathes in Rādhā-kuṇḍa attains perfection in that sweet mellow through his bath.

Serving the Divine Couple eternally in a transcendental mood, that devotee of Hari attains the lotus feet of Rādhā.

When will Śrī Vārṣabhānavī, accepting him as Her own, give Dayita-dāsa a place on the bank of the Kuṇḍa?

If one passes his life holding the 'Upadeśāmṛta' in the mood of a follower of Rūpa Gosvāmī, he will obtain Kṛṣṇa's mercy.

All the devotees of Satya, Tretā, and Dvāpara yugas who were detached householders attained Kṛṣṇa's mercy.

Today, this lowly person begs for the dust of the feet of all the devotees in the society of devotees, both in the future and the present.

Whoever is a follower of Bhaktivinoda Prabhu—Dayita-dāsa makes this submission at his feet.

Be merciful, remove my faults, and say "Hari! Hari!", holding the water of 'Upadeśāmṛta' upon my head.

IDX.first_line: srivasa vacana sravana kariya
IDX.search: srivasa vacana sravana kariya sadhvi pativratagana soka parihari mrta sisu rakhi hari rase dila mana srivasa takhana anande matiya angane aila punah nace gora sane sakala pasari gaya nandasuta guna
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
śrīvāsa⋅vacana, śravaṇa kariyā,
sādhvī pativratāgaṇa
śoka parihari’, mṛta śiśu rākhi’,
hari⋅rase dila mana

śrīvāsa takhana, ānande mātiyā,
aṅgane āila punaḥ
nāce gorā⋅sane, sakala pāsari,
gāya nandasuta⋅guṇa

cāri daṇḍa rātre, marila kumāra,
aṅgane keha nā jāne
śrī⋅nāma⋅maṅgale, tṛtīya prahara,
rajanī atīta gāne

kīrtana bhāṅgile, kahe gaura⋅hari,
āji kena pāi duḥkha ?
bujhi, ei gṛhe, kichu amaṅgala,
ghaṭiyā harila sukha

tabe bhakta-jana, nivedana kare,
śrīvāsa⋅śiśura kathā
śuni’ gorārāya, bale, hāya hāya,
marame pāinu vyathā

keha nā kahile, āmāre takhana,
vipada⋅saṁvāda sabe
bhakativinoda, bhakata⋅vatsala,
snehete majila tabe
IDX.en_translation:
Hearing Śrīvāsa's words, the chaste and devoted wives gave up their grief; leaving the dead child, they gave their minds to the nectar of the Lord's service.

Śrīvāsa then, intoxicated with bliss, came back to the courtyard. He danced with Lord Caitanya, forgetting everything, and sang the glories of the son of Nanda.

Four units of time into the night, the boy died, but no one in the courtyard knew. The late night passed in the auspicious chanting of the Holy Name.

When the congregational chanting ended, Lord Caitanya said, "Why do I feel sorrow today? I understand that some inauspiciousness has occurred in this house, taking away the happiness."

Then the devotees related the story of Śrīvāsa's child. Hearing this, the Lord said, "Alas, alas! I feel pain in My heart!

"Why did no one tell Me the news of this calamity then?" Bhaktivinoda says: the Lord, who is affectionate to His devotees, was then immersed in love.

IDX.first_line: srivase kahena prabhu tuhu mora dasa
IDX.search: srivase kahena prabhu tuhu mora dasa tuya prite badha ami jagate prakasa bhaktagana senapati srivasa pandita jagate ghusuka aji tomara carita
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
śrīvāse kahena prabhu,⋅“tuhu̐ mora dāsa
tuyā prīte bā̐dhā āmi jagate prakāśa

bhaktagaṇa⋅senāpati⋅śrīvāsa⋅paṇḍita
jagate ghuṣuka āji tomāra carita

prapañca⋅kārā⋅rakṣiṇī māyāra bandhana
tomāra nāhika kabhu, dekhuka jagajjana

dhana, jana, deha, geha āmāre arpiyā
āmāra sevāra sukhe ācha sukhī hañā

mama līlāpuṣṭi lāgi’ tomāra saṁsāra
śikhuka gṛhastha⋅jana tomāra ācāra

tava preme baddha āchi āmi, nityānanda
āmā⋅du̐he suta jāni’ bhuñjaha ānanda

nityasattva suta jā’ra, anitya tanaye
āsakti nā kare sei sṛjane pralaye

bhaktite tomāra ṛṇī āmi ciradina
tava sādhu-bhāve tumi kṣama mora ṛṇa”

śrīvāsera pā⋅ya bhaktivinoda kujana
kākuti kariyā māge gaurāṅga⋅caraṇa
IDX.en_translation:
The Lord said to Śrīvāsa, "You are My servant. I am revealed in the world, bound by your love.

O Śrīvāsa Paṇḍita, general of the devotees! Let your character be proclaimed throughout the world today.

The bondage of illusion, which keeps the soul in the prison of the material world, never affects you; let all the world see this.

Offering wealth, followers, body, and home to Me, you remain happy in the joy of My service.

For the nourishment of My pastimes, let all householders learn from your life and conduct.

I am bound by your love, and so is Nityānanda. Knowing us both as your sons, enjoy bliss.

One whose son is the eternal truth does not become attached to transient sons in creation or dissolution.

In devotion, I am indebted to you forever. Through your saintly nature, please forgive My debt."

Bhaktivinoda, the lowly one, at the feet of Śrīvāsa, earnestly begs for the lotus feet of Lord Caitanya.

IDX.first_line: srivasera prati caitanya prasada
IDX.search: srivasera prati caitanya prasada dekhiya sakala jana jaya sricaitanya jaya nityananda bali nace ghana ghana srivasa mandire ki bhava uthila taha ki varnana haya bhavayuddha sane ananda krandana uthe krsna premamaya
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
śrīvāsera prati, caitanya⋅prasāda,
dekhiyā sakala jana
jaya śrī-caitanya, jaya nityānanda,
bali’ nāce ghana ghana

śrīvāsa⋅mandire, ki bhāva uṭhila,
tāhā ki varṇana haya
bhāvayuddha⋅sane, ānanda⋅krandana,
uṭhe kṛṣṇa⋅premamaya

cāri bhāi paḍi’, prabhura caraṇe,
prema⋅gadagada⋅svare
kā̐diyā kā̐diyā, kākuti kariyā,
gaḍi’ jāya prema-bhare

“ohe prāṇeśvara, e hena vipada,
pratidina jena haya
jāhāte tomāra, caraṇa⋅yugale,
āsakti bāḍite raya

vipada⋅sampade, sei dina bhāla,
je⋅dina tomāre smari
tomāra smaraṇa⋅, rahita je⋅dina,
se⋅dina vipada, hari”

śrīvāsa⋅goṣṭhīra, caraṇe paḍiyā,
bhaktivinoda bhaṇe
tomādera gorā, kṛpā vitariyā,
dekhāo durgata jane
IDX.en_translation:
Seeing the mercy shown to Śrīvāsa by Lord Caitanya, all the people repeatedly danced, chanting: "All glories to Śrī Caitanya! All glories to Nityānanda!"

What emotions arose in the temple of Śrīvāsa? How can they ever be described? Amidst a war of ecstasies, the crying of joy, full of love for Kṛṣṇa, arose.

The four brothers fell at the Lord's feet with voices choked with love. Weeping and pleading, they rolled on the ground in great love.

"O Lord of my life! May such distress happen every day, so that my attachment to Your lotus feet may continuously increase.

In distress or prosperity, that day is good when I remember You. The day devoid of Your remembrance—that day is true distress, O Lord!"

Falling at the feet of Śrīvāsa's associates, Bhaktivinoda says: "May your Lord Caitanya, distributing His mercy, reveal Himself to all distressed souls."

IDX.first_line: sthayibhavavista citta paiya vibhavavitta
IDX.search: sthayibhavavista citta paiya vibhavavitta udbhata bhavete apanara pranavrtte nyasa kare prana sei nyasabhare deha prati vikrti calaya vaivarnya romanca sveda stambha kampa svarabheda pralayasru e astavikara sancari je bhavacaya harsamarsa ara bhaya visada vismayadi tara
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
sthāyibhāvāviṣṭa⋅citta, pāiyā vibhāvavitta,
udbhaṭa bhāvete āpanāra
prāṇa-vṛtte nyāsa kare, prāṇa sei nyāsabhare,
deha⋅prati vikṛti cālāya

vaivarṇya, romāñca, sveda, stambha, kampa, svara-bheda,
pralayāśru e aṣṭavikāra
sañcārī je bhāvacaya, harṣāmarṣa āra bhaya,
viṣāda, vismayādi tā’ra

pravṛttikāraṇa haya, līlākāle rase laya,
āpane karāya anukṣaṇa
dhūmāyitā, ujjvalitā, dīptā āra se uddīpta,
ei cāri avasthā lakṣaṇa

jā’ra jei adhikāra, sāttvika vikāra tā’ra,
se lakṣaṇe haya ta’ udaya
mahābhāva⋅daśā jathā, su⋅uddīpta bhāva tathā,
anāyāse sulakṣita haya
IDX.en_translation:
A mind absorbed in the permanent sentiment, upon receiving stimuli, spontaneously places itself in an extraordinary emotional state within the life-airs. The life-airs, thus filled, then cause transformations in the body.

Discoloration, horripilation, perspiration, stupefaction, trembling, change of voice, and tears—these are the eight transformations. Transient emotions like joy, indignation, fear, despondency, wonder, and others also arise.

They become the cause of actions, and during the divine pastimes, they merge into transcendental mellows, constantly manifesting themselves. Obscured, glowing, blazing, and fully manifest—these are the symptoms of the four stages.

According to one's qualification, their physical transformations indeed manifest with those symptoms. Just as in the state of highest ecstatic love, the highly inflamed emotional state is easily and clearly recognized.

IDX.first_line: stutiyogya tumi prabhu navaghana syama
IDX.search: stutiyogya tumi prabhu navaghana syama vijuri ujjvala pitavastra paridhama navagunja avatamsa sravana bhusana sikhanda mandita kesa prasanna vadana
IDX.author: śrīla raghunātha bhāgabatācārya
IDX.verses:
stutiyogya tumi prabhu, navaghana śyāma
vijurī ujjvala⋅pītavastra⋅paridhāma

navaguñjā⋅avataṁsa śravaṇa⋅bhūṣaṇa
śikhaṇḍa⋅maṇḍita keśa prasanna⋅vadana

ājānulambita vanamālā vilolita
veṇu, vetra, viṣāṇa, kabala virājita

amala⋅kamala jini’ caraṇasundara
namo namo nandagopa⋅suta manohara

kṛṣṇa, kṛṣṇa vṛṣṇikula⋅puṣkara⋅bhāṣkara
pṛthvī⋅deva⋅dvija⋅paśu⋅sindhu⋅śaśadhara

uddharma⋅śārvara⋅hara asura⋅saŋhārī
arka⋅ādi⋅sarvasura⋅pujya adhikārī

ākalparyanta mora rahu namaskāra
ei vara māgo, nātha, caraṇe tomāra

tomāra padāravinda sevo nirantara
ei ājñā kara more karuṇāsāgara

(śrī⋅kṛṣṇaprema⋅taraṅgiṇī)
IDX.en_translation:
You are worthy of praise, O Lord! Dark like a fresh cloud. Dressed in yellow garments bright like lightning.

Ornaments of fresh Guñjā decorating Your ears. Hair adorned with peacock feathers, joyful face.

A forest garland hanging down to Your knees, swinging. Resplendent with flute, stick, horn, and morsel.

Beautiful feet surpassing the spotless lotus. Obeisances, obeisances to the enchanting son of Nanda Gopa.

Kṛṣṇa, Kṛṣṇa! Sun of the lotus of the Vṛṣṇi dynasty. Moon of the ocean of the earth, gods, brāhmaṇas, and cattle.

Destroyer of the night of irreligion, slayer of the demons. The Authority worshipable by the Sun and all the gods.

Let my obeisances remain until the end of the kalpa. I beg this boon, O Lord, at Your feet.

May I serve Your lotus feet constantly. Give me this order, O Ocean of Mercy.

(Excerpt from Śrī Kṛṣṇa-prema-taraṅgiṇī)

IDX.first_line: sucaru vaktramandalam
IDX.search: sucaru vaktramandalam sukarna ratnakundalam sucarcitanga candanam namami nandanandanam sudirgha netrapankajam sikhi sikhanda murdhajam anangakoti mohanam namami nandanandanam
IDX.verses:
sucāru⋅vaktramaṇḍalaṁ
sukarṇa⋅ratnakuṇḍalam
sucarcitāṅga⋅candanaṁ
namāmi nanda-nandanam

sudīrgha⋅netrapaṅkajaṁ
śikhi⋅śikhaṇḍa⋅mūrdhajam
anaṅgakoṭi⋅mohanaṁ
namāmi nanda-nandanam

sunāsikāgra⋅mauktikaṁ
svacchanda⋅danta⋅paṅktikam
navāmvudāṅga⋅cikkaṇaṁ
namāmi nanda-nandanam

kareṇa veṇurañjitaṁ
gati⋅karīndragañjitam
dukūla⋅pīta⋅śobhanaṁ
namāmi nanda-nandanam

tribhaṅga⋅deha⋅sundaraṁ
nakhadyuti⋅sudhākaram
amūlya⋅ratna⋅bhūṣaṇaṁ
namāmi nanda-nandanam

sugandha⋅aṅgasaurabham⋅
urovirāji⋅kaustubham
sphuracchrīvatsa⋅lāñchanaṁ
namāmi nanda-nandanam

vṛndāvana⋅sunāgaraṁ
vilāsānuga⋅vāsasam
surendragarva⋅mocanaṁ
namāmi nanda⋅nandanam

vrajāṅganā⋅sunāyakaṁ
sadā sukha⋅pradāyakam
jaganmanaḥ⋅pralobhanaṁ
namāmi nanda-nandanam

śrī-nanda-nandanāṣṭakaṁ
paṭhed jaḥ śraddhayānvitaḥ
taredbhavāvdhiṁ dustaraṁ
labhettadaṅghri⋅yugmakam
IDX.en_translation:
I bow to the son of Nanda, whose face is beautiful, whose ears are adorned with jeweled earrings, and whose body is smeared with beautiful sandalwood paste.

I bow to the son of Nanda, whose eyes are long and lotus-like, whose hair is adorned with a peacock feather, and who enchants millions of Cupids.

I bow to the son of Nanda, who has a pearl on the tip of His beautiful nose, beautiful rows of teeth, and a body smooth and dark like a fresh rain cloud.

I bow to the son of Nanda, whose hands are beautified by a flute, whose gait shames a regal elephant, and who is adorned with shining yellow garments.

I bow to the son of Nanda, whose beautiful body has three graceful bends, whose nails shine like the moon, and who is adorned with priceless jewels.

I bow to the son of Nanda, who possesses a fragrant bodily scent, who has the Kaustubha jewel shining on His chest, and who is marked with the glittering Śrīvatsa mark.

I bow to the son of Nanda, who is a clever hero of Vṛndāvana, who is adorned with playful garments, and who humbles the pride of the king of demigods.

I bow to the son of Nanda, who is the beautiful leader of the cowherd women, who always bestows happiness, and who enchants the minds of all the worlds.

Whoever recites this eight-verse prayer to the son of Nanda with faith will cross the insurmountable ocean of material existence and attain His lotus feet.

IDX.first_line: sujanarbuda radhita pada yugam
IDX.search: sujanarbuda radhita pada yugam jugadharma dhurandhara patra varam varadabhaya dayaka pujya padam pranamami sada prabhupada padam bhajanorjita sajjana sangha patim patitadhika karunikaikagatim gativancita vancakacintya padam pranamami sada prabhupada padam
IDX.author: śrīla bhaktirakṣaka śrīdhara gosvāmī mahārāja
IDX.verses:
sujanārbuda⋅rādhita⋅pāda⋅yugaṁ
jugadharma⋅dhurandhara⋅pātra⋅varam
varadābhaya⋅dāyaka⋅pūjya⋅padaṁ
praṇamāmi sadā prabhupāda⋅padam

bhajanorjita⋅sajjana⋅saṅgha⋅patiṁ
patitādhika⋅kāruṇikaikagatim
gativañcita⋅vañcakācintya⋅padaṁ
praṇamāmi sadā prabhupāda⋅padam

atikomala⋅kāñcana⋅dīrghatanuṁ
tanunindita⋅hema⋅mṛṇālamadam
madanārbuda⋅vandita⋅candra⋅padaṁ
praṇamāmi sadā prabhupāda⋅padam

nijasevaka⋅tāraka⋅rañjitavidhuṁ
vidhutāhita⋅huṅkṛta⋅siŋha⋅varam
varaṇāgata⋅bāliśa⋅śanda⋅padaṁ
praṇamāmi sadā prabhupāda⋅padam

vipulīkṛta⋅vaibhava⋅gaura⋅bhuvaṁ
bhuvaneṣu vikīrtita⋅gaura⋅dayam
dayanīyagaṇārpita⋅gaura⋅padaṁ
praṇamāmi sadā prabhupāda⋅padam

ciragaura⋅janāśraya⋅viśvaguruṁ
guru⋅gaura⋅kiśoraka⋅dāsya⋅param
paramādṛta⋅bhaktivinoda⋅padaṁ
praṇamāmi sadā prabhupāda⋅padam

raghu⋅rūpa⋅sanātana⋅kīrtidharaṁ
dharaṇītala⋅kīrtita⋅jīva⋅kavim
kavirāja⋅narottama⋅sakhya⋅padaṁ
praṇamāmi sadā prabhupāda⋅padam

kṛpayā⋅harikīrtana⋅mūrti⋅dharaṁ
dharaṇī⋅bharahāraka⋅gaura⋅janam
janakādhika⋅vatsala⋅snigdha⋅padaṁ
praṇamāmi sadā prabhupāda⋅padam

śaraṇāgata⋅kiṅkara⋅kalpataruṁ
tarudhikkṛta⋅dhīra⋅vadānya⋅varam
varadendra⋅gaṇārcita⋅divya⋅padaṁ
praṇamāmi sadā prabhupāda⋅padam

parahaṁsavaraṁ paramārtha⋅patiṁ
patitoddharaṇe kṛta⋅veśayatim
jatirājagaṇaiḥ parisevya⋅padaṁ
praṇamāmi sadā prabhupāda⋅padam

vṛṣabhānusutā⋅dayitānucaraṁ
caraṇāśrita⋅reṇudharastamaham
mahadadbhuta⋅pāvana⋅śakti⋅padaṁ
praṇamāmi sadā prabhupāda⋅padam
IDX.en_translation:
I always offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Prabhupāda, which are worshiped by millions of good people—the best of personalities who uphold the religious principles for this age, and whose worshipable feet grant boons and fearlessness.

I always offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Prabhupāda, the leader of the assembly of pure devotees enriched by worship, the sole shelter for those who are most fallen and compassionate, and whose inconceivable lotus feet are the destination for those deprived of true progress and even for those who have been deceived.

I always offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Prabhupāda, whose long, extremely tender body is like molten gold, who defies the pride of golden lotus stems with his complexion, and whose moon-like feet are adored by millions of Cupids.

I always offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Prabhupāda, who is like a moon delighting his own stars of servants, who is like a supreme lion roaring against the inimical, and whose lotus feet are a shelter for the foolish who seek refuge.

I always offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Prabhupāda, who has extensively glorified the land of Lord Gaura, whose mercy is widely distributed throughout the universes, and whose lotus feet are offered to the pitiable souls by the Lord Himself.

I always offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Prabhupāda, who is the world-teacher and the eternal shelter of the lineage of Lord Gaura, who is devoted to the service of his own spiritual master, Gaura Kiśora, and whose lotus feet are supremely revered by Bhaktivinoda.

I always offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Prabhupāda, who upholds the glories of the Gosvāmīs—Raghunātha, Rūpa, and Sanātana—the poet of the souls whose glories are sung on earth, and whose lotus feet are befriended by Kavirāja and Narottama.

I always offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Prabhupāda, who, out of mercy, has embodied the chanting of the glories of God, who removes the burden of the earth, and whose lotus feet are more affectionate and loving than those of a father.

I always offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Prabhupāda, who is a wish-fulfilling tree for surrendered servants, who is superior to any ordinary tree—being steadfast, benevolent, and the best—and whose divine lotus feet are worshiped by the best of boon-givers.

I always offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Prabhupāda, who is the best among the highest renunciants and the master of the ultimate truth, who has taken the vow of an ascetic for the upliftment of the fallen, and whose lotus feet are served by the kings of ascetics.

I always offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Prabhupāda, who is the dear follower of the daughter of Vṛṣabhānu. I am merely a speck of dust at his lotus feet, which possess a greatly wonderful purifying power.

IDX.first_line: sukhera lagiya e ghara badhinu
IDX.search: sukhera lagiya e ghara badhinu agune pudiya gela amiya sagare sinana karite sakali garala bhela sakhi ki mora kapale lekhi sitala baliya cada sevinu bhanura kirana dekhi
IDX.author: śrī⋅caṇḍīdāsa
IDX.verses:
sukhera lāgiyā, e ghara bā̐dhinu,
āgune puḍiyā gela
amiyā⋅sāgare, sināna karite,
sakali garala bhela

sakhi, ki mora kapāle lekhi
śītala baliyā, cā̐da sevinu,
bhānura kiraṇa dekhi

ucala baliyā, acale caḍinu,
paḍinu agādha jale
lachamī cāhite, dāridrya beḍhala,
māṇika hārānu hele

nagara basālāma, sāgara bā̐dhilāma,
māṇika pābāra āśe
sāgara śukāla, māṇika lukāla,
abhāgī⋅karama⋅doṣe

piyāsa lāgiyā, jalada sevinu,
vajara paḍiyā gela
kahe caṇḍīdāsa, śyāmera pirīti,
marame rahala śela
IDX.en_translation:
For the sake of happiness, I built this house, but it burned down in a fire. I went to bathe in the ocean of nectar, but everything turned to poison.

O friend! What is written in my fate? Thinking it would be cool, I worshipped the moon, but instead, I saw the scorching rays of the sun.

Thinking it was high ground, I climbed the mountain, only to fall into deep water. Seeking wealth, my poverty instead increased, and I carelessly lost the jewel I had.

I established a city and bound the ocean, hoping to find a precious gem. But the ocean dried up and the jewel remained hidden, all due to the fault of my unfortunate past actions.

Feeling thirsty, I worshipped the cloud, but instead, a thunderbolt struck. Chaṇḍīdāsa says: "My love for Lord Śyāma has remained like a spear in my heart."

IDX.first_line: suna he rasika jana krsnagana aganana
IDX.search: suna he rasika jana krsnagana aganana ananta kahite nahi pare krsna jagatera guru krsna vanchakalpataru navika se bhava parabare hrdaya pidita jara krsna cikitsaka tara bhava roga nasite catura krsna bahirmukha jane premamrta vitarane krame laya nija antahpura
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
śuna, he rasika jana, kṛṣṇa-gaṇa agaṇana,
ananta kahite nāhi pāre
kṛṣṇa jagatera guru, kṛṣṇa vāñchā-kalpa-taru,
nāvika se bhava⋅pārābāre

hṛdaya⋅pīḍita jā’ra, kṛṣṇa⋅cikitsaka tā’ra,
bhava⋅roga nāśite catura
kṛṣṇa bahirmukha⋅jane, premāmṛta⋅vitaraṇe,
krame laya nija antaḥpura

karmabandha⋅jñānabandha⋅, āveśe mānava andha,
tā’re kṛṣṇa karuṇā⋅sāgara
pāda⋅padma⋅madhu diyā, andhabhāva ghucāiyā,
caraṇe karena anucara

vidhimārgarata⋅jane, svādhīnatā ratnadāne,
rāgamārge karāna praveśa
rāga⋅vaśavartī ha’ye, pārakīya⋅bhāvāśraye,
labhe jīva kṛṣṇa-premāveśa

premāmṛta⋅vāridhārā, sadā pānarata tā̐’rā,
kṛṣṇa tā̐hādera bandhu, pati
seisaba vraja-jana, sukalyāṇa⋅niketana,
dīna hīna vinodera gati
IDX.en_translation:
Listen, O discerning people! The qualities of Kṛṣṇa are innumerable and infinite; no one can describe them all. Kṛṣṇa is the spiritual master of the world and the wish-fulfilling tree; He is the helmsman in the ocean of material existence.

For those whose hearts are distressed, Kṛṣṇa is the physician, expert in destroying the disease of material existence. For those who are averse to Him, He distributes the nectar of love and gradually takes them into His inner chamber.

Human beings are blinded by their absorption in the bondage of fruitive action and speculative knowledge; for them, Kṛṣṇa is an ocean of mercy. By giving them the honey of His lotus feet and removing their blindness, He makes them His servants.

To those engaged in the path of rules, He bestows the jewel of independence and grants them entry into the path of spontaneous love. Becoming subservient to His spontaneous love and taking shelter of the mood of paramount love, the soul attains absorption in love for Kṛṣṇa.

They are always engaged in drinking the current of the nectar of love; Kṛṣṇa is their friend and their master. Those residents of Vraja are abodes of great auspiciousness and the only refuge for poor, lowly Bhaktivinoda.

IDX.first_line: suna he rasika jana krsnagana agananaananta kahite nahi parekrsna jagatera guru krsna vanchakalpatarunavika se bhava parabare
IDX.search: suna he rasika jana krsnagana agananaananta kahite nahi parekrsna jagatera guru krsna vanchakalpatarunavika se bhava parabare hrdaya pidita jara krsna cikitsaka tarabhava roga nasite caturakrsna bahirmukha jane premamrta vitaranekrame laya nija antahpura
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
śuna, he rasika jana, kṛṣṇa-gaṇa agaṇana,ananta kahite nāhi pārekṛṣṇa jagatera guru, kṛṣṇa vāñchā-kalpa-taru,nāvika se bhava⋅pārābāre

hṛdaya⋅pīḍita jā’ra, kṛṣṇa⋅cikitsaka tā’ra,bhava⋅roga nāśite caturakṛṣṇa bahirmukha⋅jane, premāmṛta⋅vitaraṇe,krame laya nija antaḥpura

karmabandha⋅jñānabandha⋅, āveśe mānava andha,tā’re kṛṣṇa karuṇā⋅sāgarapāda⋅padma⋅madhu diyā, andhabhāva ghucāiyā,caraṇe karena anucara

vidhimārgarata⋅jane, svādhīnatā ratnadāne,rāgamārge karāna praveśarāga⋅vaśavartī ha’ye, pārakīya⋅bhāvāśraye,labhe jīva kṛṣṇa-premāveśa

premāmṛta⋅vāridhārā, sadā pānarata tā̐’rā,kṛṣṇa tā̐hādera bandhu, patiseisaba vraja-jana, sukalyāṇa⋅niketana,dīna hīna vinodera gati
IDX.en_translation:
Listen, O discerning people! The qualities of Kṛṣṇa are innumerable and infinite; no one can describe them all. Kṛṣṇa is the spiritual master of the world and the wish-fulfilling tree; He is the helmsman in the ocean of material existence.

For those whose hearts are distressed, Kṛṣṇa is the physician, expert in destroying the disease of material existence. For those who are averse to Him, He distributes the nectar of love and gradually takes them into His inner chamber.

Human beings are blinded by their absorption in the bondage of fruitive action and speculative knowledge; for them, Kṛṣṇa is an ocean of mercy. By giving them the honey of His lotus feet and removing their blindness, He makes them His servants.

To those engaged in the path of rules, He bestows the jewel of independence and grants them entry into the path of spontaneous love. Becoming subservient to His spontaneous love and taking shelter of the mood of paramount love, the soul attains absorption in love for Kṛṣṇa.

They are always engaged in drinking the current of the nectar of love; Kṛṣṇa is their friend and their master. Those residents of Vraja are abodes of great auspiciousness and the only refuge for poor, lowly Bhaktivinoda.

IDX.first_line: suniyachi sadhumukhe bale sarvajana
IDX.search: suniyachi sadhumukhe bale sarvajana sri rupa krpaya mile jugala carana ha ha prabhu sanatana gaura parivara sabe mili vanchapurna karaha amara
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
śuniyāchi sādhumukhe bale sarvajana
śrī⋅rūpa⋅kṛpāya mile jugala⋅caraṇa

hā! hā! prabhu sanātana⋅gaura⋅parivāra
sabe mili’ vāñchā-pūrṇa karaha āmāra

śrī-rūpera kṛpā jena āmā prati haya
se⋅pada āśraya jā’ra, sei mahāśaya

prabhu lokanātha kabe saṅge lañā jābe
śrī-rūpera pāda-padme more samarpibe

hena ki hoibe mora⋅narmasakhīgaṇe
anugata narottame karibe śāsane

śrī⋅rūpa⋅sanātane dainyamayī prārthanā
IDX.en_translation:
I have heard from the mouths of the saints, everyone says: by the mercy of Śrī Rūpa, one attains the lotus feet of the Divine Couple.

Ha! Ha! Prabhu Sanātana, Gaurāṅga's family! All of you together fulfill my desire.

May the mercy of Śrī Rūpa be upon me. He whose shelter is those feet—he is a great soul.

When will Prabhu Lokanātha take me along and offer me at the lotus feet of Śrī Rūpa?

Will such a thing happen to me—that the playful sakhīs will discipline this follower Narottama?

A humble prayer to Śrī Rūpa and Sanātana.

IDX.first_line: suno suno nityananda suno haridasa
IDX.search: suno suno nityananda suno haridasa sarvatra amara ajna karaha prakasa prati ghare ghare giya kara ei bhiksa bolo krsna bhaja krsna kara krsna siksa
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
śuno śuno nityānanda, śuno haridāsa
sarvatra āmāra ājñā karaha prakāśa

prati ghare ghare giyā kara ei bhikṣā
bolo ‘kṛṣṇa’, bhaja kṛṣṇa, kara kṛṣṇa⋅śikṣā

ihā vai ār nā balibā, balāibā
dina⋅avasāne āsi’ āmāre kahibā
kahena prabhura ājñā ḍākiyā ḍākiyā

“bolo ‘kṛṣṇa’, bhaja kṛṣṇa, laha kṛṣṇa nāma
kṛṣṇa mātā, kṛṣṇa pitā, kṛṣṇa dhana⋅prāṇa

tomā sabā lāgiyā kṛṣṇer avatāra
hena kṛṣṇa bhaja, saba chāḍa anācāra”

śuno dvija, jateka pātaka kaili tui
āra jadi nā karis, saba nimu mui

para⋅hiṁsā, ḍākā⋅curi, saba anācāra
chāḍa giyā ihā tumi, nā kariha āra

dharma pathe giyā tumi laha harināma
tabe tumi anyere karibā paritrāṇa

jata saba dasyu cora ḍākiyā āniyā
dharma⋅pathe sabāre looyāo tumi giyā
IDX.en_translation:
Listen, listen Lord Nityānanda! Listen, Haridāsa Ṭhākura! Spread My command everywhere.

Go to every single house and beg this of the people: "Chant 'Kṛṣṇa!', worship Lord Kṛṣṇa, and follow His teachings."

Do not speak or cause to be spoken anything else besides this. Come back to Me at the end of the day and tell Me what happened. He speaks the Lord's command, calling out repeatedly:

"Chant 'Kṛṣṇa!', worship Kṛṣṇa, and take the Name of Kṛṣṇa. Lord Kṛṣṇa is your mother, father, wealth, and very life."

"For all of you, Lord Kṛṣṇa's incarnation has come. Worship such a Kṛṣṇa and give up all unrighteous behavior."

Listen, O twice-born! Whatever sins you have committed—if you do not commit them again, I will take all of them away from you.

Give up violence toward others, robbery, and all other unrighteous acts. Do not perform them again.

Follow the path of righteousness and chant the Holy Name of Lord Hari. Then you will also be able to deliver others.

Gather all the robbers and thieves by calling them to you, and lead everyone onto the path of righteousness.

IDX.first_line: suramya madhura smita sarva sallaksananvita
IDX.search: suramya madhura smita sarva sallaksananvita baliyan taruna gambhira vavaduka priyabhasi sudhi sapratibhasvasi vidagdha catura sukhi dhira krtajna daksina prestha variyan kirtimacchrestha lalana mohana kelipara sunitya nutana murti kevala saundarya sphurti vamsi gane sudaksa tatpara
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
suramya, madhura⋅smita, sarva sallakṣaṇānvita,
balīyān, taruṇa, gambhīra
vāvadūka, priyabhāṣī, sudhī, sapratibhāśvāsī,
vidagdha, catura, sukhī, dhīra

kṛtajña, dakṣiṇa, preṣṭha, varīyān, kīrtimacchreṣṭha,
lalanā⋅mohana, kelipara
sunitya⋅nūtana⋅mūrti, kevala saundarya⋅sphūrti,
vaṁśī⋅gāne sudakṣa, tatpara

dhīrodātta, dhīraśānta, su⋅dhīralalita, kānta,
dhīroddhata, lalanānāyaka
ceṭaka⋅viṭa⋅veṣṭita, vidūṣaka⋅susevita,
pīṭhamarda, priya⋅narmasakha

e pañca sahāyayuta, nandīśvarapati⋅suta,
pati⋅upapati⋅bhāvācārī
anukūla, śaṭha, dhṛṣṭa, sadakṣiṇa, rasa-tṛṣṇa,
rasa-mūrti, nikuñjavihārī
IDX.en_translation:
Very charming, sweet-smiling, endowed with all good qualities, very powerful, youthful, and profound. Eloquent, sweet-speaking, wise, brilliantly confident, expert, clever, happy, and steadfast.

Grateful, obliging, most beloved, excellent, best among the renowned, enchanting to women, and absorbed in playful pastimes. Of an eternally new form, an embodiment of pure beauty, very expert and diligent in playing the flute.

He is a noble and self-controlled hero, a calm and peaceful hero, a gentle and playful hero, and an arrogant yet brave hero—the leader of women. Surrounded by attendants and jokers, well-served by clowns and close assistants, He is a dear playful friend.

Endowed with these five types of assistants, the son of Nanda Mahārāja behaves in the mood of both husband and paramour. He is a favorable hero, a cunning hero, an impudent hero, and an impartial hero; thirsty for rasa and the very embodiment of rasa, He sports in the groves.

IDX.first_line: suramyadi gunagana hoiyache vibhusana
IDX.search: suramyadi gunagana hoiyache vibhusana lalana ucita jatadura prthuprema sumadhurya sampadera supracurya sri krsnavallabha rasapura vallabha ta dviprakara sviya parakiya ara mugdha madhya pragalbheti traya keha va nayika tahe keha sakhi hoite cahe nije ta nayika nahi haya
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
su-ramyādi guṇa-gaṇa, hoiyāche vibhūṣaṇa,
lalanā⋅ucita jatadura
pṛthupremā, su-mādhurya⋅, sampadera suprācurya,
śrī⋅kṛṣṇavallabhā rasapura

vallabhā ta’ dvi-prakāra, svīyā, parakīyā āra,
mugdhā, madhyā, pragalbheti traya
keha vā nāyikā tāhe, keha sakhī hoite cāhe,
nije ta’ nāyikā nāhi haya

nāyikāgaṇa⋅pradhāna, rādhā, candrā⋅dui jana,
saundarya⋅vaidagdhya⋅guṇāśrayā
sei dui madhye śreṣṭha, rādhikā kṛṣṇera preṣṭha,
mahābhāvasvarūpa⋅nilayā

āra jata nitya-priyā, nija nija jūtha lañā,
se du’yera karena sevana
śrī⋅rūpa⋅anuga jana, śrī⋅rādhikā⋅śrī⋅caraṇa,
vinā nāhi jāne anya dhana
IDX.en_translation:
All charming qualities and more have become her adornments, as much as befits a beautiful woman. She possesses abundant love, great sweetness, and profuse opulence. She is Śrī Kṛṣṇa's beloved, brimming with divine mellows.

Beloveds are of two kinds: one's own wife and another's wife, and these are categorized into three: innocent, partially experienced, and fully experienced. Some among them are heroines, and some wish to be companions, but she herself is not merely a heroine.

The chief among the heroines are Rādhā and Candrā, both being the abode of beauty, intelligence, and all good qualities. Among these two, Rādhikā is the greatest, Kṛṣṇa's dearest, and the ultimate abode of the highest ecstatic love.

And all other eternal beloveds, taking their respective groups, serve those two. The followers of Śrī Rūpa Gosvāmī know no other wealth except the auspicious lotus feet of Śrī Rādhikā.

IDX.first_line: suryapuja chala kari grha haite abhisari
IDX.search: suryapuja chala kari grha haite abhisari sakhisange rasavati rai radhakugu tire jaya sri krsnadarsana paya duhe matta mithah sanga pai sattvika sancari bhava vikara bhusana labha vamya ara utkanthate lola smara yajne vrati haye lalitadi sakhi laye prapta sukhe hoila vibhola
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
sūryapūjā chala kari’, gṛha haite abhisāri’,
sakhīsaṅge rasavatī rāi
rādhākugu⋅tīre jāya, śrī⋅kṛṣṇadarśana pāya,
du̐he matta mithaḥ saṅga pāi’

sāttvika⋅sañcāri⋅bhāva, vikāra⋅bhūṣaṇa lābha,
vāmya āra utkaṇṭhāte lola
smara⋅yajñe vratī haye, lalitādi sakhī laye,
prāpta sukhe hoila vibhola

dolā vanalīlā bhūri, jalakeli vaṁśīcuri,
madhupāna arkārcana punaḥ
kata kata līlā kare, sakhīsaṅge ataḥpare,
vilāse magana duijana

rādhā⋅kuṇḍe eirūpa, nityalīlā aparūpa,
sarvadina haya saŋghaṭana
premacakṣu khule jāra, nayanagocara tāra,
naile nāhi haya daraśana
IDX.en_translation:
Under the pretense of worshipping the Sun-god, the playful Rāi leaves Her home to meet Her Beloved with Her gopī friends. On the bank of Rādhā-kuṇḍa, She beholds Lord Kṛṣṇa, and both become absorbed in Their mutual companionship.

They experience all the symptoms of ecstatic love, and these emotional transformations become Their ornaments along with Their contrary moods and intense longing. Initiated into the sacrifice of love with Lalitā and other friends, They become lost in the happiness They achieve.

Many forest pastimes occur—swinging, water sports, the stealing of the flute, the drinking of nectar, and again the worship of the Sun-god. How many pastimes they perform! Thereafter, with Their friends, Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa are absorbed in amorous play.

In Rādhā-kuṇḍa, such unparalleled eternal pastimes occur every day. For those whose eyes of pure love are open, these become visible; otherwise, they cannot be seen.

IDX.first_line: svabhaktiyoga lasinam sada vraje viharinam
IDX.search: svabhaktiyoga lasinam sada vraje viharinam hari priya ganagragam saci suta priyesvaram saradha krsnasevana prakasakam mahasayam bhajamyaham gadadharam supanditam gurum prabhum navojjvaladi bhavana vidhana karmaparagam vicitra gaura bhakti sindhu rasabhanga lasinam suraga marga darsakam vrajadi vasa dayakam bhajamyaham gadadharam supanditam gurum prabhum
IDX.author: śrīla rūpa gosvāmī
IDX.verses:
svabhaktiyoga⋅lāsinaṁ sadā vraje vihāriṇaṁ
hari⋅priyā⋅gaṇāgragaṁ śacī⋅suta⋅priyeśvaram
sarādhā⋅kṛṣṇasevana⋅prakāśakaṁ mahāśayaṁ
bhajāmyahaṁ gadādharaṁ supaṇḍitaṁ guruṁ prabhum

navojjvalādi⋅bhāvanā⋅vidhāna⋅karmapāragaṁ
vicitra⋅gaura⋅bhakti⋅sindhu⋅rasabhaṅga⋅lāsinam
surāga⋅mārga⋅darśakaṁ vrajādi⋅vāsa⋅dāyakaṁ
bhajāmyahaṁ gadādharaṁ supaṇḍitaṁ guruṁ prabhum

śacī⋅sutāṅghrisāra⋅bhakta⋅vṛnda⋅vandya⋅gauravaṁ
gaurabhāva⋅cittapadma⋅madhya⋅kṛṣṇa⋅suvallabham
mukunda⋅gaurarūpiṇaṁ sva⋅bhāva⋅dharma⋅dāyakaṁ
bhajāmyahaṁ gadādharaṁ supaṇḍitaṁ guruṁ prabhum

nikuñja⋅sevanādika⋅prakāśanaika⋅kāraṇaṁ
sadā sakhīrati⋅pradaṁ mahārasa⋅svarūpakaṁ
sadāśritāṅghri⋅puṇḍarīka⋅pradaṁ sadguruṁ varaṁ
bhajāmyahaṁ gadādharaṁ supaṇḍitaṁ guruṁ prabhum

mahāprabhormahārasa⋅prakāśanāṅkuraṁ priyaṁ
sadā mahārasāṅkura⋅prakāśanādi⋅vāsanāṁ
mahāprabhorvrajāṅganādi⋅bhāva⋅moda⋅kārakaṁ
bhajāmyahaṁ gadādharaṁ supaṇḍitaṁ guruṁ prabhum

dvijendra⋅vṛnda⋅vandya⋅pādayugma⋅bhakti⋅vardhakaṁ
nijeṣu rādhikātmatā⋅vapuḥ⋅prakāśanāgraham
aśeṣa⋅bhaktiśāstra⋅śikṣayojjvalāmṛta⋅pradaṁ
bhajāmyahaṁ gadādharaṁ supaṇḍitaṁ guruṁ prabhum

mudā nija⋅priyādika⋅svapāda⋅padma⋅sīdhubhi
rmahārasārṇavāmṛta⋅pradeṣṭa⋅gaurabhaktidam
sadāṣṭa⋅sāttvikānvitaṁ nijeṣṭa⋅bhakti⋅dāyakaṁ
bhajāmyahaṁ gadādharaṁ supaṇḍitaṁ guruṁ prabhum

jadīya⋅rītirāga⋅raṅgabhaṅga⋅digdha⋅mānaso
naro‛pi jāti tūrṇameva nāryabhāva⋅bhājanam
tamujjvalākta⋅cittametu citta⋅matta⋅ṣaṭpado⋅
bhajāmyahaṁ gadādharaṁ supaṇḍitaṁ guruṁ prabhum

mahārasāmṛta-pradaṁ sadā gadādharāṣṭakaṁ
paṭhet tu jaḥ subhaktito vrajāṅganā⋅gaṇotsavam
śacītanuja⋅pāda⋅padma⋅bhaktiratna⋅yogyatāṁ
labheta rādhikā⋅gadādharāṅghri⋅padma⋅sevayā
IDX.en_translation:
I worship Gadādhara, the greatly learned guru and master, who always delights in the practice of bhakti-yoga and resides in Vraja, who is foremost among the beloved associates of Hari and dear to the Son of Śacī, who reveals the service of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa, and who is a great personality.

I worship Gadādhara, the greatly learned guru and master, who is expert in arranging the emotions of the newly brilliant and others, who delights in the varied rasas of the ocean of Gaura-bhakti, who guides us on the path of intense devotional attachment, and bestows residence in Vraja and other holy places.

I worship Gadādhara, the greatly learned guru and master, who is honored by the multitude of devotees whose essence is the lotus feet of Śacī’s Son, who is most beloved by Kṛṣṇa, who resides in the lotus of Gaura’s heart, who embodies Mukunda in Gaura’s form, and who bestows one's inherent spiritual nature.

I worship Gadādhara, the greatly learned guru and master, who is the sole cause for revealing the service of the groves and other such things, who always gives the ecstatic love of the sakhīs, who is the embodiment of great rasa, who is the most excellent true guru, who bestows the lotus feet of those always surrendered to him.

I worship Gadādhara, the greatly learned guru and master, who is the beloved sprout revealing Mahāprabhu’s great rasa, who always has the desire to reveal the sprout of great rasa, who brings joy in the mood of the gopīs of Vraja and others to Mahāprabhu.

I worship Gadādhara, the greatly learned guru and master, who enhances devotion to the lotus feet honored by the multitude of best brāhmaṇas, who insists on revealing his own body as the soul of Rādhikā, who bestows the bright nectar through teaching all the scriptures of devotion.

I worship Gadādhara, the greatly learned guru and master, by whose sweet nectar from the lotus feet of his beloved ones, he bestows Gaura-bhakti, the great rasa-ocean nectar, who is always accompanied by the eight bodily transformations of ecstatic love, who bestows devotion to his chosen Deity.

I worship Gadādhara, the greatly learned guru and master, whose mind is steeped in the waves and colors of his unique sweet love; even a man whose mind is thus infused quickly attains the qualification for the mood of a woman; let my mind, like a maddened bee, approach him, whose heart is imbued with ujjvala-rasa.

Whoever reads these eight verses on Gadādhara, which always bestow the nectar of great rasa, with great devotion, which describe the festival of the gopīs of Vraja, will attain the eligibility for the jewel of devotion to the lotus feet of Śacī’s Son, through the service of the lotus feet of Rādhikā and Gadādhara.

IDX.first_line: svatah siddhi rati tare sambandhadi sahakare s
IDX.search: svatah siddhi rati tare sambandhadi sahakare s samartha kariya rakhe sada krsna seva vina tara udyama nahika ara sviya sukha cesta nahi kada ei rati praudha haya maha bhava dasa paya jara tulya prapti ara nai sarvadbhuta camatkara sambhogeccha e prakara varnibare vakya nahi pai
IDX.verses:
svataḥ⋅siddhi rati tā̐’re sambandhādi⋅sahākāre s
samartha kariyā rākhe sadā
kṛṣṇa⋅sevā vinā tā̐’ra udyama nāhika āra
svīya sukha ceṣṭa nāhi kadā

ei rati prauḍha haya mahā⋅bhāva dāsa pāya
jāra tulya prāpti āra nāi
sarvādbhuta camatkāra sambhogecchā e prakāra
varṇibāre vākya nāhi pāi
IDX.en_translation:
The self-perfected love for the Lord, combined with a realization of one's relationship with Him, always keeps a person spiritually capable. Without service to Lord Kṛṣṇa, there is no other endeavor for such a person, and they never make any attempt for their own happiness.

This mature love eventually leads one to become a servant of the highest spiritual emotions. There is no other attainment equal to this. This kind of most wonderful and astonishing desire for divine enjoyment—I find no words to describe it.

IDX.first_line: tapanamisrera putra raghunatha bhattacarya
IDX.search: tapanamisrera putra raghunatha bhattacarya prabhuke dekhite cale chadi sarvakarya kasi hoite calila gaudapatha diya sangete sevaka eka cale jhapi laiya
IDX.author: kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa
IDX.verses:
tapanamiśrera putra raghunātha bhaṭṭācārya
prabhuke dekhite cale chāḍi’ sarvakārya

kāśī hoite calilā gauḍapatha diyā
saṅgete sevaka eka cale jhā̐pi laiyā

eimate raghunātha āilā nīlācale
mahāprabhura caraṇe giyā mililā kutūhale

daṇḍavat praṇāma kari’ paḍilā caraṇe
prabhu raghunātha jāni’ kailā āliṅgane

‘‘bhāla haila āilā, dekha kamalalocana
āji āmāra ihā karibā prasāda-bhojana”

govindere kahi eka vāsā deoyāilā
svarūpādi bhaktagaṇa⋅sane milāilā

eimate prabhu⋅sane rahilā aṣṭamāsa
dine dine prabhu kṛpāya bāḍaye ullāsa

madhye madhye mahāprabhura karena nimantraṇa
ghara⋅bhāta karena, āra vividha vyañjana

raghunātha bhaṭṭa⋅pāke ati sunipuṇa
jei rāndhe, sei haya amṛtera sama

parama santoṣe prabhu karena bhojana
prabhura avaśiṣṭa pātra bhaṭṭera bhakṣaṇa

aṣṭamāsa rahi’ prabhu bhaṭṭe vidāya dilā
‘vivāha nā kariha’ bali’ niṣedha karilā

“vṛddha pitāmātāra jāi’ karaha sevana
vaiṣṇava⋅pāśa bhāgavata kara adhyayana

punarapi ekabāra āsiha nīlācale”
eta kahi’ kaṇṭhamālā dila tā’ra gale

āliṅgana kari’, prabhu tā’re vidāya dilā
preme gadagada bhaṭṭa kā̐dite lāgilā

svarūpa⋅ādi bhakta ṭhā̐i ājñā māgiyā
vārāṇasī āilā bhaṭṭa prabhu⋅ājñā pāiyā

cāri vatsara ghare pitāmātāra sevā kailā
vaiṣṇava⋅paṇḍita ṭhā̐i bhāgavata paḍilā

pitāmātā kāśī pāile udāsīna haiyā
punaḥ prabhura ṭhā̐i gela bhāvete galiyā

pūrvavat aṣṭamāsa prabhupāśa chilā
aṣṭamāsa rahi’ prabhu punaḥ ājñā dilā

“āmāra ājñāya raghunātha, jāha vṛndāvane
tathā giyā raha rūpa⋅sanātana⋅sane

bhāgavata paḍa sadā laha kṛṣṇa⋅nāma
acire karibena kṛpā kṛṣṇa bhagavān”

eta bali’ prabhu tā̐’re āliṅgana kailā
prabhura kṛpāte kṛṣṇapreme matta hailā

cauddahāta jagannāthera tulasīra mālā
chuṭā⋅pāna⋅ciḍā⋅mahotsave pāñāchilā

sei mālā, chuṭā⋅pāna prabhu tā̐’re dilā
iṣṭadeva kari’ mālā dhariyā rākhilā

prabhu ṭhā̐i ājñā pāñā āilā vṛndāvane
āśraya karilā āsi’ rūpa⋅sanātane

rūpagosāira sabhāya kare bhāgavata⋅paṭhana
bhāgavata paḍite preme āulāya mana

aśru⋅kampa⋅gadagada prabhura kṛpāte
netre aśru, ruddha⋅kaṇṭha nā pāre paḍite

pikasvara kaṇṭha tā’te rāgera vibhāga
eka śloka paḍite phirāya cāri rāga

kṛṣṇera mādhuryaguṇa jabe paḍe mane
premete vihvala haiyā kichui nā jāne

govindera caraṇe kaila ātmasamarpaṇa
govinda⋅caraṇāravinda jā̐’ra prāṇadhana

nija śiṣye kahi govinda⋅mandira korāilā
vaṁśī, makara, kuṇḍalādi bhūṣaṇa kari dilā

grāmavārtā nā śune, nā kahe jihvāya
kṛṣṇakathā⋅pūjādite aṣṭaprahara jāya

vaiṣṇavera nindyakarma nāhi pāḍe kāṇe
sabe kṛṣṇa bhajana kare⋅eimātra jāne

mahāprabhura datta mālā mananera kāle
prasāda⋅kaḍāra soi bāndhi lana gale

mahāprabhura kṛpāya kṛṣṇaprema anargala
mahāprabhura raghunāthe kṛpā⋅mahāphala
IDX.en_translation:
Tapanamiśra's son, Śrīla Raghunātha Bhaṭṭa Gosvāmī, set out to see the Lord, leaving all other duties behind.

From Kāśī he journeyed by the path through Bengal, accompanied by a servant.

In this way, Raghunātha arrived at Jagannātha Purī and eagerly met Mahāprabhu at His feet.

Performing full obeisances, he fell at the Lord's feet, and the Lord, knowing Raghunātha, embraced him.

"It is good that you have come; behold the lotus-eyed Lord Jagannātha. Today you will partake of sanctified food with Me."

The Lord told Govinda to arrange a dwelling for him and introduced him to Svarūpa Dāmodara and other devotees.

In this manner, he remained with the Lord for eight months, and day by day his joy increased by the Lord's mercy.

From time to time, he would invite Mahāprabhu to his home and cook various delicious preparations.

Raghunātha Bhaṭṭa was very skilled in cooking; whatever he prepared was like nectar.

With supreme satisfaction the Lord would eat, and Raghunātha would then partake of the Lord's remnants.

After eight months, the Lord bade him farewell and instructed him: "Do not marry.

Go and serve your elderly parents, and study the Śrīmad Bhāgavatam with a devotee scholar.

Come again to Purī once more." Saying this, the Lord placed a garland around his neck.

Embracing him, the Lord bade him farewell, and Raghunātha, overwhelmed with love, began to weep.

Taking leave from Svarūpa Dāmodara and the other devotees, he returned to Varanasi.

For four years he served his parents at home and studied the Bhāgavatam with a devotee scholar.

When his parents passed away, he became detached and again went to the Lord, melting in divine emotion.

As before, he stayed with the Lord for eight months, after which the Lord again gave him an order:

"By My command, Raghunātha, go to Vṛndāvana and reside there with Rūpa and Sanātana.

Always read the Bhāgavatam and chant the Holy Names of Lord Kṛṣṇa; soon the Lord will bestow His mercy upon you."

Saying this, the Lord embraced him, and by the Lord's mercy, he became intoxicated with divine love.

He had received a fourteen-cubit Tulasī garland from Lord Jagannātha, along with leftover betel leaves and rice from a festival.

The Lord gave him those items, and he kept the garland, considering it his worshipable Deity.

Receiving the Lord's command, he came to Vṛndāvana and took shelter of Rūpa and Sanātana Gosvāmīs.

In Śrīla Rūpa Gosvāmī's assembly he would read the Bhāgavatam, and while reading, his mind would become overwhelmed with love.

With tears, trembling, and a choked voice, he could sometimes not even continue reading.

His voice was sweet like a cuckoo's, and he was expert in the melodic modes; while reading one verse, he would express four different melodies.

When Lord Kṛṣṇa's sweet qualities came to his mind, he would be so overwhelmed with love that he knew nothing else.

He surrendered at the feet of Lord Govinda, who was his life's treasure.

He instructed his disciples to build a temple for Lord Govinda and had ornaments made for the Deity.

He would not listen to worldly gossip, nor would he speak it; his twenty-four hours were spent in worship and topics about Lord Kṛṣṇa.

He would not hear any criticism of the devotees; he only knew that everyone was engaged in the worship of the Lord.

During meditation, he would wear the garland given by Mahāprabhu and keep a token of the Lord's remnants around his neck.

By Mahāprabhu's mercy, divine love flowed from him unimpeded. The Lord's mercy upon Raghunātha Bhaṭṭa was indeed a great fruit.

IDX.first_line: tatala saikate varibindu sama
IDX.search: tatala saikate varibindu sama suta mita ramani samaje tohe visari mana tahe samarpinu av majhu haba kon kaje madhava hama parinama nirasa tuhu jagatarana dina dayamaya ataye tohari visvasa
IDX.author: śrī⋅vidyāpati
IDX.verses:
tātala saikate, vāribindu⋅sama,
suta⋅mita⋅ramaṇī⋅samāje
tohe visari mana, tāhe samarpinu,
av majhu haba kon kāje

mādhava, hāma pariṇāma nirāśā
tuhu̐ jagatāraṇa, dīna⋅dayāmaya,
ataye tohāri viśvāsā

ādha janama hāma, nide̐ goṅāyanu,
jarā śiśu katadina gelā
nidhuvane ramaṇī⋅, rasa-raṅge mātanu,
tohe bhajaba kon belā

kata caturānana, mari mari jāota,
na tuyā ādi avasānā
tohe janamipunaḥ, tohe samāota,
sāgara⋅laharī samānā

bhaṇaye vidyāpati, śeṣa śamana⋅bhaye,
tuyā vinā gati nāhi ārā
ādi anādika, nātha kahāosi,
av tāraṇa⋅bhāra tohārā
IDX.en_translation:
On the heated sand, like a drop of water—in the society of sons, friends, and wife, forgetting You, my mind, I dedicated myself to them. Now, of what use will I be?

O Lord Mādhava! I am hopeless about the final outcome. You are the deliverer of the world and merciful to the poor; therefore, I place my faith in You.

Half of my life I spent in sleep; how many days have passed in old age and childhood? In secluded groves, with women, I was intoxicated by amorous sports; when will I ever worship You?

How many four-headed Brahmās die again and again, but You have no beginning or end. They are born from You and again they merge into You, just like the waves of the ocean.

Vidyāpati says: "At the end, fearing the God of Death, I see no other refuge besides You. You are called the Beginningless Lord without origin; now the burden of delivering me is Yours."

IDX.first_line: thakura vaisnava pada avanira susampada
IDX.search: thakura vaisnava pada avanira susampada suna bhai hana ekamana asraya loiya bhaje tare krsna nahi tyaje ara saba mare akarana vaisnava carana jala prema bhakti dite bala ara keha nahe balavanta vaisnava carana renu mastake bhusana vinu ara nahi bhusanera anta
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
ṭhākura vaiṣṇava⋅pada, avanīra susampada,
śuna bhāi, hañā ekamana
āśraya loiyā bhaje, tāre kṛṣṇa nāhi tyaje,
āra saba mare akāraṇa

vaiṣṇava⋅caraṇa⋅jala, prema⋅bhakti dite bala,
āra keha nahe balavanta
vaiṣṇava⋅caraṇa⋅reṇu, mastake bhūṣaṇa vinu,
āra nāhi bhūṣaṇera anta

tīrtha-jala pavitra guṇe, likhiyāche purāṇe,
se⋅saba bhaktira pravañcana
vaiṣṇavera pādodaka⋅ sama nahe ei saba,
jāte haya vāñchita⋅pūraṇa

vaiṣṇava⋅saṅgete mana, ānandita anukṣaṇa,
sadā haya kṛṣṇa⋅parasaṅga
dīna narottama kānde, hiyā dhairya nāhi bāndhe,
mora daśā kena haila bhaṅga
IDX.en_translation:
The feet of the Vaiṣṇava ṭhākura are the great treasure of the earth. Listen, oh brother, with a single mind. One who takes their shelter and worships, Kṛṣṇa never abandons them; all others simply die in vain.

The water from the Vaiṣṇava's feet has the power to bestow prema-bhakti; no one else is so powerful. The dust from the Vaiṣṇava's feet is an ornament for the head; there is no end to such ornaments.

It is written in the Purāṇas that holy water from pilgrimage sites is pure by nature, but all that is merely a deception of devotion. All these cannot compare to the water from the Vaiṣṇava's feet, by which all desires are fulfilled.

In the association of Vaiṣṇavas, the mind is joyful at every moment, and one always experiences the direct association of Kṛṣṇa. Humble Narottama Dāsa Ṭhākura weeps, his heart finds no patience: "Why has my condition become so broken?"

IDX.search: thakura vaisnava pada avanira susampadasuna bhai hana ekamanaasraya loiya bhaje tare krsna nahi tyajeara saba mare akarana vaisnava carana jala prema bhakti dite balaara keha nahe balavantavaisnava carana renu mastake bhusana vinuara nahi bhusanera anta

ṭhākura vaiṣṇava⋅pada, avanīra susampada,śuna bhāi, hañā ekamanaāśraya loiyā bhaje, tāre kṛṣṇa nāhi tyaje,āra saba mare akāraṇa

vaiṣṇava⋅caraṇa⋅jala, prema⋅bhakti dite bala,āra keha nahe balavantavaiṣṇava⋅caraṇa⋅reṇu, mastake bhūṣaṇa vinu,āra nāhi bhūṣaṇera anta

tīrtha-jala pavitra guṇe, likhiyāche purāṇe,se⋅saba bhaktira pravañcanavaiṣṇavera pādodaka⋅ sama nahe ei saba,jāte haya vāñchita⋅pūraṇa

vaiṣṇava⋅saṅgete mana, ānandita anukṣaṇa,sadā haya kṛṣṇa⋅parasaṅgadīna narottama kānde, hiyā dhairya nāhi bāndhe,mora daśā kena haila bhaṅga

The feet of the Vaiṣṇava ṭhākura are the great treasure of the earth. Listen, oh brother, with a single mind. One who takes their shelter and worships, Kṛṣṇa never abandons them; all others simply die in vain.

The water from the Vaiṣṇava’s feet has the power to bestow prema-bhakti; no one else is so powerful. The dust from the Vaiṣṇava’s feet is an ornament for the head; there is no end to such ornaments.

It is written in the Purāṇas that holy water from pilgrimage sites is pure by nature, but all that is merely a deception of devotion. All these cannot compare to the water from the Vaiṣṇava’s feet, by which all desires are fulfilled.

In the association of Vaiṣṇavas, the mind is joyful at every moment, and one always experiences the direct association of Kṛṣṇa. Humble Narottama Dāsa Ṭhākura weeps, his heart finds no patience: “Why has my condition become so broken?”

IDX.first_line: thakura vaisnavagana kari ei nivedanamo bada adhama duracaradaruna samsara nidhi tahe dubaila vidhikese dhari more kara para
IDX.search: thakura vaisnavagana kari ei nivedanamo bada adhama duracaradaruna samsara nidhi tahe dubaila vidhikese dhari more kara para vidhi bada balaban na sune dharama jnanasadai karama pase bandhena dekhi tarana lesa jata dekhi saba klesaanatha katare teni kande
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
ṭhākura vaiṣṇava-gaṇa, kari ei nivedana,mo baḍa adhama durācāradāruṇa⋅saṁsāra⋅nidhi, tāhe ḍubāila vidhi,keśe dhari’ more kara pāra

vidhi baḍa balabān, nā śune dharama⋅jñāna,sadāi karama⋅pāśe bāndhenā dekhi tāraṇa⋅leśa, jata dekhi saba kleśa,anātha, kātare teñi kānde

kāma, krodha, lobha, moha, mada, abhimāna saha,āpana āpana sthāne ṭāneaichana āmāra mana, phire jena andhajana,supatha⋅vipatha nāhi jāne

nā loinu sat mata, asate majila cita,tuyā pāye nā karinu āśanarottama⋅dāse kaya, dekhi’ śuni’ lāge bhaya,tarāiyā laha nija⋅pāśa
IDX.en_translation:
O venerable devotees, I make this petition: I am a very fallen and wicked person. Fate has drowned me in the terrible ocean of material existence; please grab me by the hair and carry me across.

Fate is very powerful and does not listen to religious knowledge; it always binds me with the ropes of my own actions. I see no trace of deliverance—all I see is distress. I am helpless, and therefore I cry out in anguish.

Lust, anger, greed, illusion, pride, and false ego—each pulls me toward its own place. Such is my mind that it wanders like a blind person, knowing neither the right path nor the wrong one.

I did not accept good counsel, and my mind was absorbed in evil; I placed no hope in Your feet. Narottama dāsa says: seeing and hearing of this condition, I feel fear. Please deliver me and take me into Your company.

IDX.first_line: thumaka calata ramacandra bajata painjaniya
IDX.search: thumaka calata ramacandra bajata painjaniya kilaka kilaka othata dhae girata bhumi latapatae dhaya mata goda leta dasaratha ki raniya
IDX.author: tulasī⋅dāsa
IDX.verses: ṭhumaka calata rāmacandra bājata paiñjaniyā kilaka kilaka oṭhata dhāe, girata bhūmi laṭapaṭāe dhāya māta goda leta daśaratha ki raniyā vidurama se aruṇa adhara, bolata mukha madhura⋅madhura subhaga nāsikā me cāru laṭakata laṭakaniyā tulasī dāsa ati ānanda, dekhi kai mukhāravinda raghuvara chabi ke samāna raghuvara chabi baniyā
IDX.en_translation:
Lord Rāmacandra walks with toddling steps, and His anklets are jingling.

Giggling, He gets up and runs, then falls upon the ground, stumbling. Queen Kaushalyā runs and takes Him into her lap.

His lips are reddish like rubies, and He speaks very sweetly. On His beautiful nose, a charming ornament dangles.

Tulasīdāsa feels great joy upon seeing His lotus-like face. The beauty of the Lord is His only comparison.

IDX.first_line: tuhu daya sagara tarayite prani
IDX.search: tuhu daya sagara tarayite prani nama aneka tuya sikhaoli ani sakala sakati dei name tohara grahane rakhali nahi kala vicara
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
tuhu̐ dayā⋅sāgara tārayite prāṇī
nāma aneka tuyā śikhāoli āni

sakala śakati dei nāme tohārā
grahaṇe rākhali nāhi kāla⋅vicārā

śrī⋅nāmacintāmaṇi tohāri samānā
viśve vilāoli karuṇā⋅nidānā

tuyā dayā aichana parama udārā
atiśaya manda nātha! bhāga hāmārā

nāhi janamala nāme anurāga mora
bhakativinoda⋅citta duḥkhe vibhora
IDX.en_translation:
You are an ocean of mercy, to deliver living beings. You have brought and taught us many of Your holy names.

You have invested all power in Your holy name. In chanting it, You have kept no consideration of time or rules.

Your wish-fulfilling jewel of the holy name is equal to You. You have distributed this source of mercy throughout the world.

Your mercy is so supremely magnanimous. O Lord! My fortune is extremely wretched.

No love for the holy name has developed in me. Bhakativinoda's heart is overwhelmed with sorrow.

IDX.first_line: tuhu se rahili madhupura
IDX.search: tuhu se rahili madhupura vrajakula akula dukula kalarava kanu kanu kori jhura jasomati nanda andha sama baithai sahase uthai na para sakha gana dhenu venurava na suniye vichurala nagara bayara
IDX.author: govinda dāsa
IDX.verses: tuhū se rahili madhupura vrajakula ākula, dukūla kalarava, kānu kānu kori jhura jaśomatī⋅nanda, andha sama baiṭhai, sāhase uṭhai nā pāra sakhā⋅gaṇa dhenu, veṇurava nā śuniye, vichurala nagara bāyāra kusuma tyajiyā ali, kṣititale luṭata, taru⋅gaṇa malina samāna mayūrī nā nācata, kapotī nā bolata, kokilā nā karatahi gāṇa virahiṇī rāī, virahajvare jara jara, caudike viraha hutāśa sahaje jamunā jala, āgi samāna bhela, kahatahi govinda dāsa
IDX.en_translation:
You have remained in Mathurā. The residents of Vraja are distressed, crying out and lamenting, "Kānu! Kānu!"

Yaśodā and Nanda sit as if blind, unable to find the courage to rise. The cowherd friends and the cows are desolate; the sound of the flute is not heard, and the city marketplace is empty.

The bees, abandoning the flowers, roll on the ground, and the trees look sad. The peahens do not dance, the doves do not coo, and the cuckoos do not sing.

Rādhā is wasting away with the fever of separation; the fire of separation is everywhere. Even the waters of the Yamunā have become like fire—so says Govinda dāsa.

IDX.first_line: tumi ta dayara sindhu adhama janara bandhumore prabhu kara avadhanapadinu asat bhole kama timingile gileohe natha kara paritrana
IDX.search: tumi ta dayara sindhu adhama janara bandhumore prabhu kara avadhanapadinu asat bhole kama timingile gileohe natha kara paritrana javat janama mora aparadhe hainu bhoraniskapate na bhajinu tomatathapi je tumi gati na chadiha pranapatimora sama nahika adhama
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
tumi ta’ dayāra sindhu, adhama janāra bandhu,more prabhu kara avadhānapaḍinu asat bhole, kāma⋅timiṅgile gile,ohe nātha kara paritrāṇa

jāvat janama mora, aparādhe hainu bhora,niṣkapaṭe nā bhajinu tomātathāpi je tumi gati, nā chāḍiha prāṇapati,mora sama nāhika adhamā

patita⋅pāvana nāma, ghoṣaṇā tomāra śyāma,upekhile nāhi mora gatijadi hoi aparādhī, tathāpiha tumi gati,satya satya jena satīra pati

tumi ta’ parama devā, nāhi more upekhibā,śuna śuna prāṇera īśvarajadi kari aparādha, tathāpiha tumi nātha,sevā diyā kara anucara

kāme mora hata cita, nāhi śune nija hita,manera nā ghuce durvāsanāmore nātha aṅgīkuru, tumi vāñchā⋅kalpataru,karuṇā dekhuka sarvajanā

mo⋅sama patita nāi, tribhuvane dekha cāi,‘narottama⋅pāvana’ nāma dharaghuṣuka saṁsāre nāma, patita uddhāra’ śyāma,nijadāsa kara giridhara

narottama baḍa duḥkhī, nātha more kara sukhī,tomāra bhajana⋅saṅkīrtaneantarāya nāhi jāya, ei se parama⋅bhaya,nivedana kari anukṣaṇe
IDX.en_translation:
You are indeed an ocean of mercy and a friend of the fallen. O Lord, please be mindful of me. I have fallen into bad association and have been swallowed by the great whale of lust. O Master, please deliver me!

All my life I have been absorbed in offenses and have not sincerely worshiped You. Yet You are my only refuge. Do not abandon me, O Lord of my life, for there is no one more fallen than me.

The name “Purifier of the Fallen” is Your declaration, O dark-complexioned Lord! If You neglect me, I have no other shelter. Even if I am an offender, still You are my only refuge; this is as true as a chaste wife’s husband being her only refuge.

You are indeed the Supreme Deity; You will not neglect me. Please hear, O Lord of my life! Even if I commit offenses, still You are my Master. Please make me Your servant by giving me service.

My heart is ruined by lust; it does not hear what is good for itself. The evil desires of my mind do not cease. O Master, please accept me; You are the wish-fulfilling tree of desire. Let everyone witness Your mercy.

There is no one as fallen as me in all the three worlds. Take the name “Purifier of Narottama!” Let this name resound throughout the world: “The Lord delivers the fallen!” Make me Your own servant, O lifter of the hill.

Narottama is very distressed. O Lord, make me happy through worship and congregational chanting. The obstacles do not go away, and this is my greatest fear. I submit this prayer to You constantly.

IDX.search: ujjvala varana gaura vara deham vilasita niravadhi bhava videham tribhuvana pavana krpaya lesam tam pranamami ca sri saci tanayam gadgada antara bhava vikaram durjana tarjana nada visalam bhava bhaya bhanjana karana karunam tam pranamami ca sri saci tanayam
