gati⋅atimanthara⋅nṛtya⋅vilāsaṁ
taṁ praṇamāmi ca śrī⋅śacī⋅tanayam

cañcala⋅cāru⋅caraṇa⋅gati⋅ruciraṁ
mañjīra⋅rañjita⋅pada⋅yuga⋅madhuram
candra⋅vinindita⋅śītala⋅vadanaṁ
taṁ praṇamāmi ca śrī⋅śacī⋅tanayam

dhṛta⋅kaṭi⋅ḍora⋅kamaṇḍalu⋅daṇḍaṁ
divya⋅kalevara⋅muṇḍita⋅muṇḍam
durjana⋅kalmaṣa⋅khaṇḍana⋅daṇḍaṁ
taṁ praṇamāmi ca śrī⋅śacī⋅tanayam

bhūṣaṇa⋅bhū⋅raja⋅alakā⋅valitaṁ
kampita⋅vimvādhara⋅vara⋅ruciram
malayaja⋅viracita⋅ujjvala⋅tilakaṁ
taṁ praṇamāmi ca śrī⋅śacī⋅tanayam

nindita⋅aruṇa⋅kamala⋅dala⋅nayanaṁ
ājānulambita⋅śrī⋅bhuja⋅yugalam
kalevara⋅kaiśora⋅nartaka⋅veśaṁ
taṁ praṇamāmi ca śrī⋅śacī⋅tanayam
IDX.en_translation:
I offer my obeisances to the son of Śacī, whose body is of a bright, golden complexion and who is ever filled with ecstatic love. He possesses a trace of compassion that purifies the three worlds.

I offer my obeisances to the son of Śacī, whose heart is overwhelmed with emotions, whose loud roars threaten the wicked, and who is the merciful cause of destroying the fear of material existence.

I offer my obeisances to the son of Śacī, who wears saffron garments, has beautiful cheeks, and whose toenails are more radiant than the moon. He delights in chanting His own names and qualities.

I offer my obeisances to the son of Śacī, from whose lotus-like eyes a stream of tears flows, whose body exhibits new and fresh ecstatic emotions, and who dances with extremely slow, graceful movements.

I offer my obeisances to the son of Śacī, whose charming and restless foot movements are beautiful, whose sweet feet are adorned with ankle bells, and whose cool face surpasses the moon.

I offer my obeisances to the son of Śacī, who wears a waist-string, carries a water pot and a staff, whose divine body has a shaven head, and whose staff dispels the sins of the wicked.

I offer my obeisances to the son of Śacī, whose body is adorned with dust and curly hair, whose trembling red lips are beautiful, and who is marked with a bright tilaka made of sandalwood paste.

I offer my obeisances to the son of Śacī, whose eyes shame the red lotus petals, whose divine arms extend down to His knees, and whose youthful body is dressed as a dancer.

IDX.first_line: upasita padambujas tvam anurakta rudradibhih
IDX.search: upasita padambujas tvam anurakta rudradibhih prapadya purusottamam padam adabhram udbhrajitah samasta nata mandali sphurad abhista kalpa drumah saci suta mayi prabho kuru mukunda mande krpam nu varnayitum isate gurutaravatarayita bhavantam uru buddhayo na khalu sarvabhaumadayah paro bhavatu tatra kah patur ato namas te param saci suta mayi prabho kuru mukunda mande krpam
IDX.author: śrīla rūpa gosvāmī
IDX.verses:
upāsita⋅padāmbujas tvam anurakta⋅rudrādibhiḥ
prapadya puruṣottamaṁ padam adabhram udbhrājitaḥ
samasta⋅nata⋅maṇḍalī⋅sphurad⋅abhīṣṭa⋅kalpa⋅drumaḥ
śacī⋅suta mayi prabho kuru mukunda mande kṛpām

nu varṇayitum īśate gurutarāvatārāyitā
bhavantam uru⋅buddhayo na khalu sārvabhaumādayaḥ
paro bhavatu tatra kaḥ paṭur ato namas te paraṁ
śacī⋅suta mayi prabho kuru mukunda mande kṛpām

na jat katham api śrutāv upaniṣadbhir apy āhitaṁ
svayaṁ ca vivṛtaṁ na jad gurutarāvatārāntere
kṣipann asi rasāmbudhe tad iha bhākta⋅ratnaṁ kṣitau
śacī⋅suta mayi prabho kuru mukunda mande kṛpām

nija⋅praṇaya⋅visphuran⋅naṭana⋅raṅga⋅vismāpita
trinetra⋅nata⋅maṇḍala⋅prakaṭitānurāgāmṛta
ahaṅkṛti⋅kalaṅkitoddhata⋅janādi⋅durbodha he
śacī⋅suta mayi prabho kuru mukunda mande kṛpām

bhavanti bhuvi je narāḥ kalita⋅duṣkulotpattayas
tvam uddharasi tān api pracura⋅cāru⋅kāruṇyataḥ
iti pramuditāntaraḥ śaraṇam āśritas tvām ahaṁ
śacī⋅suta mayi prabho kuru mukunda mande kṛpām

mukhāmbuja⋅pariskhalan⋅mṛdula⋅vāṅ⋅madhūlī⋅rasa⋅
prasaṅga⋅janitākhila⋅praṇata⋅bhṛṅga⋅raṅgotkara
samasta⋅jana⋅maṅgala⋅prabhava⋅nāma⋅ratnāmbudhe
śacī⋅suta mayi prabho kuru mukunda mande kṛpām

mṛgāṅka⋅madhurānana sphurad⋅anidra⋅padmekṣaṇa
smita⋅stavaka⋅sundarādhara viśaṅkaṭoras⋅taṭa
bhujoddhata⋅bhujaṅgama⋅prabha manoja⋅koṭi⋅dyute
śacī⋅suta mayi prabho kuru mukunda mande kṛpām

ahaṁ kanaka⋅ketakī⋅kusuma⋅gaura duṣṭaḥ kṣitau
na doṣa⋅lava⋅darśitā vividha⋅doṣa⋅pūrṇe’pi te
ataḥ pravaṇayā dhiyā kṛpaṇa⋅vatsala tvāṁ bhaje
śacī⋅suta mayi prabho kuru mukunda mande kṛpām

idaṁ dharaṇī⋅maṇḍalotsava bhavat⋅padāṅkeṣu je
niviṣṭa⋅manaso narāḥ paripaṭhanti padyāṣṭakam
śacī⋅hṛdaya⋅nandana prakaṭa⋅kīrti⋅candra prabho
nija⋅praṇaya⋅nirbharaṁ vitara deva tebhyaḥ śubham
IDX.en_translation:
Your lotus feet are worshiped by devoted ones like Śiva and others. Having attained the supreme abode of the Supreme Lord, You shine greatly. For all assemblies of surrendered devotees, You are the shining wish-fulfilling tree that fulfills their desires. O son of Śacī, O my Lord, O giver of liberation, please show mercy to me, the dull-witted.

Indeed, even those with vast intelligence like Sārvabhauma are unable to fully describe You, who are considered a great incarnation. Who else, then, can be skillful enough? Therefore, I offer my supreme obeisances to You. O son of Śacī, O my Lord, O giver of liberation, please show mercy to me, the dull-witted.

That which was not revealed in any way by the scriptures or even the Upanisads, and that which was not even revealed by incarnations in other ages—O ocean of mellows, You are now scattering that jewel of devotion on this earth. O son of Śacī, O my Lord, O giver of liberation, please show mercy to me, the dull-witted.

You astonish the stage of dancing with Your own expanding love. You manifest the nectar of love for the assemblies of surrendered devotees, just like the three-eyed Lord Śiva. O You who are difficult to understand for those arrogant people tainted by false ego, O son of Śacī, O my Lord, O giver of liberation, please show mercy to me, the dull-witted.

Those people born on earth who have taken birth in low families—You deliver even them with Your abundant and beautiful mercy. Thus, with a joyful heart, I take refuge in You. O son of Śacī, O my Lord, O giver of liberation, please show mercy to me, the dull-witted.

From Your lotus-like mouth flows the sweet honey of gentle words. This creates a multitude of devotees who, like bees, are eager for that nectar. O ocean of jewel-like Holy Names which bring auspiciousness to all people! O son of Śacī, O my Lord, O giver of liberation, please show mercy to me, the dull-witted.

Your face is sweet like the moon, and Your eyes are like shining, ever-awake lotus flowers. Your lips are beautiful like a cluster of smiles, and Your chest is broad. Your arms are powerful like raised snakes, and Your splendor is like millions of Cupids. O son of Śacī, O my Lord, O giver of liberation, please show mercy to me, the dull-witted.

I am a wicked person on this earth, yet You are golden-colored like the ketakī flower. Even though I am full of various faults, You do not show even a trace of finding fault in me. Therefore, with a humble mind, I worship You, O Lord who are compassionate to the fallen. O son of Śacī, O my Lord, O giver of liberation, please show mercy to me, the dull-witted.

O festival for the entire world! For those people who, with their minds fixed on Your lotus footprints, recite these eight verses—O delight of Śacī's heart, O Lord whose fame shines like the moon—O Lord, please bestow upon them abundant good fortune born of Your own love.

IDX.first_line: vacanera agocara vrndavana dhamamaya
IDX.search: vacanera agocara vrndavana dhamamaya svaprakasa premanandaghana jahate prakata sukha nahi jaramrtyu duhkha krsnalilarasa anuksana radha krsna duhu prema jini laksavana hema dohara hillole rasasindhu cakora nayana prema kamarati kare dhyana piriti sukhera duhe bandhu
IDX.author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
vacanera agocara, vṛndāvana dhāmamaya,
svaprakāśa premānandaghana
jāhāte prakaṭa sukha, nāhi jarāmṛtyu⋅duḥkha,
kṛṣṇalīlārasa anukṣaṇa

rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa du̐hu prema, jini’ lakṣavāna hema,
dohāra hillole rasasindhu
cakora nayana⋅prema, kāmarati kare dhyāna,
pirīti sukhera du̐he bandhu

rādhikā preyasīvarā, bāma aṅge manoharā,
kanaka⋅keśava⋅kānti dhare
anurāga⋅rakta⋅śāḍī, nīlapaṭṭa manohārī,
pratyaṅge bhūṣaṇa śobhā kare

karaye locana pāna, rūpalīlā du̐hu prāṇa,
ānande magana sahacarī
veda⋅vidhi⋅agocara, ratana⋅vedīrapara,
seva niti kiśora⋅kiśorī

durlabha janama hena, nāhi bhaja hari kena,
ki lāgiyā mara bhava⋅bandhe
chāḍa anya kriyā⋅karma, nāhi dekha veda dharma,
bhakti kara kṛṣṇapadadvandve

viṣaya viṣama⋅gati, nāhi bhaja vrajapati,
śrī⋅nanda⋅nandana sukhasāra
svarga āra apavarga, saṁsāra narakabhoga,
sarvanāśa janamavikāra

dehe nā kariha āsthā, manda rīte jama śāstā,
duḥkhera samudre karmagati
dekhiyā śuniñā bhaja, sādhuśāstramata jama,
jugalacaraṇe kara rati

karmakāṇḍa, jñānakāṇḍa, kevala viṣera bhāṇḍa,
‘amṛta’ baliyā jebā khāya
nānā joni sadā phire, kadarya bhakṣaṇa kare,
tāra janma adhaḥpāte jāya

rādhā⋅kṛṣṇe nāhi rati, anya jane bale pati,
premabhakti kichu nāhi jāne
nāhi bhaktira sandhāna, bharame karaye dhyāna,
vṛthā tāra se chāra bhāvane

jñāna karma kare loka, nāhi jāne bhaktiyoga,
nānāmate hoiyā ajñāna
tāra kathā nāhi śuni, paramārtha⋅tattva jāni,
premabhakti bhaktagaṇa prāṇa

jagata vyāpaka hari, aja⋅bhava ājñākārī,
madhura madhura līlākathā
ei tattva jāne jei, parama uttama sei,
tā̐ra saṅga kariba sarvathā

parama īśvara⋅kṛṣṇa, tā̐he hoo atitṛṣṇa,
bhaja tā̐re vrajabhāva lañā
rasika⋅bhakata saṅge, vihara niyata raṅge,
vrajapure vasati kariñā

divāniśi bhāva⋅bhare, manete bhāvanā ka’re,
nandavraje rahiba sadāi
ei vākya satya jāna, kabhu ithe nāhi āna,
paramāṇa śrī⋅jīva gosā̐i

śrī⋅kṛṣṇa bhakatajana, tā̐hāra caraṇe mana,
āropiyā kathā anusāre
sakhīra sarvathā mata, hoiñā tā̐hāra jūtha,
sadā vihariba vrajapure

līlārasa⋅kathā gāna, jugalakiśora dhyāna,
prārthanā kariba abhilāṣe
jīvane maraṇe ei, āra kichu nāhi cāi,
kahe dīna narottama⋅dāse
IDX.en_translation:
Vṛndāvana is beyond the power of speech to describe—a spiritual abode dense with the bliss of divine love. In that place of manifest happiness there is no suffering from old age or death, and the mellows of Kṛṣṇa's pastimes are always present.

The love of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa defeats many-times refined gold; in their joyful waves, they are an ocean of mellows. Their eyes, like chakora birds, drink in that love; both are friends of the happiness of love.

Rādhā, the best of beloveds, is enchanting on the left side and bears the effulgence of golden Kṛṣṇa. Her sari is red with affection, and she wears charming blue silk; ornaments adorn her every limb.

Their eyes drink in the pastimes of beauty; both are the very life of the companions, who are immersed in joy. Beyond the perception of the Vedas, upon a jeweled altar, they serve the youthful Divine Couple daily.

Why do you not worship the Lord, having obtained such a rare human birth? Why do you die entangled in the bonds of material existence? Give up other ritualistic activities and do not look toward mundane religion; instead, perform devotional service to the lotus feet of Kṛṣṇa.

Material objects lead toward danger; you do not worship the Lord of Vraja, the essence of happiness, the son of Nanda. Heaven, impersonal liberation, and the enjoyment of hellish material existence—all are the destruction and perversion of human birth.

Do not place faith in the material body; the lord of death is a strict disciplinarian. The results of actions are an ocean of sorrow. Seeing and hearing this, worship according to the saintly scriptures and fix your attachment upon the lotus feet of the Divine Couple.

Fruitive activities and speculative knowledge are merely pots of poison; those who eat them thinking them to be nectar always wander through various species, eating abominable things, and their birth leads only to degradation.

One who has no attachment for Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa and yet calls another their lord knows nothing of loving devotional service. Such people have no trace of devotion; they meditate in illusion, and their worthless conceptions are in vain.

People perform knowledge and work but do not know the yoga of devotion, thus remaining ignorant in many ways. Do not listen to them; knowing the ultimate reality, loving devotion is the life of the devotees.

The Lord pervades the universe, and Brahmā and Śiva are His order-carriers; His pastimes are sweet. Whoever knows this truth is the most excellent, and I will always associate with such persons.

Kṛṣṇa is the Supreme Lord; become extremely thirsty for Him and worship Him with the mood of Vraja. In the company of devotees who relish divine mellows, always enjoy with great joy while residing in Vṛndāvana.

Day and night, filled with spiritual emotion and meditating in the mind, I will always reside in the Vraja of Nanda. Know this statement to be true and never bring anything else into it; this is the authoritative word of Śrī Jīva Gosvāmī.

Fix your mind upon the lotus feet of Kṛṣṇa's devotees and, according to their words, always become like the companions; joining their group, I will always wander in Vraja.

Singing the narrations of the mellows of His pastimes and meditating on the youthful Divine Couple, I will pray with longing. In life and in death I desire nothing else—so says humble Narottama dāsa.

IDX.first_line: vacya vacaka ei dui svarupa tomara
IDX.search: vacya vacaka ei dui svarupa tomara vacya tava sri vigraha cidanandakara vacaka svarupa tava sri krsnadi nama varnarupi sarvajiva ananda visrama
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
vācya, vācaka⋅ei dui svarūpa tomāra
vācya⋅tava śrī⋅vigraha cidānandākāra

vācaka⋅svarūpa⋅tava śrī⋅kṛṣṇādi nāma
varṇarūpī sarvajīva⋅ānanda⋅viśrāma

ei dui svarūpe tava ananta prakāśa
dayā kari’ deya jīve tomāra vilāsa

kintu jāniyāchi nātha, vācaka⋅svarūpa
vācyāpekṣā dayāmaya⋅ei aparūpa

nāma⋅nāmī bheda nāi⋅vedera vacana
tabu nāma⋅nāmī ha’te adhika karuṇa

kṛṣṇa⋅aparādhe jadi nāme śraddhā kari’
prāṇa bhari’ ḍāke nāma⋅rāma, kṛṣṇa, hari

aparādha dūre jāya, ānanda⋅sāgare
bhāse sei anāyāse rasera pāthāre

vigrahasvarūpa vācye aparādha kari’
śuddhanāmāśraye sei aparādhe tari

bhakativinoda māge śrī⋅rūpa⋅caraṇe
vācaka⋅svarūpa nāme rati anukṣaṇe
IDX.en_translation:
Vācya and Vācaka—these two are Your forms. Vācya is Your divine form, the embodiment of eternal knowledge and bliss.

Vācaka is Your holy name, which in the form of syllables is the resting place of all souls' bliss.

In these two forms, Your manifestation is endless; out of mercy, You grant Your divine pastimes to the living entities.

But I have known, O Lord, that Your name is even more merciful than Your form—this is wonderful.

The Vedas declare that there is no difference between the Name and the Named. Still, the Name is more compassionate.

If, even after committing offenses against Kṛṣṇa, one has faith in the Name and calls out names like Rāma, Kṛṣṇa, or Hari with a full heart,

then the offenses vanish, and that person floats effortlessly in the ocean of bliss, in the vast expanse of divine mellows.

Though one may offend the Deity form, one can cross over those offenses by taking shelter of the pure Name.

Bhaktivinoda begs at the lotus feet of Śrī Rūpa for constant attachment to the Holy Name.

IDX.first_line: vada vada hari chadma na kariha
IDX.search: vada vada hari chadma na kariha vipade bedhala desa e tattva janiya age palaola sravana dasana kesa tara pache pache locana vacana tara dohe dila bhanga mora mora kari ratra dine mari jamadute dekhe ranga
IDX.author: śrīla locana⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
vada vada hari, chadma nā kariha,
vipade beḍhala deśa
e tattva jāniyā, āge palāola,
śravaṇa daśana keśa

tāra pāche pāche, locana vacana,
tārā dohe dila bhaṅga
mora mora kari, rātra⋅dine mari,
jamadūte dekhe raṅga

sundara nagare, prati ghare ghare,
viṣama jamera thānā
daṇḍa je divasa, vatsara gaṇiche,
kona dine dibe hānā

dārā⋅putra⋅vadhū, jatana kariche,
sakali nimera titā
maraṇa samaye, hāte gale bāndhi,
mukhe jvāli dibe citā

vadana bhariyā, hari nā baliyā,
śamana taribe kise
dāsa locana, kahiyā phāraka,
maricha āpana doṣe
IDX.en_translation:
Speak, speak the Name of Lord Hari! Do not pretend. The country is surrounded by calamity. Knowing this truth, they have already fled—your ears, your teeth, and your hair.

Behind them, your eyes and your speech have both broken their promise. Saying "mine, mine," you die day and night while the messengers of the God of Death see this as a show.

In this beautiful city, in every house, there is a terrible outpost of the God of Death. Counting the days and years of your punishment, on which day will he finally strike?

Your wife, sons, and daughters-in-law all take care of themselves; everything is as bitter as neem. At the time of your death, they will bind your hands and neck and light the funeral pyre at your mouth.

If you do not wholeheartedly chant the Name of Lord Hari, how will you ever cross over the messenger of death? Locana Dāsa says clearly: "You are dying only due to your own fault."

IDX.first_line: vaikuntha hoite srestha mathura nagari
IDX.search: vaikuntha hoite srestha mathura nagari janama labhila jatha krsna candra hari mathura hoite srestha vrndavana dhama jatha sadhiyache hari rasotsava kama
IDX.author: śrīla bhaktisiddhānta sarasvatī ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
vaikuṇṭha hoite śreṣṭhā ‘mathurā’ nagarī
janama labhilā jathā kṛṣṇa⋅candra hari

mathurā hoite śreṣṭha ‘vṛndāvana⋅dhāma’
jathā sādhiyāche hari rāsotsava⋅kāma

vṛndāvana hoite śreṣṭha ‘govardhana⋅śaila’
giridhāri⋅gāndharvikā jathā krīḍā kaila

govardhana hoite śreṣṭha ‘rādhā⋅kuṇḍa⋅taṭa’
premāmṛte bhāsāila gokula⋅lampaṭa

govardhana⋅giritaṭa rādhā⋅kuṇḍa chāḍi’
anyatra je kare nija kuñja⋅puṣpavāḍī

nirvodha tāhāra sama keha nāhi āra
kuṇḍa⋅tīra sarvottama sthāna premādhāra
IDX.en_translation:
The city of Mathurā is superior even to the spiritual world, for there the Supreme Lord Kṛṣṇa appeared.

Superior even to Mathurā is the abode of Vṛndāvana, where the Lord fulfilled His desire for the Rāsa dance festival.

Superior even to Vṛndāvana is Govardhana Hill, where Lord Kṛṣṇa and Śrīmati Rādhā⋅rāṇī performed Their pastimes.

Superior even to Govardhana Hill is the bank of Rādhā-kuṇḍa, where the Lord of Gokula was immersed in the nectar of divine love.

Leaving Govardhana Hill and Rādhā-kuṇḍa, anyone who makes their place of worship elsewhere—there is no one as foolish as them.

The bank of Rādhā-kuṇḍa is the supreme place and the very abode of divine love.

IDX.first_line: vande giri vara dhara pada kamalam
IDX.search: vande giri vara dhara pada kamalam kamalakara kamalancitamamalam dhvaja vajrankusa pankaja kalitam vraja vanita kuca kunkuma lalitam
IDX.author: govinda dāsa
IDX.verses:
vande giri⋅vara⋅dhara⋅pada⋅kamalam
kamalākara⋅kamalāñcitamamalam

dhvaja⋅vajrāṅkuśa⋅paṅkaja⋅kalitam
vraja⋅vanitā⋅kuca⋅kuṅkuma⋅lalitam

mañjula⋅maṇi⋅nūpura⋅ramaṇīyam
acapala⋅kula⋅ramaṇī⋅kamanīyam

atilohitamatirohita⋅bhāṣam
madhu⋅madhupīkṛta⋅govinda⋅dāsam
IDX.en_translation:
I worship the lotus feet of the Holder of the mountain, which are spotlessly adorned by the Goddess of Fortune.

They are marked with the flag, thunderbolt, elephant goad, and lotus, and are beautiful with the saffron paste from the breasts of the women of Vraja.

They are charming with beautiful jeweled anklets and are desirable even to the most steady and noble women.

They are very reddish in color and have a very mysterious nature, making Govinda Dāsa into a honeybee enchanted by Their sweetness.

IDX.first_line: vande gurunisabhaktanisamisavatarakan
IDX.search: vande gurunisabhaktanisamisavatarakan tatprakasamsca tacchaktih krsnacaitanyasamjnakam mantraguru ara jata siksagurugana tahara carana age kariye vandana
IDX.author: kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa
IDX.verses:
vande gurunīśabhaktānīśamīśāvatārakān
tatprakāśāṁśca tacchaktīḥ kṛṣṇacaitanyasaṁjñakam

mantraguru āra jata śikṣāgurugaṇa
tā̐hāra caraṇa āge kariye vandana

śrī⋅rūpa sanātana bhaṭṭa⋅raghunātha
śrī-jīva gopāla-bhaṭṭa dāsa⋅raghunātha

ei chayaguru śikṣāguru je āmāra
tā̐’ sabāra pāda-padme koṭi namaskāra

bhagavānera bhakta jata śrīvāsa pradhāna
tā̐hāra caraṇa-padme sahasra praṇāma

advaita⋅ācārya prabhura aṁśa avatāra
tā̐’ra pāda-padme koṭi praṇati āmāra

nityānandarāya prabhura svarūpaprakāśa
tā̐’ra pāda-padma vando jā̐’ra muñi dāsa

gadādhara paṇḍitādi prabhura nijaśakti
tā̐’ sabāra caraṇe mora sahasra praṇati

śrī⋅kṛṣṇacaitanya prabhu svayaṁ bhagavān
tā̐hāra padāravinde ananta praṇāma
IDX.en_translation:
I offer my respectful obeisances to the spiritual masters, the devotees of the Lord, the Lord Himself, His incarnations, His manifestations, and His potencies, all of whom are known as Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya.

I first offer my obeisances to the feet of my initiating spiritual master and all my instructing spiritual masters.

Śrīla Rūpa Gosvāmī, Śrīla Sanātana Gosvāmī, Śrīla Raghunātha Bhaṭṭa Gosvāmī, Śrīla Jīva Gosvāmī, Śrīla Gopāla Bhaṭṭa Gosvāmī, and Śrīla Raghunātha Dāsa Gosvāmī—these six are my instructing spiritual masters.

To the lotus feet of all of them, I offer millions of obeisances.

To all the devotees of the Supreme Lord, headed by Śrīvāsa Paṇḍita—I offer a thousand obeisances to their lotus feet.

Advaita Ācārya is the Lord's plenary portion incarnation; to His lotus feet, I offer millions of obeisances.

Lord Nityānanda Rāya is the Lord's own manifested form; I offer obeisances to His lotus feet, of which I am a servant.

Gadādhara Paṇḍita and the other associates are the Lord's personal potencies; at the feet of all of them, I offer a thousand obeisances.

Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya is the Supreme Personality of Godhead Himself; at His lotus feet, I offer unlimited obeisances.

IDX.first_line: vande ham sri guroh sriyuta
IDX.search: vande ham sri guroh sriyuta pada kamalam sri gurun vaisnavams ca sri rupam sagrajatam saha gana raghunathanvitam tam sa jivam sadvaitam savadhutam parijana sahitam krsna caitanya devam sri radha krsna padan saha gana lalita sri visakhanvitams ca
IDX.author: śrīla bhaktisiddhānta sarasvatī ṭhākura
IDX.verses: vande 'haṁ śrī⋅guroḥ śrīyuta⋅ pada⋅kamalaṁ śrī⋅gurūn vaiṣṇavāṁś⋅ca śrī⋅rūpaṁ sāgrajātam saha⋅gaṇa⋅ raghunāthānvitaṁ taṁ sa⋅jīvam sādvaitaṁ sāvadhūtaṁ parijana⋅ sahitaṁ kṛṣṇa⋅caitanya⋅devaṁ śrī⋅rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa⋅pādān saha⋅gaṇa⋅ lalitā⋅śrī⋅viśākhānvitāṁś⋅ca śrī⋅guru⋅praṇāmaḥ ajñāna⋅timirāndhasya jñānāñjana⋅śalākayā cakṣur⋅unmīlitaṁ jena tasmai śrī⋅gurave namaḥ śrī⋅nārāyaṇa⋅vandanā vande 'haṁ śrī⋅guru⋅varam śrī⋅rūpānuga⋅pravaram vraja⋅rasa⋅rasikaṁ ca nārāyaṇam taṁ prapannam śrī⋅guru⋅caraṇaṁ vande ramaṇa⋅preṣṭhāya bhūtale, rūpānuga⋅bhaktidaṁ ca kṛpā⋅mūrtiṁ nārāyaṇam sevā⋅kuñje vraje ramye govardhana⋅girau sadā rādhā⋅kuṇḍe rasānande tat tat sevā⋅pradāyakam guruṁ nārāyaṇākhyam tam vande ramaṇa⋅preṣṭhakam jat pāda⋅saṁsmṛti⋅mātreṇa dāmodara prasīdati śrīla⋅vāmana⋅vandanā namo sajjana⋅sevaka sajjana⋅preṣṭhāya bhūtale bhakta⋅bāndhava rādhānātha⋅vallabha⋅iti⋅nāmine śrī⋅vinoda narottama narahari priyatama śrī⋅vedānta⋅bhāgavata ṣaḍ⋅gosvāmī suhṛda ca māyāpura⋅dhāma⋅preṣṭha gauḍīya⋅vaiṣṇava⋅sevaka tṛṇād api śrī⋅vigraha bhakti⋅vedānta vāmana śrīla⋅svāmi⋅vandanā namaḥ oṁ viṣṇu⋅pādāya kṛṣṇa⋅preṣṭhāya bhūtale śrīmate bhakti⋅vedānta⋅svāmin⋅iti⋅nāmine namas te sārasvate deve gaura⋅vāṇī⋅pracāriṇe nirviśeṣa⋅śūnyavādi⋅pāścātya⋅deśa⋅tāriṇe śrīla⋅keśava⋅vandanā namaḥ oṁ viṣṇu⋅pādāya ācārya⋅siŋha⋅rūpiṇe śrī⋅śrīmad⋅bhakti⋅prajñāna⋅keśava⋅iti⋅nāmine atimartya⋅caritrāya svāśritānāñca⋅pāline jīva⋅duḥkhe sadārtāya śrī⋅nāma⋅prema⋅dāyine gaurāśraya⋅vigrahāya kṛṣṇa⋅kāmaika⋅cāriṇe rūpānuga⋅pravarāya vinodeti⋅svarūpiṇe prabhupādāntaraṅgāya sarva⋅sadguṇa⋅śāline māyāvāda⋅tamoghnāya vedāntārtha⋅vide namaḥ śrīla⋅prabhupāda⋅vandanā namaḥ oṁ viṣṇu⋅pādāya kṛṣṇa⋅preṣṭhāya bhūtale śrīmate bhakti⋅siddhānta⋅sarasvatīti⋅nāmine śrī⋅vārṣabhānavī⋅devī⋅dayitāya kṛpābdhaye kṛṣṇa⋅sambandha⋅vijñāna⋅dāyine prabhave namaḥ mādhuryojjvala⋅premāḍhya⋅śrī⋅rūpānuga⋅bhakti⋅da śrī⋅gaura⋅karuṇā⋅śakti⋅vigrahāya namo’stu te namaste gaura⋅vāṇī śrī⋅mūrtaye dīna⋅tāriṇe śrī⋅rūpānuga⋅viruddhāpasiddhānta dhvānta⋅hāriṇe śrīla⋅gaura⋅kiśora⋅vandanā namo gaura⋅kiśorāya sākṣād⋅vairāgya⋅mūrtaye vipralambha⋅rasāmbhodhe pādāmbujāya te namaḥ śrīla⋅bhaktivinoda⋅vandanā namo bhakti⋅vinodāya saccidānanda⋅nāmine gaura⋅śakti⋅svarūpāya rūpānuga⋅varāya te śrīla⋅jagannātha⋅vandanā gaurāvirbhāva⋅bhūmes tvaṁ nirdeṣṭā sajjana⋅priyaḥ vaiṣṇava⋅sārvabhauma⋅ śrī⋅jagannāthāya te namaḥ śrī⋅vaiṣṇava⋅vandanā vāñchā⋅kalpa⋅tarubhyaś ca kṛpā⋅sindhubhya eva ca patitānāṁ pāvanebhyo vaiṣṇavebhyo namo namaḥ śrī⋅pañca⋅tattva⋅praṇāmaḥ pañca⋅tattvātmakaṁ kṛṣṇaṁ bhakta⋅rūpa⋅svarūpakam bhaktāvatāraṁ bhaktākhyaṁ namāmi bhakta⋅śaktikam śrī⋅nityānanda⋅praṇāmaḥ saṅkarṣaṇaḥ kāraṇa⋅toya⋅śāyī garbhoda⋅śāyī ca payobdhi⋅śāyī śeṣaś ca jasyāṁśa⋅kalāḥ sa nityānandākhya⋅rāmaḥ śaraṇaṁ mamāstu śrī⋅gaurāṅga⋅praṇāmaḥ namo mahā⋅vadānyāya kṛṣṇa⋅prema⋅pradāya te kṛṣṇāya ‘kṛṣṇa⋅caitanya’⋅nāmne gaura⋅tviṣe namaḥ śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅praṇāmaḥ he kṛṣṇa karuṇā⋅sindho dīna⋅bandho jagat⋅pate gopeśa gopikā⋅kānta rādhā⋅kānta! namo’stu te śrī⋅rādhā⋅praṇāmaḥ tapta⋅kāñcana⋅gaurāṅgi rādhe vṛndāvaneśvari! vṛṣabhānu⋅sute devi praṇamāmi hari⋅priye sambandhādhideva⋅praṇāmaḥ jayatāṁ suratau paṅgor mama manda⋅mater gatī mat⋅sarvasva⋅padāmbhojau rādhā⋅madana⋅mohanau dīvyad⋅vṛndāraṇya⋅kalpa⋅drumādhaḥ śrīmad⋅ratnāgāra⋅siŋhāsana⋅sthau śrī śrī rādhā⋅śrīla⋅govinda⋅devau preṣṭhālībhiḥ sevyamānau smarāmi śrīmān⋅rāsa⋅rasārambhī vaṁśīvaṭa⋅taṭa⋅sthitaḥ karṣan veṇu⋅svanair⋅gopīr⋅ gopīnāthaḥ śriye’stu naḥ śrī⋅tulasī⋅praṇāmaḥ vṛndāyai tulasī⋅devyai priyāyai keśavasya ca kṛṣṇa⋅bhakti⋅prade devi satyavatyai namo namaḥ śrī⋅pañca⋅tattva śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅caitanya prabhu⋅nityānanda śrī⋅advaita gadādhara śrīvāsādi⋅gaura⋅bhakta⋅vṛnda mahā⋅mantra hare kṛṣṇa hare kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa hare hare hare rāma hare rāma rāma rāma hare hare
IDX.en_translation:
I offer my obeisances to the lotus feet of my Spiritual Master, to the preceptors, and to the Vaiṣṇavas; to Śrīla Rūpa Gosvāmī with his elder brother (Śrīla Sanātana Gosvāmī), to Śrīla Jīva Gosvāmī, along with Śrīla Raghunātha Dāsa Gosvāmī and his associates.

I offer obeisances to Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya Deva, along with Śrī Advaita Ācārya, Śrī Nityānanda Avadhūta, and His associates; and to the lotus feet of Śrī Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa, accompanied by Śrīmatī Lalitā-devī, Śrīmatī Viśākhā-devī, and their associates.

[Śrī Guru Praṇāma]
I offer my respectful obeisances unto my Spiritual Master, who opened my eyes, which were blinded by the darkness of ignorance, with the torchlight of knowledge.

[Śrī Nārāyaṇa Vandanā]
I offer obeisances to the best of Gurus, the excellent follower of Rūpa, who is a connoisseur of the mellows of Vraja—to that Śrīla Bhaktivedānta Nārāyaṇa Gosvāmī Mahārāja, who is surrendered.

I offer obeisances to the lotus feet of the Guru, who is the beloved of the Lord on earth, who bestows Rūpānuga-bhakti, the embodiment of mercy, Śrīla Bhaktivedānta Nārāyaṇa Gosvāmī Mahārāja.

Who always bestows service in the delightful Seva-kuñja in Vraja, at Govardhana Hill, and at Rādhā-kuṇḍa, which is full of the bliss of rasa.

I offer obeisances to that Guru named Śrīla Bhaktivedānta Nārāyaṇa Gosvāmī Mahārāja, the beloved of the Lord, by the mere remembrance of whose feet Dāmodara becomes pleased.

[Śrīla Vāmana Vandanā]
Obeisances to the servant of the saintly, the beloved of the saintly on earth, the friend of the devotees, known as 'Rādhānātha-vallabha'.

Most dear to Śrīla Saccidānanda Bhaktivinoda Ṭhākura, Śrīla Narottama Dāsa Ṭhākura, and Narahari Sarakāra Ṭhākura; the well-wisher of Śrī Vedānta, the Śrīmad-Bhāgavatam, and the Six Gosvāmīs.

Dear to the abode of Māyāpura, the servant of the Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇavas, more humble than a blade of grass—the beautiful personality, Śrīla Bhaktivedānta Vāmana Gosvāmī Mahārāja.

[Śrīla Svāmī Vandanā]
I offer my respectful obeisances unto His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivedānta Svāmī Prabhupāda, who is very dear to Lord Kṛṣṇa on this earth, having taken shelter at His lotus feet.

Our respectful obeisances are unto you, O spiritual master, servant of Śrīla Bhaktisiddhānta Sarasvatī Gosvāmī Prabhupāda. You are kindly preaching the message of Lord Caitanyadeva and delivering the Western countries, which are filled with impersonalism and voidism.

[Śrīla Keśava Vandanā]
I offer my respectful obeisances unto Viṣṇupāda Śrī Śrīmad Bhaktiprajñāna Keśava Gosvāmī, who is like a lion among the Ācāryas.

Whose character is superhuman, the protector of those who take shelter of him, always distressed by the suffering of the jīvas, the giver of the Name and Love.

The embodiment of shelter in Gaura, whose only desire is Kṛṣṇa, the excellent follower of Rūpa, whose form is like Vinoda.

The intimate associate of Śrīla Bhaktisiddhānta Sarasvatī Gosvāmī Prabhupāda, possessor of all good qualities, the destroyer of the darkness of Māyāvāda, the knower of the meaning of Vedānta.

[Śrīla Prabhupāda Vandanā]
I offer my respectful obeisances unto His Divine Grace Śrīla Bhaktisiddhānta Sarasvatī Gosvāmī Prabhupāda, who is very dear to Lord Kṛṣṇa on this earth, having taken shelter at His lotus feet.

I offer my respectful obeisances to Śrī Vārṣabhānavī-devī-dayita Dāsa, who is favored by Śrīmatī Rādhā⋅rāṇī and who is the ocean of transcendental mercy and the deliverer of the science of Kṛṣṇa.

I offer my respectful obeisances unto you, the personified energy of Śrī Caitanya's mercy, who deliver devotional service which is enriched with conjugal love of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa, coming exactly in the line of revelation of Śrīla Rūpa Gosvāmī.

I offer my respectful obeisances unto you, who are the personified teachings of Lord Caitanya. You are the deliverer of the fallen souls. You do not tolerate any statement which is against the teachings of devotional service enunciated by Śrīla Rūpa Gosvāmī.

[Śrīla Gaura-kiśora Vandanā]
I offer my respectful obeisances unto Śrīla Gaura-kiśora Dāsa Bābājī Mahārāja, who is renunciation personified and always merged in the ocean of the mellow of separation from Kṛṣṇa.

[Śrīla Bhaktivinoda Vandanā]
I offer my respectful obeisances unto Śrīla Saccidānanda Bhaktivinoda Ṭhākura, who is the personification of the energy of Lord Caitanya and the best of the followers of Śrīla Rūpa Gosvāmī.

[Śrīla Jagannātha Vandanā]
Obeisances to you, Śrīla Jagannātha Dāsa Bābājī Mahārāja, the Emperor of the Vaiṣṇavas, dear to the saintly, who indicated the place of Gaura's appearance.

[Śrī Vaiṣṇava Vandanā]
I offer my respectful obeisances unto all the Vaiṣṇava devotees of the Lord. They are just like desire trees who can fulfill the desires of everyone, and they are oceans of mercy and the purifiers of the fallen souls.

[Śrī Pañca-tattva Praṇāma]
I offer my obeisances unto the Supreme Lord Kṛṣṇa, who appears in the form of a devotee (Lord Caitanya), as His expansion (Lord Nityānanda), His incarnation (Advaita Ācārya), His internal potency (Gadādhara), and His marginal potency (Śrīvāsa).

[Śrī Nityānanda Praṇāma]
May Śrī Nityānanda Rāma be the object of my constant remembrance. Saṅkarṣaṇa, Śeṣa Nāga and the Viṣṇus who lie on the Kāraṇa Ocean, Garbha Ocean, and ocean of milk are His plenary portions and the portions of His plenary portions.

[Śrī Gaurāṅga Praṇāma]
Obeisances to the most munificent incarnation, who bestows love of Kṛṣṇa—to Kṛṣṇa Himself, who has assumed the name Kṛṣṇa Caitanya and a golden complexion.

[Śrī Kṛṣṇa Praṇāma]
O my dear Kṛṣṇa, ocean of mercy, You are the friend of the distressed and the source of creation. You are the master of the cowherd men and the lover of the gopīs, especially Rādhā⋅rāṇī. I offer my respectful obeisances unto You.

[Śrī Rādhā Praṇāma]
Reverential obeisances unto you, O Rādhā, whose complexion is like molten gold and who are the Queen of Vṛndāvana. You are the daughter of King Vṛṣabhānu, and You are very dear to Lord Kṛṣṇa.

[Sambandha-adhideva Praṇāma]
Glory to the all-merciful Rādhā and Madana-mohana! They are the only shelter of my depraved and crippled intelligence. Their lotus feet are my everything.

[Abhidheya-adhideva Praṇāma]
In a temple of jewels in Vṛndāvana, underneath a desire tree, Śrī Śrī Rādhā-Govinda, served by Their most confidential associates, sit upon an effulgent throne. I offer my humble obeisances unto Them.

[Prayojana-adhideva Praṇāma]
Śrī Śrīla Gopīnātha, who originated the transcendental mellow of the rāsa dance, stands on the shore in Vaṁśīvaṭa and attracts the attention of the cowherd damsels with the sound of His celebrated flute. May they all confer upon us their benediction.

[Śrī Tulasī Praṇāma]
I offer my repeated obeisances unto Vṛndā, Tulasī-devī, who is very dear to Lord Keśava. O goddess, you bestow devotional service to Kṛṣṇa and you possess the highest truth.

[Śrī Pañca-tattva]
Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya, Prabhu Nityānanda, Śrī Advaita, Gadādhara, Śrīvāsa and all the devotees of Lord Gaura.

[Mahā-mantra]
Hare Kṛṣṇa, Hare Kṛṣṇa, Kṛṣṇa Kṛṣṇa, Hare Hare; Hare Rāma, Hare Rāma, Rāma Rāma, Hare Hare.

IDX.first_line: vande krsnam nanda kumaram
IDX.search: vande krsnam nanda kumaram nanda kumaram navanita cauram muni jana lobham mohana rupam murali lolam madana gopalam
IDX.verses:
vande kṛṣṇaṁ nanda⋅kumāram
nanda⋅kumāraṁ navanīta⋅cauram

muni⋅jana⋅lobhaṁ mohana⋅rūpam
muralī⋅lolaṁ madana⋅gopālam

śrīdharaṇīṣam jagadādhāram
veṇu⋅vilolaṁ vedānta⋅sāram

upātta⋅kavalaṁ parāga⋅śavalam
vande kṛṣṇaṁ nanda⋅kumāram
IDX.en_translation:
I worship Kṛṣṇa, Nanda's son. Nanda's son, the butter-thief.

Desired by the sages, of enchanting form. The one who delights in the flute, Madanagopāla.

The Lord of Śrī (Lakshmi) and Dharaṇī (Earth), the support of the universe. The one who delights in the flute, the essence of Vedānta.

Holding a morsel of food, smeared with dust. I worship Kṛṣṇa, Nanda's son.

IDX.first_line: vande sri vrsabhanu suta pada
IDX.search: vande sri vrsabhanu suta pada kamalanayana locanasampada nakhara mukurajita koti sudhakara madhava hrdaya cakora manohara
IDX.author: śrīla viśvanātha cakrabartī ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
vande śrī⋅vṛṣabhānu⋅sutā⋅pada
kamalanayana locanasampada

nakhara mukurajita koṭi sudhākara
mādhava⋅hṛdaya⋅cakora manohara

kamala⋅nuta saubhaga⋅rekhāñcita
lalitādi⋅kara⋅yāvaka⋅rañjita

saṁsevya giridhara mati maṇḍita
rāsa⋅vilāsa⋅naṭana⋅rasa paṇḍita
IDX.en_translation:
I worship the feet of the daughter of Śrī Vṛṣabhānu, whose lotus eyes are a treasure of beauty.

Her toenails are like mirrors that conquer millions of moons, captivating to the heart of Lord Mādhava.

Adorned with auspicious lines of beauty, She is painted with red lac dye by Lalitā and Her other friends.

Adorned with devotion that is served by the holder of the Hill, She is an expert in the mellows of the Rāsa dance.

IDX.first_line: vande visvambhara pada kamalam
IDX.search: vande visvambhara pada kamalam khandita kaliyuga janamala samalam saurabha karsita nijajana madhupam karuna khandita viraha vitapam
IDX.author: rādhāmohana dāsa
IDX.verses:
vande viśvambhara⋅pada⋅kamalam
khaṇḍita⋅kaliyuga⋅janamala⋅samalam

saurabha⋅karṣita⋅nijajana⋅madhupam
karuṇā⋅khaṇḍita⋅viraha⋅vitāpam

nāśita⋅hṛdgata⋅māyā⋅timiram
vara⋅nijakāntyā⋅jagatāmaciram

satata⋅virājita⋅nirupama⋅śobham
rādhāmohana⋅kalita⋅vilobham
IDX.en_translation:
I worship the lotus feet of Viśvambhara. He destroys all the impurities of the people of this age.

He attracts His devotees—who are like bees—with His fragrance. His mercy alleviates the distress of separation.

He destroys the darkness of illusion situated in the heart. By His own supreme effulgence, He quickly illuminates the world.

He is always resplendent with incomparable beauty. He is the charming Lord who enchants Rādhā.

IDX.first_line: vande vrndatavi candram radhikaksi mahotsavam
IDX.search: vande vrndatavi candram radhikaksi mahotsavam brahmatmananda dhik kari purnananda rasalayam caitanya caranam vande krsna bhakta janasrayam advaita mata dhaureya bharapanodanam param
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
vande vṛndāṭavī⋅candraṁ rādhikākṣi⋅mahotsavam
brahmātmānanda⋅dhik⋅kāri⋅pūrṇānanda⋅rasālayam

caitanya⋅caraṇaṁ vande kṛṣṇa⋅bhakta⋅janāśrayam
advaita⋅mata⋅dhaureya⋅bhārāpanodanaṁ param

guruṁ vande mahā⋅bhāgaṁ kṛṣṇānanda⋅svarūpakam
jan⋅mude racayiṣyāmi kalyāṇa⋅kalpa⋅pādapam

aprākṛta⋅rasānande na jasya kevalā ratiḥ
tasyedaṁ na samālocyaṁ pustakaṁ prema⋅sampuṭam

ayaṁ kalpa⋅taror nāma kalyāṇa⋅pādapaḥ śubhaḥ
vaikuṇṭha⋅nilaye bhāti vane niḥśreyasāhvake

tasya skandha⋅trayaṁ śuddhaṁ vartate viduṣāṁ mude
upadeśas tathā copalabdhis tūcchvāsakaḥ kila

āśritya pādapaṁ vidvān kalyāṇam labhate phalam
rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa⋅vilāseṣu dāsyaṁ vṛndāvane vane

sampūjya vaiṣṇavān viprān sarva⋅jīvāṁś ca nityaśaḥ
kīrtayāmi vinīto ‘haṁ gītaṁ vraja⋅rasāśritama
IDX.en_translation:
I offer my obeisances to the Moon of the Vṛndāvana forest, who is a great festival for Śrī Rādhikā's eyes. He is the abode of complete, nectar-like bliss that condemns the happiness of Brahman realization.

I offer my obeisances to the lotus feet of Śrī Caitanya, the shelter of the devotees of Kṛṣṇa. He is the supreme remover of the burden of the leaders of the Advaita philosophy.

I offer my obeisances to my Guru, who is greatly fortunate and the embodiment of the bliss of Kṛṣṇa. For his pleasure, I will compose this 'Kalyāṇa-kalpa-pādapa' (Wish-fulfilling Tree of Auspiciousness).

This book, which is a casket of love, should not be studied by one who does not have exclusive attachment to the bliss of transcendental rasa.

This auspicious wish-fulfilling tree named 'Kalyāṇa' shines in Vaikuṇṭha, in the forest called Niḥśreyasa (Supreme Good).

Its three pure branches exist for the delight of the learned: 'Upadeśa' (Instruction), 'Upalabdhi' (Realization), and 'Ucchvāsa' (Outpouring of Emotion).

Taking shelter of this tree, a learned person obtains the fruit of auspiciousness: servitude in the pastimes of Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa in the forest of Vṛndāvana.

Having honored the Vaiṣṇavas, the brāhmaṇas, and all living entities eternally, I, being humble, sing this song based on the rasa of Vraja.

IDX.first_line: varaja vipine jamuna kule
IDX.search: varaja vipine jamuna kule manca manohara sobhita phule vanaspati lata tusaye akhi tadupari kata dakaye pakhi
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
varaja⋅vipine jamunā⋅kūle
mañca manohara śobhita phule

vanaspati latā tuṣaye ā̐khi
tadupari kata ḍākaye pākhī

malaya anila vahaye dhīre
alikula madhu⋅ lobhete phire

vāsantīra rākā⋅ uḍupa tadā
kaumudī vitare ādare sadā

emata samaye rasikavara
ārambhila rāsa muralīdhara

śatakoṭī gopī⋅ mājhete hari
rādhā⋅saha nāce ānanda kari’

mādhava⋅mohinī gāiyā gīta
harila sakala jagata cita

sthāvara⋅jaṅgama mohilā satī
hārāola candrā⋅ valīra matī

mathiyā varaja⋅ kiśora mana
antarita haya rādhā takhana

bhakativinoda paramāda gaṇe
rāsa bhāṅgala (āji) rādhā vihane
IDX.en_translation:
In the groves of Vraja, on the banks of the Yamunā, there is an enchanting platform beautifully decorated with flowers.

The sight of the majestic trees and creepers delights the eyes, while many birds chirp upon their branches.

The gentle Malayan breeze blows softly, and swarms of bees hover around, greedy for honey.

The full moon of spring distributes its moonlight with constant affection.

At such a time, the best of connoisseurs, the Holder of the Flute, began the rāsa dance.

In the midst of hundreds of millions of gopīs, Hari dances with Rādhā in great joy.

Singing Her songs, the Enchantress of Mādhava stole the hearts of everyone in the world.

That chaste lady enchanted all moving and non-moving beings, and even Candrāvalī lost her composure.

Having churned the mind of the Youth of Vraja, Rādhā then suddenly disappeared.

Bhaktivinoda considers this a great calamity; today the rāsa dance has ended in the absence of Rādhā.

IDX.first_line: varaja vipine jamuna kulemanca manohara sobhita phule
IDX.search: varaja vipine jamuna kulemanca manohara sobhita phule vanaspati lata tusaye akhitadupari kata dakaye pakhi
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
varaja⋅vipine jamunā⋅kūlemañca manohara śobhita phule

vanaspati latā tuṣaye ā̐khitadupari kata ḍākaye pākhī

malaya anila vahaye dhīrealikula madhu⋅ lobhete phire

vāsantīra rākā⋅ uḍupa tadākaumudī vitare ādare sadā

emata samaye rasikavaraārambhila rāsa muralīdhara

śatakoṭī gopī⋅ mājhete harirādhā⋅saha nāce ānanda kari’

mādhava⋅mohinī gāiyā gītaharila sakala jagata cita

sthāvara⋅jaṅgama mohilā satīhārāola candrā⋅ valīra matī

mathiyā varaja⋅ kiśora manaantarita haya rādhā takhana

bhakativinoda paramāda gaṇerāsa bhāṅgala (āji) rādhā vihane
IDX.en_translation:
In the groves of Vraja, on the banks of the Yamunā, there is an enchanting platform beautifully decorated with flowers.

The sight of the majestic trees and creepers delights the eyes, while many birds chirp upon their branches.

The gentle Malayan breeze blows softly, and swarms of bees hover around, greedy for honey.

The full moon of spring distributes its moonlight with constant affection.

At such a time, the best of connoisseurs, the Holder of the Flute, began the rāsa dance.

In the midst of hundreds of millions of gopīs, Hari dances with Rādhā in great joy.

Singing Her songs, the Enchantress of Mādhava stole the hearts of everyone in the world.

That chaste lady enchanted all moving and non-moving beings, and even Candrāvalī lost her composure.

Having churned the mind of the Youth of Vraja, Rādhā then suddenly disappeared.

Bhaktivinoda considers this a great calamity; today the rāsa dance has ended in the absence of Rādhā.

IDX.first_line: varane tadit vasa taravali
IDX.search: varane tadit vasa taravali kamala manjari nama svananda sukhada dhama karpura seva lalitara gana radha juthesvari hana mamesvari natha sri nanda nandana amara parana dhana
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
varaṇe taḍit, vāsa tārāvalī,
kamala mañjarī nāma
svānanda⋅sukhada⋅dhāma

karpūra sevā, lalitāra gaṇa,
rādhā jūtheśvarī hana
mameśvarī⋅nātha, śrī⋅nanda⋅nandana,
āmāra parāṇa dhana

śrī⋅rūpa mañjarī, prabhṛtira sama,
jugala sevāya āśa
avaśya serūpa, sevā pāba āmi,
parākāṣṭhā suviśvāsa

kabe vā e dāsī, saṁsiddhi labhibe,
rādhā⋅kuṇḍe vāsa kari’
rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa⋅sevā, satata karibe,
pūrva smṛti parihari’
IDX.en_translation:
Her complexion is like lightning, her garments are like a constellation of stars, and her name is Kamala Mañjarī. She is an abode of self-bliss and happiness.

Camphor is in her service; among Lalitā's associates, Rādhā is the leader of the group. The Lord of my mistress is Śrī⋅nanda⋅nandana; He is the treasure of my life.

Like Śrī Rūpa Mañjarī and other friends, my hope is for the service of the Divine Couple. Certainly I will attain such service; this is my supreme and firm faith.

When will this maidservant attain spiritual perfection, residing in Rādhā-kuṇḍa? Constantly engaged in the service of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa, abandoning all previous memories?

IDX.first_line: vibhavari sesa aloka pravesa
IDX.search: vibhavari sesa aloka pravesa nidra chadi utha jiva bala hari hari mukunda murari rama krsna hayagriva nrsiŋha vamana srimadhusudana vrajendranandana syama putana ghatana kaitabha satana jaya dasarathi rama
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
vibhāvarī⋅śeṣa, āloka praveśa,
nidrā chāḍi’ uṭha jīva
bala’ hari hari, mukunda murāri,
rāma kṛṣṇa hayagrīva

nṛsiŋha vāmana, śrī-madhusūdana,
vrajendranandana śyāma
pūtanā⋅ghātana, kaiṭabha⋅śātana,
jaya dāśarathi⋅rāma

jaśodā⋅dulāla, govinda⋅gopāla,
vṛndāvana⋅purandara
gopīpriya⋅jana, rādhikā⋅ramaṇa,
bhuvana⋅sundaravara

rāvaṇāntakara, mākhana⋅taskara,
gopī⋅jana⋅vastrahārī
vrajera rākhāla, gopa-vṛndapāla,
citta-hārī vaṁśī-dhārī

jogīndra⋅vandana, śrī⋅nanda⋅nandana,
vraja⋅jana⋅bhayahārī
navīna nīrada, rūpa manohara,
mohana⋅vaṁśīvihārī

jaśodā⋅nandana, kaṁsa⋅nisūdana,
nikuñjarāsa⋅vilāsī
kadamba⋅kānana, rāsa-parāyaṇa,
vṛndāvipina⋅nivāsī

ānanda⋅vardhana, prema⋅niketana,
phulaśara⋅yojaka kāma
gopāṅganā-gaṇa⋅, citta⋅vinodana,
samasta⋅guṇagaṇa⋅dhāma

jāmuna⋅jīvana, keli⋅parāyaṇa,
mānasacandra⋅cakora
nāma⋅sudhārasa, gāo kṛṣṇa⋅yaśa,
rākha vacana mana mora
IDX.en_translation:
The night ends and light enters; rise up, O soul, and leave your sleep. Chant the names of Hari, Mukunda, Murāri, Rāma, Kṛṣṇa, and Hayagrīva.

Chant the names of Nṛsiṁha, Vāmana, Śrī Madhusūdana, and Śyāma. All glories to the killer of Pūtanā, the destroyer of Kaiṭabha, and to Rāma, the son of Daśaratha.

He is Yaśodā's darling, Govinda, Gopāla, and the Lord of Vṛndāvana. He is the beloved of the gopīs, the lover of Rādhā, and the most beautiful in all the world.

He is the destroyer of Rāvaṇa, the butter-thief, and the one who stole the clothes of the gopīs. He is the cowherd of Vraja, the protector of the cowherds, the captivator of the mind, and the player of the flute.

He is worshiped by the great yogis—the son of Nanda who removes the fears of the residents of Vraja. Like a fresh cloud with a charming form, He is the enchanting flute-player.

He is the son of Yaśodā, the killer of Kaṁsa, and the one who enjoys the rāsa dance in the groves. In the kadamba forest, He is absorbed in the rāsa dance as a resident of the forests of Vṛndāvana.

He is the enhancer of bliss, the abode of love, and more enchanting than Cupid. He is the enchanter of the minds of the cowherd girls and the abode of all good qualities.

He is the life of the Yamunā, absorbed in pastimes—the moon for the chakora bird of the mind. O my mind, sing the glories of Kṛṣṇa, the nectar of the Holy Name, and follow these words of mine.

IDX.first_line: vibhavita rati jabe kriyapara haye tabe
IDX.search: vibhavita rati jabe kriyapara haye tabe anubhava haya ta udita cittabhava udghatiya kare bahya suvikriya jakhana je haya ta ucita nrtya gita vilunthana krosana tanumotana hunkara jrmbhana ghanasvasa lokanapeksita mati lalasrava ghurna ati hikkadaya atta atta hasa
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
vibhāvita⋅rati jabe, kriyāpara ha’ye tabe,
anubhāva haya ta’ udita
citta-bhāva udghāṭiyā, kare bāhya suvikriyā,
jakhana je haya ta’ ucita

nṛtya, gīta, viluṇṭhana, krośana, tanu-moṭana,
huṅkāra, jṛmbhaṇa, ghanaśvāsa
lokānapekṣitā mati, lālāsrāva, ghūrṇā ati,
hikkādaya, aṭṭa aṭṭa hāsa

gātra⋅citta⋅jāta saba, ‘alaṅkāra’ suvaibhava,
nigadita viṁśati prakāra
‘udbhāsvara’ nāma tāra, dhammilla⋅sraṁsana āra,
phullaghrāṇa, nīvyādi vikāra

vilāpālāpa, saŋlāpa, pralāpa o anulāpa,
apalāpa, sandeśātideśa
apadeśa, upadeśa, nirdeśa o vyapadeśa,
‘vācika’ ānubhāvera viśeṣa
IDX.en_translation:
When awakened love becomes active, then external manifestations of emotion arise. Revealing the inner emotions, it creates excellent external transformations, whatever is appropriate at that time.

Dancing, singing, rolling on the ground, crying loudly, twisting the body, roaring, yawning, heavy breathing, disregard for public opinion, salivation, excessive reeling, hiccups and so on, and loud laughter.

All born from the body and mind, these 'ornaments' are excellent manifestations, declared to be twenty types. One is named 'udbhāsvāra'; and disheveling of hair, a radiant scent, and disarray of clothing are other transformations.

Lamentation, conversation, incoherent talk, repetition, denial, excessive messages, pretense, advice, commands, and indirect references—these are specific types of verbal manifestations.

IDX.first_line: viracaya mayi dandam dina bandho dayam va
IDX.search: viracaya mayi dandam dina bandho dayam va gatir iha na bhavattah kacid anya mamasti nipatatu satakotir nirmalam va navambhas tad api kila payodah stuyate catakena pracinanam bhajanam atulam duskaram srnvato me nairasyena jvalati hrdayam bhakti lesalasasya visvadricim aghahara tavakarnya karunya vicim asa vinduksitam idam upaity antare hanta saityam
IDX.author: śrīla rūpa gosvāmī
IDX.verses:
viracaya mayi daṇḍaṁ dīna⋅bandho dayāṁ vā
gatir iha na bhavattaḥ kācid anyā mamāsti
nipatatu śatakoṭir nirmalaṁ vā navāmbhas
tad api kila payodaḥ stūyate cātakena

prācīnānāṁ bhajanam atulaṁ duṣkaraṁ śṛṇvato me
nairāśyena jvalati hṛdayaṁ bhakti⋅leśālasasya
viṣvadrīcīm aghahara tavākarṇya kāruṇya⋅vīcīm
āśā⋅vindūkṣitam idam upaity antare hanta śaityam
IDX.en_translation:
O friend of the distressed! Whether You punish me or show me mercy, I have no other refuge but You. Even if a hundred million thunderbolts or pure fresh rain should fall, the chataka bird still praises the cloud.

As I hear of the incomparable and difficult worship of the ancient devotees, my heart—devoid of even a trace of devotion—burns with despair. But upon hearing of Your all-pervading wave of compassion, O remover of sins, this heart, sprinkled with a drop of hope, attains coolness within.

IDX.first_line: virajara pare suddhaparavyoma dhama
IDX.search: virajara pare suddhaparavyoma dhama tadupari sri gokula vrndaranya nama vrndavana cintamani cidananda ratnakhani cinmaya apurva darasana tahi majhe camatkara krsna vanaspati sara nilamani tamala jemana
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
virajāra pāre, śuddhaparavyoma⋅dhāma
tadupari śrī⋅gokula⋅‘vṛndāraṇya’⋅nāma

vṛndāvana cintāmaṇi, cidānanda⋅ratnakhani,
cinmaya apūrva⋅daraśana
ta̐hi mājhe camatkāra, kṛṣṇa vanaspati⋅sāra,
nīlamaṇi tamāla jemana

tā̐he eka svarṇamayī, latā sarvadhāma⋅jayī,
uṭhiyāche paramapāvanī
hlādinī⋅śaktira sāra, ‘mahābhāva’ nāma jā’ra,
tribhuvanamohana⋅mohinī

‘rādhā’⋅nāme paricita, tuṣiyā govinda⋅cita,
virājaye parama⋅ānande
sei latā⋅patra⋅phula, lalitādi sakhīkula,
sabe mili’ vṛkṣe dṛḍha bāndhe

latāra paraśe praphulla tamāla
latā chāḍi’ nāhi rahe konakāla

tamāla chāḍiyā latā nāhi bā̐ce
se latā⋅milana sadā-kāla jāce

bhakativinoda milana dohāra
nā cāhe kakhana vinā kichu āra
IDX.en_translation:
On the other side of Virajā river, is the pure transcendental abode. Above that is Śrī Gokula, named the forest of Vṛndāvana.

Vṛndāvana is a Cintāmaṇi, a mine of transcendental bliss jewels. It is a spiritual, unparalleled vision. Within it is a wonder: Kṛṣṇa is the essence of all trees, like a sapphire-like Tamāla tree.

Upon Him is a golden creeper, conquering all abodes, risen, supremely purifying. She is the essence of the bliss-giving potency, whose name is Mahābhāva (the highest state of ecstatic love). She is enchanting even the enchanter of the three worlds.

Known by the name Rādhā, pleasing the heart of Govinda, she shines in supreme bliss. That creeper's leaves and flowers, the group of confidantes like Lalitā, all together, firmly bind the tree.

By the touch of the creeper, the Kṛṣṇa tree is joyful. Without the creeper, it cannot exist at any time.

Without the Tamāla, the creeper cannot survive. That creeper always yearns for union.

Bhaktivinoda sees their union, and desires nothing else at any time.

IDX.first_line: visaya vasanarupa cittera vikara
IDX.search: visaya vasanarupa cittera vikara amara hrdaye bhoga kare anivara kata je jatana ami karilama haya na gela vikara buddhi sese prana jaya
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
viṣaya⋅vāsanārūpa cittera vikāra
āmāra hṛdaye bhoga kare anivāra

kata je jatana āmi karilāma hāya
nā gela vikāra buddhi śeṣe prāṇa jāya

e ghora vikāra more karila asthira
śānti nā pāila sthāna, antara adhīra

śrī⋅rūpa gosvāmī more kṛpā vitariyā
uddhāribe kabe jukta-vairāgya arpiyā

kabe sanātana more chāḍāye viṣaya
nityānande samarpibe hoiyā sadaya

śrī⋅jīva gosvāmī kabe siddhānta⋅salile
nibhāibe tarkānala, citta jāhe jvale

śrī⋅caitanya⋅nāma śune udibe pulaka
rādhā⋅kṛṣṇāmṛta⋅pāne hoiba aśoka

kāṅgālera sukāṅgāla durjana e⋅jana
vaiṣṇava⋅caraṇāśraya jāce akiñcana
IDX.en_translation:
The distortions of the mind in the form of material desires In my heart, they enjoy uncontrollably.

Alas, how much effort I made! The distorted intelligence did not leave, and finally my life departs.

These terrible distortions made me restless. Peace found no place, my inner self is agitated.

When will Śrī Rūpa Gosvāmī bestow mercy upon me, and deliver me by offering appropriate renunciation?

When will Sanātana Gosvāmī free me from material objects, and, becoming merciful, surrender me to Śrī Nityānanda Prabhu?

When will Śrī Jīva Gosvāmī, with the waters of established philosophical conclusions, extinguish the fire of arguments, in which my mind burns?

Hearing the name of Śrī Caitanya Mahāprabhu, when will ecstasy arise, and by drinking the nectar of Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa, when will I become free from sorrow?

This person is a wicked soul, poorer than the poorest, a humble soul who begs for the shelter of the feet of the devotees of Viṣṇu.

IDX.first_line: visaye sakale matta nahi krsnanama tattva
IDX.search: visaye sakale matta nahi krsnanama tattva bhaktisunya hoila avani kalikala sarpavise dagdha jiva mithya rase na janaye keba se apani nija kanya putrotsave matiya achaye sabe nahi anya subha karmalesa jaksa puje madya mamse nanarupa jiva himse ei mata haila sarvadesa
IDX.author: vaiṣṇava⋅dāsa
IDX.verses:
viṣaye sakale matta, nāhi kṛṣṇa-nāma⋅tattva,
bhaktiśūnya hoila avanī
kalikāla⋅sarpaviṣe, dagdha jīva mithyā⋅rase,
nā jānaye kebā se āpani

nija kanyā⋅putrotsave, mātiyā āchaye sabe,
nāhi anya śubha karmaleśa
jakṣa pūje madya⋅māṁse, nānārūpa jīva hiṁse,
ei mata haila sarva-deśa

dekhiyā karuṇā kari’, ‘kama-lākṣa’ nāma dhari’,
avatīrṇa hailā gauḍadeśe
vraja⋅rāja⋅kumāra, sāṅgopāṅga avatāra,
karāiba ei abhilāṣe

sarva āge āguyāna, jīvere kariyā trāṇa,
śāntipure hoilā prakāśa
sakala duṣkṛti jā’be, sabe kṛṣṇa-nāma pā’be,
kahe dīna vaiṣṇavera dāsa
IDX.en_translation:
Everyone is intoxicated by material sense objects and knows nothing of the truth of the Holy Name of Kṛṣṇa. The entire world has become devoid of devotion. Bitten by the poisonous snake of Kali-yuga, living entities are scorched by the false tastes of illusion, and they do not even know who they really are.

Everyone is busy celebrating the festivals of their own sons and daughters, and there is not even a trace of any other auspicious activity. They worship demons with meat and wine and commit violence against various living beings. This is the condition throughout the land.

Seeing this and feeling great compassion, Lord Kṛṣṇa, assuming the name 'Kamalākṣa' (the Lotus-eyed One), descended in the land of Gauḍa. His only desire was to cause the Prince of Vraja to incarnate along with all His associates.

Taking the lead before everyone else to deliver the living entities, He manifested Himself in Śāntipura. Vaiṣṇava Dāsa says: "All sins will now be destroyed, and everyone will receive the Holy Name of Kṛṣṇa."

IDX.first_line: visuddha vairagi kare nama sankirtana
IDX.search: visuddha vairagi kare nama sankirtana magiya khaiya kare jivana japana vairagi haiya jeva kare parapeksa karyasiddhi nahe krsna karena upeksa
IDX.verses:
viśuddha vairāgī kare nāma⋅saṅkīrtana
māgiyā khāiyā kare jīvana⋅jāpana

vairāgī haiyā jevā kare parāpekṣā
kāryasiddhi nahe, kṛṣṇa karena upekṣā

vairāgī haiyā kare jihvāra lālasa
paramārtha jāya, āra haya rasera vaśa

vairāgī karibe sadā nāma⋅saṅkīrtana
śāka⋅patra⋅phala⋅mūle udara bharaṇa

jihvāra lālase jei samāje veḍāya
śiśnodara⋅parāyaṇa kṛṣṇa nāhi pāya

vairāgī bhāi grāmyakathā nā śunibe kāṇe
grāmyavārtā nā kahibe jave milibe āne

svapaneo nā kara bhāi strī⋅sambhāṣaṇa
gṛhe strī chāḍiyā bhāi āsiyācha vana

jadi cāha praṇaya rākhite gaurāṅgera sane
choṭa haridāsera kathā thāke jena mane

bhāla nā khāibe, āra bhāla nā paribe
hṛdayete rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa sarvadā sevibe

baḍa hari⋅dāsera nyāya kṛṣṇa⋅nāma balibe vadane
aṣṭa⋅kāla rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa sevibe kuñja⋅vane
IDX.en_translation:
A pure renunciant performs the congregational chanting of the Holy Names. He maintains his life only by eating what he receives through begging.

If a renunciant depends on others for his needs, his purpose is not achieved, and Lord Kṛṣṇa neglects him.

If a renunciant indulges the desires of his tongue, his spiritual goal is lost, and he becomes a slave to material tastes.

A renunciant should always perform the congregational chanting. He should fill his belly only with vegetables, leaves, fruits, and roots.

One who wanders through society just to satisfy the desires of the tongue, being dedicated only to his stomach and genitals, will never attain Lord Kṛṣṇa.

O renunciant brother! Do not let mundane talk enter your ears. Do not speak of worldly news when you meet others.

O brother! Do not even converse with women in your dreams. Remember, you have left your wife at home to come to the forest of renunciation.

If you wish to maintain your love for Lord Gaurāṅga, then always keep the story of Choṭa Haridāsa in your mind.

Do not eat palatable foods, and do not wear fine clothes. Always serve Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa within your heart.

Like the senior Haridāsa Ṭhākura, always chant the Holy Names of Kṛṣṇa. During all eight periods of the day, serve Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa in the groves of Vṛndāvana.

IDX.first_line: visve udita nama tapana
IDX.search: visve udita nama tapana avidya vinasa lagi chodata saba maya vibhava sadhu tahe anuragi harinama prabhakara avidya timirahara tomara mahima keba jane ke hena panditajana tomara mahatmyagana uccaihsvare sakala bakhane
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
viśve udita, nāma⋅tapana,
avidyā⋅vināśa lāgi’
choḍata saba, māyā⋅vibhava,
sādhu tāhe anurāgī

harināma⋅prabhākara, avidyā⋅timirahara,
tomāra mahimā kebā jāne
ke hena paṇḍitajana, tomāra māhātmyagaṇa,
uccaiḥsvare sakala bākhāne

tomāra ābhāsa pahilahi bhāya
e bhava⋅timira kavalitaprāya

acire timira nāśiyā prajñāna
tattvāndha⋅nayane karena vidhāna

sei ta’ prajñāna viśuddhā bhakati
upajāya hari⋅viṣayiṇī mati

e adbhutalīlā satata tomāra
bhakativinoda jāniyāche sāra
IDX.en_translation:
Risen in the world, the sun of the Holy Name destroys ignorance. Giving up all the opulence of illusion, the saintly soul becomes devoted to it.

O Holy Name of Hari! You are the sun, the dispeller of the darkness of ignorance. Who indeed knows Your glory? What learned person can recount Your many glories and proclaim them all in a loud voice?

Your glimmer first appears, and the darkness of this material existence is almost swallowed.

Quickly destroying the darkness, supreme knowledge bestows sight upon the eyes that were blind to reality.

That supreme knowledge is indeed pure devotion, which awakens an awakens an inclination focused on the Lord.

This wonderful pastime is always Yours; Bhaktivinoda has understood its essence.

IDX.first_line: vraja dhama nityadhana radha krsna duijana
IDX.search: vraja dhama nityadhana radha krsna duijana lilavese ekatanu hana dhamasaha gaudadese prakata hoila ese nija nitya parisada lana mana tumi satya bali jana navadvipe gaura hari nama sankirtana kari premamrta gaude kaila dana
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
vraja⋅dhāma nityadhana, rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa duijana,
līlāveśe ekatanu hañā
dhāmasaha gauḍadeśe, prakaṭa hoilā ese’,
nija nitya⋅pāriṣada lañā

mana, tumi satya bali’ jāna
navadvīpe gaura⋅hari, nāma⋅saṅkīrtana kari’,
premāmṛta gauḍe kaila dāna

sannyāsera chala kari’, nīlācale sei hari,
śrī⋅kṛṣṇacaitanya jatīśvara
dāmodara, rāmānanda, laye kari’ parānanda,
gūḍhatattva jānāya vistara

raghunāthe sei tattva, śikhāiyā paramārtha,
pāṭhāilā śrī⋅rūpera kāche
śrī⋅dāsagosvāmī vraje, rūpasaha kṛṣṇa⋅bha’je,
‘manaḥśikṣā’⋅śloka likhiyāche

tā̐hāra dāsera dāsa, haite jā’ra baḍa āśa,
e bhaktivinoda akiñcana
‘manaḥśikṣā bhāṣā’ gāya, jathā śuddhabhakta pāya,
dayā kari’ karena śravaṇa
IDX.en_translation:
Vraja-dhāma is the eternal wealth, the two persons Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa, Who have become one body absorbed in divine pastimes. Along with their abode, they have appeared in Gauḍa-deśa, Bringing their own eternal associates with them.

O mind, know this as the absolute truth: In Navadvīpa, Gaura⋅hari, performing nāma-saṅkīrtana, Distributed the nectar of prema throughout Gauḍa.

Under the pretext of sannyāsa, that same Hari in Nīlācala, Is Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya, the master of ascetics. Taking Dāmodara and Rāmānanda, feeling supreme bliss, He revealed deep confidential truths in great detail.

Teaching those truths and the ultimate goal to Raghunātha, He sent him to the presence of Śrī Rūpa. Śrī Dāsa Gosvāmī in Vraja, worshipping Kṛṣṇa with Rūpa, Has written the verses of "Manaḥ-śikṣā."

To be the servant of the servant of those whose hope is great, This Bhaktivinoda is most humble and possesses nothing. He sings this "Manaḥ-śikṣā Bhāṣā" wherever pure devotees are found, May they mercifully grant him a hearing.

IDX.first_line: vraja jana mana sukhakariradhe syama syama syama
IDX.search: vraja jana mana sukhakariradhe syama syama syama mora mukuta makarakrta kundalagala vaijayanti malacaranana nupura rasalaradhe syama syama syama
IDX.author: śrī śrīmad bhaktivedānta nārāyaṇa gosvāmī mahārāja
IDX.verses: vraja⋅jana mana sukhakārīrādhe⋅śyāma śyāmā śyāma mora mukuṭa makarākṛta kuṇḍala,gala vaijayantī mālacaraṇana nūpura rasāla,rādhe⋅śyāma śyāmā śyāma sundara vadana kamala dala locana,bā̐kī citavanahārīmohana vaṁśīvihārī,rādhe⋅śyāma śyāmā śyāma vṛndāvaname̐ dhenu carāve,gopī⋅jana manahārīśrī⋅govardhana dhārī,rādhe⋅śyāma śyāmā śyāma rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa mili ava doū,gaura rūpa avatārīkīrtana dharma pracārī,rādhe⋅śyāma śyāmā śyāma tuma vina mere aura na koī,nāma rūpa avatārīcaraṇaname̐ balihārī,nārāyaṇa balihārī,rādhe⋅śyāma śyāmā śyāma
IDX.translation_intro: Śrīla Bhakti Prajñana Keśava Gosvāmī Mahārāja wrote: “Nārāyaṇa balihārī, Rādhe-śyāma Śyāmā-śyām” after it was written by Śrīla Gurudeva.
IDX.en_translation:
Rādhā-Śyām, Śyāmā-Śyām! You give happiness to the minds of the people of Vraja.

Peacock-feather crown, shark-shaped earrings, Vaijayantī garland around the neck. Sweet ankle-bells on the feet. Rādhā-Śyām, Śyāmā-Śyām!

Beautiful face, eyes like lotus petals, Your crooked glance is captivating. Enchanting player of the flute. Rādhā-Śyām, Śyāmā-Śyām!

Grazing cows in Vṛndāvana, stealing the minds of the gopīs. Holder of Śrī Govardhana. Rādhā-Śyām, Śyāmā-Śyām!

Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa, both have now met, incarnating as the Golden Form (Gaura). Preaching the religion of kīrtana. Rādhā-Śyām, Śyāmā-Śyām!

Without You, I have no one else; the Name and Form are the Avatāra. I surrender at Your feet; Nārāyaṇa (Śrīla Bhaktivedānta Nārāyaṇa Gosvāmī) surrenders. Rādhā-Śyām, Śyāmā-Śyām!

IDX.first_line: vrajendranandana bhaje jei janasaphala jivana taratahara upama vede nahi simatribhuvane nahi ara
IDX.search: vrajendranandana bhaje jei janasaphala jivana taratahara upama vede nahi simatribhuvane nahi ara emana madhava na bhaje manavakakhana mariya jabesei se adhama prahariya jamaraurava krmite khabe
IDX.author: śrīla locana⋅dāsa ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
vrajendra-nandana, bhaje jei jana,saphala jīvana tā’ratāhāra upamā, vede nāhi sīmā,tri-bhuvane nāhi āra

emana mādhava, nā bhaje mānava,kakhana mariyā jābesei se adhama, prahāriyā jama,raurava kṛmite khābe

tārapara āra, pāpī nāhi chāra,saṁsāra jagat⋅mājhekonakāle tā’ra, gati nāhi āra,michāi bhramiche kāje

śrīlocana⋅dāsa, bhakatira āśa,hariguṇa kahi likhihena rasa⋅sāra, mati nāhi jā’ra,tā’ra mukha nāhi dekhi
IDX.en_translation:
Whoever worships the son of the King of Vraja, their life is successful. There is no equal to them mentioned in the Vedas, and in the three worlds there is no other.

If a human does not worship such a Lord, when they die, that wretched person will be struck by the lord of death, and worms in a hellish planet will consume them.

Thereafter, the sinner will find no release in the material world. For them, there is never any progress or destination; they are uselessly wandering in their activities.

Śrī Locana dāsa, hoping for devotion, speaks and writes the glories of Hari. I do not even wish to see the face of one who does not have the intelligence to appreciate such essential nectar.

IDX.first_line: vrndavane viharatoriha kelikunje
IDX.search: vrndavane viharatoriha kelikunje matta dvipa pravara kautuka vibhramena sandarsayasva juvayorvadanaravinda dvandvam vidhehi mayi devi krpam prasida ha devi kaku bhara gadgada jadya vaca jace nipatya bhuvi dandavadudbhatartih asya prasadamabudhasya janasya krtva gandharvike nijagane gananam vidhehi
IDX.author: śrīla rūpa gosvāmī
IDX.verses:
vṛndāvane viharatoriha kelikuñje
matta⋅dvipa⋅pravara⋅kautuka⋅vibhrameṇa
sandarśayasva juvayorvadanāravinda⋅
dvandvaṁ vidhehi mayi devi! kṛpāṁ prasīda

hā devi! kāku⋅bhara⋅gadgada⋅jāḍya vācā
jāce nipatya bhuvi daṇḍavadudbhaṭārtiḥ
asya prasādamabudhasya janasya kṛtvā
gāndharvike! nijagaṇe gaṇanāṁ vidhehi

śyāme! ramā⋅ramaṇa⋅sundaratā⋅variṣṭha⋅
saundarya⋅mohita⋅samasta⋅jagajjanasya
śyāmasya vāma⋅bhuja⋅baddhatanuṁ kadāhaṁ
tvāmindirā⋅virala⋅rūpa⋅bharāṁ bhajāmi?

tvāṁ pracchadena mudira⋅cchavinā pidhāya
mañjīra⋅mukta⋅caraṇāñca vidhāya devi!
kuñje vrajendra⋅tanayena virājamāne
naktaṁ kadā pramuditāmabhisārayiṣye?

kuñje prasūna⋅kula⋅kalpita⋅keli⋅talpe
saṁviṣṭayor⋅madhura⋅narma⋅vilāsa⋅bhājoḥ
loka⋅trayābharaṇayoścaraṇāmbujāni
samvāhayiṣyati kadā juvayorjano‛yam?

tvat⋅kuṇḍa⋅rodhasi vilāsa⋅pariśrameṇa
svedāmbu⋅cumbi⋅vadanāmburuha⋅śriyau vām
vṛndāvaneśvari! kadā taru-mūla-bhājau
samvījayāmi camarī⋅caya⋅cāmareṇa?

līnāṁ nikuñja⋅kuhare bhavatīṁ mukunde
citraiva sūcitavatī rucirākṣi! nāham
bhugnāṁ bhruvaṁ na racayeti mṛṣā⋅ruṣāṁ tvā⋅
magre vrajendra⋅tanayasya kadā nu neṣye?

vāgyuddha⋅keli⋅kutuke vraja⋅rāja⋅sūnuṁ
jitvonmadāmadhika⋅darpa⋅vikāsi⋅jalpām
phullābhir⋅ālibhir⋅analpam⋅udīryamāṇa⋅
stotrāṁ kadā nu bhavatīmavalokayiṣye?

jaḥ ko‛pi suṣṭhu vṛṣabhānu⋅kumārikāyāḥ
samprārthanāṣṭakam⋅idaṁ paṭhati prapannaḥ
sā preyasā saha sametya dhṛta⋅pramodā
tatra prasāda⋅laharīmurarī⋅karoti
IDX.en_translation:
O Devi! Be pleased! Bestow mercy upon me! Show me the pair of lotus faces of You two sporting here in the play-groves of Vṛndāvana with the playful movements of the best of intoxicated elephants.

Alas, O Devi! With a voice choked with the weight of piteous words, falling on the ground like a stick, I beg with intense distress: making this ignorant person an object of Your mercy, O Gāndharvikā, count me among Your own people.

O Śyāmā! When will I worship You, whose body is bound by the left arm of Śyāma, who has enchanted all the people of the universe with a beauty superior to the beauty of the Husband of Ramā (Viṣṇu), and who possesses a wealth of form rare even for Indirā?

O Devi! Covering You with a cloth of the color of a cloud and removing the anklets from Your feet, when will I joyfully make You go on a tryst at night in the bower where the Son of the King of Vraja is shining?

When will this person massage the lotus feet of You two, the ornaments of the three worlds, who are seated on a play-bed made of multitudes of flowers in the bower and enjoying sweet, joking pastimes?

O Queen of Vṛndāvana! When will I fan You two with a yak-tail whisk, seated at the root of a tree on the bank of Your Kuṇḍa, Your lotus faces kissing the drops of sweat from the fatigue of pastimes?

O beautiful-eyed one! You are hiding in the hollow of a bower with Mukunda. "Citra herself indicated it; not I!"—when will I lead You, who are thus feigning anger with knitted eyebrows, before the Son of the King of Vraja?

When will I see You, having conquered the Son of the King of Vraja in the curiosity of a war of words, intoxicated with pride, with speech expanding with great arrogance, being praised profusely by the blooming friends?

Whoever surrenders and recites this prayer of eight verses to the Daughter of Vṛṣabhānu nicely, She, meeting with Her Beloved and holding joy, accepts a wave of mercy towards him.

IDX.first_line: vrsabha danuja nasan antaram jat sva gosthim
IDX.search: vrsabha danuja nasan antaram jat sva gosthim ayasi vrsabha satro ma sprsa tvam vadantyam iti vrsa ravi putryam krsna parsnim prakhatam tad ati vimala niram syama kundam gatir me tri jagati nivasad jat tirtha vrndam tamoghnam vraja nrpati kumarenahrtam tat samagram svayam idam avagadham jan mahimnah prakasam tad ati vimala niram syama kundam gatir me
IDX.author: śrīla raghunāthadāsa gosvāmī
IDX.verses:
vṛṣabha⋅danuja⋅nāśān⋅antaraṁ jat sva⋅goṣṭhīm
ayasi vṛṣabha⋅śatro mā spṛśa tvaṁ vadantyām
iti vṛṣa⋅ravi⋅putryāṁ kṛṣṇa⋅pārṣṇiṁ prakhātaṁ
tad ati⋅vimala⋅nīraṁ śyāma⋅kuṇḍaṁ gatir me

tri⋅jagati nivasad jat tīrtha⋅vṛndaṁ tamoghnaṁ
vraja⋅nṛpati⋅kumāreṇāhṛtaṁ tat samagram
svayam idam avagāḍhaṁ jan mahimnaḥ prakāśaṁ
tad ati⋅vimala⋅nīraṁ śyāma⋅kuṇḍaṁ gatir me

jad ati⋅vimala⋅nīre tīrtha⋅rūpe praśaste
tvam api kuru kṛśāṅgi! snānam atraiva rādhe
iti vinaya⋅vacobhiḥ prārthanā⋅kṛt sa kṛṣṇas
tad ati⋅vimala⋅nīraṁ śyāma⋅kuṇḍaṁ gatir me

vṛṣabha⋅danuja⋅nāśād uttha⋅pāpaṁ samāptaṁ
dyumaṇi⋅sakha⋅jayoccair varjayitveti tīrthaṁ
nijam akhila⋅sakhībhiḥ kuṇḍam eva prakāśyaṁ
tad ati⋅vimala⋅nīraṁ śyāma⋅kuṇḍaṁ gatir me

jad ati sakala⋅tīrthais tyakta⋅vākyaiḥ prabhītaiḥ
sa⋅vinayam abhiyuktaiḥ kṛṣṇa⋅candre nivedya
agatika⋅gati⋅rādhā varjanān no gatiḥ kā
tad ati⋅vimala⋅nīraṁ śyāma⋅kuṇḍaṁ gatir me

jad ati⋅vikala⋅tīrthaṁ kṛṣṇa⋅candraṁ prasusthaṁ
ati⋅laghu⋅nati⋅vākyaiḥ suprasanneti rādhā
vividha⋅caṭula⋅vākyaiḥ prārthanāḍhyā bhavantī
tad ati⋅vimala⋅nīraṁ śyāma⋅kuṇḍaṁ gatir me

jad ati⋅lalita⋅pādais tāṁ prasādyāpta⋅tair thyais
tad atiśaya⋅kṛpārdraiḥ saṅgamena praviṣṭaiḥ
vraja⋅nava⋅yuva⋅rādhā⋅kuṇḍam eva prapannaṁ
tad ati⋅vimala⋅nīraṁ śyāma⋅kuṇḍaṁ gatir me

jad ati⋅nikaṭa⋅tīre klapta⋅kuñjaṁ suramyaṁ
subala⋅baṭu⋅mukhebhyo rādhikādyaiḥ pradattam
vividha⋅kusuma⋅vallī⋅kalpa⋅vṛkṣādi⋅rājaṁ
tad ati⋅vimala⋅nīraṁ śyāma⋅kuṇḍaṁ gatir me

paripaṭhati sumedhāḥ śyāma⋅kuṇḍāṣṭakaṁ jo
nava⋅jaladhara⋅rūpe svarṇa⋅kāntyāṁ ca rāgāt
vraja⋅narapati⋅putras tasya labhyaḥ suśīghraṁ
saha sa⋅gaṇa⋅sakhībhī rādhayā syāt subhajyaḥ
IDX.en_translation:
After the slaying of the bull demon, when Rādhā told Lord Kṛṣṇa, "O enemy of the bull! Do not touch Me!", Kṛṣṇa dug this pond with His heel. That very pure water of Śyāma-kuṇḍa is my refuge.

All the holy places in the three worlds that destroy darkness were brought together by the Prince of Vraja. He personally immersed Himself in this pond, whose glories are now manifest. That very pure water of Śyāma-kuṇḍa is my refuge.

Lord Kṛṣṇa, with humble words, requested Rādhā: "O slender-limbed one! O Rādhā! Please take a bath here in this pure water, which is praised as a holy place." That very pure water of Śyāma-kuṇḍa is my refuge.

Any sin arising from the slaying of the bull demon was considered finished by Lord Kṛṣṇa after He bathed here. But Rādhā, having victoriously rejected this holy place, manifested Her own pond along with all Her friends. That very pure water of Śyāma-kuṇḍa is my refuge.

When all other holy places, terrified and speechless, humbly approached the moon-like Kṛṣṇa and submitted: "If Rādhā, the refuge of the helpless, avoids us, then what refuge do we have?", the Lord showed His mercy. That very pure water of Śyāma-kuṇḍa is my refuge.

When the other holy places were extremely distressed and Lord Kṛṣṇa was present, Rādhā became greatly pleased by their very humble words and prostrations, and then She Herself became rich with various playful prayers to the Lord. That very pure water of Śyāma-kuṇḍa is my refuge.

Having pleased Her with their humble approach, all the holy places obtained Her own pond. They entered with excessive mercy, gathered together, and took refuge in the pond of the youthful Rādhā of Vraja. That very pure water of Śyāma-kuṇḍa is my refuge.

On its nearby bank, charming groves are arranged. These groves were given to Subala and the Lord's other friends by Śrīmati Rādhikā and Her companions. It is a royal place, abounding with various flowers, creepers, and desire trees. That very pure water of Śyāma-kuṇḍa is my refuge.

Whoever, being intelligent, recites these eight verses in praise of Śyāma-kuṇḍa with love for Him who is like a fresh cloud and for Her with golden effulgence, quickly attains the Son of the King of Vraja. He will become worshipable by Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa and Their gopī companions.

IDX.first_line: vrsabha danuja nasan narma dharmokti rangair
IDX.search: vrsabha danuja nasan narma dharmokti rangair nikhila nija sakhibhir jat sva hastena purnam prakatitam api vrndaranya rajnya pramodais tad ati surabhi radha kundam evasrayo me vraja bhuvi mura satroh preyasinam nikamair asulabham api turnam prema kalpa drumam tam janayati hrdi bhumau snatur uccaih priyam jat tad ati surabhi radha kundam evasrayo me
IDX.author: śrīla raghunāthadāsa gosvāmī
IDX.verses:
vṛṣabha⋅danuja⋅nāśān narma⋅dharmokti⋅raṅgair
nikhila⋅nija⋅sakhībhir jat sva⋅hastena pūrṇam
prakaṭitam api vṛndāraṇya⋅rājñyā pramodais
tad ati⋅surabhi⋅rādhā⋅kuṇḍam evāśrayo me

vraja⋅bhuvi mura⋅śatroḥ preyasīnāṁ nikāmair
asulabham api tūrṇaṁ prema⋅kalpa⋅drumaṁ tam
janayati hṛdi bhūmau snātur uccaiḥ priyaṁ jat
tad ati⋅surabhi⋅rādhā⋅kuṇḍam evāśrayo me

agha⋅ripur api jatnād atra devyāḥ prasāda⋅
prasara⋅kṛta⋅kaṭākṣa⋅prāpti⋅kāmaḥ prakāmam
anusarati jad uccaiḥ snāna⋅sevānu⋅bandhais
tad ati⋅surabhi⋅rādhā⋅kuṇḍam evāśrayo me

vraja⋅bhuvana⋅sudhāṁśoḥ prema⋅bhūmir nikāmaṁ
vraja⋅madhura⋅kiśorī⋅mauli⋅ratna⋅priyeva
paricitam api nāmnā jac ca tenaiva tasyās
tad ati⋅surabhi⋅rādhā⋅kuṇḍam evāśrayo me

api jana iha kaścid jasya sevā⋅prasādaiḥ
praṇaya⋅sura⋅latā syāt tasya goṣṭhendra⋅sūnoḥ
sapadi kila mad⋅īśā⋅dāsya⋅puṣpa⋅praśasyā
tad ati⋅surabhi⋅rādhā⋅kuṇḍam evāśrayo me

taṭa⋅madhura⋅nikuñjāḥ klṛpta⋅nāmāna uccair
nija⋅parijana⋅vargaiḥ saṁvibhajyāśritās taiḥ
madhukara⋅ruta⋅ramyā jasya rājanti kāmyās
tad ati⋅surabhi⋅rādhā⋅kuṇḍam evāśrayo me

taṭa⋅bhuvi vara⋅vedyāṁ jasya narmāti⋅hṛdyāṁ
madhura⋅madhura⋅vārtāṁ goṣṭha⋅candrasya bhaṅgyā
prathayati mitha īśā prāṇa⋅sakhyālibhiḥ sā
tad ati⋅surabhi⋅rādhā⋅kuṇḍam evāśrayo me

anudinam ati⋅raṅgaiḥ prema⋅mattāli⋅saṅghair
vara⋅sarasija⋅gandhair hāri⋅vāri⋅prapūrṇe
viharata iha jasmin dam⋅patī tau pramattau
tad ati⋅surabhi⋅rādhā⋅kuṇḍam evāśrayo me

avikalam ati devyāś cāru kuṇḍāṣṭakaṁ jaḥ
paripaṭhati tadīyollāsi⋅dāsyārpitātmā
aciram iha śarīre darśayaty eva tasmai
madhu⋅ripur ati⋅modaiḥ śliṣyamāṇāṁ priyāṁ tāma
IDX.en_translation:
After the slaying of the bull demon, when Rādhā and Her gopī friends filled this pond with Their own hands after a playful dispute with Lord Kṛṣṇa, and even though it was manifested with great joy by the Queen of Vṛndāvana herself—that extremely fragrant Rādhā-kuṇḍa alone is my refuge.

In the sacred land of Vraja, for the dearest beloveds of Lord Kṛṣṇa, this pond quickly generates the desire-tree of divine love in the heart of anyone who bathes here. To that soul it is exceedingly dear. That extremely fragrant Rādhā-kuṇḍa alone is my refuge.

Even the enemy of the Agha demon, Lord Kṛṣṇa, intensely desires to attain the merciful side-long glance of Śrīmati Rādhā⋅rāṇī here and eagerly follows Her while performing the service of bathing. That extremely fragrant Rādhā-kuṇḍa alone is my refuge.

This is intensely the land of love for the moon of Vraja, Lord Kṛṣṇa, and it is as dear to Him as the crown-jewel of the gopīs herself. Even though known by Her Name, it is intimately connected to Her. That extremely fragrant Rādhā-kuṇḍa alone is my refuge.

If any person here, by the grace of serving this pond, may quickly have the creeper of divine love for the Son of Nanda Mahārāja manifest in his heart—service which is praised as the flower of devotion to my Mistress. That extremely fragrant Rādhā-kuṇḍa alone is my refuge.

The sweet groves on its banks, given beautiful names by Her own associates, are divided and sheltered by them. These groves are charming with the humming of bees and shine with desirable features. That extremely fragrant Rādhā-kuṇḍa alone is my refuge.

On the beautiful platform on its bank, the Mistress reveals sweet and charming topics concerning the moon of Vraja among Her dearest friends. That extremely fragrant Rādhā-kuṇḍa alone is my refuge.

Daily, with great delight and accompanied by the gopīs who are intoxicated with love, the Divine Couple sports here in the captivating waters filled with the fragrance of lotuses. That extremely fragrant Rādhā-kuṇḍa alone is my refuge.

Whoever flawlessly recites these eight verses about the beautiful pond of the Goddess, with a soul dedicated to Her joyful service—the enemy of Madhu will very soon, even in this very body, reveal to him his Beloved Rādhā as He embraces Her with great joy.

IDX.first_line: vrsabhanu nandini nava anuragini
IDX.search: vrsabhanu nandini nava anuragini turite karala abhisara sangini rangini prema tarangini mandira hoi bahara caloite carana nupura tahi bolata sumadhura rasala hamsa gamane dhani aola vinodini sakhigana kari sei satha
IDX.author: govinda dāsa
IDX.verses:
vṛṣabhānu nandinī, nava anurāginī,
turite karala abhisāra
saṅginī raṅginī, prema taraṅginī,
mandira hoi bāhāra

caloite caraṇa, nupura tahi̐ bolata,
sumadhura rasāla
haṁsa gamane dhanī, āola vinodinī,
sakhīgaṇa kari sei sātha

rasika nāgara vara, vidagadha śekhara,
turite milala dhanī pāśa
du̐hu dohā daraśane, ulasita locane,
nirakhata govinda dāsa
IDX.en_translation:
The daughter of Vṛṣabhānu, newly filled with love, quickly set out for Her secret meeting. Her cheerful companions, like waves of love, emerged from the palace.

As Her feet moved, Her anklets jingled, producing a very sweet and charming sound. With a swan-like gait, that delightful Lady arrived, accompanied by Her friends.

The playful and clever Lord Kṛṣṇa, the crest-jewel of the wise, quickly met the Lady by Her side. As They both gazed upon each other, Their eyes shone with joy, as observed by Govinda Dāsa.

IDX.first_line: vrsabhanusuta carana sevane
IDX.search: vrsabhanusuta carana sevane hoiba je palyadasi sriradhara sukha satata sadhane rahiba ami prayasi sriradhara sukhe krsnera je sukha janiba manete ami radhapada chadi sri krsna sangame kabhu na hoiba kami
IDX.author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
IDX.verses:
vṛṣabhānu-sutā⋅, caraṇa⋅sevane,
hoiba je pālyadāsī
śrī-rādhāra sukha, satata sādhane,
rahiba āmi prayāsī

śrī-rādhāra sukhe, kṛṣṇera je sukha,
jāniba manete āmi
rādhā-pada chāḍi’, śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅saṅgame,
kabhu nā hoiba kāmī

sakhī-gaṇa mama, parama suhṛt,
jugala⋅premera guru
tada-nugā haye, seviba rādhāra,
caraṇa⋅kalpataru

rādhā-pakṣa chāḍi’, je⋅jana se⋅jana,
jebhāve sebhāve thāke
āmi ta’ rādhikā⋅, pakṣa-pātī sadā,
kabhu nāhi heri tā̐’ke
IDX.translation_intro: From The Harmonist, October 1932. Edited by Bhaktisiddhanta Sarasvati.
IDX.en_translation:
For serving the Feet Of the Daughter of Brishavanu I shall be Her protected maid; To serve constantly The pleasures of Sree Radha Shall be my endeavour.

The Pleasure of Krishna By Sree Radha’s Happiness I shall experience in my mind; Abandoning Radha’s Feet To seek my union with Sree Krishna Shall never be my desire.

The sakhis are My best friends, Teachers of love frr the Divine Pair; By following their example I shall serve The Purpose-Tree of the Feet of Radha

Whoever the person, Whatever his ways, Who breaks away from Radha, Myself who am Radha’s constant partisan Shall never brook his sight.

śrī-rādhāra sukhe, kṛṣṇera je sukha,jāniba manete āmirādhā-pada chāḍi’, śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅saṅgame,kabhu nā hoiba kāmī

sakhī-gaṇa mama, parama suhṛt,jugala⋅premera gurutada-nugā haye, seviba rādhāra,caraṇa⋅kalpataru

rādhā-pakṣa chāḍi’, je⋅jana se⋅jana,jebhāve sebhāve thākeāmi ta’ rādhikā⋅, pakṣa-pātī sadā,kabhu nāhi heri tā̐’ke
IDX.translation_intro: From The Harmonist, October 1932. Edited by Bhaktisiddhanta Sarasvati.
IDX.en_translation:
For serving the Feet Of the Daughter of Vṛṣabhānu I shall be Her protected maid; To serve constantly The pleasures of Sree Rādhā Shall be my endeavour.

The Pleasure of Krishna By Sree Radha’s Happiness I shall experience in my mind; Abandoning Radha’s Feet To seek my union with Sree Krishna Shall never be my desire.

The sakhīs are My best friends, Teachers of love frr the Divine Pair; By following their example I shall serve The Purpose-Tree of the Feet of Radha

Whoever the person, Whatever his ways, Who breaks away from Rādhā, Myself who am Rādhā’s constant partisan Shall never brook his sight.

IDX.first_line: yadi gauranga nahita tabe ki hoitakemane dharita de radhara mahima premarasa simajagate janata ke
IDX.search: yadi gauranga nahita tabe ki hoitakemane dharita de radhara mahima premarasa simajagate janata ke madhura vrnda vipina madhuri pravesa caturi saravaraja yuvati bhavera bhakatisakati hoita kara
IDX.author: narahari⋅dāsa
IDX.verses:
(yadi) gaurāṅga nahita, tabe ki hoita,kemane dharita de ?rādhāra mahimā, premarasa⋅sīmā,jagate jānāta ke

madhura vṛndā⋅, vipina⋅mādhurī⋅,praveśa cāturī⋅sāravaraja⋅yuvatī⋅, bhāvera bhakati,śakati hoita kā’ra

gāo punaḥ punaḥ, gaurāṅgera guṇa,sarala hoiyā manae bhava⋅sāgare, emana dayāla,nā dekhi je ekajana

gaurāṅga baliyā, nā genu galiyā,kemane dharinu denarahari⋅hiyā, pāṣāṇa diyā,kemane gaḍiyāche
IDX.en_translation:
If there were no Gaurāṅga, then what would have happened? How would I maintain my body? Who would have told the world about the glories of Rādhā and the limit of the rasa of love?

The sweetness of the forest of sweet Vṛndā, the essence of the skill to enter it, the devotion of the mood of the damsels of Vraja—whose power would there be (to know this)?

Sing again and again the qualities of Gaurāṅga, keeping the mind simple. In this ocean of material existence, I do not see even one person as merciful as Him.

Saying “Gaurāṅga”, I did not melt; how have I maintained my body? Narahari’s heart—how has the Creator fashioned it with stone?
